Comment: Grubby, grotty covered in rubbish. What a tragedy for what was once a beautiful pristine beach.

We were on 18.11.2025 at An Bang Beach. Unfortunately, the beach is not a pretty sight after the storms, everything is full of garbage. The water is very brown and there is plenty of wood and garbage floating in the water. The beach bars are in the process of being cleaned up.

Filthy beach, I really don’t get all the glowing reviews. Not sure if these people are blind or have never been to a beautiful beach before. Rubbish everywhere, very run down - reminds me of Bali. Seems to be popular with backpackers and Russians. Dont bother!

We went as a family in December 2024. It is one of the most dirty beaches I have ever seen. Absolutely unpleasant to walk along the beach.  It is shocking , the beach is full polyester and plastic . I do not recommend to spend a beach stay here.

Due to the typhoon many beach bars have unfortunately been severely damaged and many things have not yet been repaired. Unfortunately, the beach is more like a dump than a dream beach. One can only hope that everything is cleaned again. Right now, you have to advise against visiting.

The beach is catastrophically littered. Why doesn't anybody put the plastic stuff away? In addition, you can not walk continuously at the sea at high tide. Due to increasing erosion, the beach is getting narrower and narrower. Everywhere you have to pay: for toilet and bicycle parking. And you are constantly approached by sellers.

I don't understand why both Lonely Planet and any website and various reviews list it as this great paradise. It's super touristy, all overpriced, hyper crowded. It is a large beach with a succession of restaurants, bars, with umbrellas and chairs literally attached to each other (LITERALLY), as soon as you arrive everyone falls on you trying to sell your place on the beach, if you consume you do not have to pay, mediocre food format tourists, otherwise they ask you random figures (who 200,000, who 150,000, you can bargain for 100,000), they ask you for money to park even the bicycle, and complete with a sign with price list, where for the bike are indicated 2,000 dongs, they still ask you for 5,000. In short, everything is a half scam to take money.You can safely park in the alleys first, and go to the beach without using umbrellas. But above all: you can NOT go there and opt for Cua Dai, much more beautiful, natural and crowd-free

Cyclists- BEWARE aggressive, threatening and rude people forcibly trying to make you stop and park before you get to the beach so they can scam you into paying for bicycle parking!! It seems there are multiple liars targeting people from the west and/or anyone on bikes who can't tell them to get lost in Vietnamese.The picture of the street is where you should start being alert for these con artists.In particular there is a man who will run out from Restaurant Phuong Uyen (not seen but to the right of the tree in the picture showing the store front with the writing in blue and red) as soon as he sees you approaching to say you can't cycle further down or park. He forced us off the road and literally pulled my bike away to park it before we could do much. We didn't really believe him, but walked on and then read about these tricksters when we checked online. Alas too late, but hopefully this will save some of you.Since it's only VND10k most people said they paid we thought that was fine to let go. However, the behaviour is so not necessary, malicious and mean and the main reason for this review.That guy from that restaurant will get in your face and demand money as soon as he sees you get back. He literally ran over as soon as he saw us at the bikes again and was screaming for money. He tried to block me in to prevent me even being able to manoeuvre out. So he's not a nice man to target a woman specifically and shout at me to give him money. I wanted to give him a piece of my mind, but my husband was mindful this guy was so aggressive he told the guy to just wait and let me pass or he wouldn't give him anything. Honestly with some reflection we should have got the police on him. It's clear he does this to many people and it's intimidation and malicious. Although not sure what use the tourist police are.Do not pay more than VND10k if you're checking this after having been tricked! You can park your bike for free in the streets it seems or at a restaurant if you're planning to eat there, if you haven't been lied to yet and are just looking up the beach.If your hotel has a shuttle bus service use that!On to the beach area- it's alright. There's signs saying no swimming in places but people were in the water. At your own risk! The sea looked a bit rough but not massive waves that will knock you down. You can do surfing and parasailing here. Sea is not clear. Beach could be cleaner.It's basically a resort town full of foreigners. Dining options along the shore. If you want a lounger/beach chairs they have those too and will serve you where you sit. There are ladies who will try to get you to come to their restaurant as soon as you walk down the steps and I think one tried to get these tourists to follow her even tho another one was trying to lead them in the opposite direction. A firm no is fine. They won't bother you more. Unlike that sorry excuse for a man making money as a bicycle parking attendant, they take no for an answer.

Be aware that although the signs clearly say 4k for motorbike parking the old hag will insist you pay 20k (if you are white) the other man agreed to 5k before the woman ran over and snatched our ticket and told us to leave. Basically, she insists you either pay 5x the amount on the sign or you have to leave. Aside from this the beach is very nice, just don’t give in to racially based scamming. Better to Park across the street by a restaurant instead and spend your money on a drink inside

Absolutely disgusting! And that's putting it mildly. Seriously the whole beach no matter which direction you walk in is littered with every kind of rubbish imaginable. Inclusive of a dead dog ! I kid you not. Seeing the state of this beach made me realise just how spoilt Australia is for beaches. Whilst having a lovely coffee and incredible croissant earlier on at  Thom's Sourdough Bakery, I overheard an American young couple who presumably have been living in the area for sometime. I heard them mention to the owner that they were going to head to the beach later on and do some rubbish collection. After seeing the beach I admire their selfless volunteering.But it's really the people of Hoi An and An Bang along with the authorities who should really be stepping up and come up with a plan to seriously turn things around. I would not be swimming or walking barefoot on this beach until some serious efforts are made, and I don't see things improving any time soon or quickly. It truly is a shame as they could have something special. Sadly It's a lonnnnnnnggggg way off being anywhere near that.

The beach is ruined by the amount of rubbish that is washed up each day but not cleared away. I will never understand why people who rely on the tourist industry but then fail to clear up the mess . When will they realise that by going green and positively promoting recycling will be a great selling point for the resort. Unfortunately it's not just the beach but the surrounding area that would also benefit from a good clean up

We have just been to An Bang beach and found our feet and thongs covered in black tar spots. We didn’t see anything walking in the sand but later discovered them. We had to scrub our thongs and feet. Very worrying as our children had these all over their feet. Luckily we did not swim. After searching on the internet apparently it is beach tar. I gave the one star rating purely for the cooling sea breeze.
Känns som om alla turister styrs hit. Finns gott om fina stränder runtomkring som är mycket bättre. Men gillar man Gran Canaria så gillar man An Bang.
Scruffy, dirty and a mess unfortunately. I would imagine at the beginning of the season they make sure it's nice but certainly its not at the moment.... How disappointing!
Terrible beach. The water is very dirty looking and when you walk along the beach all you see is plastic bottles and other rubbish everywhere. Left after about 20 minutes. Would not go back

We went to this beach and it was full of plastic bottles, plastic bags, polystyrene and lightbulbs (!). We put some of them in the bin but we weren't here for long. I don't know how the people can put their towel among the trash, that is disgusting. And therefore crooks are trying to make you pay 5 dollars to let your bike next to the beach.
Extremely crowded, touristy and overpriced.  Dumping murky waves. Sooo disappointed. The world has so much better to offer, so huge pass, you can do better ☹

Careful when riding your bike here, as there are people trying to force you to park at their spot and pay them for it. I had a guy run after me and grab my bike, preventing me from passing him in an attempt to convince me to park at his lot. You can park for free at the restaurants as long as you get a drink there, so don't fall for that.Also the beach is unbelievably dirty, with trash all over the sand and a thick foam layer of pollution on the water. See the attached pictures to judge for yourself, but I prefer to swim far away from used condoms and industrial waste...
I felt harrassed every 10 seconds by the local sellers... The best thing to do is to walk a lot further and to avoid this place at all cost.Beach is dirty.

My friend and I came to the beack and spent about 15 minutes there. We payed 50k per chair, for dirty and uncomfortable chairs. The beach itself was full of garbage and very agressive salespeople. No bathrooms or anything of the sort, and a pretty fishy smell too.

패러 세일링 구경하는데 타러오라고 키 160cm 나이 30대 중반 아저씨가 허리 잡으려하고 데려감. 구명조끼 입는데 가슴쪽이 작으니 구명쪽기가 작다면서 다시 벗는데 개저씨가 양쪽 엄지로 가슴 꾹 눌러봄. 여자 두명이서 해외 와서 얼타고 아무말못했던게 개빡침. 서양남자들한테는 잘 말도 못걸더니. 게다가 한국인들이 한국말로 크게 얘기하는거 듣고 웃으면서 우다우다라고하며 비꼬듯 따라함. 지들 나라말 듣고 꽥꽥꽥 하며 따라하면 좋겠음? 그 개저씨 때문에 기분 잡침. 이 일을 계기로 여행시 개저씨 트라우마 생겼음.
Ang Bang Beach was fine,Under no circumstanses try the parasiling unless you want injury and enjoy pain.Looks like fun but its a nightmare.You have been warned.
Sadly the Vietnam government need to address the rubbish and pollution problems. Not just at the beach everywhere we went there was dumping of plastic bags full of rubbish. It’s so sad to see.
Heel erg druk. Weg er naar toe is lang, moet met taxi. Verschillende eetgelegenheden. Ellende om daar weer weg te komen
Obrovské množství lidi, přestože není sezóna, lehátka pod slunečníky (nutné, velmi silné slunce) za 100, jedno lehatko nalepene na druhem. Mnoho odpadků v písku.

Os van a cobrar por aparcar la moto,la bici,por tumbarte,.... negaros absolutamente es free, iros a la esquina siguiente y dejad la moto. AUNQUE OS CHILLEN.Yo no cogería resorts con playa privada porque no merece nada la pena.Los que hayáis estado en las Perhentian o Ko Samui,...no merece la pena estar ni dos días en estas playas.

We rode from our resort and left all our money at home so we it didn’t get it stolen. Locals wouldn’t let us leave the bikes unless we paid them at one beach and then another only if we bought food. We rode 10 km in the heat and were refused to even swim. We spent a lot of money in this country on our holiday and frankly I will never return and spend my money in my own country (Australia) which has free beaches without hagglers and much nicer too. i would t even call it a Bach rather a murky lake.

Typical of many southern-Asian shores: a few spots look quite nice, and there they try to charge you for any little thing, like attaching your rental bicycle, and if you don’t comply instantaneously, there comes someone with a whistle to tell you to move on. Instead of annoying tourists like mosquitoes, these people should clean their environment. Trash is everywhere, the minute you step away from their protected turf. And no need to even mention the quality of the sea water, if you look around, you’ll know these are not mountain streams that go into the sea. Thousands of Europe pan beaches are way better. Disappointing

Crowded dirty and noisy...the worst side of Hoi An, now the once delightful beach is blotted out by straw umbrellas and recliners...'Hidden Beach', further down towards Hoi An much nicer.much cleaner and quieter!
Beach too crowdy ! Lot of Vietnamese and have to pay to park ... hidden beach is so much better. I don’t recommend to go there.
To many tourists packed together liked sardines with an Aussie sound floating over your head. A place to avoid.However , the beach itself is very good

I travel from Hoi An with easy ride, and stopped at this beach, the local children and adults play with plastic bottles, plastoforrm and plastic bags, making the beach very dirty and leaving it all behind. The restaurant doesn't seem interested to clean the place. It was the worst food I had and the most expensive.
Don't go here, unless you like floating in other tourists and people selling stuff, in that case you have found the right place!
Une enfilade de restaurants et de transats. Les transats sont payants beaucoup trop de vendeuses qui vous harcelent.

En séjour sur Hoi An, nous avons loué un scooter pour longer la côte et les plages. Nous nous sommes arrêtés sur cette plage qui a été une grande déception. Enchaînement de restaurants touristiques et de transats sur la plage qui a été privatisée. Vue nulle a part un alignement de transat et de parasol. Déception
Super druk en erg toeristisch. Geen bijzonder strandje... Als je aan komt fietsen trekken ze je al van de straat om ergens de fiets te parkeren. Waardeloos.
Surprenant de voir la mer grignoter la plage les transats sont pour les premières lignes dans l eau bcp de restaurant assez bon si on mange local
I must say that the an bang beach is the most disappointing point in our journey... my recommendation is not to go there!

I’ve lived in Asia for nearly two decades and seen a lot of Asian beaches. This one is a giant ash tray / trash pit that is over crowded and ruined by 100s of thatch roof umbrellas and aggressive local males in the parking areas and touting services. Hoi An as a whole is wonderful, they do an amazing job maintaining the city but the beach is a mess. I wouldn’t recommend or return to this beach. I’m sure there are surrounding beaches that are nicer and we will explore to find them, but don’t waste your time here if you’ve already been to picturesque Asian beaches that can be quite stunning. This will be a huge disappointment unless you’re here in the off season and there’s no one around. For reference, the My Khe beach in Da Nang is MUCH nicer.

How do I start???The beach is terrible and not cleanThe restaurant is horrible, the good is disgusting, the tables had table cloth with holes, the service is not service... So, in the end of the day is a touristic trap!!!
there is no beach, they are trying to fix but you cannot swim here and no beach to sit on. washed away in a storm some time ago (years)

Achtung absolute Touristenabzocke!!!!!Wir hatten heute vor einen schönen Tag am Strand zu verbringen und wurden am Anfang bitter enttäuscht. Wir wurden mit dem Taxi zum Hauptstrand gebracht an dem sich ein Restaurant und ein kleiner Markt befand. Wir begaben uns zum Strand und wurden gleich von Strandverkäufern umzingelt. Wir suchten uns einen Liegestuhl und uns wurde gesagt, wenn wir später im Restaurant essen würden, wäre die Liege gratis. Als wir nach einer knappen Stunde die Menükarte am Restaurant betrachteten, sahen wir wie unverschämt hoch die Preise waren. 200 000 VND für 1 Fischgericht!!!Wir erklärten den Herren, dass wir nicht im Restaurant speißen würden und wir etwas weiter den Strand raufgehen wollen, von uns wurde dann verlangt 100 000 VND pro Liege zu zahlen für die Stunde die wir sie nutzten!Wir waren damit natürlich nicht einverstanden und die Herren wurden nach einer kurzen Verhandlung richtig unangenehm und frech ( einer trat Sand nach uns!!!!) Wir bezahlten schließlich 150 000 VND für 3 Liegen und wurden lautstark beschimpft und aufgefordert den Strand zu verlassen.Ich kann keinem empfehlen den Strand an dieser Stelle zu besuchen, 15 min entfernt fanden wie nämlich ein wunderschönes ruhiges Plätzchen, ohne lästige Verkäufer!
EXTREMELY dangerous “sport” they don’t care about your life only your money!!! Go like crazy next to the beach.Please don’t risk your life for this dangerous experience
Plage non entretenue,beaucoup de déchets traînent,huttes détruites par le vent laissées en l état....
Underwhelmed, dirty and disgusting. I’m sorry I wasted my time going out to look at this place. I wouldn’t even swim here.
La plage est jonchée de déchets, en marchant 3 min on a vu 5 seringues dans le sable. C’est lamentable de ne pas entretenir cette plage qui pourtant semble être une des fiertés de la ville !
Wir waren Anfang Dezember in einem Homestay und wollen eigentlich ab und zu wenigstens einen Strandspaziergang machen. Aber bei dem Müll vergeht einem das sehr schnell. Schade.
Rubbish and filth everywhere.We have such beautiful clean beaches in Australia so this was nothing short of disappointing.So glad we chose to stay in Ancient Town.

How Trip Advisor sees Ang Bang Beach VS RealityA ghost town of rubbish and unswimable seas. ?Seasonable weather or something more permanent? However the way it looked I would guess it has been like that for awhile with no intention of remediation. One circle because of the disparity between what is advertised and reality.  Very sad.

쓰레기와 나무등이 널부러진채 방치되고 있음.태풍이 휩쓸고 지나간지 몇일 지났지만 복구안됨우기에 갔는데 물은 탁하고 하늘은 흐리고쓰레기 굴러다니고 지붕 박살나고 난리도아님.아름답다고 많은사람들이 추천한건 예쁘게 꾸며진 카페에 바다가 있기때문인거고 사실 별거없음.약 15분정도 머물다가 바로 다른장소로 이동했는데날씨가 좋을때는 어떤지 몰라도 기본적으로 물가가 비싼편이고 유명한 레스토랑도 매우 실망스러웠음.시간내서 갈 정도는 아님
Het strand was gewoon een vuilnisbelt. Zoveel rotzooi spoelt eraan en het wordt niet opgeruimd. Misschien omdat het nu het regenseizoen is, ik mag hopen dat het er in het droge seizoen beter uitziet.
Don’t waste your time going to this beach!!The beach is covered in litter!!The local community is going to kill tourism if they don’t clean up their beaches!!
13-15 Décembre 2018... la période des pluies se poursuit. De forts vents, d’immenses vagues et de la température maussade. Dommage :(
Rubbish scattered along the beach. How times have changed, the beach was a nice place to take a stroll back in 2013 and even 2015. Not anymore! Not worth a visit.
Dyrt og dårligt, sikkert en udemærket strand men restauranterne er meget dyre i forhold til resten af Hoi An

Can’t say the pictures are a true depiction of what this beach really looks like. It is obvious that at one time money was put into making the beachfront look inviting. That time has gone. It has a tired and worn look and like Seminyak in Bali, rubbish now litters the shoreline.So disappointing - save your money - go to Australian beaches if you want clean, beautiful and safe.
Wir hatten auf weißen Sandstrand gehofft und sind auf einer Müllkippe gelandet.Auch der Weg zum Strand besteht nur aus Müll

People saying its a clean beach must have really low standards.Im staying at "an bang beach resort" and am glad i went swimming in the pool and not the ocean. It was disgusting. I walked 30 min up the beach and saw loads of plastic rubbish including a syringe which was concerning given the amount of kids playing here. I really enjoyed eden resteraunt for great value lunch and spa service and I loved salt pub for dinner and drinks but if you value your health id save the swimming for your hotel pool.

The beach is beautiful so it's a real shame you can't relax without being constantly hassled. Make sure you pick where you'd actually like to sit if you sit on the beach chairs as there are different sections and they rip into you if you dare move. Honestly worst beach experience I've ever had and I've been to a lot of places in Asia. Do not go here, there are much nicer places.

An average stretch of sand with some average restaurents (with staff with an attitude of “i dont Care about desent service) and some overpriced Beach-chair. We MUCH prefered the hotel-pool and the lush hotel-garden

엄마가 물을 굉장히 무서워하셔서 옷도 따로 안 챙겨 간 상태에서 하고싶어하시는 눈치길래 구입 후 타러감.문제는 여기서부터임 애초부터 구명조끼 입혀주는 사람 ㅆㄱㅈ였고 진짜 직원들한테도 수없이 말했ㅇ음 우리 엄마 수영 못하고 물 무서워하니까 좀 더 신경써달라고 떨어지면 우리 엄마 먼저 챙겨달라고 예상대로 엄마는 바다에 떨어지자마자 무서워서 어푸어푸거리면서 소리를 엄청 지르시면서 내 머리를 계속 물 밑으로 넣으시려해서 바닷물 이빠이먹고 엄마 진정시키는데 당연 진정 안되고 무튼 제트스키 타고 데릴러 온 애들이 당장 엄마를 건져주는게 아니라 지네끼리 빠개면서 우리 엄마 비명소리를 따라하는거임 계속  아무것도 안하고 지네끼리 웃으면서 너무 화가나서 우리 엄마 수영 못하니까 너 조심하고 신경쓰라고 얘기하는데 무시하고 계속 쳐 웃더라구요 그러다 엄마 데리고 가고 저 뒤에 태워서 허리춤 정도 깊이에 엉덩이 살짝 흔드는거마냥 날 떨구고 가더라구요 엄마 비명소리를 한번 더 따라하면서 .걸어나오면서 물에 귀 이빠이 차서 탈탈 털고 있는데 구명조끼 채워주는 직원이 온갖 썩은 표정을 하고 나한테 빨라 오라고 손가락 까딱까딱 두번하고 짝다리하고 쳐다보는데 진짜 인내심 한계였어요 미케비치도 있는데 굳이 안방에서 하지 마시길 직원들 진짜 두고두고 욕할거임
Laatste kans voor dat de grote resorts het overnemen. De ‘ banaan ‘ gesignaleerd als mede de speedboot met de bekende parachute. Jammer. Er is (nu nog?) een rustig stuk. Mooie zee.
I came here because it was famous . Then I went here it was so many people . I don't think I will come back this beach again .

On the way here today and got robbed next to the paid fields as you travel there by bike.whole bag snatched from the front of my bike. Guy must have followed me there and drove by at speed! Seems like crime is rife here.
Very average and brown water.Not a nice or clean as Danang beach.Such a shame as I booked to be close to this beach after reading reviews.

First it takes 25 minutes by bike to reach this beach. Second, the parking fee is two times higher than hoi an city. Lastly, the beach is not as good as my imagination (for a person who born in the island country, the beach is just meeh)

We went to the beach with bicycles and when we arrived, the woman who was the parking keeper (?) ask us to pay 50.000 VnD for 2 bicycles, meanwhile in Hoi An Ancient Town we just paid 10.000 VnD each. I don’t want to back to the beach again. It’s better visiting my own country’s beach.

This is a super crowded beach, lots of tourists, bad restaurants and it offers nothing but the feeling of being on a charter holiday in Spain. I left after 2 minutes, went approximately 2 km further up north on my bicycle and ended up at a beautiful little beach all by myself.
Nice beach but terrible food. We ate at the restaurant to the right of the beach front if you were looking at the beach. Bad food, bad service and more expensive than better places off the beach!

Préférez cua dai et sa tranquillité plutôt que la fête à neuneu et ses jets ski.la foule les parkings payants pour vélos! Les coups de sifflets de partout les transats gras.ok cuai dai est érodé mais calme et spacieuse. An bang c'est la côte d'azur en pire

Took a taxi to An Bang from Hoai An and we literally could not believe how many hundreds of people were in the water and on the beach. Sad state that in order to enjoy this lovely area, you have to book a private hotel with private beach access. Stayed to have over-priced cocktails and appetizers. Overall, it made me sad that Vietnam seems to be turning into Mataztlan but even worse.
Not a great beach. The sea was extremely rough and it was very windy. We couldn’t go anywhere near the water.
Skip it. The beach is dirty , over crowded. The swimming area is not coordonned off. While we were there someone on a water scooter kept coming too close to the are people were in the water.

I've been to Da Nang - Hoi An 2 times last year. But today is 3rd one i'm in Hoi An but the first time with An Bang beach. I & my friend just had really terrible experience here. We'd sit on 2 navy couches on the beach just 10 mins after arrived by Mai Linh taxi then i went back to the street to look for a bikini. After i came back, my jpn friend was talking with a woman who was supposed the owner of thise navy couchs. She was asking my friend to pay for 15 mins sitting on the couches 200,000 vnd if we wannted to continue to sit there, or we could leave right after oaying 100,000 vnd for 2 couches which she implied that she had compromise for us cause it was just in the morning and we were her first customers or sitter, lol. (Taxi driver said we didn't need to order food, just drinking was enough to sit there as long as we wanted.) The point here is before that woman asking, when we just sit there right after 1 min a man comming asked us if we wanted to sit on his couches which are different colour but none of it was navy and offerred for a menu of drink, not even mentioned about the fee for sitting. The women owner of navy couches claimed that we could sit on any seat of any restaurant on this beach the charge would be still the same. If i had have been in hanoi which was my hometown then that women could have been paid for her action. I've been on Phu Quoc island once and paid for the swing just 20,000 vnd and could borrow it as long as i wanted. The amount of money which that woman charged us today was so ridiculous. I have to say that i still can pay happily if she had been gentle with us. But she sounded like she was so angry (and actually every one there was so agressive person, how terrible the service there is). I just wanted to stay away from trouble to get back to The old town soon so my friend gave her 100,000 vnd and left. She felt guilty after that and gave us back 50,000 vnd. Then we went to the beach, left the luggeagues next to a couch. Then that woman went there and threw our luggeagues away far from the couches which were not navy agressivelly. I just don't know what happened with her. After that a man next to her area invited us to sit on his couches for free, just had to need to order drinks. But we said no. We were so tired with that cheat. And also the women who seemed to he un charge of the parking lot. We just sit accidently in a shadow which we didn't know it was for car parking, she insulted us like we even didn't have brains sitting there. Maybe it depends on every one and we just got bad luck today but definetely i won't ever sit on navy couches on this beach again.

Disgusting strip of gross sand mixed with pieces of plastic and cigarettes butts.So many longchairs and sun umbrellas that you can barely see the sea. Anyway there is nothing to miss: the water is muddy... Avoid it.

If you like crisp clean beaches, uncluttered with pleasant graduated entry to the sea then this is NOT the place for you.  Quite disappointing after reviews we had read.  Just so pleased that at the last minute we cancelled our beach based hotel & opted for one on the river in town.

It was  a real shame to see plastic being brought to shore at this beach. I thought we would be spending a lot of time at the beach while in Hoi An but the utter discard for the environment was a turn off and we didn't. They should put up signs saying that littering is not allowed and provide dustbins.

Having read the excellent reviews of this beach and small town we were not only disappointed but horrified by this run down and  dirty place. There is a lot of building going on, there is a rubbish tip at the end of the road to the south and the whole impression is most depressing.

Went to the beach today by motorbike . To park bicycles and even motorbike there , they charge us . The beach itself is full of big waves not very good for swimming in fact swimming is prohibited and i did not see much people surfing also only few i don’t think it’s suitable for surfing.

Beach is very ugly. Brown sand and brown water and the receptionist in our hotel told us that this is the most beautiful beach in hoi an. Many people trying to sell you things and a horrible smell of rotten seafood.
possibly gets busy but there winter but changeing rooms derelict must date back to when the area was communist run sea vwey rough and steep slopeing beach
Ich war im Dezember hier, also im Winter. Die Temperaturen waren für Nordeuropäer durchaus badetauglich, der Strand aber nicht. Total verdreckt, braunes Meer. Muss man nicht hin.

Horrible. Trash for days countless pill containers and multiple needles, yes needles! I also came across a dead dog about 100m up the beach from the tourist area with umbrellas. Luckily I was walking ahead of my girlfriend and let her know it would be best to turn around before she seen. Had been washed up and looked like it was there for a couple of days. I will not post a picture as I didn't take one of it. I don't know if it is because I'm Australian and have high expectations of beaches but this was just a horrible experience. On the upside, lovely cafes and places to eat before the beach.

I visit the beach near by Sunrise Resort and got sad. Human made pollution has made the weather worse and now destroy the beautiful beaches.Take care of this when you book a Resort, it is unable to take a bath in the ocean so be sure there is a swimmingpool to use!The pictures are taken from the beach.
It's a dump, do not go and do not believe the other reviews. Get on a plane and leave....... nothing else to say
Don't know why anyone would bother going here when you can go five minutes down the road at chill at hidden beach which is just stunning!
한국 찜질방보다 더 더운곳찜질방은 더운 정도지만안방비치는 뜨겁습니다너무 뜨거워서 사람이 있을곳이 아니란 생각이 들었어요도저히 비치에 못 있겠어서인근 레스토랑에서 음료만 마시고 얼른 돌아왔네요
Nothing more to say than it is a very touristic beach and not cozy a t all! If you have a bicycle. Go to the villagr nearby!

Do not recommend entering first shack on the left from the beach entrance. The lady is really rude. We ordered a couple of drinks and sat on the sun bed. She said no sunbeds of you don't order food. It was 10:30 am in the morning and we had just eaten breakfast minutes back. She didn't accept the order, only because we ordered drinks which were nothing but beer and cocktails. She rudely asked us to leave the sunbeds since we hadn't order food. We politely asked her the charges for sunbeds. To our surprise, she said 10 dollars (200k dongs) for one sunbed. We gladly left and moved towards the other end of the beach and found plenty of warm and welcoming shacks. The food and drinks here were priced almost half of the first shack.

Do not recommend stepping into first shack on the left from the main entrance of the beach. When we asked for a beach bed, the lady said you need to place an order to get the sunbed for free. We ordered a couple of drinks. When she insisted for food, we said may be in a while because it was only 10:30 am and we had just eaten breakfast. She asked us for 200k dongs (about 10 dollars) for sunbed. Think about that, you can actually buy a couple of sun beds in that much money. When we refused to pay this much, she rudely asked us to leave. We happily left and walked towards the other end of the beach to find nothing less then more welcoming and friendly shacks.

Parking here is mandatory. Dont park at the end but park a few (50-100) meters earlier. People at the end yel at you for no reason and are very rude. Very very touristy. Prices are still reasonable for drinks. Beeach ok if thats your thing. It was 37 degrees when we were there so to hot for us pale europeans ;-)
Een veel te druk strand, onverzorgd en afzetters. Je betaalt gewoon te veel voor wat je krijgt, alleen omdat je aan zee bent. Je kunt je fiets of motor alleen op een betaalparking kwijt.

We went to the beach with an open mind and got caught by a lady called Hanna when we looked for sunbeds. She worked at her parents restaurant on the lefthand side when you go down to the beach. She speaks good English and became "a friend "- we thought. We went to her restaurant and she charged us far to much, and her behavior was hopeless. I am still annoyed and feel scammed. The beach is beautiful but because of Hanna the experience at the beach was no good. Be careful with your money when she and her selling friends are around.
아니요 세계 666대중엔 하나겠내요솔직히 제주도가 더 예쁩니다여긴그냥 바다 수위가 낮고 넓고파도가 세지않아 어린아이들이놀기 좋은정도? 그다지 매력적이진않습니다

The guidebooks say that this beach is so nice. We arrived and found that the water is murky and so many tourists.  At 5pm, the locals flocked in, almost like the big migration in Africa! Luckily we found this really cool bar/restaurant called the Soul Kitchen. This was where we spent the entire afternoon.
Overcrowded, dirty and people constantly trying to sell you stuff I personally wouldn't touch the water.

Hello,Me and my girlfriend visited this place and it was absolutely awful besides the water itself. First, the restaurant that runs the covered lounge chairs. We did order a good portion of food to earn a chair or two. Food was absolutely awful!! We ordered shrimp w/fried rice and vegies to share. When we got our food, it was nothing like the same order an Asian couple next to us ordered. Our shrimp was small and fishy tasting. Rice was under cooked and had dirt and grit in the vegetables....I took three bites and said forget this s*#t!! Couple next to us with the same dishes had nice full size shrimp and ate everything as if it was the best dish they ever had....really!!Next when my girlfriend asked a local female staff member a question, she was shooed away complete with hand gestures and extreme attitude!!Next, yes there's more....we moved to the covered lounge chairs. And again the same situation. We got moved several times with extreme attitude and then it was obvious who they where catering too, anyone non-American....Last, yes there is more, the shower incident. Again my girlfriend asked a local female worker where is the beach showers to rinse off. The old hag just looked at my girlfriend smirked, laughed and went back into the kitchen to mock and gossip with her fellow coworkers. If it wasn't for a local vendor at the showers ushering us to distract me, this could have turned uglier than what actually happened.....As for the rest of Hoi An. I will comment on that later. It was mixed but OK.

According to some locals the water quality is still very bad due to the pollution because of a power plant nearby. They warned us from swimming here (however, this is nowhere else mentioned. Trusted the locals more on this one than the tourist information points. But up to you if you want to give it a try).

Beach was so dirty and was packed with local people trying to rip you off with a chair at the beach! Don't waste your time here the water is so rough as there is no protection and an unpleasant swim in the dirty water.Have a swim in a hotel pool or take a trip to Da Nang beach
Very crowded beach area. Parking is a scam. Food and drinks are expensive too. Not the cleanest beaches in Vietnam. Best to avoid this area  and drive more south.

If the Riviera is your thing then An Bang Beach will make you feel right at home.  The last 100 metres to the beach is lined with pay-to-park parking lots with proprietors standing in your path urging you to park your vehicle/bike and buy some water.  Then you hit the security booth where the friendly (not!) guards refuse to let you pass if you do have your hands on the handlebars.  You are not getting to that beach unless you park and pay.  Period.Then there are the many restaurants.  You have to run the gamut of those proprietors importuning visitors to come in and eat (over-priced) seafood.  Keep walking!A glance at the beach reveals lines of thatched sunshades with beach loungers under them - mutiple rows stretching to the right and left as far as the eye can see.  No open sand anywhere.  An Bang Beach may be a great place for back-packers or travelers on a budget but if it is pristine, clean, empty beaches that you are seeking - look elsewhere.

Very average beach, very windy and sand is not nice at all. Most importantly, we almost got beaten up by the local sunbed guy. He said the sun bed was free when we asked but after an hour, we decided to leave and walk around, he was trying to charge us 100k (a beer cost 25k here), we decided to walk off and told him we were told by him that the sun bed was free, he then said it would be free if we buy drinks. We continued walking and he ran away and came back with a stick and a brick on his hands and tried to threaten us that he would beat us up if we don't pay. But the coast guards saw him and took his weapons away. After a long long argument, coast guards took the guy away and we walked off. No tourists were helping us at the point albeit loads watching which was very disappointing. Avoid this beach and avoid Vietnam! Horrible people here!

Direkt am Haupteingang massenhaft Liegen, mit 10 cm Abstand zum Nächsten, krass, Meer ist schön, Palmen und Strand auch aber überfüllt, Besser: neben Palm Garden Ressort den Fußweg zum Strand und da ist ganz wenig los, man bekommt auch zu trinken und essen, Da wars klasse!
The best I have been to. Powdery sand, clear waters, relatively shallow, no fish or plants near the beach so very safe & no rocks or pebbles. Just sea, sand and clear water.

The best bit about the beach is the scenic bike ride we took through  the rice fields.  The beach is busier than I have seen anywhere in south East Asia.  The beach is  sandy but the sea is not clear and fairly rough. All the restaurants near by are overpriced and like any beach the food is not half as good as you can get 1 minute inland. Unless you really need a tan or a beach then spend your time exploring beautiful  Hoi An and give the beach a miss.

One of the worst experience during our vacation and really disappointing. We wanted to take the bicycles to the beach but we're supposed to pay for parking. Very rude people that just want to rip off the tourists.
Никаких эмоций, узкая полоса, грязновато, грязные столовки для китайцев на берегу. Не вызвал приятных эмоций!
Cold water, big waves, people harassing you, horrible food in restaurants . Just go for a ride with the bike and come back.
Aucun intérêt, la plage est assez éloignée de la ville, la mer est moche, le sable est moche, les transats sont gratuits si on consomme mais honnêtement si votre hôtel a une jolie piscine restez y!

The beach is average, but as we rode our scooter there, there's ppl ask us to park and have to pay for 20000, then as soon as we walk on the beach, those ladies started to calling you and ask you to hire beach lounge, it′s FREE!!but you have to spent money at their restaurant later, one lounge for 1 meal. The price is totally rip you off , and poor quality food, we ordered grilled pork, only got a very small serve but it is cold, dry and very chewy. We also had stir fried  chicen, that's the worst meal experience I had ever!!!! So get your beach towels ready, enjoy the Sun and go somewhere else for a better feed!

Coming from New Zealand i was hanging out for a beach and was i let down by the hype of this place. For a start you have to pay to park your bike and of course its just rip the westerner off again. I suggest you go down one of the side alleyways on your left and park for free. You cant see the beach for the dozens of restaurants/cafes in the way. When you finally get to the beach its full of sunchairs and umbrellas. The water looked polluted and  not inviting at all.I went back to my 50000 dong bike park (bike cost 40000 dong rental) and headed back to my hotel swimming pool where i shouldve stayed in the first place.

This is my first and last time to Vietnam!I have never seen such polluted waters and trash on the beaches.  You may find a section that they pick up the trash,on 5 star hotel (there property lines) but trash is constantly being washed up on the beaches!! The Vietnamese has no sense of clean environment. They simply dump their trash in the oceans and rivers and have for decades.The oceans are pucking pollution!So horrifying what is going on in the South Sea and no one seems to care or stop it!!Don't waste your money coming to what is a fake clean beach,far from clean!!!!

Going here for about 2hours this morning was the second worst experience of my month-long holiday spanning four countries. Showed up on a bicycle and was funnelled into a paid-parking area (which is the only real place to leave your bike). The beach itself had very few people (I was there at 11am) and the water and sand were fairly dirty and was bad compared to beaches in Australia and Hawaii.Had a drink at one of the dozens of empty beachfront restaurants (iced coffee = 60k dong, relatively expensive) and was approached by 5 street vendors who relentlessly tried to sell me turtle figurines and tiger balm (restaurant owners made no attempt to stop them coming in). Thank god I wasn't drunk.. I would have dropped 200k on junk.Paid 10k to the guy at the bike parking lot to get my bike back (he was insistent on 10k) and did the 20min bike ride through the rice farms getting honked at by crappy taxi drivers back to my mosquito filled hotel. Maybe a good place to go if youre with a big group and wanna spend the whole day (and a wad of cash) at the beach.. but stay away if youre alone or in a couple.
Im Januar kein Platz zum verweilen. Fast alle Bars zu, Meer braun, viel Dreck... Also zu der Zeit sicher kein Postkartenbeach.
Dirty and overcrowded with nothing to see or do.A million miles from the guide books profile.I would never go back and would tell others not to go there, it was awful.

We did not enjoy our experience at this beach. We biked there from Hoi An (which was a short, pleasant bike ride!) but the second we got to the beach there were people (guards?) blowing whistles at us and frantically motioning us to park our bikes places (of course, it's Vietnam so someone is trying to make a buck off you!). We parked our bikes and walked to the beach. There was a big sign saying "no swimming"... I think it's in case someone drowns, they don't want to be responsible? The second you step onto the beach, everyone is yelling and waving you over to their beach chairs. You have to pay to sit or else buy a meal. We ended up sitting for an hour or so and paying for the chairs instead of eating (since the food looked pretty terrible). Overall,not very impressed with the beach. People are wandering around with their goods trying to sell you sunglasses and other trinkets. Basically, a beautiful beach ruined by commercialism.
비치 자체는 좋지만 해안 너무 가까이 음식점이 즐비하고 음식점의 수준도 너무 불결하여 호이안 인상 파괴하는 것 같아요. 주변 정리하여 깔끔하게 관리하면 호이안 관광객 2배로 증가 할거에요.

We are from Australia where the beaches are beautiful. This beach has been mostly eroded away due to the recent monsoon and there is about 20m of sand left out to the beaxh break.The water was a dirty brown colour. Kids still insisted on going for a swim though! Many restaurants line the edge if the beaches and beach chairs can be hired to sit on. 200m up the beach is much rubbish and building sites for new hotels. Yuck! 3 out of 10 for this beach.

The beach is littered with garbage and the food there is not fantastic.  There are people around that keeps trying to sell you souvenirs and disturb you when you are trying to enjoy and relax at the beach... next comes the public toilet and washroom.. I didn't know that we had to pay 10000 Vietnamese dong each to wash our feet after using the beach!! It's ridiculous. An old woman there will guard the toilet at a corner and suddenly pop out to collect money from you. I feel that this is a scam because there is not sign board that says need to pay to use the toilet. But there is nothing much we can do. We were with our kids and she held on to our pram and didn't want to let go unless we paid her. She ruined our beach trip too.. considering the beach itself was also a let down. Not worth going in my opinion.

Must be better in the summer. And maybe the people who love it here don't have beaches in their home countries. And I imagine if you were watching fisher people going out and plying their traditional craft that would be cool. But when we were there it was a grey rubbish covered sad looking beach. A lot of plastic water bottles...
ホテルの送り迎えがあり、プライベートビーチということで、行ってみたが、ビーチ沿いのシートでは、ホテルと関係のない人間から金を要求され、ホテルと全く関係のない店が乱立していました。海自体は、透明度がなく、砂地で魚は、私が見た限り、1匹もいませんでした。
Wir haben heute den Beach besucht und leider ist er durch den vielen abgespülten Müll nicht schön und man bekommt eher ein schlechtes Gefühl, wenn man das sieht. Aber das mag an der Regenzeit liegen.

Hassled on the drive down to beach to leave your transport, as no transport allowed at the beach.  Very commercial, very busy and not a great place to stay.  We turned around and found Hidden beach which was heaven just a couple of shack type cafes and beach beds.

Over rated, pushy & unaccommodating for just a day out at the beach... Water temperature was a nice relief however.Pushy & rude restaurants over looking what could have been a nice family/ friends day out.

Vietnam isn't known for its beaches , but still this wasn't pleasant. Got bitten by sand fleas, the water is not exactly refreshing (better off in a hotel pool) and not much to look at. The best part was he fun scooter ride to and from , but don't spend a day there for sure .
안방비치 들어가서 얼마안있다가 온몸이 따가웠습니다 혹시나 해파리인가싶어서 봤는데 그런건없었구요 애들도그렇고 저도 그렇고 사타구니와 등 다리 팔 온몸이 따끔거려 죽는줄알았네요8월1일 갔었어요최악입니다ㅠ

Different vendors rent out lounge chairs, but who you pay depeneds on which restuarant the chairs are in front. Be careful of pissing the local Vietnamese people. I was attacked over a misunderstanding over who paid for what. Was not a pleasant experience to have an angry Vietnamese charging at you with glass bottles.  The beach is very nice though.
사람 많고, 더럽고, 별로에요 :(머무는 곳의 집주인이 자기가 좋아하는 장소라며 대리고 와줬는데 저는 집 앞의 다나 비치가 훨씬 아름답고 좋았어요.

This beach is just full of locals trying to get your money they are annoying!  They charge for sunbeds charge you to park your push bike it's a joke they can get really nasty to!  Beach is ok although water was murky and beach was over crowded!  The locals made it really stressful and we went to relax it was impossible they won't leave you alone unless your giving the money it's annoying!  There is much better beaches in Vietnam without locals charging you for  parking your push bike it's a joke!

Before I start, we are 6 year veterans of Southeast Asia, and living in Jakarta. Regulars in bali and most southeast Asian countries. So this is not a knee jerk review. An bang beach is a typical and very nice beach,  clear water, clean sand , uniquely characterized by the large fishing basket boats. Do watch out for jellyfish , we saw one but they are easy to spot and the swimming area is netted. We actually had a lovely time at this beach until the chair ladies tried to scam us . These ladies are rude, pushy, dishonest And aggressive. After a disagreement on price she became abusive and physically aggressive . Whilst most tourists just put up with this, we shouldn't. It gives Asia a bad reputation and ruins the livelihoods of the many friendly polite beach vendors around the world. I strongly suggest DO NOT use the chairs within 50m left of the entrance. Walk further or go right. Negotiate a clear price up front with the person who runs the chairs, usually the person who gives you the chairs is not the one who comes to collect the money. They will offer the chairs free if you purchase from their food stalls, better to just pay the chair fee. There is much better, nicer food in hoi an.

First Restaurant on the right as you walk towards to beach - staff rude and you are forced to eat or else pay to use deckchairs.Beach itself is beautiful.Rather travel towards habour to receive more friendly service and less crowds.

Aggressive old ladies control the sun bedsEndless very pushy vendorsBeautiful place but an overall very unpleasant experience We gave up and left after two hours of incessant hassleEveryone we meet here has had the same experience Is there a secret bit that we missed as I'm amazed at the number of good reviews on here
החוף הרבה פחות טוב מחוף ממוצע בארץ. לא שווה נסיעה במיוחד. אנחנו נסענו במונית לשעתיים ואחרי שעה כבר ביקשנו מהמונית לחזור.
Dirty, crowded, touristy, and expensive.  Not worth a visit, in our opinion.   Not sure why anyone would give it high marks on any level.

Had lunch at a nearby restaurant, parked our bikes there and walked down to the beach. The sand was so hot it was burning our feet through our thongs. We didn't want to pay for sunbeds so we parked our towels on the sand near the water and went in. The water was not cool at all and we all got stung by lice. Just after we got in a lady came down and starting grabbing our stuff and putting it on a sunbed and told us we had to buy food or drinks to use it. I tried to tell her no several times but as it was so hot I eventually gave in and purchased a bottle of water. So we had just sat on the sunbed when the souvenir lady showed up on queue pushing us to buy something. She would not take no for an answer and wouldn't leave us alone so I just gave her 20,000 to shut her up, packed up our things and left. Being from the Gold Coast I was not impressed at all with the beach or the treatment we received there. Zero relaxation, couldn't wait to leave.

We have been to way better beaches. Nothing wrong with the water but the surrounding locals are rude and ruined the experience. We had a guy threaten to pop my tires if i didnt move my bike. We had a lady confront us standing in front of us because we didnt pay for free chairs even though we bought drinks from there. Its a rip off beach filled with locals trying way too hard to make some cash. I dont mind that on really nice beaches but this one isn't that great. Cold water... that was the only bonus.

La plage est certes très joli mais il y a 10000 transats collés les uns aux autres et des restos de plages bordant toute la plage... Un rassemblement de touristes en fait...Si vous faites 1km de plus en vous dirigeant vers la plage de cua bai vous verrez un panneau avec écrit "go to the beach 100m" ici on ne paye pas pour laisser son vélo ou son scoot et il y a des transats pour 20 K vd et on est tout seul!Meme plage mais sans personne on y est nettement mieux!

Unfortunately this was our worst experience in Vietnam. The beach is ok, but nothing special (though, we are Australian, so are perhaps a bit spoiled). Far worse, was the constant hawking and interruptions as you are just trying to relax. Far worse than anywhere else we encountered on our holiday. You basically get a 'free' seat in exchange for buying lunch at the accompanying restaurant. The subsequent lunch was very ordinary and way overpriced.

Went here on our bikes after the water wheel! The beach is nice enough but you have people pushing you to park your bike and charge you, and music pumping from every speaker. The chairs and umbrellas are packed in although I'm sure they're super full in peak season. Can imagine you would pay an arm and a leg for lunch, a chair and a drink! (Someone said they spent $100usd for two for a day) Can find better way to spend your cash.
Did not stay very long at this beach very pushy people telling us where to sit and having to eat at their restaurant also got sick of the hawkers

We enjoyed our morning lying on the sun beds (VND 40 000 if you don't eat there). The water is clean, reasonably clear and cold ish. The food at the restaurant was average. My advice is enjoy the beach and a beer, pay the VND 40 000 and have your meal somewhere else.

Went there cycling from old town. Once there everyone wants u to park your bicycle and pay for parking .... on public space ! Very pushy people !  You have to go inside the small village and find then some lanes going to the beach to be able to have free parking ! Don't forget then to lock your bike as suggested in Hoi An by rental shops ! The beach then is just normal, nothing special for asian standards.

We have visited this beach twice now and twice we have left after less than an hour due to the aggressive vendors on the beach. If you do decide to visit An Bang be prepared to run the gauntlet of three types of vendors. Upon arrival you will need to park up your bike but this will involve cycling through a series of vendors jumping out at you, shouting at you and blowing whistles (NOTE: if you try to park in any of the free locations just before the main entrance be prepared for some very angry Vietnamese men to hassle you). Once you've managed to park up and paid you then get the pleasure are walking past a series of passive aggressive women on the beach trying to get you to sit on their beach chairs. They let you sit there for free, but only if you then eat at their restaurant. Check out the prices before you do this and do not be bullied into sitting at the first set of chairs you come to like we were. We saw a couple sit down for literally 10 minutes and then try to move on whilst been screamed out by a vendor for not having lunch - a very uncomfortable experience. We were also squashed in between two families with little to no room for our belongings. The third trial is that of the actual sellers along the beach. Every 10 minutes someone was trying to sell us sunscreen, or a pineapple, or sunglasses, or hats, or some strange candles. I've been on beaches before and most of them have vendors (there is actually a big sign next to this beach prohibiting this) but these guys are relentless and when you say no they often just hang around where your sat leaning on the chairs.A beach is supposed to be somewhere you are supposed to go to relax but after going through this we found it impossible. As I mentioned before we did visit the beach twice in the hope the first time was just a one off but no we got even more hassle the second time. Maybe it's just something we're not used too but we have been travelling across SE for 7 months now and to be completely honest it's a very average beach and in my opinion just not worth the effort. If you do decide to go I would just ensure you have some smaller notes ready for parking, check out the menu prices before sitting down and keep a good eye on your valuables.

Si vous recherchez de la tranquillité, fuyez ! En arrivant en scooter à 11h du matin on nous saute dessus pour nous faire payer le parking 10000dongs, on arrive sur la plage, une immense étendue de transats, il n'y a même pas la place d'étendre sa serviette à même le sable ! Une marée humaine de touristes au mois de mars... Qu'est ce que ça doit être en grosse saison ? De plus le Resto où nous avons manger avant de repartir était tout simplement dégeulasse.

We went to the beach on a cloudy and rainy day, so we just wanted to take a quick look. We came with bicycles and was stopped by a parking lady. They wanted us to pay 1 USD per bike. Because we just intended to be there for maybe 10 min we said no thanks and walked from there with our bikes and that's when one of the bike ladies THREW SAND on me! So please don't support these people with no respect, you can just turn left in the intersection before the parking and park your bike on the side of the road for free. We saw several bikes parked there and no one cared. In other than that I think this beach can be nice on a sunny and hot day.
I expect that the water is more clear, but it's full of sand and dirty. I dont want to come back to this beach any more!
An Bang Beach is exactly that - a beach...and not a particularly attractive one.  The bike ride there was way more interesting.

We visited An Bang beach today and it is the worst experience of our Vietnam holiday do far. Our hotel offers a free shuttle bus every 2 hours and we only lasted one hour before getting a taxi back to Hoi An. To be fare it was a cool day but very little was open at 11am and it really looks run down and dated. We found that after walking up and down a dozen dirty alleyways we had had enough. Possibly the worst beach we have ever visited.

Cycled to An Bang beach today and were very confused when we arrived by multiple 'officials' telling us we needed to leave our bikes and pay for parking (started at 10,000 VND per bike but negotiated to 5,000). We went past this first guy and were waved down by numerous people, and eventually whistled at by officials. Overall very aggressive reception by locals at the beach and would never go back. Both south of and north of An Bang are more quiet, less touristy and no parking fees.

We visited the beach by bike and the parking attendant insisted that we have to park at the parking Area, after that, they wanted to collect 50000 dong per bike, we end up paying 30k but still feel it is a ripped off, will not go back there again, beware of the parking!!!
it is very sad to see what was once a beautiful beach disappear. Today the whole beach is lined by sand bags and there's no access to the water. I pity the hotels there. They must be going broke.

We got there on the bicycles, first thing we ask for is to pay them to park each one of our bicycles. And then we get down to the beach one of the local woman start to shout at us to sit at her chairs for free of charge. The after a few hours when we were on the way out the manger (probably) of the near restaurant didn't let us leave because we didn't ordered anything from her, we explained her that the older woman on the beach told us it's completely free to sit on the chairs without ordering, but she didn't listen and eventually after making laugh of us in front of all her local friends and other visitors we paid her each on of us about 30$!!!!! The worst day of my 1 year trip

Well Vietnam is not known for its beaches and this is one of them, I am not sure why people say its nice, most of it has washed away and what is left is awful its situated in a wasteland with derelict buildings all around and locals hassling you, the sea is so rough you cannot swim in it and its just an ugly beach in general, no palm fringed white sand here.

If you are terribly desperate to go to a beach, well this would be your last choice. The beach was washed away in big storms and unfortunately there is very little actual beach left. There was a storm water pipe right near where our beach chairs where...distinctive smell coming from the flow. Gross!

We are beach people, we come from Australia, we love to swim and play in the Ocean.  We made the trek from our Hotel to An Bang, on horrific bikes, doing the 3km (another mislead piece of information).  The ride there was challenging through traffic, however eased up through the rice fields, but came to a terrible end when we arrived at the beach.  I crashed in to a lady who jumped in front of me trying to stop me to make me park my bike in her stall and pay her.  She has to got to be kidding if she thinks I am going to pay her for cutting up my leg and leaving me bruised.This information wasn't provided by our hotel, lucky we bought money and not just left with our towels and bathers.The beach was over crowded, full of rubbish, a truly horrible sight.  The water was murky, can't see anything through it. Locals were very rude, taking photos f us, very obviously, right up in our faces and laughing about it with their friends.  Men pushed their children into us, their sons particularly, which we found very uncomfortable.  Followed us into the water and through it and out, lurking around and saying inappropriate comments. This beach is also betted off, so smells with all the fish dead or alive, and the rubbish caught in it.This was not worth the effort of going, and we are disappointed we didn't have time to explore their beaches

Save yourself the trip - the coastline has been very badly destroyed by storms. Extremely dangerous to swim here and nothing worth seeing. So very sad for the locals and the economy. Hoping it improves soon.
An Bang beach viel ons tegen in vergelijking met de Cub Dai beach!!Personeel hier is veel minder vriendelijk en probeerde ons op te lichten! Sfeer is minder strand zelf is prima.

Certes il y a moins de transats sur la plage, moins de restos à la queue leu leu, Certes la plage est plus sauvage,Certes la vue est belle,Certes il n'y a pas de jetskiMais on est tout aussi sollicité qu'à cua daï ,dès l'entrée de la plage, pour les parkings, pour les restos , pour les transats, pour tout. Un peu usantDouches misérables et tous les transats sont collés les uns autres . Que des anglo saxons qui boivent leurs bières dans la mer ,on se croirait à Cancun ou à fort myers en Floride . C'est tout sauf le VietnamA part la vue.....pas de quoi partir de la plage de cua daïEn plus à la fin on est allé consommer mais comme on n a pas commandé 4 plats ,la fille nous a fait regler 160000dg pour 4 transats alors que ce qu on a commandé faisait un total plus élevéDonc attention commander une boisson n'est pas suffisant pour les transats"gratuits,il faut 1 plat et 1 boisson par personneEviter le resto CHIEN ,le plus près de la plage à droite face à la mer

We were advised that An Bang was the best beach in the area and arrived early to get a good spot and avoid the midday sun. The beach is beautiful but it is a tourist trap really. A quick swim and i was confronted by human faeces so i would not advise swimming! As with many beaches around the world we were pounced upon by local sellers, which is fine, but the restaurant owners are particularly pushy. We were told that the beach chairs were free to use but if we were eating or drinking during the day we eat in their restaurant. Sensing a scam we explained we weren't going to eat but would drink sonething later. we went for a drink there a few hours later and when we went to pay all hell broke loose!! We explained again that we didn't want food and that we would pay for the drinks and go. The scam became clear, they expect you to pay for the food or they try and embarrass you into paying more. The main problem was the food looked distgusting and many of the tourists were complaing about how bad it was. A trip to the toilets earlier had confirmed my fears as I saw them cleaning the plates and glasses in the overflow water from the toilets! There was no way we were going to eat there. The restaurant owner started shouting, swearing and tried to cause a scene pretending to call the police etc. My wife fought a good fight and in the end we parted with 120000 dong for our two drinks. Just so you know the restaurant in question is the first on the left as you enter the beach.
Not a beach i would use ,the beach was full with  garbage whenwe visited it so we had to go back to  hotel  for a swim

It is almost a joke the parking police want you to pay to pay your bicycles. Then there is the dude who yells at you & angrily waves his baton from his little hutch if you dare ride your pushy past him to look at the beach. Get over yourself! You can rent a lounger or if you have a meal can get the use  of one. Beach is average but water is warm if a little murky. Of interest is the local fishermen who paddle round boats (why I'm not sure) Food in restaurant is ok but watch out for the hawkers. Nice to visit if staying in Hoi An but wouldn't take a holiday at beach.

As with many beaches in Vietnam we have heard great things and are then disappointed when we arrive. It's quite dirty and has rubbish. It's a very narrow beach and you have to buy something from one of the cafes to use a sun lounger but there are few places to stop and rest if you're not using these. There are frequently people stopping to try and sell you things. We loved our holiday in Vietnam but wouldn't put it down as a good beach destination.

The first part of the beach, where all of the restaurants have their chairs set up, is nice and clean. But if you wander further down like we did (and got sales pitches from about a million people wanting us to sit on their loungers) the part where the sand meets the grass is full of garbage. We were not able to swim due to multiple rips and a strong undertow.If you ride your bike like we did, ignore the people who try to wave you down to park your bike. They will charge you for it and there are free places to park further down.

As above. We were told of we bought drinks we could use the loungers. When we tried to leave we were told we had to order food or pay more money. They then followed and threatened us when we tried to leave.See photo.If you want to rent a lounger here make damn sure you get a receipt saying you've paid for it.

We were in three years ago rubbish galore along the beach very sad to see. Swept around the resort areas. Sand washed away in a great deal of areas although there are projects in place to restore sand. Worth taking a walk along the beach and looking st the round basket type fishing boats.

Although it looks like the best beach of Hoi An it can not compete with other beaches in SE Asia. It's a bit dirty. To park your bike is annoying. As soon as you arrive they try to stop you and want you to park your bike for a fee. A little bit further there are some places where you can park for free if you buy a big cold bottle of water for 10,000 VND. Prices are inflated (like at most other beaches).

Wer sich auf den Weg zum An Bang Beach begibt wird bereits deutlich vor dem Strand von aufdringlichen Verkäufern bedrängt, sein Fahrrad kostenpflichtig abzustellen.Wer abseits von den Massen einen ruhigeb Strand sucht, sollte vor dem Strand rechts oder links abbiegen und dort einen ruhigen Abschnitt suchen.Wir sind links gefahren und nach einem kleinen stück auf der Hauptstraße geht rechts ein Weg zu einem einsamen Strandabschnitt ab - sehr zu empfehlen!
It was nice to have a break from history and shopping but this beach wasn't worth returning to. Maybe I've just been spoiled by other beautiful beaches.

Der Hauptweg zu dem Strand ist komplett überlaufen und voll mit Leuten die dir was aufquatschen wollen, bzw. für's Parken Geld verlangen...  Tipp: Fahre an der Kreuzung 1 Km nach Rechts. Dort wird man nach Entspannung und ruhe ehr fündig. Außerdem sind  viele kleine süße locals und moderare Preise. Wenn man Glück hat ist man sogar der einzigste am Strand!

Pas très glamour à la période où j'y étais, beaucoup de travaux, pas très jolie, quelques transat seulement, cela ressemble à une plage qui était belle.il y a des années ou rien n'as été entretenu.. vers danang les plages sont plus belles bien que pas non plus paradisiaque.. Bref c'est peut être juste une question de timing.. passez faite un tour on ne sais jamais!
Disappointing overcrowded pesky beach.Too many hawkers. Another beach nearby is much better.Trying to find a beach lounge costs money, the other beach does not & the food is better & cheaper.

ホイアンのもう一つのビーチ、クアダイビーチが「侵食されてしまってビーチがないから代わりにアンバンビーチに行ったほうがいい」とホテルの人に言われたので、自転車でアンバンビーチに行ってみました。ホイアンダウンタウンから一本道なので迷いようもありませんでした。町を抜けると、道の両側に椰子や田んぼが広がり、牛が草をはんでいたりして、のどかな雰囲気でした。炎天下を20分余りでしょうか、自転車で走る内、長袖でしたがうっかり手の甲を出していたので真っ赤になりました。ビーチが近づいてくると駐輪場の呼び込みが右から左からすさまじい中、ビーチの一番手前の駐輪場まで行って停めました。250円程度の駐輪代だった記憶があります。なんか、高いですよね。周りを見渡すと、マイリンタクシーが何台も待っていたりもしたので、タクシーでも行き来できたのかもしれません。炎天下のサイクリングは結構厳しいものがあり、車にすればよかったと後悔しました。ビーチの脇にはカフェやレストランが結構ありますが、活気がない感じでしたが、余り美しくないビーチには大勢の人がパラソルの下、寝そべっていました。ビーチはいうまでもなくお店の中にも物売りがよくはいってきました。ビーチの美しくない砂の色や、なんとなく裏寂れたお店など、ビーチとしての総合点は低い方だと思いました。
This beach is very beautiful but have a lot of restaurants is crowded and parking is expensive. There is no space on the sand because of the armchairs.
Des transats à perte de vue, une mer pas forcément propre et pas mal de vague. Faisable une après midi mais ne comptez pas vous y éclater !

We visited the beach by bycicle from Hoi an. The way to their on the main road was quire boring. When we got to the beach we werent let to enter with the bikes and had to pay 10 dong for both bikes. The beach was a luttle stormy that day, and didn't look like abything special. There are a few restaurants bearby the beach but we didn't try them. There is the vegtable village near by - maybe worth a visit if you are around. Free showera look quite poor.On The way back we took a way through the rice fields which was very nice.

The lounge beds are piled right on top of each other. They wanted 50,000 VND for the bed whether you bought a drink from them or not. It was free if you ate lunch in their overpriced restaurant. We preferred to go a couple of hundred metres south to a little restaurant called Salty Sea. Their beds were free if you bought a drink, lounges were nicely spaced apart and they even have a beachside shower to rinse that salt off. A much nicer option than An Bang

Yup, it's a beach alright! Sun, sand and bloody traders who just will not go away. Here's a tip - divide their prices by 3 or 4 and then go into the tourist rip-off paradise that is Hoi An old town and buy the stuff there. The beach is okay if you like plenty of company but after 35 years in New Zealand I'm spoilt for pristine, deserted beaches and few match them. This one doesn't.
我這次騎自行車去到這被形容爲越南最美麗的沙灘時却是另一感覺,尤如海嘯來臨!可能是這個月份雨季的關係吧.一層高一層的巨浪拍岸及部分湧上岸,好在有大型沙包檔住,不然岸上很多沙將被拖入海中!

Vous êtes accueillis à la plage d'An Bang par une succession de restaurants et de boutiques touristiques sans charme. Même déception sur la plage qui n'est qu'un alignement de fauteuils de plage et parasols sur une plage assez banale. Si vous préférez la tranquillité, évitez les plages d'An Bang et de Cua Dai et préférez les plages alentours.

Strand ist sehr sauber jedoch absolut überteuerte Liegestuhl Preise und unfreundlicher Service und auch die strandberkäufer sind ganz schön aufdringlich.. gibt schönere und vor allem günstigere Strände

I went to An Bang beach on the only day I had sun during my stay in Hoi An. The ocean had a strong current and jellyfish were everywhere. It was nice lying on the lounges but it was nothing to write home about. The group of men trying to get all you can drink cocktail deals at 8:30 in the morning also gave it a bit of an icky vibe. The shuttle bus trip from my hotel to the beach was more interesting.

A friend and I bird here about 6 or 7am to avoids the heat and crowds. It was nice to be two of just a small number of people on the beach.This beach gets a 2 because the water isn't very clear and it's really set up for tourists who want to lounge and sit under an umbrella. I've been to some mind-blowing beaches in SEA. Don't go too far out of your way, but if you need a look at the bay, this beach is trash-free and very accessible. If you go early you might see fisnermen rowing about in their circle-shaped boats.

Une grande et jolie plage de sable mais très mal aménagée, des dizaines de transat alignés les uns à côtés des autres sans aucune intimité. Le prix du transat excessif (100,000d la journée ou gratuit si vous mangez au restaurant avec minimum 1 plat et 1 boisson par personne). Des vendeurs ambulants peu aimables par dizaines qui tournent sans arrêt autour des transats.Il vaut mieux marcher un peu plus loin pour être plus au calme et mieux profiter de la plage.
Playita regulera, llena de medusas el día que fuimos y los sitio sde comer un auténtico asco!! Creo que es mucho más recomendable Hidden Beach, más pequeña y con más encanto! Yo iría a otro sitio...
Bãi biển tuy còn hoang sơ nhưng chưa sạch sẽ lắm. Một số người bán hàng rong còn chèo kéo khách hàng.

In comparison with the beach in Da Nang this was not good. No bathroom facilities, (except for a hole in the ground full of pee without paper) high prices for food (100.000 for a sandwich) you could only rent beds for a period of time (before 12.00 it costs 50.000 dvn for two hours per bed, so bring towels and your own parasoll. And a coolbag for drinks. You have to rent a bicycle in Hoi An to get there. (Recommend retning a scooter). No activites that i could detect. Lots of tourists. But the beach itself is anyway beautiful 👌🏻

We really wanted to love this beach but the water was basically impossible to get into at this time of year due to all the jellyfish. There are plenty of restaurants and bars all along the beach and beds are free if you eat at any of them. The beds are all good quality and the beach itself is lovely and clean.

Biked here from Hoi An... very pretty ride. Upon arrival yelled at to park bikes for a fee ... parked up continued by foot to an overcrowded beach... to get a lounger you have to buy food that was over priced and unappetizing, while being hassled by hawkers...  left and pedalled further up the coast much more relaxing.

Wat viel het tegen dit strand, zo druk en toeristisch. Het strand is mooi maar druk. De eettentjes willen je dwingen om bij hen Iets te eten, terwijl je alleen iets wil drinken. Dan worden ze onbeschoft, dit kennen we absoluut niet van de vietnamese bevokking, dus dit viel erg tegen. Heel jammer van zo n mooi strand.

La playa como tal para mí no vale mucho la verdad. La arena poco cuidada, los vietnamitas son muy patriotas pero viendo a un grupo de nativos cómo han dejado tras marcharse su parcela (latas, colillas...) no defienden mucho su patrimonio natural. Así estaba la arena. El agua con algún que otro plástico y tras dos baños tenia el pecho lleno de puntitos rojos. Cómodas hamacas por toda la playa a 1 dollar o consumiendo. Mejor pagar el dolar, a mí tras tomarme 2 zumos de piña y mi mujer otros dos en 1 hora... Nos han dicho que o consumíamos... Jajaja! Me descojonaba. Al final no me han vuelto a decir nada. Las vistas de las islas no están mal. Ah, se come buen marisco.

Достаточно много путешествую, есть с чем сравнивать. Байк или велосипед нужно оставлять на парковке у пляжа (зазывалы перекрывают дорогу собой, хватают за руки, когда пытаешься их объехать и матерят, если ты не заруливаешь к ним). Останавливались на парковке самой ближней к пляжу. Место на одного стоит 5 донг (обмануть пытаются все, так что будьте внимательны). Парковка закрывается в 9 вечера. Пляж песчаный. Хороший. Его убирают и чистят. Есть лежаки и зонтики от кафешек (купил коктейль - отдыхай бесплатно). Вода в море мутная и не очень чистая... Мелко. Утром полно местных, которые и плюют и едят в воде. Вечером полно и местных (прямо на песке готовящих еду, из-за чего на пляже стоит шлейф неприятных запахов) и туристов. Очень шумно и людно. В прибрежных кафе вечером играет живая музыка. Еда, как по мне, вкуснее в городе. В общем, пляж хороший, но люди всё портят.
They say its the best beach in Hoi An but once you reach there its over crowded with people and its quite dirty.

Het is een fijn en schoon strand. Let op met zwemmen ivm de kwallen. De mensen die de bedjes beheren zijn vaak onvriendelijk en proberen je te dwingen om te komen in hun eettent. Het eten en bediening is overigens matig. Je zit op het strand op elkaar gepropt. Verder betaal je voor je bedjes & om je fiets te parkeren.

This beach was lovely to swim at, the water was warm and it looks like something out of a magazine. However locals trying to sell jewelry were very annoying and rude, once saying no they would just stand in front of our beach chairs and would not leave until we told them "no" multiple multiple times, they would also come back every 15 minutes and it was difficult to relax. Also there should be a clear sign indicating the price of the beach chairs instead of locals naming there price dependent upon how far away the seat was from the entrance and if food and drink were brought from the local cafes.

Many tourist around and many too many sellers who want to sell you chairs and umbrellas...as in any white sandy beaches in Viet Nam ... but here it's so crammed to each other and nearly no chairs leftWe left after a while and went to a coffee and here too quite disappointing service

We went to An Bang Beach whilst staying in Hoi An. The place is packed with all kinds of tourists, and it seemed almost impossible to get any kind of beach chair. Sellers immediately swarm upon you, which is fine, that's Vietnam. But they really don't leave you alone.  It's like Bondi Beach, but in Vietnam. Bondi is way nicer (and has good waves).AVOID 'KIM CUC' RESTAURANT. It's the first restaurant on the left as you walk through the general entrance to An Bang. It's a really large restaurant, and you can sit near the beach. The service was fast, and good, but the food was terrible. I ordered a salad which was clearly made of very old produce - the lettuce had almost dissolved andturned that dark, slimy texture. My partner ordered noodles with vegetables, and it tasted (at best) like a packet of Maggi noodles.. The smoothies tasted, again, as if the fruit was old. They had a slightly fermented flavour. It's not hard to recognise the difference in taste between fresh fruit and souring fruit. When I asked for the bill, we told the waitress we would pay for the fruit shakes, and the noodles, but not the salad, as it was old and starting to go off. The owner came over and argued that we had to pay half, as we had eaten half of it. We ended up paying a bit more than our two shakes and noodles, and walking out. The owner was calling out after us, and the waitresses were giggling hysterically. We've eaten in many, many restaurants in Vietnam, and this was what I would call our first, and only, BAD meal experience.  Hoi An has far more to offer than An Bang, which is really just a tourist beach strip, vastly overcrowded and very commercialised. If you are looking for a beach, I am told that Hidden Beach is much nicer. I'm not sure how you get there, though, but I think's it's nearby.
This is a naturally lovely beach. Unfortunately hordes of Chinese and Vietnamese leaving tons of rubbish behind themselves ruined our experience.

Rode to the beach on push bikes from town. Found that you need to pay to park your bike before going onto the beach. Beach sand is clean and tidy with options to pay foe shelters and sun beds. In June, the water is quite warm, which may appeal to some but unfortunately, there is a lot of rubbish floating in the water. There are  restaurants along the beach and 'a must' is to go to Tuyet (shop 3) for Wotld's Best Spring Roll.

The beach itself is pretty nice, nothing amazing though. No waves as someone below mentioned. We had lunch at a beach side place, the one to the left of the road when arriving by taxi. The lady serving us asked us if we wanted fries with our food, and why not. They turned out to be 85 000 vnd per tiny plate. She was incredibly sly and left us with a bitter taste.

Decent beach but you'll get charged for everything. They even tried to charge me for parking my bike before and after. When I refused to pay a second time, they wouldn't let me go. Even the farmer tried to charge me for taking a photo of his water buffalo ;)

We were unsure whether we’d stay in the old town or out here, during our visit to Hoi An. Very glad we chose the old town! We rode out here on bikes on day 4 and it was honestly pretty depressing. To be fair, the weather wasn’t great and it was windy as well. Looked like there had been some damage caused by rain over previous months too. The beach was in very poor condition (eroded), the water brown and there was a lot of rubbish around. Felt like a bit of a ghost town really. So much so, I didn’t actually take any photos. This was only our experience though, in January, and for context, we are very spoilt in Australia for beautiful beaches.
Trash. Eroded beaches. Ghost town. Decrepit hotels. Abandoned lots. Depressing. Trash all over the beach. Do not go here in December. Sorry, but wish we knew before we arrived.
Beach polluted by tar or other petroleum products. Very limited coastal strip and dilapidated state of beach huts.

Unfortunately so much plastic waste in the sea and on the beach not all washed in on the tide. Although the sea is warm and the beach has the potential to be a great go to place there are cleaner places to go. Found an Australian couple with two kids picking up the plastic waste -what a great lesson.
