Don’t bother visiting unless your desperate to see the sea.. beach was boring not much going on apart from the sun bed ladies shouting at every poor sod that walks onto the beach to user their beds. We fell into this trap after practically been forced to say yes to a bed, to save paying for the beds (that are by the way all touching one another in huge rows) you have to eat in their restaurant fair enough.Managed only 45 mins on the bed thought we would do lunch then taxi make to the hotel but wish we hadn’t pizza was like a made yesterday and re-heated although not  heated well at the cheese wasn’t really melted ended up throwing it away. Nearly 500,000 for crap food and a taxi back to the hotel wasted! Would never visit again.
Coincée entre deux resort , accessible par un petit bois absolument dégoûtant, cette plage décrite comme exceptionnelle dans les guides est tout simplement horrible
Lovely stretch and sand with docile waves and lots of restaurants.Unfortunately, the sea itself is repulsively polluted with plastic and human waste from ships.

When we visited this beach in Nov 2019 it was full of rubbish and the water looked exteremely polluted. Although you can rent a deck chair and enjoy the sunshine. There are some restaurants and bars to cater for the tourist.

An Bang is only convenient.  Keep driving south a bit.  More beautiful restaurants, resorts, spas, all with free parking and gorgeous beaches.  There’s more luxury and less hassle 500+ meters south of An Bang.
A praia nao é  muito bonita. Casa de banho muito má. Muito servico de hotelaria mas com staff que chega a ser mal educado.

Comment peux-t on lire sur des commentaires très belle plage? Tout simplement plage sale, longée de transats où des sardines se font bronzer . eau peu clair , et dangereuse à la baignade pour les enfants . Opter pour une balade à vélo dans les ruelles de An Bang

Plage agréable, ...  , si on fait abstraction des ordures omni- présentes, des feus pour brûler les ordures et plastics divers, du harcèlement agressif pour louer un transat et consommer boisson ou repas dès que vous vous approchez de la plage. Du jamais vu, pour nous qui voyageons beaucoup. Déception de l'accueil après séjour dans plusieurs villes du pays; meilleur accueil à Siem Reap, qui est pourtant très touristique
This beach is not so great the sand is brown and the water is dirty .it is very windy and has some pretty big waves

Beach is ok- more notably having our pushbikes aggressively taken from us, without asking, by an extremely angry man on arrival wasn’t great (he was part of a motley crew of slightly intimidating old women greeting people on arrival).When I refused to agree to the charge, he evicted us from the beach shouting at my wife. I thought parking wardens in the UK were bad.Nice one.
We were so disappointed with this beach, it needs some investment and a really good clean. There was plastic everywhere.

spiaggia lunga e stretta.Se ci si trova a hoi an, di giorno diventa una meta tranquilla. Per me è un po' caotica..piena di ombrelloni che la maggior parte dei locali mettono  a disposizione dei clienti in cambio di una consumazione ..verso le 16 il sole cala e la spiaggia rimane in ombra

I was really looking forward to visiting the beaches (An Bang) in particular while in Hoi An. So disappointed, really dirty with lots of trash washed up and in piles (mainly plastic bottles and broken polystyrene) and a used condom metres from where a child was digging in the sand.

You have to pay for a chair to sit on and even if you sit on the sand, you may get “moved on” by the particular group who manage that section of the beach. Constant harassment by hawkers selling sunglasses, bracelets etc. the water was a decent temperature. Quite nice for a swim. (That’s what have it 2 stars instead of 1 :-)

Wir sind heute mit dem Fahrrad zum Strand gefahren und wurden kurz vor dem Strand mehrfach von sogenannten Offiziellen, teils mit Trillerpfeife bewaffnet, angehalten, wir sollten unser Fahrrad auf dem Fahrradparkplatz parken, selbstverständlich gegen Gebühr. Haben alle ignoriert und sind bis zum Ende der Strand Straße gefahren. Am Strand selber hat man keine Ruhe vor Liegen-vermittlern und Krimskramsverkäufern. Der Strand selbst ist OK.

Bof cela vaut le détour pour voir la mer mais ceux qui ont l’habitude de la voir en France .. c’est pareil mais en plus sale.Si le temps vous le permet vous verrez la vue sur les montagnes mais sinon non

We cycled to the beach from our hotel in Hoi An. As we approached the beach we passed a parking lot where a man and woman both came screaming at us telling us we had to pay straight away and park up. We couldn’t even see the beach from here and so turned around and cycled parallel to the beach until we were able to turn down a different slip road.We arrived at a private beach club where we were able to park our bicycles and enjoy lunch by the sea. The club was nice but overpriced (50k for a coke compared to 20k almost everywhere else we had been in Vietnam). The beach itself was dirty with lots of plastic. Waves were pretty big though if you like surfing. All in all would not go back.
Place was ok, we went to soul restaurant and didnt like the food at all, the seating area was nice though.
동남아 해수욕장의 이국적 느낌이 없음. 식당만 널려있고 뭔가 어수선해서 택시비 들여서 방문할 필요는 없다는 생각이 든다.호텔 수영장이 훨씬 좋음 ㅋ

Une plage qui pourrait être belle mais pourrie par les restaurants surtout beaucoup de pollution sur la plage. Si vous souhaitez profiter de belles plages je vous conseille d'aller plus au sud (2/3 km plus bas)

On the day we visited the amount of water bottles strewn around the beach was unreal. Every 5-10 metres was another.The water in Vietnam is undrinkable so everyone is served in plastic bottles. But the places along the beach front appear to make no attempt to collect them. They were floating in the water, getting washed up in the waves, and rolling around the beach.They did not float along from elsewhere, these were from the places on An Bang Beach.

This place can be a hit or miss. In November when the skies are cloudy and there aren’t that many tourists yet, it’s okay—there are relatively few touts, and I was able to find deck chair to use for free.Even so, I can’t honestly say this is a beautiful beach. There is trash along the beach, and the water was varying shades of brown. If you visit, be prepared to walk a little further off the main entrance to find a better spot to relax. One of the previous reviews mentioned how some touts near the main entrance act like they’re entitled to your money, and unfortunately, I have to say there’s some truth to that. Like the shopkeepers in Old Town, most are alright, but some are unpleasant to deal with. It’s the unpleasant ones that can spoil your experience.Definitely consider visiting but don’t feel like you’ve missed out if you don’t get the chance.

Murky water, overpriced restaurants, overpriced parking for bicycles, too many touts and so busy, even in the off season. If you're into spending top dollar on sun beds, food and drink with constant touts trying to sell you things, this is the place for you.

We arrived by bike. We knew we could ride our bikes to our preferred dining venue and park there for free.  However men manning the 'parking' space as you arrived jumped out in front of us and told us to stop. We said no, they followed us. Only when we explained we were going down the road to a restaurant did they leave us alone. It's a scam, you do not have to pay 150,000 per bike.  They were very rude and aggressive, with an entitlement to our money. When we did go to the restaurant,  we parked our bikes for free, unfortunately the drinks were overpriced and it was the worst coffee we had in Vietnam.
제목대로 사람이 너무 많아 그냥 도착하자마 그냥 나왔습니다.차라리 한적하고 사람없는 이름모를 해변이 나을것 같습니다...

If you are looking for a secluded expanse of sand this would not be it.On the other hand if you want a continuous strip of bars & restaurants, cheek-by-jowl loungers as far as the eye can see & hot,hot sand - then this place is idyllic. The current was also too strong for swimming when we visited
We strolled on the lovely beach, warm water which was desecrated by the amount of litter and rubbish that was strewn around.  More care should be taken of this beautiful beach.

I just don't get the positive reviews - the beach was dirty - not covered everywhere in plastic but way dirtier than anything you would see in Europe or Australia. And you only have to walk a little way to see all the huge developments being planned which are going to ruin this obviously once lovely beach even further. The water did not feel overly clean and I got covered in sea lice. Not an enjoyable experience at all but sounds like this is the norm for Vietnam. We won't be back and only made me appreciate my own backyard - south coast NSW Australia - even more.
Requires a look to get idea of area. Possibly more suitable for younger age group as there are ample eating and drinking areas overlooking beach. During late afternoon, it is too crowded

C est la plage la plus proche de la ville...donc la plus fréquentée. Aucun charme et en plus des vendeurs souvent agressifs pour vous obliger  à payer l accès à la plage, l accès au parking vélos et qui vous attribuent le transat à la tête du client.Conseil: en velo faites 3 km plus au nord et vous trouverez encore quelques rares plages sauvages et superbes comme Ha My Beach dont l'accès se fait par des petits chemins depuis la grande route de Danang
Posto molto caldo e stressante a causa delle persone che insistentemente offrono lettini e cibo. Spiaggia non molto pulita..
Arranged a Sunrise visit to the Beach - very busy with locals. Disappointed with rubbish on and around beach and lack of options for quality breakfast options.

Abbiamo visitato la spiaggia che da Hoi An arriva fino a Da Nang. Raggiungibile in bicicletta e di facile accesso.Sabbia fine e spiaggia profonda con qualche lettino e ombrellone.Nulla di indimenticabile ma qui sembra essere il Top.

Money for chairs but free if you pay for overpriced food ... for whole family. Selling flags, bracelets, hats, sunglasses, banana rides. Tap you on shoulder if you are asleep. Looks like it is organised by couple of mafia types who respond to seller texts. Worst of vietnam.

Vendue comme l une des plus belles plages du coin nous avons choisi un hôtel à proximité pour terminer notre séjour à vietnam par du balnéaire mais grande déception. Les vagues sont tellement énormes qu on est parterre à chaque fois tout en perdant la totalité de son maillot ! Super dangereux avec des enfants sachant qu il n y a aucune surveillance. Le sable est loin d être blanc, pas mal de déchets ( plastiques, cigarettes...) et la couleur de l eau (du coup très brassée) pas formidable.
A handfull of Vietnamees earn a fortune with dragging Chinees people across the sea on a parachute behind two noisy, smelly speedboats, ruining the beach for peace seekers.
A handfull of Vietnamees earn a fortune with dragging Chinees people across the sea on a parachute behind two noisy, smelly speedboats, ruining the beach for peace seekers.

I found this beach just ok. Although they provided lounges etc, it was nice to lounge, the swimming was not great. The water did not feel clean and refreshing to me.Not a great atmosphere either.To be fair, there were water activities that you could do if you were inclined to do so.

Went past for a look with my family on our bikes and before we got too close a man stupid in the middle of the road and stopped us saying that bikes were not allowed and we had to pay him to park them there.  We told him no and rode on.  There were closer places you could pay to park your bike which I read is a good idea our they get stolen but we ride right up to the beach and it was nothing special for how crowded the entrance is.  Go further down the beach without the scammers and crowds.

Since last visiting this beach area in 2013 the change has been enormous.Development has run riot and the infrastructure has not kept pace. At first sight the beach area looks inviting. Look a little closer and you confronted with plentiful rubbish and debris from the adjacent restaurant areas. Swimming was more of the same with dodging plastic bags and bottles the norm. Lunching in the restaurant area our family had nice food but watching the rats regularly scuttle between the cracks in the floor meant we wont be back here again. It is difficult to imagine how the problems at An Bang beach will be turned around.

the moment i rode up to this crowded beach entrance i was alerted that i had arrived at scam central!   parking a motorbike in Danang is usually free and at the most 5000 dong. here they imediately demanded 20000 dong. the beach chairs were shoddy and they wanted 8 us dollars for 2 chairs packed tightly together.  drink and food prices were double what they are in Danang and the whole feel of the beach  was only perhaps you were in Paradise lost! after the marvelous beauty and tranquility of Danangs sprawling beaches this is a toral letdown. small swimming area teemibg with hucksters.   what a pity!  just a zone for tourists to be gouged. if yoy want a gorgeous beach, go to Danang and avoid this town. the popularity of hoi an is turning it into a circus  Danang is a much better choice.  ive been travelling the globe for 50 years. please heed my warning!

Hmm...this is a difficult one.  The beach seems pretty clean and the sea is glorious, shallow, transparent and the perfect temperature for cooling off. I would advise to just stay in the sea!!! The touts all started off friendly and polite, but they are relentless, at one point this bracelet guy was being entertaining and I checked my purse to see how much cash I had, he announced I had enough money for him, when I explained it was for the whole week he was very unhappy. I could use a card for the rest of my holiday I was unfair and he cursed my entire family. He would not go away, really unpleasant. Basically ignore all touts, some just sit with you and join in conversations for HOURS, you either buy something or leave. Great beach but I would never go again, not restful at all
Cycled from Hoi An. 5km, very busy roads but lovely cycle! Beach really busy, slightly chaotic. Would not take a family there, would find quieter spot. Would not return.

Beach itself is ok on the not so crowded part of it (southern). There is a lot of restaurants but seems impossible to get any service in 95% of them. Only in two places i few places i got some attention (wild beach, soul beach) In some of them you might get someone waving at you after you sit down but mostly you need to find the staff. Its a shame because most of the places are nice but their attitude towards customers is 0/5. Make customers walk out and lose money. Prices are high for vietnamese standards, basic meals around 100k and drinks 20-40k.

On voulait aller a la plage a Hoï An vu la chaleur de la journée. Donc directement An Bang Beach et là, surprise : toute la plage est privatisée par des chaises longues. Donc obligé de prendre une chaise... Par terre on retrouve les mégots de cigarettes et des bouts de verre. Concernant la baignade, les jet-ski passent très très proches des baigneurs Juste derrière les chaises longues, vous trouverez les bar-restaurants pour tout avoir sur place.
We did not like this beach,have been too spoilt with going to Thailand. No crystal clear water Surrounding area was unkempt and smelly in places. Up by the village .

Non ci aspettavamo una spiaggia in stile “Caraibi” ma neanche così mediocre, nonostante viene decantata da molti come bellissima.Purtroppo non è assolutamente così.Va bene giusto per qualche ora di sole e niente più.

I first came to this beach nearly 30 years ago, when it was all fishing villages and beach huts. 10 years ago, the tourists had arrived but it was still nice. Now, it's a clear case of overbuilding. Crowded, dirty, and worst of all: ugly.

Small beach and not clean at all. It’s look like something going to built there.  I’m not sure if I saw just small area from it... The advantages of this place is quite and not crowded, I used a bed for 30k dong (in other beach they wanted 60k).
Choisissez un endroit un peu à l’ecart  Des bateaux avec parachutes ascensionnels  pour vous baigner....

While this beach is okay it often gets way to crowded. Most of the time you are much better off going a little further north and take one of the small side streets from the main road that lead to a more relaxing part of the coastline here.

Plaża zatłoczona, jeśli chcesz się rozłożyć ze swoim ręcznikiem na piasku musisz iść dość daleko, bo wcześniej napchana jest płatnymi leżakami (w teorii jak coś zamówisz to leżak jest za darmo, ale okazało się już po fakcie, że napojów to nie dotyczy a leżaki są gratis pdzy zamówieniu jedzenia). Cena z 30k dongów za 2 leżaki na koniec zmieniła się na 30k za 1. Tam gdzie leżaków nie ma plaża nie jest sprzątana - spacer zakończyłam trafiając  na zdechłego napuchniętego psa.
The beach has been decimated by a recent typhoons and has enormous sandbags the full length of the beach. You could access the water though.
Havde hørt godt om stranden ved hoi an, men jeg blev slemt skuffet. Det er en fin strand, men jeg ville hellere tilbringe en dag ekstra ved da nang, som har en meget bedre strand

The Benidorm of Hoi An. We stayed all of 5mins before getting back on our bikes and getting out of there. There was not an inch of beach without anyone on there. Stay away if your wanting a relaxing afternoon at the beach!!!!
그 인근에선 제일 이쁜 바다라 해서 찾아가니....바다는 그냥 바다고 좋은데...비치에 쓰레기가 넘 많았다. 그 근처 숙소에 머문다면 한번쯤 산책 삼아 나올만 한데, 먼 곳에서 일부러 찾아보긴 별로. 젊은 서양인들의 활기는 좋아버였으나 위생이나 깔끔함 등을 신경쓴다면 비추 장소.
Een strand, het is geen mooi strand zoals in andere plaatsen in Azië. Veel plastic en veel drukte...

Very run down beach which is in need of some TLC if it wants to keep the tourist. Parked our pedal bikes for 10k. So many better beaches in the world. Not worth the time but there are some brilliant cycle routes around.
Alguna parte de la playa estaba sucia , el agua no está transparente, te acosan intentando venderte algo . Yo no repetiría,  buscaría otra playa.

this Strand is full of people, loud and dirty in February. The restaurants on the beach are too rude most probably because they have so many customers coming from China and Korea. Maybe it is better in other months. We are really disappointed after taking the long way to Vietnam for the allegedly best beach of this region.

Best bit of this was the cycle from Hoi An through the paddy fields and the meal at the Blue Moon restaurant at the far end of the beach (tip from another TripAdvisor review where you park your bikes for free and just walk onto the beach or use the sun lounger from Blue Moon restaurant and have a drink or meal with them which was some of the best food we had in the area). Beach did not look anything like the photos, fairly dirty and nothing special.
Miles and miles of sandy beach, sadly no swimming today because of the weather. Place is very turistic, even parking for the bicycles had a price. -Tero-
Op de fiets vanuit Hoi An. Onze dochter kan overal zandkastelen maken, maar hier wel een hele grote van alle troep en rotzooi. Wat ontzettend zonde!!!

What a shame !!. This would be a lovely place if it wasn't for the litter on the beach. It's awash with all types of litter but mainly polystyrene beads broken down from the big lumps that come ashore. Worth a visit as they do try to clean it up daily.

During my stay in hoi an we decided to take the scooters up to An Bang beach. I was very disappointed as it didn’t look anything like the pictures. The sand was dirty, beach was littered with rubbish, there were bamboo beach umbrellas stacked up everywhere along the shore line as they weren’t being used. The restaurants are bars along the beach were deserted & the few that were open looked dreary & unkept. There were several dozen tourist about but Unfortunately the weather was too cold & wet to enjoy the beach. But despite the weather the water was rough & choppy & it felt sad that it’s just left in this state during the off season.  We stayed for 10 minutes to look around but felt it was too dreary & left.  The beaches in Da Nang were much nicer & clean. If you plan on visiting Hoi An between November & January I wouldn’t stay near the beach, it really isn’t worth it. In summer however this place probably gets a well deserved spruce up & could be a very nice but touristy rustic beach.

An Bang beach has been hit with storms in recent years and is not as advertised.The water is more inland and there is only a thin stretch of beach remaining.The picture below shows the tide in with no beach available and sandbags protecting the bars on the shore.Decent selection of bars and restaurants though.

I would say this beach is below average, especially if you've been to the Carribean or Italy, Spain, or Croatia. It was crowded and loud. In the area we were. We payed to park our bikes. Also, we paid for food in order to get the beach chairs for "free". Of course, everything was dirt cheap it's fine. There were big waves when we went, which was cool.
날씨는 우중충 하고 무슨 쓰나미 온 다음 해변에 썩은 나뭇가지랑 쓰레기들 뭉쳐있네요. 바다색도 흙탕물이고.. 덱하우스, 소울 키친 등 유명하다는 곳들도 별로여 보여서 그냥 이십분정도 해변보다가 이동했습니다. 날좋고 해 쨍할때는 또 어떨지 모르겠네요
사진이 현재상황 이에요. 현재 18.12.12.얼마전 10년만의 폭우와서 난리도 아님요.원두막(?) 지붕 날라와서 바다에 떠있고 해변엔 동네 개들만 ㅋㅋㅋ 복구될때까진 당분간은 방문해도 의미가...

150vnd for two lounges and towels with shower and toilet available afterwards. Surf was very dumpy. Towel was small and thin. Shower was three sprays out of a very small shower rose. Wandering craft sellers appear every couple of minutes. Not quite the experience I was expecting.

What an absolute shame this beach has been totally destroyed.It's meant to be the nicest beach in the area.The only patches that are relatively clean are out the front of restaurants.We found a little gem, fully vegan but very good food.I wouldn't bother going if you want a nice beach day .. head to Da Nang
Il n'y a que des parasols. Les vagues sont assez fortes (ce qui est cool mais peut être dangereux). Un gris câble passe dans l'eau. Enfin ce n'est pas une plage extraordinaire quoi

Het hoofd strand is wat schreeuwerig en ligt best veel zwerf afval, beter als ga je wat verderop naar de familie strandjes loopt, bv lady may dat zijn locale families die veel beter voor je zorgen en niet opdringerig zijn.
Hidden beach is much nicer if  you want a beach with less people. We lay on a really nice place between Hidden Beach and Cua Dai.

Der Strand mit seiner Infrastruktur würde einiges hergeben. Aber dank dem nahen Da Nang ist doch allzu viel Unrat, Öl und anderes im Wasser. Abgesehen davon hat die Lage und Sand schon was für sich. Sprich für ein paar Tage sicher gut geeignet, aber definitiv kein Traumstrand.

We were really disappointed after cycling from the Old Town to An Bang Beach. The beach was covered in litter, much of which looked like Drugs or Medical refuse. We walked along the beach from Salt bar (nice bar with tasty food) up to Shore Bar (no atmosphere and expensive), the beach itself and the surrounding scenery is pretty average at best. The weather had been very mixed which perhaps may have landed more rubbish than usual and certainly compromised the views but I would definitely give this a miss.
Better than the other beach of Hoi an But nothing great! Cycle or taxi here for an arvo break or coconut by the water but probably not an entire day
날은 덥고 습하고 바다는 안예쁘고 일부러 찾으갈 곳은 아니더라구요 물가도 비싸요해변 곳곳에 쓰레기 ㅠㅠ 안방비치보다 리조트 수영장과 바다가 관리도 되고 훨 나아요

Ok for a few hours of down time. We were at our hotel's private beach so there's a nice pool.The ocean water doesn't look too good but water temperature in August is nice.Wouldn't make a big trip our here. Better to Spend time snorkeling at cham island.

Although it’s a nice and clean sand beach, there are too many people there. Rent for sunbed if pretty expensive for Vietnam (100.000 VND), restaurants close to the beach are expensive as well. If you want better experience go to Hidden beach, which is few hundreds meters away from this one.

The beach itself is quite nice. The water is lovely for a swim! However, I thought that the beach was a bit dirty. The restaurants and bike parking area staff were so rude to us and very pushy. As 2 young women, we found them a bit intimidating. We did not like the vibe in the area one bit so only stayed on the beach for a few minutes, had a swim and left. It was very commercial also.
Far better beaches elsewhere in the country. Overpriced tourist trap. The town itself is a construction zone. Everything else was dirty and the smell of sewer gas overwhelming. Cannot recommend.
In my opinion only....not worth visiting. Did have an amazing squid dish at Soul Kitchen though. Located right on the beach. Cold beer there also which took the edge of the disappointment.

The beach is about a thirty minute bycicle ride from Hoi An center. Our hotel told us that there is no parking fee for the bikes, yet we were shouted at by a local guy at the beach for not wanting to pay him to park our bikes on his lot. There are several restaurants just before the beach that offer free parking if you get a drink (20.000d) which comes in handy after the bike ride anyway. the beach itself is nothing special, too crowded, and the jetskies kind of ruin the tranquility. We left after onky a short dip in the ocean.

Arriving by bicycle is horrible as there's 3 or 4 locals trying to aggressively push you into their parking zones to charge you money. We were told by our hotel we could park for free but these guys kept pushing until we decided to move our bikes a few roads away from the beach. The beach is okay, just a long shoreline with lots of restaurants alongside it, to cope with the many many many tourists. Nice for an hour or so but wouldn't come back again.
Típica playa turística de vietnam. No tiene nada especial. Hay todo turistas pero esta bien para darse un baño tras haber estado visitando el pueblo.

If just was controlled as for accesibility, cleanliness and citting arrangements.The authorities should care more about this beach naking it safer and nicer, with no restaurants taking over the sandy area.

This the only place in Vietnam where we saw aggressive, rude Vietnamese. We had to park our bikes somewhere so could go down onto the beach. We wanted to cycle down an alleyway, but were not allowed too. Parked in one place and a woman wanted us to then buy 4 drinks at 20 dong each ( large waters). Went closer to the intersection and found a woman who only made us 2 bottles ( at 10 dong each). Look for her sign on the right!

La spiaggia è di sabbia fine ma assolutamente non bianca, il mare niente di che.. Ci sono molti ristoranti e lettini, ma complessivamente a me non è piaciuta. La cosa più bella e arrivare in bicicletta da Hoian passando per una stradina con le risaie  intorno.
跟期望中有廣闊沙灘，水清沙幼的出入頗大，到現場一看沒啥吸引人之處，且一進入口處就有人來強索停車費，分明是個開放空間但還有人佔地為王，印象觀感不佳

The beach itself is nice, long and wide though most of the space is taken up with beds that are aggressively pitched to you by vendors, often for extortionate rates.It’s tough to find peace and quiet - there is no natural shade on the beach and you are regularly approached by tours selling trinkets and sunglasses. You need to pay to park your bike... from the moment you get here it feels like you are forking out.The water is nice and there is a roped off section to swim. In the two weeks we were there it was pretty dirty with trash, plastic bags etc about 50% of the time. There is a constant buzzing of jet skis and speed boats just outside the roped off zone that is the soundtrack of the beach for most of the day.Great place for a quick swim but not very pleasant for much else.

An Bang Beach was not my thing at all. Harassed by many sellers and restaurateurs the moment you walk on the sand. We walked down towards Danang and were able to find 2 lounge seats that was willing to let our family sit on in the shade (sorching heat / 40 degrees plus) whilst the kids swam, for a purchased can of soft drink/water each. The actual sand is nice, and compares to Australian beach sand. The sea water is murky and dark and you cannot see the bottom. We got our toes bit by tiny crabs!!! No mossies or water stingers noted. I wouldn’t book a beach holiday here!  No way......
Over the years this once peaceful strip of beach has become a trough for local businesses to wet their snouts. If you like crowds, noise and litter, and pushy vendors then this is the place for you.

We tried this beach for a day on our 19 day Pre-teen family excursion. There's many reviews here saying the beach was clean - well that wasn't our experience. I guess we are used to beaches in NSW which are groomed and raked every morning. But the Asian obsession with plastic continues - straws with every drink - bottled water everywhere rather than sanitation and treated water and their culture of throwing everything into the sea thinking it magically disappear. My family and I are determined to at least one bag full of rubbish for every beach we go ton on this trip. Hopefully the locals will see us and at least make them think why are these westerner picking up rubbish on our beach? Well, may be your beach, but we all share the ocean!  Now I can only think from other reviews that this 'comparably' a clean beach in Vietnam - it's the first we have visited so just hoping that's not the case!! The water has some stingers in it but were only a little less than an nettle so not really an issue but didn't make you feel comfortable in the water. My wife a regular clean swimmer asked me to look closer at the water  - it was full of life, load of plankton or something - I swam with my mouth shut for the rest of the day!

Very average beach, full of lounge chairs and tourists and haggling vietnamise to 'buy' crappy stuff or food/ drink. If you're from a land locked or cold climate country then i guess you would enjoy it but i live in south western Australia on the coast so we left after an hour. I wouldn't even bother.

The beach itself is beautiful but it just felt like there were people trying their luck at every corner. We were charged to park our bikes which wasn’t a surprise and we fully understand. We then went for some food which was underwhelming and rather greasy and it took a long time for service given how quiet it was. Then I went to get cash at the ATM where someone came in to the cubicle to try and take my money from me - luckily I hadn’t finished putting in my details and pressed cancel and left. But it was a massive fright. On the beach we saw people being moved from the sand so that food vendors could set up around them and you are approached a lot by people trying to sell sunloungers. The scenery was nice, but unfortunately the atmosphere and my experience means we won’t be going back.

Prob best to steer clear of this beach on a Sunday afternoon as all the Vietnamese tourists come here to relax and swim and eat - it was chaotic and we only stayed for 5 mins best to visit earlier in the day I suggest
One of those beaches where you either attend the restaurant/café the people who own the lounges on the beach or you pay a high price to sit out of the sun

We came here on Sunday evening after 5pm but it was fully crowded with vietnamese mainly, couldn’t see the empty spot, I didnt feel nice here so we just had a beer a walked away, maybe better another day or in the morning time:(

This beach was a real let down. So crowded, with a whole heap of plastic covered beach loungers that you pay to use, jet skis and boats pulling up on the beach right beside you, the air was choked with fuel fumes from them, local hawkers were hassling tourists trying to sell things on the sun loungers, not a nice beach experience at all.

Definitely not our favourite beach in Asia or Vietnam. The sand erosion means the ebaches in Hoi An are disappearing and An Bang is one of the ones with still some sand, so it should be beautiful. But it is covered in sun lounges, almost to the water level. It is overcrowded with tourists and it has lost its beauty, a problem we have found in all Vietnam- the beaches are not at all protected and too many buildings are allowed. The restaurants are very passable on the beach and massively overpriced. Walk South or North of An Bang, to find a quieter spot or scooter your way out of Hoi An to the South!

We went on Sunday evening and it was so busy (Festival) you couldn't see the beach!. It was also impossible to get a taxi back to Hoi An as the traffic was so heavy so we had to walk back to our hotel (6km)! I'm sure the beach would look better on a less busy day but to be honest it's nothing special as beaches go and a bit disappointing unlike the rest of Hoi An which is fabulous
我住宿的酒店有專車接送到此，因此來參觀了一下。從餐廳穿過到海灘，排滿密麻麻的沙灘椅，感覺密集一點都不relex。而且水質肉眼看很一般，所以沒有游泳，走了一圈就走了。

Unfortunately, a typhoon a couple of year's ago destroyed some of the beaches near Hoi An, so An Bang is now the main beach. The restaurants here are far too pricey, the itinerant traders far too pushy and the beach far too crowded. I appreciate that people who don't live near good beaches would love An Bang, but I live in Western Australia where we have much better beaches, many of which you don't have to share. To be fair, I had visited before the typhoon when An Bang was much better because there were other gorgeous beaches around to filter out the crowds.

We had a terrible experience of an bang because we went on a day that was grey and cloudy. Check the weather before you go!!All the pictures looked amazing but the sea was choppy and looked really murky and brown. No one was sunbathing and the restaurant that we sat in was full of women trying to sell souvenirs.

Небольшой пляж под завязку забитый туристами. Цены в пляжных ресторанах выше, чем на остальных пляжах. Нет аквариумов со свежими морепродуктами. В некоторых местах пытаются содрать плату за шезлонг, несмотря на то, что у них покупаешь еду. Но при отказе платить - особенно не настаивают

It’s a bit of a boring beach with not a lot to do. It’s quite wavey so swimming isn’t easy or for kids as much and there’s a bit of rubbish however nothing compared to the likes of Cambodia or Thailand

Nice beach extremely long and only a short drive from the old town Hoi An.Beach lounges available with umbrellas being the only shelter.It is so polluted which is such a shame but it did make us appreciate how beautiful ozzy beaches are.

여긴 다 좋은데 밤에 조명이 없어서 너무 무서워요.밤바다의 낭만 이딴거 생각하시면 안돼요 ㅋㅋ개짓는 소리 저 멀리 어둠속에서 들려서 그쪽으론 가지도 못하구요.너무 어두침침해요.낮에는 파도가 많이 쎄고 바람도 많이 불고..제가 갔던때는 바다색깔이 흙탕물 색깔이였어요.사진도 이쁘게 안나오고..그리고 오토바이나 자전거 타고 가지마세요주차장쪽에 정신나간 여자있는데 자꾸 돈달라고 주차비달라고 길을 가로막아요.호텔 가려면 거길 지나야 하는데 자꾸 타고있는 자전거를 지네 주차장에 주차하라고 하고..말도 안통하고..지켜보면 옆 식당 상인들이 몰래 지나가라고 손짓해줘서 도망갈 수 있었네요..그후로 그앞으로는 안지나 다닙니다..

There was sand. There were waves. Though you're not supposed to swim. There were 300 tourists, all of whom apparently paid $5 for a beach chair. Or got it free if if you ordered food. Meh, I'd rather hike the countryside or explore the town.
Rien de particulier à voir. Bains de soleil entassés l un sur l autre. Pas très propre. Vendeuses insistants à l entrée de la plage

I feel sorry for people who pay a lot of money to come here from Europe or Korea for a beach like this. Sand is not clean and water not very blue. Any Australian who comes here for a beach holiday needs their head read.
You have to sit behind huge sandsacks holding up the remaining sand. Not nice on the part we were at.

Not bad but the beach has a big slope making it a bit dangerous for the kids. And at 4.50 pm or 55 if you are lucky, the life guard comes with his wristlet in order to kick everybody out of the water. The beach offers a hot free shower, bars, restaurants and small shops (like convenient store and clothes store if ever you didn't bring your own swimsuit...).

We zijn op de fiets van de stad naar het strand gegaan. Al met al een hele onderneming met het langsrazende verkeer. Op het strand lag erg veel rommel en dat maakte het niet aantrekkelijk. Het is bij een korte strandwandeling gebleven. Trouwens in Vietnam wordt het vuilnis overal gedumpt. Het verbaasde ons dat we geen ratten hebben gezien.
We didn’t have great weather, but this was really something of nothing. Dirty beach, average average restaurants with pushy sun lounger sellers. I wouldn’t return!

Upon arriving the locals will fight for you to park you bikes, some quoted ridiculous prices $1-2 although you can get it for 10,000vnd. The beach itself is nice but smothered in deckchairs for tourists so it is not very picturesque, the water is also very wavey so is not very suitable for swimming, especially if you are a weak swimmer.

This is not a review of beach but of the first restaurant on your left ( v large)facing the sea as you go down the steps to beachTo be avoided , food not good at all & double the price of excellent restaurants in Ancient Town To be avoided !
This is the authentic Vietnam beach club. Not a 5 star resort owned by foreigners but one owned and operated by locals for locals.

Très mauvaise expérience sur cette plage ou des rabatteurs du reztaurant M. Ca vous propose des transats gratuits en contrepartie vous devez mangez chez eux , une arnaque; c'est pas bon et cherAlors que autour, il existe 3 très bons restaurants ou c'est gratuit et surtout ou la nourriture est bonne

Not great beach you cant even go for a walk without being harrased. People are trying to sell you something or shouting that they have free beds. They are not free you have to eat in their restaurants. And the food there is bad. It is not only my opinion. I talked to other people and everyone complained of quality of food. My husband lost wedding ring there we asked lifeguards for help to find it but nobody had english.

Pensare di fare anche solo tre giorni qui è da paura. Caos, disorganizzazione, pulizia, igiene... sono un optional. Certo... il mare è azzurro, la sabbia è bella, ma il merito è di madre natura, non dell'uomo.Se pensate a Caraibi, Maldive o spiagge Thailandesi, ...scordatevelo.Guardate le mie foto, rispecchiano la realtà.
Dirty beach would not swim there rubbish every where and unsure of water quality stay in your resort pool .

Though we were there on a crappy day weather wise, I was still very underwhelmed by this beach. Filthy with litter and hardly comparable to Australian beaches.Would be interesting to see how this place shapes up on a nicer day, but if I return to Hoi An, I won't be in a hurry to go here again
우선 선배드는 유료 ,음식점에서 음식시키면 무료 호객심함식당들은 좀 지저분함(먹기싫음) 화장실도 음식점 손님 또는 유료 선배드 이용객아니면 유료, 생각 보단 별로였음,큰기대 않하시는게 좋음

We ventured out to the beach once during our two day visit to Hoi An, having suffered the 25 min ride by bicycle on dusty dangerous roads currently being dug up we were greeted by gangs of intimidating locals demanding we use their parking facilities for a good price with free water. I managed to continue a little further and parked up with a friendly chap right next to the beach. The beach itself is nice and clean, the water a bit murky but you are continuously hassled by the local restaurant staff who want you to use their sun beds for a high price or a reduced price if you eat from their joint. We choose the latter because we wanted a hassle free few hours but unless you order in a few lobsters and a few bottles of wine at extortionate prices be prepared to be treated like scum or criminals right into your face. The locals here are a complete tourist trap and rude beyond belief. Left the beach after 2 hours never to return, the  hotel pool never looked so good.
Touriabzocke. Braucht kein Mensch, noch nicht mal besonders schön der Strand, Wasserqualität wie halt zu erwarten im südchinesischen Meer. Plastik und sonstiger Unrat.

My boyfriend and I had a few weeks in Vietnam and were looking forward to seeing a more local beach. We found it really busy, more touristy than we expected, we were hassled to buy stuff everywhere we turned and the beach was more developed than others we'd visited. There are nicer beaches in Vietnam

Ein sehr touristischer Strand mit einer Liege neben der anderen. Überall muss man zahlen sogar fürs Fahrrad abstellen.  Umso näher am Strand umso mehr muss man für den Parkplatz zahlen. Ein Tag reicht dort völlig aus.

Our family of 5 had biked from Hoi an old town enjoying the ride. What a shock we were in for when we got to An Bang Beach. We were hounded from every corner so didnt stay long only long enough to buy some sweet potato chips. We got on our bikes and rode further along the beach. Little An Bang is not far away and lovely. Cua Dai beach is further again, and despite the erosion had an altogether much nicer atmosphere.

A resturant lined beach with shaded chairs on the sand.  The locals swim and excercise from 5:30am until 7:30am.  The tourist don't arrive until 10-11am.  My favorite time was 8am-10am where I virtually had the place to myself.  It's a flat swimming beach rather than a surf beach.  No good for snorkeling but excellent for splashing about. Yellow sand.  Netted area with life savers. Parasailing and jet skiing available.  Locals will try to sell their wares to you but are polite and not pushy. Don't leave your valuables on the beach when you swim though.

It is a very crowded beach with brown/black sand. The sand quality is excellent but tides are high and water is not clear. All beach huts are owned by beach restaurants and rented by around VND 50k per sun bed. So many locals and many are insisting to sell you toys and souvenirs, not allowing you to relax. No shower places except inside restaurants (you need to pay for some of them). Food prices in the beach restaurants are astronomical. If you need an alternative nearby beach then head to Cua Dai which is quieter and the beach restaurants are more friendly with reasonable prices.

Our visit in the main part of the beach fell out pretty short. We went from Hoi An by bicycle, which is a 4km bike ride over a busy street crowded with motorbikes and taxies and trucks. When arriving at the entrance, the locals practically try to tear your bike away from you into one of the 'free' parkings, which get more expensive the closer to the beach you get. The beach itself is very crowded and not too clean. When you go swimming, you have to watch out for jetskis or motorboats which drive by a little too close for comforts. However, there are plenty of bars and opportunities for shopping at the waterfront to pass the time.If you just want some quiet and some more relaxed swimming, I recommend heading ca. 1km south along the main road and keeping a look for small beach access roads. Taking one of these leads to a more secluded area of the beach with beautiful sand, shallow entrance into the water, small local vendors who sell coconuts and drinks as well as the typical umbrellas. You can watch the fishing boats riding the small waves and swim and enjoy the beach more or less undisturbed. Also, you get a beatiful view of Da Nang's skyline.All in all, unless if you really look for a crowded beach and resort-style animations, I recommend avoiding the main access to the beach and take a short drive towards more secluded areas.

Loin des photos de mer turquoise et sable blanc, banale plage (sale), avec mer très chaude et vraiment bcp de monde. Pour les transats ne pas hésiter à marchander et changer de couleur de matelas car LE prix est à la tete du client !

A seguire la guida lonely planet sembra una spiaggia dei Caraibi, quando ci arrivi in realtà ti accorgi che sei molto distante dai Caraibi. Nulla di eccezionale anche perché qualsiasi cosa tu desideri fare devi pagare qualcosa

Was not the visit I was hoping for before leaving Hoi an. We arrived at the beach and got some loungers when a lady approached and said they were 100000vnd each or we could get food later from the restaurant. We agreed and enjoyed our time in the sun. We then ordered at the restaurant- 2 drinks and a pizza to share but we were told we would still have to pay for one of the loungers as this was only a meal for one. We felt this was ridiculous as firstly the lady didn't specify how much or what quantity of food we needed to order and secondly, we weren't even that hungry and were ordering for the sake of it. After a heated disagreement, they eventually said it was fine but it was an embarrassing situation so be forewarned all those who read this. Also, the "lifeguards" are more interested in renting out jet skis then actually doing their job so be cautious if you have children in the water as the water is very powerful and is not under supervision.

Three years ago my wife and I visited this lovely spot. It was during the middle of the day and was very quiet with not too many people venturing out onto the hot beach. Yesterday we returned and it was bustling with locals and tourists. We were offered one of the many vacant beach chairs and when we sat down and ordered a few drinks, we were also asked to pay 150,000 Dong (just under $9 Australian Dollars) to use the 2 chairs. We politely declined and sat up in a bungalow hut over the beach, ordered our drinks and watched other tourists also walk away scratching their heads. We moved on and ate in one of the many eateries. We were approached by a beach vendor and we took a few minutes to look at her wares. We purchased a few small items and bought her a cold bottle of water. It still amazes me that these vendors trudge up and down the hot beach for hours on end to sell a few trinkets for only a few dollars or cents and dressed for winter. Later sitting on the beach, my wife went for a swim and by late afternoon you could hardly find a spare spot in the water. Loads of people. I sat on the beach in disgust as I watched a lady change the diaper on her child and discard the dirty one in the sand. She then got up and walked away!!! I watched others discard their litter with zero regard for who was watching. It's a real shame that people have so little regard for such a beautiful beach. Unfortunately witnessing these things left me not wanting to return and angry at others behaviour.

Niente di eccezionale, piena di beach bar con lettino uno attaccato all'altro, alcuni senza nemmeno 10 cm tra uno e l'altro. Mare pulito ma non trasparente, va bene per passare un paio d'ore in relax ma nulla di più
Грязно, дорого, много народу.  Парковка  выпивка и напитки в 3 раза дороже чем на других пляжах. Хамоватые вьетнамцы.
plage surpeuplée et très bruyante, de mauvais restaurants sur celle-ci. Si vous aimez bien la tranquillité, tracez votre route vers Cua dai Beach.
Sea-lice and over-run with us tourists!We really have spoiled this beautiful beach by disrespecting its beauty.
Je ligt mannetje aan mannetje en om de 5 minuten komen ze je iets verkopen. We zijn na 45 minuten vertrokken
Шумно, грязноватые кафешки ценник высоковат для такого сервиса, платные парковки с зазывалами. На самом пляже много народу, не очень чисто. Есть прокат лежаков,  гидроциклов.

An Bang Beach is the Beach where most local people meet to swim and play. It' okay but really crowded, when We were there. You even have to pay for parking your bicycle. The restaurants near by, are expensive and the food is no good quality.
Mooi strand! Mensen van strandtentjes zijn onvriendelijk en bedjes zijn meer dan 2x zo duur als bij andere stranden. Of je MOET eten en drinken.

Stay away from this beach if you want to avoid crowds, bad attitudes, poor customer service and over priced food/chair rentalWe were advised to visit this beach for its beauty which I admit is very nice, nice clear still water which is perfect for relaxing in the sun However it's very busyYou will need to hire chairs which is fine but you will probably also feel obliged to eat there in order to avoid paying for a chair which is just a sales scamThe food is over priced and the service is horrid There's plenty of beaches further down from this beach which are quieter, cheaper with friendlier locals
Stick to the Old Town......we just popped over on bikes, and maybe we caught it on a bad day....but it made me glad we weren't staying on the beach.
Found the beach to be overcrowded- also couldn't relax, too many people trying to sell you stuff. If you do go get a hotel shuttle, you have to pay to park your bike.

I do not know why this place has so many good reviews. Expensive drinks, overcrowded beach and crappy food. Combine that with nowhere to park and people constantly hassling you to buy rubbish you don't want or need.Ride 5 minutes down the road to Cua Dai beach (same stretch of sand just closer to the river) and you will have an almost empty beach with a few restaurants around who are charging half as much for food and drink.
Not sure if was just a bad day but the water was not like the postcards. Nice to sit on beach chair and enjoy food and drink but didn't swim.

Beach is nice but be prepared for lots of people. Parking your bike cost 10k or "free if you buy a big water" from the shop you're parking in front of - this can be a little aggressively communicated which isn't nice. Our bikes got stuck behind a million others as we tried to leave at peak time so maybe parking at a smaller cafe further away from the beach pays off for an easy get away. Also have to pay 30k for a deck chair, or buy quite a few drinks at the owners cafe - fair enough but annoying if you're only staying a short time.

We went they on a Sunday afternoon (5pm). Just about 600-700 meters to the signal lights intersection of the beach, we encountered a huge traffic jam; of cars, hotel coaches and hundreds of motorcyclists. It appears the beach is the only place everyone - locals too - can go to for the afternoon. When we arrived at the beach we were taken a big surprise. It was flooded with thousands of people, like ants, packing the whole stretch of the beach. You can't get to the sea without trampling over people who have set their picnics on the beach.The noise was worst that a morning market.

When visiting An Bang Beach I have to say I went on a Sunday late afternoon for dinner, big mistake, as of course all Danang and Hoi An seem to take their children and families there for their day off - it was absolute bedlam.  Once things quietened down a little I found a quiet spot to eat it was reasonable and the food was fine.I am sure it would be more pleasant if I had not visited on such a hectic Viet Nam Sunday holiday.

This beach would have received a higher star rating because it was actually quite pleasant & fun during the day, but we ended up having one of the worst experiences since being in Vietnam. We ignored the various people trying to usher us in for the paid parking and drove down one of the allies to the left where you can park for free or use the restaurant parking. We visited the deckhouse restaurant to have some lunch first. The food was average and the prices were steep. After eating we moved down to the sun loungers where an angry man told us we had to buy food to sit here. He calmed down after we told him we already ate upstairs. The beach was basic with sun loungers covering the stretch. It wasn't too busy which was nice and there were some average size waves which were fun to dive into.The fun stopped at 4pm when a load of locals started putting the sun loungers away and started asking tourists to move so they could lay their mats down. They covered the whole beach with mats and started cooking their BBQ right where all the sun loungers were. We saw one old woman destroy a girl's' sand castle when the mum wasn't looking because she wanted to put her mats there. Within an hour most of the tourists were pushed out and we also decided to leave. The beach was getting very crowded with locals and it wasn't calm anymore. We wanted to put our shoes on but one of the ladies put a mat right in front of our sun loungers so we kicked it away with our feet to get to our shoes. She came over to pick up her mat and shook the sand off in my face. Another old lady was just laughing but I gave her some verbal abuse back and that was the end of it. We left the beach feeling really sad & disappointed in how they are treating their tourists here.
This is not at all a paradise like beach if that is what you looking for.  Small entrance with a few restaurants, the waves are medium size.

Title says it all.  From the arrival to your departure, expect to be hounded by locals on everything from parking your bike to food the umbrellas, to a spot the beach...if you're looking for a beach, you found one, if you're looking for a quiet secluded beach... this isn't it

wir hatten leider keine schoene zeit am strand. beim ersten mal sind wir genau dort wo die hauptstraße langfuehrt zum strand gegangen, haben vorher unsere raeder fuer je 10.000D geparkt und dafuer ne flasche wasser bekommen. der strand ist soweit man blicken kann von liegen und verkaeufern belagert, es hat wirklich keinen spaß gemacht. beim zweiten mal sind wir links abgebogen, statt direkt zum strand zu fahren und haben uns soweit links wie moeglich hingelegt. umsonst geparkt, weniger verkaeufer, aber schoener ist es leider dennoch nicht.

We took our free shuttle bus from our hotel to this place and the driver ushered us to a specific place on the beach and shaded beach chairs.  Didnt really think much of it till we were asked to pay 200,000 dong for a pair of chairs.  We upped and left. Upon some googling   we found out  they charge only 50,000 per chair, or free if u buy a drink from them(1usd) .    So be aware as some will try take advantage.   Other than that beach is okay.

As soon as you get to the main beach access area you'll be harassed to pay to park your bike or scooter. Keep going either left or right and you'll find more access points. The beach is covered in garbage, the sea is murky. Not worth your time.

Living in Australia and being from Europe, the beach seems quite average to me. Plenty of trash everywhere, water is quite dark and dirty. On the bright side, the bike ride from hoi an was quite fun and entertaining with few nice spots on the way
Feiner Sand und warmes Wasser. Aber sonst ein Strandrestaurant nach dem anderen und davor 3 Reihen eng gestuhlter und kostenpflichtiger Liegestühle.

An Bang Beach ist ein wunderschöner Strand, das Meer ist klasse... Aber hier wurde der gleiche Fehler gemacht wie an so vielen Stränden, der letzte Quadratmeter wird mit Sonnenschirmen / Liegestühlen gefüllt und das ganze ist eine einzige Massenabfertigung. Selbst Fahrräder müssen auf einen Parkplatz, weil sonst die Massen alles verstopfen würden. Keine Empfehlung

Well the restaurants own a lot of the beach beds across the front and quite frankly they are all rude. I'd just avoid yourself the hassle of having to walk down the beach bartering the staff to lower the price of loungers and bring your towel and lay on the beach. Other than that the beach is clean 😀
It was so hot & I really wanted to swim but the rubbish in the water is just too scary. It's not their fault but we all have to do something to clean up our oceans.

My husband and I went to this beach today but were so disheartened when we arrived. It is packed with sunloungers and people trying to get you into their restaurants. If you buy something from the beach bars you can stay on the sunloungers for free, they are positioned out front. But beware when they say buy something to get a sunbed free, they mean you must buy food not just drinks!It is a very windy beach and the tide is quite strong so not ideal for swimming. You cant really relax as the sand is blown in your face constantly and some locals are trying to sell their products.There are much nicer beaches around Vietnam compared to this one!

Jam packed with deck chairs literally cms from each other. Reminded me of  concert seating. Restaurants either side of the walkway. Being from Qld Aus we are spoilt for beaches..Could have spent 10mins and left but spent time conversing with some locals instead. Had a light snack and left.Would not add to your bucket list.

Riding your bike up to An Bang beach, you'll be hounded by street vendors yelling at you to park your bike. Once parking and paying a stupid and pointless fee, we maid our way to some sun beds on the beach. We definitely got ripped off and paid about 250,000VND for two beds but couldn't be bothered haggling with the staff as they were so rude. The beach itself is nothing special - not very clean, full of tourists, etc. We didn't stay for long and I don't really recommend it.
旧市街からは自転車で30分ほど。ビーチはそこまで綺麗ではなく残念でした。自転車で行くと、ビーチが見える位でここに止めろ。この先は徒歩だ。と言われたので仕方なく止めたら、帰りに1ドル請求されました。言われてないとブチ切れしたら1万ドンでいいと言われました。悪質な詐欺なので注意！

I recently visited An Bang beach and was disappointed - it was very crowded with lounge chairs everywhere.  The water was gray and not blue and there were hawkers everywhere.  It wasn't relaxing at all.  Maybe the remote ends are better, but I left as soon as I could.

I returned to the An Bang beach and the small village behind the beach after my first visit in Jan 2015. What a change! They have demolished many small houses, sometimes a whole row of them in the village to make space for 'modern' hotels. Other houses appeared empty, probably awaiting the same fate. Especially the southern end of the village seems effected. Construction is on-going everywhere , noise, dust, garbage all around. The Beach itself with its many small bars / restaurants seems largely untouched ( so far ) . But I would not like to spend time in the village.

Wir haben uns dazu entschlossen, diesem Strand einmal einen Besuch abzustatten. Mit dem Hoteltransfer ging es in die Nähe des Strandes, danach mußten wir noch ein paar Schritte gehen. Doch was wir zu sehen bekamen war ein häßlicher Strand, weder sehenswert noch lohnenswert. Wir haben direkt wieder den nächsten Hoteltransfer genutzt um dort zu verschwinden.

Si vous arrivée en vélo on vous arrêtés pour mettre votre vélo au parking (25000d)Puis a peine descendu sur la plage des racoleur de transat vous accoste de tous les côtés. On en voulait un. Le prix est de 50000d ou gratuit si vous déjeuner au restaurant (bui bui) du dessus. Ensuite j'ai été 2 fois aux toilettes la première fois sans problème la seconde fois un homme posté devant demandé 5000 d. Alors que l'hygiène n'y est pas. Bref plage quelconque sans réel intérêt.

The beach is not something to right a postcard home about. It's windy all the time and quite dirty, the water is not that warm either. The first thing that happens when you get there is local people screaming to you because they want you to rent a beach chair - and there's a lot of competition. The only thing I liked about the beach was the waves- pretty fun!:)
Parking avec Velo payant, Plage envahie de transats, sable et eau pas du tout paradisiaque... mais un bon resto au bord de l eau...celui qui est blanc avez des parasols bleus

There are sections of the b each that are dirty--plastic bags, broken styrofoam coolers discarded by vendors. T?he clean parts of the beach have those covered lounge chairs with people who guard them. This is Asia, you have to be either clairvoyant or read up on the mores of the beach before you go. So, you can sit in the lounge chair for free if you eat at the restaurant that hosts it. If you just order drinks, you have to pay a fee between 100,00-500,000 dong. Yeah that is about $4-$20 to sit in that lounge chair for the day. I ordered only a drink since I ate before I got there and just because someone wanted to sit in the lounge chair next to the one where my stuff was, the restaurant staff moved my lounge chair and belongings out into the sun. Needless to say, since they expected to collect fees from me on the lounge chair, then don't move it into the sun. I did get into an argument with them about it with them which made them cringe. They don't like it when you raise your voice and get upset. Not to mention that the walking vendors will aggressively attempt to sell you souvenirs that you can get at the stores for cheaper. There is also a strong undertow and the signs do recommend no swimming. Personally, I would skip this beach.
Circulez Y a plus rien à voir. En plus on se gele les miches en fevrier. La sinistrose completeCirculez Y a plus rien à voir. En plus on se gele les miches en fevrier. La sinistrose complete
Une plage. Que dire de plus. Elle est sale, à peine arrivé (en scooter) que 3 personne me tombe dessus pour me faire payer le parking alors que je n'ai même pas mis les pieds à terre. Sans plus.

Admittedly I think when you're an Australian you're spoilt with nice beaches but I found the beach very dirty, lots of litter and nothing too appealing. We were there on an overcast day so no blue water or lovely colours like some people's photos. Out of season I really wouldn't bother.

We visited once, the moment you step foot near the sand they pounce! To get you to lay on their beds and eat at their restaurant- you dont pay for the beds if you do.  The beds are lined up side by side no space to feel comfortable! Then there is a continual stream of people selling stuff.  They dont accept your first no thanks but the fifth!!!And some even try to sell time share!!!! Sea was quite rough and the sand quite grainy.  Food at the restaurant was questionable and the toilet facilities in these places - hold it in till you get home.
La plage a peu d’interêt (en tout cas par temps gris en venteux, comme nous avons eu, qui ne faisait ressortir que les innombrables déchets accumulés sur le sable).

We went to this beach on an average day, it was windy and the waves were very rough. There were lifesavers there and even though the signs said no swimming there were some people in the water. We just wanted to experience the beach and sat and watched the waves. There were a lot of people and restaurants there, can't advise on any as we didn't try them. Da nang Beach would by far be the best beach to go to.

We walked here from our hotel in the ancient town in hoi an, we were told it was about 2km ----in fact it turned out to be 4km so it took about an hour, if your up for it you can hire bicycles sadly my old knees wouldn't take the strain.The beach is narrow with a few sun beds for hire, women are onto you as soon as your foot steps onto the sand to take one of their beds, most of them will provide you with drinks at reasonable prices and you can also get food. The waves onto the beach are quite rough and so it's not suitable for swimming, also it's not a beach where young children can splash about in the shallows, we did see a 'life guard' who was pretty aggressive with a small girl playing in the water. Behind the beach are the eating places but most have seen better days--- and having used the loo and witnessing the women washing the dishes directly outside the toilet I don't think I would risk the food.
Garbage everywhere and small bits of tar in the sand. If you put a towel down there's a good chance it will be ruined. Worst beach I've been to in SE Asia.
Vendors are nice people but you can't enjoy your meal. Beach is nice but whatever you do; wherever you go people are selling! I am shocked that that local businesses tolerate this level of annoyance!

It's a little hard to lounge and sun bathe, etc. with so much trash...plastic, bottles, foam, meat, nets, etc. why not use some tourist $ to offer free drinks to whomever collects a bag of trash? This is a global problem that all this human-power ( in the form of tourists) could help with. What is nice is the distance you can walk and the VN people!
Problem with this beach erosion has claimed most of it. No place to lie on beach. Renting beach chairs is available but limited.
우기라서... 파도가 심해서... 그럴 수도 있지만...뭐가 아름다운 비치라는 건지...청소도 제대로 안되는 비치에 쓰레기들...보기 안타까웠다.미케비치나 안방비치나 베트남중부에선 바다는 기대 안하는 걸로!

Beach is not safe for swimming - I think it's because of the rough sea. A lot of rubbish on the beach that the waves have brought in from the sea. A local guy bullied us into parking our bike in his shelter and then charged us 20000 dong. I gave him 200000 and thought I didn't realise I needed change back.

Rethink your desire to jump in especially since they do advise that the undercurrent is strong and dangerous. People do push you and hassle you to pay for a beach chair and there's lovelier beaches back home for me in Australia or even in other parts of Asia, so I wouldn't recommend this one. Not as dirty as some other beaches I've seen, but still the rubbish and lack of care is quite disturbing.

We rode from our hotel to the beach. Parking our bikes was cheap (5,000) however once we got on the beach we were constantly hassled to sit and pay for a   Beach chair. There were no signs for the restaurants visible from the beach so we had to look for other tourists to ask them. The beach is deemed dangerous and signs say you cannot swim. The view of the boats and mountains was nice, however if you missed out on going it wouldn't be devastating. Hotel pool would be just as good.

For a beach that you are not supposed to swim at because it is so dangerous this was one popular little place. We had to pay to park our bikes, there were hawkers everywhere, the beach was steep, water murky, waves dumpy and the place was crawling with tourists ... not my favourite destination obviously.
Sadly, I would not recommend bothering to visit this beach - we visited in November, and it was dirty, trash all around.

Like many beaches in Asia now, this beach is full of rubbish. As soon as you walk past the sun lounges where no one cleans up, you will be walking through trash. You also risk to get bashed in the face by rubbish when swimming. It is just so sad what we are doing to our oceans. Moreover, when I visited this beach five years ago, there were lovely white sand dunes, which are now gone. I guess erosion... If you want to be reminded of where this world is headed while on holidays, go to this beach... If you prefer to ignore it, you are better off to swim at your hotel pool.

Perhaps it was the time of year we visited, but the beach was strewn with rubbish. If you hire a lounger the rubbish is picked up in front of you. During December it's not the best swiming beach for kids as rips and backwash quite strong. Saying all this, we only went to the beach once...over the more popular sunny periods it may be a better experience. Bargain for your paid parking if you go via scooter or bicycle.
파도 엄청 높고수영금지ㅡ게다가 주차비를 지불해야함약간 떨어진 곳에 주차하면 가격이 떨어지니 참고

... Not very nice at all.. It must be nice sometimes the way people rave about it..but not today.. (We are from Australia and may be a little spoilt when it comes to beaches) We were looking forward to a nice relaxing swim and afternoon at the An Bang beach.. Rode out on motorbike and were flagged down and made to pay 20,000 VND to park in what seems to be a public area.. No one mentions this before you go, even bicycles were made to pay..The lady who takes your money can only speak English when she's taking your money. Walk down to the beach ready for a nice swim.. "No Swimming" signs all down the beach.. The sand that meets the water is nice and clean, but any area that is not maintained by a restaurant is full of rubbish. Seems the staff just rake all the garbage to an area thats no their problem anymore.. which is sad.. I asked the life guard where a place to swim was but he could only say.. "No swim".. We took our bike further down to a quieter area a few Ks to the left.. our of range of the guard.. saw a few fishermen and a small "restaurant".. Place was deserted so we just sat in 2 wooden chairs on the beach.. 15 mins later an American guy came out.. starring at us and says.. "can I help you"... We asked about swimming and if he had food and drink and he just said.. "its 50,000 VND to use these seats.."So we just sat in the dirt in front of them for a few mins before leaving.. was pretty rude considering we were the only people for a few Ks each way and there were loads of seats.. The water crashing onto the beach is a greenish brown color and not very inviting or "blue" as people say.. I dont think we'll be going back.. People hassle you to buy their crap.. and pay to sit on a seat.. pretty bloody awful.

It’s a good escape from the noise of Hoi An. Not a particularly wide beach, it is all sand and has a gentle slope into the South China Sea.Numerous boutique hotels and villas line part of the beach, some have sun loungers just for their guests. There are plenty of others, which seemed to be controlled by restaurants. Some charge more than others to use. We managed to use the loungers for the price of a drink during the day. There are a lot of traders wandering up and down selling the usual overpriced souvenirs or snacks. A firm ‘no thank you’ usually does the trick. There is a section where Lifeguards are on duty. You can hire paddle boards, surf boards, body boards and jet skis.

Stayed here 3 days in the beautiful life beach villas. The beach is clean, narrow and touristy. The sea is clean and was wonderfully warm in July. In terms of snorkeling, there is nothing special to see, the waves loose the sand, which also obstructs the view. If you are going for a quiet, pearly white beach with palm trees, then this is not the place. For a nice swim it is fine and clean!For 1 million dong you can go there for 15 minutes on the jet ski.

The beach, which they tell as the most beautiful in the area, is pretty but don't expect a tropical sea.All in all to spend a few hours on the beach you can do, but the water is like that of our Adriatic Riviera

The beach is narrow, the length of meters 800(with signs of civilization)Very close to the shore of the buoy High prices in coastal restaurants.After 4 p.m. a large influx of locals.The place is wild and unsettled, there are not even bins for garbage.At the same time sandy entrance,clean water

It's an okay beach on the north Hoi An coast. The sea is warm and there's plenty of restaurants and sun beds with umbrellas. Shouldn't pay more than 50,000 dong for a sunbed for the day, some people try to charge more like 100,000, or 150 for two. Still, it is only a couple of pounds. The beds are operated by the restaurant you are in front of. Some of them will give you a free bed if you buy a cocktail, people haggle with the vendors for the best price.You get hassled quite a bit by the local beach sellers, jewellery, fruit, sunglasses. It is annoying because they're very persistent, but on the other hand, the Vietnamese are really nice people, friendly and chatty.It's not the cleanest beach I've been to.

Beautiful beach!! Beware of vendors!  There are Vendors everywhere and they try to be a bit aggressive with you too.  Not fun.  I charged me 70,000 VND for a bed.  At the lady told me that I was to purchase a drink at 100,000 VND or food.  I choose a coconut ( that’s what I had a taste for ) she then told me No.  Then I checked the price of the coconut it was 60,000 VND I figured out what she was saying and that wasn’t enough money for her lol. Overall, go to the beach but be firm I paid to lay on the bed and enjoy the ocean.  I politely told vendors NO to anything else.

The beach itself is OK,  there are lifeguards, tanning beds (either paid or in exchange for buying food and drinks at the restaurant they belong to) if you go south from the main entry it's a bit less crowded

It is tropical storm season so there weren't many tourists around, but the day was sunny so we went for a walk along the beach. Storms had littered the beach with debris, but otherwise, it was clean. The water is shallow and brown from wind and wave action. The fresh sea breeze was a delight.

The beach is nice, warm water and sand.Umbrellas and deck chairs are free for those who consume in the premises. Towels are rented for just over 1 euro per day.Drink and food prices are a bit higher than elsewhere. Many sellers a little pushy but the pearls they sell are real.Don't eat grilled crabs...they're burned!

Nice beach and clean.Went to the sound of silence coffee/Bar, ordered and took their sunbeds- great all round. Waves are big which made it fun.Nice break from hoi an and close to the surrounding paddy's fieldsGreat beach, grab is only a few ££ so better to do it than have the hassle of cycling back at night time after sunset
Nice beach with many local bars and restaurants. Fairly clean. The sea was lovely in terms of temperature and waves. Free sunbeds
Very nice clean beachDownside is the very loud noise from fishing boots that constantly trawl up and down very close to the beachSadly a number of them throw their garbage overboard into the sea

Very crowded, the sea is not inviting, but the walk between the restaurants is very beautiful, shaded by colorful plants and flowers...Nice, slightly hippy atmosphere, and in the evening it's very nice to have dinner by the sea...Furthermore, in the evening they are not as annoying as during the day, insisting at every step on buying or going into bars, and you can't park your scooter or bike. without them attacking you for parking money.

Sad to see the beach filled styrofoam and plastic waste while doing my morning walk from The Deck House to Four Seasons hotel. The Four Seasons Hotel has the cleanest beach. Glad that I didn't pay too much to stay in this area. Two nights is good enough.

Beach looks well from distance, but once you come closer you can see a lot of rubbish flowing from the sea and scavengers collecting the plastic bottles and cans.That's a pitty because the beach has great potential.
이상 고온 현상이 있다고 하더니, 정말 여기는 넘 더워서 있을수가 없었어요. 모래 한번 밟고 다시 차타고 숙소로 왔습니다. 아이가 얼굴이 빨개져서 땀을 비오듯 흘리며 넘 힘들어하니, 정말 무슨일이 날까 걱정되더라구요. 바다는 예쁜것 같던데 제대로 보지못해 아쉬웠어요.
beautiful beach with lots of locals trying to catch the waves and tides as it comes in. it's peaceful though as you walk by the sea

Cette plage est très fréquentée. Un grand parking attend les vélos au tarif de 5 000 dongs par vélo. Il y a de nombreux restaurants qui se livrent une féroce concurrence. C'est tout juste si le personnel du restaurant concurrent ne vient pas vous chercher, alors que vous êtes en train de consulter la carte d'un autre restaurant ! Mais les prix se tiennent. Nous avons déjeuné sur place et comme souvent, c'était bon. Cela nous a permis de profiter d'un transat avec parasol sans frais supplémentaires. Quant à ceux qui profitent des transats avec la promesse de consommer, et qui auraient l'idée de s'éclipser, les serveuses ont l'oeil et savent remettre les resquilleurs sur le bon chemin. La plage est propre, l'eau claire et la baignade agréable. Mais nous avons largement préféré Cua Dai beach.

It was very hot when we were in Hoi An so we decided to visit An Bang Beach for some respite. This beach is full of sun lounges and restaurants.  On the day we went the beach was flat so nice for a swim.When we first arrived we were being yelled at by numerous ladies to use their sun lounges.  This was very surprising as we had been to the beach in Da Nang and it was a lot calmer.  Overwhelmed by them all we just picked one spot that had an umbrella big enough to keep the sun out. When I asked how much she said don’t worry if you buy something from us its free.  I asked again how much and she said 50K for each bed but free if you buy something. We got a couple of lounges and settled in, next thing you know she is pulling up another lounge and putting it in between ours and the next ones effectively meaning you had 6 lounges against each other with no room between them.  This is not comfortable, you don’t want to be that close to strangers.  We did not get this in Da Nang.  There you had reasonable space between everyone and no one yelling at you to go to their lounges.Whilst we were sitting there a young man came around looking for his credit card.  He told the ladies that his credit card was in his phone and it had been there before he went for a swim and missing when he came back.  They told him he must have left it at the Hotel.  He was adamant he did not and that it had gone missing when he had gone for a swim.I have to say there was interesting behaviour by these ladies.  There were 3 Korean guys sitting right beside us.  When they went for lunch at the restaurant one of the ladies draped herself across the sun lounge with her arm around one of the back packs.  I thought this was strange why are you laying on the lounges when they are being used.  Go lay on an empty one.  Having just heard a young man say his credit card was missing I sat up in my lounge and looked at the lady in an obvious way.  She started yelling at a lady who was selling bracelets and next thing I know the lady with the bracelets is coming directly at me.  I though here we go again I have already said no thanks 5 times but instead of trying to sell me her bracelets she lay down on the lounge beside me and tried to engage with me so that I would turn around and face her not the lady with her arm across the backpack.  She was asking where have your friends gone, how long are you here etc.  I did not engage with her and kept looking at the lady with her arm across the backpack.  Both the ladies then got up and went elsewhere.  So just be careful when you are at this beach.  Always take your belongings with you when you go to their restaurants for lunch and if you are on your own and want to go for a swim invest in a waterproof plastic cover for your phone so you can take it with you into the beach.We ate at their restaurant and the food was ordinary (as expected) and expensive. In hindsight we should have paid for the lounge chairs and gone into the town to look for lunch but having lunch at the beach restaurants is convenient.

Pretty ordinary . Sunset ? Nope faces wrong way so unless some astrological anomaly was taking place the evening I visited don’t expect a sunset .Nice sand , a good option for those with young children .

A Bang beach is a long and fairly narrow Sandy beach populated by many tourists on a good day.  The sea varies from tranquil to quite rough at times..  I would not want to swim if the wind is strong and seas are rough.  It is full of interest with eateries and sun lounges along  the shore.  The water itself is fresh feeling but only moderately clear,It has a lovely open vista  but is subject to weather changes.
The beach is okay, but nothing special at it. It's very touristy place and people trying to sell you and get you at their sunbeds. Liked Danang's beaches better.
Glass on the beach and rubbish unfortunately, if you walk away from the bars you can put a towel down at the end and enjoy the waves and water. Not the best beach but worth a visit if in the area
Still very wildly natural beach ! Lot of waste on the beach ! Seafood restaurants are various/ but not cheap ! You can swim here but i dont see many people swimming here...just local did

The beach itself is quite lovely. However, we found a lot of rubbish along the shoreline, some of which were broken glass and dangerous if you're barefoot on the sand. There were loungers along the beach which were free to use as long as you purchase food and drink from the host restaurant and bar. There were of course quite a few touts selling cheap souvenirs.

Beach was okay but be aware if you’re cycling there is a guy with a whistle pretending to be security who will try to charge you an extortionate amount to park your bicycle. You DONT have to do this. It’s clear he does it every day and it really annoyed us when we realised we’d been scammed. Be wary
