Not for us. All along the shore are beach huts/cafes/bars geared towards the tourists. The beach is long but not very wide. The waves can get a bit brisk with the wind. However, it did not (on the day we visited) look like an attractive beach where you could lie and soak up the atmosphere. Not sure what the attraction is because it is definitely not pretty.

We took a quick bus ride with our hotel shuttle to the beach. It was a rainy day but we enjoyed a walk. Sadly the beach is very dirty with loads of plastic pollution. Hopefully something that the authorities and local businesses can work together to fix. And for all of us humans wherever we live, let’s stop using plastics.

We stayed at Tan Thanh, a 10 minute walk along the beach from the main An Bang area. The further you get from the An Bang restaurants, the dirtier the beach gets- lots of plastic, polystyrene, even a dead dog. There was a yellow/ brown scum on the water right along the coast. It may have been natural rather than pollution but didn’t make the sea appealing. It’s hard to do anything about that but the beach could easily be kept clean. There are numerous hotels, condos and restaurants backing onto the beach and surely they could take responsibility with a daily clean up after the tide has gone out?

Poor An Bang Beach. Hit by the last typhoon they are really struggling here and desperately need the tourists to return. Covid just about wiped them out and now they are cleaning up after the typhoon. I will definitely return, it’s got a great vibe. We had a coffee and some meals and would love to return next year.

The beach itself is very nice, far from the tropical Caribbean view but nice.Places to eat around it are ok, and also there is a separation in zones between the hotel zones and the local places.What is a pity is the level of small dirt and plastics around the ends of the beach and the entrances.The good thing if you are lucky is that you can surf on nice waves.
Bãi biển ít dịch vụ vui chơi, bãi tắm hơi nhỏ và k có chỗ để tắm nước ngọt. Các nhà hàng xung quanh nhiều Hải sản tươi và giá cả phải chăng
The beach was crowded with people and activities. It was great to see the beach town coming back to life after 2 years of lock-down.
Bái biển không có gì đặc sắc, đông người và giá dịch vụ cung hơi cao so vơi tất cả. Nếu tắm biên thì không nên đến đây.

We visited on a cool, windy day, biking in from our hotel in central Hoi An, so we were not visiting for the beach scene. Upon arriving down the main road from the Tra Que Vegetable Villages, we were stopped in our tracks by attendants manning the pay parking, barking at us and practically steering us directly into their lot - when I said I was just turning around, they didn't seem to accept this, and grabbed my handle bars. It was very stressful - we did manage to turn around and get out, parking at an information center closer to the main crossroad. I think it might have been pay parking as well, but no one was manning the site when we arrived, so we locked up our bikes and walked to the beach.The beach seems very large, nice and sandy, though windy on the day we visited. There are tons of beach chairs and parasols, all of which are run by one restaurant, hotel or other, and all charge fees for use - there are attendants here too, to ensure you don't sit for free. The beach feels so very crowded in by businesses and amenities, that I don't see how it would be a relaxing place to hang out, even on a hot, sunny day. I say give this place a pass.

The weather we experienced here in february was very windy and of course with big waves. The temperature felt like not that warm. We didn't stay more than one hour. The bars and restaurants are so Vietnam style - giving a sort of cosy feeling mixed with some exotic experience. Try it out.
Banana boats, parachutes and filthy sandy beaches have ruined this once gorgeous beach. Straw umbrellas cover the sand like vicious acne.    If you like mass tourism this place is for you.

Username: Hoi An Da Nang tourist taxi cheap
Comment: Đẹp,giá cả bình dân...Nhiều địa điểm ăn uống.nước biển sạch..có bãi giữ xe lớn và nhiều chỗ tắm nước ngọt.

The petrol station on Hai Ba Trung street, on the way to the beach, is notorious for scamming tourists so if you  motorbike to the beach, make sure they reset the pumps before filing your tank.  If you bicycle, don't put your stuff in the front basket because there have been a rise in cases where people on motorbikes snatch them and take off quickly.  Once you get to the An Bang area, there is an official looking guy across the street from Blue Mart (great food there) who has a whistle and demands tourists park their bike in a paid lot.  You can ignore him, you don't have to park there.   Besides that, An Bang is okay.  I recommend you park your bike in front of a restaurant of your choice and order some food or drinks, that way they will allow you to use their beach chairs.  It can be annoying to be charged for sitting in a beach chair, but honestly if you owned a restaurant you wouldn't want people using your space for free all day.   Lastly,  if you want a quieter beach, just go about 1km to the south to Tam Thanh beach area.  Resorts there are much more friendly, it's quieter and you won't be hassled by as many sellers.
The facilities were not great. You can rent a bed but they’re mostly dirty and full of sand. Unless you’re staying at the hotel there it’s not worth a visit.
A nice beach with some great restaurants along the beach front but seen better beaches in Australia.

Went here twice the first time the beach was fairly clean, the second time there was an awful lot of rubbish lying about, I would say mainly from fishing boats! The sand is quite nice and soft and if you walk along the beach away from the sun loungers it is very quiet no problem finding a spot under the trees, while we were there the sea was fairly rough & not suitable for swimming.

Lekker strand voor een paar uurtjes. Niet heel bijzonder. In de omgevende landen van Vietnam zijn mooiere randen. Er vinden zich wel gezellige barretjes en restaurantjes. Wanneer je hier eet en drinkt, kun je gratis gebruik maken van de zonnebedjes.

This beach was a little too chaotic for our liking. There were a lot of vendors walking around the beach selling trinkets, and many restaurant owners trying to get you to sit in their lounge chairs and eat at their restaurant. When we go to the beach, we prefer to relax, unwind and read a little. We eventually chose a restaurant and enjoyed the lounge chairs for a little bit, but the whole beach experience was more chaotic than I was hoping for. We preferred some of the quieter beaches down the street.

First impressions weren't great being harassed by a screaming lady and man with a whistle telling us we had to pay extortionate amounts to park our bike and we weren't allowed on the beach with it. Scammers.Then walking along the beach you still cant relax as you keep getting harassed to sit on beds and buy food or drinks to "pay" for the beds. Eventually we found a clear strip with no beds or restaurants so we could actually take in the view. Finally some peace!Sea was nice, waves a little powerful, not good enough for surfing. Sand was gritty and beach had rubbish strewn over it making finding somewhere to sit uncomfortable. We stayed for about 2 or 3 hours and left.
Went there by bike but not allogeen into the beach you have to pay parking fee. Lots of parasols and chairs, bit chaotic.

The first thing you see when you get to this beach are permanent signs saying “no swimming”. This is a little extreme, as there were children (supervised) playing in the waves.  The water was very rough though.  Rows and rows of padded lounge chairs, restaurant hawkers letting you lay on them for free if you promised to buy either a drink or food from them.  A single beer (I suppose, never could have just one) was enough to use it the whole day.  Lots of activities, para-sailing, jet skiing going on.  Overall an ok place to lay in the sun, but very limited ability to go in the water.

What was goodRelaxing on a loungerWhat was badOverpriced drinks, of courseRubbish everywhere, especially on the parts of the beach without sun loungersFeeling like there was a dollar sign target on my head along with every other visitorPeople being hassled to move away from the loungers, I can see why the sellers need to protect those spaces but it's all so unclear and feels so petty and money hungry

This could be such a lovely beach as it stretches for miles and miles but sadly it is blighted by so much rubbish. We came here last year but there seems to be more rubbish this year. Somebody needs to take responsibility for cleaning the beach, whether it is the local council and/or restaurant/bar owners. Such a shame.

This beach wasn't perfect for me. Alittle too busy and they try make you sit on their beach chairs. We didn't we just found a place and lied on the beach. The water was refreshing for a nice swim though and clean enough. Just too busy for my liking!
We enjoyed our time for sure. It's lovely, just very busy and full of tourists. Maybe walk for a ways once you get there and you'll find your own space.

Wide, clean beach at least, clean in front of the affordable, beach clubs like The Deck House, The Shore Club and Salt Pub, having the beach so close by, it's less than a 10 minute car ride, is a nice bonus to all the historical charm of Hoi An.

We were staying in Hoi An town and decided to have some time relaxing on the beach rather than around s pool.The hotel runs a complementary shuttle to an associated beach bar with free sun loungers and wc facilities. Great idea.We had an enjoyable morning and the beach was clean. Only shame is that, in my personal opinion, there were too many lining the back edge of the sand, meaning that your first twenty metres on the sand was trying to find a way through!!There's clearly demand as they were largely all in use but it just gets s big too 'touristy' for me.

Belle plage! Prévoyez vous parcontre à arriver avec des serviettes ou autres car évidament les chaises de sont pas gratuite à moins d’être client d’un des restaurants! Si vous arrivez en vélo vous n’avez ps à vous stationnez ou ils vous le demandent vous pouvez aller plus loin dans la ruelle gratuitement!
Per noi che veniamo da una località turistica di mare con spiagge chilometriche, non è che sia il massimo, ma comunque un buon spicchio per respirare un po di salsedine. A soli 5km da Hoi An
Fin strand men dyrt att hyra solstolar. Lite jobbigt med alla försäljare som gick omkring där. Fanns ingenstans på stranden att handla något att äta eller dricka

We went to the beach for a day trip from Hoi An which was a good call. The beach is quite crowded with people, bars, restaurants and vendors everywhere. We even had to pay to park our bikes. The beach bars are nice though and give a relaxing atmosphere.

Username: SAS Holidays & a bit of Business on the
Comment: Limited beaches available due to many many rocky waterfronts. Most resorts create their own sand and water experience.
Kohtuu siisti ranta, hieman muoviroskia. Vesi puhtaan näköistä. Muutamana päivänä isohkoja aaltoja. Vain muutamia kauppiaita, mikä oli hyvö juttu.

Our hotel had a partner beach club, so we were able to take a shuttle down to the beach.  Had plenty of loungers, the actual staff working there were great!  We were bothered a LOT by hawkers, though.  I assume it depends on the season and perhaps how many other people are around.  I tried to pretend like I was asleep, and the hawkers repeatedly would even wake me up, tap me, whatever, until I would listed to them.  It took many, many no's before I got each one to leave us, and another would come soon after.  I have world traveled plenty, and am a little used to this, but I thought it was REALLY excessive.We had cocktails which were good.  Only waded into the water, as it's quite rough in January, and not that warm.  Mainly, I just needed a beach day since I live inland, and this was enough to quench that need.

This beach is nice but coming from Australia we are spoilt with beautiful beaches everywhere so An Bang pales in comparison. The whole stretch of beach is covered in beach beds and bars and restaurants so while the view is nice it has an over populated feeling.The beach has "no swimming" signs everywhere which is a good thing because it looks very rough and dangerous.If you are riding in on the main road (bicycle or motorbike) vendors try to stop you to say; free parking if you buy a drink. We didn't know what it was all about so when we rode to the end there was a parking lot with some sort of security guy stopping everyone from taking bikes closer to the beach. We parked the 4 bicycles for 40,000 VND, which is very little, but just a note to be prepared.Eating and drinking in any of the beach front restaurants is a bit pricier but totally worth it for the atmostphere, view and people watching.Overall, nice if you don't mind the crowds and cluttered feel.
추천 장소로 높아서 기대하고 방문 했는데공사중이어서 정신 없었어요사람들은 많았는데..날이 약간  추워서 그런지 해변을 걷다가사진만 찍고 돌아가더군요..샤워 대충하고 1시간 반정도 점심만 대충 먹고 왔어요..

Ne vous y rendez surtout pas en taxi au risque de vous faire arnaquer.. Ils demandent le prix à la tête du client, marchandez. Sur place, la plage est belle mais comme bcp d'autres et il y a tellement mieux à faire dans cette région.
예쁜 식당들 많아서 사진 찍기 좋으니 여자들끼리 방문하면 좋을 것 같습니다. 저는 저번에 갔던 한적한 히든비치가 더 좋네요.
Water was freezing but beach is huge and lots of restaurants, stores and bars in the area. A few vendors but not harassing.
Dieser Strand lohnt sich für einen Ausflug doch für einen Strandurlaub für ich ihn nicht empfehlen. Allerdings gibt es einige gute Bars und Restaurant die ihre Liegen anbieten.

Pas très propre et très bling bling cela n'est pas ce que nous recherchons d'une plage.  De plus la baignade est interdite et pour couronné le tout le parking est payant vélo est payant bous sommes resté 20 min et avons perdu 10.000 vnd/pers
Der angeblich schönste Strand in der Nähe von Hoi An- ziemlich schmutzig. Leider wird er gerade komplett mit Hotels zugebaut und verliert dadurch seinen Charme.

An Bang beach is close to the city center. We visited the beach around noon. There are many shops & shacks along the beach. You can have a few drinks and food. The sunbeds are free if you order food. The beach hardly has space available for really enjoying the view. Afternoon the swimming was prohibited too. Water was cold so didn’t take bath. Overall nothing great about the view. It’s a good place to visit for a change.

Como la mayoría de personas que están varios días en Hoi An cogimos unas bicis y en unos 20 min aproximadamente nos plantamos en esta playa. La playa en sí no me gustó, está muy dejada, el mar la mayoría de veces muy movido y las infraestructuras dejan bastante que desear pero la excursión en sí vale la pena. El camino, una vez sales del tráfico de la ciudad, transcurre entre arrozales y es muy agradable, luego llegas a la playa y si el mar no está para bsñarte te puedes dar un paseíllo y o bien comer por allí en uno de los restaurantes o tomarte algo y volver a Hoi An. Recomendable
Nærliggende restauranter havde ikke noget super lækkert at byde på og selve stranden var lidt skuffende.

Paikalliseksi rannaksi varmaan parasta tasoa, yleisesti ei mikään ihmeellinen. Merivirrat olivat niin voimakkaita, että uinti oli kielletty. Turistikaman kauppaajat häiritsivät ja tunkivat juttusille liian hanakasti, eivät antaneet periksi ja jopa koputtivat nukkuvaa lomailijaa jalkaan, jotta saisivat kauppaa tehtyä.

Arrived on bicycles. Accosted by women blowing whistle and demanding $1 to look after our bikes. THIS IS A SCAM. Dont pay her just chain bike around the corner for free. Beach and sea beautiful. Hire a sunlounger for 50,000 dong (£1.50 ish) And crack on. Well wprth the 3km cycle from Hoi An centre. Easy flat ride and relatively safe amongst the crazy Vietnamese trafic!
This beach is just lower average, compared to others in Vietnam.Hoi An is not a beach destination of course.

We were not even expecting to visit a beach except we had some time and googled and this came up. We arrived early before 8am and the stretches of sand were wide and nice and the sea is rather choppy. But the facilities are a bit run down and there were strays every where. If all you are seeking is a stretch of sand n sea, then yes, this is acceptable

For those of us here in the middle of November, we arrived right as a storm was hitting the south.  It was raining quite a lot and the water was a bit rough.  The first day we went out to the beach, our heart was broken at the amount of rubbish on the beach.  By our third day though most of that rubbish was gone.  I think it was due to the storm that the beach had been really dirtied, and it took the locals a few days to clear out there little patches of sand.  Mind you in some cases they seem to sweep it off to the part of the beach where there are no restaurants.  We saw people collecting recyclables, and even some foreigners cleaning up - a really great attitude. The beach at this time of year is not clear blue,but again hard to say what the storm caused.  We are here for another week, so will review again before we leave!
I was looking forward to relaxing on this beach for a few days. However, I was just disappointed in it. There is trash everywhere, which is extremely sad.

Long sandy beach with bar owned loungers on. Our hotel did a shuttle run to and from the beach although we used their free bikes to visit the beach, which was clean! You could hire jet skis and go parasailing if you wanted although the ride was only around 2 mins airtime so not worth it in my opinion.
sea is warm and sortof grey-green, bars are a bit rip-offy until you wander far down from main road. it's just a bit tacky, even though the splash and beer after hot day is refreshing
No es una playa para recordar , agua limpia pero  no turquesa , mucha construcción. Restaurantes agradables para comer con vista de mar

An Bang Beach ist ein netter Strand. Es gibt viele Beach Bars und dort kann man liegen leihen und essen/trinken.Es ist kein paradisischer Strand, aber für einen Tag zum ausspannen gut geeignet.Ich fand es für vietnamesische Verhältnisse recht sauber.

Der Strand an sich ist ein schöner weißer Sandstrand. Dennoch werden für fast alles Gebühren erhoben: Fahrradparkplatz, Strandliege, Toilette. Die Strandliegenverkäufer "kämpfen" um jeden Gast. Auf dem Weg zum Strand und danach werden sehr viele Taxi Angebote offeriert.Wer dies alles ausblenden oder in Kauf nimmt, kann einen schönen Tag am Strand verbringen.

After travelling non stop for days we were wanting some beach time and we found it here. Really friendly people relaxing beach water was nice and warm. If you wanted to do water sports they were available as well.With showers and beach towels available complimentary with smiling staff.Had a nice fresh seafood lunch. Overall this place was great to rejuvenate our batteries. Would differently go back there .

we went to just to have a few hrs at a beach.  you get the usual people selling tacky stuff.  lots off bars we were at moyo which had good food and the service was good.  bad thing is theres lots off rubbish so watch your walking.

Arrivati gratuitamente con la navetta dell'hotel abbiamo trascorso qualche ora di relax assoluto. Spiaggia abbastanza grande e pulita con varie attività, qui abbiamo fatto un bagno e pranzato più che decentemente.
沖縄みたいに綺麗な魚はいないが、波が穏やか、遠浅でとても綺麗。砂浜も真っ白。アンバンビーチ入り口辺りでマリンスポーツも出来ます。サンベットもドリンク一杯注文すれば一日中借りれます！！また行きたい！
If your after a gorgeous beach with good waves and clean, white sand then this beach is not it (try the Australian beaches)

wij gingen hier s'morgens  op de fiets uit het centrum naar toe. het geen prima te doen is.we werden gelijk aan gesproken door mensen die hun ligsteol aan ons wilde verhuren of een vd attraties.in begin hebben we in alle rust kunnen geniet van de zon en zee.echter na de middag kwam een groep dronken engelse jongere dit verpesten door luide muziek en misdragingen

Nice enough beach but nothing special. A string of eateries lines it, each with beach chairs and umbrellas. Some seem to rent you towels too. You can stay for hours and go for a swim as long as you buy something. Be aware that some will charge extra for use of the chair if you only get a drink (seems fair enough to me)There is a road directly up to the beach - they won’t allow bicycles up the last bit so you need to pay for parking. Instead if you prefer you can just access the beach via one of the eateries on the road running parallel to the beach.

i may be bias - comparing it to all the beaches we have visited, this is just an average one for us. it’s probably good enough if you just want to have a few hours of getting away from the crowd in hoi an old town, walking in the sand and playing with waves. just don’t expect white sandy beach and turquoise water. we were there in the morning. we heard the beach gets rather and crowded in the afternoon.we visited the beach as our hotel provided free shuttle service and we got to use umbrellas and deck chairs for free. plus the kids obviously prefer beach than walking the old town. it’s about 10 minutes by car from the old town. use Grab to book taxi easily.personally i don’t see hoi an or danang as destination for beach holiday.  but surprisingly the beaches are filled with korean tourists.  it’s not the reason for my visit, so i am not going to be too fussed about it.

Na een heerlijke fietstocht aangekomen in An Beach. Je moet je fiets stallen tegen een vergoeding bij de ingang. Aangekomen op.het strand staan de ligbedden bijna in zee... Wij hebben de pech dat de rode vlag is gehezen. Dus niet zwemmen ;-) Dan maar een strandtent opgezocht en hier heerlijk gegeten en gedronken. Het strand is erg snel aan het verdwijnen waardoor er weinig ruimte overblijft voor recreatie.....
Nie jest to bynajmniej najpiękniejsza plaża na świecie, ale jest dosyć czysta i schludna. To powinno wystarczyć.
To be honest, a very average beach in my opinion. However, it is a good place to relax and offers watersports if that's your thing. Super hot in August 🥵

Alors je vis dans le sud et cette plage vaut Palavas les flots, c'est la dire, pas grand chose, donc si nous n'aimez pas Palavas inutile d'y aller  . Attrapé couillon pour touriste partout mais Hoi an... C'est souvent ça.
Une plage comme toute les autres ,mer ,sable ,vendeurs ,resto ....etcRien d’exceptionnel, rien à critiquer pour un peu de detente
Eettentjes smerig , te lang op de drankjes moeten wachten. De ligstoelen werden erg duur verhuurd. Veel vliegen door vuil.

È il meglio che abbiamo trovato in Vietnam, il che non significa che sia un paradiso. La spiaggia è ampia ( il solito spiaggione kilometrico del Vietnam) e abbastanza pulita. Il mare, in agosto, al mattino abbastanza calmo da poterci nuotare, al pomeriggio più agitato. Comunque apparentemente pulito. Noi abbiamo utilizzato i lettini forniti dalla struttura dove si dormiva. Ma basta consumare nei loro bar/ristoranti e puoi starci gratis, al massimo un paio di euro. Ci sono dei tratti più o meno affollati a seconda delle preferenze. Valido per una pausa di un paio di giorni. Stanno costruendo molto e male. Noi eravamo in una struttura semplice, a boungalouws che diventeranno una rarità
They've paved paradise. The beach was fetid and very crowded. We spent an hour there before getting out of dodge. I recall this beach in 2003 and it was stunning. It's a shame really.

An sich ist der Strand schön, die Wassertiefe ideal (auch für Nichtschwimmer geeignet). Man bezahlt allerdings für die Liegen - sei es in bar oder mit überteuerten Getränken. Dafür könnte man sich einen sauberen Strand/Sand erwarten. Neben unzähligen Zigarettestummeln liegen Getränkedosen und die ein oder andere Glasscherbe. Für eine Liege maximal 50k bezahlen und Getränke im Supermarkt direkt vor dem Strand holen - dann habt ihr einen angenehmen und preiswerten Tag. Für die Liegen werden euch sonst 100k bzw 50k pro Getränk abgeknöpft.

A nice enough beach and relatively litter free, but very crowded and lots of stuff scattered around (tires for floating/vendors/never ending rows of beach chairs). Chairs should cost 50k, but it doesn't mean they won't try to sucker people into 100k. It's really not expensive either way, just a head's up. Some solid restaurants in the area is why I'd give 3 stars instead of fewer. Sections of My Khe in Da Nang and Cua Dai (maybe misspelled) are definitely better than An Bang's main strip.
Very popular beach but off course very crowed even if it offers a lot of fun and recreations. Enjoy this nice sandy beach and relax !
낮에 잠깐만 들른곳이긴 한데일단 뷰는 정말 좋아요! 그러나 낮에가서 너무 뜨거워서 오래 있을수도 없어요. 베트남 사람들은 대부분 새벽에 수영한다고 하더라고요<~

Very crowded so don't go for peace and quiet lots of facilities bars food went 2 times first one good waves 2, time none you could only swim out so far para gliding speed boats the public entrance is at the end of the street

La plage de An Bang ressemble à de nombreuses plages du monde entier : du sable, eau à 30 degrés, fond de cocotiers... Et beaucoup de monde.Le sable n'était pas si sale que lu précédemment dans d'autres commentaires.A savoir la plage est très longue. Vous trouverez des zones surveillées, surpeuplées, et d'autres plus calmes.Si vous êtes nageurs, il n'est pas recommandé d'aller très loin, il y a quelques courants et les va-et-vient des scooters des mers (j'en ai compté 3 en mouvement) sont plutôt gênants.

C'est  une plage propre de sablePas de possibilité  de faire du snorkelling rien à voir.On est sollicités en.permanence pour les activités  et des ventes de tout et rien. Possibilité de prendre des transats gratuitement si on prend un plat.Vers 16h les vietnamiens et les vendeurs de nourriture s'installent.. les étrangers partent.

An Bang beach serves as respite from the hustle of Hoi An town with some decent eateries on the beach stretching up the coast.However, the beach itself is not pretty and the sea is almost always merky.A worthwhile visit but don’t expect Phu QuocQuality here.

貸し自転車でアンバンビーチへ。ビーチ入口の横の自転車パーキングに停めようとしたら、かなり前のパーキングの兄ちゃんにストップと手を広げられ停めされられた。私達以外にも欧米系の家族も同じように停めさせられた。帰ってきて、自転車取り出したら、2台で100000ドンと。おいおい、高すぎだろと、もっと安くしてと言っても、駄目だ、どこも同じ値段だ！と、結構な勢いで。もう、子供連れだし面倒だから払ったけど、ここに自転車停めさせられないように皆さん気をつけてください。

An Bang beach is nice enough, although I was expecting something more picturesque given the description in Lonely Planet. We cycled from Hoi An, which is pleasant once you’re out of central Hoi An (there’s a cycle lane of sorts). What was very irritating was that we were stopped as we approached the beach and told we could not take our bikes any closer and had to leave them there (as though this were an official rule) - only for money to be demanded from us when we came back to collect them later. When we refused to pay because this hadn’t been mentioned previously, the man walked away with some of our belongings until we paid him (in the end we paid him half of what he was asking for so that we could get our belongings back).
Única playa bonita por la zona de Hoi An. No es una playa con agua cristalina pero fue la mejor que visitamos por esta zona. Agua caliente.

ホイアンから自転車20分で行ける素朴なビーチ。このあたりでは真夏といえる6月でしたが、それほど混んでないし、まだまだリゾート地といえるほど施設などが充実してはいません。だから、海の家的なレストランで飲食すればただでゆったりと座って一日過ごせたりするので、のんびりしたい方にはお勧めです。端のほうではパラセーリングもできますが、リーズナブルなのでトライしやすいと思います。ただ水はそんなにきれいではないし、魚も見られないので、シュノーケリングなどしたい方は対岸の島に渡るといいらしいです。ダナンのほうは建築ラッシュで次々とリゾート開発していたので、ここも5年後ぐらいには様変わりするかも。でも、私は今のままのほうがのんびりしていていいな。

Nice beach, not stunning. Nicely set up beach chairs with umbrellas which you don’t pay for if you eat there. Food was average at best. Cheap drinks and relaxing views. Water is salty and cooler than hotel pool so refreshing.Was alright but nothing to rave about.

We hired scooters and rode here from our hotel.We were ushered into a parking spot and charged 50,000 vnd even though we only wanted to have a lookIt was crowded with overpriced eatery’s and the beach was all lounges and umbrellas- for a feeThe was very little vibe just a feel for a cash grabWe stayed 10 minutes and returned to find our bikes had been moved

Not sure what the fusd about this beach is as it's just a beach. Things get a bit interesting after 3pm as stalls are set up on the beach with people selling food. I tried the pineapple with salt and chilli and it was certainly a different but enjoyable taste.Will be back but it really has no "wow" factor.

This beach is ok. No tthe cleanest, there are Hawkers selling their wares, but will leave you alone eventually. Check prices of the beds if you want to rent them as they differ. SOme offer a shower also where some dont. The water is very pleasant, dont expect blue sea but its clean and warm. We spent two days here. It is relaixing ands there are water sports if you want them.

Decidimos venirnos a la playa desde Hoi an en bici. Quizás tuvimos mala suerte porque el agua estaba movida con un color bastante verde. Quizás estamos acostumbrados a las aguas del mediterráneo o quizás ese día simplemente estaba revuelta porque había olas. Mucha gente intentando captarte para que ocupes los sillones que tienen en la arena, decidimos decir que si para tener sombra. Pagamos las bebidas (50000 por  casi todo) y usamos las sillas gratis, no te cobran únicamente por las sillas, te piden que consumas. En la misma calle que va a la playa te captan y cobran por dejar las bicis en una especie de parking. Lo único agobiante es que se te acerca mucha gente muestras estás tranquila en tu tumbona a venderte de todo.
We walked along from the ship pub to the shore club and apart from in front of the many restaurants there was so much rubbish! Could be a good beach if only it were kept clean!

Just been to My Khe public beach and Non Nuoc beach along the 5-star resorts in Da Nang (main city area) just a day before visiting this beach, and had great expectations for this famous An Bang beach at Hoi An. Sadly, my dreams of an even better beach was shattered.Assessment criteria:1. PrivacyMy Khe was a public beach in the main city area, and yet there were at least 10 times less people decked like sardines on the beach lounge chairs than at this An Bang beach. I believe it's called, "the butterfly effect of being close to Hoi An, the famous Unesco Heritage Ancient Town". Ok I made the name up but you probably get what I'm trying to say.2. Quality of sandYes this matters too. The white powdery kind always preferred over the coarse kind, since the sensation from each is just so different. Unfortunately, since I've just visited Non Nuoc beach, the sand here is just much coarser. Also, there were many black particles. 3. AmenitiesPerhaps my 5-star hotel dropped me at a bad part of this beach but I really doubt so. To be fair I did not walk along the entire beach to check it out, but the amenities at this "beach club" were too basic. A number of wooden beach lounge chairs were crumbling. The drinks selection here were mostly canned drinks from a cooler box. Forget about nice soft cabanas or even tents. They're not here. We showered from a pipe near the ground (not even a proper shower head that works gosh). It made me wished that I had spent more time enjoying the cool amenities at the 5-star resorts at Non Nuon beach.Overall:Born, raised, and travelled to most of Asia's great beaches - and this is not one of them. Non Nuoc beach at Danang city is just SO much better.End note:To be REALLY fair, there are many more worse ones around the world (think: much more overcrowded beaches in Pattaya/Phi Phi in Thailand, or Mar Bella in Barcelona, or extremely crowded yet expensive ones in Sorrento, Italy). That's why An Bang beach is more towards average than bad. To be honest, the waves, the comfortable breeze and temperature, and the sheer size of the beach still makes it an a-ok, quite alright beach to visit.
Very nice beach, but they take advantage of tourists. Further down is much nicer and quieter beaches, with same ambiance, just lot more bang for your buck.
The beach is flooded with tourists and many small business trying to make money. We found it far more relaxing at our hotel pool.

The sand is fine and white and gently shelves at the seas edge; but the backdrop is derelict building sites and the beach has building debris and lots of litter, discarded and derived from jetsomMuch tidier in front of the private resort hotels’ frontages
とてもきれいなビーチと聞いていたので、ホテルから自転車で行ってみました。自転車で約20分。日曜日に行ったせいでしょうか、大混雑で、江の島なみの大混雑。朝いちばんで行くといいかもしれません

Visited An Bang during the past 3 weeks, and while it was a beach with Palm trees and some restaurants, it is still is still just a beach.Power boats, Jetskis, buzzing around tear into any relaxing mind set and the touts and beach sellers make it less than idyllic.Beer is over priced and the food is poor as it caters for the easy touch backpacker or young pisshead market.there is better just down the road

Pleasantly suprised and enjoyed but did not like the the people selling us things on the beach, they hassled us when we declined to buy things. Great restaurant with good food and drinks. Beach clean and tidy. Loved the umbrellas and loungers to enjoy the beach.

Nous avions fait le découverte de cette plage il y a quelques années et nous avions aimé. Que de changements depuis. La plage a perdu beaucoup de sa largeur à cause de la montée des eaux visiblement. L'endroit reste agréable pour s'y baigner avec la chaleur que nous avons eu durant notre séjour mais a beaucoup perdu de son interêt. Quel dommage.
数年前の浸食でビーチが半分くらいになってしまった。元々ローカルビーチで、地元の人がのんびりしていたけど、最近はお店も増えて賑やかというか騒々しくなっている。アジアの他のビーチほどではないけど、音楽がうるさかったり、物売りが多かったりしていて、リラックスするという感じではない。旅行者が南国のビーチを想像していくとがっかりするとおもいます。

de passage en vélo, j'ai pu voir ce que c'était. Une plage que je dirais commune, avec des restos tout le long. Franchement, quitte à me délasser, je pense que si votre hôtel possède une piscine, le bien -être y sera sûrement plus au rdv, surtout si il fait très très chaud.
cette plage ne m'a pas plus, cela m'a fait pensé à l'Italie, les gens se front griller au soleil !!!trop chaud, pas de palmiers pour avoir un peu d ombre...
너무 더울 때 방문해서인지 큰 감흥을 느끼지 못했음.주변에 평이 괜찮은 식당에서 식사를 했는 데 가격은 매우 비싸고 맛은 기대에 못미치고... 바닷가에 사람은 많은 데 경치는 그리 안좋고...  1시간정도 구경하고 호텔로 돌아옴. 한여름 보다는 조금 선선한 가을이 좋을 듯
Too hassley people dont leave you alone to relax we had a drink in a bar and went very crowded loungers lined up right next to each other
Aardig strand wel schoon en wel rustig. Door de weeks is het nogal rustig in het weekend veel lokalen

This beach area is vast in length..Various beach bar/ food chalets dotted along its shoreline..Its situated aprox 25mins from the town centre. Our hotel gave regular shuttle bus to and from it..The sea current is strong, so if you're taking young children to the area, be extra vigilant..Sunbeds are available along the beach, but be prepared to pay for them as it appears the individual bars/ food places have their own pitch..But if you're eating/ drinking then they wont charge you.Worth a visit if you have a spare few hours, but not a must do..

We visited this beach, and it is just a beach.  There seems to have been a fair bit of damage done to the coastline from storms, and maybe other parts of it were better.  There were a lot of people in a fairly small area, people to bring drinks and sell stuff, but not our favourite place.
a really beautiful beach with great waves and decent fish restaurants  but  busy - touristy -overpriced

An Bang beach is a nice sea change from the normal business of Hoi An . It’s certainly not up there in my top beaches but I live in Queensland and am spoilt for choice with some of the most beautiful beaches . You can use the beach chairs if you buy drinks or good at the restaurants . There are a few sellers on the beach but weren’t pushy at all . We walked down to the end to Soul restaurant , seemed a bit quieter down there and they weren’t pushy at all for us to use their chairs . A nice day at the beach .
A nice beach but sadly increasingly spoilt by the rubbish washed up on the foreshore ...but a good half day trip from Hoi An

Plage tout a fait commune, l'eau n'y est pas turquoise mais c'est propre ce qui est appréciable au Vietnam pour nous Européen. Vous pourrez "louer" un transat gratuitement à condition de consommer ce qui ne gâche en rien le plaisir de se baigner dans une eau relativement chaude.

The beach is worthwhile visiting, especially given the heat and proximity to Hoi An. Waters are just the right temperature to cool off.  The beach itself is a strip, nothing special, though has many hotel and restaurant touts vying for your attention - so not necessarily the most relaxing environment.
Plage prise par les locations de transat qui gâchent le paysage. Sentiment d'attrape touriste un peu loin des avis que j'avais lu un peu partout. Bref un peu décevant
Disappointed overall Soul Food was a perfect little gem there!Busy too many lounges hassled by hawkers couldn’t see the water for people!

Go here either early in the morning or an hour before the sunset (preferable). The beach is not a must visit. Go if you have 2 hours in hand. You can relax on the shacks for 20000 VND per shack. Get a can of beer and watch the people swimming. The beach is suitable for swimming.

was disappointed with the beach and services.  way to crowded and we were there off season.  It was fun to see the woven round fishing boats, otherwise no desire to return to that portion of the beach.

Belle plage au sable blanc très fin.... mais tellement touristique maintenant !! 🙁Jet ski, parachute acsentionnel, plage privé tout le long...c’est vraiment dommage.De préférence, y aller le matin... à partir de 11 heures, le sable est bouillant !! Nous y sommes allés en scooter, attention à payer le stationnement par une consommation ..

Visited here on a group trip. The beach itself is ok, and the sea relatively clean and unpolluted here (although we found clearer water up near Hoi An). There’s little by way of shade or beach facilities though, so perhaps avoid if that’s what you’re looking for

Plage propre contrairement à ce dont j'avais peu après avoir lu les avis. Si vous venez à vélo ou en scooter, le parking est payant via des gens qui vous alpague sur le chemin. Attention les tarifs varient à la tête du client. Idem pour les transats. Vous pouvez mettre juste une serviette sur le sable mais ça manque un peu de place. A partir de 17h tous les locaux viennent prendre un bain et manger sur la plage et tout est archi bondé pendant l'heure et demie qui suit. A fuire si vous aimez le calme. Les restau en bord de plage ne sont pas terrible. Mieux vaut éviter.

In short it’s a beach you would expect next to all these big Hotels etc. It’s busy with a lot of walking sellers...a bit pricey like 50k for a coconut I will never pay 😜 but hey a lot of ppl seems to like it and the Restaurants around...some of them are very good.
We were there in November so it was a little cold. We could still swim, but there was a large boom to prevent the beach from being eroded by the waves. It was a beautiful view for sunrises

It's not as bad as some other venues, but there are a lot of beach huts before you get to the beach, but it's lovely after you walk past the neatly-arranged rows of beach umbrella-huts set three-deep.You can see Danang in the distance off to the left. Well worth a dinner at the Tuyet Restaurant at sunset, but you won't get to see much once it gets dark - time your dinner right!

We are perhaps a little harsh being from Queensland Australia where we have some of the best beaches in the world but An Bang was disappointing the beach has suffered from erosion so not much of it and what was left is covered with sun lounges which you can use as long as you pay to get lunch or dinner from one of the restaurants. Too much commercialism unfortunately which is not we are after when we just want to have a swim and relax on the beach.
If you have been before in a busy town like Ho Chi Minh City, you will love this place. On the opposite to the city but just a normal beach. Not so touristic

Flew into Hoi An from Saigon,Made a nice change from the chaotic  city life,beach is 4km from Hoi An,Minutes by scooter,Beware of the locals coming up demanding you pay to park up your scooter,Ignore them and keep going park on the side of the road  anywhere you like ,No probs for the 10 times we went there apart from the beach side cafes etc demand a 200,000 fee to use a sun bed if you are not eating there ,They don't like it at the first on the right beach cafe the one near the steps  if you plonk your towel on the beach in front of their cafe /sun beds they try to get you to move thinking they own the beach to,.Just tell them to piss off .There is another more quiet beach about 1 and half km to the  right  of you ,Loads better no one bothers you there,no beach sellers no bother  and the cafes and the owners attitudes  are better quality all round ,We ended up there in the end for a lot better atmosphere  .Overall An Bang is still  a great beach though .
Sandy beach, palm trees, helpful beach staff, delicious crab lunch Just sad that the water and sand is polluted ,we all have to do something about cleaning up our planet.

We wanted to check out An Bang Beach...we live on the Gold Coast so was interested....the water that day was a bit choppy but it was hot and I was gonna go in.....got dumped quite a few times which hubby thought was very funny...there were a lot of Asians in their undies getting dumped at the same time...very funny.....you get hassled too much to buy the wares.....hubby decided to have a beer and I went to the loo to wash off the salt...was told I had to pay which is fine .....when finished I was surrounded by 3 women to pay but I didn't have my wallet and I said that my husband was just across at the bar and there was nearly a lynching hahaha.....one lady saw what was happening and she spoke to them in Vietnamese and I went and got 10,000 dong for my shower.....I must admit after this little episode  I did have  the best ice cream I have ever eaten......so be aware of being hassled and have some notes for a shower would I go back  to this beach........nope!!!

Beige sand, good swimming.  Is not cleaned up by anyone so you see bear bottles and other garbage here and there.  Good food service but varies in price by restaurant serving the beach.  The best quality food and drink with the best price was south end.  Could tell you the name, it was off the strip, the menu says MENU, but the beach chairs are free and they have good plastic tables.

This place was probably gorgeous and could still be if it were not for the volume of litter & cigarette butts. If you walk a few yards from the main entry point to the beach there are piles of rubbish at the rear of the beach. It’s a shame that we as tourist are happy to take the pics and explore for “hidden gems” but perhaps don’t take care enough about the litter we create & leave behind. It’s a pity as this could be an outstanding location. Especially given the great accommodation & eateries in the local.
ホテルや店がいっぱいできています私にはあまりベトナムらしくなく面白くないです地元の雰囲気を楽しみたいならマーケットがおすすめです人々の生活が見られます
Een aantal dagen hier geweest. Fijn strand met helder water. Alleen langs de duinen veel rommel net als in de rest van Vietnam.

Bien entendu il faut y faire un saut si vous êtes à Hoi An, mais nous n’avons pas trouvé ce lieu particulièrement enchanteur. Sympa pour se poser un moment et boire un verre dans un des bars de plage, mais oubliez les termes idyllique, magnifique, exceptionnelle, paradisiaque… c’est du pipeau marketing. Une plage, point.
La playa no está mal aunque no esperes mucho, llena de turistas pero hay buenos sitios para comer y tomar algo, lo mejor el camino en bici desde Hoi an.

Une plage mise dans les 10 plus belles au monde : laissez moi rire . On se demande quel lobbys se cache là dessous . C est une plage noire de monde avec des transats de partout . Vous pouvez mangez sur les transats, et vous baignez mais attention plage assez dangereuse pour la baignade  pour les enfants .

My wife and I went here one morning as our hotel in Hoi An had an area set up at the Deck House. We were disappointed with the beach as it was covered with garbage and cigarette butts. Seemed like the staff had missed a couple of days cleaning the litter off the beach.
ビーチには藁のパラソルとビーチチェアが並んでおり、その周辺にレストランが並んでいますが、朝9時頃にはどこも開いていませんでした。シーフードを楽しみたいならそれ以降に行くことをお勧めします。尚、水はさほど綺麗ではなく、個人的には海に入りたい気持ちにはなりませんでした。なお、自転車で行った場合、ビーチ手前で自転車を預ける様に言われます（出る時に2万VND支払いが必要です。）

Maybe it was the month we went but there was really not much beach to see here. The water was murky and the beach was washed up with jellyfish and rubbish. We had a drink at one of the many restaurant/bar and asked the waitress if the beach was always like this. She replied no, usually summer time the water is blue and the sand is nice and clean... so i guess visit only during summer time if you want to see a nice beach.
This sea is not clean. Sometime is black color but feel is good.Very quite and people is friendly lol

Stopped here on the way to Hoi An. Was thinking about eating lunch here. Burgers, sandwiches, seafood were all 5x the price. No thanks. I get it you get a nice view of the ocean, but not worth that much more in my opinion. Beach was crowded with mostly Chinese and Euopreans. Beach chairs and umbrellas with service people are around.

It’s clean and no garbage scattering around . It’s fine if just come here for sunbathing and relaxing . But there were local people trying to sell handcrafted souvenirs and bothered us after seeing us bought from the other lady apart from that everything is okay .

Went to an bang beach after reading reports about being best beach in Hoi An.arrived on bicycles and was herded into a parking area by security where women screaming you pay 40000 dong leave bicycle which we did and walked down to beach where people asked up to 400000 dong for 2 sunbeds leave it alone go to other beaches like hidden beach where beach is just as nice no hassle ,free parking and sunbeds and nice places to eat and drink

Erstens gibt es schönere Strände und zweitens ist er dreckig. Umweltschutz steht hier noch ganz am Anfang.Pluspunkt: feiner SandstrandMinus: alles was angeschwemmt wird, bleibt liegen. Aber er ist breit und ewig langBitte nehmt eure Zigarettenfilter wieder mit und lasst sie nicht im Sand stecken.

I remember the first time we saw the beach, that’s exciting: Typical coast beach, waves are not too big to pull you in, water’s not too cold, sand’s really soft. My husband and I walked along the beach for miles every day for a week straight, kept extending our stay. We did see a lot garbage on the beach but nothing like our last day —— We had dinner and headed back to our room around 10pm, saw maximum amount of rats and cockroaches on the streets, smelled sewerage as soon as we opened the door. We had to turned on the AC with windows open. It never happened before. We woke up super early in the morning and went for a walk along the beach. OMG, miles of garbage washed up on the shoreline, locals who run business on the beach were collecting garbage and burning them right on the side. It must be a high tide last night. When we’re putting on our shoes we saw a little sand bump—a dead baby pig was buried under it with its head out. We decided to leave town that day. I wouldn’t recommend this beach cuz it’s not well maintained, water is not that clean and warm and calm for people who want romantic atmosphere, and waves are not big enough for surfing. Besides, you can never see blue sky cuz the fog ( or air pollution? I’m not sure ), most place you eat is not sanitary, and you have really limited food options. You pay a lot more money here for low quality food and water just because locals rule the place.

Spiaggia lunghissima con tanti piccoli stabilimenti dove puoi affittare lettini e ombrelloni al costo di 100k dong, se a pranzo mangi qualcosa non ti fanno pagare i lettini. Mare niente di che, giusto x passare qualche ora

Not the best of beaches and certainly not as good as Thailand. South end of beach is bunked up with huge sand bags from the sea erosion. It is recommended Da Nang beach is cleaner and nicer.Good choice of beach restaurants and bars to chose from.Spent good part of day there and couldn’t swim in sea.

You get mithered here from beach vendors although they are very nice and polite it is a sales tactic to get you on side with their story to buy something. Beach not that nice. Water not what I expected from Vietnam. It wasn't that clean looking. Bars on the beach were cheap though

I am currently staying here for two months and have been visiting An Bang many times before. I think it might be interesting for people coming here for the first time and still relying on old and outdated information.An Bang village and beach used to be a very relaxing and unspoiled place until a couple of years ago. Most people came by bicycle or bike from Hoi An, spent a day on the beach, eating and drinking at one of the simple restaurants that had appeared on the central part of the beach. Very few people at that time ventured into the village at all and all went back to their homestays in Hoi An in the evening.Then some local people started renting out rooms in their home or opening a small restaurant in front of their house.Within a year lot's of homestays, restaurants, travelagents and Spas were suddenly appearing everywhere in the village.Then the first foreigners and big vietnamese investors started to get interested in An Bang, in some international newspaper An Bang was listed as one of the top beach destinations in Asia and from then on it has been "gold digger time" in An Bang.That includes now several expensive beach clubs where the manager claims that they want to become the number one beachclub in Vietnam, several foreign owned restaurants and hotels, several places on the beach and in the village with loud music even late at night, a beach venue already trying to run a "full moon party", at least four companies offering watersport activities with parasailing, waterscooter, bananaboats and so on.......At the northern end of the beach The Pearl Hoi An Festa Hotel has recently opened and if you look at the pictures there is much more to come.At the southern end of the beach is a big area fenced off ready to start building the Hilton Hotel An Bang.Along the very short main road of An Bang village they have torn down at least four old houses during the last two weeks and there's now lot's of building activity going on on the "main road" and in the village towards the beach.While there was hardly any traffic here two years ago there's now a constant traffic flow of lorries, numerous taxis and tour-vans all day long.Despite all that what I have mentioned above do I enjoy my stay here very much!An Bang feels surpringly still like the old remote vietnamese fishing village. People still go out fishing from the beach in their traditional boats, most of the local restaurants still operate out of their home kitchen with very unprofessional but superfriendly service offering superb food for a reasonable price, the local market is still operating and you can have a lot of fun with the extremly friendly people here.There is no "hardsell" neither on the beach or in the village but you pay of course more than the local price everywhere and prices have gone up recently quite a lot.An Bang is on the way to become more expensive than Hoi An.I still really love An Bang and would definitely recommend to come here now, but you should know what to expect. The beach is nice but far away from a tropical dream beach.It's the village and it's people which make this place interesting and special but looking at the current exploration I doubt very much that it will be like this a lot longer.And yes, this is of course also a very biased personal experience but it still might help somebody to make his own decision.

Plage avec vagues et courants comme sur notre océan atlantique (en février 2019) mais ce n'est peut-être pas forcément tout le temps ça. Des resto payottes au dessus de la plage sont agréables mais la plage est trop touristique à mon gout : beaucoup de transats, des rabatteurs pour les sports nautiques, mer agitée (mais à une température sympa).Il faut y aller voir et manger mais pas bien plus ..

Kann man auf jeden Fall besuchen. Wie an jedem anderen Strand in der Umgebung auch, gibt es auch an diesem Plastikmüll. Um etwas leerere Abschnitte zu finden muss man schon etwas spazieren, aber das lohnt sich dann auch allemal, denn an manchen Abschnitten ist es unglaublich voll.

We were staying at a lovely hotel and we were told we could use the private beach of the hotel opposite. When we went down we were looking a massive sand bags about 2 meters high to retain the beach from being washed away, whilst I understand the need for this it wasn’t nice to look at, but if you walk along the beach for a few minutes the beach opens out and is quite pleasant.
Pleasant Beach , Bars and Restaurants just off the beach to the rear, Parasols and chairs to hire on the beach , Gets breezy , TANS you very quickly with the sun , DO NOT FORGET your SUN LOTION.

Found a seat at shore club on beach. Prices are 2-3x that of Hoi An and there is a constant stream of people selling trinkets. The beach is fairly clean, water has some plastic debris but nice to be on ocean.

Marginally better than Cua Dai Beach i.e. at least it doesn't have the monster sized sand bags! Nice outlook to Cham Island and down the coast to Da Nang and its multi Resorts 'Strip'. But over commercialised etc. Better to do the riverside & old Hoi An imho.

waren einen nachmittag da. an der aus hoi an ankommenden strasse befinden sich rechts und links beachbars/ - restaurants.entweder man stellt sein mofa/ rad bei ankunft ab(dong) oder nimmt dieses mit. manche bars bieten kostenloses parken und wifi an, welches man dann mit dem kauf eines getraenks rueck finanziert. aber alles im rahmen: z.b. ein 450 ml bier: 1 euro.strand sehr zugestellt mit liegen. traumstrand geht anders.
Elle serait belle... si elle était mieux entretenue ...des transats...des parasols usagés..des restaurants...peu de poubelles......c’est dommage..

We cycled here from our hotel and found we had to leave the bikes just before the beach with a caretaker. We didn’t stay long and there did not appear to be a cost for this facility. The tide was in and the sandy beach was crowded with loungers, umbrellas and coracles and other water amusements to hire. The water was warm and although I only paddled others were enjoying a swim. We were only approached once by a vendor and would have had to pay for loungers if we had stayed longer.

An Bang Beach is a long stretch of white sands and blue sea. There are sunbeds all along, that you can either pay to use, or buy drinks and food from the bars who own them. Beach sellers frequent the beach selling souvenirs and love a good haggle, but aren't pushy or rude when you say no.Different water sports are available and the guys are open to a good haggling session too.I've only given this an average rating due to personal preference. I love a secluded little bay rather than a long stretch of sun bed filled beach, but if you like this kind of beach it could well be an excellent for you. While in Hoi An go down and see what you think.

Cette plage de sable blanc s’étale sur plusieurs kms. En même temps, les restaurants de plage ont envahi une partie de cette plage avec leurs transats et parasols. L’on est donc loin d’une plage sauvage. Donc, celles et ceux qui souhaitent avoir des bars et restaurants sous la main apprécieront cet endroit. Quand aux autres qui recherchent plus de quiétude, ils devront marcher pour s’éloigner.
lorsqu'on vient visiter la cité historique, c'est un plus d'avoir une plage tout près! Il y a toujours beaucoup de courant mais cela rafraichit.

This beach deserves 5 stars but for the rubbish. Hoi An does so well from tourism, so there is no excuse for having so much rubbish on it. The restaurants/bars should be responsible for cleaning their area and the local council for the areas where there are no restaurants as these are the worst areas. You can walk for an hour each way and be nowhere near the end. We had a sunbed at the Fisherman Restaurant, which is Vegan and a good central spot.Such a shame about the rubbish.
Agréable mais très fréquenté, oubliez les photo de plages paradisiaques (ou alors faut ce lever très tôt...)

Liked the beach...in the main clean and tidy ! As a Cornish man I Appreciated the waves, although no way up to Atlantic standards. Good fun, nice sand and plenty going on. Beach vendors a bit persistent...best thing to do is to buy something cheap early on, and ignore the rest. Spent our time on the sunbeds at the Deckhouse,...good all round spot
Being Australian it’s hard to beat our beaches there however this is one of the better beach’s  in Hoi An

L'ensemble offre un sentiment de beauté privilégiée...quelques regrets cependant,  la plage centrale n'est pas large et supporte malheureusement de nombreux déchets divers,  cannettes métalliques,  polystyrène...etc, Ça n'enlève pas tout le charme,  mais...peut mieux faire !

Não tem areia fina e branca nem águas tao transparentes e verdes como as praias da Tailândia, mas vale pelo passeio. Tem alguns restaurantes e estrutura de apoio rústica. Dali podemos apreciar os pescadores em suas curiosas canoas em forma de cestas. Não gostei do assedio de vendedores locais que insistem até vc comprar algo para se livrar, sendo, em seguida abordado por outro...

After spending time on the beaches of Thailand and the Philippines this beach can’t quite compare. The beach is large and wide but it is a bit dirty and sad to see how many tiny pieces of styrofoam were floating around... not to mention the large pieces and general plastic and trash. The water is not crystal clear but it was still fun to take a dip in for a little while. I was able to bargain for two loungers under an umbrella and had a relaxing time. Though I wouldn’t return to this beach again it’s a nice escape from the ancient town.

Visited An Bang beach for Ted morning. The highlight was the bike ride from Hoi An through rice paddies. Bike trip is easy and not too much traffic to battle. Be aware if people trying to park your bike as you each An Bang. Just ride past them as there are plenty of places to see abc park closer to the beach The beach is average at best. Looks ok to swim but was rubbish on the sand that ruined it.Something to go see while in Hoi An - nothing special

We went here to enjoy some sun but it was quite dirty, probably do to inactivity during the holidays (Tet). It seemed as if mostif the garbage has been washed ashore by waves but there were quite some areas that were littered by tourists and other businesses. It's really a shame. This could be quite a nice beach.

A fine sandy beach and a wonderful view of the mountains horizon. Sadly dirty and untidy. In search of the most suitable place for you, you are verbally agitated by all sorts of "entrepreneurs" who offer you everything and for everyone. The most disturbing is how you are asked, not at all respect, to release the beach if you somehow have the inspiration to put yourself on the aisle of young people who, for a modest amount, raise you up to MAX 5 minutes above the water. It's not a place to enjoy your day. Inspired by the terraces inspired along the beach.
Nicht unbedingt der schönste Strand der Welt. Aber wirklich schön und unerwartet sauber! Für einen Tag wunderbar...

The part of the beach where our resort was situated was not damages by storms although further southwards the beach had been swept away.The beach was clean along the waterfront but the upper part was full of plastic debris. It was nice to see the typical round boats of the fishermen coming ashore in the morning
Wir sind von unserem eigentlichen Strand, der ca. 2 km vom An Bang Beach entfernt lag, gelaufen. Viel zu viele Leute, der Strand ist nichts besonderes mehr.
Für Badeferien ungeeignet, da zu wenig abwechslungsreich und nicht spektakulär. Flacher Einstieg kindergerecht. Halt einfach ein Strand. Punkt.

Nice ride out on bikes from town.  Beach clean and options for chargeable sunbeds or just find a spot on sand. We paid for sunbeds although a bit pricier than elsewhere on Vietnam coast we had experienced so far. Sea nice with gentle sloping beach. Watersports too close to swimmers for my liking.

It’s a nice beach close to Hoi An. We biked there and the ride was scenic and quite beautiful as we rode through the vegetable and rice fields. The annoying thing about he beach is that many folks will prey on tourists to get every penny out of you. We got ripped off parking our bikes which ruined the experience of visiting the beach. I wish the city would try to deter these predatory practices.

Unbelievable, but this area of Vietnam has a nice beach, with nice little places behind, nice guesthouses, cafes, lots of relaxation, it impressed me positively. I don't know if I would take a beach holiday here, but it could also be a viable alternative to sleep outside Hoi An but easily accessible by scooter

I was a bit hesitant to visit due to the mixed reviews, but I enjoyed my time at An Bang Beach! We arrived around 9am before the crowds, and I was surprised at how windy it was. The beach is relatively clean. We spent  few hours on the sun lounges at Dolphin Restaurant, and it was really peaceful.Definitely worth a visit!

A refreshing urban escape with beachside restaurants and bars—some offering free service, others not. The beach isn’t spectacular but does the job. Water was chilly in Dec. Easy access via Grab or, for the adventurous, a rented scooter.

Lovely beach, though quite windy during our mid-December visit. Plenty of vendors offering bracelets, each using the same engaging tactic—tying their pitch to stories from Vietnamese astrology to make a personal connection.

The beach is a 15 mins. drive from the main town of Hoi An. Our hotel organised a free shuttle service to and from the beach at fixed timings. We visited the beach in the morning. It was absolutely deserted yet a beautiful sight. Unfortunately given the rainy weather no one was allowed to swim in the sea. We walked on the sand and had a good time.

An Bang Beach in Hoi An boasts soft sand, clear water, and a relaxed vibe—perfect for swimming, walking, or unwinding seaside. Beachfront cafes serve drinks and meals with great views. It gets busier later, but the atmosphere stays friendly and laid-back. Ideal for a sunny afternoon!
Lovely beach warm water. Watch out for jellyfish season. Lots of hawkers but easy to politely decline their wears. We used the beach club related to our resort

Lovely beach, fine white sand with a shallow waters edge. Lots of beach bars offering sun bed hire, drinks and food.Just be careful of the sun as not all the beach umbrellas block the rays. You may still burn even when sitting under the umbrellas!!

Nice beach with nice water, but there are a few downsides: 1. After lunch (3-4 p.m.) many locals come to the beach, making it difficult to swim.2. The buoys are close enough to shore (apparently local rescuers decided to absolve themselves of responsibility). By noon, you're up to your waist by the buoys.3. There's a lot of biting plankton in the sea, it gets a lot bigger in the afternoon.4. I strongly advise against taking food in restaurants that are almost on the beach, because prices are two to three times higher than on the street, which is 100 meters from the beach. Apparently ripping off tourists.5. For the locals here, the prices are completely different, the sunbeds, I understand, for them at all are free or cost pennies.Tip: only take sunbeds with an umbrella here. The locals offer a drink at their restaurant, then a snack for lunch, after which you do not pay for the sunbed. You shouldn't do that, because food and drink are very expensive.

Very nice beach with lovely sand and good infrastructure. We sat in the same place all the time for the service was very good and a cappuccino that was world class. A big plus is that the beach is clean of debris and it is kept clean.

I spent 9 days in A Bang staying 50 meters from the beach and one street away from the main village. I loved the way the local people enjoy sunrise as a time to exercise and enjoy coffee and local street food for breakfast. Every morning I would go down to the beach for sunrise. I would get a local Vietnamese coffee and a delicious bun with vegetables and egg from a street vendor and soak up the atmosphere. During the day I spent morning or afternoon depending on my plans on a beach chair I rented from one of the many beach bars that scatter the beach. For $5 you can enjoy the day or afternoon in comfort and order drinks and food to your chair. Never once did I feel like I couldn’t take a swim due to not having anyone to mind my belongings. The local people are so beautiful and amazing and in peak season you can just relax and enjoy. I chose to read  and swim but there are lots of water activities to partake in if you fancy it. The local town that runs a block from the beach has great local food and different bars & restaurants to enjoy. The shopping isn’t amazing, but a 10 minute taxi for $7-10 bucks has you in the old quarter with everything you could possibly want. I loved staying here and having somewhere to come and relax in between exploring Hoi An and most tours will pick you up from your hotel. Loved An Bang Beach!

A short break in our circuit 10 minutes by Grab (1€) from our hotel.Beach supervised this lack of maintenance a lot of waste everywhere when we arrive on site women jump on you for an essential umbrella deckchair given the how the sun hits in this month of July, we paid 500 000 VND or 2.30€ person this also includes the toilet and shower. You can spend the day there. The water is very hot the sea is clean the fishermen baskets were on the beach at high tide. We ate just above for 10€ /pers eating light and with a beer the restaurant was very decent with a great view.

We had realistic expectations for An Bang Beach, and it delivered exactly what we needed - a pleasant beach day without pretension. The sand was sugary soft, and though the water wasn't crystal clear, it was refreshing and clean. We enjoyed watching the changing colors as afternoon turned to evening. Best visited when winds are low!
Well maintained beach there are cafes and restaurants all along with sunbeds and solarium.Very nice experience
A place called The Resort Beach. It was the perfect time to have lunch on the terrace, feeling the sea breeze, with the blue sky, white sand and sea blue.

My relatives and I decided to go to An Bang beach for some relaxation and to watch the sunset. We brought some food to eat while drinking beers and singing for fun. The wave current was somewhat strong, so we decided not to swim. Overall, we had a great time at the beach.

We have been at the beach for three days and it is clean and tidy - at the Dolphin Kitchen bar area. We bought a coffee and a couple of drinks through the day (a beer is 30,000) and didn’t pay for beds or umbrellas. Towels were available free too. There are a few lady hawkers selling bracelets mainly targeting female tourists. A friendly ‘no thank you’ in Vietnamese seemed to work - don’t get involved in a chat and they will move on. Great chilled vibe with a mix of nationalities - to escape the crowds in Hỏi An. A Grab taxi around 60,000.

An interesting experience, from paying to park our bicycles to seeing the fishing boats working so close the the shore.As a resident of Australia seeing sun chairs on a beach was a bit disconcerting but accept that it's part of the culture of tourism. The Thung chai, [basket boats] on the the beach and being used to move crew from the fishing vessels was eye catching and made for great photo opportunities.

Very nice beach just 4 km from the center of Hoi An. It is literally full of more or less beautiful beach clubs where you can relax while sipping a cocktail or enjoying an excellent avocado toast. In the period we were there (March) the sea was clean and calm but rather cold. The beach is white and fine sand. A good place to recharge your batteries during a tour of Vietnam.

The soft sand was pleasant to walk on, and the vibrant beach culture added a lively atmosphere, though it felt a bit crowded at times. We enjoyed the stunning sunsets, but the beach itself wasn’t as pristine as we expected—some areas had litter. The local beachfront cafes were convenient for snacks and drinks. Overall, it’s a decent beach for a casual visit, but don’t expect it to be a hidden paradise.

The sand feels warm and pleasant in nice weather, while the sea water is refreshing. However, when the sky is cloudy, the wind picks up. The soft, warm grains of sand contrast with the chilly sea water, creating a nice sensation at the beginning of February.

I loved this long, sandy beach—it’s peaceful and uncrowded. While some areas near the beach feel a bit worn, others are well-maintained and lined with a variety of restaurants and bars. The whole area has a laid-back, rustic charm, reminiscent of the beaches along Mexico’s west coast. It’s a great spot to unwind and enjoy the surroundings.

Ampang Beach, well, it's pretty clean. The water was quite cool, and it was a little cold in the water. There are some bars by the beach that feel average. It was nothing special for the two of us to just walk around and look at the sea. The sand is fine, not too fine. Probably too much to expect, it feels like that. It's okay to play around, there's no need to come.

We spent a lazy afternoon here. The beach is clean enough, but the constant approaches from vendors got annoying. Beach chairs cost extra unless you buy food from restaurants. Water was warm and pleasant. Good for a half-day trip, but not the secluded paradise some make it out to be.

The sand is soft and pristine, and the soothing ocean breeze was a welcome relief from the heat.  It's definitely popular, so don't expect total peace and quiet, but we snagged a spot to ourselves and enjoyed a relaxing afternoon.
Went for a drive here the other day. Hoi An is a nice relaxed area with a decent beach. Enjoyed walking on the shore and visiting the local shops. A nice relaxing day
Wide, extensive beach. Sand can get very hot once, so bath slippers are recommended. Difficult without umbrella and shade.

Loved our afternoon here. The beach was gorgeous..lovely gold sand and very clean. The water was  clear and warm. We swam in front of a beach club (recommend doing this)  and lay on sun beds..the whole area was well looked after. A Bang beach has definitely improved since our last visit a few years ago.

We took a cab from the hotel to the beach and enered the first caffe-restaurant on the left side, next to the beach. The beach was clean, sea is marvelous, even too warm. However the toilet in the restaurant was a horrible, smelly untidy with no paper. I went to the beach, occupied one of the deck chairs (there were really plenty of them free), put my towel and went for a swim while my husband stayed at the caffe having another drink. When I came back and wanted to rest on the mentioned deck chair, I was approached by an older woman, who spoke lousy english, and basically told that I was not allowed to take that specific deck chair and that I should move to another. The point was that we did not order any food and therefore cannot occupy deck chairs. I tried to argue because there were only 5 deck chairs occupied and approximately 90 free, but with no success. After that the woman became very rude so we decided to leave the place. So, based on that experience, I advise everybody to check the rules and especially the toilet before entering the place.

Stayed nearby as soon as we arrived in Vietnam. Was a great place to relax and get out bearings before going out and exploring this beautiful country. Hawkers on the beach are a bit of a pain. Locals set up little tables and cooked food on the beach in the evening and it was delicious. Swam before breakfast every morning and the water was the perfect temperature. The local guys that worked in the surrounding hospo places world so be playing volleyball on the sand before their breakfast shifts started. Very relaxing vibe.

Beach near Hoi An. Long sandy beach with several beach clubs with sun loungers and umbrellas.The water is not transparent in color but it is clean. When we were there in March the sea was rough and the water temperature was not the best.I think summer will be differentBut a beautiful beach to go to if you are in Hoi An

It's certainly not Zanzibar, nor the Seychelles, nor even the Cuban Cayos. But I wonder what we would have done for two and a half days in that canal in Hoi An if there hadn't been this possibility of spending two days at the beach.Walk at least half a kilometer and you will be alone. It's better to go to the restaurants later: I'm not a fan of Vietnamese cuisine, but the one we went to was the worst of the worst.If you go to the right, at the end of the beach (see photo) there is a small establishment with blue sunbeds, and a lady who cooks quite well
Nice beachGreat waves View on Cham islands Plenty of beach restaurantsEasy access from hotel by bus or bike Pleasant day!
Very wide sandy beach with lots of cheap loungers (2€ per day) and umbrellas. Restaurants everywhere where you can eat; lots going on
“It’s the season to be jolly! 🏖️🏝️Enjoying a fun day at Bang Beach in Hoi An,  It was bit windy but certainly a relaxing beach for sunbathing and there are many restaurants around to choose
Very beautiful beach, good temperature water. Very pleasant swimming in January Restaurant and deckchairs available. Fine sand. Clean beach. Walk by the sea with your feet in the water on fine sand.
Beautiful beach, but not safe to swim in during the rainy season due to runoff.  While we were there, a young man died when swimming during a storm.  Walks along the beach are very nice.

Great beach spot but be prepared to be hassled and ripped off. We are not talking major $ here but just the fact they can and will rip off the touristAs you are not allowed to cycle up to beach you must ‘Park’ your bike in designated areas which of course cost $ .  We were charged 5000vnd per bicycle but clearly ( if you read Vietnamese!😂) it was only 2000vnd per bike!! otherwise was nice to escape bustle of Hoi An on 6km each way bike ride.  Have a dip in the sea. Designated bike lane on long stretch along buffalo/ paddies. Relatively flat ride.
Bãi biển còn vẻ đẹp tự nhiên, đời sống phố thị về với biển thì cảm giác còn gì bằng. Lối xuống biển hơi nhỏ, kinh doanh hơi xâm lấn
Good beach experience, there is a mix of tourists and locals. Beach is relatively clean. Standard protocol of beach sellers, catering real pearls, jade, and lava stone- just kidding-all fake.

Nice little beach with a variety of places to grab a drink or bite to eat and then sit on a lounge chair to enjoy the view of the ocean.  Sadly there is some litter along the beach however it appears the local restaurants do their best to keep their area tidy.

Pretty beach with local fishermen, lined with beach bar/restaurants. The water and sand were very nice, except for some trash which seems standard in Vietnam. There were many locals trying to sell things who became quite annoying and pushy.

Clean water, white sand, and sunshine. If you do not want to surf on a busy beach like as ( Da Nang ) , I recommend An Bang beach. Our Family like An Bang beach and seafood here although it has less activities than My Khe Beach.
This beach is okay, maybe for laying out.  The heavy rain gave a special touch to the beach this day, but the sand was clean.We will come back tomorrow. Hope it 'll be seen clearly.
Such a great experience. It is an excellent experience for those looking for local food and craftsmanship. We found unique herbs over there.
Bãi biển khá nổi tiếng với khách du lịch thế giới, nơi thư giãn và ăn hải sản tuyệt vời khi du lịch Hội An. Bạn có thể thuê giường để tắm nắng hoặc uống nước ngay trên bãi biển.

Great beach and surprisingly clean with no rubbish on the beach or in the water.  We settled in on a couple of sun loungers with umbrellas for a couple of hours at Shore Club.  This had a reciprocal arrangement with our hotel in Hoi An.The water was the perfect temperature and the beach itself quiet at 9:30am in the morning.  The sun was already hot!  We left at 11:30am and as we left more people were arriving so can only imagine the beach gets packed as per previous photos.Be mindful of the locals trying to sell you everything from sunglasses, sunblock however, they are pleasant enough and leave you alone once you tell them a few times to move on.We would go back to this beach again
Biển An Bàng rất sạch sẽ, bờ biển dài cho du khách đi dạo. Các món hải sản ở đây cũng rất tươi ngon, nhâm nhi ly bia ngắm cảnh sẽ là rất tuyệt vời 👍👍👍

Nice beach, cool restaurants, charged to park bicycles? Is that normal to be charged for parking  2 bicycles. 100K VND! Seems bizarre - more like local mafia squeezing money from tourists. We questioned some sort of policeman in his office next to said mafia man, and he just laughed. Maybe it’s normal. Not that we are tight for money, but it’s probably better to take a taxi to this spot so you don’t need to pay for parking or bicycles!

Nos hospedamos en el mejor hotel de Hoi An, el Little Riverside. Entre sus servicios disfrutamos del club de playa en An Bang Beach. Un autobús gratuito del hotel te lleva y trae hasta el club. La playa es bonita, con arena dorada. En el club de playa disfrutamos un rico almuerzo, una copa de sobremesa y pasamos una tarde excelente antes de conocer la noche de la ciudad antigua. Cuando llevas varios días recorriendo Vietnam, agradeces pasar unas horas en la playa, reponer energías y seguir disfrutando de este gran país.

Malebné historické vietnamské město Hoi An nabízí také pěkné pláže a Ang Bang k nim rozhodně patří. Krásná a za našeho pobytu klidná pláž se zlatavým pískem a mnoha restauracemi, které vám umožní použít jejich lehátka, pokud si u nich koupíte jídlo a pití. Na moři byly neustále vlny, protože pofukovalo. Osvěžení ve vlnách však  bylo příjemné.

Spent pleasant days here, not too busy with the option of different types of bars and restaurants at the rear. A few shops off the beach where you can pick up drinks and beach items if you don’t want to use the bars with facilities.

The deck chair touts are totally scammers and avoid at all costs and ignore. A walk down beach couple hundred mtres either way of entry to beach Will ensure cheaper prices. Pay 1/4 what they ask. And always make sure one guards belongings. Some good food nosh but check out prices as always. About 70 K Dong either way to Hoi An or reverse.  Great beach but touts saying free chairs if u eat at their restaurant is total crap. Ignore big time. And be dam outright firm with these mongrels.
nice spot lots of things going on , shops and restaurants on the beach, locals doing there own thing
A nice clean beach with lots of cool restaurants. We had lunch at "Soul Beach Restaurant & Bar Live Music" and had a lovely time.
Hier 2 heerlijke dagen aan het stand doorgebracht. Kijk bij welke beach club je wil liggen en meestal zijn de ligbedden gratis als je wat te drinken besteld of tegen een kleine vergoeding.
