Nous avons la chance d'effectuer notre quatrième voyage au Vietnam et à chaque fois nous venons à Hoi An, notre destination préférée avec la vieille ville. La plage d'An Bang mériterait effectivement un minimum de réglementation ne serait ce qu'un affichage des tarifs de parcs à deux-roues et des transats qui empêcheraient les arnaques au préjudice des européens. Il faut toutefois savoir qu'il y a de très bons restaurants avec transats gratuits qui permettent de passer une belle journée. Quant à la plage elle-même, large, de sable blanc. Elle n'est pas plus sale que certaines de Méditerranée et moins dangereuse que le littoral Aquitain. Et pour l'instant il n'y a pas de méduse. Difficile de critiquer l'accueil cette année avec notre regard occidental, les vietnamiens, habituellement souriants et accueillants souffrent des conséquences de la crise sanitaires et sont suspicieux avec nous. Respectons les.

We rented a motorbike and went around hoi an..first stop was An Bang Beach. It was an easy short ride..after parking in the designated area where we pay 20k dong..we went to the beach area and went to the first resto with free umbrella and sunbed for people who will order or eat in the resto. Food was fresh and reasonably priced. Toilet was ok and there were wash basins to use. Waves were big..good for playing with waves. Go in the morning till after lunch to enjoy this short break from shopping and eating in the ild town

We had an afternoon here whilst staying in Hoi An. Great fine sand, with a strong onshore breeze blowing creating a decent swell ( wouldn’t recommend for young families unless closely supervised at all times). Beach front filled with many places to eat ( though at time of writing some were shut down due to C19). Street vendors offered delicious snacks too. Sun loungers under parasol cost 200k Dong for 2 for the day. Great place to sit in the sun and swim, beware the breeze versus sunburn though! Have fun
נחוף נחמד ודי נקי, כשאנחנו היינו גם לא היו יותר מידי אנשים.ניתן לקנות מיטות שיזוף ב150 אלף לזוג (יש כאלה שמבקשים יותר, אל תתפשרו).. בנוסף החוף מלא במסעדות וניתן להזמין אל המיטה בחוף.

A part à Phu Quoc, le Vietnam n a pas vraiment les plus belles plages d Asie.Mais celle ci reste agréable pour agrémenter votre séjour à Hoi An. Un conseil s éloigner des principaux parkings pour avoir des prix raisonnables sur les restos de plage ou choisir ceux ou vous voyez le plus de locaux

Great beach to visit. You can relax on the sand or you can have a dip in the sea or even play beach games. It’s suited to families, couples and solo travellers and the atmosphere is great. You might be bothered by the odd beach street sales person but they’re friendly!

Found this beach a lot nicer and less crowded/busy than Nha Trang beach. A few hotels in Hoi An offer free shuttle service to a private area of the beach, which includes towels and sun chairs. We were a lot less hassled to buy things compared to Nha Trang

Spiaggia magnifica e tranquilla, sabbia accogliente e acqua pulita. Ormai si trova piccola spazzatura qua e là portata dal mare e dalle barche che la navigano. Simpatiche moto acquatiche da noleggiare.
Our hotel in Hoi An shuttled us to the beach and picked us up. We sat on a lounger under an umbrella most of the morning.The waves were small, the water warm, and the sand was soft.
Very laid back and great for relaxing , walking , lunch , local drinks and wave jumping . I guess the beach you dream of

An Bang is a fairly popular beach with sections dominated by beach chairs but also quieter sections. When we visited at low tide there was a lot of beach though it reduced a fair way at high tide. There are restaurants a plenty along the An Bang section though our best meals were inland. The beach is relatively litter free which is a nice change! Beach hawkers are infrequent in Feb and there’s a friendly vibe.

An Bang Beach is a great day trip if you’re spending time in Hoi An. We visited via a short shuttle trip from our hotel (Lantana Riverside Hoi An - highly recommend!). The beach is not as clean as other beaches in Southeast Asia, but it does the trick if you’re looking for a relaxing day by the sea, or some time spent playing in the sand. There were not a lot of rocks near the area of the beach we spent time at, making it fun to play in the water. There’s an area of restaurants nearby for a snack or lunch, with fresh seafood! Stop by Bikini Bottom for lunch if you have the chance - they have many options from burgers to poke bowls, beers on tap, and yogurt bowls as well!
Biển đẹp, các nhà hàng hải sản rất đông. Hải sản tươi ngon . Rất thích hợp để thư giản và tận hưởng.

Got the shuttle form the hotel, led down a path to the bar which led onto the a lovely golden beach.Difficult to beat sitting on a sun lounger with a cold beer.Water was warm(ish) and in her opinion a bit dirty. I thought it was fine.

We werden met de shuttlebus van het hotel naar het strand gebracht. Daar ontvangen door een gastvrouw die ons naar een strandtent bracht met ligbedden en parasols. Daar hoefde niet apart voor betaald te worden. Uiteraard wel voor de consumpties maar die waren niet duur. Erg mooi strand en in de loop van de middag raakte het ook redelijk vol. Om 17 uur weer met busje opgehaald.
Grande plage où l on peut se baigner sans problème et également preprendre un Transat avec matelas pour 50000vnd (2€) avec une boisson comprise!

The beach is nice and clean. Sand is fine. In February was not too hot to spend some time in there. The main area of the beach is taken over by all the bars and restaurants. The beach goes way north and south, so you can find a quiet area.On the other hand the beach restaurants have live music starting at 7pm. There was a band from Seattle, when I was there.

Le temps ne s'y prêter pas aussi mais ce n'est pas une plage paradisiaque! Longue plage pour se promener, c'est un peu sale. Pas envie de s'y baigner.Nous ne sommes posés 2 jours de suite dans un restaurant plage avec transat, le dolphin beach. Tres bien accueil et service. Je conseille.

Visited this beach as our hotel had a shuttle serviceBeach restaurants offered sun loungers free...wow Food was very reasonable with a decent selection and beer very coldSea was rough so no opportunities to swimI was concerned about the litter along the beach and collected some for disposal As a scuba diver this is a passion of mineI did see an old lady coming along collecting plastic bottles which was refreshing to see

Spend few days at AnBang beach recently and found it a great place to relax.  It's also very convenient with lots of food available. If you wish to go Hoi An, it ain't far you can cycle or take a grab.
Kilometerlanger Sandstrand mit unendlich vielen Strandrestaurants und Kaffee‘s!Leider zu unserer Zeit etwas vermüllt!Hier kann man ewig weiterlaufen - aber halt den Rückweg nicht vergessen!

Our hotel shuttle dropped us about 10am and the beach stayed pretty empty until late lunchtime (early February). After that, there were more people, but the beach never felt too crowded, even in the area around the many hotel’s "beach partners". Various comments have said the beach is dirty and spoiled by too many building sites - we didn’t feel that. The sand and water were clean, and the there were some building sites, but not many and they were far back from the beach. I wouldn’t travel a huge distance just for this beach (Da Nang beach is probably nicer) but if you are staying in Hoi An and like the idea of a beach day, this is worth a visit.

Belle plage mais qui ressemble à un autre côte atlantique, la mer est marron pas très chaude et en plus beaucoup de vagues. En revanche, il y a beaucoup de restos qui longent a pas cher et des petites boutiques pour les vêtements de plage. Ainsi que des taxis en revanche difficile de se garer à vélo.
A nice calm beach where you can get a drink or coffee and enjoy the view.Comfy couches and umbrellas are available and is family friendly. It wasn't crowded at 4 pm in January
We enjoyed a good couple of hours relaxing at the beach. Don’t forget to bring your towels as the hotels do not tell you there are non available at the hotel area on the beach.
Has all that you need. Clear water, lovely sand. Lots of loungers. Plenty of beach restaurants. Gets busy with day trippers from Hoi An.

As soon as you arrive you will be approached to buy a cocktail which includes the hire of a sunbed and brolly. It was cheap so worth doing. Ladies selling various trinkets also drop by. Ours was lovely and not pushy and once we bought something left us alone. Sand and sea were clean and the waves were good Tuesday we were there so you could hire a body board if you wanted. Good parking for bikes and mopeds. Only down side were the loos which were disgusting and not what we have seen elsewhere in Vietnam (usually of a good standard).

Renta una motocicleta o bicicleta y anda a esta linda playa junto a Hoi An. Está solo a 15 minutos, y si bien no tiene las aguas paradisíacas de otras playas de Asia, si permite descansar y disfrutar del mar.Recomendable

Really nice beach and fun to bicycle out there. Be aware that you do not have to pay for the sun / beach chairs if you tell the ladies asking for money  that you will eat at their restaurants. They will not tell you themselves. And if you walk down further the beach it gets cheaper.
Very nice. Surf looks good. Maybe a little dangerous today for swimming. Relaxing location. Very close to Hoian town.
Bus desde mi hotel, restaurante asociado al hotel la siesta que permite uso de sus instalaciones, con préstamo de hamacas y sombrillas
Lækker strand pæn og med høje bølger. Der var ikke mange sælger. Ikke de store aktiviteter, hvilket er dejligt hvis man bare ønsker at slappe af
Typical beach...sun beds for hire and pretty clean and not crowded at all. Nice place to chill out away from the hustle and bustle of town.

When coming straight from Hoi An, you arrive to covered parking lot of bikes. This is run by super rude person who will want to over charge you and scream at you. Do not give money to this person. There are bars around where you can park, get a drink with no drama. Just turn a bit to the right and there is small street leading to some bars or to the left or just in front of this parking lot scam idiot.
Nice beach when weather good.Sunbeds and umbrellas.Plenty of places to eat and drink although they are expensive.
해변가 예뻐요~ 수영하시는 분들도 많고패러세일링도 많이 하시네요~날이 좋아서 더 예뻤네요 바다가주변에 식당도 많은데 배도 안 고프고 평도 별로라길래 카페만 다녀왔네용~~~~~~

Notre logement était sur An Bang.Plage Ok, relativement propre. Pas mal de vagues avec parfois baignade interdite. La couleur de l’eau est sans plus. Pas exceptionnel si vous avez vu les plages aux Philippines par exemple. Quelques restos sympas dans le coin néanmoins

The beach isn’t as nice as home (Australia) but the sand is fine and soft and there are sunloungers and umbrellas set up everywhere, free to use as long as you eat or drink at their establishment. It is relatively clean too. A nice place to relax for a while.
Cycled from Hoi An. Beach was clean. Everyone was friendly. Sea was warm. Beer was ice cold. Perfect.

The weather was perfect in January. Beautiful and sunny with a nice cool breeze. Long sandy beach with plenty of beach beds, ours cost 50,000 per bed. The water was cool and refreshing, but a bit brown for me to swim in. But was nice lying on the beach bed and having a beer in the sun. Got a good tan!
가질 수 있는 곳. 카페에서 배드에 누워 바다를 바라보며 시원한 쥬스를 마시고 이야기도 나누고 좋았어요패러세일링 하는 곳이 카페 바로 근처에 있어요 미케비치에서 하려고 안했는데 미케비치는 따로 에약을 안했더니  하는 곳 찾기가 어렵더라구요

We visited the beach and use the facilities of the Wind & Moon restaurant through their hook up with the La Siesta hotel.It was not too crowded.  The sea was nice (a little cold but it was December) and the sand was great as wellA few people selling stuff but they did not persist when we explained that we were not interested.Definitely worth a visit
Da Hoi An ci siamo recati in questa spiaggia in bicicletta, 5 km.Una bella passeggiata tra le risaie.Il mare non è tra i migliori ma per una giornata di relax va benissimo.
Although it not the most beautiful beach we have visited in south east Asia, it is a nice beach to spend a day or two during your visit to Hoi An.

Plenty of beach umbrellas and cafes along the beach. Quite peaceful even though you do get the hawkers approaching you to buy their souvenirs and sun glasses etc. A lot of development and construction going on so sadly in a few years time the primitive charm will be lost and with modernisation will come the crowds.
Lengthy beachfront with multiple restaurants, bars, and lounge chairs. Not too busy earlier in the morning and easy to grab a pair of chairs in a quieter are.

We visited beach on a shuttle from hotel in Hoi An. There were good bars, sunbeds, umbrellas etc and the beach was better carried for than Cua Dia with regular rubbish collections. The beach also does not suffer from erosion and it was pleasant to walk along it beachcoming. Best of the local beaches.
Lots of restaurants to choose from and it’s a nice white sandbeach. Some restaurants have live music or band.
Praia lindissima.com uma extensão enorme. Imensos restaurantes e bares ao longo de toda a praia. Se estiver em hoi an vale a pena visitar

We enjoyed lunch at a little cafe above the beach to enjoy the breeze and the view.  Loads of places offering you to use their sun loungers and pay for drinks and food or vise versa.  Odd concept for us Aussies, beaches are public land and such things are illegal at home.  Still, its nice to get a legal beer at the beach!
1월이지만 수영하는 사람들 꽤 있었고 음료수 주문하면 비치에 있는 썬베드와 파라솔이 사용가능 합니다.호이안에서 그랩 미터기로 8만동 택시비 나왔어요

This beach was within walking distance of our home stay. For only $1.50 you can get a lounge chair and umbrella (or buy an iced coffee for $1.50 and get them for free!). It's very clean and my children found it a great place to build sand castles. Not too crowded, at least not when we were there in late December. The surf can get pretty rough when the tide is in, so be aware of the time of day you go. Lots of fun activities available, such as para-sailing.
Very beautiful place to stay for a day and relax from the city. Nice bars and restaurants to enjoy the beach and relax

Es war nett mit den Rädern mal hinzufahren. Entlang zu laufen und etwas zu trinken.Gemütlich baden würde ich woanders.Man muss Fahrrad nicht zwingend kostenpflichtig abstellen. Einfach zurück auf die Straße und anderen weg zu Beach abbiegen .Gut von hoi an mit Rad zu erreichen .
Had a lovely morning on the beach. Paid for sun loungers under thatch umbrella. Weather was great. No wind. Lovely walk along the beach.

Lovely beach only draw back was the huge pile of sand bags but of course that’s due to the safety precautions I guesss... but otherwise lovely with locals offering you chairs and coconut to chill, number of good restaurants very close by the beach. Super nice to ride a bike there.
그이상도 그이하도 아님... 서핑하기 좋을듯 함. 스노쿨링이나 해양 레포츠를 즐기기엔 뭔가 무리가 있어보임. 백사장은 정말 끝없음

nice sand and a reasonable selection of cafe’s and restaurants without being overdeveloped .The beach slopes quite a bit and then flattens making it ideal for older but not so good for the sea inexperienced under 5’s .The waves are good for body surfing especially for the novice as the backswell is relatively gentle .

The beach is a bit dirty but from my perspective it is better than other nearby beaches. Lot of drinking and eating options, sunbeds are also available. There were huge waves during our visit but still a decent place to relax.
the beach is not as nice as my khe beach but it’s near to old town, u can try sky diving, speed boat ride here. come if u have nothing to do

A beach with lively atmosphere, lots of people around, cafes, beach beds... the beach has fine sand and nice water. You can walk quite far into the water and the waves are quite nice, not too strong but let’s you enjoy!!

We went twice here, first time just to wander and the second time to relax on one of the sunbeds for the day.It's an easy cycle from Hoi An old town. You park your cycle before the beach with one of the parking people. It's 10000dong per bike for the day. I'm not sure how 'official' it is, but you can't take your bike anywhere in the beach area without paying these people. As soon as you get to the beach you are 'asked' to hire a bed. The first time we just wanted to walk, and 15 mins walk in either direction you could find a place to sit down  on the beach. The second visit, we wanted to spend the day at the beach, so we hired a couple of sunbeds under an umbrella. We bought a pizza and fries for lunch from the sunbed women (from a local restaurant) and got our sunbeds for free. We stayed for about 4 hours, watching people, enjoying the sea , reading, listening to music on a beautiful day, so we think we got our monies worth.
We went to Dolphin bar and grill. Nice walk up and down the beach followed by a few drinks and snacks.Didn’t swim as it was a bit cold but would be ok in hotter months.
우기때 다녀왔는데 (2019년 12월 초) 파도가 매우 높고 흐려서 볼 게 별로 없습니다 ㅎㅎ (물론 수영은 못한다는 금지판도 있습니다) 하지만 날씨가 좋다면 좋은 비치입니다. 주변에 외국 음식과 씨푸드 파는 레스토랑이 많고요, 외국인들이 많이 찾아오는 곳입니다.

Lekker strand en genoeg strandtenten aan het begin waar je een bedje kan huren tegen betaling. Als je een stukje verder loopt zijn er wat minder luxe bedden, maar die zijn gratis tegen aanschaf van een drankje die bijna niks kost. Je kan hier een lekkere strandwandeling maken en kijken naar de plaatselijke vissers. Er ligt wel veel aangespoeld afval en verwacht geen wit zandstrand.

I grew up in New England with 8 miles of non stop saydy beach 75-100 feet at low tide so it takes a lot to impress me when it comes to beaches.  An Bang is GORGEOUS!  This is a much more low key beach than looking north to Da Nang.  We were told this is where the locals go so since it was only 85 degrees in November there were very few people here.  We arrived at 11:00 and got 2 loungers under an umbrella and there were plenty more. (At Moyo beach club) Free to access the beach, free for the lounge chair, at least is in November.   The surf was strong and ifyou got off your feet it was hard to get them back under especially after one beer so I would not recommend going in if you are not a strong swimmer....not sure if this condition was common so judge when you go.   Water was warm, clean and clear with no trash in water or on the sand. There were several lovely little shells dotting the sand. (I'm a collector)  There is wait service if you would like a drink or food brought to you or plenty of seating in the "club" if you prefer being off the beach for your meal. Other than relaxing there is not much to do. Manicure pedicure $4 Massage $8  If you want action such as parasailing or jetskis that was north to DaNang.

À découvrir, on peut y venir à vélo depuis le centre de Hoi An. La plage est belle. Pas de déchets plastiques contrairement à beaucoup de plages au Vietnam. Belle vue sur Da Nang depuis la plage. Restos de fruits de mer tout le long, c'est frais et bon. Le seul hic, ce sont les sollicitations trop nombreuses des vendeurs ambulants, qui viennent jusque dans les restaurants pour essayer de vous vendre tout un tas de produits différents.

An Bang makes for a delightful half-day or full day trip from Hoi An. There are many restaurants that will let you use their beach chairs and umbrellas if you buy food and drinks. It is very quiet in the morning but gets busy in the afternoon.
We found ourselves at the extreme north and south ends of the beach which were nice and quiet and also very clean.
We will be back tomorrow! Loving the relaxation on the beach. Very few people here at this time. Great food and service!
Tres Belle plage pas loin de Hoian, j'ai fait du velo pendant 20minuites pour y aller. La mer est chaude.

A nice beach, within cycling distance of Hoi An which added to the experience. If you have had enough of being in the city or doing tours and need a relaxing day on the beach then An Bang is nice enough. You can park your bike in the bike park for a small 30,000 fee where a man looks out for your bike. There were quite powerful surf waves when i went and the no swimming signs were up but people were still wading in and enjoying the waves. There are restaurants all along the shoreline and jet ski and paragliding experiences to enjoy. Its not a stunning beach but its still pretty with yellow sand, waves, good views and pleasant restaurants. The clothes in the shops were cheaper here too compared with Hoi An, possibly as in November its coming to the end of the season so not so many people going to the beach. Street stalls offer good food at mega cheap prices
beach is a good place for relaxing. Nearby you find seafood-restaurants low budget and lecious food. around streets in small village you find many massage and coffe-shops for  having a breake.

Het is er heel gezellig aan het strand er zijn leuke Restaurantjes waar je heerlijk kunt genieten van de zee en het uitzicht we hebben er niet gezwommen maar lekker zitten genieten met een Glas Bier van de uitzicht

The area of the beach straight across from the traffic light is very commercialized. To park bikes and scooters there are charges. The restaurants at the end of the road are big and impersonal. The one on the left wouldn't seat me because I was a solo. The food is ok. Service is ok as well. During the meal expect to be bothered by some one selling something. There seems to be no way to tell them no or leave me alone. Make sure you have a large amount of money when you go. There is a charge for everything.

This is a nice beach in easy reach of Hoi An. There’s lots of nice waterfront options for drinks and food! Unfortunately the swell was quite big when I visited so swimming was not permitted, but it was cheap to hire a sun lounger so I sunbathed instead! Reading other reviews on here I was worried about being bothered by touts a lot, however the people that were selling things were very friendly, and would always take no for an answer. A nice way to spend a day if you’re in the area for a few days!

自転車で行きました。ホテルの人に「すぐ」と言われましたが実際は30分ほどかかりました…行く道は幹線道路沿いでわりと危ないので気を付けてください。海は泳ぎやすい感じではありましたが、ベンチが有料です。水のきれいさはそこそこですが、シュノーケリングができるほどではないです。ただ込み合うほどではないので快適に過ごせると思います。パラセーリングも日本よりは安くできます。日本人も多く、様々な国の人がいますが行ったときは日本人の子どもが迷子になったらしく大騒ぎになってました(数時間後に見つかったようです)。日差しがものすごく強いので日焼けには要注意！
물색이 흙색...ㅋㅋ...ㅠㅠ원래는 푸른 바다빛이겠죠?!서양인들이 많아서 외국온거같은 느낌이  팍팍나긴했는데...결론은 음식점들이 대부분 비싼듯 ㅎㅎㅎ..어린이가 옷을 다 버려서 근처에서 옷을 흥정해서 저렴하게 구입할수있어서 좋았습니다~^^
A clean and sandy beach with good waves for fun swimming. Plenty of sun beds and bar / restaurants with toilets and fresh water showers
Was very clean beach and food was really really tasty, but expensive for vietnamBring own water, and be prepared to pay premium price if u have a meal

We visited the beach twice. The first day we parked our bikes in the paid area (beware they try to charge you extra as a foreigner, pay no more than 10,000 each). It was a windy day, the beach was closed to swimming and we were hassled by every restaurant and seller on the sand. The food is well overpriced as it’s touristy. Not a great experience. The second day we rode our bikes straight past the parking place (the guy literally jumped in front of us yelling stop and tried to force us to park and pay). We turned left and headed down the street parallel to the beach. About 500m down the road there are little side tracks that shoot off down to the beach. Here you can park for free and and buy food/drink from a vendor set up on the beach. For the price of a drink you get a sun lounge and umbrella away from the tourists and most of the sellers. Much more relaxed, much cheaper and less crowded. The water on a good day is lovely and warm and perfect for swimming, beware the undulating bottom.

By now you can guess the secret it out about Hoi An so any way we can avoid the crowds, we did. We rode our Silk Marina Hotel bikes to An Bang Beach and it was a pretty cool ride passing rice paddies and water buffalo. It took us about 20-25 minutes and mostly flat riding. Once we got to the end of the road we were told by some local guy to stop and we had to pay him to park the bikes. I think it was like 20K per bike.We walked down and turned right and it was pretty deserted at about 8:30/9am. We walked a way and once the beach split and you didn't see the restaurants and cabanas we just decided to use the services of one of them (I think ours had a dolphin on it). We had read that there is no charge to use the loungers and umbrellas but we had to order food and drink which we did. The drinks were cheap, the food pretty good and we spent several hours there for under $20. There were water sports being advertised but we didn't use. There were bathrooms and showers and the upper part of the restaurant which was nice. The beach got pretty crowded with tour groups after a few hours so we decided to leave. The water was great but we didn't venture out too far as the waves were pretty big and we didn't see any lifeguards around. Looking at the hotel map there was more riding to be done down the beach and back around via a loop but we wanted to head back into the city to do some shopping. Our parked bikes were fine; and it was a real nice day overall that didn't break the bank as you would expect from these beach side restaurants! Thumbs up for sure for a visit to An Bang!
Beautiful beach with benches to relax on. You just have to order food and drinks to get free benches. Otherwise you have to pay.

Nice long sandy beach nearing to the old town. Sand was clean, water was quite rough so not good for swimming and a fair few water sport motor vehicles were also jetting around in the vicinity (not in a separate area) so might be a bit dangerous if they don’t see you. Lots of sun loungers - some available to hire from 100k VND for the day for two, but we managed to get two for free for the whole day from Dolphin restaurant and bar for just buying a drink (coca cola or beer for 30k VND) and no pressure at all to buy even the first drink, let alone any subsequent drinks - it’s the place where the man shouts “no money!” quite aggressively at you, but what he really means is that the sun beds are free, and he’s actually fine once you sit down on one of his loungers. The bar also has clean toilets and showering facilities too for you to use, which is convenient.If you cycle to the beach, there are several vendors who blow a whistle at you telling you to stop and park your bike before you get the beach. They charge around 10k VND per bike  to park your bike at their stand (but this figure seems variable depending on what they think of you). The whistle is just to get your attention, and you can actually cycle and park for free closer to where all the bar/hotel entrances are near the beach, which we only discovered on our way back from the beach exiting the Dolphin bar.

A great location and easily accessible by taxi (approximately 100,000 Vietnamese Dong from Hoi An city centre). The beach is environmentally friendly, clean, tidy and well maintained. We experienced a relaxed vibe throughout the day, Kukun bar & restaurant (separate review) provided chilled out techno as day turned to dusk.

Plaża ma turystyczne zaplecze (bart, leżaki), do tego jest piaszczysta, szeroka, i - jak na standardy Wietnamu - czysta. Morze z falami. Leżaki płatne, w niektórych miejscach wystarczy zamówić napoje. Dzięki zabudowie, można z widokiem na morze spędzić też deszczowe dni. Minus za męczący handel obnośny.
Nette beach bars und schönes Meer vor Ort - sehr sauber 😉 Schaukeln und Wassersportmöglichkeiten wie Parasailing, Jetski und banana boat on the beach
I've given 4* because the ocean was fierce and I could not swim in it.   Nice authentic restaurants in front of the beach.  Great for relaxation.  Nice assortment of sunbeds available.
We spent two quiet and relaxing days at the beach with mango smoothies and local beer! Waves are just incredible! If you are around definitely worth the visit!
비치는 생각보다 깨끗했고요 주변 레스토랑은 돌아보니까 좀 위생이 안좋은듯했어요 호텔프라이빗 비치에 좀 있다 왔네요
Biển đẹp, víew đẹp, đồ ăn ngon. Người dân khá thân thiện. Đã đên đây với gia đình 1 lần và cảm thấy khá hài lòng. Thích hợp để chụp ảnh kỉ niệm

We came to get out of the heat, and allowed the children to swim. Nice beach, with a slight undercurrent, however safe for children. Taxi was 70 VN from Old Town. Plenty of restaurants and places to drink along the beach.

Great for the kids, warm water and nice sand. You can pay to rent sunbeds but we didn’t. Great to watch the parasailing - no pressure from salesmen to do it. Women come onto the beach to sell you souvenirs.
호이안 리조트 수영장에서 놀다가 지겨워지면 꼭 가봐야할 곳. 해안가 식당들에서 밥먹으면 선베드사용이랑 샤워시설 사용 할수 있으니 바다물놀이 실컷하고 시원한 해풍 맞으면서 밥먹고 사진 찍고하면 괜찮다. 베트남 야외에서는 거의 처음으로 시원하다고 느꼈다. 해안가가 긴편이라 놀기 좋다. 추천

A Nice place to spend a few hours swimming. Bargain for a seat under an umbrella or check with the local restaurant to see if they own chairs. Then they’ll be free if you eat at the restaurant after or during

Took a taxi from our Hoi An Hotel for 100,000VND each way.  We didn't stay long as we just wanted to visit the beach and had had a long day touring.  The water was warm but the day overcast so not so inviting.  Plenty of people enjoying it but not crowded. Nice beach...
Fantastic seafood available. We stayed  under the umbrella for 4 hours and even Tho it was cloudy. We got roasted, be careful.

La playa de An Bang es una visita obligada si pasas unos días en la cuidad de Hoi An. Esta a tan solo 3,5km de la cuidad antigua y merece mucho la pena ir a pasar unas horas y darte un baño. Esta limpia y tiene varios chiringuitos de playa en los cuales poder relajarte y comer o tomar algo.
砂が舞ってるせいか、沖縄の海を想像していたら期待外れだと思います。ビーチ自体はゴミもなく、水も澄んでいました。デッキに寝転がり、スローライフを満喫できます。物売りやパラセーリングの勧誘は来ますが、しつこくありません。店からビールや食事を運んでもらえます。欧米人、韓国、中国人が多い印象です。楽しみ方も国民性が出ていて面白いです。
Nice place to rest a little bit. Many tourists but enough space to lay down and chill. Many bars to eat something or drink fresh coconut.
Clean beach with nice restaurants. There was some good waves so could body surf. A few people trying to sell you stuff but after saying no didn't bother you again.

it is more relax than Da nang bigger beach.there are a lot of chairs to sit on for free.just sit there relax reading, sleeping or swimming, there have shower and toilet area.water not very deep and soft sand.

This is a very clean beach and wasn’t too crowded when we visited. The water is lovely for swimming, if a little rough. The only downside would be that the beach and bars close quite early so you then need to make your way into town if you want to make a day of it.

This was a great way to relax! I rented a bike and went from the ancient town down the road to the beach. It’s very simple, no turns, so just follow the road and look out for motorbikes. You have to park your bike somewhere and there are lots of pushy people that try to get you to use their space, so look out for that. The beachfront people also wheel and deal for prime beach estate with the lounge chairs, but it’s worth it if you want some shade near the beach.The beach itself is clean, and the water is lovely. It drops off fairly steeply, so you don’t need to go too far to really swim. The wind picked up a lot when I was there, and the waves got huge. It might not be ideal for children in that situation because there isn’t a lot of shallow water. Overall, nice beach.

Arrived early to avoid the heat. PARKINGGot the shake down to park the bicycles at parking station. When we arrived there was 1 bicycle,  the people watching the bikes knew exactly which 2 bikes were ours,  even though there was a sea of bikes on our return. In hindsight with the paid parking, the bike is being watched and when you get back on, the vinyl seat is not red hot on your bottom.The 40k per bike parking, can be avoided , as suggested by another traveller, by riding past the parking station and turning right. Apparently down that road/ track you can park your bike for free and get a free deck chair, if you buy a drink.BEACHWe walked directly on from the parking station to the beach. And were greated with an expanse of white sand and a sea of umbrellas and chairs. We were hot and bothered and i was off my game and got stitched up for the deck chairs and paid 100k per chair for the day. The water was beautiful and clean and the water temperature was perfect. The sand was very hot getting to the water. For the perfect photo take a shot from the water looking back at the beach. I didnt because I didnt want to cross the hot sand back to the deck chair for my phone, but trust me.THE BUT'sThe hawkers drove us crazy, 1 after another,  I guess when it's busier there would be more tourists to spread them across,  so it would be less annoying. After laying still for a while the sand flies started attacking, that was the final straw!It was beautiful but I wouldn't  go back to linger without insect repellant.

We went twice here. We stayed in the old town and had a bus service to get there and back. The beach lounges were also part of the deal. The beach is no way to Australian standards so don’t expect that if you are from Australia. We did not swim as it is not as clean as what we are used to having. I still loved it and would go back. I loved the Deckhouse were you can sit down on  a nice outdoor setting for a meal or drink. The music is fantastic.

The beach is a fabulous place to be at, but unfortunately it is very crowded with lounges right next to each other. The cafe/restaurant is large with lots of room for diners, and the menu is great with a lot of variety.

We went to the more quiet part of An Bang Beach 2 times during our stay in Hoi An. The first day, we approached some lounge chairs and the lady told us we needed to order food and drinks for each person (there were 8 of us). We obliged, but the next time we just said we were just ordering drinks. We had kids so, they were playing on the beach and not using chairs. Other than a few people trying to sell us items we didn't want, it was a nice beach. I would go back.

It’s actually a lovely beach early in the morning. Great places to chill all day. I like the laid back vibe. The morning is best. The ocean is calm and there aren’t crowds. It’s absolutely worth spending a relax day here. by late morning the wind picks up and the sea gets choppy.

Nice for a chill, drink coconuts in the water. 150k dong for a couple sun beds. Heaps of places to get a drink and eat. Beautiful view of Cham island. Jet ski and parasailing available. The biggest drawback is the amount of hawkers. You’ll be saying no all day. Some will go on and on like you’re at the market.
Not too over-crowded and relatively clean. We went towards late afternoon, prior to sunset - around 5pm. The sun wasn't so hot at the time so it was enjoyable.
Bãi biển đẹp, sạch và dễ thương. Đang lo ngại sự quá tải. Gần thành phố, người dân thân thiện. Thức ăn ngon rẻ. Có thể ở lâu dài.

Nice beach, commercial and touristy but expected that. You need to pay to park bicycles. Can be a rip. We parked for free 100 m up road at a lovely rustic cafe called the Juice Bar. If you love fresh healthy food and drink I highly recommend. Had a delicious healthy juice and amazing fresh avocado sandwich.
ホテルからのシャトルバスで行きました。色んなホテルからバスが来てて、各ホテルの名前のビーチベッドがズラリとしてます。雨季なのでそんなに混んで無く、でも海も透明度も高く砂浜もゴミもなくキレイですので安心して入れました。
Foi uma surpresa, praia limpa, areia fina e àgua quente. Não tem a beleza das praias da Tailândia mas é um bom destino de praia no Vietname em Agosto.

As far as beaches go in comparison to Australia we have some of the best. You have to go to experience it. You can pay to sit on sun beds or just take your own towel for the sand. There are lifesavers and it looks like there can be some undertow. Not much surf. There are plenty of cafes to sit and watch the crowds. The service and people are lovely. You can ride your bike there. It took us 30 mins.
occorre il protettivo, le spiagge sono lunghissime il mare la zona dove fare il bagno è delimitata per le correnti forti e per le attività che non mancano per divertirsi

If you want to hang out at a busy beach this place has it all food ,cocktails,beer, music and safe beach no shortage of taxis and Purple Lantern restaurant(great food)just around the corner this place is happenin !!!

Voglio premettere che in Vietnam non si va certo per il mare, ma se vogliamo concederci qualche giorno di relax, questa spiaggia offre tutti i comfort, lettini, bagni e docce, servizio bar ristorante, navette che arrivano dagli hotel e taxi.Il mare e la spiaggia sono puliti. Se siete ad Hoi An la consiglio.

Great location. Plenty of water sport options. Loads of small shops. Cycling distance from hoian, you will be charged to park at the many bike parks but it’s generally minimal. Less than 75pThe restaurants lining the entrance to the beach were busy. Would definitely recommend for drinks, the food however was poor. Loads of seafood which is cooked fresh. But the fried rice/noodles dishes were poor. Like bad Chinese take away sweet and sour.
The beaces here are fairky clean the restaurants nearby orpbably detract the ambiance of the olace. But its nice to swim in and deoending in time or day not too busy.

By chance we took the Little Hoian bus from our hotel to An Bang beach. A half of the beach is occupied by the people who do the commercial things (parasailing, place for hiring,...). The bar service is very nice!

I went to this beach on a sunny day in August. The water was super warm. They had a water trampoline to use that was a good time. Be prepared to have vendors harass you while you try to relax. It is worth renting a lounger.

Propre agréable eau claire - Bien sur évitez de vous entasser là où sont les bars de plage et les activités de parachute En allant vers la gauche près du resto de plage de Phuong c plus tranquille Transat gratuit si vs consommez et les « plagistes «  sont très sympas
Nice beach had clear water and marked off sections for swimming and had jet skiing and other water activities would recommend
직원들이 매우 친절하고 서비스가 굉장히 좋았습니다.이틀간 좋은시간 보내고 가네요 ㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣㅣ호이안에 오시면 란타나 추천합니다

We experienced quite a few traders here, one or two of whom weren't always happy to be told "no thank you." BUT you can't fault the beach itself and the water was lovely and warm. Felt more than safe leaving our valuables on the sun lounger, which itself didn't cost much at all. Nice way to spend half a day when staying in Hoi An.

Spiaggia lunga e ampia, mare buono anche se nn è mare tipo thai o Malesia... acqua fresca e pulitissima ... purtroppo ci sono le attrazioni da turismo occidentale come banana boat, acqua scooter,ecc ...Per staccare qualche giorno o ora dalla calura di Hoi An e prendere dell ottimo sole è stato perfetto
A lot of holiday accommodation provide free sun beds with sun shades at the beach. The Salt pub offers free wifi and has good food choices. Very relaxing.

Durante il soggiorno a Hoi An abbiamo frequentato la spiaggia An Bang, ed è stata una bella sorpresa: sabbia chiara e mare limpido, vista sulle isolette cu lao cham. Ogni ristorante con affaccio sulla spiaggia offre anche lettini ed ombrelloni, oltre che buon pesce a buon mercato

Quick, ten minute trip from Hoi An by taxi..around 100000 Dong..beach is filled with sun lounges and eating venues, and very relaxing atmosphere. The water was very refreshing and a great spot away from the hustle of city centre..worth a few hours visit. Plenty of food and drink options right on the beach.

Ce n’est pas une plage de rêve mais elle était propre. Possibilité d’être sur un transat gratuitement si on consomme dans le restaurant choisi. Le seul bémol sont les vendeuses qui n’arrêtent pas de vous solliciter

Las playas de An Beach en Hoi An son unas playas tranquilas por encima de todo. Playas limpias de aguas calientes,tranquilas y con muy poca gente. Para relajarse después del trecho y cansancio acumulado en las montañas del norte de Vietnam en Sapa y Lao Cai.Además para activar adrenalina se pueden realizar actividades movidas de moto de agua, parapente con lancha, etc, Para todos los gustos y ganas.
パラセーリングがしたくて行きました。貸し椅子がたくさん、人出も多く、活気に溢れていました。徒歩で行ける範囲にシーフードレストラン、ベトナム料理店もたくさんあり、とても便利な場所です。

We had a most enjoyable day at An Bang Beach. Water clean, safe, warm etc. Didn’t think we would stay as long as we did. My daughters had a go at the Parrsailing and jet skis which was reasonably priced. The sand gets very hot so you need thongs or shoes to get to the waters edge etc. The only thing that was a bit annoying was the people selling stuff walking up and down the beach.

Only went here the once and although it was relaxing and well kept it was a bit over crowded, suppose that happens when it is popular. Good for a couple of hours to get away from the hustle and bustle.

Jolie plage avec transats ombragés accessibles via location ou consommation. Certains endroits sont vraiment plus paisibles que d’autres. Attention toutefois les jets ski et les parachutes ascensionnels sont en action au bord de l’eau. C’est à vous de vous écarter!! Pas mal de restos avec jolies vues sur la mer accessibles depuis la plage avec des espaces pour chiller. En dehors de cela, encore beaucoup de zones - décharges : poubelles, sacs plastiques, canettes .... dommage, il faudrait s’y mettre pour nettoyer tout cela mais avant tout en prendre déjà conscience.
キレイなビーチを求めてダナンから海岸線を色々行ってみましたが、ここが海の色、店など含め１番リゾート感がありました。透明度は低いけど、ダナンほどどんよりした色ではなく、入って泳ぎたくなる感じはあります。サマーベッドはそれぞれその奥のレストラン(海の家)のもので、何か注文すれば時間の縛りなくずっといてOKのシステム。ごはんはありきたりではありますがそれなりに味は良く、ビールなどもあります。
We cycled from our hotel in Hoi An over to An Bang beach and spent a few nice hours here. Water incredibly warm but beach not the cleanest to be honest so was slightly disappointed.

We enjoyed a couple of great days on this beach. The water is warm with enough waves for enjoyment. There are water sports, paragliding, banana boats, jet skis etc. There are plenty of sun beds and refreshments available. Also there are many cafes selling local food. Beware of the hawkers trying to sell sunglasses, jewellery etc., but this is common. I only stopped short of giving this 5 stars because of a little bit of rubbish (cigarette ends not picked up). A highly recommend beach- enjoy.

Très belle grande plage, où des transats sont à votre disposition pour 60K dong (2€) ou gratuit si vous prenez une consommation qui sont relativement pas cher par rapport à la France (1/2 litres de Mojito... 4€
Sin embargo no es tailandia ni ningun otro pais asiatico con playas paradisíacas. De agua caliente. Hay tumbonas y parking de pago 5000 VDN

La plage est superbe .Le matin 5h00 les vietnamiens sont la pour le levé du soleil (unique) le soir c est le repas en famille sur le sable .En journée, chacun fait ce qui lui plaît....Bref,à goûter sans modération.

No es una playa paradisiaca con aguas cristalinas, simplemente es una playa para darse algún chapuzón, tomar el sol y como optamos alquilar unas hamacas, beber unas cervecitas y disfrutar del día... y a un precio barato

Он находится примерно в 10-15 минутах езды от старого города и считается одним из лучших пляжей в этом районе. Пляж широкий, с мягким песком и длинной береговой линией. Здесь довольно спокойно и нет ощущения переполненного курорта. Вдоль берега расположено много уютных кафе и ресторанов, где можно поесть или просто выпить кофе с видом на море. Кстати, у многих кафе есть лежак и зонтик, и часто ими можно пользоваться бесплатно, если заказать что-то из еды или напитков. Место отлично подходит для расслабленного отдыха: можно гулять вдоль воды, купаться или просто сидеть в кафе и смотреть на океан. А вечером здесь особенно красиво - многие приходят встречать закат у моря.
Quiet and quaint. Very laid back vive
Đây là một bãi biển thư giãn tuyệt vời với làn nước trong vắt. Bạn nên đến sớm, vì các nhà hàng ven biển bắt đầu đông nghẹt từ 4 giờ chiều. Tiếc là không ngắm được hoàng hôn.

While spending time in Hoi An, I was looking for snus Hoi An options and came across this small but well-organized shop. As an expat, it’s not always easy to find Scandinavian-style products here, so this place was a pleasant surprise.The shop is clean, calm, and easy to browse. The staff were friendly and took time to explain different nicotine pouch options without pushing a sale. If you’re searching for a snus shop in Hoi An that feels relaxed and trustworthy, this is worth a visit.I’d recommend it to travelers or expats who prefer tobacco-free alternatives while staying in the city.
Hi everyone—October’s storm caused severe damage here. The ocean is now murky and not especially scenic, but the local food is absolutely outstanding.

Clean, beautiful stretch of beach. Warm water, soft sand, and calm waves perfect for the whole family. Plenty of good restaurants lined along the beach, if you order food from them then you dont have to pay the lounger and umbrella.We are lucky that we are staying just a good 5 minutes walk away from the beach. During our stay, we get to do our early morning walk and swim in the morning.

A pristine, picturesque beach with warm water, powdery sand, and gentle waves. Numerous great restaurants line the shore, and if you order from them, you get complimentary use of loungers and umbrellas.

We ditched the crowds for An Bang and scored a quiet stretch of soft sand, warm water, and cold coconuts. Not much going on—just sunbathing, swimming, and lazy chats. Exactly the reset our friend group needed.

An Bang Beach in Hoi An was, honestly, one of my favorite places during my trip. The sand was very soft and clean, and the water warm and calm. There were few people, which made it even better to relax and enjoy the sound of the waves.

An Bang Beach is a short Grab ride from Hoi An and a great place to just relax for the day. Ladies are ushering you to their beach loungers for a price, but you get them for the whole day. We ordered food from the lady who got us our beach lounger, and it was great, fresh and very reasonably priced. The water is very warm, but be aware that there can be a bit of rubbish floating in the water. We had a nice, relaxing day and would recommend putting it on your list of things to do when in Hoi An.

The beach is incredibly beautiful with white, fine sand. The beachside kiosks have unique designs and offer very special dishes, suitable for the taste of the sea. The space is airy, cool, and especially the coconut juice is very refreshing when drink cold on hot days like this in Hoi An.
An excellent choice for a weekend trip or a longer stay, featuring swimming, sunbathing, and the chance to enjoy the distinctive culinary offerings of An Bang beach.

It’s only a 10-minute taxi ride from the city, but the water there is clearer than I expected—crystal blue and so refreshing. The beach is quiet, clean, and not overcrowded, making it a perfect escape from the urban hustle. We enjoyed swimming, sunbathing, and even a quick seafood snack from a local vendor. Such a pleasant surprise so close to the city! Definitely worth a half-day trip.

An Bang is the closest beach to Hoi An Ancient Town, so it might be the only one you visit while you're here. It's close enough that you can easily head back for a lantern-lit dinner, but far enough to give you a real beach escape vibe. Whether you're there for a few hours or a few days, An Bang Beach offers that slow, coastal magic that's hard to leave behind!

Beautiful serene beach with friendly beach staff. Food is good. No loud music banging so you can hear the ocean. Visited a few days in a row. Despite showering after each swim im covered in sea lice bites all over my body 😕
We found a quiet stretch with soft sand, and the kids splashed safely in the calm waves. Simple beach shacks served cold drinks and grilled corn, and the views at sunset were pure magic.

The fine sand, the sound of the waves, and a cocktail in hand... An Bang was our haven. We loved the relaxed atmosphere of the small beach restaurants. It was the perfect place to relax and just enjoy being together.

Anbang Beach is a short distance from the old town.Leaving the hustle and bustle of tourist destinations, we were able to spend some time relaxing in the blue ocean, white sandy beaches and pleasant breeze.It was easy to get there with a free shuttle at the Little Oasis Hotel where I was staying. It was a lovely beach that was quieter than I had imagined and had a resort feel.Beachfront restaurants and cafes are also stylish, offering delicious seafood and cocktails. It is very recommended for those who want to relax.

We came for the sun and sand, but stayed for the surprisingly good cocktails at a little beach shack. The vibe is super relaxed, almost to the point where we forgot what day it was. It wasn't the most pristine beach we've ever seen, but it had a charm (and a happy hour) that was hard to leave.

An Bang Beach: Hoi An's Coastal GemAn Bang Beach is a clear, beautiful stretch of sand, truly a highlight of Hoi An. It's incredibly clean and offers a refreshing escape from the ancient town's bustle. Perfect for a relaxing day by the sea, enjoying the gentle waves and serene atmosphere. A must-visit coastal spot when in Hoi An!

Best beach in Da Nang / Hoi An region! Great beach,ggood swimming, and not overcrowded.If you want a sunbed, you can get one. Parasailing, banana boating, all available. Street food vendors on the beach, resturants and bars aplenty, but so relaxed! We loved it.
It was the perfect place to just relax and do nothing. The vibe is super chill, and the beach wasn't too crowded.A great little break from all the sightseeing.

The beach was clean, with soft sand and gentle waves. We rented a couple of loungers and just relaxed, enjoying the peaceful atmosphere and the delicious seafood from a nearby restaurant. Highly recommended for a chill day.

We came to An Bang Beach with no agenda other than to relax, and it delivered completely. We just lounged on the sand, sipped on cheap, cold beers, and watched the waves roll in. It was the perfect, lazy beach day for us to just unwind and enjoy the simple life together.

It was the perfect romantic escape with its soft white sand and clear, calm waters. We spent our days lounging under an umbrella, sipping cocktails from the nearby bars, and just enjoying the serene, laid-back atmosphere.

Lovely beach with great restaurants and bars ! The beach peddlers are fun and great to talk to and ideal place to get your souvenirs from.The sea is refreshing and safe to go into !Can’t wait to go back 👍

Great beach, white sand and gentle sea! There are enough beds for everyone, 10-15 people per kilometer of beach! Restaurants on the beach for every taste, from the most budget with local cuisine to the most expensive with European. Every restaurant has a toilet and shower. You have to understand that if you want to find the downsides, you can do it: there are drops, cigarette butts and garbage, but not in a way that interferes with rest.

This beach is definitely worth visiting if you are in Hoi An and your hotel does not have a private beach. At the time of our holiday from June 3 to 11, 2025, the Koi Resort beach was undergoing renovations, so we had to find other options. The water is very clean, clear, even cool in the morning and swimming in it is a pleasure, especially since there were practically no waves. The descent is comfortable, the beach is sandy, the sand is light. The sunbeds are offered from local quaffeche, they are quite comfortable, the umbrellas are large and form enough shade. We got here at 8:00 a.m., it was pretty desolate, but with the sun rising early here, it's better to get there early. The cost of sunbeds and drinks is given below. A one-way taxi cost 40 thousand dong, two sunbeds and an umbrella for the whole day - 150 thousand, 2 cold coffees (45 each) and 2 frèches (50 each) in 190. Running around, one trip to the beach cost us 420 dong or 16 dollars.

The soft sand at An Bang was a hit with the kids – they were covered! We grabbed some fresh seafood from a little shack; it tasted like, well, seafood. The water was...watery. Decent spot for a family beach day if you're in Hoi An.
Super sunny, as advertised! The beach is just a sea of colorful umbrellas – you can't miss 'em. Sand felt pretty pristine, which was a plus for our solitary wanderings.
We used an bang beach as our base and loved it there the beach is great Plenty of restaurants bars Hoi an is not far Da Nang about 30 mins awayI would definitely recommend a visit to An Bang

We rented sunbeds at 100k each at La Plage but if you eat there then the cost is taken off the bill. Beach is lovely and wasn't very busy. The sea was nice with small waves. This was a wonderful spot to relax at the end of our vacation

Great place to chill out.An Bang Beach is clean and safe for swimming. During our visit in April, it wasn’t particularly busy and had a nice, chilled-out vibe. Sun loungers are available along the beach and are nicely spaced out. You can order reasonably priced food and drinks to be delivered to you on the beach or enjoy them at the various beach bars. Basic toilet facilities are available.In the evening, there are plenty of bars and restaurants to choose from nearby, or it’s a short taxi ride to Hoi An.

Beautiful white sand beach, clean in most part around the An Bang center. We were sceptical because of some reviews and ended up coming a second day since the beach is so nice and the sea perfect to swim at high and low tides. Highly recommend renting chairs at Soul Kitchen where the service was impeccable.

For a tranquil experience, ask your driver to take you to Hidden Beach (there is a restaurant by this name, and several other cafe's nearby) -- all are beachfront and lovely.It's a chill vibe with easy access to the ocean, as well as food and drinks.If you use the blue cushion beach chairs, there is a 50VD charge or buy a drink and it's free!We loved our time here - arriving early and leaving at noon when it was busier but still not busy like An Bang.

Quite the experience. Positives include the soft, sandy beach, wonderful views of the fishing vessels and their basket boats, beachside cafes, restaurants and of course the somewhat calm, clear blue waters. Negatives are the extremely aggressive vendors, their prices, heat and the crowds. Positives far outweigh the negatives in the special beach.

There is a feeling that it is more filled with Westerners than Mieke beach, but it is a beautiful beach It is well maintained, there are many kiosks, it was good exotic atmosphereThe nearby seafood porridge shop was excellent!
Excellent food and service, right on the beach. Heaps of table and chairs. You can hire and book a cabana for minimum spend of VND 1,000,000
Một bãi biển đặc biệt, chúng tôi rất thích tới đây vào các dịp hè. Biển An Bàng luôn mang đến cảm giác thư giãn, thanh bình nhưng vẫn rất đầy đủ các tiện ích từ nhà hàng hải sản, quán bar, spa…

I visited this area in March. The locals said that the wet season this year lasted longer than expected, so the beach wasn’t as nice as usual. However, I still found it enjoyable and had a great time.
Ystävämme vuosien takaa. Täällä on hyvä palvelu. Taas saimme hyvää palvelua tuulisena päivänä.Kahvi ja jäätelö maistui. 😊🥰

Our family was fascinated by the beautiful sand rivers and the steady waves. At the beach restaurant we had a loose time enjoying fresh seafood and local food. In particular, the landscape of the Sunflower remains an unforgettable memory. Perfect for family trips, this place offers comfort and pleasure at the same time.
The beach was so nice and Bree they do have the congee soup claim was so delicious . Recommend come about March till September

Spent two days at the beach and loved it. Plenty of sun beds and unlike some other reviews we had no problem with people trying to cheat you out of money . Our beds were 50,000 each for the day including an umbrella .I mention the lack of sun protection offered by the umbrellas as we got caught out one day .

An Bang Beach is a beautiful and peaceful destination near Hoi An, Vietnam. It features soft white sand, clear blue water, and a relaxed atmosphere. The beach is less crowded than other tourist spots, making it perfect for a quiet getaway. There are plenty of beachfront cafes and restaurants offering delicious seafood and refreshing drinks. It’s an ideal place to swim, sunbathe, or simply enjoy the scenic view. Overall, An Bang Beach is a great spot for relaxation and enjoying nature.
Very large beautiful sandy beach, clean, the water is warm with beautiful waves, swimming is excellent, there are lifeguards, safe, there are plenty of restaurants on the edge

An Bang Beach in Hoi An is a hidden gem along Vietnam’s central coastline. Located just a short drive from the UNESCO-listed town of Hoi An, it offers a more peaceful, relaxed alternative to the bustling tourist spots. The beach is beautifully clean, with soft golden sand and gentle waves, making it perfect for a day of relaxation or a swim. The clear waters, while not as pristine as some of Vietnam’s more remote beaches, are still inviting.The charm of An Bang lies in its laid-back atmosphere. Unlike some of the more crowded beaches, it’s not overly commercialized. A handful of beachfront cafes and restaurants offer fresh seafood and local dishes, many of which are affordable and delicious. You can enjoy a cold drink under a bamboo hut while watching the local fishermen at work.The area is also known for its beautiful sunrises, making it ideal for early risers. Plus, it’s a great spot for a bike ride from the town of Hoi An, which adds a scenic and active element to your visit. For those looking for more than just a beach day, An Bang offers a peaceful vibe, stunning scenery, and an escape from the tourist-heavy areas of Vietnam. It’s a fantastic place to unwind and soak in the natural beauty of the coast.

The chill vibes made it easy to unwind think soft sand, gentle waves, and cozy beach loungers. By day, we soaked up the sun; by evening, the vibrant atmosphere came alive with twinkling lights, live music, and beachfront cocktails. The mix of relaxation and energy was just right for a couple’s getaway. We left with sandy toes and full hearts!

Site 5 kms from Hoi An very beautiful beach where it is good to come for a walk , run or come to taste fish or seafood . There are many restaurants , guest house or apartment for rent ... to choose from. Located on the other side of Da Nang you can see the great hotels of this city from the beach. Place to discover absolutely.

The sky turns into a canvas of soft pastels, making our morning coffee even more special. Just us, the sand, and the most beautiful sunrises. It's like nature's own romantic show, and we were the only audience. A serene start to our days in Hoi An.

Eating delicious at the beach restaurant, walking in the sea wind, there was no paradise apart? The two are holding hands and hearing the waves walking, completely romantic! It's quiet and peaceful, so I feel comfortable. Next we'll have to dig out here all day!

An Bang! 🏖️ We loved it! Romantic beach walks, hand-in-hand, were pure bliss. The sand was soft, the sea a gentle turquoise. And those colorful umbrellas! 🌈 So vibrant, like a painting. Perfect for lazy days and sunset gazing. ✨ A truly dreamy escape for us two. Highly recommend! 🥰

So sunny and chill. We loved the beach huts, perfect for relaxing. The calm seas were amazing for a swim. It was exactly what we needed – a laid-back beach day. Definitely a great spot for couples looking to unwind. We spent hours just soaking up the sun. We'll be back for sure!

The soft sand and the blue sea comforted our hearts. We had a relaxing time enjoying a cocktail at the beach bar, and the sunset was fantastic. This place with your loved one is the place of a true heling. I really want to come back!

Unlike the crowded beaches elsewhere, An Bang offered us the perfect escape. Clean sand, gentle waves, and surprisingly few tourists made our beach day perfect. The local seafood restaurants are a must-try! We spent hours just lounging under the palm-thatched umbrellas, watching local fishermen with their traditional boats.

Bathroom beach was just a nice beach. There were plenty of beach restaurants. The beach was clean. It wasn't bad to play water, but it wasn't so good to say. It was just a good place to relax. It was nice to have a beer on the beach.

The soothing ocean breeze wrapped around us as we strolled hand in hand along the shoreline. The vibrant beach culture buzzed with energy, from locals selling fresh seafood to laughter echoing from nearby cafes. We soaked in the picturesque views, with golden sands and swaying palms framing the horizon. Each sunset painted the sky in hues we’ll never forget. We can't wait to return!

Seriously, we almost didn't find it. The gentle waves were perfect for a relaxing swim, and the sand was so soft. But the stunning sunsets? Absolutely unforgettable. We just sat there, mesmerized. It was the perfect escape. We're already planning our return!

The stunning sunsets painted the sky in shades we didn't even know existed. The beach's gentle waves and soft sand made for perfect evening strolls. Less crowded than nearby beaches, An Bang felt like our own slice of paradise.

The waves were gentle enough for a relaxing swim, though the beach isn't exactly pristine. Found a decent spot at one of the beachfront restaurants - average food but nice views.Less crowded than we expected, which was nice. The sea was calm and perfect for floating around. Beach chairs cost a bit if you're not ordering food, but that's tourist life! 🌊Decent spot for a beach day, but don't set expectations too high. Bring sunscreen - the umbrellas are hit or miss.

An Bang Beach was our go-to for, you guessed it, sunset cocktails!  The sand is soft, the water is calm (most days!), and the sunsets are seriously impressive. We spent a few evenings here, soaking up the vibrant atmosphere and enjoying the cheap drinks. It's definitely a bit touristy, but hey, aren't we all? 😉

We spent two mornings here. Sunrises are pretty, but you have to arrive early to avoid the crowds. Beach restaurants are nice but a little expensive for Vietnam. Street vendors can be insistent. The sand is clean, the water correct. Take a scooter from Hoi An.
We went there through the shuttle that our hotel la siesta provides. We stayed at the beach club looking at the sea and the sky.It was occasionally raining but we managed to have a good time

We lounged under swaying palm trees, sipping fresh coconut water. The laid-back vibe was just what we needed. Watching the sunset together was magical, painting the sky in hues of orange and pink. A perfect beach 🏖

We found this beach to be a perfect escape from Hoi An's bustling streets. The crystal-clear waters and pristine sandy shores provided an idyllic setting for our romantic beach day. The beachfront restaurants offered excellent seafood, and the sun loungers were reasonably priced.

Soft sand and blue sea welcomed us and spent a relaxing time in a comfortable atmosphere. We watched the sunset together on the beach, and once again felt the value of each other. If you need a real rest, I highly recommend!!!!!!!💕

What a pleasant surprise! An Bang Beach offered us a perfect respite from the hustle and bustle of Hoi An. The water was warm, the sand soft, and the beach restaurants delicious. We loved watching the sunset while sipping cocktails. This is a great place to relax.

Away from Hoi An's bustling streets, this pristine stretch of sand offered the ideal mix of relaxation and local charm. The beachfront restaurants served amazing seafood, and watching the sunset from our loungers was magical. The gentle waves and friendly locals made this our favorite beach day in Vietnam.
