Comment: A good beach ⛱️ to swim in Hội An in the summer. From September to December , you should not swim here because of the rainy season and storms

Restaurant, cafe, long beach and activities are all good.It's only heavily influenced by the weather. On the contrary, great. You can win waves.In the cafeteria you can enjoy the cool wind with Ocean View.

We had a perfect day in Hoyan's jewelry bathroom beach! The soft sand that swallowed the feet was like a puffy cloth. Beach massage was not a paradise. Make it a secret that I was slightly asleep while my wife was being massageed! I would recommend tourists to stay out of Hoi An and spend a romantic time here.

The beach is not as crowded as other attractions and the sand is delicate and white. We rented beach chairs, basked in the sun and listened to the waves. In the evening, we enjoyed fresh seafood at a small restaurant by the sea and watched the sunset slowly fall to sea level. The atmosphere is perfect for couples and the staff are friendly. Definitely worth recommending for couples who want to get away from the hustle and bustle.

We found our slice of paradise at An Bang Beach, away from Hoi An's bustling streets. The gentle waves provided a perfect soundtrack as we strolled hand in hand along the shoreline. Local seafood restaurants became our daily ritual, and the friendly fishermen's morning routines added authentic charm. Perfect for couples seeking both relaxation and local culture.

The calm seas were perfect for our lazy swimming sessions, and the chill vibes had us feeling like local beach bums in no time. Grabbed some cold beers, snagged comfy loungers, and just watched the world float by.Zero stress, maximum relaxation - exactly what we needed from our beach escape.

The "tranquil" atmosphere was shattered by a gang of overzealous sandcastle builders. The "calm seas" turned into a splash zone, soaking our picnic. But hey, nothing says love like sharing a soggy sandwich! Despite the chaos, we laughed our way through it. An Bang Beach: where romance meets reality TV!

As a couple, we were drawn to the laid-back atmosphere. The beachside bar provides the perfect place to relax and we enjoy delicious drinks while enjoying the waves tapping the sand. The atmosphere allows us to relax completely and stay away from the hustle and bustle of the city. Ampang Beach is our highly Recommended destination for couples looking to enjoy a quiet and relaxing time.
Beautiful sandy beach. Shallow sea with permanent big waves 🌊 more clean and better than My Khe Beach.  I love the peace of having a beer while listening to the cool breeze and sound of the waves.

We recently visited the dormitory beach of Hoi An and were impressed by the quietness, soft sand and rugged sea. The beach is well managed, perfect for walking, and ideal for spending time with friends. The steady sound of the ocean comforts the mind and removes stress from everyday life. Bathroom beach is a great Recommendation for those looking for a quiet relaxation.

The sandy stretch was perfect for our lazy couple's getaway. Gentle waves provided a soothing soundtrack as we hopped between beachside bars, sipping cocktails and soaking up the laid-back vibe. It's the ideal spot to unwind and forget about real life for a while.
Nice relaxed beach.Soft sand and calm atmosphere.Very hot day when I visited, remember to use suntan lotion. Restaurants nearby with food and drinks.About 10 mins drive from main town area.

The well-lined colorful umbrellas on the beach not only provide a comfortable shade, but also add a unique aesthetic to the beach.It's worth mentioning that the fresh seafood here is of excellent quality. We tasted a variety of local specialities, fresh taste and authentic cooking. The restaurants around the beach are professionally serviced and the sanitation is satisfactory.Ampang Beach is well-infrastructured and suitable for all types of tourists. Whether you're looking for peace or enjoying water activities, there are different needs to be met. Overall, Ampang Beach is a Recommended tourist destination, especially for sharing leisurely time with friends.

Beautiful sunrises that'll make you question if you're still dreaming, and seas so calm you could practically ice skate on them. We attempted yoga on the beach, but ended up looking like pretzels with sunburn. The relaxing vibes were so strong, we almost forgot to leave!

Come and play with friends, I heard that Ampang Beach is good, so I came to experience a "lying flat" holiday! 😂 This beach is really famous, the water is clear and calm, not to worry about being overturned by the waves, it is a "dry duck" gospel! The beach was also delicate and soft, it was comfortable to step on, we simply took off our shoes, enjoy the sun, sand and waves! 😎 There were not many people, you can lie quietly on the beach chair, blowing the sea breeze, listening to the waves, feel the whole person relax, it was so healing! Highly Recommended for those who want to relax and enjoy a quiet holiday!

The sand is so soft you almost float and the sea breeze has blown away all our worries.  For hours we lay like stranded whales on the beach, enjoyed the sun and just did nothing. Perfect for a couples holiday full of relaxation and fun sandcastle building sessions!

An Bang offered a vibrant yet relaxed atmosphere. We strolled along the sandy shore, a soothing ocean breeze constantly at our backs.  Plentiful sunshine meant perfect beach days, whether swimming, sunbathing, or enjoying fresh seafood at a beachfront restaurant.  An Bang provided the ideal balance of energy and tranquility for our couple's getaway.

We had the pleasure of visiting An Bang Beach, a stunning stretch of sandy coastline in Hoi An. We appreciated the tranquil atmosphere and picturesque scenery, particularly during the beautiful sunrises. The beach is well-maintained, with plenty of amenities and activities available. We enjoyed leisurely strolls, swimming, and relaxing in the sun.

This beautiful stretch of sand had us grinning like kids with ice cream. We attempted paddleboarding (spoiler: we're not quitting our day jobs) and got more salt water up our noses than fish. But hey, that's what memories are made of, right? Nice beach, nicer laughs. An Bang, you're a bang-up good time!

The pristine, clean beach offered a perfect retreat from the hustle of Hoi An. We were captivated by the clear waters, inviting us for a swim under the sun. The tranquil atmosphere allowed us to relax and unwind, making it an ideal spot for couples seeking a peaceful getaway. We enjoyed leisurely strolls along the shore and indulged in delicious local seafood at nearby cafes. An Bang Beach truly embodies the beauty of Vietnams coast, leaving us eager to return.

The soft sand was perfect for our dramatic slow-motion runs... until we face-planted. Oops! We rented a beach hut, feeling fancy until a crab decided to be our roommate. Talk about unexpected company! The fresh seafood was to die for, literally - we nearly choked laughing at each other's attempts to crack open crabs. Who knew a relaxing beach day could be such a workout? An Bang Beach: where couples come for the views and stay for the giggles!

Wow! We hit the jackpot with An Bang Beach in Hoi An! This slice of paradise had us grinning from ear to ear. The moment our toes touched the soft sand, we knew we were in for a treat. The chill vibes were off the charts  perfect for us lovebirds looking to unwind.But the real showstopper? The beachfront restaurants! We indulged in fresh seafood while gazing at the sparkling ocean. Talk about romantic! The sound of waves crashing mixed with the clinking of our cocktail glasses  pure bliss.We spent our days alternating between lazy sunbathing and refreshing dips in the crystal-clear water. The beach wasn't overcrowded, giving us plenty of space to spread out and soak up the sun.An Bang Beach stole our hearts. It's the perfect escape from the bustling streets of Hoi An. If you're looking for a beach day that ticks all the boxes  this is it!

We spent a tranquil morning at An Bang Beach, enjoying the soothing ocean breeze and witnessing breathtaking beautiful sunrises. The beach is pristine and peaceful, with plenty of loungers and umbrellas for relaxation. A perfect spot for couples seeking a calming getaway from Hoi An's bustling streets. Come early to catch the sunrise – it's truly unforgettable!
The soft sand and gentle waves were ideal for leisurely strolls, and the sunrise views were simply breathtaking.  For couples seeking a tranquil escape, An Bang Beach is highly recommended.

An Bang Beach was perfect for a romantic escape.  The sand was so clean we didn't even find one stray shell to make a cheesy "I love you" message.  But hey, more time for soaking up the sun and enjoying the view... which included each other!
This beach was the perfect escape from reality. Soft sand, clear blue water, and endless chill vibes. We spent our days swimming, sunbathing, and sipping on fresh coconuts. Work who?

An Bang Beach is a paradise for lovers looking for tranquility (and a perfect tan!). We spent hours lounging on the fine sand, lulled by the sound of waves.  Between two swims, we looked at each other with fried whiting eyes...  Sure, An Bang Beach is our little romantic secret in Hoi An!

We came, we saw, we got ridiculously sun-kissed. An Bang beach is definitely one of Vietnam's best! Picture this: soft, white sand that's somehow always clean (magic?), crystal-clear water, and sunsets that'll make you want to write bad poetry. We're talking holding-hands-on-a-romantic-stroll level beautiful. Bring your swimsuit and your A-game for epic sandcastle building - we may or may not have gotten competitive.

The long sandy beach invites you to long walks, while the gentle sound of the waves creates a calm and peaceful atmosphere.  We enjoyed the time together at sunsets that bathed the sky in warm colors. An Bang is ideal for escaping the hustle and bustle of Hoi An and relaxing.

This beautiful stretch of coastline is perfect for romantic beach walks, with soft sand and crystal-clear waters. We spent our days swimming, sunbathing, and enjoying delicious seafood at the beachfront restaurants. But the highlight was definitely the stunning sunsets - pure magic!
This tranquil beach boasts soft sand and crystal-clear water, perfect for swimming and sandcastle. Friendly locals added to the chill vibes, making us feel truly at ease. Highly recommend!!!

The Beach is truly beautiful. Very easy access to many restaurants and bars for food and drink.It was surprisingly quiet between 10am and 12am before slowly getting busier into the Arvo. Never too busy though. There was also some local dogs that came over for some pats who were beautiful! They never intruded though and wandered off to do their own thing after the scratches were over.Overall enjoyable and pretty.
Một trong những bãi biển mình thích nhất :)) Cát trắng mịn, sạch sẽ, nước biển trong veo, nhìn thấy cả cát và cá bên dưới. Ngày nắng nóng được tắm ở đây đúng là như thiên đường.

Great beach, anything to get a break from the heat.  But we loved it.  Did not hire one of the many deck chairs and umbrellas as we wanted to swim and walk.  Keeping our jandals on at all times except in the water as the sand was literally burning our feet.  Had a few swims.  The water is deceptively rough.  Does not look it but I was wiped out a few times.  Went at different times of the day and night.  Saw the surfers.  Saw Da Nang lights at night, along with the fishing boats.  Saw the bobble boats out there as well.  Really enjoyed this beach and walked up and saw the old concrete building there - not sure what it used to be.  Good time.
What an amazing beach - pay for a deckchair for a couple of hours, then ask for the menu. Food and drinks are wonderful. And the beach itself is divine.

We came here because of wonderbot s review and we are so happy we did. Quieter part of the beach and great service and food. Yes we were probably scammed by nana selling us bracelets, but she was so nice who could refuse. Lovely experience.

A Bang Beach is a lovely spot at any time; early morning right thru the day. For a cool dip in the ocean go before 7am. Afternoons swim might need a skin protection from bities in the water, I use citronella oil. Always use 50+ for sun protection. Restaurant called SEA AND SAND run by local family, short walk towards DaNang next to Shore Club. Buy a drink and use the chairs and then ask for food menu. We’ve eaten there numerous times and it is cheap and very good. Seating available, fresh water shower, no toilet. Ting is the man running it and he is friendly and very accommodating, we highly recommend a visit.

The beach is very clean and beautiful, the water is clear and you can see everything 🐡 🐟 many restaurants close to the beach, with lounge chairs. The sea water in this area is a bit cold, so bathing is best done at noon

The sea is beautiful, the coastline is long enough to play on the beach with your family. My baby loves it very much. The restaurants right on the beach are also comfortable and convenient for eating. We will stop by again next time we come to Hoi An.
The beach 🏖 is very beautiful 😍 and clean. The sea water is clear and you can clearly see the fish swimming. Many restaurants and bars where you can enjoy drinks.🍹

The beach is known for its chill vibes, and we certainly felt them! Colorful umbrellas dotted the sand, coconut trees provided shade, and the calm water was perfect for swimming. It's a great place to relax and soak up the sun.

We have been here 6 nights and are going to extend.  Stunning beach, beautiful location, friendly people and a fantastic spot for swimming, with lots of fantastic restaurants,  morning market and local shops.

Fabulous beach, busy and lots of hassle from beach bars in the centre. We walked 30 minutes to the right along to Coconut Beach. Soft white sand, blue sea and much quieter. There are beach bars but smaller and more spread out. We paid £2 for 2 beds with cushions and a brolly for the whole day. Enjoyed plenty of inexpensive drinks served to our beach beds.
The beach is very clean, the water is clear but too cold to swim until you have to go ashore. The water below my feet is as cold as ice cream.Probably have to come back this summer.

A wonderful beach very close to Hoi Ann. A super one-day getaway and you can very affordably rent out the beach loungers and umbrellas if you ordered very reasonably priced and good food from the many beachfront restaurants.
The beach is very beautiful, white sand and especially very clean. Many bars close to the beach. Clear water. However, the water was quite cold, so I had to go ashore after a while.
The sea is beautiful, the sand is smooth. The air here is very fresh, suitable for vacationing and hanging out with friends. The people here are also very friendly

Lovely beach.  We entered through the Soul Kitchen to the left as you come through the public entrance.  You can get a bed for the day if you have drinks or lunch.  Lovely people.  We also hired boogie boards from a wee place to the right of soul kitchen (not on the beach) 150,000 for two hours very cheap. The sellers do approach but leave if you don’t engage.  The second day we were there they never approached us at all. As we had been asked already. There are plenty of taxis outside the beach to get back to your accommodation.

My hotel in Hoi An Rivertown Hotel & Spa has an associated place on A Bang Beach called the Beach club (or similar). You can have complimentary use of the comfortable beach beds and umbrellas. I took my own towels from the hotel but I assume they may provide them if you forget!Brilliant beach, warm and sunny when I was there - I did read there could be riptides so dont go out too far or let children or adults enter alone or without good and close observation and supervision. There are a number of good lunch eateries including the Beach which may appear more expensive than central Hoi An prices, but compared with Western European prices they are still great value for money and in a brilliant and thoroughly enjoyable relaxed environment.If you are in the same hotel as I was you may find a free shuttle bus to get there - it’s only about 15mins. Of course take the usual stuff - bottle/s of water to have something as necessary to begin with.Suncream particularly with hign UV protection - spf50 recommended as with all beach temperatures/sun intensity at beaches with cooling breezes is considerably deceptive. and to remember to regularly reapply particularly if you enter the sea/ it at the beach got a few hours. Sorry lots of precautions but it’s a wonderful beach and got me it was a great and enjoyiabke days from the touristy crowds I’m not a medical practitioner but just applying common sense from experience.Have a great time 🥰Brian T

Very nice beach, a few waves. Very pleasant swimming in April. Restaurant and deckchairs available. Fine sand. Clean beach. Large beach for walks with your feet in the water on fine sand. Lots of Westerners, from 4 p.m. the locals arrive very friendly! Beach food is taking over, I loved this atmosphere!

An Bang Beach provides a welcome escape from the bustle of Hoi An's Ancient Town.  The wide, sandy beach and gentle waves create an instantly relaxing atmosphere.  We loved feeling the soothing ocean breeze as we strolled along the shore, admiring the traditional fishing boats dotting the horizon.The beachfront restaurants were a highlight, offering delicious seafood dishes and refreshing drinks with ocean views.  Locals and tourists alike mingle at these casual establishments, contributing to An Bang's vibrant beach culture.  The friendly vendors and warm smiles from the locals added to the welcoming ambiance.Whether seeking a quiet spot to sunbathe, a place to savor fresh seafood, or just to immerse yourself in the coastal life of Hoi An, An Bang Beach offers a delightful and authentic experience.

Very nice beach. We went there by bike rented from our hotel. When you arrive at the beach, you can leave your bike for €2 per day.Not too crowded in the morning, you can reserve 2 deckchairs and parasol for €6 for the whole day. Fresh fruit juices are delicious and very inexpensive. Like everywhere in Vietnam, people are very kind.From 5 p.m. a lot of people arrive.

An Bang Beach offers a refreshingly relaxed atmosphere compared to some of the more crowded beaches near Hoi An.  Its clean, well-maintained shoreline and relatively shallow waters make it an excellent choice for swimming and sunbathing.  We found the beachside restaurants offered delicious local seafood and refreshing beverages, perfect for enjoying the ocean views.The consistent winds attract windsurfers and kiteboarders, adding visual interest and a sense of activity without disrupting the beach's peaceful vibes. An Bang Beach strikes a delightful balance between relaxation and active possibilities.  Whether seeking quiet sunbathing, water sports, or fresh seafood by the shore, An Bang Beach delivers a charming coastal experience.

Find 3 ladies sun loungers and support their business,  Located in a quieter area on the beach, 3 ladies offer free sunbeds as long as you buy a drink from them. The drinks are the same as everywhere else, super cheap and they offer freshly cooked food.I read on a previous post how previously these 3 poor fisherman's wives were gifted 150 dollars from a tourist to start up this business, and since then various other tourists have supported them by helping to write menus etc.Please support these enterprising ladies, their husbands catch the fish and they cook it fresh for you whilst you relax on the beach, the ladies are lovely and friendly.

Think soft white sand, water so clear you can see your toes, and just enough gentle waves for a perfect dip. We spent whole days swimming, sunning ourselves on lounge chairs, and sipping cocktails at the cool beachfront cafes. The vibe is relaxed, not too crowded, and way more local than some of the other beaches. Vendors strolled by with delicious snacks, but weren't pushy at all.  Sunsets here?  Unbelievable. We even rented paddleboards one day, which was hilarious (and a great workout!). Honestly, if you want that perfect blend of pristine beach and chilled-out atmosphere, An Bang is a must-visit.

My partner and I loved the vibe at An Bang Beach. It's the perfect mix of chill relaxation and lively energy. The beach itself is beautiful – perfect for swimming and sunbathing.  We spent our days hopping between the awesome beach bars, munching on fresh seafood, and sipping cocktails with our toes in the sand.  If you're looking for a beach destination with delicious food and a fun atmosphere, An Bang Beach is the spot!

A stunning beach lies close to the historic town. The beach boasts pristine cleanliness, and the vendors there are refreshingly unintrusive. While the water may be a tad turbulent, its shallowness ensures safety.

Discover a charming beach near Hoi An, conveniently accessible by renting a bicycle. Pedal through picturesque rice fields and a quaint village where an abundance of fresh vegetables thrive. The best part? No parking fees or charges for sun beds. Just before reaching the beach, take a right turn—you’ll find a delightful row of restaurants offering complimentary parking and sun beds. 🚲🌾🏖️
Was so impressed at how clean it was! Easy to swim in as it gradually gets deeper with no steep drop offs. Not far from the centre of hoi an either!

Beautiful sandy beach, white, long and wide and equipped. Many tourists present. Behind the beach there are many restaurants. Highly recommended. Near the beach I recommend the "Le rive" restaurant managed by Alessandro. If you want to take away some oriental flavours, Italian cuisine is better than in Italy.

Unexpectedly beautiful and quiet, I extended my stay. It is worth staying in An Bang and avoiding the mass tourism of the city of Hoi An, which although wonderful, must be visited in small doses at a time because it is invaded by tourists.

Away from the bustling crowds of Hoi An Ancient Town, we came to An Bang Beach, which is not far away. This is a paradise for leisure and relaxation, with long white sand beaches, clear water, and gentle sunshine, which makes people feel very comfortable.Friends who like water sports must not miss this place! Whether it is windsurfing or leisurely kayaking, you can enjoy the fun of the sea. Tired and hungry, the seafood restaurant by the beach can definitely satisfy your taste buds. The delicious and juicy seafood with cold coconut milk is simply a fairy-like enjoyment!An Bang Beach not only has charming scenery and rich activities, but also a very leisurely and relaxing atmosphere. Lie on the beach chair to bask in the sun, listen to the sound of the waves, forget all your worries, and let your body and mind immerse in the embrace of nature.
Awesome spot to rival Bali. Perfect weather, great surf and with Hoi A just down the road, will be back!
It's one of the nicest beaches in Hoi an. It's clean and people here are very friendly and very nice. The water was cold but still good to dive in.

An Bang Beach is a hidden gem in Hoi An, Vietnam. Its shallow waters, white sand, and abundance of seafood restaurants make it a perfect destination for a relaxing getaway. The beach is also great for swimming and sunbathing, and the nearby shops and restaurants offer plenty of options for entertainment and refreshment.
Beautiful beach, even during the rainy season. Many restaurants with fresh seafood and fish.
The clean beaches in Hoi An offer a delightful experience where you can enjoy a variety of seafood dishes that suit your taste, all with a direct view of the sea.
The beach wasn't fantastic but the restaurants and surrounds were incredible, we had always planned to come here for a visit during our stay in Hoi An but I wished we could have spent longer.

Nice clean beach. we picked one of the seafood restaurants on the beach to have lunch. With lunch, came use of their beach umbrellas, chairs and shower.  Beach is clean, water is warm, waves are very small. There was a steady stream of vendors selling all sorts of things like many tourist spots. Nice relaxing couple hours spent here after lunch

An Bang beach is devine, but be careful which part. The best part is the strip in North West on Nguyen Phan Vinh road. There are plenty of restaurants, spas, some bars and a couple of gift shops, a tailor, atm’s, small convenience shops and of course a lovely long beach. Some of the best restaurants are between the road and the sea.As you travel South East - beware of two huge restaurants- Kim Cuc and Chien - rated worst in Hoi An. They also have a bad reputation with their beach chairs.At that spot though are lots of bars and other restaurant choices.Not sure about the section further down the South East through Lac Long Quan, but once you get to Hidden Beach, go no further. Cua Dai beach not as good and few restaurant options.

Contrary to many reviews (possibly from the past), the beach here along the coast is very clean and well-kept. There is a beach bar with drinks and smoothies every few hundred meters. Not too many annoying beach vendors and no 50 rows of parasols, just one or two :-) very relaxed and beautiful here!

An Bang is the perfect beach holiday. Beach is clear and clean and not overcrowded. You can walk everywhere and the cafes, bar’s and restaurants were all fabulous. Don’t forget to try all the different types of coffee.

An Bang was an awesome destination for a holiday.  Beautiful beach, people are scrupulously honest, kind and friendly.  Food and accommodation were excellent.   I stayed at Coco Villas which was superb.  Clean, friendly, small, beautiful setting and a fantastic pool.  Food was delicious.  Highlights were Passion Restuarant which I recommend highly.  Food never failed to satisfy not just hunger but the taste was second to none.  The French Bakery was our morning destination.  Excellent coffee and croissants were the best I have ever had, both plain and chocolate !  People are friendly, helpful and will go out of their way to make sure we had everything we needed.  Highly recommend.
Honestly way better than I was expecting. Beautiful water. Not at all crowded! Most places give free lounge chairs with food/ drink purchase
3rd time to AnBang. A little oasis away from the big resorts. Clear waters, long clean beach. So relaxing what's not to enjoy?  Lots of little cafes to visit and loungers along the way.

An Bang Beach is the perfect destination for a secluded getaway. This pristine beach is surrounded by lush greenery and offers stunning sunsets that will take your breath away. The sand is soft and white, and the clear, turquoise waters are perfect for swimming. Whether you're looking for a peaceful retreat or an adventure-filled vacation, An Bang Beach is the perfect spot.

A paradise for those looking to enjoy the best of both worlds. Whether you're a fan of fishing or windsurfing, this beach has it all. Not to mention, the beach bars are a great place to relax and enjoy a cold drink. If you're looking for a beach that has something for everyone, An Bang Beach is the perfect choice.

An Bang Beach in Hoian, Vietnam is a hidden paradise waiting to be discovered. With its quiet atmosphere and shallow waters, it's the perfect spot for a peaceful day of relaxation. The beautiful white sand and crystal clear waters are ideal for swimming, while the coral reef is a great spot for snorkeling. A Bang Beach is the perfect place to escape the hustle and bustle of city life and enjoy a tranquil day in nature.

An Bang Beach is the perfect spot for a day of beach fun! The shallow waters are perfect for swimming and the colourful fish will keep you entertained as you explore the area. The beach is also scenic, with plenty of natural beauty to take in. Whether you're looking for a relaxing day or an adventure, An Bang Beach has something for everyone!

Clean and wonderful beach. Ideal for swimming. Crystal clear water. Children can also swim easily. It wasn't difficult to find a parking spot. I totally recommend checking out this beach. Clean and safe :)

Very long stretch of beach with torquise water and soft white sand.. very clean beach with many activities ..or clean enough to just go for a swim.. at the wind moon restaurant we could just buy a drink and use their facilities as well as sit on their beach chairs for hours.. other places were charging $4 for 1 beach chair for 4 hours so I felt this was a better deal. Strongly recommend this beach which apparently was also listed as one of the top 20 beaches in 2017.. during the peak season it may get very busy but quite empty in June and this also magnified our pleasure!!
Schöner langer und breiter Sandstrand, gesäumt mit vielen kleinen  Restaurants, die auch Liegen und Schirme am Strand anbieten, im Normalfall “umsonst”, wenn man dort etwas isst oder trinkt.
Bãi biển đẹp, mình cực kỳ thích The Deck House, mỗi lần về Hoian đều phải phơi nắng và nằm ở đây. Ăn hải sản thì ghé quán Sáu. Siêu tươi, siêu ngon và giá hợp lý

The beach is lined with seafood restaurants and street vendors selling all kinds of delicious food. From savory seafood dishes to sweet treats, there's something for everyone. The beach also has beachside massages available to help you truly unwind. The atmosphere is laid back and the locals are friendly. An Bang Beach is the perfect place to relax and enjoy the beauty of Vietnam.

Not only does it have stunning white sand and turquoise waters, but the beach also offers plenty of activities to keep you entertained. Whether you want to browse the souvenir shops, play beach volleyball, fly a kite, or go kayaking, An Bang Beach has something for everyone. The beach is also very family-friendly, with plenty of restaurants and cafes nearby to keep everyone happy. Plus, the beach is well-maintained and kept very clean. You can even hire beach umbrellas and chairs to enjoy the sun in comfort. If you're looking for an unforgettable beach experience, An Bang Beach is the perfect place to go. With its beautiful scenery, fun activities, and excellent amenities, this beach is sure to give you an unforgettable vacation!

Whether you're an adrenaline-junkie looking to shred some waves with wakeboarding, a foodie looking for the freshest seafood restaurants, or a beach bum looking for a relaxing beachside massage, An Bang Beach has something for everyone! And if you're looking for a place to cool off, the crystal-clear waters of the beach are perfect for a refreshing swim. So don't hesitate - come to An Bang Beach and treat yourself to a little R&R!

An Bang Beach is one of the best beaches in Hoi An, Vietnam, with its cool blue water, long white sand, and light waves rushing to the shore. This is a great place to relax your mind. I have been to Hoi An many times but I always come back here.

Beautiful white sand beaches, crystal clear waters, and stunning views await you. The beach is perfect for a variety of activities, from windsurfing and wakeboarding to sunbathing on the sun loungers. The beach also has plenty of restaurants and bars to enjoy, making it the perfect place to relax and take in the stunning scenery. If you're looking for a beach holiday with a difference, An Bang Beach is the place to go

The crystal clear water and the white sand make it a stunning beach to relax and unwind. The windsurfing is great here, as the waves are just right for beginners and more experienced windsurfers. There are also plenty of beach umbrellas and sun loungers to make your stay comfortable. The beach is also close to the town of Hoi An, making it a great spot to explore the local culture. A relaxing beach day or a fun day of windsurfing, the perfect spot for your next vacation.

With its clean white sand and crystal-clear waters, it's the perfect place to spend a day relaxing and soaking up the sun. The beach is lined with palm trees and surrounded by lush greenery, creating a serene atmosphere. The beach is also known for its vibrant nightlife, with plenty of beach bars and restaurants to choose from. Whether you're looking for a peaceful day in the sun or a lively night out, An Bang Beach is sure to provide a memorable experience. With its stunning views and friendly locals, it's no wonder this beach is a popular destination for locals and tourists alike.
So cool, it is one of the most beautiful beach in Hoian. There are a lot of famous seafood restaurant nearby, so you can enjoy fresh seafood. Highly recommend this beach to other guests.
Je suis pas d accord Belle plage et en mai très peu de monde L eau est propre ainsi que la plage Oui beaucoup de restos mais bon marché quand même Taxis pas d arnaque, compteur okL eau est chaude

The shallow, clean waters are perfect for swimming and the white sand and blue sky make for a perfect backdrop for any beach day. The beach is also lined with palm trees and other tropical plants that make for a stunning view. The beach is also close to the city of Hoian, so you can easily explore the area and enjoy the local culture. I highly recommend An Bang Beach Hoian for anyone looking for a peaceful yet beautiful beach experience. The atmosphere is so tranquil and the views are simply breathtaking. It's a perfect spot to relax, swim, and take in the beauty of nature.
beautiful beach and the round boats were so fascinating.  The water was warm and lovely sand at the beach.

The beach itself is stunning, with white sand and crystal clear waters that are perfect for swimming and sunbathing. The scenery is breathtaking, with lush green mountains in the background and the ocean stretching out before you. It’s the perfect spot to relax and take in the views. There are plenty of activities to do here, from water sports to beach volleyball, and the locals are always happy to join in on the fun. Whether you’re looking for a romantic getaway or a family adventure, An Bang Beach Hoian is sure to provide you with the perfect setting.

Very nice beach.Clean of plastic waste. Fine sand. Not many pushy sales people. Food and drink options. We love it!!Be careful of the rip tides when the swell is strong. Keep an eye out for your kids.
Decent beach with lots of vendors to work out a deal with for a chair and food.  Definitely barter w/ the servers as some want money just to sit in the chairs, others you can just buy a drink
Great views, reasonably priced drinks, great burgers and great service.  Happy hour from 5pm-6pm, what more could you ask for!
Very nice beach - one of the best ones we have been to. Very clear water and clean. there are many choice for food here

visited the beach in the morning. lovely sand, long beach. Ideal for long walks, and to spend some time in the cafes nearby. Accessible by Grab from Hoi An centre, it is about 50K dong one way. worth a visit

We had a great beach day at An Bang.  We got a small cabana which provided service and a terrific seafood lunch.  When we were there the beach was practically deserted, not sure what it would be like in peak season.

Nơi thư giãn vào ngày cuối tuần rất tốt. Bãi biển rộng rãi, có nhiều nhà hàng hải sản tươi sống chất lượng, bạn có thể thuê 1 chiếc giường nằm trên bãi cát để tắm nắng với giá khá rẻ hoặc có thể ăn tại nhà hàng để được miễn phí giường.
Địa điểm du lịch nên đến khi du lịch tại Hội An. Hải sản tươi sống, tầm nhìn tốt, bãi biển sạch sẽ, nước trong.

If you want a great coffee and a low key atmosphere, go to "The sound of silence" coffee shop. It's a small place, away from the crowd and you can spend a few hours on the beach transats, enjoying a nice meal while sunbathing!
Was staying in Hoi An and visited this beach twice in 3 days. Such a nice place to relax, with clean water, nice sand. Nothing really bad to say about the place.
Bãi biển du lịch sạch sẽ, đông khách đến tắm biển và ăn hải sản. Bờ biển dài và có tầm nhìn rất tốt. Một địa điểm đáng đến khi đến Hội An.

This nearby beach surpassed my expectations. It is clean, the water a very comfortable temperature, and great for chilling. The bech is gentle sloping, and there are demarcated swimming areas, safe from water sports.
Bãi biển rất đẹp, nước biển trong xanh. Tắm biển rất thích, đặc biệt là vào các nhà hàng phía trên, có các khu bãi tắm riêng nên rất sạch. Khu bãi công cộng thì hơi đông người

When I arrived at An Bang Beach I thought I had died and gone to heaven.The beach is packed with bars and restaurants full of happy staff. One in particular that we loved was the Fisherman. It’s a vegan restaurant with the most amazing tasty food that even non vegans would like.There are ladies on the beach selling things but they are not pushy and actually become friends that you can sit and have some great and interesting chats with.There is a real sense of community filled with beautiful people.It’s 15 minutes drive to Hoi An Old Town which deserves a visit too.

SEA and SAND restaurant, run by locals TIng and Taw, husband and wife team, almost opposite the water sports staff/ banana boat, surf ski and boat. Right on the beach. Guest may use the banana lounge, as long as they have a drink. Icy cold beer and soft drinks available. Taw cooks and the food has been excellent. Love the spring rolls.  We are Australians who have visited vietnam many times, and have been very happy with the standard of service and food from Sea and Sand. Also has free wifi, give it a go and support this lovely family.
sooo relaxing, the water was perfect temperture, not many tourists so easy to hire a shaded lounge VND 50, clean beach, taxi from old town Hoi An only 91,000 each way.
해수욕장 초입 경관이 멋있음 미케비치보다 좋아요거기 유달리 손님많은집에 식사도 괜찮았습니다해변가에있는 카페들이 안방비치를 더 매력있게 만들어줘서 다행임. 다음에 놀러오면 재방문하겠습니다~~
The highlight of Hoi An. This place is good for a relaxation. Many seafood restaurants to choose from, the clams porridge is really delicious.

Had 3 wonderful days at the beach at Dolphin kitchen and bar, stop complaining and comparing other countries with the beach.We love it and travel 24 hours to Vietnam to relax enjoy the food and everything else what we like to do or not to do.Met some great people from Australia and had some good conversations.Tomorrow we fly to Nha Trang but will be back next year if we are still in the position to travel.Around 16.00 hours local eateries are built on the beach and they all git their own specialty.Great to watch and see how the children and people enjoy the beach.
It is one of our favorite beaches in Vietnam. A clean beach, nice people, and delicious seafood are what made us love it. Highly recommend this Hoi An beach for a half-day spend.

Pleasant, the food is good for not very expensive and the view is magnificent. Beautiful rather quiet beach if you move away from the first visit. Very friendly team, and take advantage of the comfortable lounges above the beach to eat.

Bãi biển rất đẹp cát trắng và mịn. Nước biển xanh sóng không quá to nên phù hợp cho tắm biển. Nhiều nhà hàng quanh đây giá cả cũng phù hợp. Ăn uống ở đây khá thích. Nhiều đồ ăn hải sản. Ở đây ba ngày, ngày nào cũng ra bãi biển này tắm.

Mình ghé biển An Bàng vào giữa tháng 7, biển khá sạch, sóng không quá to nên tắm rất thích. Ở đó có nhiều nhà hàng có cho thuê ghế ngồi ở biển nên có khá nhiều lựa chọn. Ngoài đường có nhiều chỗ bán đồ ăn vặt với tắm nước ngọt, giá cả rẻ. Lúc mình về tầm 5h thì đường khá đông nên hơi kẹt.

Mình thích ra biển vào buổi chiều, bờ biển sạch nước ấm, nhiều hàng rong được bày bán trên bờ biển nhưng người dân luôn ý thức vệ sinh môi trường, bãi cát sạch ngồi nằm thật thoải mái, đừng quên lưu lại vài hình ảnh đẹp nhé👌

Đây là một trong những bãi biển tốt nhất mà tôi đã từng đến. Bãi biển rất lớn, khách sạn của chúng tôi đưa chúng tôi qua một đoạn bãi biển riêng của ks rất đẹp và sạch sẽ. Nếu bạn đã ở đây, bạn sẽ muốn quay trở lại.
Rất sạch và đẹp! Mọi thứ dường như lặng lẽ, dịu  hơn vào buổi sáng.Nếu ko còn người bán hàng rong dưới bãi biển thì sẽ sạch đẹp hơn nữa.

Having visited in 2019, w noticed 2020 was busier. Beach loungers are rented by separate businesses. Read the name on your lounger and if you like the service, be sure to show your loyalty and return next time you come to the beach. For a nominal sum you get 2 loungers and an umbrella and constant (and very good value) food and drinks service all day. Can highly recommend The 3Ladies Kitchen, run by Thu who will treat you like an honoured guest!

My Vietnamese girlfriend and I had an apartment one block from the beach in Da Nang.  With that said, we found ourselves often motor scooting (25 minutes) South to An Bang Beach outside of Hoi An.  We prefer to stay on the North end of the beach, less people.  Food is available perhaps not as much as locations further South on the beach (where we also would camp out, Soul Beach, The Deckhouse, The Dolphin).  Water is clean and there are water activities if you so desire.  You can see Da Nang city skyline to the North and if you look closely you can see the "Lady Buddha" in the bay of Da Nang.  Go! Catch some rays! Enjoy the day!

Do not support the parasailing company. I was swimming in the water well away from the launching area for parasailing and the speedboat passed close to me and between me and a father and one year old boy on a paddle board. The jetski man was angry with the paddle boarder so he went within one metre of him and sprayed a big wall of water forcing the man and his son to fall into the ocean. I had to swim out to help the man and baby get back onto the paddle board. It was an extremely dangerous and frightening thing to witness. The parasailing people do not own the ocean. They have a roped off area and people can’t swim there but we were over 50 metres away and I was in water I could still stand so not out deep.

The beach was great and clean. However, come sundown, it gets crowded with locals or Vietnamese. The tourists or foreigners who laid their beach blankets/towels to relax and chill get shooed away by hawkers who sell food and lay their own mats to rent out to locals. My husband and I were the only ones left with our own beach blanket while the hawkers’ green mats already occupied the area. The hawkers didn’t attempt to approach us and ask to leave so they can lay their green mats, probably because either I look like a local, a Vietnamese, or because I’m pregnant. They seem to give priority or importance to pregnant women, hence we got to stay. But when my husband and I went to the waters, that was when one of the hawkers touched and moved our blanket to make space for their mat and that was when my husband got angry. They could’ve asked us nicely if we can move a little but touching our things without permission when we were in the waters so that they can do their “business” was a big NO! We ended up leaving anyway because it was getting too crowded and on can no longer relax. We thought of never coming back to this beach and probably just find something less crowded and peaceful.

Der Strand ist tatsächlich schön, das Wasser ist toll, aber was ich da heute erlebt habe, ist von so einer ungeheuerlichen Unverschämtheit, dass es mich fast sprachlos gemacht hat.
Da gibt es anscheinend eine sehr "geschäftstüchtige" Familie, die großflächig ihre grünen Strandtücher verteilt haben, um sie zu vermieten. Um den Sonnenuntergang herum, bevor die Einheimischen den Strand stürmen, hat die eine ältere Frau ein paar junge Damen und ein Paar, die auf eigenen Decken lagen, aufgefordert zu gehen. Das wurde mir dann auch auf Nachfrage bestätigt, da ich diese Szene beobachtet habe und neugierig wurde.
Als die jungen Leute am gehen waren, hat die alte Frau gleich damit begonnen, ihre grünen Strandtücher dort zu verteilen wo diese Menschen eben sich noch sorglos entspannt haben.
Der Gipfel wurde aber erst erreicht, als ich und meine Frau im Wasser waren. Der Sohn dieser Familie, sich an unsere Sachen zu schaffen gemacht hat, um noch mehr Platz für andere zu machen... ohne zu fragen, einfach so. Sein Pech war aber, dass ich ihn gesehen und angeschrien habe. Eine Erklärung oder gar eine Entscheidung kam nicht.
Diese Familie ist so etwas von unverschämt und geldgierig, dass sie nicht weiter denken und erkennen, dass so ein Verhalten gar nicht gut bei den Menschen ankommt und sie sich und allen anderen Geschäftstreibenden langfristig Schaden.
Ich bin der Meinung, dass wenn das auch anderen Leuten passiert, dass man vom freien Strand verjagt oder derart belästigt wird, man die Autoritäten einschalten sollte. Ich werde es beim nächsten Mal tun.
Ich für meinen Teil werde mir einen anderen Abschnitt suchen, wo ich nicht so dreist belästigt werde.

We visited this beach for  3 consecutive days before we found something far better.

Anyone giving this beach has a very strange idea of what a great beach is like and it makes a nonsense of relying on reviews.

The beach is narrow and very crowded and noisy (if you scream and shout as a normal form of communication this beach is perfect for you) although if you go to the beach bars towards either end (away from the main entrance) the free sun beds on offer (with the assumption you buy food/ drinks) made it tolerable.

The sand is grey from pollution and there is rubbish immediately beyond any beach bar demise. The rubbish is local fly tipping and didn’t seem to be generated from the tourists.

Each morning the sun beds we sat on had large rat imprints below them. The water is free of rubbish mainly however so swimming was fine but there is a thin film of diesel on it from the fishing boats who decided out of the entire coast to fish opposite the stretch of sun beds strangely.

In summary this beach is best avoided as it will taint your impression of the wonderful country that is Vietnam.

I totally agree with Victor Neamtu's review from a month ago - these reviews cannot be real! An Bang Beach probably was beautiful once, but like so much of Vietnam it has been ruined by rubbish ‍♀️ We visited at the end of Feb 2025 and the beach and surrounding area were overwhelmed with plastic and broken up polystyrene, which had built into banks at the back of the beach in some places. Plus huge areas of beach had been completely washed away by storms and erosion. The place felt tragic and hopeless. We watched a few cafe workers dig holes in the sand and try to bury rubbish, on the beach! ‍♀️ No effective action was being taken. We had planned to stay for 3 days but left after one because it was so grim.
A lot of the photos on other reviews appear to be of the south end of Danang beach, no An Bang at all.

The beach is quite nice if you arrive early in the morning before it gets crowded but beware of the nearby restaurant owners trying to give you "free chairs" when you arrive. These are not free chairs. They will tell you free chairs and allow you to sit there for as long as you want, then go on to tell you when you get up to leave that you now either have to eat at their restaurant or  pay for the "free chair" that you used. As we only used the chairs for a limited time, and due to the false advertisement, we refused to pay. Other tourists In the same boat also did the same resulting in the women getting hostile and muttering numerous swears at us in front of their young daughter. Not a good example to set. My advice is to ignore their " free chair" offerings, and go further down the beach where you can enjoy it without hassle.

I was in Vietnam about 4 years ago and loved this beach. We rode bikes out to it and found a secluded spot and shared it only with a few fishermen lazing in their hammocks.This trip, we did the same, rode bikes out to the beach, only to find it has changed dramatically. There is no longer a secluded place to be had, with umberallas all along the beach and you now have to pay for parking if you want to visit one of the main areas with the bars, even if you are only riding a bycicle.Also, we have heard that the chemical pollution in the area, due to China dumping their chemical waste off the coast, means that swimming here is probably not the best thing for your health. We heard mixed reviews on this, some people said their hotel staff said absolutely not to swim, whereas others had said it was fine. We do know that the fishing industry in the area has been greatly affected. We decided to keep on the safe side, and though we love swimming in the ocean, we didn't on this trip.

An Bang can be a very chilled out spot, but on many days after 16h and before the sun goes down, the beach is simply flooded with Vietnamese looking for a swim and tend to group close together.  The result at times can be a nightmare as many will group together in the shade of other peoples umbrellas (often sitting at the feet of other beach goers) while some locals will start bbqing dried calamari on the beach or screaming about their drinks for sale.  Makes the whole experience fairly overwhelming actually.IF you get to head to the beach earlier however it can be very chill and relaxed, though I would advise people not to swim until the toxic pollution from up the coast is cleaned up.  Can be very dangerous for children especially.

Took a trip to An bang beach.. lovely sandy beach but absolutely packed and bars filled with large groups of backpackers on day long binges!! Fine if that's what you're after but try turning left to Hidden Beach if you're after something more low key!

Like most places in Vietnam, you're constantly being sold too, which takes away any chance at relaxing. As soon as you get near the beach on your bike women who own parking lots are jumping out in front of you demanding that you park your bike with them. You'll be aggressively sold anything from sunglasses to towels to doughnuts on the beach so hopefully you didn't want to just hang out and take in the scenery. There are chairs on the beach but those are obviously going to cost you. Parts of the beach are more crowded than others, nothing special really. Just your average looking beach.

We rode bikes to the beach which was fun but were totally disappointed with what we found. It was a dirty beach with very poor swimming areas, with a bunch of hotel sun loungers crammed together. The shoreline is wall to wall cafes and nothing else. We decided to go and have a drink at a beachfront one which was pleasant but we were continually hassled by vendors wanting to sell us the usual range of useless goods. We rode back to Hoi An which is a much nicer place to be.

It's a nice beach with good facilities, but almost impossible to relax as we were hassled frequently to buy souvenirs, massages, drinks and food we didn't want. Average quality food, drinks not cold. 10,000 dong to park 2 bikes.

I came to An Bang Beach during the "spring" season of Hoi An. Was very chilly and the waves were too strong. Not recommended to go in there to swim. I have travelled throughout Thailand and seen beautiful Thai beaches, so An Bang beach was not attractive to me at all. if you have seen beautiful Thai beaches, it will be a bit of disappointment see this beach. It is ok to just relax with a book and a nice beverage though when it is sunny or probably during the "summer" season.

This beach was not nice at all, the water was murky, you cant walk down the beach without someone harassing you to site on there sunbed for free, but wait you have to buy food from them.We rode our bikes here, you will have to pay to park your bike right at the beach so maybe do some research before going if you don't want to pay as I am sure there a places you can park for free and we were likely paying someone who didn't even own the land we parked on.We ended up sitting down and just paying 15,000 Dong each and just sun bathing for a few hours.The water was cold we only took a short dip.  I would't go to Vietnam expecting to go to the beach, Thailand has much more beautiful warmer beaches.

We cycled there for the day from Hoi An and that was a bit scary.  On arrival we had to pay to park our bikes (not much though).  Found the sea was floating with rubbish and no one swimming - not surprising. Very loud music started from a karoke session  - awful noise from megga speakers.  We left.

지금 가니 쌀쌀하고 휑하고 하네요... 뭐 별로 다른건 모르겠어요 ㅋㅋㅋ호텔에서 연계한 셔틀타고 갔다가 5분정도 보고 바로 도로 돌아왔습니다.무료셔틀 있으면 갈만한데 일부러 찾아갈 필요는 없는듯 하네요서양인들은 태닝하고 하던데여름엔 좋을지 모르겠는데 겨울에는 그저그래요

My wife and I biked from our hotel in Hoi an to An Bang Beach. It is only about 2 km from Hoi an.  When we got close to the beach, we could see other cyclists ahead and different local people yelling at them.  We stopped while another tourist was explaining to others that in order to park you bike at the beach, you must go to a local business and pay 10,000 VND to buy a large bottle of water and the business will let you park your bike there.  Other locations closer to the beach will just ask cyclists for 5,000 VND each to park their bikes.  We paid the 10,000 VND, got the bottle of water to prove we had paid and walked to the beach.  It was a overcast day and it was drizzling lightly.  The beach had a sign posted saying no swimming but there were a few people down the beach swimming in the large waves.  It appeared to be quite a large beach and if it would have been a better day, I have no doubt it would have been quite enjoyable walking on the beach and the water.  Also, if it was a sunny and warm day, I would assume it would have been quite busy.  However, it was the paying for water to park our bikes at the start that just made us feel like it was another tourist trap.

Die Beach wäre schön, aber leider alles voller Liegestühle und nervenden Strandverkäufern. Kommt man mit dem Fahrrad kämpfen etliche, laut schreiende Menschen um uns, geldbringende, Radfahrer. Mir hats hier nicht gefallen. Ein Tag wäre genug gewesen.

We visited An Bang beach on a very wet and windy day, so was not very picturesque, but still worth a visit, but even on bicycle, you have to pay to park to have a look. The people are quiet aggressive, but forwarned is forearmed. We paid 20,000 dong to park 2 bikes. They started at 100,000, but we paid too much apparently.

So the beach itself is ok, having been to Thailand, Fiji and living in Australia I don't think any beach in Vietnam will take my breath away but it was ok for what it was. However when walking down onto the beach you are approached by various men and woman offering free sunbeds (Nothing's free, especially in Vietnam) I was aware their would be a catch, we got apparoached by a man standing just on the left as you walk down the slop, he showed us to are "Free" sunbeds and then said "You eat in my restaurant , after..ok" so I asked if they were free "yes yes but you eat in ,y restaurant" I thought that wasn't a bad deal as I was planning on eating and paying for a sunbed to figured I had a deal, I agreed and he left. About 1 hour later as I lay enjoying me book he interruptes me and by saying " come eat in my resturant" I explained over and over I wasn't hungry yet so he demanded I pay for the sunbed, this went back and forth untill a woman who spoke better English approached, I explained the situation and after sometime they left me alone. I honoured my word and sat down in their resturant after I was finished on the beach (The resturant is the big one on the left hand side, you can't miss it) The prices in the resturant were higher than I'd seen in similar establishments in Vietnam but anyway I ordered food and a shake and my gf ordered just a shake, well this wasn't good enough she now had to pay for the sunbed even tho the shake cost more, after some discussion I decided to leave the food and the shake and pay the meesely 40.000 dong for the beds and leave.Overl my experience wasn't great and I wouldn't return in a hurry, if you do go don't expect white sand and blue seas and also expect to be harrased and bullied into eating expensive food

Honestly I am probably biased. Spending time on the Gold and Sunshine Coast Australia, this just doesn't compare. Let's face it, if you came to the Hoi An region to stay on a beach then you must have lost your Vietnam Lonely Planet guide book during a typhoon on Ha Long Bay. The scruffy strip of sand that is An Bang Beach only exists as a potential destination because it sits alongside Hoi An, so is therefore a good way to while away the hours while you wait for Me Xe to stitch together your dodgy suit. Overweight expats and undernourished backpackers lounge about whilst the poor locals weave between them, trying to sell stuff. As for the various "bohemian" bars the aforementioned Lonely Planet recommends, they're basically shacks serving three-minute noodles and alcohol, presided over by infinitely jaded looking Frenchmen, who clearly made a bad choice somewhere down the line. If it's a beautiful beach you're after, ask your Hoi An tailor to stitch faster, then hot foot it to Phu Quoc.

The fresh seafood was tasty, but that's about it. The sellers are downright annoying.. like vultures they line up - just like sliding 4-5 beers down a bartop they sidle up and rally themselves in a cluster, dump their bangles and junk in a heap, virtually on top of you and proceed to invade your space by eyeballing you with an insincere grin and beady, glinting eyes.I found it completely offensive and felt quite threatened.The main 'offensive seller' named Hiep promptly went and sat behind my travel partner on the stairs, DIRECTLY in my line of vision and proceeded to glare (they call it evil eye). A few minutes later she went and sat closer to two earthy-style tourists AND TURNED ON THE TEARS. I was utterly sickened by this and went sat with my back to her. I got my camera out to take a picture of her, she VANISHED within seconds!They do not understand that we work our bums off to 'holiday' overseas. The sellers need to be registered and issued numbers to be clearly displayed so that this sort of appalling invasion of privacy can be curtailed. This is exactly the reason people stay behind the 'protection' of resort walls.  I keep a stash of 50's in a compartment in my bag to give to each maimed, beggar and trash forager I encounter. I just don't want to be hassled when I sit down to eat, I don't CARE what your problem is in that moment - it is MY time in that moment.

As stretches of sand go  its ok but the whole relaxing experience is marred by persistant and sometimes rude sellers and lounge chair touts. We wlked along the sand until we came to Blue Skies (the bright blue loungers with the purple cushions) . She is a lovely young girl setting up this business and provided us with great food and drink in a relative, y quiet part of the beach. But BEWARE the owners of the beach chairs next to her were unscrupulous and should not be patronised. They pounced on us about 100 metres away and pestered and begged us to use their chairs. When we refused saying we were going to Blue Skies the young guy became angry and we were told they were no good and their food would make us sick. Hue the owner of Blue Skies, later told us of a history of intimidation and threats over the 3 weeks she has been operating

We drove pass first time on a tour and our tour guide advised us it wasn't a beach for sun bathing. We came back again when we were cycling around Hoi An to have a look and only spent 5 minutes here as there is also nothing to see. The waves are strong and the beach is not even. A few hotels/ restaurants have sun loungers out but it felt too chaotic to sun bath or have a drink on the beach.Our tour guide had said the government are working on putting wave breaks in the sea so it would be swimmable and cleaning up the beach and making it more appealing. Until then, there is not much to see or do.

Rode our bikes here after reading great reviews. When we approached the beach and were flagged down by about 10 people yelling at us to park our bikes at their spot. We didn't expect this, so ended up turning around and finding a local restaurant to park and have a quick drink! We then walked down to the beach and before our feet hit the sand we were then being bugged by various restaurant owners wanting our business. We had a quick look at the beach, thought it looked average, and then turned around and went for a quiet ride elsewhere! Too many locals trying to make a quick buck out of a natural attraction, causing it to lose any appeal it could have had...

when we arrived at the beach with the bike it was nearly sunset we thought we could enjoy a little beachtime. But the first thing was a screaming lady who wanted that we park the bikes and pay for it. we ignored her first but out of the blue another guy showed up and said we have to park them there. i asked how much it was and he said 10. 000vnd each. later we found out that it is to much and the normal price was 5.000 vnd but who cares. on which beach in the world u have to pay for a parking spot for your bike. anyway when we went down to the beach a lot of people came and wanted to sell us something. here is the trick to have great beach experience there. Bring your own towels and a little lunch with you go down to the beach and walk maybe 5 to 10 minutes on the left or the right pass the restaurants and then just sit down and enjoy thr day.

We went to the beach today by bikes, and had to pay 5k for 'parking' for each bike where in most of the places in the world, no one charges 'parking' for push bikes. As soon as we went to the beach, lots of people want business from us, by telling us their chairs are free. We know there is nothing is free in the world, so it turned out that you need to order seafood from the restaurant. We decided to order one Juice first for me while my boyfriend went for a swim. We were told that we could not sit on the chair if we just order one drink. As soon as we were about to leave, they said ok for us to stay. since my boyfriend did not order anything to start with, every time he came to the beach, he was asked is he was going to order anything. when we decided to leave the beach, we could not find our key to the bike, the 'parking' lot person told me that he would charge me 50k to cut the chain, or I could buy a new lock from him for 100k, then cutting the chain would be free. People around have no concept of helping people, everything is associated with money.

Totally agree with previous reviews and with defusilage! The locals have now spoilt this idyllic spot and they have become too greedy. We had been staying at cuidai beach with very little hassle from vendors but someone suggested we try An Bang - I read up on LP and they recommend the laid back atmosphere. Well it was the opposite. We took a taxi from Hoi An -80k. When you walk out to the beach there are c 10 -15 restaurants to the left and the the right all vying for your business. We turned right and walked almost to the end. Each touter was saying free sun beds so we chose ones right outside the Restaurant Loi. We then found out that they wanted to charge 40k per sunbed or you eat at the restaurant. We went for a swim and the water is amazing (which is why I gave this review at least two stars otherwise there would be none). There are tiny jelly balls in the sea but they do not sting. There are also more fish than in cua dai beach. The swimming area is also marked off with life buoys which were great to swim to and bop around in. When we went to the restaurant we had drinks and some fried squid rings. When they came with the bill, they added 2xchairs adding 80 k to the bill! This was because we had only ordered one meal! I refused to pay as I felt that we we had been conned and also they turned very quickly into very aggressive and intimidating people shouting and hissing at us. They eventually let us go once we threatened them with calling the police. Overall, we left this beach very agitated and upset. The hawkers here also pester you much harder than at the other beach. If you are happy to be ripped off and hassled by aggressive hawkers, then that is fine but the locals here have become too greedy and prey on tourists. Avoid!

A pleasant 20 min bike ride out of Hoi An, but when you get to the beach the hassle begins. People are fighting over you to park your bike with them, and we got charged 50,000 VND per bike which is a real rip-off. Then you get bundled into a restaurant with the promise of free sun beds. We were dragged into the first place on the left as you go to the beach. The owner/manager is so rude to customers and staff alike. We waited for an hour to get the wrong food which was cold, then they heated up our prawns and were angry when we refused to eat it or pay for it. We scarpered to the sunbeds, which are rammed next to each other, and although the sea was warm and lovely I couldn't wait to get out of there. My advice would be to have look along the beach and choose where you want to eat and hang out; we gave in too easily to the pressure. I think the other beach Cudai is much nicer, less hassle and the food and service infinitely better.

We were told this beach was better, food cheaper, but overall I felt more scam in the air, starting with trying to knock us off the bicycles to park at THEIR spots. Found a spot that looked 'free' (of course, later someone chased after us yelling 'money money') and then we were lured into the 'free' beach lounger offer 'if you eat lunch here' .. i looked at the menu and it was apparent that the beach chair price was in there, but still better than Italy / France by a mile. The beach itself is enjoyable and appeared clean enough, the pesky sellers are on all beaches, but a firm 'no, thank you' was enough. The lunch was very meh (first restaurant on the left ' Suong') but we did not have any trouble for only ordering one dish and one drink. I still rather return to Cua Dai.

Der Strand ist an sich wirklich schön, das Meer, die Aussicht und die Lage sind toll. Aber ehrlich gesagt ist es schade, wie wenig auf Sauberkeit geachtet wird.

Vietnamese are one of the most frindly people on this planet. Always friendly and with a sincere smile. But An Bang is a Paradise full of garbage. How long will it take for the local people to understand the people they want to earn money from?
Most tourists do not like garbage thrown everywhere. Even if it is on abandoned lots. Please invest 0 00005% of your earnings to collect the garbage. It is a brilliant investment. We do not like to be run over by scooters driving too fast and honking to warn any unlucky person happening to walk on the narrow streets. And we dont like that you burn the garbage, especially plastic.
If this is respected, An Bang hotels can charge more and guests will come back to a uniquely clean village.  I also understand that An Bang is a place in rapid development. But please refrain from starting building works before at least 8.00. Now it is 06.25 a.m on a Sunday and the noise is defining from building works. Now the majority of  people are coming here mainly because it is cheap.

I recently visited some of Vietnam's beaches, and while I truly love the country for its rich culture and vibrant cities, I have to say the beaches themselves were a bit disappointing for me. The main issue was how crowded they were-there were just too many people trying to enjoy the same stretch of sand, which made it hard to find a peaceful spot to relax. On top of that, I noticed a lot of plastic litter scattered around, which really detracted from the natural beauty of the area. Another thing that caught my attention was the numerous holes dug into the sand by visitors, which made walking along the beach a bit tricky and less enjoyable.

That said, Vietnam is such a wonderful destination with so much to offer beyond the beaches. The history, food, and landscapes inland are truly remarkable and worth exploring. If you’re planning a trip, I’d recommend focusing your time on those experiences rather than the beach scene here. There are definitely better places if you’re looking specifically for pristine, relaxing beaches. But for everything else Vietnam has to offer, it’s an unforgettable place to visit.

What could have been a beautiful natural spot is now poorly maintained and not worth visiting. Avoid it if you're looking for a clean and relaxing experience.

Joli court d’eau bordé d’une végétation luxuriante mais juste sur les premiers mètres en amont de la rivière. Ensuite beaucoup trop de monde, eau polluée, mauvaise odeur et cerise sur le gâteau, à un endroit des « gardes » vous laissent passer pour aller en aval de la rivière sans rien vous dire mais vous demandent de payer pour remonter. Et c’est bien sûr le seul chemin pour retourner à l’endroit où vous avez poser vos scooters en amont de la rivière et ne veulent rien entendre, même si vous avez payé un parking sur un des restaurants bordant la rivière, qui lui aussi ne vous avait pas parlé d’entrée payante.

Вы спускаетесь вниз в ручей и метров 300 идёте по ручью где по сторонам обычная помойка с Ее прекрасным запахом , даже вонь от страусов на которых вас предлагают покатать по дороге не перебивает этот запах.  Основной путь ручья упирается в водопад который представляет собой перепад небольшой высоты где вода не может пока проточить камень . Основная красота это красный песок с левой стороны по ходу ручья , так называемые красные дюны . Радует что это бесплатная замануха , ну а если с кого берут за это деньги то это классный развод ! Красный песок если двигаешься со стороны Камрани в Муйне видно вокруг около часа при подъезде , то есть это обычная почва в данном месте просто протекающий ручей преподносят как уникальность . Хвала Вьетнамцам быстро учатся и как говорится рвут подьметки . Интересней наверно утром с этих дюн встретить рассвет или покататься на досках .
They ask you to pay to see the canyon and it's not really a canyon. More like walking round a quarry.

Von Fairy weit weg! Eine Frechheit sowas als Touristenattraktion anzubieten! Außer Müll,Dreck, unappettitliches Wasser( in der Beschreibung steht, sie können mit bloßen Füßen durch glasklares Wasser wandern)  und abgeranzte Lokale nichts gesehen. Wir sind nach der Hälfte ausgestiegen, denn es war nicht zu ertragen! Schade um den eigentlich schönen Ort. Wenn die Leute dort nicht bald erkennen das man auf die Umgebung achten sollte, wird das alles ein böses Ende nehmen.

More airy fairy than anything else. Felt like a construction dug out left abandoned and an accidental stream (which could have been sewage) flowed at the base.Wait wait...I did come across a giant beetle. That was MAGICAL. No pun intended.

Is a pity  because is an outstanding result of Nature, but it really does not worth. The ticket is very cheap ,1$ 2 persons,  and you can walk around 30 minutes trough the stream. Water is clear and ground soft , you can take out your shoes, BUT is full of garbage, there are a lot of small shops and and of course lots of garbage . there are trash bins along the way but some humanes does not care. I think with collected money the minimum is to keep clean and healthy the beautiful natural place. You see indifference and local people has not this awareness. IS A PITY!

OK if you want to cool your feet off for ten minutes this is the place to go. Just a shallow sandy stream, the source could even be from the local effluent plant for all I know. Cost is peanuts and even that’s to much. Bore off, where’s the pub?
不建议来，黄泥水混浊小溪，水流像小区爆水管差不多，又热又晒，风景一般，很无聊。在国内你肯定不愿意脱鞋走这种小溪的，被网红攻略坑了。

Удивительная черта вьетнамцев делать деньги из воздуха. 2 года назад путешествие по данной очень странной речке было абсолютно бесплатно, в этом году стали продавать билеты и выставили посты охраны. Билет 15000 донгов на человека. Мусора при этом стало еще больше, убирать даже получай деньги никто видимо так и не собирается. Смотреть особо нечего, грязная речушка посреди песка, и в конце типа водопад, стекающая с 3-х метровой высоты водичка.

So we hadn't planned on visiting the "fairy stream" but it was part of the package tour that day which included the red and white dunes and the fish market. So about the stream, you park at the side of the road on the main street, the guide sorts out your tickets, then you have the option to walk holding your shoes in your hand  or leaving them by the entrance to the stream. The water is a few inches deep for the most part rising up to about knee high on a couple of occasions, there are high sand ridges on the left side and noisey kiosks on the other side for most of the walk. There are karaoke bars, ostrich farm and a snake handler which we'd seen on our 'pilgrimage'. I was about five meters from the end of the walk and was so bored that I didn't care to see the waterfall, (trickle of water) so I tured back for another twenty minute walk along the stream only to be hassled by a ticket seller going back, I waved them away saying I don't have my ticket. To be honest it's a complete waste of time, nothing to see here. It's almost like their clutching at straws trying to make something out of nothing. It's a stream. Hundreds of tourist trodding along a narrow stream with their heads down. My advice stay clear.

If you just pass by in Mui ne and want to visit thats okay. If you have to plan a trip here on purpose is useless, nothing special really. Do not book a tour as this is on the main road where all the resorts are so you just need to stop and buy the ticket. It is a long walk you have to do without your shoes and does not lead to anything special. They keep animals along the road just to make some money from it like snakes or ostriches. You have to pay if you want to take a picture or ride, not nice.

Seit zwei Monaten wird für den fairy Stream Eintritt verlangt 15000 dong was ja vollkommen ok ist, jedoch erwarte ich dann das ich nicht durch ein verdrecktes Flussbett laufe. Meines Erachtens wird hier nur Geld verlangt um Geld zu verdienen und nicht um damit die Attraktion zu pflegen und sauber zuhalten.

Wir kamen zum Fairy Stream in der Annahme, dass die Attraktion kostenlos sei. (Laut Internet & Berichten von August 2018 war es das auch noch) Nun wird hierfür Eintritt verlangt, was generell in Ordnung ist. ABER: wer Eintritt verlangt, der muss seine Attraktionen auch sauber halten und sie nicht vermüllen lassen! Hatten den Eindruck, dass hier einfach den Touristen das Geld abgezwickt wird und in die eigene Tasche gesteckt wird. Sehr schade!

Just went here today and it was honestly just a dirty stream. To be fair we didn’t get the entire way up the stream. The rest of our group was scammed and couldn’t get in. Then I hurt my ankle in a deep hole halfway up the steam. It smelt really really bad and was full of ribbons. I honestly wouldn’t bother. Not worth it.

At this point I believe tourists pay whatever as long as it's cheap and this is an example of it. You are charged 15k (plus 5k if you want to leave your shoes at the entrance) to go into a muddy brownish stream of water full of rubbish.

Un peu déçu de ma visite au fairy stream... on doit payer notre entrée. Bien que le prix soit très modique, on descend sous un pont où il y a beaucoup de déchets. Lorsqu'on remonte la rivière, il y a des stands où l'on peut acheter des boissons et fruits ce qui enlève le charme à l'endroit. Tout le ruisseau est jonché de déchets et sale. Si vous décidez de ne pas faire ce tour ne vous en faites pas, vous ne manquez rien.

Before you enter, you have to pay 15,000 VND to enter the stream. Then you walk up the stream bed which is ankle deep and run the gauntlet of all the business people trying to sell to tourists as a captured audience. The creek is lined with sand bags which detracts from the natural beauty.  When you come to the end, you come to a lame waterfall. If you want to see really impressive waterfalls, go to Dalat. It might have been nice once, but the water is a bit suspect as far as cleanliness goes and definitely wear shoes.

Do not leave your shoes at the entrance - take them with you. You will be charged by a lady otherwise. It’s a muddy stream. The rocks are a beautiful colour but far from magical. Litter and general untidiness spoils what could be a lovely place. Disappointing.
흐르는 흙물에 죽은쥐와 쓰레기가 떠다녔다..다시는가고싶지않음가는 내내 죽은쥐 시체만 생각남...왜 요정의 샘일까?너무 억지같다
听名字，感觉不错，但是到了地方发现就是一个垃圾遍地的一条小溪流，脏脏的，不会再来第二次，美奈我觉得也没有什地方好玩的，整个海滩都是脏脏的。
Be careful. Depending on the entrance you chose you will have a „guide“, that is doing nothing, but will charge you 100.000 dong, before you get your shoes back. The place is beautiful.
We visited this place on a fair sunny day. However, the stream is just a dirty polluted pond.They even charge 30000vnd for 10 minutes car park.Totally recommend for kid!
like what the other posters have mentioned, young children who should be at school hanging around you for money is very annoying.i did tip them a dollar and they told me "not enough!"

We went here as part of the Jeep Sand Dunes Tour. Yes it was nice views and the walk through the streams were refreshing but we had locals following us trying to beg for money which ruined the vibe. Not only that, there were some locals who stopped us from entering the Fairy Stream unless we take off our shoes so we did out of respect and gesture; upon return, the local demands money and states " i look after your shoes" which I thought was a scam. I wouldn't recommend any friends and family here as it's not worth it.

Не понимаю зачем ходить на этот ручей!!!!!! Воняет ужасно примерно метров 300, далее запаха нет, но вода становится глубже. Чтобы дойти до так называемого водопада, либо идёшь по супер грязной реке, где на дне всякая хрень; либо по горячему песку сбоку, но при этом ещё и скалолазанием придётся заняться. Водопад - это не водопад, водопадик, подходя к которому начинает ужасно вонять. По пути какие то кафешки, вьетнамцы ржут над теми, кто идёт по реке, снимают видео. Короче, я категорически не советую ездить на этот так называемый ручей.

Rubbish! Looks good in pics but in reality is just a muddy old stream with cows pooing in it. One of the worst sights I’ve been to in SE Asia so far.Also the 5000 charge to go in is a farce - go down the riverbank and you can walk in for free. There’s really nothing to pay for, they’re taking money for nothing, not maintaining it, there’s trash everywhere.If I could give it 0 stars I would!

MUI NE et ses environs sont à voir mais ne nécessite pas un séjour très long. Dommage que ce ne soit pas entretenu , des sachets plastiques étant éparpillés un peu partout. Nous avons déploré ce manque de propreté

Si scende e si risale, o viceversa, il corso del fiumiciattolo in mezz'ora. Originariamente doveva essere un posto interessante e picevole. Oggi, eccetto pochi metri, il percorso é costeggiato di bancarelle e bar, posizionati anche sul corso del ruscello, edificati alla peggio con mottoni di cemento e sacchi di plastica riempiti di terra. Ad un certo punto devi passare un bar ristorante con musica alta e karaoke. Immondizia un pó ovunque cone nel resto della sporchissima Mui Né. Il posto piú sporco che da nord a sud ho visitato in Vietnam. Nella parte piú alta del ruscello l'acqua é di un colore piú sul nero e si sentono cattivi odori come di fogna. La parte piú in alto, dove c'é una piccola cascata é forse l'angolo migliore e piú tdanquillo. La parte piú in basso, vicino la strada, é un vero squallore.
Carino ma pieno di rifiuti e cacca di mucca ..Belli i colori e la natura ...Lo.consiglio solo se non avete di meglio da fare.
