Devi pagare il pizzo di 10000 dong a un bambino che ti guarda le ciabatte, inizi a risalire quest'acqua di dubbia provenienza destreggiandoti tra un fiume di cinesi che fa selfie. Arrivi in cima dopo circa 15 minuti e torni indietro. Bella, bella, bella scimitá!
Its a joke, to get there you have to wade up a small stream with all sorts of crap & garbage on the banks. Taking your good health into consideration give this one a miss

Du går 1300 meter längts med rinnande vatten 10 cm djup.  För att se ett litet vattenfall. Sen får du gå tillbaka igen. Om inte utflykten har hämtning på slutet. Denna utflykt gav mig absolut ingenting..
Wir waren Gestern dort, auf der ganzen länge rechts und links nur Müll.....Fast am Anfang wurden zudem noch in der prallen Sonne 3 Staußenmänchen zum reiten angeboten.......BITTE meiden!!
What to say..... only rubbish. Don't waste your time, you will be disappointed and will not be able to bring back a picture without tons of plastic bag.Really sad because it could be nice.
Schön sind sie nicht gerade, dafür total überlaufen. Vorsichtig es wird ungerechtfertigerweise Eintritt verlangt. Der Junge wurde ziemlich böse als wir nicht zahlen wollten
Uninspired - walk through dirty stream Sands beautiful and some lime stone colonies Lots of tourists and def not worth it Saw our first beggar - which was sad

Я была в шоке откучи мусора который находится в этом каньене. Мы спустились туда прошли 10 метров и вернулись обратно ,я даже не стала делать фото там. Может быть в сезон дождей это место выглядит прекрасно но в сухой сезон это просто помойка.

Fairy Springs is all nice and all (No entrance fees! Cooling water, not dirty, enjoy a Coconut drink as you walk)... but beware of those men under a bridge who ask you to place your shoes with them. I wanted to take my shoes with me but with their limited English, it seems they're telling you to leave your shoes with them. When you've enjoyed the Springs, they'll demand 50,000 VND for "looking after your shoes". We said no and were shouted at. Terrible. We were there for < 30 min maybe. Worse still, our Muine Go driver didn't bother to assist us. SO TAKE YOUR SHOES WITH YOU, DON'T BOTHER LEAVING IT THERE. People like these spoil my impressions of Vietnam. Vietnam is beautiful, its people more often than not NO. Very Disappointing.

Dette sted har bestemt været skønt engang - vi oplevede affald og for at opnå at tjene penge er der flere små steder der sælger drikkevarer. I iver for at etablere disse steder har naturen måtte betale desværre. Skuffelsen var stor efter at have læst flere gode anmeldelser herinde.

It is a beautiful and large park, but there was nothing to do. Very few visitors and almost no attractions open. We came especially for the crocodiles, but this was closed due to maintenance work. That is unfortunate, but nothing can be done about it. When we went to the pool, we were not let in, because we were only two, we were only allowed in if we were at least four people, because there was nobody inside.  It was a very disappointing day!!
물이 깨끗하지 않고.. 찝찝합니다.끝까지 가면 뭐가 있겠지 하고 갔는데.. 제일 더럽습니다..
물 색깔은 이름과는 다르게 흙탕물 이에요 개울 옆 산 역시 특별하지 않았습니다. 이곳을 방문하는 이유는 지프투어시 필수 방문 코스이기 때문일겁니다
물에 옷만 젖고 볼게 없음물도 불결하고무이네 이렇게 해서는 ...그냥 네이버 블로고만 보세요
Walk up a dinky little stream, watch Chinese tourists fall over, walk back, Leave. It's part of the jeep tour, but it was forgettable.

Non raccomandiamo questa esperienza, dato che un buon tratto del ruscello che si attraversa a piedi nudi è costellato di rifiuti. La parte del percorso che vale la pena visitare e fotografare non giustifica la visita.
Der er affald alle steder. Det ødelægger oplevelsen fuldstændigt. Generelt er jeg meget skuffet over Vietnams mangel på at holde landet fri for affald.
Господа, это речка, в которую сливаются все какашки, если вам нравятся вьетнамцы, готовые вас обмануть на каждые 10 vnd, welcome, ноги моей не будет в этом грязном месте

My guide left me unattended among several dogs and just showed me "this way". My guide's name was Quyet and his right mobile number was 0975516611 - he was just a driver not a guide at all, please stay away from him. There is no pathway to walk by the muddy (NOT FAIRY) weak water. Not good for older people. I did not want to step on muddy water blindly with a lot of dogs around, I did not feel myself safe. If there is a waterfall I did not see it at the end. Disappointing.

visited there on rainy season, so we can understand why it was all murky and muddy. that doesnt explain why the sides of the creek are full of garbage! its so dirty up to the point that we had whole rubbish bags float past us un the way.in addition to that, while we were wating for our jeep driver at the shop located at the entrance from the road, the shop owner was clearing all the waste from his shop - right into the river! including rotten coconut shells that smelt like a dead body...  awful experience. we recommend avoiding this place.

We were really disappointed to see the state this place has gone to after previously visiting 4 years ago and it being reasonably untouched then, this time locals were dumping rubbish into the stream as were tourists, the walls had graffiti all over it in Chinese and Vietnamese this time as well.
Ручей Фей - рязный загаженный ручей на окраине Муйнэ и одна из главных достопримечательностей. Туда продаются экскурсии, хотя  можно дойти пешком или на автобусе за 9 даньгов.
nothing to say ... just dirty and too much business surrounding ... seems to much eople want to participate without doing something for there as it need really a clean up
Мы действовали по рекомендациям друзей, которые были 2 года назад. И в поисках Ручья Фей, нашли мусорку.(((

Вы спускаетесь вниз в ручей и метров 300 идёте по ручью где по сторонам обычная помойка с Ее прекрасным запахом , даже вонь от страусов на которых вас предлагают покатать по дороге не перебивает этот запах.  Основной путь ручья упирается в водопад который представляет собой перепад небольшой высоты где вода не может пока проточить камень . Основная красота это красный песок с левой стороны по ходу ручья , так называемые красные дюны . Радует что это бесплатная замануха , ну а если с кого берут за это деньги то это классный развод !

Samen met mijn vriend hiernaartoe geweest. Gratis entree maar naar mijn mening moeten ze geld toegeven. Een gids is niet nodig want het is alleen rechtdoor. Zoals de titel omschrijft vond mijn vriend het mooi maar ik vreselijk Het is enorm goor en daardoor zie je de pracht niet meer. Beekje wordt gebruikt als een soort riool. Veel afval waaronder gebroken glas en een drol met afwisselend veel plastic. Je moet door het beekje op blote voeten wat voor mij toch een extra gore beleving was. Wellicht ben ik wat te hypochondrie. Ik dacht constant aan het krijgen van een infectie door in het glas te staan met daarna tig vuil. Onderweg kan je op een ostrich rijden. Enorm zielig lijkt me dat, maar heb dat dus niet gedaan dus kan er niets meer over vertellen.
Actually a nice place but unfortunately very dirty as people throw away their rubbish and destroy a beautiful landscape.

If you've ever wondered what it's like to walk through a landfill site then the fairy streams for you. A stream with a sandy bottom that you can walk through with some coloured sand on the side and a tonne of rubbish floating past your feet. About as interesting as a stroll through a builders merchants looking at various aggregate.

Esta visita fue parte de nuestro tour, es una quebrada en medio de dos lomas que baja por casas, está muy sucio, lleno de gente. Es lindo el color de la arena y es muy diferente a lo que se ve normalmente. Pero no vale la pena para nada!
A small ankle deep stream that leads to no where. Don't even bother stopping. Head to the beach for a swim instead.

The biggest joke in mui ne. Probably a guy once decided to make a tourist attraction from his little backyard canal. Unbelievably filthy water, with glass, plastic, cans, chairs and human waist. Nothing special to see either.In short, just a waste of time.

We went as part of a day tour around Mui Ne. We were dropped off at a car park and proceeded to follow bus loads of Chinese tourists up a sandy stream. The stream was dirty with rubbish all over the banks. We got to some sandy banks and turned back and had lunch. If we missed some hidden cultural meaning we apologise but we weren't told and there're nothing explaining what it is!

It is essentially a 20 feet wide ankle deep stream on a bed of sand Feels nice to walk in it, wouldn't loose out on much if you give it a miss. Quite underwhelming really, but given Mui Ne is slim of tourist spots this should do.

A lot of trash in the begining, the stream seems to be used as an open air sewer or dump. You can find plastic bags , broken tiles and glass. Charming.....There is less trash after the begining .  And then at some point...wait for it...You can ride...an ostrich .....Seriously.....So i do not recommend going there exept if you like walking barefoot in trash and if you like animal cruelty...

The idea of a quiet outdoor experience is great but this doesn't really deliver.  Must walk through littered area.  Ironic.  Then, the stream is filled with tourists and locals selling various things that take away from the idea of a quiet, peaceful natural area.  Sad to see chained up monkey and promotion of rides available in the river on an ostrich.
This place is terrible - you just walk in ankle deep water along a standard river bank and see a few sand formations, totally not worth the visit!

Full of sand bags and full with woven plastic, something makes me think this is man made!! Litter covers the banks. You see people traipsing back down past you with faces like 'is that really it!!?!?' Don't waste your time!!

The sign said 500 m until waterfall, - if this is true, it was deff. the longest 500 m I have ever walked. The walk along the stream was very pleasant and beautiful, but at some streches very crowded. At the end of the stream we saw a small waterfall, and were attacked by small local children playing in the water. They splashed water on turists and had a violent behaviore towards them. I saw a little girl grab several mens arms and then started to cry hysterically when they tryed to get away, and she was making movements with her little fist towards me and others. We walked away very fast and felt a little bit sad for those local children.

The stream begins opposite a dump.  I walked back up to the nearest  cafe and waited until our   tour  guide  was ready  to go. Apparently  there was ostrich  riding further  up which I don't  agree  with either.

Fairy Stream såg väldigt vackert ut men efter vi hade redan betalat 50 USD för rundturen (och alla ställen vi stannade skulle kosta extra) skulle vi betala här 100.000 VND/person. Det kändes så där och vi bestämde att inte göra det.

Not sure how so many people visit this place without noticing the 15+ sewage outfalls pouring into the 'stream'. All water from people's toilets, sinks and kitchens ends up here. Besides the horrendous amount of trash, tour groups were urinating in the water and small children were splashing around in the filth. In all my travels this is the most disgusting thing I have ever done. Not to mention the horrid animal abuse along the way: ostrich rides and old gibbon monkeys chained to a tree. The scenery is fine but this place is a serious health hazard. I cannot imagine the infections and diseases possible from walking here barefoot. AVOID.

Ebenso wie Sanddünen, absolut nichts besonders, man watet ewig einen kleinen Bach hoch und am Ende ist ein nicht so toller Wasserfall. Steingebilde auf der Seite ganz nett aber nicht unbedingt einen Besuch wert.
very boring walk along a litter strewn river. Not worth going to see better to spend a day at the beach.

This was the most pathetic tourist attraction I have ever seen. You have to walk up a stream that's so murky from all the sand so god knows what else is in there. We walked up far enough and saw nothing of note. I don't know if we were misinformed but we were expecting a waterfall at the end, this is literally nothing more than a small river

the so called fairy stream is a place I would recommend to avois at all cost! there were cows everywhere, which use this stream as a toilet, litter and the constant fear of meeting a snake in the water of the stream

Любителям фоточек может и понравиться, но до сих пор не поняла, зачем мы туда пошли. Да, ландшафт интересный, песок красного цвета. При спуске в "ручей" понимаешь почему много туристы в отзывах его называю речка-вонючка. так оно и есть. До определенного момент идти прикольно, но метров через 200 после минизоо глубина увеличивается, и далее - либо в брод по пояс, либо в обход по песчаным холмам. Приятного мало и в том и в другом. Одежда испорчена. песок-то ярко рыжего цвета. Но если вы преодолели все преграды, зрелище, которое вы увидите вас поразит: " И это все?!" Да, друзья, тот скудный поток высотой 2 метра и толщиной с палец ребенка - водопад. Фотайте быстрей, пока не иссяк!

Waste of time. There is a lot of garbage along the way and at the very end there is a poor excuse of a waterfall.It is a part of the standard tour unfortunately. We had to see it before going to the dunes.

Like other people have written, this stream is a complete waste of time. It's a tourist filled stream which you walk up and can see some cows in/next to the stream, and some coloured rock and sand on one side of the stream. You may also have the pleasure of being shouted at loudly by a local trying to demand extra money from your groups, we had this today, the rudest person we've met in Vietnam so far. I guess if you're on an organised group tour you have no choice but to go here, but if you have the choice, I'd recommend skipping it.
Đường vào bẩn, toàn rác, khi ngang qua nhà sản xuất nước mắm  mùi khó chịu. Thất vọng nhất là khi nhìn thấy suối cạn nước, rác ở bên bờ như là nơi để hoang chứ không phải địa điểm du lịch.

The rotten smell of  fermentation pots accompanies you through a muddy trickle. You walk barefeet through the waters, where cattle is defecating alongside. Hords of tourists make fotographs of the mediocre landscape nearly impossible.
The stream is dirty with garbage and cow feces. Sure, the red sand cliff is sort of nice, but not worth walking barefoot through the dirty stream for. Skip this and head to the sand dunes instead.

На красочных рекламах всё выглядит впечатляюще. А по факту сильно загаженный ручей с микроскопическим водопадом. Ваше посещение начнется с захода в кучу мусора с ужасным трупным запахом. Хорошо, если вы приехали самостоятельно на байке, экскурсию брать 100% не советую. Когда-то это действительно было красивое место с живописными скалами, но оно очень сильно пострадало от туристов и местных жителей. Сравнивали мои фотографии и фотографии друзей которые были здесь полтора года назад. У них чистота и ни единого намека на мусор, у нас самая настоящая свалка. Могу посоветовать посещение этого места только если вы хотите устроить фотосессию. Но до водопада доходить все равно не стоит.

Don't bother - it's a walk through a trickle of a stream to some rather unimpressive stalagmites. Also don't wash your feet at the place where you enter/exit the steam otherwise the guy will aggressively try to charge you for the pleasure! We witnessed him and a lady loudly harassing (and grabbing) a poor tourist who had done this.

Đến suối tiên nhạt nhẽo không thể tả nổi.Chỉ là dòng nước chảy, mọi người đi lại trên dòng nước đó, thỉnh thoảng có đợt gió cát thổi qua bui mù.Đi mãi mà không thấy một cảnh nào hay ho...Nếu có lần sau sẽ không bao h quay lại suối tiên.

The stream it's self is beautiful so definitely take a walk. But stay away from the zoo/cafe and the ostrich riding. At first we thought it would be funny to ride an ostrich and check out the monkeys, but if you go, you will quickly see that the monkeys are on chains 3 feet long and the ostriches have no desire to run with you. There is ostrich riding all over Vietnam GO ELSE WHERE it's over priced and the people running it literally run behind you pushing the bird to move. Not just to start, but the entire length of your one lap. Under the best of circumstances these attractions are morally questionable. But when a curious monkey doesn't have enough leash to climb the base of a tree and the ostriches need to be pushed. Its not even fun.
Worst thing in vietnam? You literally walk down a tiny muddy stream filled with rubbish. After about 40 minutes you see the smallest waterfall in the history of mankind... Thats it.

Cannot for the life of me work out what this place is meant to be and why people like going there?? It is a filthy puddle of water full of rubbish and cow dung with an ostrich riding attraction and a disgusting zoo where they keep beautiful animals caged up in tiny boxes. Waste of time and effort. Seriously don't bother.

This should be a nice attraction, if locals took care of the place. Rubbish and animal poos are everywhere along the way. Vandals on the wall is noticeable.So disappointing seeing beautiful place like this turns into waste.

Visiting the fairy stream was included with my Jeep sand dune tour. If you enjoy walking through a barely flowing stream with rubbish floating past amongst average scenery than go to the fairy stream...my recommendation would be to spend your time on the beautiful beaches instead.

Hier versucht man wirklich bei jeder Gelegenheit den Touristen das Geld aus der Tasche zu ziehen. Die angeketteten, leidenden Affen geben mir noch den Rest. Ganz Mui Ne hätten wir uns sparen können. Never again.

Maybe when you are on your first travel ever this can be "nice" or maybe even good. But for all experienced travellers, dont have any expectations and only go if you pass. Lot of litter everywhere, loads of tourist and lets be honest.....its just a small river with red sand on the bottom..
You have to walk in the dirty river / spring and there is nothing to see.Except for all the rubbish on the shore and some stones on the side.Not a lot of fun

saya berjalan selama kurang lebih 2km dan ternyata sampai akhir saya tidak menemukan sesuatu yang luar biasa dari tempat ini selain aliran sungai dangkal yang dikelilingi bebatuan dan terdapat air terjun yang sangat kecil dan biasa di akhir perjalanan. saya sangat kecewa dengan tempat ini

I've rarely told myself that was a waste of 25 cents but this was! Sure it's got some natural beauty but nothing I haven't seen somewhere else. We got dropped off by our tour guide. It was the last stop of the tour and we also needed to pay to enter. A few minutes later the people I was with all looked at each other. We agreed we felt ripped off for a U.S. quarter. If you're not on the tour skip it completely. I used the large hill as another opportunity to get in a hill workout. There was also a blind woman begging along the path who we all felt guilty and gave change to. The entire stop felt like a waste of time and money. It's not the amount. It's the principle of the matter! You have been warned!

It was... I don't know. I can't really tell. I don't know if what I saw is really it. They charge for the entry. You can clearly see that they are just people around there block the gate and ask for money. You walk along this very little river and thats it.
Horrible muddy/sandy walk along a slow moving streamGive it a big miss and stay in your resort.Very disappointing to say theleast

Около входа сильно "пахнет",но оно и понятно, страусы,коровы. На входе с вас потребуют 5000 донгов за охрану тапок , котрые вам скажут снят, НЕ СНИМАЙТЕ иначе можно поранить ноги. Дальше к вам подойдет вьетнамец , и будет вам говорить куда идти,  хотя дорога там только одна , это типа гид , но он ничего не рассказывает , через 15 мин хотьбы по ручью с коровами, скакания по камням и лицизрения гор из песка (довольно интерсных) , "гид" скажет вам "ван хенгрет" и пока вы не заплатите 100 000 донг он не отвяжется. Кстати потом мы поехали в другие экскурсионные места и там с нас конечно же тоже пытались требовать ванхангред

I still wonder what was I doing here. It's just litter dump, not much more. One cannot see the natural beauty beyond this garbage. I just couldn't stop thinking how many unknown infections and diseases one can catch over here. Looked like a paradise for hepatitis and staphylococcus.
If you've never been out of your home town then I guess it's worth a visit it was like paddling up an open sewer!!? Avoid!!! Have a beer instead, go and watch the kite surfers on the beach,

5,000VND, per person, to walk up a muddy river. I suppose there is the opportunity to jump on a defeated ostrich (100,000VND) for half a minute, or visit the "Popular Cafe" (severely unpopular) and it's display of half-dead fish. Each to their own; some people do enjoy it :)

When we arrived on site we were given hell by the parking attendant. We couldn't get any peace. We still wandered in because we heard good things before. But, then the place was a landfill. Absolute disaster. We were disgusted, so we went back to our scooters. But, the parking attendant in his anger stole our rented helmets! There was no way to get our helmets back in the middle of nowhere. Our rental company was pissed at us and we had to getting seen riding helmetless until we could buy some more. Stay away at all costs!
We got to the entrance, smelled the putrid smells, saw the amount of trash in the stream, and walked away. It could be beautiful further down, but we really didn't want to continue through the trash.
Such a shame this could be a much nicer place to visit if it was cleaned up but unfortunately the rubbish spoils it and does not lend itself to the natural beauty .

As part of a jeep tour to sand dunes we stopped outside an alley and told by our driver we had 40mins and he pointed "that way" through the alley and past a stinking fish factory or something similar. Get to a handwritten sign on a tree stating 5000dong entry fee a teenager collecting money. Walk along a creekbed with a trickle of dirty water in it being tailed by a kid about 12yrs who kept asking to take a photo of us we declined. Just as we get back to start point the kid demands 100,000dong for being our guide, when I said no he gestured the older kids were to beat me up, I still said no and got some angry abuse from him. As with just about everywhere there was rubbish strewn around and locals oblivious to it. The horrible stink was sickening. If you do a jeep tour be aware that there are hidden costs/entry fees and the jeep driver is not a guide and has limited English. Best to ask booking agent to bypass this place and go straight to sand dunes which also has an entry fee.

I've read many reviews before really going to Fairy Spring and I should had followed the negative ones...you have absolutely nothing interesting to see there besides mud! You have to walk barefoot over a place where there's animal poo, and when you get there, there's a sign asking you to pay 5.000 vnd; for the unadvised ones, it may seen you have to pay for the sight, but actually this is what the family whom owns a house near the Fairy will charge to take care of your shoes! Just take them off and carry yourself. We also saw a guy from this same family encouraging his pet dog to attack and kill a little kitten and I felt so bad for it, cause the poor cat was really hurt and the only thing I could do was shout at the dog so it could leave the kitten, but it didn't work. Sad!
Not worth to visit unless you have extremely a lot of time. Nothing special and not really nice. Rather spend more time at the fishing village where you can capture more nice photos.

Поехали сами на мопеде,хорошо что не платили за эту экскурсию!!! В муине берут 15$...жесть, смотреть точно не начто , тапки оставляешь на входе 5тыс хранение -мзда у местных не оставляйте мой совет потому то дальше вам без них будет трудновато,идешь по речке по щиколотку -да прикол ные дюны красно белые песок,бычки какают и писают в речку рядом,дальше уже мал экстремизм 0,5м глубиной и через берег и конечно местн магазин, водопад... Если это можно так назвать 3 м высотой и купающиеся вьеты в грязной воде.. Страусы на кот покататься 200 тыс донгов 1 круг маленький -ну если только для галочки-замученые они какие то..да кстати попасть туда можно ипо объездной асфальт дороге там мост и метрах в 50 этот водопад и все видно и идти не надо 800 м в пред крушении" данного чуда"

Don't bother. It smells of death on the way down, they charge 150 dong for the Ostrich ride which lasts 30 seconds and they abuse the animals. Horrible to see. The stream you walk in isn't that pretty and although it's quite colourful, there's nothing much to see.

please make sure that the attendant - old lady resets the gas meter before filling up.We stopped with motor bike and there were many filling up she was taking Dong from each. Then when I filled up she wanted me to pay total which was 240,000 this is enough for 10 litres.As I was with another bike I was not sure how much they put in their bike. It turned out to be 3 litres tops. + 1 litre in my bike. So I was stooged! Need to be very careful with this one. My guess is she pockets the money from the station owner. Waits for a group to come in with a white guy be it Russian, US, Brit or Aussie then get them to pay the total.

I went to the Fairy stream as I hired a private jeep for 4-5 hrs doing the sunset dunes.The Fairy stream was included as the first stop and I would advise you to give it a miss if you want to spend more time at the dunes as there was really not much to see. And there were so many locals trying to "show" you the way (it's just a straight path) and ask money from you at the end of it. The guy that followed us ask for VND 100k and we refuse to pay him and offered VND20k. He got aggressive, glared at us, called us bad people and even bumped into me. I flat out refuse to give him a single cent then, and just walked away. There were many tourist ard so I thought he won't dare to harm us. Overall a bad experience.

We decided to go to the stream against our better judgement. As others have said: the stream is dirty; you are unsure of what you are walking on in the filthy water; you are harrased by kids wanting to be yor guide and the mud gets really deep in places (I almost lost my sandals). The waterfall at the end is disappointing and not worth the effort.Avoid it
dirty little stream full of rubbish. It's unfortunate that its not taken care of as it would be nice if it weren't for the filth.

I decided  to go and see this otherwise lovely place. Two of local ,,tour guide,, join me and on the end of 10 minutes walk to the most poorest waterfall(if we can call it waterfall) they want 400 000,- VD for guiding. I refused because that is most expensive tour I had in my whole life. when we came back to the starting point that was lots of locals shouting at me how much I will give him. I quickly start walking to the enter where I had motorbike. Band of local followed me to the entry and still want their money. I sad I will pay, but normal price. Gave him 20 000. Group of locals come closer and start shouting at me. Luckily for me I managed drive away. If you go there Do not let anybody walk with you. You can easy walk this lovely place by yourself with NO Guide need. It said that most of Vietnamese people think you are walking money and If you traveler it mean you have a lot to give them for nothing.

The brochure made this look really great unfortunately the reality was very different. If you want to walk down a very smelly alley and wade through a sewage outlet this is the place for you. The worst part was a guy who attached himself to us uninvited, to be our tour guide and then demanded money. We thought ok we didn't tell him to go away so a tip is in order. When we offered him an amount he demanded a considerable sum more and became very angry when we wouldn't pay what he wanted. We did give him a tip but not what he demanded.

we visited this place as part of the tour we booked to see the sand dunes. Nothing to do except to keep walking along some streams. Maybe its because from where we come from, we have access to such sandy stream easily.

Well...I must admit I didn't know what to expect when I was booked to visit the fairy spring.  Perhaps my experience would have been better if we went earlier in the day.  Depending on the time of year, be prepared for a lot of direct sunlight and heat.  Definitely cover-up and wear sunscreen.  Bring water.  And if you don't mind exposing yourself to cow dung, have at it.After a short walk from the main road, you take off your shoes and walk barefoot through running water over soft sand.  There are a bunch of local teenagers who will "watch" your shoes for what amounts to about 75 cents.  The short hike is easy.  There are interesting rock formations along the way.  However...(1) it is pretty crowded with other tourists - this really takes away from the serenity of the activity and it's hard to take pictures because there will always be a tourist in the shot.  (2) there are many cows and bulls - they too use the fairy stream and are tied up along the stream and herds are run through the area.  They defecate and urinate in the waterways.  Not only must you watch out for stepping in cow patties, you also are likely stepping right in to them when you cannot see the stream bottom.  Moreover, your feet are swimming in whatever cow-patty-run-off is coming from up-stream.  This is not a good situation and not healthy.  Had we known there were was livestock everywhere - we would have NEVER done this activity. (Livestock exposure - especially directly with their feces and urine can be very dangerous.)Finally, the small waterfall at the end of the hike was anti-climatic and crowded.  I never like posting negative reviews but the potential exposure to livestock waste makes this risky.
на реке много змей, в пресной воде есть паразиты, которые могут попасть под кожу, будьте осторожны...

Ignore the kids who tell u to deposit your shoes. They are quiet persistents. Even if you walk along the stream, kids will follow up and start asking where you are from and start to be your tour guide. I repeat :Ignore them!!!!. They wanted to charge us VND 500,000. Digusted!!

I visited the fairy stream as the last stop of a jeep tour me and my friends booked. The route in is rather hidden, past a lizard farm and ostrich farm (be careful not to step on the chicken droppings on your way in!). The entire fairy stream was rather underwhelming. In my country, we would call this a "longkang" (drain). We walked past some white and red cliffs along the way but I have seen more impressive structures elsewhere. Along the way, we saw some of the local kids showing some tourists the mimosas (whose leaves close when touched) growing along the stream. In my country, we consider that plant a weed and definitely not a tourist attraction. However, I must admit the experience of walking along the stream was therapeutic. Imagine yourself walking along a drain and there you have it, Fairy Stream!
Dont bother going....was not impressive in the least bit
just a pleasant stroll along a gully carved by stream. would not make a special effort to see this. Usually part of trip to dunes

Mình thấy chỗ này không có gì đặc sắc hay do mình ko đi vào sâu ko biết nữa. Chỉ thấy có con suối, được cái là dưới cát cũng mịn lội mát chân. Nhưng thấy bình thường, đi vô tầm 10-15 phút không thấy có gì nên mình đi ra lại.

Nếu đây được coi là một điểm du lịch có thu phí thì mình đánh giá quá tệ luôn. Nhưng rất tiếc lại là điểm du lịch không mất phí.Cảnh quan khá đẹp khi vào sâu bên trong tuy nhiên điều gây mất thiện cảm của mình là ở lối đi vào bên trong suối, rác nhiều quá kèm thêm dân địa phương thả bò, chăn bò nên các bạn thử nghĩ mà xem, khách thăm quan lội bì bõm (mực nước chỉ đến mắt cá chân) còn phân bò thì thi thoảng có 1 bịch to đùng...buồn luôn
初见之时，你会觉得很恶心，这不是一条臭水沟嘛，下去之后，脚下踩着的是细软的沙子，水也是清澈，沿途溪水旁有不少垃圾（向前看，不然你会恶心到自己，但溪水本身始终清澈。）溪本身很长，本人走了23分钟，走到一处平台以为到头了，回去后才知道尽头有小瀑布，还得走个3分五分钟，两旁的山岩呈现红色，也有红沙，还有类似椰林的地方，如果没有两旁的垃圾，真的还挺有一番风味的

A place worth to visit but beware of the locals who walk with you through the stream. They will ask for tips at the end of the tour. My so called 'tour guide' ask for 300k vnd for tips. I was shock and i gave his 40k vnd at the end. I did not expect that he will refuse my money when i give him 20k initially. You dont really need a tour guide to walk through the stream and its quite safe.

The further in you walk the better. While it is nice to walk along the stream, there are trash everywhere. The entrance to the stream at a bridge smells kind of bad. I hope it was not due to someone relieved themselves the previous night.Been there, done that, but not coming back.
Nothing to see except you can walk along the stream since the water very shallow...and some odd looking stone.
Not special at all and not as beautiful as in the picture. But street food here is worthy to try. That called Banh Trang

Наш отель в Муй Не, поэтому до ручья дошли пешком, до мостика, а дальше по воде вверх до водопада. Чтобы не нести обувь в руках, взяли с собой рюкзак. Сначала бросается в глаза мусор, но потом как-то перестаешь его замечать. Вода приятно прохладная, дно песчаное. Ручей образовал что-то вроде мини каньона в песчаной почве и на склонах четко видны слои красного, белого и желтого песка - зрелище конечно интересное. Дошли до водопада, все сфотографировали. В любом случае, посещение ручья разнообразит отдых.
Мне нечего сказать, там можно просто погулять и добраться до ручья самостоятельно-это не сложно даже на обычном автобусе. Платить как за экскурсию я бы не стала
Lovely to walk, water warm but everywhere dirt - what a shame. you can hardly find the waterfall as signage very poor, than you walk through a very neglected dirty "restaurant".
ต้องเดินผ่านลำธารเล็กๆ น้ำตื้นสีขุ่นๆไปตามทางจะเห็นแอ่งหินที่ถุกน้ำกัดเซาเป็นรูปร่างแปลกๆ คนเยอะมาก ต้องคอยหามุมถ่ายรูปเอง หินไม่ได้สวยตื่นตาอะไรขนาดนั้น

You have to walk about 2-3 km to see some sightseeings. In my opinion, its not worth to walk an hour to see some "red dune" not really beautiful. The water of the stream sometimes is not clean because of some shops at the bank.
騎電單車十分鐘可以到的地方，不太遠，入口很不起眼，是在紅沙小溪中赤腳走，兩旁是沙岩，紅黃白很多色彩，全程十幾二十分鐘，可以拍些照片，有點小單調.......會有當地人自動幫你導游，會要小費很正常，不過開口就要400k就有點過份了

Lower reaches of the stream would be not so clean and with some bad smell from the zoo. The view's quite good when it's sunny and the sky's clear. I would be worth a visit if you've got plenty of time.
무이네에서 가장 멋진 곳이다. 신발을 벗고 시원한 물 위를 걸으며 멋진 주변 경관을 보고 힐링하고 싶으면 강력 추천. 무이네 방문자라면 꼭 방문하길 바란다.

I visited there on 2 April 2017. It was part of the itinerary of the local tour.It took me about thirty minutes to walk to and fro in the stream. I do not think it is worthy to walk through the whole stream, it is good to take photo in certain spot.It is no harm to skip in your itinerary.
Bus from centre of Mui nee strip, drops off at start of walk that took about 15 mins, plenty of places along the way for refreshment, if you can't be bothered to walk back taxis available.
We went to this place in the afternoon, long walking in the red water of the stream.. Mostly the red water is just small deep, can walk slowly...looking surrounding will see the rock wall.

Взяли байк, поехали сами. Были определённые ожидания от дюн и озера лотосов. Приехали рано утром, как писали в путеводителях, что бы застать распускание лотосов. И что мы видим? Озеро, самое обыкновенное, и пару лотосов на нем. Реально пару. И мусор от кафе у берегов озёра. Но, что бы туристы смогли посмотреть лотосы, создали искусственную лужу и насажали их там ( фото прилагаю). Дюны хороши только до красивых фото. Чуть дальше от основной дороги можно найти кучи сами знаете чего ))
Бродя по Ручью Фей испытываешь странные чувства. Мешанина из песчаных карьеров детства, уменьшенной копии отрогов Гранд Каньона и декораций фильма о Марсе!Однозначно стоит пройтись!
Der Spaziergang durch den "red caynon" und den Bach ist rech amüsant und hilft bei Fusspilz. Also als medizinische Aktivität ansehen.
I was there in the morning (but it was very hot already)It seems to be just a normal stream and water was not very cleanThis is not my favorite place in Mui Ne
만일 한국에서 이런 곳을 관광코스로 삼았다면 욕먹을 것같아요 그래서 베트남에서만 경험할수있는 곳이죠.특히 초입은 구정물같고 냄새도 나지요.그러나 인내심을 갖고 가다보면 붉은 모래와 석회모래같은 것에 이채로움을 느끼게 되지요. 점토같기도 하고 신기한 느낌에 아이들도 좋아해요.큰 기대없이 한시간 정도 선선히 걷는다 생각하면 좋을듯 해요
A nice place for walk but the quality of water is not looks good. Some bubbles are floating and drain pipes poured out water to the stream

До них можно доехать из любого места Муй-не на 1 или 9 автобусе,ехать до конечной остановки в противоположную сторону от Фантьента. Можно полюбоваться красивым закатом. Рядом много кафе и ресторанчиков.

There is a sign saying 5000dong entrance fee but I am informed by many people it is free and the 5000dong is for looking after your shoes. Personally, I wouldn't mind paying some money if they used it to clean the trash up. There is plastic rubbish everywhere and I would guess the water is none too clean either. It's sort of interesting to see the erosion of the sand dune by the river, but at the end of the day, you are just walking up a stream bed like any other.It took about 20 mins to walk up to a couple of Karaoke bars which were going strong with local Vietnamese pop idol type rubbish. I didn't see the small waterfall, as I like everyone else turned around rather than endure the aural onslaught of the local crooners.

Easy to get to but a little hidden until you know where it is.Worth a visit because it's practically free and the snad and rock features are interesting.Watch out for the local guy who wants to walk with you and be your unofficial guide.
В сухой сезон никакого ручья нет. Всё равно красиво. Дешевле обходится самостоятельная поездка . Расположено рядом .

My husband and I came here as part of a day trip to the sand dunes.  You can visit the Fairy Stream on your own. Access to the stream is by the side of the road with steps leading down. It is nice walk through the stream with vivid red and white coloured sands.  You can walk as far as you wish, there is no time limit. Get there early to avoid the crowds.

I know it is a big issue for the country, but having garbage in one of the main touristic spots it is quiet incredible. It spoils all the fashion of what this beautiful country could offer. It is still pleasant to walk in the water in the middle of that "little canyon", but could be much better with really just a bit of effort to clean up the place, it would take 1 day if enough people work on it.

Сходить погулять в ручей конечно можно. Приготовьтесь к хождению босиком. Идти по времени нормально, в нашем случае мы поздно пришли и начало темнеть, поэтому до конца мы идти не стали. Это было правильным выбором, потому что на обратном пути уже было не очень приятно, постоянно думали как бы не наступить в мусор и крысы там бегают иногда. Сама природа интересная, ну такая же как все холмы в этом районе.Да, парковка 10 тыщ донгов, если вы в самом муйне рядом со входом хотите припарковаться.

The place is beautiful. Walking around through the small river gives a feeling that it's not as hot as it is. From the other hand there is a lot of garbage left near to the river. One of the attractions is the 'mini zoo' located in halfway including small monkey chained to the tree which is totally unacceptable. I would really appreciate if the monkey was closed in at least a cage.

This was a pleasant surprise behind the busy road in Mui Ne. Don't book this with a tour company as it's easy to find and only cost 5000 dong each which is 30p just to park the scooter. Anyway the walk is pleasant and through the red sand leave your flip flops at the start. There are various drink stalls and places to eat on the way. We choose a very good place to stop which had a fishing lake and small animal holding. The rubbish an eye sore and annoying that anybody would throw their cans and bottles but the locals don't help this situation as there are no bins on the route and they are probably the culprits! At the end there is a small waterfall and some metal ladders which you can climb and look across the stream. These ladders can get extremely hot! Be careful in bare feet! I'm glad I did this despite some of the reviews on TripAdvisor. Rubbish is a problem everywhere in Vietnam!
很原生态的风景……小溪很浅，刚过脚踝，小溪是黄色的，因为下面是泥土（可能是细沙）游客好多，是日出游的最后一个景点。我们去的时候，有人在放牛，牛群走过来的时候特别壮观（小心牛粪哦）

We visited the Fairy Stream as part of our morning tour. That being said, anyone can visit it anytime and you should enter down under the bridge that crosses the stream as it's free there, not like where the tour guide directs you. They only charge a small fee but you are still paying just to step off the bank into a dirty steam. We walked/waded through the gorge all the way until we reached a small waterfall at the end. The color of the water changes along the way as different sands in the bank are eroded away and wash into the stream. It's a totally different attraction that I didn't expect to see in Vietnam but it ties in well with the sand dune tour and is good spot to get some unique photos.
To much people here and so many kid to get money from tourist.not so well for tourist. You trust come and walk alone is bester

You don't have to take a tour to go here, it's in town. You walk up the stream to the waterfall, about 15 mins each way. The saddest thing is all the trash lying around, heartbreaking, and lots of people.

От скуки дошли от нашего отеля до туда (2,5км). Заранее почитали отзывы про мусор, так вот, все правда, просто жуткая антисанитария. Идешь по ручью, мимо плывут салфетки, пластиковые стаканы и пакеты, детские подгузники (!), фантики и т.д. Когда заходишь в это место, метров 30 вверх жуткая вонь, потом ничего. Вся проблема в том, что туристы сорят, а местным убираться не к чему, им и так нормально. К этому правда надо быть готовым и абстрагироваться. Мы были готовы. Вид поймать хороший можно без проблем. В начале ручья мини-водопад около 4м, ничего особенного. Можно сходить доя разнообразия, на один раз.

Пишу максимально подробно, чтобы вы определились наверняка,посещать ли это место. Были там 3 февраля. Таксист привёз к мосту, спустились под него по ступенькам. Сначала даже идти не хотелось: вода грязная,жёлтого цвета, в ней плывут пакеты, по берегам мусор сплошной, на ветвях деревьев висят потерянные тапочки.Сам ручей шириной наверное не более 2 метров, глубина по щиколотку,редко где больше. Сначала метров 500 шли с тихим ужасом. Вьетнамцы кругом продают сувениры, сидя прямо в воде, тут же готовят местную шаурму, народу тьма тьмущая, были мы где-то в 3:30-4. Были и иностранцы, и местные, много русских. На берегу в начале располагается ферма со страусами, запах там соответствующий. Местные дети прямо купаются в этом ручье, довольные и счастливые. Есть в начале и песчаная горка,по которой можно забраться наверх. Но делать там совсем нечего,дальше прохода нет. Есть и кафе, где столы и стулья стоят в воде. У водопада тоже было много людей, на дне камни в основном. Можно забраться на водопад, рядом есть лестница железная, но ступени погнуты, да и мокрыми ногами железке под наклоном не очень приятно. А вот на обратном пути открылась вся красота этого места,которую ты не можешь разглядеть вначале из-за огромной толпы людей. Виден срез, разные цвета песка: от белого до красного. Различные маленькие ручейки и проч. Да и вода уже ближе к водопаду была чистая, было приятно идти хоть.

Пожалуй это самое многолюдное место, так как оно расположенно в пешей доступности от деревни Муйне. Представляет из себя красные пески, переходящие в горы, между которыми течёт ручеёк. Пройтись по нему с кучей туристов, увидеть страусиную ферму, где тебе предлагают за 150 тысяч донгов сделать кружок по загону на замученном страусе. Чаще весь мусор люди бросают прям в ручей, ну сами понимаете что не чисто.... если нет возможности уехать подальше, то имеет место быть

Entrance fee is 5k vnd and you can leave your slippers and shoes with them if you want to. The stream is quite narrow and touristy at around 11am and it would be nicer if it was earlier. The water from the stream is flowing on a sand that's why it is very easy to walk. As you walk further you will see the red sand slopes or like a canyon view on one side. I think it was nice but what's sad are the trash everywhere. It's a nice walk and view.

Very touristy, much garbage in the stream and in teh sand on which you walk. It has some interesting geology. We gave up and turned before reaching the falls. Bring a towel to dry your feet and water to wash off the sand when you finish.

Es gibt in der Umgebung von Mui Ne nicht allzuviel anzuschauen, der Fairy Stream ist nett, aber wenn man australische Gorges oder die Canyons bspw. in den USA scbon besucht hat, bietet der Fairy Stream nicht mehr viel.....

Went as part of half day trip lovely area and walk through pretty countryside as in many places we visited in Vietnam a place of beauty was tainted by the amount of litter in some are as where there's seems no effort to pick up, locals and tourist alike seem to just drop cans,plastic bottles wrappers where they stand such a shame if you visit please dispose of your litter correctly
딱히 꼭 가봐야 한다고 하기는 어렵다. 시냇물을 따라 걷는 주변경치도 그저그렇고 흘러 오는 물에 가끔 쓰레기들도 흘러오고 막바지쯤 모래 산은 사막투어에서 보면 되니 굳이 반드시 와봐야 한다고 얘기하기는 어렵다
Intersting place for foto & walking.   There are zoo, when you can feed animals.    It's anytime free . It's better to go on sunset
其实我觉得挺一般的，这条小溪名字叫做仙女溪，我在酒店报了一个参观日出的旅行团，平均下来一个人17美元，这是其中的一站。我穿着运动鞋来了，很快发现了自己的失误，这里应该穿着拖鞋来，因为要踩水，所以我就脱下了鞋子。仙女溪是红色的泥土，走在小溪中两侧的景观乏善可陈，但是走到一定程度就可以看到那些卡斯特地貌了，但总的来说，这些地貌不算惊艳，没有很吸引我

Красивое место, но для одного раза, к сожалению. Видимо, из-за того, что вход на сие творение природы бесплатный, местные жители не особо "напрягаются", чтобы убрать мусор от туристов-грязнух. 😔 Водопад просто кишит пластиковыми и жесняными бутылками 😭Ехать лучше на байке самостоятельно, за платную экскурсию будет жалко денег. На входе в ручеёк вьетнамцы предлагают заплатить 5000 донг за "парковку" шлепок) берите с собой, по песку в некоторых местах без них не обойтись.

Basically, cool little walk up a sandy stream into some very pretty red sand canyons. Unfortunately, like most of the area in general (and surprisingly, considering it is reliant on tourism) the Fairy stream is littered by blithering lazy idiot tourists - and no doubt some locals too.   Shame.
Экскурсия не стоит ни рубля! Идите сами пешком! ПО дороге по ручью покатаетесь на страусе, зайдете в контактный зоопарк и конечно же полюбуетесь неземными пейзажами!

Mit Fairys hat allenfalls die schöne Landschaft rund um das Flüsschen zu tun. Man muss allerdings durch braunes,  eklig aussehendes warmes Wasser waten. Ich möchte nicht wissen, was da alles herumschwimmt. Durch den Wind wird auch viel Sand durch die Luft geweht. Sehr unangenehm. Außerdem muss man noch 5000 Vnd pro Person bezahlen, um darin zu waten. Die weißen Dünen haben mehr Ambiente und sind lohnenswerter.

through the stream/creek. gave it a average because of the mess/dirt/plastic/garbage that is just laying around. you want us to visit it but why dump your garbage there. it just takes away the beauty a bit.we also saw a little snake so be care full.The bright red and orange colors are just as awsome as the limestone formations.
Грязновато, но для интересных фото ракурс найти можно. Людей достаточно. Для прогулки - сойдет . Странно, что вход еще не сделали платным)
wenn man nicht den ganzen Tag am Strand rumhängen will und schon den ganzen Rest gemacht hat ist das o.k. weiße Dünen sind jedoch besser

Nou het is omdat er niet veel te doen is in Mui Ne anders was ik er niet eens naar toe gegaan. Je hoeft dit niet te boeken. Je kunt er zo heen en beginnen aan de wandeling door het water naar een watervalletje. Voor kinderen is dit misschien leuk. Je hoeft geen entree te betalen. Ze kunnen geld gaan vragen om op je schoenen te passen. Maar dat is onnodig die kun je in je hand meenemen.

Отдыхаем в муйне, тут не так то много развлечений поблизости, поэтому мы поймали такси и доехали самостоятельно до "ручей фей". Сам вход нам ничего не стоил, мы только на середине зашли в кафе где есть контактный зоопарк и заплатили за напитки:) обезьяны, змеи, крокодилы, кролики, дикобразы - все это вы сможете там найти. Очень вкусные свежевыжатый соки, добрый и приветливый персонал. Также на fairy steam вы можете прокататься на страусах - стоит это 100 донгов, но если честно то катание не очень. Сами пейзажи и водопад - красивый. Получаются такие красивые фотографии. Всем рекомендую посетить кто живет рядом.
Being an Australian, we have these unique environments in our country, so may interest some and not others, surprisingly lots of tourists. Worth a look!

The stream is actually very small and not that impressive.  Came as part of a tour and walking through the river was enjoyable.  Opportunities to pay to ride emus in a paddock along the way.  Definitely not "must-see" on it's own, but as part of an inclusive tour its a pleasant walk through the river/valley.
说实话，本来以为是一个至少像山间小径的地方，其实就是一条小溪流，让你趟着水去参观，没有什么特别值得看的地方，来逛一下拍个照的话还行
The landscape is quite interesting due to the orange and yellow sand and cows running freely.It was refreshing to walk with our feet in the water.
Fairy stream? Not a great experience. Dirty and you're walking in shallow water, sometimes with pits, so be careful. Some nice views. You support local people, I hope.

Was taken here as part of an organised tour to the sand dunes, calling here en route.Not worth visiting, nothing scenic about it and dangerous underfoot. As the water is brown, you cannot actually see what is below the surface of the water. Consequently where the steam bed flow it not only undulates so you can go from a shallow path, to a deeper path without being aware of this. Any rocks/pebbles cannot necessarily be seen either so you risk either turning your ankle or damaging your feet  by scraping the skin. This could lead to infection. It doesn't matter how mobile you are the fact that these is no clarity in the water you could be treading on anything. Not the cleanest area either.Had the scenery been spectacular it could have been worth the risk, but you so busy looking where you are stepping you hardly have time to look up. An hour of my life I won't get back.

The name is great, the reality is different. Ultimately, you walk through a small and dirty stream towards its origin. The edge is lined with garbage and other junk. You'll be sold an entry with a shoe storage option. Isn't the world, but it's still a rip-off for those who don't understand. Is it possible to safely save yourself unless you have absolutely nothing else to do. Would absolutely not recommend !!!

I was here the first time in 2006 and what an experience. True unspoiled nature. But coming back with our teenagers was such a disappointment. The vietnamese has with their usual sense for developmemt totally ruined the experience. Along the stream they have build several party places with karaoke bars etc making a lot of noise and pollution of the stream. You will see lots of plastic cups and other dirt as you go. At the end there is a waterfall that was possible to enjoy earlier. But that is hard to access now and very dirty. Above it there is yet another karaoke place / zoo / leisure place. How ever the stream still has it moments. But its not a beautiful nature experience. Most positive is its still free to access it.
How dirty can a place be? We were used to a lot, but this was really worse.Absolutely not recommended

C’est notre 2e fois à cet endroit et ça ne s’est pas amélioré du tout.  C’est sale, rives non aménagées c’est à-dire, ramasser lau moins les vidanges, sac de plastiques et autres.  On marche là-dedans! Et on nous amène là comme si c’était une fierté, ce lieu.  Dégueu.
Walk bare foot or with waterproof shoes. The most beautiful part is the colorful sand walls and vegetation around the creek. It's really not a waterfall, but fun visting anyway.

Если едете сами на автобусе за 16 тысяч донгов и платите на входе 15 тысяч, этот мелкий ручей с водопадом в 1 метр высотой и кучей туристов можно посетить. Если вас разводят на экскурсию за 25 баксов с катанием на джипах в песках, то лучше не надо. Будете разочарованы и обмануты. На картинках в интеренте лучший вид. Большего там и нет.

An absolute joke. It’s basically some muddy water (I’d rather just jump in a puddle). It was automatically included in our sand dunes tour (I think it only cost 15,000 dong), so I didn’t feel too disappointed. However, probably not worth going to for the one trip. Also very touristy.
To go here was not really worth it to walk up a stream but the sand formations were ok to look at.  It is very busy and along the sides are places to buy drinks etc. Maybe ok with young children.
En liten fors som man vadar igenom. Det var mysigt men ingen jätteupplevelse. Man går i vatten upp till fotknölarna. Vattnet är färgat av röd sand vilket såg lite speciellt ut.

In my opinion this attraction is overrated. There is barely any water in the stream, it is dirty, and it smells bad in the area. The restaurants/stalls surrounding it is also nothing to get excited about. Maybe it used to be nice here, but not anymore. Overall it is not worth much of your time.

Après avoir déboursé 16500VND/0.64€.. pieds nus, vous vous balader en file indienne dans les pas de ceux devant vous et cela en admirant de chaque côté de ce mini ruisseau,des centaines de mètres de parois identiques, certaines avec boutiques et semblant de bars. Des milliers de pieds dont certains pas trop "aseptisés" contre les mycoses et la crasse et retour par le même parcours. Une légende rapportant gros pour les régionaux, le défilé incessant et ininterrompu à coup sûr les amusent .

My friend and I went there as a part of a guided tour. The place is nothing spectacular, and I would not recommend it unless you have plenty of time.However, the place is alright if you want to go for a stroll and/or have a chat with someone.We spent an hour which was appropriate.
You pay 15.000 Dong for the entrance and walk for an hour ... nice but not really necessary. You can walk the creek by feet.

Je pense que le lieu devait être très beau avant toute intervention de l’homme. Il y a des restaurants tous les 120m au bord de l’eau ce qui gâche le paysage. Au début du chemin, il n’y a plus de végétation et il y a des pneus peints en guises de décoration. Vraiment dommage d’avoir dénaturé cet endroit !
красные дюны - фуфло!Грязь ужасная в начале,мусор и т.д.,потом почище,но просто идешь идешь и приходишь к маленькому ручью и всё...

Nous avions hâte d’explorer ce petit chemin qui nous semblait si sauvage en photo. Finalement, il s’avère que la « ballade » dure 10min, que le sentier est noir de monde et que l’on marche dans un lit d’eau plutôt repoussant. Cela n’a malheureusement que très peu d’intérêt. De plus les commerçants et gardes croisés sont désagréables au possible... Je déconseille...

So boring. A bit of water trickling towards me.  Didn't get it at all.  Such a pointless exercise.  Stroll up and back for 40 minutes.  No. Avoid it.  Avoid Mui Ne in general.  The whole town is awful and there's nothing of interest to do.
Catch a taxi and walk up the stream,the rubbish is less the further you go, ignore the urchins. Short walk, red sand dunes are very close by.

Die Beschreibung des Ausflug hört sich viel versprechend und romantisch an! Man kann auch das ein oder andere schöne Bild schießen aber ist halt nur ein kleiner Rinnsal der mit Besuchern überfüllt ist! Fazit kann man machen muss es aber nicht!

I came here the first time in 2008 and loved this place so much so I decided to come back this year and really disappointed, the view is still nice but atmosphere is bad, not free visiting anymore and many shops, cafes and stores along the spring have been ruined the beautiful natural view here

예상했던 것보다 더 역한 냄새가 나서 찝찝했어요게다가 애들이 막 뛰어다녀서 물튀고 하.. 제발 천천히 가줄래 라고 말했죠.부모님들이 전혀 그런 부분을 가르치지 않더라구요...그들에겐 깨끗한 물이라고 생각해서 그런걸 수도 있으니 최대한 웃으면서 부탁했네요.암튼 햇볕이 쨍해서 끝까지 가지는 못하고 통나무 나오는 곳까지만 다녀왔어요. 풍경이 비슷해서요. 사진은 잘 나와요. 빛이 워낙 강렬해서요 ㅋ

Very disappointing as the photos are very beautiful and makes you think that the place deserves a visitLoads of plastic bottles, cans and papers everywhere even with lots of bins along the walkDirty black water running It’s a shame the pollution of the place It’s not free as has been advertised

Là 1 rãnh nước xung quanh bao bọc bởi đồi cát và có chiều dài vài km. Thu phí 15k cho 1 khách là không hợp lý. Rửa chân sau khi đi ở suối lên cũng tốn tiền. Quá buồn cho du lịch Việt Nam kiểu tận thu như vậy

Pay your money and walk through a trickle of a stream, red sand bottom, walls of the stream cemented up ten feet and then red sand walls, we made it as far as the guy with a snake for photos we saw no reason to go further, knowing Vietnam the water was probibly polluterd. It was an experience, but nothing more, only high point was watching people cring at putting the snake around them.
입장료받는거는 얼마안받아서 내나안내나 상관없다만 이게 관광지인 이유를 아직이해를못하겠음 그냥 흙탕물 흐르는 계곡이라기도 애매하고 여튼비추

Ich habe lange überlegt ob diese Bewertung fair ist. Ist sie aber. Der Stream ist ein brauner Rinnsal aus nach Fäkalien stinkender Brühe. Die Umgebung ist völlig vermüllt, damit meine ich Berge von Müll. Im Minutentakt treffen hier Busse weise kulturell hoch interessierte Touristen aus Russland und China ein. Ein völlig überlaufenes Desaster. Der Eintritt kostet 15k. Fazit: Das schlimmste was ich in Vietnam bisher gesehen habe.

На ручье второй раз. Вход стал платный - 15 донгов с человека. Берега обзавелись сувенирными лавками, кафе и ресторанами, поэтому, бредя по мутным водам волшебного ручья не оставляет мысль, что твои ноги омывает вода, перемешанная со смывными водами от многочисленных кафе. Воды в ручье очень много, порой она доходит выше колен, вода мутная и идти в ней очень неприятно, лично меня не покидало чувство брезгливости. Хотя сами берега остались такими же живописными красно - белыми, с причудливыми формами.Остается надеяться, что фото на память не оставит неприятного осадка от мутной воды.

Kostenpflichtig - 15.00 dong. Ist weniger als 1€. Überall Plastik Müll, Verkaufsstände, Restaurants mit lauter Musik, Fotografen mit Schlange ( finden wir besonders schlimm)     Und Massen von Menschen, haben wir allerdings auch erwarten 1€ = 26.000 dong

Всем кто предлагает тур оператор походы туда посылайте их по дальше.Можно и самому добраться.Вход 15.000 на одного взрослого.И так что бы вы понимали что это за место представьте, все что сливают с соседних домов да и еще плюсом к этом мусор, просто очень много мусора.Доходите до конца и там вас встречает типа водопад высотой 5 метров.По пути есть зоопарк где ты покупаешь еду и кормишь животных. Кролики, удав, обезьяны, рыбы, дикобраз. Там же можно выпить настойки из змеи и трав.

Fairy stream was actually located within walking distance from my hotel. just take a slow walk to enjoy the stream and flora. i was actually hassled by some kids along the way. they are asking for money. just ignore and dont engage them.

The Fairy Stream is easy to find. The smell in the beginning is pretty bad. There are bins in every 20m but seems like tourists don’t use them than just throw rubbish in the water :( You need to buy a ticket and adult price is 15000VND. I was quite disappointed about the size of waterfall in the end. I don’t think this is must see in Mui Ne.
Young children might enjoy walking in this river bed for a while...other than that, not really worth it
走的非常累，并没有什么好看的。关键是没有出口，要沿路返回。不懂为什么这个都能当一个景点收费，还不如在酒店私人沙滩躺着

After much hype I was very disappointed . The banks of the stream are covered in litter but the water is clean . There are rocks in the stream and we were told to go bare footed as everyone else was, but a note in the trip information about this would of helped as , I have marine shoes and would have taken them . The rock formations on one side are lovely but the other side isn't the jungle that was quoted . It was pleasant but that's all

Nice activity, but not too special in our opinion. Just a stream to walk through to a small waterfall. Big downside is the mess next to the stream and local merchants everywhere in the middle of nature. Also it's not free anymore as told. We tried to walk past the guard, but really had to pay (15K DNG pp).

На входе снимаешь обувь и заходишь в этот чертов ручей. «Берега» ручья состоят,в основном,из различного мусора,от чего ходить в нем становится не так уж-то и приятно. Ничего фееричного и особенного я тут не нашла. Прошла около 1,5-2 км и увидела прямо-таки скучные сталактиты и сталагмиты-по самому ручью было идти интереснее чем на них смотреть. Единственное-есть большие песчаные горки в самом конце-они ещё ничего. Но в целом не рекомендую.
물이 첨벙첨벙한 길을 걸을 뿐....시간이 짧아서 끝까지 가보지도 못해서 아쉬워요.그래도 물에 발 담그고 있어서 시원했어요. 근데 바닥 잘 보고 걸으세요. 전 가시 박힘

stream nice to walk through feels nice on your feet, the rock bits would be nicer if a bunch of tourists hadnt scraped their names into it .. its far too commercialised litter everywhere and big building blocks and stalls when 1st enter the stream and lots of tourists blocking a nice picture and view

This is a little walk through a stream to what is descibed as a waterfall but I have seen better waterfalls in gardens. There are some interesting limestone features on the walk which is free...part of it is quite deep with stale water so you will e could.ter a number of mosquitoes. Why it's called what it is I have no idea but I suggest only doing this if you are really bored and desperate to do something.

На ручей можно спокойно добраться самому, не тратя ни копейки. Красивые виды только в самом начале, дальше идти по ручью нет смысла. Он вонючий и грязный, что сам Джон Рэмбо туда не полез. В конце есть маленькая струйка которые называют водопадом. Не вздумайте идти.

Vanuit het buiten het hotel een ticket geboekt om de sunrise op de white sand te bekijken. Moeite waard om te gaan! Ook naar de the red sand, fishing village en fairy stream geweest. The red sand vond ik minder interessant dan de white sand, gelukkig bleven we 15 min.

This is a rubbish strewn cesspool, with beggars, market stalls and water that is probably sewerage. Yes the view at the end is pretty however i am insure if it is worth it . FYI Take your shoes with you or the local "heavy fairy" threatens to steal them if you dont pay him to look after them

Nu under den torra perioden fanns det inte mycket vatten att vada i. Dessutom smutsigt, främst i början. Massor med människor som vandrar i vattenfåran, i hopp om att få se något speciellt. Turistfälla.

We took a jeep tour that included a stop here. However the entrance is near many hotels so you don't need a tour to access it. The walk takes you up a stream that had a fair bit of rubbish in and around it. There would have been at least one hundred people we crossed paths with during the thirty minutes we were there so you can see how it gets so dirty. The colours of the sand and geography around it were cool with deep red and white sand. The water gets really deep after about ten minutes and you're about thigh high in murky water if you want to keep going. Eventually you reach a racous bar but you can keep going up the stream to an underwhelming waterfall about 4-5m high. Then you turn around back through the yucky water. It was ok but you probably aren't missing much if you don't go or only go until it gets deep. There's quite a few stalls along the way selling drinks and there's ostrich riding (?!) available too. Make sure you're wearing short shorts if you want to go all the way to the falls!

We started at the end where a local demanded $10,000 dong from myself any my boyfriend. The walk in the shallow water and sand is nice as you go upstream,picturesque and photo worthy. However, as you try to get to the waterfall you are in thigh deep water, the more upstream you went, the more rubbish was floating around, then we saw a rat and decided to abort mission. Unfortunately we also saw a log of poo floating in the shallow water. I felt so disappointed and disgusted and I wanted to sterilize myself ASAP. Honestly so pretty but ruined by rubbish and faeces in the water which pretty much makes it stepping in sewage water.
Mooi, dat is zeker.Maar ga vroeg op de dag of t einde van de dag. Bij ons waren er veel te veel toeristen. We zijn halverwege omgedraaid.

The stream itself is very nice. Unfortunately like in many other places in Vietnam everything is covered in trash. As you walk barefoot through the water this is even more unpleasant than in many other places.Moreover Ostrich riding is offered which is a clear case if animal abuse.

Go with your sun glasses or eye protecting glass, because if it is winding sand would blown in your eye.This place is just an tourist trap, though it is a free to visit, but you just cannot pass the local barrier asking for 50,000/person WITH ticket showing you they have right to ask tourist to pay. Saw some tourist were being shouted and yelled by the local when they insist not paying for it.It just a stream walk, not that an must see or something different you may see along stream walk. We walk till the end, a tidy waterfall. that is it..

Walked for 20 minutes and understand the potential of this place, naturally pretty, nice colors, but covered in litter and overrun with people. Really a great example of the negative effect people can have on the environment.
Some nice photos can be taken, but after all...nothing interesting. If you really have nothing to do, maybe it is a place to visit
알았더라면 안갔을것같다. 끝으로 가면 뭔가 나오나? 싶어서 계속 걸었지만 그게 전부였다. 그냥 계속 얕은 흙탕물을 걷는다. 뱀도 나온다..........

Aikamoinen turistirysä. ”Opas” jätti meidät kyydistä ja osoitti puroa ”you go there”. Ei oikein tiedetty mitä piti tehdä mutta käveltiin muiden turistien kanssa jonossa, purossa kahlaten. Joenpenkat jälleen täynnä roskia. Masentavaa. Käännyttiin jossain vaiheessa takaisin kun ei tiedetty mitä odottaa tai etsiä, kuultiin myöhemmin että puron päässä kuulemma oli vesiputous. Se jäi meiltä näkemättä.

Nous nous sommes rendus en scooter à ce lieu. Nous nous garons et passons sous un passio où on nous fais enlever nos chaussures. Endroit sale et mauvaises odeurs. Nous remettons un cours d'eau ou le sable est plus ou moins doux et coloré. Le cours d'eau est jonché de détritus. Pour nous faire arrivé a une cascade de 3m que voici... Déçu.

Very dirty around... you will not miss much...Only the name "fairy stream" is nice.We went there in a hot afternoon and stopped in a busy comer street and went down the stairs. Walked up stream about 550m ... we saw the "fairy ...  hahaha".

My girlfriend and I decided to go here cause it’s free and the pictures looked great. It’s very deceiving. The stream was filled with trash in and around it. There is a boy at the entrance that makes you take off your shoes and even sandals to leave in his care while you wade barefoot up the stream. I ended up cutting my foot on a rock in the stream and was bleeding everywhere. When we got back to our shoes he demanded 10.000 Dong each cause he “watched our shoes”. He never stated that we must pay beforehand, and even made my girlfriend leave her sandals (she wanted to wear them in the water, duh). We ended up telling him we would not pay and we just walked away. I would not bother going here, spend your time on the beach instead.

I don't care if they flutter or queef, there wasn't much ferrying going on. The stream was dirty and heaps of people, I'm unsure what the hype is about. Potentially, if I knew more about rocks + sand formation or the significance of the place, I might have enjoyed it more. Probably better to take a tour or some fairies of your own ;-)
Am Anfang noch ganz schön, aber gegen Ende zu viel Müll. Die vielen Verkaufsstände tun ihr übriges um das Ambiente zu zerstören.
không như mình tưởng tượng. chỉ là khe nước nhỏ. ở dưới là cát. hai bên rất nhiều rác. còn thả bò ra giữa suối nữa. nếu chọn lại mình sẽ k đến đây

Die Sandsteinformationen sind ein Foto wert. Gerade die roten Steine sehen toll aus. Allerdings läuft man nicht am Fluss entlang, sondern im Fluss. Hier verstecken sich leider auch spitze und rutschige Steine. Wenn man passende Schuhe dabei hat, super. Wenn nicht, ist es eher gefährlich.Leider liegt auch sehr viel Müll am und im Fluss. Dieses Problem begegnet einem aber überall in Vietnam...
입구라길래 봤는데 쓰레기잔뜩쌓이고 지저분한 다리밑으로 내려가라고 해서 당황했어요. 입구가 다리를 가운데두고 양쪽에 있는데 다른 한쪽은 그나마 아주 째끔 더 나았지만,,, 그래도,,,,, 입구를 보는 순간 포기했습니다. 언젠가 후회할지도 모르겠지만 지금까진 다시 돌아가도 가지않을거예요

When we arrived at the entrance there is this local young guy showing us the direction and informed us that there is a 50,000 VND for each person, and he show us a small pieace of brochure like paper showing the amount. I didn't pay immediately and start googling for the information. When he saw me doing that after a minute, he told me we can go in, and he didn't collect anything from us! This terrible and should be reported to the local authority. Will definitely spoilt the image of this tourist attraction.The stream itself is quite nice, and good for a slow walk. Nice places for photo taking along the stream.
Don't bother to go, not worth the trip, really lame.  Better just go to a beach nearby, or wandering on the street outside of the Fairy Stream.

It's a mud stream. That's all. I don't think its not worth to spend too much time there. The stream maybe combine home used water. Who knows? There are so many restaurants around the stream and don't believe that Vietnamese has good notion about cleaning.

+ nett wenn es nicht so überfüllt wäre- Touristen über Touristen- "Quads" können zu überteuerten Preisen gemietet werden -> defintiv keine gute Idee! -> lediglich Frontantrieb und wenig Power, daher kommt man zu Fuß schneller voran :(
Ein Bach an dem man entlang läuft. Mann hat 50' Zeit, was viel zu lange ist. Hat wieder viel Müll der liegengelassen wurde. Ist halt verbunden mit den Dünenbesichtigung...

Когда проезжал над этим ручьем на автобусе по дороге с Далата видел мусор вокруг этого ручья, поэтому не хотел туда идти. Когда попал на ручей, мои ожидания оправдались - по берегам ручья куча мусора, иногда невыносимая вонь, загон с облезлыми страусами также вонял, желание покататься на них даже не возникало, порадовали только утки с утятами
Not sure why this is a tourist attraction but if you're into walking in a very shallow stream of water mixed with clay then this could be the place in mui ne. Otherwise I'd give this a miss.

Warm water stream which flows along some brightly coloured sands deposited from the nearby white and red sand dunes.  We paid to visit this steam which is actually free to go to and can be accessed via a side street as you leave Mui Ne (its signposted).  Its a nice walk with your feet in warm water but it was not what I expected.  Our guide turned us around before the end as there was clearly a group of local kids having a party (we could hear loud music and lots noise!).I'd rather spend an hour walking along the beach!

We rode motobike for visiting and the guy at parking lot offerred us 50k each to take us a tour. We dont care them and he drop the price to 10k for parking motobike. We are not sure what his intention is since his English was poor, so we decided no to get in. One of us just wanna walk inside to see if there's any interesting and were obstructed by such guy and they chased us away by using word 'get out'.

I had a really nice walk with mom. The rock formation and desert was amazing. It kind of reminded me of Cappadocia in Turkey.  It was sunny day but in the valley it was breezy and feet in the muddy water made it definitely cooler. I didn't mind the mud until I saw a sewage pipe and it was flowing into the very stream that I have walked for the last 30 min, I felt itchy all over my body. Poorly managed place. If the government or local community took better care, it could be really amazing place.  Be careful of young boys running around you and asking you to pay for entrance. YOU DON'T NEED TO PAY!!

Der Fairy Stream ist schön anzuschauen(10 Bilder), wenn nur nicht überall der Müll rumliegen würde! Ist total überannt der schöne Fleck. Wir sind extra wegen der roten und der weißen Dünen nach Mui Ne angereist, aber nach dem wir wortwörtlich fast im vermüllten Dreckwasser hinauf zur Quelle gelaufen sind, ist es uns schon gleich vergangen.

It's a little stream that runs through coloured sands. Moderately picturesque but that's not the problem. You will be hastled to leave your shoes for a price and the a guide who will demand an exhorbitant tip saying his family will beat him up if he doesn't get it. Ignore the guide. It's a rip off.
Begriper inte vad för sensation denna s k attraktion är. Knappast värt ett besök. En massa människor som vadar runt i en bäck med sopor runt om kring.
아무리 갈데가 없는 베트남이라지만 여긴 가는 이유를 모르겠어요.우리로 치면 작은 개울가인데 바닥이 모래로 깔려있고 얕게 물이 흐르는 곳일 뿐인 곳을 1km정도 걸어가는 거에요. 바닥이 모래인 이유는 바로 옆이 사구이기 때문이죠. 지프투어를 이용해서 많이들 가시는데 여기만 보시려면 그냥 시내버스 타고도 갈 수 있습니다.
Inget jag rekommenderar . Vandring ca 1 kilometer i vattnet likadant hela vägen, fullt av turister så att det bitvis var trångt att gå. Smutsigt, vattenfallet var inget att se väldigt litet.

De omgeving als je door het beekje loopt is wel mooi! Mooie rotsen en rode zandduinen waar je ook op kunt lopen om wat mooier uitzicht te hebben. Alleen zie je wel dat er veel afval neer gegooid word.De waterval is zelf niet heel speciaal, en als je via het trappetje ernaast naar boven gaat dan zie je dat er weer best wat afval in de bosjes naast de waterval is gegooid. Verpest wel het uitzicht helaas.Ook kom je best wat klavertjes vier tegen dus dat is wel leuk om te zien!

We booked a jeep tour and this was one of the stops, it was very disappointing. Lots of people so you could've really walk and lots of trash all over. There's a part were you can ride an ostrich which is really sad to see. Not worth the visit.

You go down a bumpy track to a dirty muddy stream. No pleasure in that and they also ask you to pay for moped parking (10k Dong)I would defiantly recommend the white sand dunes at sunrise and go by moped so you can travel independently. Also get a quad which is expensive but worth it to save walking

It could be a nice place if there wasn't that much tourists messing around. If you have a chance and want to visit, walk here during the hours there's no jeep tour ongoing. Jeeps bring most of the folks.
Вид там нормальный, но пока вы дойдёте, вы почувствуете все  ароматы вьетнамских запахов и виды мусора по берегам ручья.Кто чувствует энергетику - не рекомендую. Высасывает.

Niet echt de moeite waard maar het enige om te doen in Mui Ne als het strand geen optie is. Wij hebben de White en Red Sand Dune tour gedaan icm Vissersdorp en de Fairy stream. Je hebt wat te doen maar het stelt niet heel veel voor. Icm een jeep tour van 4 uur kun je wel in 1 keer alle plekken bezoeken en ontloop je de kans om een boete te krijgen wanneer je als westerling op je motor scooter gaat.
Fint ställe. Lite svårt att hitta till. Väldigt smutsigt. Finns skräp överallt längs vatten dammen där man går.
не впечатлило, совсем. не стала бы тратить время на это место. куча песка не производит никакого впечатления. ходить приходится босиком по сомнительному ручью, похожему на сточные воды.

A good idea to visit the fairy stream, while you are tired to lay on the beach enjoying the cocnuts)) You can make some nice pictures with red mountains, walk along the creek ... next to  cows... Well, the stream can be reached on your own, FOC, half an hour walk at least from Boke. The signs are everywhere .The boys will try to cheat by offering to pay for the passage the stream, just go your way, it's free, shoes better to put into a backpack with yourself...

Можно доехать на автобусе или на такси (такси стоит 30-40к от фантьета). Все развлечение заключается в прогулке по ручью в ущелье, которое заканчивается миниводопадом. Если подняться потом наверх, то кроме свалки мусора ничего не увидеть. Развлечение из разряда "когда совсем скучно и надоело лежать на пляже". По мне так фотографии гораздо красивее, чем вживую. Делать тут особо нечего.

If you are wearing shoes there are vendors from whom you can hire a pair of thongs/flip flops.  You can walk bare foot in the stream In the dry season the water is very shallow and you can see where you are stepping. Not sure about the rainy season. Don't wear light coloured or expensive clothing because the water splashes may stain your clothes. Interesting to see the sand formation but I would not want to do the walk when the weather is too hot.
Great visit as a Quick stop on the way to the sand dunes. You can take nice photos, but otherwise dont expect too much.

Vi var där i December 2016 och kollade. Det var skönt att gå i det grunda vattnet och svalka av sig, men personligen tyckte vi att det inte var något extraordinärt.Självklart är detta högst personligt. Det är gratis att gå så har man tid över kan det vara ett alternativ.

Come here only if it is a part of tour. There's nothing very especial and interesting. And be ready for garbage near the river at first. You can make good photos there.And don't follow all route. You can return after a somekind of a cafe.And remember you will walk into the water. Pay attention to your shoes and shorts.
挺晒的，就一条小溪踩踩水拍拍照，不需要花太多时间的地方。不过要走好久，中间有鸵鸟看看，外面停车的地方卖的果汁还凑合
Not sure what this was all about. Strange muddy river. Don't pay money to have your shoes cared for - just carry them
怎么说呢，就是一条小溪，特别的地方就是整条溪流很浅很平缓，都是软软的沙子，踩上去蛮舒服的，但是么什么特别好看的，不太推荐专门来玩

The stream was littered with rubbish and tourists. The waterfall is not really worth seeing if you've seen waterfalls before. Was part of our package tour which was cheap, so we didn't mind. Something a bit different from the beach if you need something else to do, but don't go out of your way to see this if you have limited time to spare
계속 물속을걸어가야하니  반바지입는것이좋고  상처가있는분은 안가는게좋을듯 물이약간 좀 더러움  초입에 스프링롤구워서파는것 만동  맛있었음
For me it's noting to see here. Just walking in the stream to reach mini canyon. I just walk half way and turn back but for who love nature I suggest you should come here.

Ne savons pas où garer notre scooter et c'est donc dans un petit bouiboui qui nous demande 5000 dong qu'on le laissera. On laisse nos chaussures à une des entrées du ruisseau et on remonte les pieds dans l'eau. Des déchets partout sur les côtés, une eau par vraiment claire .. rien de sensationnel !

I was looking forward to visiting this stream after reading good reviews on here but was left very disappointed. You walk up a dirty stream that cows most likely urinate and defecate. If you are stuck for time you will miss nothing if you skip this place. Very uneventful and would not re-visit.

Do not pay for an excursion to this place its basically a long walk up a shallow stream to a very small and unimpressive waterfall.  The walk isn't unpleasant but it's really not that special that you would want to spend money on.Anyone can walk up the stream there is no entrance fee and it's not that far from Mui Ne centre so either hire a scooter or jump in a taxi
Este lugar es muy bonito pero realmente nada del otro mundo. Si vas a Mui Ne y pasas está bien, per no bases en ello tu visita.
찾아가서 보기에 아깝다는 생각이드네요 냇가에는 온통쓰래기에 소들이 다녀서 찝찝한 느낌입니다
