Jedoch ist das das Leben dieser Einwohner, was ich sehr eindrücklich finde. Man erhält einen sehr guten Einblick.Der letzte Stop, die Fairy Springs, waren schön. Man konnte durch einen kleinen Fluss (?) hochlaufen und die Natur nebenan geniessen. Aufgepasst! Sie verlangen 5‘000 Dong Eintritt. Es ist aber gratis. Ca. 20Meter weiter links könnt ihr gratis nach unten ins Wasser. :)Alles in allem war es ein spannender und abwechslungsreicher Morgen. Um 09.00Uhr waren wir wieder im Guesthouse :)
그냥.. 냇간데.. 물이 계속 흐르는 냇가.. 주변에 용암이 흐르다 만들어진 것 같은 돌과 붉은 모래들이 있음신발 벗고 물 걷는게 다임 그냥 그게다임..

It was some what pleasant on your feet to walk through the stream but the overall experience was not what I had expected. You walk down it with loads of other people who seem to stop ever five steps for a photo shoot. Then there are restaurants on the sides with really loud awful sounding karaoke going on which was a real shock. You have to pay 5000 dong each to enter which I also wasn’t expecting. The canyon is kinda cool to look at but to be honest this and Mui Ne in general is missable. There are far better and nicer places to visit in Vietnam.
요정의 샘물이라고 해서 샘물에 기대하고 가시면 실망하실 것 같습니다. 오히려 가는 길에 예쁜 바위들이 많아요~!
Ở đây vài năm nay xuất hiện nhiều hàng quán 2 bên suối, làm cho nơi đây ko còn đẹp và xuất hiện rác thải nhiều
Wir hatten einen halbtägigen Ausflug gebucht und kamen hier vorbei. Aufgrund TET war alles überfüllt und vermüllt.

On a adoré marcher les pieds dans l'eau chaude, le décor est splendide (5* pour la balade). Cependant le nombre de touristes, les gens qui demandent de payer une entrée pour ce site gratuit (refuser de payer!) et surtout la pollution omniprésente me donnerait envie de mettre une note proche de 0 (marcher dans les déchets c'est pas mon truc..) . Les déchets sont le principal problème au Vietnam et autant aux dunes que le port de Mui Ne sont une poubelle publique...

Taking a tour here would be a rip off. Just go there on your own and have a wander. I'd recommend walking barefoot as most of the time you will want to walk through water and it can get a little deep at some point. If you're bringing kids they might have to be carried across the deeper parts otherwise they'll be up to their chest in the water. It looks beautiful, but it's a shame that there is a lot of litter everywhere... No one seems to be interested in keeping this place clean or cleaning it up. It is free to go there, but in my opinion it would be better to charge a small entrance fee or accept donations so that the place can be kept clean from litter.

Называют ручей фей, фей нет, есть куча китайцев, которых сюда привозят на экскурсию, наверное, еще и за деньги. Благодаря расположению нашего отеля, из ворот сразу направо и вот спуск по лесенке на ручей, ходят по нему босиком, хотите в сланцах, только не оставляйте их на берегу, а то ушлые вьетнамцы   по возвращению предъявят вам иск, якобы за охрану обуви)). Вода теплая, дно безопасное, красивый песок кирпичного цвета, идете до водопада минут сорок, главное, не слиться с группами китайцев, а то тишины не дождетесь: ор, ржач, визг, как в зверинце. Вот и все, особо смотреть нечего, только фотографировать интересные пейзажи. По дороге сначала будет вонять их знаменитым рыбным соусом не пугайтесь, это скоро пройдет, мы его ассоциировали с кошачьим китекэтом, поэтому привыкли, правда никогда не пробовали: его сначала маринуют под солнцем из рыбы месяца 2-3, а потом протухшую жижу варят и едят.

Aside from the people guarding the entrance and forcing you to pay for something that is advertised as "free" since you've already paid for the tour, its great. The waterfall at the end is garbage, and some say its not worth the walk, but I enjoyed myself.

J'ai beaucoup aimé faire cette promenade les pieds dans l'eau. On suit le chemin en passant par des rochers rouges et blancs pour aboutir à une cascade.Attention à l'entrée un vietnamien vous propose de garder vos chaussures le temps de la balade (environ 1h aller-retour) pour 5000 vdn. Ne surtout pas lui donner. Cette balade est gratuite et vous pouvez garder vos chaussures à la main.
Nice to look at the sand formations and the brilliant juxtaposition of the red and white sands.  Its cheap to get in and theres restaurants and toilet facilities along the way.

Если Вы решили посетить знаменитый ручей, то возьмите с собой фотоаппарат, камеру или телефон, чтобы сделать красивые снимки. Лучше всего спуститься к ручью, пройдя по мосту, если идти со стороны Муйне в Фантьхиет, и свернуть налево под мост. Чтобы дойти до водопада, потребуется минут  30, но со съемками красот возможно больше. идти можно босиком, в шлепках не удобно. Места действительно красивые.
Inget märkvärdigt med Fairy stream. Man gick en promenad i vatten och mer än så var det egentligen inte.
Tolle Gegend um etwas ab zu spannen.Ideal für einen Spaziergang.Der rote Sand und die Felsen sind sehr imposant.

This was just ok. If you need something to do its a nice walk up a shallow stream. Over the years water erosion has created what almost looks like termite mounds in the sand stone. It in no way looks like fairy homes! LOL Also there are small shops built on the banks where you can stop for food & drinks.
Petite ballade sympas . Sans plus . On y a été en scooter. Le temps était nuageux donc ça nous a donné l’occasion de faire une petite balade .

Pitkä kävely joen uomaa pitkin vesiputouksen luokse. Välillä vesi oli polven yläpuolelle asti ja likaista. Menimme iltapäivästä ja silloin tuli paljon kiinalaisia turisteja vastaan mutta putouksella oli rauhallista. Paluumatkalla oli myös rauhallista nauttia hiekkarinteistä.
It is worth preparing your shoes for a walk. take water and have a bite - the journey can drag on...
Красивые виды разноцветного песка и песочных сталактитов, но грязновато, с учетом того, что для просмотра красоты необходимо разуться и идти пешком по ручью.

Sehr viel eindrücklicher und genussvoller wäre das Erlebnis, wenn die Besucherströme reguliert (immerhin kostet ein Eintritt VND 5'000) und der die Ufer säumende (Plastik-)Müll fachgerecht entsorgt würde.
Went early morning so not too many people about.  First part of the walk in the stream was ok but nearer to the waterfall a bit rocky so would recommend wearing some kind of footwear.

It's free to get in. You can leave you shoes with the. Locals but will be charged . We carried ours as the steam was shallow and at the worst up to the knee but this was only for a few mins.It was an unusual walk and passes an 1 hour away.
물에 발담그고 걸으며 독특한 지형을 보는 경험은 좋았습니다. 그런데 웬 현지인 한명이 따라붙으며 자기 맘대로 가이드를 해버리고는 돈 내놓으라고 하더군요 조심하세요

Leider ist es die einzige Attraktion in Mui Ne und wird gnadenlos ausgeschlachtet. Wir waren am frühen Nachmittag dort und gefühlt waren 5 Menschen pro Quadratmeter anzutreffen. Auch finde ich die Stände entlang des Bachlaufes mit ihrer lauten Musik sehr störend.Die Felsformationen sind sehr schön.

A beautiful 1 hour return walk that you should do if in Mui Ne. Unfortunately rubbish is a problem- had to really work to get photos without rubbish or graffiti in them. The locals charge a modest fee to walk the stream - I have no issue with this but please get them to to pick up the rubbish and stop tourists from dropping it. The shops that go down to the waters edge could be removed - offer the locals some space back from the river to sell their goods and make a living.

Was here in 1999 and took a walk back in memory lane or should I say stream.  In 99 we walked the stream hoping we would run into someone for fear that we could be captured and held against our will.  Today that walk came with a young lad watching our sand Les while we walked the street.  When returning he wanted 10000dg for each pair.  The scenery was still beautiful and worth your time to see.  Unfortunately there were a greater number of people than from our past with some garbage left in the stream and on the banks.  Some locals have started a business down on the stream with lots of garbage on the banks.  This stream is nature in a most beautiful way of colours and scenery.  It’s worth your time put preserve the beauty as it was and should remain.

Keinen privaten Guide notwendig. Fairy Stream sind locker zu Fuss zu erreichen. Es führt ein kleiner Weg dorthin, relativ am Anfang von Mui Ne. Auf Beschilderung achten und nicht wundern, dass man durch eine kleine Seitengasse mit Häusern und Läden durch muss. Man wird kurz zuvor von einem Mann gebeten die Schuhe auszuziehen und dort am Ufer zu lassen. Mein Tipp, einfach in Rucksack mitnehmen. Es folgt zugleich ein kleiner Bach oder Fluss mit Sandboden, knöcheltief wo man Richtung Fairy Stream laufen kann. Bei warmen Temperaturen ist das kalte Wasser an den Füssen sehr angenehm. Fairy Stream sind schön zusehen, aber nichts besonderes, wenn man schon mal schöne Dünen in Amerika oder Dubai gesehen hat. Schade ist das überall immer wieder Müll zu sehen ist und kleine " Retaurants" Getränke oder Essen anbieten. Am Ende von den Fairy Stream kommt ein kleiner Wasserfall. Nichts besonderes meines Erachtens. Vorsichtig, da können die letzten paar Meter etwas steiniger werden.Für eine Aktivität die nichts kostet und leicht zu finden ist mit Hilfe von Google Maps, ist es mal eine kleine Abwechslung für Mui Ne.
一般，就是光着脚在水里走，拖下来的鞋放在墙边一双还需要5000盾，如果拿着走就不要钱，差不多10分钟走个来回。没跟团，不知道为什么叫仙女溪。
Вход расположен в деревне Муй Не, поэтому грязно. Выше по течению намного лучше: мини зоопарк, хорошие ракурсы для фото..
下午到的這裏，陽光很好，小溪掩藏在濃密的樹林里，映襯在藍天白雲之間，腳下是細軟紅色的沙泥，淺淺漫過腳踝的細沙，很不錯，

Kanske inte det häftigaste, dock är det något annat att göra. Lite mysigt att strosa runt i ”bäcken” och bitvis fanns det häftiga vyer av vad naturen åstadkommer. Finns orm för den som är rädd för detta.
There is a along way with rock terrain view. You can find a little sand hill. That’s very beautiful and hard to be forgotten.
It's average place, not thing interested, only channel sand, No need guide and entrance fee also your shoes can keep on car.

(+) Salah satu tempat yang menjadi icon di mui ne. Tidak banyak yang dapat kita lakukan disini. Sampai di tempat kita akan disambut oleh anak-anak yang akan menawarkan papan selunjur untuk di sewa. Jangan lupa menawar, karena warga lokal cukup agresif kepada turis.(+) Wajib mencoba sensasi berselunjut dari atas padang pasir sampai turun. jangan lupa pakai pakaian tertutup jika tidak ingin gosong karena terik matahari sangatlah cukup membuat kulit perih(-) Warga lokal yang cukup agresif kepada turis

Неплохо, но у нас гида, как такового не было, был только водитель, который говорил только на вьетнамском, джип - это раздолбанный уазик, у них везде джип-сафари на уазиках. Берите крем от солнца, экскурсия долгая и воды побольше. Ну и это место для людей, любящих дикую природу, бездорожье и длинные пешие переходы

Hade klarat mig utan detta. Men gjortr gjort. Hade blivit sjukt besviken om jag gått på en guidad tur o betalat för detta. Längst vattnet som man gick i så låg det en hel del skräp och ibland flöt det även förbi i vattnet. Synd att naturen inte har bevarats. "Vattenfallet" på slutet va mkt mindre än jag tänkt.Har man inget o göra kan de vara värt ett besök.
It's a long way to small waterfall. In the beginning it is a very crowded place, further you can meet no one.

Le 4x4 nous a un peu laissé en plan là, on a vu le mec qui gardait les chaussures sous un pont et vaguement compris qu'il fallait marcher dans l'eau.... L'accueil était donc un peu glauque et nous a laissé méfiants. Finalement il s'avère que c'est un très joli parcours les pieds dans l'eau pendant 1km à peu pres, avec des falaises rouges et blanches impressionnantes.On peu prendre de très belles photo et le site est propre, en tout cas le matin vers 9h !

We got there via a little restaurant which want so have either 5000VND or you having a drink. But the drinks are worth having them.you cann enter the stream there and walk up. It was very nice, but there are a lot of tourists. And the closer you get to the waterfall, the uglier it gets. Trash everywhere, loud music, muddy water.If you just stop right before the loud music starts it is a great walk. But don't go further.
It wasnt nothing special but a nice way to spend an hour. Walk was easy, water not deep. Chinese taking selfis blocking the path at some points. Wayerfall at the end is very small.

Der Fluss ist zumindest Ende November nur minimal gefüllt und der Wasserfall am Ende ist winzig.Das durch den Sand laufen ist recht schön und auch die roten und weißen Dünen am Rand sind recht schön (abgesehen vom Müll überall).Selbst zu dieser Zeit waren recht viele asiatische Touristen gleichzeitig im Bach unterwegs, so dass wir zeitweise stehen bleiben mussten weil wir nicht gleichzeitig durchgehen konnten. Unser Highlight auf diesem Trip war allerdings ein „Fischteich“ am rechten Rand des Baches mit „Minizoo“ (Achtung, leider nicht artgerecht).

Ходили только, потому что рядом был отель наш.итак,на входе вонь,грязь,далее такая е вонь и грязь и через 15 ходьбы все так же плюс ещё опасные камни,об которые можно легко плранится,в конце вас встречает водопад небольшой и в довольно не широкой расщелине.единственное ,что понравилось,это в некоторых местах идёшь как- будто по ковру,прикольно.советую туда алйти,только ,если вы рядом,но никак не брать специальную экскурсию)всем хорошего отдыха)

Экскурсию можно приобрести в “турфирмах”,которых по всему Муйне очень много,но мы решили посетить данное место самостоятельно.Ничего особого место из себя не представляет,мелкая речушка,принявшая красноватый оттенок из-за известняка,изрядно загаженная мусором,который по всей видимости не убирается.Рекомендуется взять собой пакет для тапочек,или можно заплатить местным 5000 донгов что бы они присмотрели за вашей обувью.Но голыми ногами идти крайне не приятно,если в начале вы будете топать по песочку,то в лкнце будут каменистые участки,где можно изрезать все ноги.Суть реки Фей,в том что вы в течении примерно 30 минут идете по реке,в итоге до ходите до небольшого водопадика.На этом собственно все.По пути будут попадаться мини-зоопарк（вход 5000 донгов）,где бедные животные совершенно без воды в жару,страусинные бега,рыбалка.Из положительного,будет пару красивых локаций,если вы любитель фото.
может погода нам не помогла, моросил дождик, может мы обленились, но после белых дюн по красным уже не было желания ходить. Поэтому ставлю оценку неплохо.
사막투어중 가장 마지막에 있었는데 물색깔이 조금 아쉬웠지만 한번쯤은 가볼만하네요무이네에서 필수로 가기보단 시내부근이니 잠깐 내려서 산책하긴 좋아요
Anfangs sehr schön und enspannt, angenehm ohne Schuhe, dann jedoch Müll und steinig. Haben mehr erwartet. Der Wasserfall am Ende war nichts besonders.

Im Fairy Stream watet man durch einen sandigen roten Bachlauf mit niedriger Wasserhöhe durch eine bizarre Landschaft. Das machen auch viele andere Touristen, die mit ihren Selfiesticks nerven. Am Eingang lümmeln Jungs, die ihre Dienste als Aufpasser anbieten. Das braucht man wirklich nicht.

A free attraction on the outskirts of Mui and. Despite it being quite popular, the place isn't particularly interesting after 10 minutes of walking round it, but still free nonetheless, so worth visiting if you have ample time. If not, you aren't really missing much

Not very interesting but it's okay for a 30 min visit and get some pictures taken. Btw please don't take the ostrich ride. I personally think it's one of animal abuse. You can still have fun without riding on such a delicate animal, right?

Шли вверх по течению, вход в речку был крайне захламлен. Но чуть выше и мусора стало в разы меньше. Виды просто супер, красные пески - просто виды с Марса. Шли до водопада, ожидали увидеть нечто, но на практике оказалось - водопадик метра 3 в высоту. Сходить стоит если недалеко живёте, на экскурсию - не советую.

Wie schon gesagt - ganz nett mehr aber auch nicht. Wenn die shops rechts und links nicht wären könnte man es als idyllisch bezeichnen. Wenn ihr das Schild auf dem Photo seht seid ihr richtig. Aber VORSICHT, der alte Mann versucht euch bei den Parkgebühr abzuzocken.
어디에서 흘러 오는지는확인을 못했지만 물살을 거슬러 올라가면 붉은 모래와 흰모래가 섞여 만들어진 경관을 보게 됩니다.
Röd och vit sand möts. Väldigt fint. Men man kan verkligen inte säga att det fanns något vattenfall.
A few miles (there and back) of walking barefeet in the sandy stream, cools you down nicely, too.  The "waterfall"  it leads to, is nothing special tho.
무이네 무이네 너무 난리들이다 기대 많이 했는데그렇게 기대 만큼은 아니었어요그쪽 땅들 색이 모두 빨강  황토빛이더라구요 무인에서 달랏으로 오다보니

Fairy stream is one of the attraction included in the sand dune tour. It is located somewhere around the resort area. Easy access.Free admission and please do not pay anything. Keep your shoes with yourself.Pretty bored to walk the whole stream, about 40 minutes back and forth. Nice view along the walk.
생각보다 모래 색깔이 빨간색이 아니였어요. 그냥 인생샷 하나쯤 건지기 좋고 사람이 너무 많아서 사람 없는 곳 가서 사진 찍어야되요~

We had mixed feelings about this place. We did this as part of a jeep tour of Mui Ne. The entrance to the stream is via a rubbish strewn and graffitied bridge. Was very strange! We didn't have a guide up the steam so we weren't too sure what it was we were looking at or for. After dodging some cows, we made it up the steam. It was very touristy however we didn't make it to the waterfall at the end as we didn't know about it! So maybe we missed the best part. Es un paseo bonito. Para ir con calma y disfrutar del paisaje y caminar por el riachuelo tranquilamente se necesita 1 hora y media o 2 horas.El pequeño riachuelo se puede recorrer descalzo y es muy refrescante. El paisaje es bonito y muy cambiante a cada paso.Mejor hacerlo por la mañana temprano.

Reached at the starting point (or maybe not?) by our jeep and the place doesn't have a obvious entrance. We were attended by a local young chap who first, told us to take off our shoes. So after that he was with us the entire journey at fairy stream! He guided us, walked ahead of us, talked a bit and that's when my bf and I realiseddw other groups were different from us (they didn't have a guide with them?) and we then suspected he might ask for tips for his "service". True enough he really asked for tips at the end of the walk. So we thought we should tip him at about 100,000 dong as he helped us take photos, but he asked for more, like 400,000 more! That's crazy. We didn't cater that much for tips and we could eat several meals with 400,000. That did ruin the overall experience as halfway after our 100,000 tip he told us there's nothing much ahead and we should leave. Wow, unbelievable. If you are going, just be careful.

It was very fun. We didn't get to reach the end because some people who passed said it stinks down there but we regret it because we heard it was really pretty. Definitely recommend for people who aren't afraid to get a little dirty & wet and are interested in sightseeing.

untuk kesini terlebih dahulu melalui kolong jembatan yang kecil dan agak banyak sampah , tap ya kalau sudah sampai mui ne memang tidak ada salahnya untuk kesini juga . tapi kalau menurut saya biasa saja.i
Der fairy stream an sich wäre ganz schön, wenn da nicht der Müll und die Reisegruppen wären. Schade. Kein Must See

sangat menarik. sebenarnya ini hanya sungai dangkal,... tapi pemandangannya menarik. saya datang kesini sebagai bagian dari paket wisata. jadi paketnya itu white/red sanddunes, terakhir fairy stream ini.pertama diajak masuk agak ke dalam gang. di ujung gang ini ada tempat masuk sungai ini. jadi ya wisatanya jalan jalan di dalam sungai sambil menikmati pemandangan sekitar.pertama kali datang sih,... haah apaan nih cuman gini ajah? tapi dijalanin aja. lama2 menarik kok. sungainya sendiri bersih banget merskipun dekat kampung2. pasirnya lembut banget. kiri kanan sungai disuguhi pemandangan seperti bukit pasir? atau karst. instagrammable. cukup menarik :)
Можно посетить просто, чтобы отметиться, по сути делать там нечего. Грязный ручей, как и везде, животные, красный песок, можно покататься на драненьком страусе :)
This was the last stop of our tour and it was kinda refreshing to walk along the creek, but there was nothing special about it. There was a waterfall at the end of the walk, still not impressive.
Take off your shoes and enjoy the stream (average ankle's height).However, the walk is a bit hot, so bring your hat or umbrella.

I put 3 bubbles instead of 4 because the placed could be cleaner if the locals have been involved in an environmental program.Association as WWF or Let's do it should organize a clean up day there.The locals can lobbying the government on this issue.After the scenery is amazing and you can walk in the shade.

Fairy Stream is a shallow, ankle-deep waterway that carves a magical miniature canyon through striking sand dunes and limestone formations. Its name comes from the whimsical, otherworldly beauty of the colorful sand walls in shades of red, orange, and white—the red hue is a result of iron oxide, which contrasts vividly with the white stone and green foliage.Your adventure begins by leaving your shoes on a rack before descending a staircase into the stream. From there, you'll wade through the cool, gentle water on a soft sandy bottom. The 1–1.5 km round-trip walk reveals an ever-changing landscape of shapes carved by water and wind, including mini caves, smooth cliffs, and intriguing rock formations.A 40-minute round-trip walk is a perfect amount of time to enjoy the scenery at a relaxed pace. For the best experience, visit in the early morning (7–9 AM) or late afternoon (3–5 PM) to avoid the intense midday sun, as there is very little shade.Local Flavor: Along the path, you'll encounter local children offering guide services for a small tip. Near the start, small vendors sell snacks and drinks. As a unique local encounter, you may also find a man handling snakes, offering visitors the chance to take memorable photographs.Be Prepared: Bring water, sun protection (a hat and sunscreen), and a towel to dry your feet. While the walk is generally safe, do watch your footing in areas where the sand is softer.

We co-booked the Fairy Thale Stream on an early afternoon excursion.First things first: The ratings are very different. Everything from a dirty mud hole to a wow is there. I can only say we were here in April and we enjoyed it very much here!At the top is a larger parking lot and at the entrance to Fairy Thale Stream there are shoe shelves for your own shoes.Then you walk barefoot down the stairs and then always towards the small course of the river.There you can easily wade through the shallow water (never higher than ankle-deep with us). Sometimes the small course of the river is a little wider, but sometimes quite narrow. Only rarely are there larger stones, but you can already see them sticking out of the water. As far as my feet are concerned, I am extremely sensitive, but this path was also very easy for me to master!On the way, you always pass stalls, just like food stalls.It's fun to walk through water there and you can expect a great photo motif! Of course, the gradations from the bright rock over to the red sand are particularly beautiful.It's not for nothing that it's also called the Mini Grand Canyon.In between there is also a man who hangs a snake around your neck for 50,000 VND, if desired 🤷‍♀️All in all, we didn't expect much extra and can now say that we really liked it!

About 1.5 km, about 40 minutes walk, plus a photo break, takes about two hours to get back and forth. There is shade along the way and less sun exposure. More than half of the journey is shallow stream water, but there are also heights as deep as about knees, and in some places even legs are only 30cm wide, which happens to allow only one person to pass through. The special karst terrain makes the anadromous stream interesting, with white limestone and red dunes, and if you're lucky you'll run into a herding line. It's fun, but be careful not to get too close to them. In addition, there are a number of restaurants and cafes on both shores with relaxing views, each corner is a different picture, perfect for taking pictures. In addition to the lotus pool, I came to this room with a big python that paid to be photographed. It might be a little disappointing if you just want to see the waterfall at the end, but it's a pleasant and memorable memory if you think of it as a retracing exercise after enjoying Mina's seafood, enjoying the views along the way and finding a feel-good landscape restaurant to relax in on a beautiful afternoon.

Fairy stream Mui Ne or Phan Thiet is a great experience.  Easy walk in the stream but certain part you need to step on rocks. Can stop and sipping coffee at coffee places along the stream. See the red land scape at both side of the stream and white rocks caved into caves by nature. Make sure to empty your bladder before the 30 to 45 minutes stroll in the stream. After that take a foot massage and soaked your feet in warm herbal foot bath

Well I read some reviews and most say, don’t bother.But I’m glad that we did, It’s a unique experience and we often found ourselves just laughing.Where we were walking to I have no idea at all. We just went down the steps and walked, as did lots of other people.The water is warm and not very deep. Although occasionally you will come across a few deep places with slippery rocks underneath. But it’s easy to walk along.Along the way you will see people say at tables eating their dinners.Or bars set up along the stream.But the main thing is the different coloured sand and rock formations on either side.So ignore any negativity and experience it for yourself

Unique out of the world experience to walk through the stream with red mountains on one side & greenery on the other. Walk for at least 1 Km to enjoy the beauty of this place & also with lesser crowds. You can also visit the Vita Garden Zoo on the way. Approx duration 1 hour

What is fascinating is how something so small has been packaged so well. The colourful stone bed and rocks are beautiful. Stream cold but muddy as so many have gone in that. But a beautiful picture moment. If you are visiting that area, worth just exploring they hype.

The Fairy Stream is free , we parked our scooter outside and walked  15 -20 minutes up the narrow sandy stream , clear water and no rubbish , the strata of different coloured sand formations were interesting, we’ve never seen anything  like it .We even saw a small snake in the water a testament to the quality of the water .
Promenade très agréable, les pieds dans l’eau, semi ombragée, cours d’eau qui conduit à une petite cascade. Franchement on y a passé un bon moment
lovely experience walking in the stream and along the banks. The cliff are made up of different coloured sand. a nice way to spend an hour or two

We were lucky to be the only ones on the stream when we went.  I would imagine, when tourists return, this would lose a lot of it's charm.You simply approach the stream, take of your sandals and walk to the waterfall at the end and back again.  No litter, which is an unusual treat in Vietnam.  We enjoyed it, others might not see what the fuss is about.
Clean but nothing special...i just dont like the smell along that street....i think its a fish sauce factory

The stream opens at 6am. We were there at 9am and it was already way too hot. It's a nice walk, kids will love it. Don't get bothered by your shoes, you'll have services around the stream who allow you to drop your shoes (and clean your feet afterwards with fresh water) for only 5k per person.

It’s a winding sand flatbed shallow stream. Barely covers your toes. It’s a pleasant walk with many photo opportunities, including an opportunity to have your photo taken with a 6 foot snake (non venomous). There are refreshments available including ice cream rolls more seem in Phu Quoc. Very calm and serene , cheap entrance fee, allow for an hour or two.

Kids love splashing around as you walk up the stream. Sand colour and formations very interesting. Shame further up near the end restaurants sweep there rubbish into it.Nice couple of hours walking and icecream in later afternoon.

We headed here just after 4pm to avoid the heat and it was a great arvo excursion for the whole family to explore the stream, the geographic landscape and the waterfall. There are some nice drink spots along the way to keep hydrated and it is really well maintained. Great place
Cảnh đẹp kết hợp với đồi núi rất phóng khoáng và mát mẻ. Suối chảy tạo thành một lối đi rất mát mẻ. Một phần thiết rất khác
A beautiful place , it’s natural , entrance fee is very cheap 15000VND/ person , it’s valuable to visit it. Enjoy your trip .

15,000vnd entrance fee plus 5,000vnd  to park a bicycle. A lovely walk along a stream leading to a waterfall, above knee deep in a few places-I am quite short, slippery underfoot from rotting leaves in one place, other than that it’s an easy stroll. Beautiful scenery, cafes along the stream are currently closed. Definitely worth a visit

Этот ручей был в минутах 10 езды от нашего отеля.Въезд первый раз проехали мимо, настолько он неприметный. Байк бросить негде, оставили через дорогу.Прогулялись по ручью, посмотрели.Место необычное, фото получаются зрелищные

최소 20분 정도는 걸으셔야 사진찍고 예쁜 볼만한 곳이 나옵니다.처음에는 아무것도 없지만 당황하지 않으셔도 괜찮아요..ㅋㅋㅋㅋ 다만 물이 흐르는데도 불구하고 관리가 잘 되지않아서.. 그점이 조금 아쉽습니다.(전형적인 물 썩은 냄새가 좀 나요) 그리고 그 외에는 한번쯤 가볼만한 곳이었어요.저는 개인적으로 택시 이용해서 갔습니다.왔다갔다 다녀오실만 해요. 그리고 입장료 있습니다 ! 아주 저렴하지만, 원래는 입장료 받지 않았다고 들었는데 인기가 많아지면서 최근 생긴것같아요. 걷다보면 카페도 있고 쉴수있는 곳이 있긴하지만 오래 있을순 없고 저는 코코넛 한잔 먹고왔네요 :) You might be in a panic when you arrive at Fairy Stream, but you don't have to. You have to walk for at least 20 minutes, so you can enjoy beautiful nature. As you walk, you'll find a coffee shop that drank coconut milk. You can take a taxi or a package trip (one-day jeep tour).

We hired a local guide to take us out for the day and went here. You don't need shoes. You leave the car park and drop down the steps into the stream which is clear and refreshing to the feet. Just a short walk to where the water flows thru the rocks with a chance to buy drinks food etc.

Идти приходится непосредственно по руслу ручья, Очень живописные склоны из красного песчаника. В конце пути, если подняться на берег, будет кафешка, от которой идет дорога в сторону Майне, чтобы не возвращаться по воде.

We did the sunrise tour to the white sand dunes using the tour through our Mui Ne Budget Hotel, and part of the tour is to visit the fairy trail which I would recommend doing.There is an extra entry fee which I can’t remember exactly the price (I think it was 20,000 each). It isn’t expensive anyway.The stream and surroundings make for some lovely pictures, and there is a very small waterfall at the end. The walk itself is actually quite short but nevertheless it’s worth seeing.
어찌보면 모래가 고운 일반 진흙탕 느낌?오전에 방문할 것을 권한다. 작은 사막언덕이 있고 그늘이 없는곳은 매우 뜨거울 수 있으니 주의.
Interesting red dunes and river/stream in the middle of a busy built up area. Nice part of the 1/2 day tour.
Aardig om hier even doorheen te lopen als je in de buurt bent.Niet wereldschokkend, wat rommelig.Petje op tegen de zon!

I went at 7 am and had a great time. I think due to Coronavirus, not many visitors overall so the experience was even better. It is not fantastic, but you can take really cool pictures and videos.Many people stop half way, but if you continue all the way you will end at a small waterfall. Very small...You can watch our video about this place and Mui Ne on our Youtube Channel - RoamingRebels Vlog 19 (link in bio)

Der Ausflug hat uns sehr gut gefallen, es war zwar wenig Wasser im Bachlauf, aber die Eindrücke der mehrfarbigen Sandformationen waren beeindruckend!Der Eintritt ist sehr günstig und man ist bemüht das Ambiente noch mit Blumen, die gepflanzt wurden und Schaukeln, die sich für Fotomotive eignen, aufzuwerten.Ja, leider ist das Thema Müll in Vietnam sehr groß, aber das Bewusstsein der Menschen ist auf das Thema Umweltschutz einfach noch nicht eingerichtet. Das muss ganz vorne, z.B. In der Schule anfangen und vllt auch bei Verstößen mit Strafen belegt werden.

It’s a nice little attraction for this town. The walk is easy and can be done in 10-15 minutes to the main attraction that is shown in most of the pictures. One tip is to walk upstream. When we went there,  there were not a lot of people and once you enter the stream you may be tempted to walk down stream, don’t, go upstream against the current instead. Probably best to avoid this spot if you have a cut on your foot, since you have to walk barefooted. Not sure how clean that water is.
Thời điểm đi khám phá nơi đây đẹp nhất là vào tháng 7 trở đi. Chúng ta sẽ thấy thác nước, lội nước và cảm nhận vẻ đẹp thơ mộng

Wij waren hier nog redelijk vroeg (8:30u) en er waren nog weinig andere mensen waardoor we een hoop foto's konden maken. Je loopt door een ondiep waterstroompje op zacht zand. Je schoenen kun je voor 5000 aan het begin afgeven. Na zo'n 100m kun je een kaartje kopen en het stroompje volgen tot. aan een (klein) watervalletje. Al met al de moeite waard!

You wonder where you are heading when you turn in. It's a mess. But the stream is actually quite charming. A slow moving flow of water maybe 2m wide and 2in deep. Meandering for hundreds of metres through sand dunes that are slowly being eroded away. Quite busy.
Nice to see this but Way on the end is dirty and where water is deeper there water stinks. There were lot of visitors....

You have to pay a ticket but it is very cheap (15.000VDN) One of the few places I see they take care of. No shoes are allowed ( they will keep them), there are bins on the way, it is clean and refreshing for the hot summer days. They are also growing flowers around and put swings. It was a little bit crowded but yet you can relax in there.
Kosleg gåtur. Nydeleg fargespill med rød sand, små klipper, hvit sand, vann og vegetasjon. Tok 50 minutter fram og tilbake, med utallige fotostopp.

Wie muss man sich das vorstellen? Ein fußhoher Wasserlauf, welcher sich durch eine schluchtenartige Landschaft schlängelt. Teilweise gesäumt durch hohe weiße Felshänge, welche in roten Sand übergehen. Immer wieder tauchen Kaufstände am Rand auf. Die Händler lassen einen jedoch in Ruhe. Sofern der Wasserlauf mehr Wasser führt, hat man die Möglichkeit am Ende einen kleinen Wasserfall zu sehen, unter den man sich auch drunterstellen kann.Einen kleinen Ausflug ist das Wert. Man muss jedoch einen kleinen Eintritt zahlen. Wenn man jedoch über einen Reiseleiter bucht, muss man sich darum nicht kümmern.
Very relaxad place. We were walking in the water and looked at all the beautiful colours. Peaceful and relaxing. Much people! An advice go there early on the morning.
Petite ballade sympa, le pieds dans l'eau. On remonte le ruisseau, entre végétation et falaises. Il faut y aller le matin car beaucoup moins de monde...

C'est une balade agréable les pieds dans l'eau au milieu de paysages assez inhabituels. Allez y le matin pour avoir de l'ombre. Et faîtes la partie payante parce que l'autre partie est vraiment sale. Par contre, vous ne serez probablement pas seuls (c'est un euphémisme), à moins de venir à 6h du mat peut-être.
입장권이 성인 1만5천동 소인 6천동으로 기억하고 상점에서 신발을 맡아주는데 비닐 하나 챙겨가서 담아가시면 돈 절약 됩니다.끝까지 다 가면 50분 가량 코스인데굳이 끝까지 갈 필요는 없는 듯 합니다.

This was part of a day trip out that we took from our hotel (Victoria Phan Thiet Beach Resort & Spa) ... and we did 3 or 4 other things as part of that day trip .. this would have been a good one on it's own too for a casual walk up the Fairy Stream.  It was a huge surprise directly off the road and walk up the stream ... very interesting geology and geography.  Cool water on your feet and soft sand underneath.  The day we went, it was about 40C, so very hot.  The walk was interesting, at the end near the waterfall the footing is slightly precarious only because is rocky under your bare feet.   The Fairy Stream is well worth doing, as it's just so very different to most things you'll do.  Recommended.
Eintritt 15.000 VDSchön gelegen. Tolles Farbenspiel, sauber!Ist im Tour Paket zu den Dünen enthalten. Der Eintritt ist aber nicht includiert.
I really enjoyed the walk through the riverbed , the colours and plants and other curiosities to see.  Specially when its hot this is a good idea.

Det er et lille vandløb man kan gå op langs. De første 100 m er der mange små boder hvor man kan drikkevare mad med mere, men det aftager heldigvis lidt op da Vandløbet. Man behøver ikke sko eller klip klapper, man kan sagtens gå med bare tæer. De gør noget for at samle affald op på denne strækning, hvilket er dejligt. Der er et lille vandfald for enden, men det er ikke specielt stort, men klipperne langs vandløbet er flotte. Tage shorts eller lignende på, i slutningen af Vandløbet bliver det dybere, da vi gik(november) var der steder hvor vandet gik til knæet.

Nice and relaxing place! Amazing how this Stream is coming just in the middle of Mui Ne, but it’s taking you to the totally different world. Beautiful red sand and afternoon time it wasn’t too crowded. Worth to visit!

Le fairu stream est un lieu depaysant et riche de travail de millions d année de la nature entre la couche rouge et la couche blanche.Ce lit de riviere rouge et ses.petites tortues dans le lit de la riviére si vous faites tres attention.Ce que l on pourra regretter , c est ce nombre de paillote régulièrement retrouvées le long du tracé.Neanmoins a voir absolument et seulement r a cause des paillottes.Entrée 15000 vnd par personne

Mis à part la masse des gens qui font cela en même temps que vous, c'est plutôt sympa de remonter ce petit cours d'eau à pieds nus, comme les autres. Chouette balade qui dure environ 2h.On est un peu transporté ailleurs, côté nature, et ca fait du bien.

Very nice walk through beautiful red cliffs and jungle, the walk is about 1km to the end. Was very peaceful when we went at around 3:45 but on the way back, hoards of Chinese were on their way so I imagine the earlier you go the better 😊
It was a long walk to the end, approx. 1km according to our tour guide. We stopped few times for photography as both sides of the stream have beautiful scenery.

The start of the fairy streem is at a bridge where you have to buy a ticket.The beginning is kinda smelly and seems dirty but after a few meters it get better.The water is clear and the sand and the cliffs look amazing.Was totally worth it

인터넷에서 <요정의 샘>에 대한 정보를 찾으면 <동양의 그랜드 캐년>이라고 불린다고 소개되어 있습니다.  무이네 지프투어에서 제일 처음으로 방문하는 곳인데, 인터넷에서 소개하기로는 입장료를 받지 않는다고 했는데 입장료가 있었습니다.  <요정의 샘>이라는 이름 때문에 물이 맑을 것으로 생각했는데 모래 바닥이라 거의 흙탕물이었습니다.  동양의 그랜드 캐년이란 이름을 붙이기가 부끄러울 정도로 너무 작은 규모였지만, 나름대로 붉은 흙, 작은 규모의 협곡과 실개천은 아기자기한 멋이 있었습니다.  계속 맨발로 다녀야 하니 가볍게 신고, 입고 갈 것을 추천합니다.
졸졸 흐르는 물을 따라 걷다보니 덜 덥게 느껴졌고 군데군데 구경할 거리가 많아서 좋았습니다. 거대한 절벽은 아니었지만 멋진 풍경을 볼 수 있었습니다.
仙女溪是美奈一道奇特的风景线，溪水底部是红色的岩石，你可以拍张照片，赤脚漫步在清凉的溪水中，享受凉爽的空气。仙女溪的溪水里有很多很多细小的沙子，脱了鞋子走在上面，感觉在做足底SPA，非常舒服。Suối Tiên Mũi Né mang một vẻ đẹp bồng lai tiên cảnh, là điểm dừng chân tham quan hấp dẫn.

Leuke wandeling door het water. Bij de ingang even een toegangskaartje scoren en dan mag je ca 2 km door het water banjeren. Lekker zachte ondergrond alleen net aan het einde van de Tour - net voor de waterval - wordt het wat rotsachtig in het water maar met beleid bereik je de waterval.

It takes an hour and is well worth the 15000 dong. A nice little stroll with some great views. There a quite a lot of cheap restaurants along the way so you can hydrate. You don’t need a guide for this. Just go up and down the nice little creek 😊

The scenery and rock formations around the stream were beautiful but there was a lot of litter around the banks! The canyon like rocks around the stream were lovley and made for brilliant pictures - however there was a lot of litter on the banks of the stream!! Lots of places to stop, along the way, for a drink. Overall good place to have a walk along and spend a couple of hours.
sau khi đi đồi cát khá nắng nóng thì gia đình mình ghé đây, tụi nhỏ rất thích lội nước, còn chụp hình với con trăn, tiếc là suối có đoạn làm bê tông giả đá ở lòng suối nên khá đau chân

Walk thorough this stream running through a canyon of red rock cliffs and jungle trees. the water flown from Ham Tien's Dinh Mountain made you feel chill during walking under the hot sun. Few checking points for tickets. Keep your ticket nicely as they tend to ask you to show the ticket again.

Wij zijn er vroeg in de ochtend naartoe gegaan. Het was even moeilijk te vinden. Eenmaal beneden schoenen uit, broekspijpen omhoog en door zanderig water lopen. Heerlijke afkoeling. Het eerste deel wat wij hadden was nog rommelig met vuil langs de kant. Als je eenmaal langs de kassa bent wordt het prachtig. Rood zand, overhangende bomen, zandrotsblokken, en als je tot het einde loopt een watervalletje. Daar wordt het ook rustiger omdat de meeste toeristen niet zo ver doorlopen. Duurt ong 30 min heen. Je kunt er makkelijk lopen, af en toe een kuiltje of een steen maar als je goed kijkt zie je dat. Langs de route natuurlijk drink en eetgelegenheden.

Lovely place to visit, go early to avoid the heat and mass of tourists! There are lots of vendors along the way to grab a drink and food. There is a small waterfall at the end of the stream. There is a fee of 15KVND.

Der Fairy Stream trennt den Regenwald vom roten Sandstein. Man kann durch einen kleinen Fluss wandern und es bieten sich schöne Fotoperspektiven. Mehr als 1 Std. braucht man hier allerdings nicht einplanen, da sich die Landschaft widerholt. Der Eintritt mit 15.000VND ist sehr human und erschwinglich.
Interesting and worth a lookBeautiful surroundings, with the colourful sand, cliffs and palm trees!

Exactly like the pictures and adverts, but the adverts says you are aloud to climb the hills and you are not, take money with you as there is an entrance fee. Difficult terrain if you have small children or older persons with you.Takes about 40min-60min to walk through. Walk carefully as you can not see the bottom and there is dips. Well worth it tough.
想像していたよりも楽しかったです。足首くらいの小川の中を歩くのですが砂が気持ちよく思っていたよりも楽しくて気持ちよかったです。暑い日だったのでもう少し水が冷たかったらもっとよかったかも。往復４０分くらいかかりますが、途中にカフェなどもあり休憩もできます。靴を脱いで歩くので手に持つのが嫌な人はリュックなどに入れるようにビニール袋などがあると便利かと。街からも近いのでオススメです。

Bạn sẽ phải đi bộ dọc con suối. Con suối khá thú vị. Khung cảnh khá đẹp với những đồi cát đỏ và trắng. Bên dưới là suối chảy. Điểm đến khá mát mẻ do đi bộ dưới suối cà trên thì có nhiều cây che bóng mát. Nên đến đây.

We visited Fairy Stream while staying in Mui Ne. The landscape is just breathtaking, beautiful! We did not have enough time to do the whole stream but we took a lots of pictures of this awesome stream.

Khi nắng vẫn còn oi ả và gay gắt, bạn nên đến đây, sục chân vào dòng suối mát lành. Đi dạo thong dong dọc con suối và chiêm ngưỡng những biến đổi kì diệu của các đụn cát với màu sắc khác nhau

Nach vier Jahren besuchten wir die Fairy Strem wieder. Es hat sich nicht viel verändert, außer das man nun eine geringe Eintritsgebühr bezahlen muss. Kann kann seine Schuhe am Eingang abgeben. auch dafür wird eine Gegühr fällig. Diesmal war sehr viel Wasser im Fluß und es macht echt Spaß bis zum Wasserfäll zu laufen

The thing is to walk in the stream. The depth varies from ankle deep to thigh deep. At the end is a small waterfall. The sand is soft and the water is warm. Take sandals as its best to walk barefoot. There are restaurants along the way.
Színek játéka, kellemes séta a patakban, napközben megszorul a levegő a szurdokban és nagyon meleg van. Érdemes a kora reggeli órákra időzíteni a kirándulást.

You have to walk up the stream approximately 800m before you arrive to these water eroded (in my opinion) granite stone. Look authentic, coupled with the red sand and blue sky as background is surely a nice picture taking spot. The is an pay ostrich ride for you to cross the stream if you are not intend to walk. According to local, the stream water can be as high as knee level during wet season, so suggest on dry season which is barely above ankle. Also don't wear shoe, slipper or bare foot are the best.
Ce n’est pas exceptionnel mais c’est à voir si l’,on est pas loin. Dépaysant de marcher pieds nus en regardant ce paysage

Typowy spacer po wodzie 45 minut w jedną stronę.  Kolorowo super foty można porobić.  Wstęp 15 000 A dzieci 7000. Na trasie są stragany np z lodami albo siedzi sobie pan z wężem można foty robić (nie pytałam po ile😂) płytka woda A ostatni odcinek do kolan

В общем и целом делать тут нечего.Но когда проводишь в одном месте довольно долго времени, начинаешь думать чем занять свое время. И мысли первым делом приводят сюда, когда все активности уже перепробованы.Если вы не живете рядом, то лучше всего ехать сюда на такси.Вас высадят перед мостом и внимание, вам под мост)))Спустились по лесенке вниз, вы упираетесь в, на первый взгляд, кошмарный кошмар, похожий на наши стоковые воды. Такое ощущение складывается из-за цвета воды и консистенции под ней. Красная глина под водой делает воду неприлично грязной. НА САМОМ ДЕЛЕ она идеально чистая и не бойтесь снять тапочки и направился под мост.По ручью вам топать от 10 до 30 минут, в зависимости от цели. Если вы хотите посмотреть на песчаные холмы то вам все время прямо, если хотите встретить закат, то прямо минут 10 до первых красных холмов и резко влево и вверх.По дороге весьма колоритненько. Пасутся коровы. Живут местные аборигены, в кафешках готовят еду.Сам закат занимает минут 15, но это неописуемая красота, которую надо видеть.
Мне по душе такие прогулки, я это понял сразу) если не брезгуете проплывающую мимо крысу, не против толп туристов (иногда), то обязательно прогуляйтесь пешком от Муйне до водопада по речью.
The fairy stream need to walk in small stream with shallow sand. If you want to get the best picture you can climb up the small hill.
초입에 베트남 전통 모자 팔아요! 15만동인가 그랬던거 같은데, 여러개사면 할인도 해주셔요~ 그거 처음부터 사서 쓰고 들어가세용! :) 군데군데 사진찍을곳도 많고, 재밌었어용
Będąc w Mui Ne lub okolicy warto to miejsce zobaczyć, zwłaszcza, że tą jak i większość atrakcji w Wietnamie bilety są tanie.
This is about 1,5 km walk along the shallow and clean stream. Very picturesce (so good luck for you cameras)

We decided to visit the stream as a last minute decision. We found it on our own and would suggest that if you are staying in Mui Ne there is no reason to pay for a tour. The beauty of the stream is the enjoyment of the landscapes. They are varied and quite different from anything we have seen. The a ability to walk barefoot in the stream to the waterfalls barefoot was wonderful. There are a few places along the stream to purchase drinks and food from locals. Would suggest a visit in the early morning when it is cooler and less busy.

I read many mixed reviews before going here. Finally we decided to go, since we were in the area and we do not regret it. The kids (9 and 10 years old) loved the experience. We went in the middle of the day and it was quite crowded. Not exactly a lonely walk, as we hoped for. I don't mind paying a small entrance fee, as long as they keep the area clean. The stream and the surroundings are really colourful, we got lots of nice photos. Ostriches at the beginning are a nice addition, especially interesting for children. You can ride them for 150k dong - quite expensive.That's ok for children, but adults are maybe a bit too heavy to be taken around on the bird in my opinion.I missed some kind of signs explaining how far it is to the waterfall. After some time, the water gets deeper and you don't get to see any nice rocks around anymore, so we turned back. We didn't know how for to go to reach a waterfall. Anyways, it was a nice stroll, we enjoyed it, got many nice photos and I would recommended to other families.

Balade sympathique d’une heure, les pieds dans l’eau et le site est propre!! C’est payant ( 0€60 ) mais le résultat est là, poubelles et toilettes sur le chemin mais au moins ce site est propre. La cascade à cette période ne donne qu’un petit filet d’eau.

The Fairy Stream was a nice little activity, we did it in the afternoon not so many tourist. It’s 15.000 per person. You have to walk 500 meters before it really starts, but when it starts it’s good. It’s nice walking in the stream, enjoying the red sand and the weather. If you are passing by Mui Ne, you might as well a spent an hour in the Fairy Stream.

Ondanks de drukte leuk om te doen.  Eerste 100 meter doorlopen dan wordt de kloof steiler en echt mooi.  Ook nog de mogelijk een stuk door te steken over de rode duinen. Kom vroeg want het kan ongenadig heet worden.  Toegang is niet meer gratis 15.000VND. Helemaal doorlopen naar het einde dan wordt het rustiger. De meeste Koreanen en Chinezen lopen niet het hele eind. De Russen zaten kennelijk nog aan de wodka want die heb ik helemaal niet gezien.  Het water is enkeldiep. De meeste lopen op blote voeten maar ik prefereren mijn crogs.

I visited the Fairy Stream as part of a tour with my hostel. I also went to the White Sandunes, Red Sandunes and Fishing Village (please look out for these respectively).I was lead through someone’s shop and down some stairs to the stream. You should have a plan for your shoes. I was allowed to leave my trainers in my tour guides cars. If you are alone, maybe a backpack is a good idea. Apparently, some locals let you leave your shoes with them saying “Don’t worry about them”, but demand money on your return.I am not sure why it is called “The Fairy Stream”. What it is, is a a small stream in meandering through a forest on one side and amazing multicoloured sand dunes on the other. The water is very shallow. It barely covers your feet. It’s lovely to walk through the water water. There are grains of beach sand on bottom of the stream so it’s like walking on the beach. It costs 15,000 VD to enter the stream. At the start there are vendors selling small things and snacks. I also saw a cafe. Carry on down and observe the wonder of nature.

Приехали мы к ручью на такси. Там висит большая вывеска. Спустились под мостиком по ступеням. Советую оставаться в тапочках так как много камней лежат на дне ручья и в мутной воде их не видно. Вход стал платным - 15 000 донгов. С прошлого раза можно заметить мусор и разные кафешки вначале пути. Поставили туалет. Сидят охраники вдоль ручья.  Также мы заметили, что изменился облик каньона - народ хочет залезть повыше и грунт постоянно из-за этого перемешивается. Пейзаж раньше был похож на марсианский, но сейчас много растительности растет по обоим склонам ручья. Но еще можно найти прекрасные места для фото и любования красотой природы. Иногда глубина ручья достигает выше колен и в конце пути нам охраник запретил идти по ручью из-за большой глубины и указал на гору. Дети не смогли по горячему песку залезть наверх и мы вернулись обратно так и не увидев небольшой водопад. Но настроение было хорошим и прогулка нам понравилась. На память остались прекрасные фотографии!Советую взять с собой зонт или солнезащитный крем -так как много открытых и солнечных мест. В кафешках продают холодные напитки и мороженое.Также недалеко от кафешек можно покататься на страусе)

We went there at the afternoon. It was a nice and beautiful walk! Not too crowded at that time. We paid 30k for 2 tickets and it was worth the money. It wasn’t too warm when we went there but we really think it’s nice if it’s really warm just walk there with the feet dipped in the stream. Took approximately 1 hour.

We spend under 40 mins here. There is a 15, 000VND wntrance fee and parking fee for motorbike of 10,000VND but we probably could have got away with leaving the bike on the main street for free. You begin the walk along the stream of water and follow it as it meanders around and gets slightly deeper in parts but ne we above knee level. The sand colours on the rock formations beside you as you walk our lovely. There is a tiny waterfall at the end.
요정의 샘이라기엔 조금 더러운데 안쪽으로 계속 걷다보면 예쁜 곳이 나와요 중간 중간 간식 사먹을 곳도 있어요. 저희 투어 아저씨가 여기에서 알아서 걸어가라며 버리고 갔어요ㅠㅠ....

A wet walk in the stream without shoes. A nice climb on the red sand. Beautiful formations and nice greenery. A beautiful walk on a hot day. Pretty crowded but everybody wants to see the place. Around 1.5 to 2 hours would be enough to enjoy the place. A nice setting for photos. The walk is easy but at places can be slippery. Definitely a must when you are in the area... You can get there during a tour, but then little time is given to enjoy the place. You can also get there by the local bus or taxi. It definitely is worth the entrance fee...
На входе берут 15000 донг. Вначале не очень чисто выглядит и босиком идти не хочется, но буквально через 5 минут начинается красота. Можно сделать кучу красивых фотографий.

I have really enjoyed it. Once again the tours are a problem because they give you 20-30 minutes. But you have the time just to see the first part of the stream (packed with tourists). Continuing there are other beautify and more quiet parts. I would definitely go back on my own if I’ll have the chance again. Beautiful contrasts of colours and very peaceful.

de reisleider had gezegd dat we tot de enkels in het water kwamen. het begin al meteen tot over de knieen, tot grote hilariteit. Dit was echter maar een meter of 5 haha. Lekker rustig en alles in je op laten nemen.

I enjoyed my visit. Can spend an hour or two walking through the stream. It didn't get 5 stars as they actually offer emu rides which is something I personally don't agree with. The actual stream is nice though.

un canyon avec des couleurs magnifiques , ils viennent vous chercher directement à l’hôtel et vous emmène sur le site .les pieds dans l’eau vous avancez dans un cours d'eau parsemé de falaises colorées

Der Besuch des Faire Stream sollte unbedingt relativ früh am Tag stattfinden, da dann noch nicht so viele Touristen unterwegs sind, Nach der Entrichtung von 15 K Dong geht man barfuß etwa 2 km einen kleinen Bach entlang, der meiner Ansicht nach relativ sauber ist. Am Anfang des Weges noch Verkaufsstände und kleine Restaurants. Schöne rote und weiße Gesteinsformationen mit wunderbaren Erosionsformen. Ein lohnenswerter Spaziergang!
เป็นทางเดินเข้าไปยาวๆ มีธารน้ำไหล ด้านข้างเป็นกำแพงดินสีแดงๆ สวยงาม ถ่ายรูปสวย เข้าไปข้างในสุดมีน้ำตก แต่ไม่จำเป็นต้องเข้าไปดูก็ได้ น้ำตกเล็กนิดเดียว น้ำก็น้อย

Entry is very cheap, try not to take much to carry, you are fine barefoot so have a bag or something for your sandals. You need a hat if it is a hot day. The water is nice and although there are some rocky bits it is mostly sand.  The walk to the waterfall at the top is about 2km and very pleasant. We stopped on the way back at the mini zoo gardens. Nice place for a cold drink, you can feed their crocodile if you want, and have a hold of a python.

Ei Mui Ne:ssa paljon muuta nähtävää ollut, joten Fairy Stream toi vaihtelua päivään. Tiketti 15d eli noin 0.50e. Kävellään punertavavesistä jokea pitkin pienelle vesiputoukselle. Joen varressa mehun myyjiä, pari ravintolaa ja myyntikojuja. Maksullinen alue on siisti, mutta ulkopuolella joen reunat roskaiset.

We hadden een jeep tour privé geboekt, wij wilde eerst niet binnengaan omdat er ons niets verteld was over die extra 15.000 dong dat je moest betalen. Ik zag dan dat vuile water en dacht ieuw daar wil ik niet in. Ben blij dat we het toch maar hebben gedaan want was zeker de moeite waard

Härlig promenad genom vattnet. Fina omgivningar och vackra vyer. Vad vi inte visste var att de nu tar betalt för att promenera. 15.000 VND för vuxna och 6.000 för barn mellan 6-9år. Det är lågt vatten så set blev behagligt.

We walked to the Fairy Stream from our hotel and bought the d15k tickets from a stall. Signs pointed down steps marked "To the Fairy Stream". These ended after about 15 steps in a running muddy brook. Looking upstream we saw overhanging banks lined with the normal plastic detritus of Vietnam. The wife stood flustered at  the bottom of the steps, convinced we had somehow taken a wrong turning somewhere on the 15 step descent. Were we really asking her to venture out, barefoot, into what may have been a sewage overflow? A couple returning downstream after completing their journey reassured her that it was much better further up. We set out!To be honest we had a good time and a laugh.After about 200m the plastic foliage thins out, you pass a ticket inspection point ant the 'fairy stream' itself starts properly. The colours of the eroding sand dunes and the way that it forms strange shapes in the bank almost like rock pinnacles, are worth seeing. Along the route are several cafes/bars that we didn't explore. The waterfall at the end is hardly Niagra Falls but it gives a sense of completion to the journey. Be aware that a) the underwater rocks can be trick to naavigate at times, mainly at the very beginning and end near the waterfall. In the middle you are really walking on a sandy bed.b) retain your ticket after you have bought it. There are several access points to the Stream so there is an inspection point at the proper entrance and also on the return. Presumably you pay again if you don't have a ticket.Finally the people who run the place should be applauded for their attempts to keep it preserved. Many prominent signs saying don't climb/erode/write on the banks, no littering and no using the place as a natural toilet. Modern waste bins and portaloos were plentiful and  well placed at intervals along the route. As a result the Fairy Stream itself was fairly clear of rubbish apart from near the neighbouring cafes,who obviously couldn't give two hoots. Sadly also it seems, neither could the rich tourists who also mindlessly charge up the banks for selfies or scribble loveharts in the sand of the side walls. Perhaps the security guards would get off their bums from checking tickets and begin to police this area a bit more in order to preserve the future of this place.Given it 4 stars, mainly for the laughs we had en route. I think you have to be in the right frame of mind for this one. Probably kids would really enjoy it.

Niet gedacht zoveel schoonheid hier in het overigens verder saaie Mui Ne tegen te komen. Echt een leuk uitje voor ieder moment van de dag, ook met kinderen. Volwassenen betalen een poepie (15k) en kinderen een scheetje (7k).Prachtige foto's gemaakt en lekker met de kinderen door het water gebanjerd. Water is lekker laag en op sommige plekken tot op kniehoogte.Loop wel door tot na de omgevallen palmboom, want daar wacht een prachtige waterval waar je nog even lekker in kunt afkoelen.Erg mooi!

Door de bedding van een zoetwaterstroompje lopen langs een mooi kleurenpalet.Voor 15.000 vnd p.p. kun je hier genieten van lokaal natuurschoon.Twee nadelen: Druk bezocht door klimmende medebezoekers en ook hier veel, plastic, afval in het water en langs de oevers.Al met al een geinige onderbreking.
Fajne kolory ,miły spacer ..Vietnamczycy z każdego miejsca zrobią rozrywke by zarobić ;) 15000 bilet wstepu a parking dla skutera 20000 😁 warte zobaczenia raz i tyle :)

We were not expecting much. We went for a tour by our hotel where we paid 32 usd for 5 pax to go to 4 places - fishing village, white sand dunes, red sand dunes and fairy stream. We arrived at the fairy stream first and it was pretty cool. We had to walk in a stream so it’s best that you take off your shoes. And we just walked down and there were little potholes but it was the best part right? There were spots to take pictures along the way and it seemed like it wasn’t going to end. We were given an hour there by our driver so I Guess it was pretty sufficient to cover most of it! It was just a relaxing walk while stopping to get pictures kinda area!
Wenn man in Mui Ne ist, sollte man sich für den Fairy Stream ein Stündchen Zeit nehmen. Es ist wirklich mal etwas anderes und man kann hier tolle Fotos knipsen :)
장점-요정의 샘물은 너무 아름답고 특별한 경험.단점-동네 양아치가 입장료를 또 달라고함.그야말로 한국의 그랜드캐니언이라고 할 만큼아름답고 신비한 경험이었습니다.무이네 여행시 꼭 가보아야할 곳이라고 느꼈습니다.하지만 입장시 굳이 설명하지도 않은 입장료를 동네 청년들이 걷어가네요. 큰돈은 아니었지만 기분이 썩 좋지 않았네요.

We really enjoyed the walk and the scenery, the only negative is all the trash lying around everywhere which was a real shame😢The entrance fee is 15000 Dong for adults and you should remember to keep your ticket the whole time, since they can ask for it along the way. Absolutely worth a visit😉Alex & Parisa
A fun little thing to do if you want to get out for the day. Not a lot to see but about 2-3 hours to the falls and back.
A nice walk down a small stream only ankle deep with water. Amazing sand dune formations along the sides of the stream.
Горы красного песка. Внизу этих гор течёт ручей, который заканчивается водопадом. Ходить по этому ручью надо босиком, обувь оставляешь либо перед входом, либо в машине гида. Вход стоит 15 000 донг.

Wir sind mit dem Mopped bis zur Brücke gefahren und ich bin ganz bequem die Treppe runter in den sehr wenig Wasser führenden Bach gelangt. Entgegen anders lautenden Berichten kostet der Zutritt wenige hundert Meter weiter 15 k VND! Es gibt sehr interessante Erosionsstrukturen zu sehen. Kann man auch mehrmals zu verschiedenen Tageszeiten ansehen, das Licht verändert die Landschaft wesentlich.

Don't look forward to the little waterfall at the end, it's the journey there through the shallow stream that makes this place worth visiting. Tickets 15,000d and they check them on multiple stops so don't try to sneak in for free 😬

El paseo por el Arroyo de las Hadas nos pareció una auténtica preciosidad: Ir andando por el cauce del arroyo, con las dunas de arenas de colores a ambos lados, la vegetación, sin apenas gente, escuchando el ruido de la naturaleza... Fue encantador.Si se va al atardecer ¡Cuidado con los mosquitos! Que no se olvide el repelente.
The fairy stream is fun and quite relaxing to do. You walk without shoes in water. Easy with kids too.You get to see nice colorful sand landscapes.
Worth a visit on a hot day. Some nice scenery and a pleasant stroll with some good photo opportunities.
如果是骑摩托去的 在大门口找地方停车 不要根据指引开进去 里面停车费要两万越南盾哟，从入口出发往右走，一路深深浅浅，因为水太浑浊看不清水下的情况。一直走 走到每一脚都是小石子在刺激你的脚 你就可以穿鞋了。终点也不远了。拍完照原路返回即可。
Une belle balade 1h30 les pieds dans l'eau. Malgré le nombre de personne à l'entrée, ça ne se ressent pas. Allez-y vous passerez un chouette moment !!
渓流を歩く観光スポットです。途中の切り立った崖は圧巻です。晴れている日は日陰が少ないので帽子や日傘を用意したほうがいいと思います。

i enjoyed walking in the stream with nice view and meet new friends from other country. The water feel cold a bit but everything fine and enjoyable. Spend my breakfast at that area while taking a break. Really recommended.

If you had a heavy night on Saturday head to Suoi Tien, fairy stream. It's a gentle walk to a small water fall with several tiny cafes along the way. It catches the sun at midday and gets hot. You must leave your shoes off and walk in the shallow stream but take care of small pot holes near large rocks.Very relaxing.

An absolute must if you’re in Mui Ne. It’s a magical little place. A little annoying that you have to pay to enter and the tourist cafes set up along the side take away some of that magic but honestly, you’d be hard done to find something similar to this anywhere in the world- if you do then please tell me.
Amazing natural sand  art at work. Beautiful tri color sand and natural sand formation. Challenging walk in knee deep waters as it was raining season.

It’s a nice walk down a shallow stream with some awesome sights. There’s a lot of buildings being built down the stream, so the time to go is now, whilst it’s still relatively quiet. Watch out for snakes, we saw two, but it may have been the same one twice!

Grappig om met je blote voeten / of met Teva sandalen aan door het waterstroompje te ploeteren. Het regende pijpenstelen toen wij er waren, voordeel dat er dus bijna niemand was, maar ook dan is het nog leuk. In de stad, stap je uit op een parkeerterrein en ga je de trap af naar het waterstroompje, daar loop je in het water tussen het groen en even later langs mooie rode zandduinen. Kids vonden het super leuk om te doen.

You walk up a stream in the water which appears muddy but it is just the coloured sand and the disturbance on the bottom from all the tourists. It was really amazing to walk with one side red and the other side white sand.
Nice walk for a couple of kilometres down a steam boarderd by sand dunes. Pity they   are starting to line the walk with market stalls and karaoke are. Get there before it is too 'developed'.

Bitte Badeschuhe mitbringen, da je nach Jahreszeit man fast knietief durchs Wasser läuft (ca halbe Stunde hin u halbe zurück). Der Wasserfall ist je nach Jahreszeit zu sehen oder auch nicht. Auch per öffentlichen Bus gut erreichbar, Eintritt Märchenfluss kostenlos
It is a beautiful place to go for a walk and eat something on the way. Many places they sell coco or ice cream. Also there is a bar and a little zoo on the way.

This stream is a nice way to spend few hours outdoors. It has been red coloured due to the collapsing sand dunes nearby and gives it a natural sand base - you can just walk for couple of kilometres  and take some spectacular pictures of the dunes. Near by is an ostrich farm where you can ride the birds for few USD.

Thiên nhiên đã ban tặng cho nơi này một cảnh quan rất hùng vĩ đúng với cái tên thường gọi của nó "Suối Tiên" với các khối cát được tạo thành do quá trình phong hóa của nắng gió mưa kết hợp với dòng nước từ suối tạo thành một khung cảnh nên thơ. Tuy nhiên, do ý thức rất kém của đại bộ phận dân du lịch khi đến đây đã dần biến nơi đây thành thiên đường ngập rác, rất mong các cơ quan chức năng của tỉnh Bình Thuận vào cuộc để giải quyết các vấn đề còn tồn đọng trên.
Lúc trước khi đi không kì vọng nhiều lắm, nhưng khi đến nơi thì cũng là một trải nghiệm thú vị, đi bộ tầm 15-20p là hết suối nên không mệt (mặc dù trời nắng nóng)

C'est un très bel endroit à découvrir, bien qu'il y a rien d'extraordinaire si ce n'est le côté atypique des lieux, le cadre et paysage avec ces couleurs magnifiques. Nous découvrons les lieux pieds dans l'eau, avec eau chaude mais agréable, entourée de ses montagnes de sables rouges, cette nature flamboyante. Bref un lieu incroyable et insolite que nous trouvons nul part ailleurs au Vietnam,Petit café locale avec stands de souvenirs se trouvent juste devant l'entrée qui je crois est payante (car inclus pour ma part dans un circuit). Autre petits stands de marchant à l'intérieur (très peu) avec jus de coco, petits souvenirs, fruits tel le Durian.
엄청 신기하거나 예쁘거나하는 핫스팟은 아니지만, 이색적인 곳이예요~입구에서 신발 맡겨준다고 하고 가이드해준다고 하시는 친절한 현지인 모녀가 있던데,왠지 나중에 돈 달라고 할 것 같아서 열심히 노노노를 외치며 신발을 가지고 다녔습니다 ㅋㅋ물가라서 모기가 종종 있는 것 같구요, 30~40분 코스로 가볍게 산책하기 좋아요~
Amazing to walk through the stream to the waterfall that's a little underwhelming. The main drawback of this place is waste along the walk and restaurants with loud music at near waterfall.
지프투어 마지막에 들르는 코스. 별로라는 후기를 보고 큰 기대를 하지 않고 갔는데 생각보다 예쁘고 신비로운 곳이었다.  짧은 길이라고 생각했는데 40분정도를 돌아다닐만큼 생각보다 길고 지루하지 않았다.

Very pleasant experience strolling up the stream to the small waterfall at the end. No fee & scenery is great. Occasionally you have to wade through knee deep water. Only downside was the amount of totally unnecessary litter - why?!

It was relaxing to walk along the cool shallow water and seeing the locals selling beautiful handicrafts especially the sand art in glass bottles! Thanks to the small trees along the way we managed to escape from the hot sun while enjoying our stroll.

If you are looking for something low cost to do in Mui ne, this place is a very good option. If you can, go there early in the morning to avoid the crowd of asian tourists and start the route from the upper end - you can even easily walk to this point - if you are staying close to the Ho Quang Canh street, from this spot its about 30 minutes walk. The route  itself takes about 30 minutes and it ends in the city, there are several restaurants on the way where you can buy water or some snack. If you have a few hours to spare in Mui ne, definitely do not miss this place

Dit is iets wat je niet verder moet vertellen. Het is zo mooi om dit te ervaren. Op je blote voeten door een beekje wandelen in een prachtige omgeving. onderweg meerdere plaatsen om te genieten van een verse kokosnoot of juice. ander soort drinken is er natuurlijk ook. Onderweg is er nog een plek waar je kunt vissen of gewoon zitten en relaxen. Dus niet verder vertellen he
무더움을 극복하고 발담구니 좋음! 구경하는데 자꾸 베트남남자가 가이드아닌 이상한 설명을 해주기 시작하더니 1인당 5만동씩 달라고함 서양인들한테는 말도안걸구 우리한테만 말검 그냥 2만동주고 땡침 귀엽긴했지만 ㅠㅠ 덕분에 설명은 들었음

Przyjemny spacer w wąskim zacienionym strumieniu, o czerwonym dnie i brzegach, z wodą do kostek, potem szerszym i wśród skał, dalej nieco głębszym, można odpocząć przy soku z kokosa, zrobić piękne zdjęcia.
start of stream is not much but later on its nice . there is waterfall on the end. and theres restorants and caffes along the way.

Przed wejściem do potoku można zostawić buty kręcącym się tam dzieciakom, opłata jest symboliczna (co łaska), a nie trzeba ich nosić w rękach i chłopcy zarobią sobie na loda. Spacer jest bardzo przyjemny, otoczenie wręcz bajkowe; chociaż dojście do wodospadu nie zawsze możliwe (przy naszym pobycie, w pewnym miejscu, stan wody był na tyle wysoki, że przeszli tylko ci, którzy mieli kostiumy kąpielowe). Po drodze jest też mini zoo

jolie balade très agréable pour faire de jolies photos, arretez vous boire une eau de coco chez la femme qui tient la buvette le plus près de la cascade vous ferez une bonne action car elle donne de l'argent aux enfants des familles qui en ont besoin et peut etre vous emmènera telle  voir les poissons dans son petit restaurant en haut
말하자면 얕으막한 계곡이지만바닥이 진흙이고 대개 평지라 신발을 벗고 한 30분 여 산책하기에 참 좋습니다입구에 있는 가게에서 신발대여료를 내라 주차비를 내라 좀 짜증나게 하는 부분은 있었으나 한국에서는 보기 힘든 광경이니 한 번쯤은 가볼만 할 듯

Been here twice with visitors, wading through the stream. Stopping for cold drinks at the watery beverage stands. The further you go, the fewer people are there. Vietnamese tourists don't like to walk.

COMMON WRITE-UP  (Here’s my common write up for 4 sights)Took the tour which is a must do in Mui Ne unless you plan to go free and easy yourself.    It’s a USD 25 for 2 persons covering 4 locations, namely the White Sand (5 stars), Red Sand (3 stars), Mui Ne Harbour (or Fishing Village – 5 stars) and Fairy Stream (4 stars).    They have 2 options, a morning 4:30am to 9am or afternoon 1:30pm to 6pm.  I would highly recommend the morning session for the following reasons:1.You get to see the catch in the morning at the fishing village.   The fishermen closes at 9am so I guess there will not be much to see in the afternoon.3.You can extend your trip (cause I think trying to cover the 4 places in the allocated time is too much of a rush) by asking the drive to leave at the last stop and taking your taxi back from the last stop which happens to be the Fairy Steam.   The trip back to my hotel was just US$5.50 (I was staying at Anantara, you are likely to stay nearer hence even cheaper fare) so it was well worth it.The tour first takes you to the White Sands where you get to see sun-rise.  From there they will proceed to the Red Sands, then Fishing Village and finally Fairy Stream.   The time allocated for each is not long, I told them upfront I will take my time (which I did).  By the time I reached Fairy Stream was 8:30am, I told the drive to leave and that I will find my own way back (i.e. US$5.50).  I had breakfast, then proceeded to Fairy Stream.   By the time I left Fairy Stream it was 11:10am.    So all in all it was great that I took the morning tour as it allowed me to extend my whole sight of the 4 locations without rushing and at the same time enjoy the morning air.SPECIFIC WRITE-UP (Fairy Stream)Nice walk along the Fairy Stream.   There were nice formations but I could not walk all the way to the waterfall as when we were near (I believe about a hundred meters), the water was too high.   There were some people coming back and I asked to see their photos.    Did not think it that great to brave the high water.    On the way back we stopped and relaxed with a nice cold drink and ice cream before completing the walk back to our starting point and headed home on our way via taxi who was available immediately.
Interesting to visit.  Nice stream thru a magic landshape.  A mystical place, go there with your friends.
Went here as part of a tour and was pretty nice, it's not the most amazing thing in the world but definitely worth a visit if you are in the area

A must see in Mui Ne, most part is sandy but there are rocks on the last 200m, it starts where the stream is knee-deep, so be careful, but try to make the full length, the recompense is a good ice cold beer :)

Nice way to spend a little while. Can get some nice pictures. Locals try to charge you 5k to mind your shoes but just carry them with you. They tagged along with us for awhile, chatting away being friendly then just asked for money. Just decline politely at the start.

Красивая природа, маленький ручеек, остерегайтесь попрошаек под мостом, которые предложат посторожить вашу обувь и "провести экскурсию". Отвечайте на все предложения отказом, т.к. потом будет тяжеловато от них избавиться
The pictures looked kind of boring but being there it was actually a pretty nice walk. The water was quite deep in some parts but we managed to get through and the waterfall in the end was pretty.
Thắng cảnh tự nhiên đầy bí hiểm, hoang sơ. Nước suối trong mát, sạch sẽ. Vừa đi vừa ngắm cảnh hai bên đường, không khí trong lành, mát mẻ

Coming from the different place every new thing excites me. I was upset as I couldn't get to the end of the fairy stream because we had to swim at the end to reach there. But the walk in the stream was very relaxing..

Место для разового посещения,стоит приехать пораньше и однозначно не в выходной день.Очень жалко, что не взимается хоть маленькая плата за вход,можно было бы нанять уборщиков..Пейзажи есть очень живописные,необычное сочетание красного песка,голубого неба и ручейка)

Wir haben den Fairy Stream als vorletzte Station unserer Easy Rider Tour von Da Lat nach Mui Ne besucht.Eine nette Erfrischung nach einem Tag auf dem Motorrad.Man watet barfuß durch einen Bach mit angenehmen Sandbett und knöcheltiefem, warmem Wasser.Wir sind an der Brücke im Ort gestartet. Die ersten ca 500m sind nicht so schön, aber dann wird es immer besser.Es sind dort zwar viele Touristen unterwegs, es war aber trotzdem OK und teilweise ist es auch ganz lustig den Chinesen zuzugucken, wie sie sich fotografieren;)

There is a beautiful stream route inside the desert. Oneside is the sand dune while another side where the local residence is located. Make sure you bring the shorts with, instead of long jeans or trousers. Many small coffee shops along the route. One local told me that camping is possible, highly recommend for one who wanna spend a night in the widerness safely and then enjoy the sunrise
Man läuft einen kleinen Bach aufwärts durch Zentimeter hohes Wasser und es gibt verschiedene Angebote am Rand. Sieht super schön aus, ist jedoch sehr überfüllt.

C'est vraiment à faire !Pour l'accès gratuit,il faut descendre par les marches juste avant le petit pont. Car si vous passez par les 2 "maisons" où c'est inscrit 'Fairy stream' on vous fait payer (de mémoire c'est 1 ou 2€, c'est pas bien grave !). Une balade vraiment sympa, pour notre part le petit bémol c'était le vent, donc pas très agréable avec le sable qui vole !

Один раз это место необходимо посетить обязательно, очень интересный и необычный пейзаж, почему-то мне показалось что как на Марсе, хотя я там не была) Водичка теплая, до водопада не дошли, там поглубже, решили не идти, мало ли что там накидано в воде.

Внесите разнообразие в свой отдых, прогуляйтесь по этому ручью до водопада и сделаете красивые снимки. Доехать можно на автобусе, Вход бесплатный. При входе на ручей, может приставать местный Вьетнамец и просить 5000 донгов с каждого, это НЕ за вход, а что бы посторожить ваши сланцы которые оставите у него. Фотографии получаются красивые, особенно на фоне красных песков. Длина ручья до водопада примерно 2,5 км, но прогуляться приятно.

От нашего отеля(сани бич) приехали на автобусе.Место необычное.К нам пристал местный "гид" показывал что есть в этом ручье.(до конца с нами не пошел).Дали ему 50 донгов.По ручью идешь пешком,сначала немого грязно и неприятно идти но потом открывается вся красота ручья.Песок переходит из красного в белый потом в ярко рыжий в конце есть маленький водопад .Поесетить это место однозначно нужно.
Schöner halbtagesausflug- nette Wanderung mit netten Cafés am Ufer im Schatten.Der Wasserfall ist eher unspektakulär Unbedingt vormittags machen , bevor nachmittags die Busse mit Chinesen kommen!
endroit tres sympa  les pieds dans  leau et de superbes degrades de couleurs de sablepas mal de monde mais super ballade d une heure
Beautiful place with natural colours surrounds with palm trees. Asking all tourists to please take your trash with you.

Экскурсия к ручью Фей является частью одной из турпрограмм. Мы жили в пешей доступности, поэтому воспользовались случаем и прогулялись к ручью совершенно бесплатно. К сожалению, это был второй день празднования Нового года, поэтому многие разлекательные пункты (например, зоопарк, рыбалка) были закрыты. Если вы отважитесь на путь по красной мутной воде, приготовьтесь идти по воде разной глубины минут 40. Поэтому обувь - в руки, штаны - покороче! На пути следования есть место, где глубина воды доходит выше колена. Кроме того, есть зыбкий участок  почвы. Если у вас в арсенале имеются турецкие "мыльницы" (обычно продаются в лавках поблизости от пляжей с галькой), то они очень пригодятся, т.к. есть - немного, правда- каменистые участки, по которым довольно болезненно подниматься босыми ногами - особенно много камней  около водопада. Когда идешь по источнику, ты окружен слева барханами красного песка, где резвятся счастливые дети... И не только дети.) По правую руку - зелень, деревья, кафешки, музыка. Ближе к выходу сконцентрировались все сувенирные лавки.Минус в том, что вблизи общепита много мусора.Наша задача выполнена не была, т.к. целью посещения этого местечка, в первую очередь, была рыбалка. Цена на рыбалку невелика: 50000. Но в этот раз не повезло... Вообще, в Новый год по китайскому календарю лучше не приезжать: много интересных туристу мест в эти дни закрыты. Тем не менее, просто прогулявшись по ручью, мы увидели природные особенности местности. Рекомендую для прогулки, но одевайте то, что не жаль запачкать.)

Rekommenderar att gå denna strecka då den inte tar så lång tid att gå, räcker med att avvara en halvdag. Finns förfriskningar längs fairy stream i form av mindre matställe och matstånd. Skulle vara till deras fördel om det fanns papperskorgar längs strömmen.För den största nackdelen var att det var skräpigt, men detta var mest i börja av strömmen (starten).
Прикольно, чтобы прогуляться. Красивые виды. Местами бывает воды больше чем по колена, но в основном по щиколотку.
Good time to visit, cool feeling. However, don't throw gabbage, don't climb up hills coz it could ruin nice sand sculptures here.

A very pretty place to spend a little time. Yes it is a bit touristy but it's still worth a look. We went in February and it was lovely to paddle through the stream and climb the sandbanks to check out the views. Not enough water to make the waterful at the end of the stream anything special worth seeing, but still a fun place to check out

Maybe we came there same time with the new year holiday so there were hundreds kids jumping around and garbage was everywhere.My husband didn’t like it at all. He always questioned where was the water came from and worry about snakes. But my little daughter seem like enjoyed alot.Its easy to come here by the local bus from our hotel. Btw, we love the sugercane juice and fresh coconut they sell everywhere here, very tasty and cheap.
