Schade dass die Natur hier den Massentourismus zum Opfer fällt :(
Not great walking up a shitty river
Śmierdzący,ściek w którym pływają tony śmieci i małe węże.

If there is a negative star i would have used it. This is not a fairy stream. I call it the stream from hell. Some if the pictures were nice, but the walk is terrible. The water was muddy. The level of the water is inconsistent. There are rocks but you cant see it. There are also areas where you can sink down to your knee. It was not a nice experience, don't recommend to anyone.
C’est sale, c’est moche, l’eau n’est pas si propre que ça, même depuis la "source"… ne vaut plus le détour….nous avons été très déçues…
Gross and a waste of time tourist trap. Save your time and pass. They were burning a couch on the side and the fumes were toxic.

The water is dirty which is really off putting since you have to go barefoot, there’s trash on the sides the whole way up, it smells like dog food and to top it all off we saw a snake in the water - NOT a snake they charge you to take photo with.
I would rather cut off both of my legs than to put my feet again in this trash and snake infested waters.

walking in stream full of garbage  with a smell of dog food really isnt my thing, and then we saw snake in the water which made us feel even more uncomfortable. (not the snake you can take a photo with).

Attention il n’y a rien de féérique dans cette rivière remplie de déchets pour laquelle il faut même payer le parking.
Tout le début de la rivière repose sur un lit textile non naturel.
Dégoûtant, rivière remplie de déchets

Very dirty and has rubbish everywhere needs to be cleaned up this has a lot of potential but it's not looked after at all ! Disappointing I hope in the future they will look after the surrounding gardens and nature and clean the plastic bottles and rubbish so nature and animals have a chance to breathe and survive! Check out and subscribe to YouTube @bananapancakenomads to see other areas of Vietnam and Mui Ne !!

Лужа, вверх по реке то, что дальше водопада лушше не смортеть, что происходит...

Горы мусора и нечистот, вонь жуткая, так что чистый ручей не такой уж и чистый, не удивляйтесь сыпи после него или на следующий день
Avoid. Trap. Trash, waste, smell, snakes and 1m deep hidden holes
Perfect if you want to walk in rubbish
Posto terribile. Andateci se vi piace camminare in una fogna piena di spazzatura.
Чесно кажучи- нічого особливого. Мене це місце аж ніяк не вразило
Could be a nice experience in theory but it’s very dirty and not pleasant to be walking along trash. Really don’t recommend this place for tourists.

I was here 8 years ago and it was a very beautiful and tranquil spot, now it's yet another spot in that has been trashed by mass tourism. Most of the stone casts have broken off and there's a lot of rubbish in the area where you enter, wouldn't recommend.

Je to milé miesto, avšak strašne veľa špiny a odpadkov všade. Samý plast pri brehu, veľká škoda, že miestni to neudržujú, rozhádzajú použitý plast, topánky, oblečenie, gumy… Tú špinu som radšej ani nefotila, je to veľký šok pre europanov.
Chả có gì để trải nghiệm
skip if possible. genuinely a waste of ur time. water and surroundings are gross and you could easily cut yourself with the rocks you blindly step on.

Das was einem in der Werbung gezeigt wird, stimmt leider nicht. Es war teilweise sehr gefährlich und ohne Schuhe sollte man es nicht machen. Es gibt in diesem Fluss sehr viele Steine, die man leider nicht sehen kann. Also nur für Leute die gut zu Fuß sind.
Totaal niet de moeite waard om hier naar toe te komen. Zeer vuile omgeving en afval in het water
허탈..
Nicht sehenswert, voller Müll und sehr dreckiges Wasser, sehr touristisch
Ужасное место. Просто отвратительное, помойка какая то. Вода мутная. Где то мелко, где то по колено. Везде валяется мусор. Находится крайне не приятно. Дошли до середины и вернулись. Не советую !

After a rainy day, this is a nightmare. Ask for the shortcut or be ready to deal with a very dangerous activity. I do not understand why they send family tourists their… there is nothing touristic. We gave up the stream at the middle! My wife was hurt at her feet, probably by glass from broken bottle.
Dreckig und touristisch. Lieber nicht machen.
중간에 뱀 보라고 하면서 미니동물농장 들어오라는 사람있는데, 절대 들어가지마세요. 저의 가방 안에 있는 지갑을 소매치기 하다가 걸렸습니다.

Ужасное место при входе встречает зоопарк  он конечно дешёвый, но над животными там просто издеваются, они все пуганые и просятся на свободу. Потом пошли по ручью , мало того он очень грязный, так если одежду ты забрызгал то потом с водой и песок летит, а он еле отстирывается, также мы не дошли до конца ручья из за того что там траву жгли, пошли обратно и вдруг из под воды змея вынырнула ну мы сильно испугались, дошли обратно до начала и с нас денег еще потребовали. Ужасное место не советую, а животных очень жалко.
Je ne vois vraiment pas l’intérêt de cet endroit. Pollution, odeurs désagréables, aucun aménagement. Dès l’escalier pour accéder au « ruisseau » vous comprenez que cela ne vaut pas le coup.

굳이 가야할 이유 1도 없는곳....
물은 더럽고 쓰레기 많이 보이고...
엄청난 모기.....
맨발로 걸으라 하는데 혹여라도 가야겠다하신다면 그냥 신발 꼭 신고 가세요

The path in the stream is only nice in the section where the ravine is. The rest of the trail is full of trash. It's also dangerous. Yesterday, I cut my foot on a sharp object in the water. The hospital said there was a 100% risk of tetanus infection, so I got the vaccine. Unfortunately, I didn't have time to get vaccinated before coming here, so I recommend doing it in your country. Also get water shoes if you want to be safe.

** The 4 Places jeep tour**
The tour was disappointing from start to finish, in every aspect!
At the agency, they described it as a 4 hour Jeep tour.
We stopped at four places the fishing village, the fairy stream, the red dunes, and the white dunes.
The first two stops, the fishing village and the stream, felt like quick photo stops just because they were on the way to the dunes, which are clearly the main part of the tour.
They offered us a shared Jeep option at 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM for around 100,000–150,000 VND per person (I don’t remember the exact price, but around that).
If you want to go in the morning, which is actually better for the experience and the weather, you can only do it with a private Jeep for 275,000 VND per person.
The whole thing felt like stop after stop just to take pictures, not a real experience.
The stop at the fairy stream was okay but nothing special, not something we’d consider a must.
The fishing village was also just fine, we stopped, took a picture, and left. The beach there was dirty and not pleasant to walk around. We expected to see a real fishing village and experience the place, but that didn’t happen.
Then we went to the white dunes, where we expected to enter with the Jeep we came with since we paid for a Jeep tour. At the agency, they told us that renting a vehicle at the dunes is optional for an extra fee, but they never said it was mandatory or that the Jeep we arrived in wouldn’t go inside. They made it sound like a bonus, not a must.
When we arrived, we found out that you can’t enter the white dunes without paying extra either by a big Jeep (800,000 VND) or an ATV for two people (600,000 VND).
We paid 600,000 VND for just a 10-minute ride around the dunes and the lake, completely unreasonable.
We already paid for a Jeep tour, but in reality, the Jeep was just a taxi from stop to stop. The least they could do is inform people in advance that you’re required to pay extra to enter the dunes. It’s a big amount and should definitely be mentioned beforehand!
You can also do sandboarding at the dunes, but of course, that’s another extra charge.
Overall, the stops were disappointing. We expected much more, especially for the price we paid.
The views and the experience felt very basic, mostly just quick photo breaks, and it was frustrating to have to pay even more during the tour.
At the end, it’s also customary to tip the driver (around 150,000–200,000 VND, from what we understood).
Our recommendation check for another way to visit these places and save your money. I’ve attached photos so you can see what it really looks like. What we saw in person was nothing like what was described by the agency or shown on Google.
In short, it’s not a must do.
If I had known in advance, I would have found another way to get there, maybe by motorbike or any other means, or just enjoyed a relaxing beach day instead.
In total, we paid 1,350,000 VND for this tour.
It was okay, but definitely not worth that price. If the price had been fair, I wouldn’t complain, but for two people, it’s just not worth it.
We left at 8:00 AM and got back to the hotel at 11:10 AM.
Most of the tour was just driving in the Jeep with short photo stops.
Overall, we don’t recommend it. This was our honest personal experience.

We stripped off our shoes for a "magical" hike,

Then soldier on through to that miniature goal.
But a man with a constrictor? A massive "Big Nope."
I’ve run out of interest and run out of hope.

A "scenic" disaster that left us frustrated.

Total überbewertet! Zum Glück kostet der Spaziergang nicht auch noch Eintritt. Man sollte besser in seinem Resort bleiben, man verpasst hier nichts. Der Anfang des Baches ist total verdreckt und vermüllt und es stinkt. Nach ein paar Hundert Metern wird es besser und man läuft durch trübes sandiges Wasser. Es kommen ein paar Sandwände und Formationen, aber kein echtes Highlight. Man sollte aufpassen, dass man im sandigen Wasser nicht stürzt, weil man die Untiefen nicht sieht.

Überhaupt nicht beeindruckend – Nur für Verzweifelte mit viel Zeit. Ich hatte mir vom berühmten Fairy Stream (Feen-Bach) in Mũi Né deutlich mehr versprochen. Ehrlich gesagt war ich völlig unbeeindruckt.

Der erste Eindruck war der schlechteste und leider auch der prägendste: Der Einstieg in den Bach ist schlichtweg ekelhaft. Man muss zunächst durch einen Abschnitt mit Müll waten, und der furchtbare Gestank nach Abwasser und Moder war kaum auszuhalten. Das ist ein denkbar schlechter Start in ein angebliches "Feenland". Zwar wird die Szenerie besser, je tiefer man hineinläuft und je weiter man sich vom Eingang entfernt, und die roten und weißen Sandsteinformationen sind nett anzusehen. Aber das Erlebnis rechtfertigt aus meiner Sicht weder den Aufwand noch den miesen Start.

Vielleicht waren wir beim falschen Eingang, aber wir waren leider sehr enttäuscht. Unser Guide brachte uns zu einer Treppe, die direkt in den Bach führte. Wir sollten dann durch den Bach stapfen um zu den fairy Tails zu kommen. Dies war uns jedoch einerseits zu feucht und zu riskant wegen der Rutschgefahr. Wenn man sich anschließend die Füße waschen möchte, kann man dies gegen Bezahlung im nebenliegenden Restaurant tun.War leider nichts für uns.

Wir wurden an einem eher unscheinbaren Ort vom Taxifahrer raus gelassen. Er beschrieb uns den Weg und wir wären dort nicht runter gegangen, wenn oben nicht schon ein paar Schuhe gestanden hätten. Unter der Brücke angekommen, war die Ernüchterung groß. Es stank zum Himmel. Naja, soll ja eine Sehenswürdigkeit sein. Also, Augen zu und durch. Ich hatte mir Wasserschuhe mit genommen und war darüber auch sehr froh. Steine, Wurzeln und scharfe Kanten von Baumaterialien waren mit bloßem Auge unter Wasser nicht zu erkennen. Die Wasserschuhe schützen dagegen richtig gut. Nach ein paar hundert Metern gesellte sich ein Guide zu uns. Klar, wollte er Trinkgeld machen. Wir haben sein Angebot gerne angenommen, weil er genau wusste, welche Stelle im Fluss tief war und wo scharfkantige Steine lagen. Also ein gemütlicher Spaziergang war das für uns keinesfalls. Mag aber vielleicht auch daran gelegen haben, dass es die letzten Tage gut geregnet hat und der Fluss dadurch mehr Wasser hatte.

Es gab auch immer wieder das Angebot, sich mit einer Schlange um den Hals fotografieren zu lassen. Das haben wir konsequent abgelehnt.

Die roten Dünen: naja, es war halt roter Sand. Keine wirklich schönen Sandformationen, wie sie laut Prospekt hoch gelobt werden. Der Hintergrund war die Latscherei durch den Fluss nicht wert.

Die am Uferrand ansässigen Lokale, in denen man auch was trinken kann, waren für mich am äußersten Rande der zumutbaren Hygiene.

Wir sind morgens früh gegen 10.00 Uhr in den Fluss gestiegen. Da war noch nicht so viel los. Knappe eineinhalb Stunden später kamen uns ganze Scharen von chinesischen Touristen entgegen, die mit Reisebussen angekarrt wurden.

Alles in allem habe ich mir von dieser Sehenswürdigkeit viel mehr versprochen.

La promenade sur le cours d’eau est très agréable. La vue sur les falaises rouges est surprenante, jolie et originale. Cependant, une question troublante s'impose : ne trouvez vous pas cela inhumain de payer pour vous faire photographier avec des biches, lapins, chameaux, crocodiles, serpents, …? Est-il normal de captiver ces animaux, contraints de subir la chaleur dans leurs toutes petites cages… en étant immobilisés toute la journée en plein soleil par leurs maîtres ? Tout cela simplement pour faire rire des enfants et des adultes immatures. Cette pratique révèle une profonde cruauté et une absurdité navrante de notre société, où le bien-être des animaux est sacrifié sur l'autel du divertissement.

2. Vui: Cảnh khá đẹp khi lên hình nhờ cát đỏ và cát trắng đang xen cùng với nhủ đá nhìn rất đẹp.

From all the things we did on Mui Ne, this is the only thing that was worth it 3/5 rating.
It’s a little stream, with a small waterfall at the end of a long walk. You need to walk barefoot or with water proof shoes/slippers in the stream. In the heat, it was a nice shaded walk. After about 250m, you will see the main attraction that show rocky formations from landslides and after 20m more, it’s over. Hence the rating.
I would actually skip visiting this Mui Ne completely, because you can’t go in the beach and there are other countries where you can enjoy in the resort if needed.
If you do visit, leave around sunrise for any tour, it’s extremely hot by 9am.

The fairy stream is a very unique place to walk around. But I want to say be careful if you drive motorbike here by yourself, the Vietnamese guy live at picture 1 and picture 2 will demanding your park at his place ( it’s illegal to force some one to do so) and if he see your parked somewhere else e.g. 50 meters away on the side of the road, you will be aggressively yelled at. It happened to me and ruined my day. He yelled and swore and threaten me and asked me to leave, tbh it looks like he is gonna kill me. The fact is, this place belongs its nation, not privately owned by someone. They have no rights to tell you what to and not to do. I have full respect for Vietnam and Vietnamese people but I won’t tolerate abusive behavior. I warn people who come visit this place do not give in for force. E.g. there are few entrance, like the one opposite of the road if you want to avoid this guy.

Đoạn suối đầu đường Huỳnh Thúc Kháng thì to và sạch hơn chút. Nhưng đi về phía cuối đoạn gần đường Võ Nguyên Giáp thì suối nhỏ và bẩn, thêm nữa mùi khá hôi thối. Có thể do cống nước thải của các hộ dân gần suối đổ ra. Nếu mọi người đi, chỉ nên đi tới đoạn red canyon rồi quay đầu. Tới red canyon chụp ảnh cũng đẹp. Nếu đi tới cuối suối, không muốn lội suối quay ngược lại điểm xuất phát, thì có thêm 1 lựa chọn là lên đường để đi bộ về điểm xuất phát. Khoảng 1,3km. Cũng có cả xe ôm chở về. 50K/1 lượt.

Sẽ rất tuyệt vời nếu Suối Tiên giữ được nguồn nước sạch. Thêm tiền vé vào cũng được, miễn sao giữ được môi trường xung quanh suối sạch cũng như đặt các biển báo nơi nước có vòng xoáy sâu và các chỏm đá hoặc đơn giản là những lưu ý khi lội suối (vì đây là địa điểm tham quan du lịch mà). Biết rằng nước không trong là do cát đỏ từ đá chảy xuống nhưng rác và mùi hôi rác và từ nhà vệ sinh cũng góp phần không kém cho hành trình lội suối bị tuột mood.
Theo quan điểm của mình, khúc đẹp nhất là khúc giữa có mấy vách đá đỏ và trắng xen nhau.
Lưu ý là nếu bạn muốn đi đến thác nguồn là phải đi bộ từ cổng vào quẹo phải và đi hơn 2km mới đến nên mang theo nước và nón vì có thể nắng. Mặc quần ngắn vì nước có thể lên trên đầu gối. Mang theo dép cũng ko sao nhưng dép nào mà có thể lội suối được ấy. Mang theo cho chắc chứ không bị trầy chân vì lội trúng đá như mình.
Tiền rửa chân 5k/người.
Mình thấy lội suối khá mát mặc dù đi buổi trưa, có nhiều đoạn có núi đẹp, lúc đi và về tổng cộng 1 tiếng
Quay lại đây sau 4năm,tuy nhiên đường vô suối khúc đầu khiến khách dễ nản lòng,tuy nhiên đi sâu vô trong thì cảnh tương đối,cũng được.Nói chung ko đẹp như xưa vì bị ảnh hưởng xây dựng và bê tông hoá

Rảnh thực sự hãy đi nhé 😄

Chỗ này nghe tên là Suối Tiên nên ban đầu mình kỳ vọng sẽ là một điểm đến thiên nhiên kiểu thơ mộng, hoang sơ, ít nhất cũng có cảnh đẹp để chụp vài tấm hình “sống ảo” hoặc ngồi thư giãn giữa thiên nhiên. Nhưng thật sự là quá thất vọng. Suối nhỏ, nước đục, không có gì đặc biệt để khám phá. Mình đã cố gắng đi bộ đến hết đoạn suối, hy vọng phía trong sẽ có gì thú vị hơn – nhưng không, vẫn chỉ là con suối cạn, rác rến vương vãi, cây cối thì mọc tự nhiên không được chăm sóc hay quy hoạch gì cả.

Tổng thể cảm giác là khu này không có đầu tư, không được bảo tồn đúng cách. Lối đi lộn xộn, khu vực xung quanh thì dơ, nhiều rác sinh hoạt bị vứt bừa bãi. Không gian thì nóng bức, bụi và không có góc nào để “chill” đúng nghĩa. Nếu ai mong chờ một điểm du lịch thiên nhiên đẹp, trong lành để thư giãn thì chắc chắn sẽ hụt hẫng như mình thôi.

Chắc chắn sẽ không quay lại, và cũng không khuyến khích ai ghé nếu chỉ vì cái tên nghe hay hay.

The Fairy Stream in Mui Ne is a nice and easy activity if you’re in the area. The walk starts off quite simple and pleasant, with a shallow stream and soft sand under your feet. At first, the scenery is interesting but not spectacular.

However, if you keep walking all the way to the end, you’ll be rewarded. The landscape gradually becomes more impressive, and the highlight is a beautiful view of the red, canyon-like cliffs. This part makes the visit worthwhile and gives the place its unique character.

Overall, it’s a relaxing experience and worth doing, especially if you take the time to go all the way to the end.

Introduction
Keywords:
Interesting. Stinky. Unique

A rather interesting experience, with the journey on foot, which though felt weird at first, later felt rewarding.
Nevertheless, an average experience that does not reflect the recognition Mui Ne deserves.

View
Cliffs of orange hue and rather unique forms reside adjacent to the stream further north, otherwise, nothing much.

Walk
As stated previously, the on foot experience was unique and interesting, though extruding rocks along the path can cause some discomfort.

Atmosphere
Very relaxed, tropical mood. It feels as though you are connected with nature.
Noise levels are moderate, though it becomes quieter upstream.
Unfortunately, one of the resting stations besides the stream smells like feces- awful.
Kinda dirty no fairies but the animals seemed happy and was nice to walk down the stream. Watch out for quicksand!

Nice experience walking up the stream - It is worth to check at least first 20min of the path. You'll see some incredible red & white sandstone and clay formations that are estimated to be 8000 year old. Be careful, on some parts the water is knee deep but you can avoid them. You can leave the shoes at the entrance but we preffered to take them with us.

But it is not worth to go all the way to the waterfall (35min walk) because It turns out the "waterfall" is just a sad, brown cascade of questionable origin that smells suspiciously and flows directly into the water you are walking in. Good thing is that if you reach that point you can get out to the road and call grab so you don't have to walk back again.

Una experiencia curiosa si tienes tiempo libre en Mũi Né. Caminas con los pies en el agua por un pequeño arroyo entre formaciones rojizas y verdes que crean un paisaje inusual. No nos costó nada la entrada, lo cual ya es un punto positivo.

Lo mejor fue subir una pequeña colina de arena roja para hacer fotos; fue más entretenido que el propio arroyo. En total, el recorrido se hace en unos 40–45 minutos tranquilamente.

No es algo imprescindible, pero como paseo relajado, puede merecer la pena si estás cerca.

It's exactly what it says it is, a walk along a stream. Once you've dodged the snake handlers and get past the trash laden start it's actually quite nice although interspersed with the occasional karaoke bar and occasional litter dump. It'll take about 30 minutes before you get to the waterfall which is quite cute but don't look too long into the plunge pool as there appears to be a lot of effluent swirling around. This country is great but going through the growing pains of mass tourism and this represents it. You can also take the side walk up the red dune for a great view across Mui Ne from the top. Definetly worth it as a fun afternoon excursion.

Fairy Stream (Suối Tiên) ở Mũi Né là một điểm đến độc đáo với dòng suối nhỏ chảy qua những vách cát đỏ cam ấn tượng. Nước suối mát, chỉ ngập đến mắt cá chân, thích hợp để dạo bộ và tận hưởng thiên nhiên. Khung cảnh hoang sơ với sự kết hợp giữa cát đỏ, cây xanh và dòng nước tạo nên điểm check-in tuyệt vời. Vé vào cửa rẻ hoặc miễn phí, có hàng quán dọc đường. Nên đi chân trần, tránh giờ nắng gắt và giữ gìn vệ sinh. Đây chắc chắn là điểm đến không thể bỏ lỡ khi đến Mũi Né!

Всем привет. Начитался я отзывов стремных, и расхотелось туда идти. Но очень хотел увидеть красные пески и в итоге заставил себя. Итак, рассказываю. На ресепшн моего отеля сказали что идти 10 минут, билет стоит 40.000 на человека, и будет русскоязычный гид.
По факту, вход свободный, никакого гида я не нашел. Сам спустился с дороги по щебенке до ручья и пошел. Сразу скажу, боялся вони, мусора, всякой тины и нечистот, что люди и писали в отзывах и что больше всего отпугивало. По факту, вода чистая, прозрачная, очень мелкая, самое глубокое наверное чуть выше щиколотки, дно просматривается, на дне мелкий песок, иногда встречаются крупные камни. Идти очень комфортно и свежо. Мусор и неприятный запах встречается разве что на входе, там несколько полевых самодельных магазинов, в остальном меня ничего не беспокоило.
На прогулку потратили в общей сложности часа два. До водопада не дошли, уперлись в какое то глубокое на взгляд место, с малым потоком.
Залезли на красные пески, поделали клевых фотографий.
На пути встретилось дерево, на котором очень много бабочек, ближе к финишу, возле непонятного кафе/ресторана.
Мы пришли утром, часов в 9. Самое подходящее время, солнце еще не жарит сильно, и туристов не очень много.

Для развлечения советую посетить, тем более бесплатно. Поделать фотки на память, поглазеть, прогуляться.

It's very difficult to review this place. It was nice taking a short walk in a natural stream but the neighborhood area and shop owners are not supposed to promote the natural beauty appropriately.

I think if they don't change the way they are running business on this natural landscape, there won't be anyone coming in the next 3 years.
Nice small waterfalls, disappointing amount of litter, tour groups seemed rushed and had no idea there was a waterfalls.
Nice to see but nothing too special. The waterfall at the end was a little disapointing. Took us about 25min to reach from the main road.
The photo shows the first 30 metres which worries me slightly due to all the rubbish, however after that it got better and is a reasonably nice short 15 minute walk down the stream.

Generally, it's a nice walk in shallow water for about 25 minutes along the top of the stream. If you're not on schedule, you can enjoy a cup of coffee while watching the canyon. It's not long, but the red sands, especially on the left side, look nice.  It is relaxing experience.

This place is a natural stream and is not under the management of any unit, so it remains completely untouched. When visiting, you should wear shorts. If you want to walk, you can either go barefoot or wear water-resistant sandals, but I think it’s best to go barefoot to fully experience nature. You can also bring bottled water to drink. The stream is cool, the water flows gently, with natural greenery on both sides and red sand dunes. Please keep the area clean by putting plastic bottles in the black nylon trash bags (which have been placed along both sides of the stream). The starting point belongs to a private business and is not related to the stream itself. If you come by motorbike, parking will cost 10,000 VND.

Une petite vallée très typique avec son filet de cours d'eau tapissé de sable fin. Il est agréable de marcher pieds nus (sans claquettes). La vue est très jolie. Il est possible de se faire prendre en photos avec un serpent sur les épaules moyennant 50 000 dôngs.

Брать специально экскурсию я бы сюда не стала. Приехали на байке, 10к донг взяли за парковку. Идёшь все время по грязному ручью,кто-то покусал за ноги,  возможно, мошки. Приехать для разнообразия, чтобы не сидеть в отеле можно.

Beautiful and peaceful walk up and down a stream. At the end of the stream there is a waterfall where you can have a shower, so bring a swimsuit if you feel like it! Beware of the locals trying to sell you a ticket or make you pay for parking, they are not associated with the attraction in any way.
Was OK, nice to walk up the creek and see the waterfall.

Activité sympa à faire, l’entrée est gratuite mais beaucoup vont essayer de voir faire payer le parking pour votre moto. Il vaut mieux aller un peu plus loin et se garer sur le bord de la route. - Lội suối ko mất phí ngoài tiền gửi xe 10k và nếu ko mua vé vào farm.
- Nước sâu lút "mắt" nên trẻ em biết đi cũng lội được. Đi chân không cũng được trừ những bàn chân nhung lụa. Quần áo gì cũng dc.

- Dọc suối có 1 số nhà ở, ống nước từ trong farm chảy ra. Đỉnh thác nguyên 1 khu farm dịch vụ. Hy vọng lâu dài suối vẫn còn Tiên.

Fairy Stream (Suối Tiên) in Mũi Né is an absolute gem and a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to the area. The natural beauty of the stream is simply breathtaking, with its soft red sand, unique rock formations, and vibrant colors that create a magical atmosphere. Walking along the shallow water is both relaxing and adventurous, offering stunning photo opportunities at every turn. The pathway is well-maintained, making it accessible for visitors of all ages. Additionally, the staff are friendly and informative, providing interesting insights into the geology and history of the area. Whether you're a nature enthusiast, a photography lover, or just looking for a peaceful escape, Fairy Stream delivers an unforgettable experience. Highly recommended for its serene environment and picturesque landscapes!

선라이즈 패키지 마지막으로 왔어요. 시간이 여유있을것이라 생각하고, 음료수를 한 잔하고 요정의 샘을 걸었어요. 다른 팀들도 비슷하게 끝나서 인지 많이 걸었어요. 샌듄의 모래와 같이 매우 가는 모래가 깔려 있고, 물이 빠르게 흐르는곳은 돌이 있기도 해서 맨발로 끝까지 가다보면 상처가 생기는 사람도 많고 발의 통증도 많이 나요. 올라가다 보면 뻘같이 변한곳은 발이 빠지기도 해요. 대부분 중간쯤 갔을때 포기를 많이 하고 돌아갔어요. 사진 포인트에서 사진을 찍고 가는 것도 괜찮은것 같아요. 저의 처가 여기를 가야 한다고 했지만 발도 아프고 투어 시간도 다 되어 걱정을 많이 했어요. 그러나 끝내 폭포까지 도착을 했고, 돌아가는 길은 내리막?이라 금방 돌아가네요. 그래도 다시 오기는 힘든 곳에 끝까지 가지 않았다면 후회는 남겠지만 다 보고 와서 후회는 없다고 하네요. 뱀사진읏 둘째 5만동 내고 찍었습니딘. 동물원 등 가는 길에 있는데 차라리 근처의 한나비치나 같은 곳 가는 것을 추천드려요. 고생이 많아서 별 하나 빼고 4개 올립니다.

Suối Tiên – Mũi Né là điểm du lịch độc đáo của Phan Thiết, được mệnh danh là “chốn bồng lai tiên cảnh” nhờ khung cảnh thiên nhiên hoang sơ và màu sắc kỳ ảo. Con suối nhỏ uốn lượn bên những vách đá cát đỏ – trắng – vàng xen kẽ, tạo nên bức tranh thiên nhiên rực rỡ hiếm có.

Du khách đến đây thường dạo bộ men theo dòng suối mát lạnh, cảm nhận sự mềm mại của cát dưới chân, ngắm nhìn những vách đá nhấp nhô được bào mòn tự nhiên theo thời gian. Khung cảnh thay đổi theo từng đoạn suối, vừa hoang sơ vừa lãng mạn, đặc biệt đẹp khi nắng chiều chiếu xuống.

Suối Tiên không ồn ào, náo nhiệt mà mang đến cảm giác yên bình, thích hợp để thư giãn, chụp ảnh và tận hưởng vẻ đẹp riêng biệt của Mũi Né

Fairy Stream (Suối Tiên) in Mũi Né is one of the most unique natural attractions in Vietnam, blending striking landscapes with a tranquil, almost magical atmosphere.

The stream itself is shallow, with cool, ankle-deep water flowing gently through a narrow valley. What makes it special are the surrounding rock formations and sand dunes—towering cliffs of red, orange, and white sand mixed with limestone, creating vivid contrasts and surreal shapes. Walking barefoot along the stream, visitors can enjoy the soft sand underfoot while being surrounded by colorful walls that seem to change hue with the light.

The walk is about 1–2 kilometers and relatively easy, making it suitable for most travelers. Along the way, you’ll encounter small waterfalls, lush greenery, and quiet corners that feel hidden from the busier parts of Mũi Né. It’s a favorite spot for both relaxation and photography, with sunrise and late afternoon being the best times to capture the glowing colors of the cliffs.

Although Fairy Stream is not a large-scale attraction, it’s an unforgettable experience thanks to its peaceful charm and unique scenery. Visitors should bring a hat, sunscreen, and a camera to fully enjoy the journey.

Overall, Fairy Stream is a must-visit in Mũi Né—perfect for those who love nature, soft adventure, and stunning, otherworldly landscapes.

Resting at the bottom of the Red Canyon is the Fairy Stream. Famous for its red sand and barefoot river walk, it is often referred to as Vietnam's Grand Canyon in miniature.

The entrance to the Fairy Stream is to the north of the main road leading into Mui Ne. At the entrance there is a small restaurant where you can enjoy Vietnamese coffee and local food while watching the river below.  It cost 5,000 dong to enter in 2024, and you can leave your shoes at the entrance.

I recommend walking the river barefoot. The sand is soft, and the water is warm, so it is comfortable to walk down. Bring a towel so you can dry yourself off afterwards!

Trees, grass and other plant life line the stream, glowing a bright green under the sun and contrasting orange-red river. There were a few places to take a rest, or for photo opportunities, including a swing beneath the canyon's walls.

The plant life eventually gives way to impressive limestone formations embedded in the red clay cliffs. These are impressive to see up close and above ground. There is a large cluster of stalagmites that look like small statues that have been weathered over the years.

Past the limestone formations, the flora returns, lining the canyon with leaves. A small restaurant is set up here, but it was closed and empty on the day I visited. Despite the cliffs being left behind, there are still signs of limestone, with small formations dotted around. One that stood out to me had a strange colour and looked more like a termite's nest.

At the end of the canyon, there is a small waterfall. This is the source of the Fairy Stream Don't get too close, I was warned by a local that you can get swept under if you do.

If you're spending time in Mui Ne, this is a must do. The short walk takes less than half an hour, but you can spend more time here if you want to take in the scenery.

Giới thiệu về Suối Tiên – Mũi Né
•	Địa điểm: Suối Tiên hay Suối Hồng nằm tại phường Mũi Né, TP. Phan Thiết, tỉnh Bình Thuận (nay thuộc Lâm Đồng theo quy định mới)  ￼ ￼.
•	Khung cảnh độc đáo: Dòng suối dài khoảng 1,8 km với độ sâu chỉ từ mắt cá tới trên đầu gối, chảy giữa các đồi cát đỏ, vàng xen lẫn – tạo nên cảnh sắc thiên nhiên như tranh vẽ  ￼.
•	Màu nước: Màu đỏ cam đặc trưng của dòng suối đến từ lớp cát bao quanh, khiến nơi đây trở thành điểm “check‑in sống ảo” hàng đầu tại Mũi Né

Suối Tiên – Mũi Né là một trong những điểm du lịch tự nhiên hiếm hoi tại Việt Nam vừa thơ mộng, vừa độc đáo về màu sắc và hình dạng địa chất. Dòng suối nông với cát đỏ cam, nhũ cát kỳ dị, khu rừng và đồi cát tạo nên bức tranh thiên nhiên cực “ảo”. Nếu bạn đang tìm một nơi để “đổi gió”, trải nghiệm và chụp ảnh nghệ thuật, thì đây chắc chắn là lựa chọn không thể bỏ qua.

Ich habe den Spaziergang genossen! Nach anfänglichen Verkaufsständen wird es herrlich ruhig. Es waren kaum Leute unterwegs. Man muss morgens gehen. Ich war um 9 Uhr da und es war nichts los. Richtig entspannend.

Ich habe einfach ein paar Sachen aufgehoben und in den Müll geschmissen. Unterwegs gibt es ein paar Müllsäcke. Auch die Betreiber der Cafés auf dem Weg haben mir angeboten es wegzuschmeißen.
Das Problem Müll ist ein globales Problem. Einfach aufheben, mitnehmen und in den Müll schmeißen. So löse ich das Problem, wenn es mich stört.

A fun walk up the fairy stream. There are a few slippery spots so be careful. But for the most part, it's lovely soft sand and lovely fresh running water. We went barefoot and had no problems. We saw a group enjoying karaoke at one such bar. The other trail walkers were not really enjoying it though haha. The waterfall at the end is picturesque and worth the trek. About 1 hour round trip, at a leisurely pace

Visited here as part of a sunrise sand dune tour and it was our favourite spot. Very nice scenery. Got there as one of the first groups and on the way back it was already really crowded so it’s a good idea to tell your driver to speed up a bit.

We went here thru a guided jeep. We walked down the stream. Our driver just gave us 45min to reach the end of the stream and go back but since we had so many picture takings, we did not reach the end of the stream which has a water falls based on the internet. It was refreshing to walk and feel the nature. Some have pointy stones and painful to your feel. You may opt to wear your shoes though it is so nice to walk at the sand. Also, you may put your shoes at the rack provided. Infairness you’ll still get your shoes back after even no one is watching on that. So many tourist walking and you may find yourself buying some drinks after walking. Try some sugar cane juice, 15K vnd just right for the price. You’ll see people selling some souvenirs also.

Suối Tiên Mũi Né là một trong những điểm du lịch nổi bật và đẹp mắt tại Phan Thiết. Với cảnh quan thiên nhiên hoang sơ, kỳ vĩ, suối Tiên là một kỳ quan thiên nhiên tuyệt đẹp mà du khách không thể bỏ qua. Dòng suối nước trong vắt, uốn lượn qua những dải cát trắng mịn màng, tạo nên một bức tranh thiên nhiên tuyệt mỹ. Đặc biệt, màu sắc của nước suối thay đổi theo thời gian trong ngày, từ xanh ngọc bích đến màu cam khi ánh mặt trời chiếu vào, tạo ra một khung cảnh huyền bí và lôi cuốn.

Điểm đặc biệt của Suối Tiên là dòng nước mát lạnh chảy qua những khe đá, tạo thành những hồ nhỏ, rất thích hợp để du khách dừng lại thư giãn. Bên cạnh đó, không gian yên tĩnh, xa khỏi sự ồn ào của thành phố, giúp du khách cảm thấy thư giãn, dễ chịu. Suối Tiên cũng là một nơi tuyệt vời để khám phá những kỳ quan tự nhiên, tham gia các hoạt động dạo chơi, tắm suối hoặc chụp ảnh lưu niệm. Đây thực sự là một điểm đến lý tưởng cho những ai yêu thích sự yên bình và vẻ đẹp hoang sơ của thiên nhiên.
Schöne wandertour durch ein Flussbett mit Wasser. Kleiner Tipp für manche Stellen besser Wasserschuhe mitnehmen.

В начале прогулки ты не понимаешь зачем сюда пришёл. Мелкий ручей с тёплой водичкой и низкими кустами на берегах. Но буквально через пару поворотов открываются потрясающие виды на красно-белые склоны. Ко всем имеющимся преимуществам добавьте бесплатный вход, и вы получите отличную релакс-прогулку на природе не выезжая за пределы Муйне.

Mũi Né là một địa danh, tên một mũi biển và cũng là một phường ở thành phố Phan Thiết, tỉnh Bình Thuận, Việt Nam. Đây là một khu du lịch nổi tiếng của tỉnh Bình Thuận và là một trong số các khu du lịch quốc gia của Việt Nam (tuy nhiên, thực tế đa phần các khu nghỉ mát lại tập trung nhiều ở phường Hàm Tiến, gần Mũi Né).

Về hành chính, toàn bộ khu vực ven biển từ Mũi Né lên đến Hòn Rơm nằm cách đó khoảng 7 km là phường Mũi Né thuộc thành phố Phan Thiết.

Dieser Ausflug ist ein absolutes Muss! Was für ein Erlebnis. Wichtig: Tragen Sie Wasserschuhe, da man durch das Wasser läuft und dabei die schönen Landschaften bewundern kann. Einfach mega!😊

Cái này người ta hay gọi là Suối Tre. Suối này nằm khuất sau những đồi cát đỏ, cát trắng khá cao nhìn mấy đồi cứ như sạt lở hơi sợ nha kkk. Mình tới tốn 10k gửi xe khi mn xuống phải gửi giày hay dép tốn thêm 5k tại cái đó phải đi dưới nước mình có gửi video mn tham khảo, lên ngta cho nước sạch mình tự rửa chân lại là oke. Phải lội từ chổ đó vô trong tầm 200 300m là tới. Đi trải nghiệm okela nhe
Lovely

찰방찰방 물소리 들으며 걷기 너~~무 좋아요.
부드러운 진흙 느낌도 좋구요. 가끔 밟히는 돌멩이 조심해야해요. 살 다 까졌어요. ㅜ
Espectacular,. Increíble,.
Am Anfang ist es nicht so schön aber wenn man die Verkaufsstände hinter sich hat fängt es an richtig schön zu werden. Empfehlenswert
Nơi dành cho ai kiên nhẫn thích lội suối khám phá, đi tận sẽ thấy thác nước đẹp
Tuyệt vời
Mình chỉ tốn 10k gửi xe, lên trên suối nguồn là có quán. Mọi người có thể order nc mình nghỉ ngơi hoặc chụp hình nè, siuuuu đẹp á
Потрясающее место. Удивительные марсианские пейзажи. Первый раз посетил в 2014, но возвращаюсь вновь и вновь.
Farm/restaurant at the very end above the waterfall offering coffee, food, and coconuts. Hammocks available as well.
Đẹp

A unique and beautiful natural attraction with red and white rock formations, combined with the soft, cooling stream, makes for a special experience. The area was clean during my visit, and there were loads of colourful butterflies and dragonflies about. I went on a Saturday at noon—busy on the way to the waterfall but much quieter on the return. Be aware that some people at various entrances try to charge a fee (amounts vary, so they don’t seem official), but it's possible to find free access points. Well worth a visit!

It is a very beautiful scenic spot. The colors of the rocks by the stream are very beautiful to take pictures. It takes about an hour to walk back and forth along the stream. There are not many tourists. It is highly recommended.

就是一條溪水，但走到盡頭還蠻美

外面有鞋架可以放，不需要領著走
Excellent very interesting
계곡에 물이 흐르고 위로 거슬러 올라가야 합니다. 슬리퍼를 신으면 불편하니 맨발이나 샌들을 신는 것이 좋습니다. 낙엽 등 부유물로 인한 하수구 냄새가 납니다만 붉은 황토벽과 물이 흐르는 계곡을 걷는 것은 특이한 경험입니다. 자세히 보려면 한시간 정도 여유를 가지고 보시는 것이 좋습니다. 입장료는 없으나 계곡을 내려와서 발을 씻으려면 돈을 내야 합니다.
không gian thoáng mát,gần với thiên nhiên

The White Sand Dunes in Vietnam are a must-see. The vast expanse of white sand is breathtaking, and the views are stunning. However, be prepared for the intense heat. Bring plenty of water and sunscreen, and consider visiting during the cooler parts of the day.
Nette, kostenlose Aktivität. Entlang des Streams immer wieder die Möglichkeit Bier, Kaffee, Wasser, etc zu kaufen.
흙도 신기하고 퇴적된 층들이 신기했어요. 5세 아이 동행해서 걸어들어갔다가 잠든 바람에 되돌아 나올때 안고 나왔는데 힘들어서 들어갈땐 요정의 샘물, 나올땐 용의 눈물이였음

ホテルが近かったのでツアーなしで行った。入場料は無料。

なかなか面白い体験。素晴らしい景色。

足場が緩く沼にはまってしまう場所もあるので、気をつけたほうがいい。太もも近くまでハマってしまう場所があった。

結構濡れるので、私は水着を着用して行った。服を汚したくない人は、服装に気をつけたほうがいいかも。

上流にはトビハゼがいた。

I would recommend wearing some sort of footwear when wadding through the beautiful Fairy Stream. At times it was hard to see where you were stepping and jagged rocks were in some of those situations. Besides that it was quite beautiful and very scenic. Would recommend a visit.
요즘은 수량이 많아서 한낯의 더위를 식히기 좋은 시원한 산책 코스였습니다.
Just be mindful of hidden rocks below the water surface

"Suối Tiên - Mũi Né là một điểm đến tuyệt vời cho những ai muốn tìm kiếm một trải nghiệm thiên nhiên độc đáo và thư giãn. Suối Tiên nổi bật với dòng suối nước mát, nước trong vắt chảy qua những tảng đá, cùng với cảnh quan xung quanh thật sự yên bình và đẹp mắt.

Điều đặc biệt ở Suối Tiên là bạn có thể đi bộ dọc theo suối qua một quãng ngắn, dễ dàng trải nghiệm sự thư giãn khi hòa mình vào không gian thiên nhiên. Cảnh vật tuyệt đẹp, những bãi cát vàng mịn màng và dòng suối mát lành khiến bạn cảm thấy như lạc vào một thế giới khác, xa rời sự ồn ào của thành phố.

Chuyến đi dạo bộ không chỉ giúp bạn thư giãn mà còn có cơ hội chụp những bức ảnh tuyệt đẹp. Suối Tiên là nơi lý tưởng để tận hưởng không khí trong lành và khám phá vẻ đẹp tự nhiên của Mũi Né. Mặc dù không quá dài, nhưng quãng đường đi bộ cũng đủ để bạn cảm nhận sự thư thái và khám phá vẻ đẹp của vùng đất này.

Nếu bạn đang tìm kiếm một địa điểm không quá xa xôi nhưng lại có thể tận hưởng thiên nhiên tuyệt đẹp, Suối Tiên là một lựa chọn tuyệt vời. Tuy không phải là điểm đến quá phức tạp hay yêu cầu kỹ năng đặc biệt, nhưng chắc chắn sẽ mang lại những trải nghiệm thú vị và thư giãn cho bạn."
Đẹp kiến tạo địa chất khá thú vị, nền đất sét nhão trên nền đá, khối đá vôi 2 màu bao xung quanh.
Nice place, really worth to visit
Awesome foods, 👍👍👍👍👍🫰🫰🫰🫰🫰😘
Walk the stream to the waterfall or play with some puppies and drink tea.

This site is really ridiculous and absolutely not worth wasting time on. Too bad because my fiancee and I left Hoi An on purpose. Between the ticket (€5 per person/150,000vnd) and the move another 20€ at least, it was just money and time lost.
The sanctuary was a disappointment to say the least and is quite run down.Additionally our tour guide was terrible and unorganised, it felt like we were being stitched up.

Normally I would say that it is worth visiting, but here is a very huge warning: Watch out at the payment process! We gave them 500.000 Dong and in a second of being insttentive the lady at the ticket desk must have switched the banknote to a 20.000 note and gave it back to us with the comment that it was not enough. By mistake we gave her again 500.000 Dong. After counting the money we recognized that we were cheated. It was a huge scam which we wouldn’t expect at a UNESCO world heritage.

This is my second visit to My Son, the first, nine years ago, was great. In the time since, my son has been ruined by absolutely terrible restoration work - courtesy of the Indian Archeological Survey. Whoever did the work must be totally ashamed of themselves, it’s absolutely barbaric to see an ancient site destroyed like this.
It charges 150k VND per person and 3 of us paid a 500k note but was exchanged with 20k note and told we gave the wrong note. It is an awful experience.

This was our second time to Vietnam so we have seen many historical sites but don't waste any time visiting this site as it's so hot and boring nothing but a bunch of rocks.We took a tour with 'An Thu Tourism Co' and it was so hard to understand the guide.He thought he had good English but we just zoned out, couldn't make out what history he was trying to tell us and he was so annoying in his mannerisms.Tour cost VND140,000 for bus & guide, plus VND150,000 entrance fee into My Son and we paid extra for a boat/lunch trip back to Hoi An for another VND100,000 this option is offered on the forward bus trip.  So whole day cost VND390,000 (AUD $24)Bus trip from Hoi An to My Son took 45 mins, spent 2 hours at My Son including a short ride on electric cars to get you out to the sites.We opted for the return to be by boat and assumed we would board the boat near My Son, but had to drive in the bus for 1/2 hour to get to the boat!  Boat ride takes 40 mins but wasn't too bad.  Vegetarian lunch was very ordinary.Wish we hadn't gone at all and spent the day down at the beach :)

Vraiment décevant ! Il y a des bus de chinois qui monopolisent le lieu. Franchement il reste très peu de temples encore debout et le reste sont refait sans faire attention à l’original. Ça coûte 150 000 pour 30 minutes de balade parmi des centaines de personnes qui te pousse pour se prendre en photo devant des briques ... je recommanderais ce lieu juste pour l’histoire et encore elle est peu présente... allez y si vous ne savez pas quoi faire de votre journée mais vraiment j’ai détesté cet endroit surtout après les 40 min de scoot .....
見る価値なし。世界遺産なのに日本語のガイドがないのに驚いた。そこで、英語のガイドを頼もうとしたが、ランチの時間だったからか断られた。英語のヒヤリングに問題はないが、ヒヤリングができないなら英語の説明があるからそれを見たほうが良いと何度も言われた(問題ないと伝えてある)。そもそも、遺跡自体も見る価値ないと思う。インドネシアのボロブドゥールの方が数百倍良い。
Wer total fasziniert von alten Bauwerken ist, kann sich überlegen dort hin zu gehen. Wobei “Bauwerke“ z.T. schon übertrieben ist.Alle anderen sollten nicht hingehen.Dazu recht teuer mit 150k

My Son Holly Land Was Built In 4th to 13 century by ancient Champa people .They was living in the Central of Vietnam from 192 to 1832 .An amazing with 72 ancient buildings but remained about 25 temples because which were distroied in Vietnam War. My Son was recognized by UNESCO in 1999.A stunning destination for the travelers.
poorly preserved archaelogical site with fake Cham dancer . The scenary is beautiful but it is not worth the long drive from Hoi An

Al llegar el Ticket cuesta 150 por persona la verdad es un precio caro para el estado que tiene el recinto , podrían tenerlo mucho mejor conservado , te cobran un parking de 5 mil , tengan cuidado por qué el guardia de seguridad intentar cobrarte 10mil pero el precio es 5 mil , la verdad nos decepcionó pero bueno es un de las cosas que ahí para hacer en Hoi An , y no nos queríamos ir sin visitarlas pero si no tienen la oportunidad no se preocupen que no tiene nada del otro mundo .
This is probably the most boring tourist attraction I have ever seen. A few pile of bricks a short walk and that’s it. I do not recommend it at all.

Op de scooter naar my son gereden. Tocht erheen is wat lang maar prima te doen. Persoonlijk vond ik maar 1 gedeelte van my son het waard om te bekijken aangezien de andere delen niet meer overeind staan. (Begrijp niet dat ze dat nog willen laten zien aan de touristen..) Hetgene dat nog overeind staat vond ik persoonlijk niet de moeite waard om te bezichtigen.. Sommige dingen kan je beter laten vergaan dan weer opnieuw opbuwen.  Begrijp dan ook niet goed de positieve recenties op internet.

We had a half day tour to My Son with bus travel up and a boat trip back.Unfortunately, we had all been to Ankor Wat in 2016 and were very disappointed at what we were to see at My Son.Indeed I do wonder why it has received such many favourable reviews but perhaps our views were coloured by visits to other ruins in other countries.  There is very little ruins to see and most could be described as a pile of old bricks.The trip back on the boat - ho hum, the included lunch was a plate of cold rice, a few carrot slices and some beans - uncovered and plated up and waiting on the tables when we boarded the boat - not sure how long it had been sitting there but the flies would have had time for a free feed.Do not expect too much from this attraction but do go and make your own judgement - but do not expect to see a big complex which some photos seem to suggest it is,
Avoid avoid avoid this place. Food is terrible. Overpriced but that's to be expected at a tourist stop like this.  The owner should be ashamed of herself.
Самое большое разочарование за всю поездку, смотреть там буквально нечего. Большая территория, ездит бесплатное электротакси, видны зачатки восстановительных работ.
The ruins are not spectacular at all and the place is over and over crowded of hundreds of tourists. The best thing there to see is the free shuttle bus that brings you up the hill and down again.
En mi opinión no merece la pena, esta muuuyy lejos para lo que es. Además nos hizo un calor terrible. Totalmente prescindible

Δεν λέει  να πάς άν έχεις δεί κάτι παρόμοιο αλλού. Πολύ φτωχό. Μόνο το πρώτο διάζωμα λέει κάτι. Τα υπόλοιπα δεν έχουν τίποτε να δείς. Θα χάσεις όλη την μέρα σου 2 ώρες να πάς και 2 να γυρίσεις και 4 ώρες εκεί και δεν αξίζει. καλύτερα να καθίσεις στην HOI AN και να χαζέψεις
Rempli de touristes, peu d'intérêt sur le site lui même, aucune liberté lors de la visite. Même si vous avez du temps, allez ailleurs nous avons vraiment pas apprécié !!

150k VND to get into this placeThe buildings are rebuilt extremely poorly and there is grass covering most of the ruins which makes it not the best experience I took a motorbike from Hoi Ann and it was a very long ride.I would hate to pay for a bus.I have been to alot of temples in my time so I may have a biased review.I would say Tanah lot in Bali would be a better experience than this place.

BE AWARE of very organized SCAM at entrance TICKET COUNTER:Arrived there with my dad on Wednesday 30th of december, around 4pm,the ticket lady wearing traditional "ao dai" costume at the counter asked for 200,000vnd for 2 tickets, I was expecting to pay 300,00 as the sign clearly says 100,000vnd for Vietnamese and 150,000vnd for foreigners (maybe smart way to distract my attention). I gave her a 500,000vnd note, she put it in the drawer on her side of the counter and while looking me she said "sorry do you have two hundred thousand?" while pulling a 20,000vnd note and putting it on the counter. (Classic tourist scam in VN: 20,000 and 500,000 are both blue and look the same)I screamed at her "hey I just gave you a 500,000 note what are you doing?" and immediately bend over the counter and see the 500,000vnd note in empty corner of the open drawer. She gave it back to me, looked down and repeated "Sorry do you have two hundred thousand?"I was paying attention so I didn't get scam but other tourists probably get robbed all day long. Beware of same trick in many touristic spot with 10,000vnd and 200,000vnd notes.
Man muss lange laufen, um eine Reihe von alten Steinen zu besichigen - dieser Ausflug hat sich für uns nicht gelohnt.

Although it was October, it was unbelievably hot. Sunburned in less than two hours though tried to keep in the shade. The ruins are really minimal. Apparently they were bombed to bits. Interestingly, the tour guide repeatedly mentioned the American bombing and only toward the end the fact that the Viet Cong were hiding there. The Americans should never have been in Vietnam, but since they were, probably not the place to hide your soldiers if you want to keep your ancient history intact. Go to Angkor Watt for a serious set of old Hindu temples.
설명없이 구경하면 한시간 반정도 소요..단 날씨는 꼭 미리 알아보고 가세요...더위와의 싸움은 힘듭니다.....

One of the biggest disappointments of the whole trip! We drove for an hour to see it and there wasn't much to see, honestly. I'm really interested in history and it did not fascinate me whatsoever. This place did not life up to the expectations at all!

Even by Vietnamese standards of style & presentation this one is a contender amongst the worst.Billed as the Vietnamese Angkor Wat and the largest and best preserved ancient ruins in the country it might be amusing as an example of marketing triumph over reality if it were not so sad.Pretty much a completely demolished set of ruins with half obliterated description signs.The extremely limited attempts at reconstruction seem to comprise of modern house bricks that make what there is even less appealing.The modern little museum focuses on the minutiae of the stones along with aimless drivel about world harmony rather than imparting any history or social insight.Save your time & money and give your hair a wash instead.Even better, go to Angkor Wat, or anyone of a number of ancient sites in Cambodia, Laos or Thailand where there is something truly well worth seeing.
Would have rather spent the day in Hoi An, rather than wasting time on travelling there. Pretty scenery though. I would not recommend this tour. Kids aged 9 & 11.

I was worried that reviewers said the bus tour would be cramped, and asked at the booking hotel if it was big enough for westerners, happy to pay more if not. They insisted it was, but not true, everyone's knees were hunched against their chests. It was like a coffin on wheels. When we set off, the guide talked, I could only understand 20% of what he said.  Then he said we needed to turn around and go back to Hoi An as some customers had been waiting two hours to be picked up. They were livid, and then there wasn't enough seats for them on the bus. Disaster.Really, pay extra and get a private driver....
was very disappointed. there is hardly any remains left and you can cover the main area in ten mintues. to travel out for 2 hrs (1 hr bus ride there and 1 hr back) is not worth the effort.

I expected quite a lot from this sanctuary, but it was a rather large disappointment. Firstly, the lady selling the tickets tried to rip me off. When I finally got in, quite a number of temples were being renovated, and thus completely covered in scaffoldings/plastic. Last, many of the former temples only have the ruins left standing - not a surprise, since they were built with uust bricks, and were heavily bombed during the vietnam war. However, to me it looked more like someone stacked a pile of bricks yesterday and called it a temple.All in all, not recommended, there isn't a complete temple left standing. Especially after angkor wat, this just feels like another cheap way to cash in from tourists.

本当に世界遺産？と思う程管理がずさんです。チャータータクシーで行ったのですが、受付の女性が支払いした高額紙幣（500.000D）を、同色の小額紙幣（20.000D）にすり替えこれでは足りないと言って来ました。あれ？と思い別紙幣で支払いをしました。友人とあれ？見間違い？という話になり、残金を確認すると500.000Dが足りないので再びチケット売り場に戻り、やりとりの説明をし返してと言うと10分ほどしてやっと返ってきました。友人３人で行った為、２人がおかしいという事に気づきましたが、１人だったら気づかなかったかもしれません。(このすり替えは２度されていたようで、初回は100.000D⇒10.000Dにすり替えしたようで、この後に500.000Dのやりとりがあったので気づきました）世界遺産登録されているところでこんな事が起こるなんて、本当に腹立たしいです。手品のように本当に一瞬の出来事です。ツアー参加ではなくこれから行く方は気をつけてください！！！！！！！！！遺跡自体もカンボジアの遺跡も見ていたので、規模感が小さく、説明も少なく管理が行き届いてない様に感じました。行かなくても良かったですね～

We booked this tour because it is a Unesco site. We took this tour from Hoi An. They also run this tour from Danang and Hue. My Son was a Champa Sanctuary that was heavily damaged during the war and is now just some ruins. It was hot, humid and buggy. The lunch was meager, just rice and cabbage sauteed with some carrots. The guide was generally unfriendly towards foreigners and especially unfriendly towards  the French and the Americans. Expect the tour guide to upsell those with just a bus tour to a bus/boat tour. Expect the tour guide to inform you that entrance fee was an additional $60,000VND. Expect the tour guide to sell you a drink with your meager lunch and then pass out the free water about 30 minutes later.

We went on a bus with about 50 others. Takes an hour to get there. Then spent 30 minutes walking to the site which was fine. My Son is interesting however the guide wouldn't wait for people to all gather around before talking. 50 people per group is poor. Came back by boat which was not worth it. Lunch was poor. They charged us twice for the tickets to My Son as well.

My cousin really wanted to go here... so we went. I expected to be WOWed by the beautiful sights that were advertised to us, but needless to say this place is unimpressive. We walked around for an hour to look at these rocks... really, just rocks. some are aesthetically pleasant, but I feel like I've wasted an entire day going here to stare at a bunch of rocks. lots of walking involved and you will FRY if it's a hot day.

Purtroppo rimangono solo rovine di questo sito archeologico che in passato credo sia stato maestoso. La guerra ha abbattuto gran parte delle strutture. Il complesso rimane dentro la foresta e offre alcuni scorci suggestivi ma non mi ha lasciato senza fiato

As always, your experience largely depends on your expectations. I see some people mention Angkor here... well if you just have been there and you expect somethink like that here, you gonna be dissapointed. Although if you do not expect much, it can make for a pleasant half-day trip, but you really should go on a dry day.We visited the site with an organised tour in order to learn more about the history. Note that the tour doesn't include the entrance ticket that cost 150.000 dong per pers and which I find overpriced compared to other attractions in Vietnam. The company that runs the tour is called Camel. We were told that the pick-up time was 7.30 am but the bus arrived a whole hour later... not a good start. After more people have been picked up around 9.00 am, we had to change to a slightly bigger bus that was already full so my boyfriend had to sit an hour with his legs up. We had a fun tour guide, he was Ok with fairly understandable English. What I didn't like about him is that he asked us for giving him tips. We spent around 2 hours at the site, most of the time walking. There were some muddy parts so walking shoes are recommended, especially in the rainy season. This applies umbrellas/raincoats, which you also can buy at the entrance (15-20.000 dong for a poncho, 80-100.000 for an umbrella). The area is really beautiful, a lush green site with a small river surrounded by mountains. But the ruins (as little as remains after the war) aren't very impressive. Some are being restored but I didn't like the way they do that. However I have to admit that the main site is quite nice and we took some very nice pictures during our visit. Another plus is that the area is being kept clean, a rarity in Vietnam.There were two options to get back to Hoi An, by bus or by boat. We did the latter that costs a little bit more but we thought that would be nicer. It also includes a very light vegetarian meal (rice with some tofu and vegetables - tasty but not really enough to call it a lunch). Note that it is not boat all the way, first you drive 40 minutes back by bus and after that you change to a small boat, and this trip takes you another 40 minutes. We imagined the boat trip would be longer as we were told we would be back around 3 pm with the boat option;our guide said 2.30 pm; finally we arrived in Hoi An at 2.00 pm. So the tour started an hour later and finished an hour earlier than we were told... All in all, it was ok, I do not regret it but it is not really worth the time and money.

About an hours drive out of Hoi An, the sanctuary was very average. Maybe because I automatically compare it to Angkor Wat or Borobudur. Most of it is in ruins or has been restored, which doesn't give it the same effect that I can imagine that it once did. The bomb craters around the site were the most interesting bit. And SO many people there at once, it was hard to get photos or walk through the structures.

Il sito mantiene il suo fascino nonostanteLe necessarie strutture turistiche ottimo l'utilizzo di car ecologiche per accompagnarvi all'inizio del percorso purtroppo non rimane molto dopo le devastazioni delle guerre se siete in zona vale la pena distante 10 km da Hoi an in questo periodo potrebbe piovere quindi utile ombrello e impermeabile e sandali  che si asciugano in fretta e potete ripulire da eventuake fango

If you have been to Angkor or Bagan this will not compare by any measure.  The setting is nice but the temples are in very poor repair (thanks in part to US bombs).  The rainforest and hilly surroundings are however atmospheric.Main disadvantage and why only 3 stars - on an organised tour from Hoi An (pretty much your only choice unless you have a private car (it is too far for a motorbike)) it was a long 6 hour effort there and back with lots of hotel stops, for a ruin that frankly can be seen in 30 minutes if you didn't have to follow the pace of your enormous tour group.Tip: hire a private car and local guide - more expensive but more comfortable and time-efficient.

We turned up one rainy December day to find we were the only visitors...at 10am!! Turns out the tour coaches couldn't get through the flooded roads, unlike our expert driver. Great we thought - no crowds. However, not only could the coaches not get there, but the guides you book on site couldn't get to work either. So no guide. Oh well we said.  So went in anyway. We quickly found there's little information on site or even sign posts pointing towards the exits! We were the only 3 people in what was basically a monsoon and kept getting lost. Very confusing!!So, all in all a spectacular place but ruined by the lack of a guide or signage. Think we just got unlucky - will try again in the future.

I thought the sanctuary was interesting. But if you are planning to visit Angkor what in Cambodia, probably you will find this visit a bit unnecessarily . We had a guide to explain everything which helped a lot. During the visit you can appreciate the beautiful surroundings and you can assist some performances of local culture and dance. In spite of being beautiful I don't think I would go again.if you have lots of time it's okay but if you are short of time maybe you can pass this.
Nice temples to visit tour last approximately 1 to 2 hours.   Inexpensive tour... includes boat ride down the river... Nice to see

Die Anreise mit einem geliehenen Roller von Hoi An klappt problemlos und ist auch bezahlbar, im Vergleich dazu ist der Eintritt mit 150.000 Dong schon richtig teuer. My Son ist als Ausflug, wenn man nichts besseres zu tun hat, ganz nett. Möchte man allerdings beeindruckende Tempelanlagen sehen in Asien, so führt an Ankor Wat kein Weg vorbei.

Wir sind mit dem Roller von Hoi An allein hingefahren (etwa 80Min Fahrtzeit). Die Fahrt war sehr anstrengend, da die Straßen staubig sind und viele LKWs fahren (schlechte Luft). Wer nicht so auf das Geld achtet, sollte ein Shuttle oder Taxi nehmen.Der Eintritt war 120k oder 150k.Der Parkplatz für den Roller 5k, obwohl er uns erst 10k abnehmen wollte (man sollte überall in Vietnam handeln oder man zahlt halt mehr als den tatsächlichen Preis)Die Anlage an sich ist ganz schön aber meiner Meinung nach nix besonderes. Nicht vergleichbar mit Ankor Wat.Es war keine Zeitverschwendung, jedoch ist es kein Muss.

My Son Sanctuary is about 45minutes to an hour away from Hoi An which is not far. Upon reaching the site, you have to buy the entrance ticket which is 150k viet dong. Then walk a short distance where you will be able to take a free shuttle buggy up to the hill where the ruins are. There is a map which you can follow and walk along to visit te ruins. If you would like to hire a guide, you can do that as well through the guide office next to the stop of the shuttle buggy.The ruins are pretty much in bad shaped compared to Angkor Wat. The authorities are trying to preserve but they used the wrong method of doing so, as pointed out by the guide. For example, if you look carefully at the walls which they tried to repair and construct with modern methods, are full of algae. And those walls that were built in the past did not have such growth.But overall, it is still a nice place to visit.

Crumbling site of temples dating back to the Champa people. Heavily bombed during the war as it was used as a military base by the VC. Looted by the French during colonial reign. Good guide, informative, but a little anti American, made me a little  uncomfortable. Site overall had a bit of a sad vibe.

Scenic temple ruins to be accessed by tourbus. The ride is a little long (1hr) and it might be better to hire a private guide on site or travel in a smaller group. Very beautiful mountains, ruins and nature in general. Interesting cultural history and very visitor friendly for walking around. Depending on the tour operator, you may be unlucky to be forced to visit some kind of craft village or something like that, which we find annoying. We can't recommend the 'return by river' option, as the boat is loud, smells of exhaust and our boat actually broke down.

It is a similar site to Angkor Wat of Cambodia, but of a way smaller scale. For me, it does not seem to worth a 1.5 hour ride from Hoi An. Anyway, if you are a lover of Hindu Sanctuary or Ancient Temples, you may still like this site very much.

We rented a scooter for the day and drove out to the ruins. Parking was 10k. Entry fee 150k each. They had a nice shuttle taking you within walking distance of the ruins. Pretty neat site. Very run down from the bombings but they are trying to rebuild and preserve. Not as magnificent as Angkor or Borobudur but if you're in Hoi An it's worth the drive.

К сожалению, во время войны большая часть комплекса была разрушена. На сегодня доступны к осмотру несколько руин древнего города. Пара небольших объектов восстановлены для представления, как это было раньше.  Не сравним с храмовым комплексом Ангкор-Ват в Камбоджи, но если Вы там не были и у Вас есть половина свободного дня, то обязательно посетите. Входной билет стоит 150 000 донгов, платная парковка для байков. На территории бесплатный шаттл к началу осмотра достопримечательностей.

If you've been to Siem Reap you are likely to find this site disappointing in it's scale & condition. The site is interesting in it's own right however & the right tour guide can bring it to life. The US bombings did the site no favours. If you've not been to Siem Reap this may whet your appetite to go. It is reasonably accessible with shuttle buses reducing the walking distance and the butterflies and flowers in bloom added to it's charm.

We took one of the cheap package deals, about $30 per person. Includes pick up from hotel, 2 hrs at the site and boat trip back. Don't expect anything more than the basics for this price. Minivan was cramped and stuffy, boat was basic and the included meal was no more than steamed rice and some fried veg. Not a whole lot to see along the river but fun to do all the same. Be warned, they don't take you straight back. Instead they stop at a village across from hoi an where they sell woodcarving products before dropping you back. Also the guides don't take you to the small site museum located at the entrance which is stupid because there are useful maps of the site and diagrams of temples. This seems a much better place to introduce the site than standing in the baking hot Sun!!
ベトナム戦争で破壊されてしまったようで、保存状態があまり良くなかった。全体的に希望はあまり大きくなく、アンコールワットの方が美しかった。土産物屋や飲食店もあまりない。監視員が常にどこかにいて、遺跡に登ろうとする人を見かけると笛を鳴らして注意していた。広い敷地内は、バス？で移動できるのは、ありがたかった。
是中越古占婆王朝所建印度教廟宇,因越戰而殘破不堪,破敗不已,不及吳哥窟千分之一,而地雷處處,危機四覆,切記勿脫隊亂跑.以免葬身異地.

We went on a tour from Hoi An, and found the experience rather rushed. The ruins were so busy that it was hard to get any sense of the atmosphere and we felt rather rushed around. I wish we'd made our own way there and come in the late afternoon, it would have been a better experience. The ruins are badly damaged, but still interesting.

Si has estado en Camboya o Tailandia... Puedes prescindir de esta visita.150.000 Dongs por persona + 5000 Dongs de aparcar la moto.Dispone de transporte interno gratis entre los templos.El calor en esta zona es infernal.Si vas en moto echale 1h a buen ritmo....y sin perdernos.

A mon avis juste génie pour les passionnés d'archéologie !! Aucun doute! Pour nous qui n'y connaissent rien et pour qui il est difficile de comprendre toutes les explications réalisé en anglais non avons trouvé le prix excessif, certainement du a la présence de hoi an à côté ! D autres visites réalisées par la suite deux a trois fois moins chère nous on d'autant plus marqué tels que les tunnel de vinh moc et les grotte de phong nha!!! jolie certes mais...

Ik vond My Son zeker wel de moeite om te bezoeken, maar ik had er veel meer van verwacht. Eigenlijk is er maar 1 gedeelte echt de moeite om te bezichtigen (zie foto's). Het museum vond ik niet zo interessant. Op de paden kun je een beetje schaduw vangen, maar anders kan het er heel heet worden.

Vanuit Hoi An met een scooter naar My Son gereden. Leuke route en prima te doen qua drukte. Wij waren er rond 12:30 en het was er rustig! De drukte kwam rond 14:30 weer op gang. Op eigen gelegenheid in my son rondgelopen. Ruïnes zijn zwaar beschadigd, wel interessant om te zien maar de ruïnes in Cambodja en Thailand zijn veel mooier. 30-60 min rondlopen is echt voldoende.

Ce site d'un grand intérêt historique est hélas bien abîmé . Les ruines restantes sont restaurées , pour certaines, pas toujours de la meilleure façon . Allez y tôt , car il y a beaucoup de monde. Facilité de circulation sur le site grâce à une navette électrique.

旅行会社の昼食付き半日観光で参加しました。ミーソン遺跡までは片道1時間半程度で、トイレ休憩はありませんでした。半年前にアユタヤ遺跡を見たので、正直、規模が小さく物足りない感じがしました。ですが、遺跡、彫刻が綺麗に残っているものもあったので、行って損はない感じがしました。ただし、往復3時間程度かかる一方、滞在が1時間程度だったのが、残念でした。夏に行ったので、メチャクチャ暑かったです。防止等の暑さ対策をしていって下さい。

Nous avons bien aimé la visite de ces ruines, celles encore debout sont belles et bien différentes de ce qu'on a pu voir dans le reste du Vietnam, mais le tarif d'entrée est bien trop cher pour ce qu'il y a à voir selon nous ...

We booked the standard tour through our hotel. We were picked op at 4:15 AM! Since we were the first on the small bus. Breakfast consisted of two slices of bread, two slices of egg and some lattuce and tomato. I suggest you bring your own snack. At 6:00 we arrived together with a larger bus. Some 40 people shared this guide that we didn't join. It was much nicer to walk around and discover the temples on your own ( do some reading yourself before you go). Much has been destroyed by US but the atmosphere is really nice. We also did some birdwatching. On the way back we opted for the boatride (last part of the way back). This was very noisy and views not really interisting. The village visit was also not interesting, I suggest to take thd boat back.

Si vous avez déjà visité des sites Cham, en particulier au Cambodge, cette visite n'aura pas de réel intérêt pour vous... A faire, si vous aimez les ruines... Entrée à 150 000 VND et pour le trajet en bus aller-retour comptez 6$. Vous poubez ausi faire le trajet aller en bus et le retour en bateau pour 8$, qui inclut une assiette de riz et légumes, et un arrêt au Kim Bong Carpentry village.

Tour vanuit Hoi An naar de My Son temple gedaan. Aardige busrit van ongeveer een uur over slechte wegen. De tempels zijn indrukwekkend om te zien mits je genoeg inbeeldingsvermogen hebt om te bedenken hoe het eruit heeft gezien. Tip ga vroeg in de ochtend met een sunrise trip dan is het nog te doen met de hitte.
Booked a US10 local tour to my son at hotel front desk, excluding the VND 1,500K  entrance ticket. Hard to compare it with Angkor but surprised by the destructive effect  of Vietnam war

If you have been to Angkor Wat in Cambodia, do not waste your time coming here as you will find this a disappointment. There is nothing much to see, most of the ruins were in poor condition plus the scorching sun were major minus points for this place.

Most of what's left is really ruins. There are only 2-3 places in good enough condition to enter, and they have been rebuilt. The site is fairly interesting at first glance, but you'll struggle to spend an hour there. Even if you use the thoughtful shuttle bus, there is still a LOT of walking to see a not particularly impressive site.

I booked the sunrise tour of My Son sanctuary via my homestay, and was a little disappointed that we arrived at My Son well after sunrise. For me the draw was seeing the sun actually rise over the temples and mountains. There's a fair amount of walking at the sanctuary, which can feel overbearing in the humid jungle, but there are shuttles between gates. There are 4 main sites to walk around, and a few stray dogs to play with. I can't imagine going without a tour group, as you wouldn't learn much. The signs are few and far between with very little information on them. Overall, I'd only recommend staying here for 1 hour if you have a lot of time in Hoi An to kill.

If you've been to Angkor Wat or Borubudur you will be totally disappointed. Nothing much to see though our tour guide has a lot of information. It was very hot and humid. Bring water, umbrella, and hat. It's not appropriate for small children.

A bit of a disappointment, I must say even when I had prepared myself not to compare it with Angkor Wat.  Unless you are a huge fan of similar types of ruins, this place would not impress you if you have been to Angkor Wat as it is much smaller and preservation works are somewhat lacking.

By the passing of time, long lost invasions, but certainly helped along by 20th century wars, very little is left of what looks like it could have been a vibrant place of imbibing the divine. The obvious traces of yogic and Hindu influence are very interesting to see.I am not sure it was worth the visit though (maybe the scorching sun was just a tad too much when I visited)
Perhaps I've visited the Angkor temples. This is comparatively less impressive, but still worth a visit to learn about the history of the Cham people.

It is a famous visit attraction in Danang, but if you don't have a tour guide to introduce the background information, your visit will be boring. The entry fee is fair, not expensive. However, I will not visit again shortly because maintenance is under processing.

Bien que passablement détruites lors de la guerre le site contient encore de belles ruines. Le site est aménagé, facile en famille donc. Cependant cela enlevé du naturel à l'endroit et il y pas mal de touristes (allez y en semaine). Il faut 1h30 voir 2 max si vous prenez du temps pour faire le tour. Le prix est de 150k dongs.

Para quem gosta de histórias e ruínas é muito bom. Um pouco longe cansativo até chegar por lá. Interessante e místico esse lugar por estar no meio das montanhas e florestas. Acesso fácil e muito boa estrutura para o turista. Limpo e organizado.

Als je tijd hebt kun je dit zeker even doen en dan het beste als het bewolkt is anders is het echt te warm. En mocht je zelf scooter rijden lukt dat makkelijk vanaf Hoi An... Een tourtje hiervoor boeken is zonde van je geld.
Very cool history. Lovely and peaceful area to walk around. Beware of entrance fee (around 6 USD). Worth a visit

Dernière visite culturelle que l'on ait fait au Vietnam : il ne reste plus grand-chose de ce site hindou detruit par les bombes américaines. Nous n'avons pas eu de chance notre guide était inintéressant. Il n'y a quasiment aucune indication sur place pour comprendre. A voir uniquement si on a du temps.

Ce site est présenté comme le Angkor Vat vietnamien...c'est sans doute exagéré et il vaut mieux ne pas avoir vu le site cambodgien avant de découvrir My Son. Le site en lui même est peu étendu et certains vestiges , suite aux bombardements  de la guerre du Vietnam sont en très mauvais état ( des restaurations sont en cours ). A découvrir pour les " vrais "amateurs de vieilles pierres . Les autres risqueront d'être déçus..
Not much left from the old city. Not much to see,but nature  is beautiful and it's good place to escape from the town. If you don't have time you can skip this place.

We went with a tour, which stopped at a shop before the entrance to my son for 'drink and toilet break'.  The toilets were filthy,  had no locks on the doors, no toilet paper in sight and to top it all off you had to pay to use them.When you get to entrance to my son there are much nicer and free toilets. So don't use the first ones if you can help it. The ruins themselves we enjoyed, it was a terribly hot day so we found it a little hard,but our tour guide was informative and learnt a lot.

ホイアンから長いことバスに揺られて着いた、「ミーソン遺跡」＝「世界文化遺産」です。山の中にレンガ作りのお寺です。いくつかの時代ごとの区域があり、判りにくい案内板を頼りに行くわけですが、見学前にショーを見てから見学です。レンガ作りのため崩れかかっているものがあり、一部には徐々に修復されてるようです。日本語ガイド付きオプション50ドル前後で6～8時間かけていくほどのものではないと思いますが、ダナンに行ったら行かないのはもったいない。半～日以上

O tour é interessante e tem bastante detalhe pra ver. Se você tem pouco tempo em Hoi An, sugiro priorizar outras atividades pois o santuário foi bem destruído na guerra, então não se vê edifícios inteiros.

Si vous êtes plusieurs jours sur Hoi An, pourquoi pas ...Certains endroits sont en rénovation.Ça reste un site sympa quand même. Vous faites une grande boucle pour visiter ce site. Prévoir de l'eau avec soi car il fait très chaud !!! Possibilité d'en acheter au milieu de la visite.
Interesting to visit and probably would have been great before it was damaged, but the long trip out there was probably not worth it. Having been to Angor Watt, there is no comparison.
Morning myson一早來myson看日出……雖沒有吳哥美，最大的竟然也越戰被炸毀了，東南亞五大王朝的美名，比吳哥早500年的歷史，世界文化遺產的名頭，人生還是不能有遺憾是吧^^

It's OK but a bit underwhelming. We found the site hard to put into context and not well preserved although restoration work is ongoing. Signage was minimal but our guide was helpful. It was very hot in late March.

The place/site is good. I just don't understand why the entrance fee (150k VND) isn't included in the $6-8 we paid at the booking agency. Also and more importantly, the tour guides SERIOUSLY need to improve their English comm skills and sense of humour.

It took over 2 hours on a bus to get to the Holy Son site and then a 20 minute uphill walk. Our guide spent 20 minutes telling us a brief history of the site and then we had another 20minutes to take photos and look around, then back on the bus. I think there were better ways to spend a day but that's just me.

Nos esperábamos unas ruinas impresionantes y no es nada de eso. Es un lugar bonito y con encanto, más por el paisaje que por las ruinas en si, además nos toco un guía local que hablaba mal ingles y nos enterábamos de la mitad. Es mejor ir lo más pronto posible ya que a partir de las 10 se llena de turistas y pierde bastante

L'endroit n'est pas loin de Hoi Han prévoir une bonne matinée car le chemin n'est pas une autoroute surtout vers la fin .À l'entrée du site ,il y a des navettes electriques vertes toutes neuves qui vous transportent jusqu' aux ruines qui ressemblent à un micro Angkor  du moins ce que les bombardements US ont épargné .La visite se fait rapidement car le secteur est peu étendu , à noter la construction étayée menaçant de s 'écrouler à proximité d 'un cratère de bombe ,ainsi qu'une sculpture représentant la fertilité masculine  dans tous ses états... Un spectacle de danse s'y déroule aux  pieds  des ruines de ce qui fut un refuge pour les maquisards .

We stayed at Danang and booked a day trip to My Son.  Maybe because I recently went to Siem Reap, and basically My Son is a mini version of Lady Temple in Siem Reap, so I wasn't too impressed.  If you enjoy temples and have time for it, then I guess it would be okay.  It was a bit of a drive too from Danang.

Nice temples in pretty surroundings. Not worth a visit if you've seen other temples. Don't do the $6 or $12 My Son tour sold in Hoi an - the price of entering the temples is conviently not included (the brocgurr mentioned that it excludes 'insurancr and personal fees', whatever this means), the village tour is a souvenir shop - overall, it is a utter scam!
A very nice place to visit for half a day, a nice wonder round what basically is a very very small ankor watt.
캄보디아를 가보지 않은분들에게는 괜찮습니다.캄보디아 앙코르 와트를 다녀오신 분들에게는 규모등 여러모로 시시하게 느껴질 수 있습니다.

Username: グローブ・タロッター
Comment: ミーソン遺跡ツアーをホテルにて予約しました。代金は7US$もしくは150,000VNDです。ホテルまでピックアップがあり、帰りは船で簡単な食事付きのコースです。遺跡までの道中は、ガイドさんの諸注意や遺跡にまつわるうんちく話で、なかなか楽しい時間を過ごすことが出来ました。遺跡到着後は少しの距離を徒歩で、途中からは電動カートで移動するという、ある意味エコな雰囲気ですが、ここだけ何処かの国や企業の援助を受けて完成したという感じが否めません。遺跡到着後は、ガイドの指示によりツアーが開始されるのですが、この荒廃した遺跡を良しとするべきなのか否とするべきなのかは判定の分かれるところです。私自身は、ベトナム戦争などの影響を受けたことなどの背景を理解しても、カンボジアにあるアンコールや東南アジアに多くある仏教・ヒンドゥ遺跡に軍配を上げたいと思います。勿論遺跡の保存状態や修復などは、比較するレベルにも達していません。また、ガイドにもよるのでしょうが、ベトナム戦争の話やリンガ(男性崇拝)の文言があまりにも多く登場して、少しばかり食傷気味でもありました。遺跡観光の起点には食堂などがあり、ひとときの安息が訪れますが、水や日よけの道具は必ず持参するべきだと思います。帰路の途中、河に遭遇し船にてホイアン中央市場付近の船着き場に帰ってきます。その途中に簡単な食事が提供されます。評価は、各人の期待度や歴史観や嗜好によって大きく変わると思いますが、一度は訪問されても良いのではないでしょうか。

Went on a tour organised by our homestay.The ruins themselves are ok to see, but if you've been to Angkor, Ayuthaya or Sukhothai they're not too impressive. My partner and I found the tragic effects on the area the most interesting-huge craters still in situ as reminder of the destruction. If you do the tour with the boat ride back its  an ok day trip for the scenery but personally wouldnt go back to My Son by itself.

I canceled online booking and booked tour through a hotel, actually it was much much cheaper!This tour actually need to prepare for it. Need to walk very long distance, very hot, recommend to bring comfy shoes, big bottle of water, sunnies, hat,if you have a towel, would be perfect! I was expected arriving exactly My son but bus arrived very far from My son, walk to electric car port, ride 2km then walk around my son. so many other tourist complained about awareness of this tour, hotness, and place.I loved this place, but I don't think I will be back.
