The temples are ok and interesting all though for older people the walk in the heat and sun could be a little much. If your based in Hoi An don`t bother doing the boat ride back the journey is an hour with nothing to see, then you get to wood making island for 20 minutes then more boating back to Hoi An . I did not find that part worth the extra dong and I couldn`t buy wooden things to bring back through Aus customs anyway.
Maybe a bit commercialized, in the sense there were so many tour groups at one time that you missed a lot because of all the hustle and bustle.
Sanctuary is well maintained n serviced but the explanation n remains of temples is not properly done.
ホテルで予約。往復ともバスは6USD、入場料150,000VNDは別。帰りが小型船でランチ付きはもっと高い。フランスやベトナム戦争でアメリカに破壊されたり、持ち去られた云々と英語ガイドが言っていた。レンガつくりや首のない像が印象的。

베트남 전쟁 때 폭격 맞아 거의 무너져서 폐허상태입니다. 그래서 큰 기대하시면 실망하고요. 호이안에서 한 시간 반 정도 버스 타고 갔나 그랬어요. 전기자동차 타고 좀 갔다 내려서 걸어야 하는데 보행이 많이 불편하신 분 아님 애들이나 어르신들도 걸으실 만 할 거예요. 저흰 2월 말이라 20도 정도라서 덥지도 춥지도 않고 적당했는데 건기에 오시면 많이 더울 거 같았어요. 돌아오는 길은 버스 타고 오는 거랑 배타면서 점심 먹고 도자기 마을 갔다 오는 게 있는데 저는 그냥 버스 타고 왔어요. 호텔에서 보트투어 따로 해 준다해서요. 버스타고 오는 게 돈은 더 쌉니다.

i joined the tour by The Sinh Tourist. It says from the web that they offer pick up from hotel at 4:30am, coffee and breakfast will be provided at my son so we could enjoy sunrise there. But no one tried to email or call to ask where to pick up. So i had to confirm in person when i arrived hoi an, and they said the pick up time would be 5am. Eventually the car picked us up on time but stopped in the middle of nowhere, still in city, to wait for no one for more than 10 minutes. so we arrived my son a lot later than sunrise. No coffee provided and the tour guide was making joke of that instead of telling me where i can get a coffee. For my son, it was just okay, not surprising. Some original details of my son was worth paying attention, but the restoration was done without details, which was quite distracting.

Our 4th visit to Hoi An and we felt it was time to visit My Son. Private car $35USD picked us up at 7.00pm, we enjoyed the drive through the countryside. Took approx 1 hour, arrived and the car park was empty, bought our tickets 150000VND each and quickly passed through the ticket booth walked a couple of hundred metres over a bridge to where the shuttle cabs were waiting. 2km later and we were at the top, we hired a guide for 100000VND and headed off to see the ruins. Like many reviewers have previously said they certainly cannot be compared to Angkor Wat, a very very small sanctuary in comparison. The guide was with us for approx 1 hour explaining when the temples were built etc She had been a guide for 12 years and personally I think she needed a change, no enthusiasm or obvious pride or passion in what she was doing. When not explaining she talked on her phone and played games. At 9.30am we watched a Cham dance demonstration, I thought it was very well done. We then walked back to catch the shuttle cab back and OMG the place was crowded, car park was filled and there was a long line at the ticket entrance. Called into the museum on our way out looked around for a couple of minutes and then jumped into our air conditioned car and left the crowds.

The site itself is not big, compared to Angkor Wat or Ayutthaya. Some of the ruins are restored recently, making some of them look a little too new. We went there with a tour operating company which costed us $7 including a tour guide and transportation roundtrip to/from Hoi An. We arrived at the site around 10am and it was really really hot. The guide's English wasn't good and he couldn't explain much about the history of the place. It'd have been nicer if we go earlier when the weather is cooler and I'd recommend that you hire a professional guide otherwise, you wouldn't understand much what you were seeing.

Took a day trip from Hoi An went by bus returned by boat. If you've visited the temples at Ankhor Wat you'll find My Son underwhelming. Returned trip by boat wasn't really worth it. Boat traveled very slowly served entre sized portion of boiled rice and luke warm vegetables, unappetizing. All goods at Artisans village were extremely overpriced. Although their craftmanship is amazing.
Het is mooi om gezien te hebben, maar niet in vergelijking met de borobodur op Java. Toch heb ik erg genoten van mijn bezoek. Niet alleen om de tempels, maar ook de omgeving doet veel.

Onze gids vond vooral zichzelf heel erg leuk. Zelfstandig rondlopen was er dan ook niet bij. Eén gedeelte is goed bewaard/gerestaureerd; de rest viel me zwaar tegen. Kan me voorstellen dat het voor Vietnam iets bijzonders is, en het feit dat de Amerikanen het in de oorlog bijna helemaal plat gebombardeerd hebben (zoals onze gids -tig keer vermeldde) maakt ook indruk. Ik zou er niet meer heen gaan.

Išli sme sem organizovane z hotela z Hoi an. Cesta trvala približne hodinu. Chrámy sú krásne a staré, samozrejme v lesoch vo Vietname. Ak sa ale chystáte do Angkoru, tak povenujte čas radšej iným miestam v tejto oblasti. Ale ak sa tam už vydáte, určite nebudete ľutovať. Je to rozdelené na cca 4 časti, prvé sú väčšie s viac chrámami, ostané sú už menšie s 1-2 malými chrámami. Všetko sú to ale menšie chrámy, nie je to ani zďaleka ako Agkor. Celú časť aj s výkladom obehnete za 2-3hod.

We took a guided tour to the site in the late morning which was very crowded.We found ourselves unable to move around freely as there were so many people taking photos. I would suggest taking an early morning tour or making your own way there by motorbike as I feel the crowds took away from the significance of the site.If you have limited time in Hoi An I wouldn't make this your top priority.

I took the dawn tour of My Son, leaving Hoi An at 5am. It was a cool January day, so that wasn't really necessary, but I'm glad there weren't many people there. We had a tour guide who explained a lot of the history and masonry, which was by far the best part of the trip. The temples are so old and reflect a nearly forgotten history that has little resemblance to modern Vietnam. As a spectacle and photo op, the site is only marginally  cool - it's no Angkor Wat or Bagan. It's also quite small. If you have more than 2 days in Hoi An, I'd definitely venture out there, but if you have les time, don't feel bad about skipping.
c'est un endroit joli mais pas mon lieux favoris.Cela permet de sortir de Hoi An pendant une bonne matinée.La route pour se rendre sur le site est très belle

Ancient temple relics worth visiting if you are into culture and history. Rather long drive from Hoi An. Not very sure it was worth the visit. Personally I think the countryside you get to see on your way there is better. Maybe if I had more time in Hoi An it would have looked better...

Alles is middelmatig, wel paar mooie tempels maar snel uitgekeken. Wat vervelender was is dat onze gids die we daar ter plekke boekten, zeer slecht engels sprak en ze liep met het tempo van een slak. Geen intentie om iets meer op ons tempo te lopen. Al met al een matig dagje

My Son was smaller than we expected and in hindsight, since we were only 3 days in beautiful Hoi An, might not have been worth it to spend the whole morning going there (1 hour trip in bus). The fact that only a few structures still remain standing due to the Americans bombs during the Vietnam war makes it less interesting than what site seems to have been.The most interesting things about this Charm culture site is that has such a big influences from so many different religions, etc. but this can only be grasped if you have a good guide with good English, we did not and he seemed to try and tell us many interesting things but we could understand only 40%.

If you are in Hoi Ann only 3-4 days I am not sure if I would spend my time again in travlling there half a day unless I was sure I had a great guide which would explain a lot of the Charm culture and the history of the site.Visually there is only a few buildings left intact, a lot was destroyed by American bobs during the Vietnam war, there are a few intresting buidings and a lot of little sculptures around the site.We had a guide we arranged through the Hotel as a group tour and we understood 50% of his english, although he seemed to be telling some intresting things, specially intresting that the site has mix of so many cultures.
Một địa điểm thú vị đẻ tìm hiểu thêm về lịch sử. Tuy nhiên hơi thất vọng về việc bảo tồn các hiện vật, nhìn các tháp được trét lại bằng xi-măng thật không đẹp mắt chút nào.

I found these ruins to be very unimpressive. They were just a heap over over grown rubble and buildings falling down. The drive there was more interesting.  There was lots of walking and climbing so it's not suitable for anyone who struggles to climb hills.
ベトナム戦争の名残と文明遺跡が一緒に共存する場所。タイのアユタヤ遺跡、カンボジアのアンコールワット遺跡とはまた違った遺跡を見学出来るかと。。。ダナン空港まで行ったら足を伸ばしてみてください。

Since we are Indians ...we didn't find it great shakes .considering the wealth and treasure we have back home..this was just a tip of iceberg of our legacy back home .It was nice nevertheless .and one feels sad for the Vietnamese xoz they lost it all in all early invasions and attacks ..leaving them bereft and with little to show the world .The complex is well maintained . Structures are mostly ruined save a few that made it for our visit.Our guide was cute with his broken English and Vietnamese accented pronunciations .he made it funny for us while he kept emphasizing on the phallus symbol.The ride back was in a boat .this trip cost us 5dollars each hence we didn't expect much.we carried our lunch and we're glad ..coz they offered us boiled veggies with miniscule rice . They promised a visit to the carpentry village which was a sham..they knew they weren't taking us .it was too much to ask for in 5 dollars .We booked through am agency called dive in. We felt we would have been better off in a spa basking in the ancient town of hoian instead of coming here all the way .Hue would have been way better but we didn't have time .Avoidable.
Very historical place with loads of history and culture of Cham and Hinduism - unfortunately mostly destroyed during the Vietnam war

Wir haben es gegen 12 erreicht. Sie Sonne und die Temperatur von 30 grad (Winter) waren schwer ertragbar. Erst als es zugezogen ist, ist es angenehmer geworden.-bei kühlen Temperaturen besuchen Vieles ist zerstört aber man erahnt die unglaublichen Dimension der Anlage. Ich bin nicht sehr interessiert in Architektur, aber in Geschichte. Ein Führer ist nötig, wenn man viel erfahrne will, da es nur wenig Schilder gibt. Für Historiker und Archäologen ein muss! Der Rest eh wenig spektakulär.

since there was bad wheather in hoi an I decided to visit my son with one of the early trips. I did the combination with a boat trip and visiting a wharf when returning. the pick up worked very well, the guide was lovely, very competent and friendly and his english was very good. the included breakfast was not what it was promised as it consisted just of an egg sandwich and water, no coffee. but fine, that's not important!my son has a great atmosphere and is situated in the middle of the jungle. you feel like you just discovered it when you go there in the early morning with no other tourist at the site. there was a mysterious fog in the jungle which made the scenery look unreal and very adventurous. however, the site is very small. on the map it seems like there's many different groups of temples but there's only two left and all the others where destroyed in the war. of the two ones left one is just a ruin without any decoration, but it's situated on a hill which is very nice. the other one is decorated slightly with reliefs and some of the buildings were restored very badly because there was all information of its former decoration missing. I've been to angkor wat before which is of course the most impressive pagoda in a very good state and it's not comparable to my son. however, my son is very ancient being built in the 4th century which is, of course, very interesting. going back by boat was a loss of time cause there's nothing to see on the river and also the short visit of the kam island is not very interesting. there's some carpenters doing great sculptures bit if you're interested you'd rather go by ferry from hoi an. the price for the trip was 15$ including the bus, the guide, the breakfast and the boat, but NOT including the ticket to my son which is another 7$. so I think it's not really cheap and you could also rent a motorbike for 4$ the day before and go there very early. so, summing up, if you didn't visit angkor wat you should take the few hours and go there!

Contratamos la excursion desde Hoi An y volvimos en barco. NO LO ACONSEJO! El grupo era enorme, perdimos mucho tiempo. Yo vendría en un grupo mas pequeño, eso si, recomiendo guía en My Son, ya que el sitio esta muy afectado y sin explicación te quedas indiferente.

Arrangements made in town to do $11 return bus tour including entrance. Overall the bus trip was enjoyable but a little chaotic and arrived back later than expected. The My Son ruins are just that and cover a small area and would recommend a guide to get the most from the attraction. Watch the weather as misty rains in the hills may hamper your photography. The place overrun by tourists so I would suggest private tour or get your own bike so that you arrive late morning after the first set of buses leave and control your own timetable.Thanks for the vote if you found this helpful.

Mon avis est certainement biaisé car ayant déjà visité les temples d'Angkor, j'ai été relativement déçue par ce site. La guerre étant passée par là, il ne reste quasiment rien et le peu qui reste est parfois entièrement recouvert de végétation, si bien que l'on passe à côté de montagnes sans se rendre compte qu'il s'agit d'une partie du site. Les complexes sont très mal indiqués, la carte pour se repérer est très mal faite et il y a peu d'informations disponibles pour les touristes sans guides.Néanmoins, mon amie et moi y sommes allées en scooter depuis Da Nang (compter environ 1h30 - 2h) et la balade est plus que sympatique.

Aufgrund des schlechten Wetters in Hoi An entschlossen wir uns zu einem Ausflug nach My Son. Der Trip war ganz ok, aber eigtl lohnt es sich nicht dafür 3/4 eines Tages zu opfern. Wir saßen 1 1/2h mit 18 Mann zusammengequetscht im Minibus, die anschließende Bootsfahrt war genauso überfüllt und bot nicht jedem Passagier einen Sitzplatz - den vegetarischen Snack ließen wir aufgrund der gesichteten großen Kakerlaken auch weg.Das positivste war unser gut englisch sprechender Tourguide und die tollen, wenn auch wenigen, Überreste einer alten Zivilisation.
ガイドがいれば別ですが、ダナンの博物館を訪れてから行く方がいいと思います。歴史に興味があれば、行くといいと思います。ホイアン市内から車で1時間くらいでした。ホテルでカーチャーターを頼みました。半日のコースで1000000ドンでした。見学を終えて車に戻ると、冷たいおしぼりとミネラルウォーターを出してくれました。

Be prepared to walk a lot, and stay hydrated when visiting this place. Good for history buff to visit as it is a UNESCO heritage site. We did it in 2 hours, not including travelling time. Good to couple this visit with Hoi An on same day.
ホイアンから一時間半くらいかかりました。チャンパ王国の遺跡。ヒンドゥー教の遺跡でアンコールワットなどのカンボジアの遺跡と似てましたが、規模は小さめなのとベトナム戦争の激戦地だったためかなり破壊されていました。ただ見て回るだけだとわからなかったと思うので、ガイドがいて良かったです。ホイアンのホテルで英語のガイド付きのツアーを7ドルで申し込みました。自力で申し込むと5ドルくらいだと思います。

Did a half day tour to My Son with a return boat trip. Easy to explore but my advice would be to go with a guide as there are very little guidance boards to provide explanations. Site itself was just okayish, its history is probably more significant. Boat trip home was a disaster as it was cold and raining with nothing much to see. Our guide wasn't very good either which marred the whole tour. Don't take the boat trip option return if it's rainy!

1 to 2 hours is all the time you'll need for My Son - it is really small with the structures clustered in 5 groups. Signages are poor and you only get to read a brief background of the conservation work that is being done to each structure. There's nothing substantial about the Champa civilisation that built the buildings.Entry costs VND 100,000 per person. Children get in free and their definition of what constitutes "children" is very relaxed so I'm happy for that as my 10 year old got in free.We paid USD 30 for a private car to take us in. It's an hour's drive there, 2 hours there and an hour back. Good for a half day tour.
ホイアンから現地ツアーで行きました。思っていたよりも小規模な遺跡でした。野原に放置されているかのようで、整備されていない感じです。連れていってくれるだけではなく、チャンパの文化、宗教など、しっかり解説できるガイドさんに当たらないと、全体に物足りないかもしれません。

We Booked a very inexpensive trip over our Hotel Prince. Good value for price. My Son was ok. We really wanted to see it, because of all the photographs, but it was not that exciting. If you have a lot of time and/or are overly interested in ruins, do it. If you don't have that much time -it's ok to skip.In any case if you do it pick the trip at 5 o'clock in the morning -it sounds insane to wake up that early, but trust me: really insane is the heat if you leave two hours later !
lugar en ruinas total,apenas queda ninguna torre ni pared en pie! si has estado en Camboya o Tailandia no pierdas tu tiempo aqui.
Si ya has estado en Ayuttaya(Tailandia) o en los templos de Angkor(Camboya)ni te molestes en parar aqui.El sitio esta demasiado ruinoso,apenas queda nada en pie.

We booked the tour through our hotel. We opted for the sunrise tour as it will be cooler and less crowds. But the morning rained and sunrise was not visible. Otherwise the view will be fantastic. The van picked us up at our hotel at 5am with sandwiches and water provided. Our hotel also packed breakfast for us. The sanctuary has restoration work going on but still manage to see some nice structures. Go prepared with umbrellas and mozzie repellent. We were back to our hotel at 9.30am whilst some opted to return via boat which will end at 11.30am. Overall it was a cool and wet morning experience. I will recommend this tour to fill up your time in Danang or Hoi An.
אתר הסטורי די משעמםהאתר כולו נהרס בזמן המלחמהאם יש דברים אחרים לעשות - עדיףזמן הנסיעה פחות שעה מהויאן

Actually, it is a cultural and historic destination which can attract those who want to  study and get experiences in culture and history of the Vietnamese. I came here with my friends and I felt so amazing at the ancient architect of many buildings left. It is really worth the name " The World Cultural Heritage".

My Son is a little bit like another Unesco World Heritage site I know: Joya de Ceren in El Salvador. Meaning it´s very important historically, but it´s small and underwhelming. The whole place takes approximately 40 minutes to visit, and it cannot stand the comparison to Angkor Wat, on any criteria. I would advice visiting My Son only if you haven´t been to Angkor Wat yet, or if you have time to kill in Hoi An.

A lot of walk and history. Many stories behind every stone. Our guide Vinh gave us nice explanation. It was very hot and tried to find a place to hide from the sun. Many visitors with a nice traditional dance show. Other shops to buy gifts, hats and soft drink. Be prepared for the walk.

Having visited Bagan and Siem Reap I can't help but feel disappointed. Most of the temples are in ruins or covered in grass (which gives it a very nice look but covers most of them so there's no much left to see, so I used my imagination). They also give some traditional dance representations which makes the whole thing even more touristic and less atmospheric...Had I not visited Bagan and Siem Reap before I would have probably been more enthusiastic as the history behind this site is really interesting. My Son is located 40km outside of Hoi An. You can go by bus (which I understand is quite long, by scooter or by car (it took us a little over an hour and cost us $30 excluding entry fee which is priced 100k VND). I would suggest going either very early in the morning or late in the afternoon to beat the crowds (and the heat). And bring insect repellent, I got my worst mosquito bites there ;)

Ruinerna var roliga att se och området helt fantastiskt men vi blev lurade av tjejen i receptionen som vi köpte våra biljetter av. Sade att vi gett henne två tior fast vi hade gett henne två hundra (dong). Riktigt tråkigt då vi visste att vi blev lurade men inte kunde bevisa det! Förstörde tyvärr dagen lite för både mig och min partner.

Ayant était à ayutthaya,  sukhothai et Angkor,  je trouve que le prix n'en vaux pas le coup...  Arrêter de prendre les touristes pour des grosses atm et considéré nous un peu plus comme des personnes. On sera plus relax et on achètera des trucs de NOUS MÊME.

Waren ohne Guide hier, als Zwischenstopp vom Hotel zum Flughafen. Die Karten und Infos vor Ort sind spärlich bis nicht vorhanden und folglich waren wir in 1h locker durch und haben im Internet noch etwas recherchiert.

If you are older or unfit be ware there is a bit of walking involved.  There is still a lot of work to be done at this site as far as restoration goes. I think more added value could be had by handing out information and history on a pamphlet on admission. You can pay to get a private guide once you are there however the day we went there was none available. Worth a look if you have a guide. I always like to know what I am looking at and the history behind it.

Wer in Hoi An schon alles erledigt hat kann sich gern auf diese günstige Tour machen. Ich habe eine Tour mit dem Bus gebucht, der einen um 8:00 vom Hotel abholt.Für nur ca. 6 Euro wird man nach My Son gefahren, bekommt eine (in unserem Fall sehr unterhaltsame (der Guide war ein Comedian)) Führung, kann mit dem Boot zurück fahren (auf dem es auch einen kleinen Imbiss gibt) und bekommt noch einen kleinen Zwischenstopp auf einer Fischer Insel geboten, wo Handwerk und Bootsbau zu besichtigen sind. Zurück um 15:00 an der Anlegestelle in der Altstadt, bekommt man noch ein Wasser in die Hand gedrückt im Austausch mit dem Trinkgeld für den Guide.

Midden in de jungle ligt dit tempelcomplex uit de vierde eeuw. indrukwekkend en verdrietig tegelijk. De oorlog heeft ook hier huisgehouden. Er is een dansshow van champa cultuur en een restaurant. er zijn toiletten maar neem zelf wc papier mee. Het is er niet want wordt anders gestolen... aldus de dame die er over ging.

The group tour was over 30 people the guide had little communication to all the group. He was not in control this was highlighted when the weather changed quickly we were on a boat for lunch not offered life jackets or any idea of how long we had on the boat. We were all down stairs the water leaking thou the roof. The guide was looking very panicky. Slow trip due to o conditions was very glad when we got ashore. No meal on the boat as per iterinary. Then no pick up to lunch base or hotel. Left to get to meeting place as per go Asia details for a lovely lunch, thank you. Then a wonder back in the pairing rain to hotel with flooded streets.
Die Anlage ist sehr interessant, dennoch von den Amerikanern zerstört wordenBesuch macht kaum Sin mit Kindern. Trotzdem interessant da man durch der Landschaft fährt
This is something to be appreciated, although it is nothing comparing to Angkor Wat or other similar sites in siem reap. Pity that most of them are ruined by bombs during war or weather.

Somewhat interesting if you're in the area but certainly not worth traveling for this. So wretchedly hot and you have to walk to get to the ruins. Just not that interesting. They have tried to add some excitement with a dance show. Was just sad and kinda gross.

I'd give it a pass if you are short on time and have been to Angkor Wat and some of the other temples in Cambodia.  Interesting but Hoi An is lovely and there are plenty of other things to do in the area so I would rather spend the time cycling in the area or going to some of the islands.

If you have been to Angkor Wat before this site will not amaze you. It is very destroyed by the Vietnam war. The climate ist very hot, when I was there it was 37 degrees...in the sun it was impossible to stay and look at the buildings. A lot of tourists. I would advise to take a guide to get to know stuff about the ruins.
車で山に向かって走ると、博物館、資料館が整備されてる。車を駐車場に止めて更に10分程歩いて行くとミーソン遺跡群が有る。自然のままの状態の物有り、修復中の物も有るが、歩きながら見て廻るので水分は必需品ですね！

We thought it was gonna be like a mini Angkor, but obviously our expectations were really high. The best thing is the trip itself, you should do it from Danang or from Ho Ian (where we started) by motorbike to feel the real vietnamese experience.
ツアーで行くなら問題ないが、個人で行こうとすると、結構面倒。バスは外国人となると吹っかけて、通常の３倍は取られる（１人１０万ドンと言われるが、粘って３人で２０万ドンにした）。バスの終点からはバイクタクシーになるが、これも往復で１０万ドンかかった。つまり、３人なら、ダナンからタクシーを貸し切りにしてもほとんど変わらなかった。遺跡は、規模が小さく、１時間ちょっとでひと回り出来てしまう感じ。

Marvel at the amazing architectural feat of My Son. You will see intricate stone carvings and stones joined without mortar in these ruins. You'd do well to have an guide there to explain what you're seeing.

So we got the $5 tour. We were picked up by a huge bus, and then driven around Hoi An for about a half hour picking up various people. We then set off on the rather pretty drive out of Hoi An to My Son. The initial sight which meets your eyes of the temple is breathtakingly beautiful, but sadly it peaks early, and the rest just doesn't compare. Especially as the sun beats mercilessly down upon your poor Caucasian skin. The sunrise trip may have been preferable, if just to escape the kiln which My Son becomes. If I'm honest I was overjoyed to get back in the bus for the return journey. It probably didn't help that our guide had not only a lack of knowledge of the site but one of the weakest grasps of the English language that I have yet come across. Lovely grin though.

The sanctuary is an interesting place to go, it has nice surroundings. We went on a tour which included a boat trip back to Hoi An.  Unfortunately the tour guide made this akward stop in a souvenirs shop in the middle of nowhere to compell tourists to buy stuff from locals. It was 1 pm and people had not eaten since 8 am. Be carefull with organized tours sold in the city and in the hotels!
Nice half day trip to My Son from Danang.  Toured the ancient structures and artifacts.  Go at your own pace.

Overall interesting tour. As with most Vietnam tours there are some safety issues that need to be addressed. Some bridges that are over 2m drops have no balustrading causing possible falls for unaware children. Lighting inside monument is lacking. My wife had a centipede (20cm) crawl up her leg. As we could not see that there was one present.

This would have once been a must see but time and war have taken their toll. Not much left to see, but still of interest. We found the drive from Da Nang more interesting than My Son as we got to see the small villages and farmlands along the way. If you only have a few days in the area it's probably not worth visiting.

Anda bisa pergi kesini dengan ikut tur murah dari Hoi An. Anda akan dikumpulkan dalam satu bus besar kemudian pergi ke tempat ini. Kawasan candi sangatlah kecil akan tetapi candi ini merupakan situs warisan dunia sehingga layak dikunjungi

Premettendo che la visita a My Sòn è stata anticipata da qualcosa di unico ed immenso come Angkor Wat, il sito è piuttosto piccolo e rimane ben poco dopo il bombardamento americano, ma vale comunque la pena se siete nei paraggi.

Having visited Angkor Wat, My Son was a little underwhelming. Having said that we selected a tour Barri Ann Travel package which involved a bus to the sanctuary, a bus half way back, and a boat the rest of the way. It was quite economical and worked quite well.We found My Son interesting but very hot. We wouldn't have missed it but it was not spectacular.

猛暑の日、ホイアンから半日バスツアー（ガイド付きのグループツアー）に参加して行ってきました。朝の8時にホテルにピックアップに来てもらい、約1時間半で到着、ミーソン遺跡を2時間ほど見学してから帰途につき、ホテルに13時半頃戻りました。大人1人8US＄でした。帰りはボートを使うオプションもありましたが、あまりにも暑かったので、涼しいバスにしました。遺跡はいくつかのグループに分かれて点在していましたが、ガイドさんの案内でスムーズでした。3〜4のグループを回りましたが、暑すぎて、2時間ほどが限界でした。戦争の爪あとが痛ましい姿でしたが、価値ある遺跡だと思います。あまり期待せずに半日なら…と軽い気持ちで行きましたが、行って良かったです。

D'abord, le site est à 2 kms du parking; Et pour mes parents de plus de 75 ans, c'était impossible à faire. Heureusement, en payant, le gardien les a emmené en scooter ! Haha Tout est possible dans ce pays.Le site est très fréquenté et il est difficile d'y faire une photo sans personne. Peu de chose à voir en fait tellement les bombardements des américains ont tout détruit.Mais suffisamment quand même pour avoir un aperçu de la civilisation Cham.L'entrée n'est pas donnée par rapport aux autres (100000 dg je crois)
It was a hot weather when we visit this sanctuary, but because of sunny day, our photos turn out extremely sharp and nice. Is a small sanctuary,

My Son Santuary is a UNESCO heritage site , must go in the morning and put on your walking shoes coz you need to do a lot of walking . During August the weather is so hot pls bring your hat & small tower along with you . On the way back we drop by the carpentervillage, nothing much there you can skip it.
ものすごく辺鄙なところにあります。チケット販売時間が16時半まで、ということなのですが、タクシーの運転手が道を知らなかったらしく、道行く人に道を確認すること8度、何度か道を間違え結局残り3㎞というところで16時半を過ぎてしまい、泣く泣く引き返しました。

I know it sounded ambitious, our family booked a tour to cover My Son Sanctuary and Hoi An ancient town all in one day.  It's 2-hour drive from Da Nang and along the way, you will be spoiled by the gorgeous view of paddy fields.  As mentioned by other reviewers, if you have been to Angkor Wat in Cambodia, this this not going to impress you much.  However, it's still refreshing to learn that it's possible for the people during that early civilization to build this monumental site of this scale during that time.After the vehicle is parked at the designated area, we still have to walk to the site on foot for 20 minutes to reach a hall where the show takes place and then continue for another 5 minutes to reach the site.  We went there in the morning and there's slight drizzle of morning shower which was good.  It's good to carry along an umbrella or wear a non la (palm-leaf conical hat) like the locals.  I wouldn't say that this place wowed us but if you are in Da Nang, it's definitely worth a visit.Top Tips:1. This heritage site has not much shade and the weather can be scorching hot in the afternoon.  Be sure to go there early in the morning and try to catch the show which starts at 9:30  am2. If you are bringing young children along, be prepared to piggyback him/her instead of using a stroller as some stretches of the pathway to the site are not properly paved.

Go for the show! The site in itself is not so impressive if you've already visited Angkor in Siem Riep.There a show at 9: 30 , 10:30 and so on. The site is quite small and at first sight not so protected.Quite busy after 9am. Go early if you want to avoid the crowd and the heat.

Pour reprendre certains avis, cela n'a strictement rien à voir avec Angkor Vat !!!Cependant le site en pleine jungle est agréable, il faut beaucoup d'imagination pour imaginer ce qu'était originellement ce lieu et les moyens manquent pour la restauration. La tentative de reconstituer avec des briques neuves un temple ancien est le comble du mauvais goût.

Wenn man gerade ein paar Tage in Hoi An ist (z.B. zum Schneidern), dann kann man den sehr günstigen Tagesausflug dorthin schon machen (100.000 VND / 150.000 mit Bootsrückfahrt und Lunch) - aber die Ruinen sind durch den Vietnamkrieg ziemlich zerstört und wenn man gerade eret Angkor Wat besucht hat, leider auch im Vergleich ziemlich wenig beeindruckend.

As many reviewers have noted, these ruins aren't much if you've spent time in and around Siem Reap, Cambodia. However, that's not the point: the central part of Viet Nam was Cham territory for centuries, and these ruins constitute the largest grouping left in VN. Many of them are quite beautiful, and the entire set can easily be seen in 45-60 minutes. That means this is an easy 1/2 day trip out of Hoi An--60-75 minutes each way by tour bus. There are a few bomb craters from the war, which will partially explain the condition of 1 ruin, but there are lots of bomb craters in central and southern VN. It is good, in my mind, to have occasional reminders of the devastation left by that conflict.The walk from parking lot to ruins is an easy 10 minutes, and the valley itself is quiet and peaceful even though crowds can be large, especially around the main group of ruins. I have been here many times, and to Angkor Wat and its associated ruins many times, and I am lucky not to have had to choose. It is an unfair comparison, so if you are in Hoi An and have 3-4 hours to spare, make the small investment of time and money to learn a bit more about VN's history. Refreshments are available near the parking area, as are toilets.
伝統のダンスを見ることができます。ダンス終了後、一斉に観光客が遺跡へと移動するので直後はどこで写真をとっても人がたくさん写ってしまいます。時間をずらして遺跡に行ったほうがよいです。しかしツアーで参加すると一緒に回らなくてはいけないのでずらすことができないと思うので、個人手配で行くべきだと思いました。

We hired a private car from Hanoi Transfer Service to take us from Hoi An to My Son then to Danang (2 legs trip) for 45USD. The ride was comfortable, just a little over an hour. We arrived at 1:30pm as others suggested and the site was pretty quiet since most tourist buses from the morning have already left. It was hot during mid day so make sure you are hydrated or get under a tree for some shades. Wear comfortable shoes because you will be walking a lot but it's not a difficult hike. We didn't stay for the cultural show not hire a guide. Entrance fee is 100,000 Dong per person. Worth going if you are in the area.
Ikke veldig interessant. Lite av templene tilbake. Dessverre ødelagt av bomber fra Amerika krigene! Jeg ville ikke brukt mye tid på dette!

Okay, ik ben bevooroordeeld. We hebben Angkor Wat gezien en dan valt dit tegen. Veel meer dan wat ruïnes is er niet te zien. Toch begrijp ik wel waarom het een Unesco site is. Dit complex is uniek voor Vietnam. Ook een verzachtende factor is dat het meerendeel is gebombardeerd gedurende de oorlog door de Amerikanen.

O Santuário: são os restos de uma antiga cidade imperial do reino de Champa, de grande importância dos séc. IV a XIII. Grande parte das construções foi lamentavelmente destruída por bombas americanas durante a guerra. O conjunto arqueológico é interessante e bonito. Vale a visita, só recomendo levar guarda sol!

Interesting Sanctuary, although, the enormous number of people made is make less impressive. People taking pictures in and outside the tempels. Had we know we would have gone much earlier, to avoid the crowd. Many tours arrive around 10- 10:30

Went on a half day trip to My Son. We were collected at 8am, the bus was old and rickety. Didn't arrive at My Son until 10am. The Sanctuary is incredible, very interesting history. Good bit of walking involved and it was quite muddy the day we were there so wear suitable shoes. We paid extra for a boat trip on the way back, not worth it. They drop you off on a island for half an hour for no reason. Nothing to do on the island except browse in a souvenir shop.I would recommend visiting My Son but maybe rent a car to go there instead.

I enjoyed walking through the sanctuary, however I did not really enjoy the guide.  While it is helpful for someone to explain things to you, the weather was a bit unbearable to stand and listen to a guide go on and on about the meanings of certain things. In my opinion, it doesn't really compare to some other temples around SE Asia.

体感温泉49度の中観光しました。ホイアンのツアー会社で180000ドンで予約、アンホイ島のホテルまでバイクのピックアップ付き遺跡の入場券は別に100000ドン支払い遺跡自体はまぁこんなもんかって感じでした。サンダルやスカートで参加してる人も何人か居ましたがパンツにスニーカーがオススメです。結構炎天下の中かなり歩きます。水と帽子は必須！帰りは途中下車してボートで川下りしながら帰る事も出来ますが、私は暑いのでエアコンの効いたバスで下車せずそのまま帰る事を選択しました。その方が早く帰れるし、この時期は暑さにやられます。トイレは、入り口におばちゃんがいて金額忘れましたが少額のドンを支払いました。便器の水は流れなかったです。

we went there during our 2D/1N bike tour from Hue to Hoi An. Unfortunately we arrived just before noon and it was way too hot. You need to walk a bit to get there and in this heat it was killing us. The guide on site was not the best, but more importantly the damage done during the was is massive. I can only imagine how beautiful it used to be.Seen many better things in Vietnam and other Asian countries, not really worth it.

yep ok site did not need 4 hours there though alas it was 39 plus heat so was taxing, no tolerance for tour guide or his dodgy bus driver. We met up with Coach ( originated from Hoi An) half way as we got bought in from Da Nang. Coach Driver was so bad we took the boat option back! As booked through hotel not sure who it was but coach has An Phu Travel on it and seemed to have mixed groups from different tours! no air con working no indicators and warning lights on! Passing a truck on a sharp bend into oncoming traffic did me in! ... and to laugh it off as oh we used to it..... get stuffed! Food on boat was warm to touch and horrific got given 1 bottle water all day ..at the end and it was hot.. Offered to buy a can of soft drink but not enough to go round!
If you have been or plan to go to Angkor Wat, maybe give it a miss, much smaller. Did the bus/boat option which was nice, we visited another town and saw some local people working with wood.

Went out here with a friend from Hoi An. The trip out was great but honestly the sanctuary itself was not that amazing. It was also quite expensive at 100,000 dong. I liked the fact they haven't gone overkill restoring the place but i think I spent about a grand total of 30 minutes exploring the ruins. I'd say it's worth the trip. But keep your expectations realistic.
It took 1h 30m from danang by taxi. Cost about 700,000 VND, and ticket was 100,000VND each one. there was something to see, but not much.

I really wanted to visit My Son while in Hoi An as the pictures looked incredible and I love exploring ruins. So, my husband and I rented a scooter and drove almost 2 hours each way. When we parked, it was another 10 minute walk to get to the ruins which were super tiny. It took us all of five or ten minutes to walk around and get all our photos...and most of the statues etc belonging to My Son had been removed and taken to a museum where you have to pay to get in. Not a huge deal but it felt like we were constantly nickeled and dimed our whole trip. There is an office near parking where you can pay for a guide to take you around the ruins. We opted to skip that but upon reaching the ruins there were no signs in English aside from a small one saying it was the My Son ruins so not exactly helpful. Was it beautiful? Yes. I'm not sure the ten minutes was worth the four hours of near-death driving along the road however. If you love ruins visit Ayutthaya in Thailand or, better yet,  Pompeii in Italy.

We had a half day tour to My Son Sanctuary that was once again organised by Hoi An Express. We were picked up promptly at 7:30am from the hotel in an airconditioned bus and our tour guide began his history lesson on the Cham people and the Hoi An area in general. Having been to Ankor Wat I was expecting a little more than what we saw and our guides' emphasis on the fertiilty aspect of the sanctuary became somewhat annoying - there's only so many times you can be told that the mountains and the sculptures resemble male and female organs before ite gets old. The boat ride to the pottery village was fun and the demonstration was entertaining for everyone. The kids enjoyed making their own vases to take home. We were more than happy to buy some of the pottery but was a bit disappointed at the inflated prices and the lack of bartering that could be done in the main shop. A tip - there were other pottery shops in the village where you could buy the same items for greatly reduced prices. My B-I-L paid a fortune for some cups and a tea set in the main shop only to find the same items in a second one at 50% of the price. I was also buy a tea set from the second shop at half price so ask your guide to let you visit them before leaving.

Sans aucune commune mesure avec les temples d'Angkor. Les temples sont fortement dégradés, il faut faire preuve d'imagination pour se les reprėsenter. Des travaux de restauration sont en cours.Si vous avez une heure de disponible pourquoi pas.

Admittedly our experience of the My Son Temple wasn't ideal as the day was unbelievable hot, so wandering around in the sun to look wasn't very pleasant.However the temples themselves were beautiful, though sadly they are severely damaged from the war. There was very little information available, so you really need a local guide to give you a greater understanding of what you see.There were food and toilet facilities available nearby.

We take a sunrise tour with 12 people for 9 $. We thougt we will be there on sunrise but ofcourse we saw it from the car.At the entrance you need to pay 5 $ that no one from the group knew about it.The place is nice but we saw much more impressive places in vietnamWe chose to came back to hoian with a  boat which was a big mistake.If you like ruins very much go there.

매우 더운 날씨에 걷는 것 외에 다른 교통수단이 없어 힘들었습니다.양산이나 모자가 필수입니다.전에 앙코르와트에 갔었는데,앙코르왓을 관람할 때는 뚝뚝이나 오토바이를 대절해서 둘러볼 수 있어 그렇게 힘들진 않았지만많은 유적이 월남전 때 손실되어 규모가 작은 미선 유적지는 걸어다닐 수 밖에 없어 더위 때문에 힘들었습니다.미선은 beautiful mountain, 곧 미산(美山)이라는 뜻이라더군요.전쟁에 상당부분 손실되어 안타깝습니다.참파 왕국의 유니크한 유적임에는 확실하지만,남아 있는 유적의 수가 적고, 몹시도 더워서^^;; 다시 방문하지는 않을 것 같습니다.

Disappointment at the appointment. We come from Yogyakarta, have already visited the temples of Siem Reap several times, are well acquainted with the styles of this Southeast Asian Hindu era. But here, the result is poor. The only group of temples worth a visit is full of tourists (good idea) transported by electric carts (but you have to wait for long periods in poorly organized queues). And since there is only one quality site, everyone rushes at the same time. So difficult to really appreciate. On the other hand, the other groups, “renovated” with the help of the Indian government are pitifully ridiculous. Archaeologists have discovered formless ruins and the engineers who imagined the reconstruction did so with their “gravy” because no document (on clay, paper, banana leaf or other element has survived, no testimony – such as that of Tcheou Ta-Kuan in Angkor – confirms the shape of the temples). So we have temples dating back to... 2023.  Honestly, it's nothing to fantasize about. Prambanan in Yogyakarta has more mouth. 2 hours drive back and forth for that.... I leave it to everyone to decide. Will I go back?: NO!

My Son was interesting but only opinion, over rated. Took the tour from Hoi An and there was a lengthy travel time. At the end you take a boat back to old town while given a bahn mi to eat. Ba Na Hills much better.

Unless you have a strong interest in archaeology or a connection to Hinduism, I wouldn’t bother coming to My Son Sanctuary. While it is a privilege to visit a UNESCO World Heritage Site, I lacked the knowledge to enjoy the experience. It was also out of the way and extremely hot. I query why it is always recommended. For most travelers, I think it’s one of those places you would regret not visiting but regret visiting more.
There is not much to see except a few brick ruins. The dance and music show were tacky. Overall, it was a boring site. I suggest you give it a miss.

We wouldn't take the 1.5-hour journey for it again. Despite private guide not really worth seeing, Hoi An offers much more if you are only short and round trip. Unfortunately, a great deal was destroyed by the war. Overpopulated the grounds with bus tourists.
Just a few sets of non-amazing ruins … disappointing to say the least.Nothing special at all - probably least interesting thing in all of VN

We booked this tour through our hotel, Laluna spa and resort. The tour was over priced, but this was a landmark that we wanted to explore so we paid the high rate any way. The tour company filled the transport van to the max, making for a very uncomfortable 1hr ride from Hoi An to the Sanctuary. The guide for our trip spoke very limited English and did not seem to know very much about the ruins which was quite disappointing. Overall, the tour we booked was very disappointing.

We spent around two hour here, the site is pretty small and a lot of the ruins were unfortunately destroyed in the war. However, there are several impressive sites still standing which were interesting to walk around. We finished off with the dance show which is actually worth it (I admit was sceptical) with cool music and choreography. HOWEVER, please note that you have to be REALLY CAREFUL when buying a ticket. We gave a 500k bill for two tickets but I was distracted for a second, so when expecting change, she claimed I had given a 20k note. These look similar so at the time I accepted mistake. I realised later this was impossible so confronted her on the way out. She instantly handed me back the 500k with a really sheepish look, saying “you sure? You sure?” complete chancer!! STRONGLY RECOMMEND YOU BRING CORRECT NOTES!! Also you can get free parking at nearby cafe, which is really reasonably priced given it’s right outside massive tourist site. Recommended!

As many others have indicated this site is mostly ruins. Not really that much to see. The day we went the heat was oppressive and there is very limited shade around the ruins. Also beware that it can extremely crowded when the bus loads of tourists arrive. If you plan to watch the performance show get there early and get a seat in the shade near a fan you'll need it! I don't think it's worth the trip out unless you are really into this sort of thing.
The ruins are beautiful,however not worth the price paid for the tour.Took an hour to visit it all!
I enjoy historical sites, but I didn't enjoy this one. Every site is quite small and boring, and the plot is large.
雨で途中の道が水没し、遺跡までたどり着けませんでした。チケットを買ったところで払い戻しが可能でした。雨季のときはカッパか傘は絶対持って行ったほうがいいです。ダナンのホテルからは約一時間くらいで、ホテルの人にお願いして車をチャーターしました。10万ドンぐらいでした。

Niestety wojna zamieniła tą świątynię w ruiny. Może w przeszłości była dużą atrakcją lecz teraz moim zdaniem to miejsce można pominąć a porównywanie go do Angkor Wat jest pod każdym względem niezrozumiałe.
真心失望，看到殘破倒塌的建築物，完全無法辨認，不識欣賞沒什麼好看。好彩跟團，有導遊講解，先清楚一些。建議報名晨光團，去到無噤熱。
6月底去的，还专门报了车，开了快两个小时。然鹅，非常非常失望的景点。景点保护的很差，看到的都是坏砖头而已 。

I was disappointed and felt it was a long way out of Hoi An for little enjoyment once thereThe Temple ruins were average at best and very hotNot for kids which is understandable but I wouldn’t recommend for couples either
這個是越南其中一個文化遺產，顯示了以前的神殿等等，但去到只能看到一堆殘破倒塌的建築物，完全無法辨認，沒什麼好看。

Sicuramente non ci aspettavamo nulla di paragonabile ai templi di Angkor ma il tour è stato un po'deludente nonostante le basse aspettative.Avremmo voluto andare in autonomia ma i prezzi dei Tour sono altamente competitivi rispetto ad un Taxi privato...e così ci siamo affidati al ma hotel che ci ha organizzato il tutto a circa 8 dollari a testa più ingresso.È stato sicuramente meglio avere una guida almeno per avere qualche informazione e rendere la visita più Interessante.I templi sono davvero pochi e nemmeno il contesto ci ha entusiasmato.Abbiamo scelto il tour al mattino presto per evitare i grupponi dei viaggi organizzati, effettivamente in tutto il sito eravamo davvero in pochi.A chi ha visto Angkor non consiglio questa visita.

La ruinas en si,merece mucho la pena ver,pero la cantidad de basura que ibas viendo en los alrededores da mucha pena,botellas de agua,bolsas de plástico...Me dio la sensación de que a nadie le importa que se estropeen esas ruinas y salí más enfadada que encantada.Tienen que hacer algo con la basura ya!!!!

The temple complex was built by the Cham people in the 12th century BCE, and dedicated it to the Hindu god Shiva. It's remarkable that Hinduism had made it all the way to the Central Coast of Vietnam from India. But unfortunately, the site is in great disrepair. Made of clay bricks, the temples and sculptures have seriously eroded. Many of the temples were destroyed by bombing during the War. Having seen the great temples of Angkor in Cambodia, this site cannot come close in comparison.

The drive to the temple was quite nice and the walk to the temple complex was quite pleasant but the actual ruins was quite a dissapointment. The temple building itself was small in comparison with complex at Angkor Wat or other such places in South India. To me it was a disappointment. The old market was the same as in Hanoi or Hue.

This is a very disappointing site. Could be worst if you don’t have a guide to explain about the Cham history. Thank goodness my guide is a funny person to keep me excited through tour the journey. If you are on the rush in the city, please spend your half day to some other activities. Nothing much to see over there.

Fuímos con un tour guiado des de Hoi An hasta My son. Tuvimos un guía que era complicado entender su inglés, además que aparte del tour dentro del bus te hacen comprar el ticket que vale 150.000VND. Una vez allí, los templos y todo esta muy mal cuidado.Recomendación: visita rápida y a poder ser sin tour.

Vi bookede en tur til My Son fra vores hostel. Vi troede bare vi havde købt busbilletterne, men det viste sig, at vi skulle med på den helt store turist-tur. Eller i hvert fald ifølge guiden. Han navngav os "Blue Team" og gik forrest med et rødt flag - ja, så turistet var vi lige pludselig blevet.. Vejret var yderst godt den dag - faktisk så varmt, at man ikke kunne andet end at stå i skyggen, hvilket heller ikke var med til at gøre oplevelsen bedre. Men vi fik nogle gode billeder og templerne (eller resterne) var flotte.

シンーツーリストで現地予約をして、英語通訳のツアーに申し込みました。格安ツアーで１人１５９，０００ドンでした。大型バスで宿泊先を回ってホイアンから１時間ほどで着きました。ツアーなので団体行動です。バス車内で入場料を徴取され代わりにツアー会社の名刺のようなカードを渡されます。これが入場券の代わりでした。英語での説明はよくわからず、適当に遺跡を見ていました。蒸し暑く、よくわからない遺跡を見るだけなので、つまらないツアーとなってしまいました。日本語ツアー参加していればもう少し遺跡の勉強ができたでしょう。

I wouldn't bother. Disappointing. Choose something else. Mostly piles of rubble. Sure it is old and has historical meaning but not much to see. A tough walk for anyone who cannot walk sufficiently or has a normal level of fitness.

As the title says, if you've visited Angkor or Ayutthaya then avoid My Son - it's like the bad movie made of your favourite book.However, if you HAVEN'T been to Angkor or Ayutthaya then this is a pleasent way to spend a couple of hours. It's approx an hour's drive from Hoi An. The organsiation of the site is very good, and even though it's not that large or impressive it IS worth seeing to appreciate the damage inflicted by US military during the Vietnam War (not a dig at the American's, just stating a fact) when the Vietnamese hid out amongst the temple. It's quite heartbreaking to see what has been done to a cultural heritage as a result of the conflicts of man.NOTE: If you go on a tour, try book a sunrise one. You leave about 5am and arrive there about 6am before it opens to the general public. There were about twenty people on my bus, and the only others were another party of about 15 who were about 15 minutes walk behind us. It's cooler, more peaceful, and the best chance to get photos without Selfie Sticks or those annoying people who just walk right in front of you as you snap a picture despite seeing you are about to take a shot. Also, websites advertise the entry fee as 100 000VND, but it's actually 150 000VND so make sure you take enough cash!
although beautiful i found this site somewhat boring. lots of steps same old same old & absolutely awful on a stinking hot day

The damage done by the bombings will take eternity to recover. I will say visit this place only if you have or intend to visit Angkor Wat.The huge contrast will make the experience more so interesting.

Ruïns are always nice, but my Son failed to impress me. We did the sunrise tour, which was good because there were No tourists. But overall you notice that this site still needs to be developed a lot before it can look amazing.

Hicimos un tour guiado desde Hoi An. El viaje es bastante largo, el grupo era inmenso, de hecho un solo guía tenía a cargo 2 buses con lo cual era imposible escucharlo. El santuario en sí no es muy grande y está en bastante mal estado de conservación. El lugar también estaba bastante lleno de turistas. Una pérdida de tiempo y dinero.

Not impressive to me. It is too hot and got to walk quite far distance on uneven surface. First, from the entrance to Buggie pick up point and again from the Buggie stop to the performance stage is another considerable distance to walk. Zero facilities to support for disability. Not able to rent a wheelchair. Cham dance is good but beside nothing much to see.
Very small compound and nothing much to see, especially if you've been to the Angkor Wat in Cambodia. But a good place to pass the time if you've been to the beach  and Ancient Town

There is transportation for a large part of the distance from the ticketing office to the site making it much easier to view the site.The site itself is only mildly interesting. I accept that this was not helped by our guide who seemed to make stuff up if he didn't know. However even if I put that aside the site was bombed during the war which left only half a dozen buildings able to be displayed.In my view what remains is not worth the effort of 50 minutes travel each way from Hoi An.
そもそもベトナム戦争時代に殆ど破壊されている為に、見るべき遺跡自体があまり残っていません。その上、修復作業をしているので、なんだか新しい部分が目立って遺跡なんだかレプリカなんだかわかんない状態で、わざわざ訪れるとがっかりです。

purtroppo rimangono solo della rovine di vari templi, il luogo è a pochi chilometri da Hoi An, si vedono anche crateri nel terreno provocati da bombe lanciate durante la guerra. Durante la visita sentivo dei suoni, infatti a fine tour c'è un locale dove si improvvisa uno spettacolino ad uso e consumo dei turisti, per non fare pentire della visita. Il nostro accompagnatore ci ha detto che quando moriva il sultano al tempo,  venivano bruciate con lui anche le concubine (in genere piu' di cento). Ho visto di molto meglio, non mi ha entusiasmato.
I thought My Son historic site would be on a comparative scale with Angkor Wat, or Macchu Picchu, but sadly, it was disappointing. Perhaps we would see major improvement in another 10 years.
Vc perde hs pra chegar la e o que tem pra ver nao vale a pena. Nao ha infrarstrutura alguma pata receber as pessoas, banheiros em pessimas condicoes.

I've read the reviews about "stunning, must see, amazing" place.It is really not much to see, several ruins that not much left of them. Maybe if are really into history it might be interesting in some way but for me it wasn't at all.Basically it just walk in the nature for around a half an hour that cost 150K dong just for the entrance and you need to add a transportation from Hoi An.For me the best part was just riding the bike through the villages and seeing the local life, but you can do it without going to My Son.
It really isn't worth going, it is in the middle of nowhere, takes about an hour to get to from Hoi An and works out very expensive.

Perhaps the fact that we just came from Cambodia and seen the Angkor temples or the very rainy day, but it wasn't very impressive to us... the place is well maintained, though and the show is quite entertaining

The drive there was nice great country scenery, but sadly the 3 of us went without a guide and there was no help on offer so as it was a hot day went to the first area the golf cart stops walked to a ruin had no idea about it thought it was all to difficult so we all left, came back to the hotel feeling like it was a wasted day

Book a full day tour here. Most of the day was taken up by picking up passengers. Our guide english was very poor and unfortunately was unable to communicate the importance of all these broken rocks. I picked up more by listening to the other guides. The museum on site is very poor and a lot could be done to improve the tourist experience.
People rude, no directions, no signs to tell you how far to walk to site, not much to see...very disappointing !!!

My Son was voor ons een leuke daguitstap in de voormiddag. We hadden dit geboekt via ons hotel maar achteraf beter op een andere manier gedaan. We betaalden 10 dollar voor bus-boot Tour. Niet de moeite. Kijk of je kan onderhandelen voor 6 dollar en bus heen en terug. Boek trouwens ook bij een Gids die goed Engels kan. My Son zelf was best indrukwekkend.

malheureusement il ne reste pas grand chose de cet endroit car les guerres et les vols sont passés par là . vous serez déçu si vous avez dans le passé découvert d'autres sites beaucoup mieux entretenu que celui ci.pour ceux qui n'ont jamais vu de ruine : vous trouverez cela intéressant....

We spend almost 3 hours watching boring ruines in the blazing heat.  The guide was hard to understand and the stories were not very interesting. Guides name was tin trying to guide a group of more than 25 people. With a soft voice and weak English skills

MySon wasn't really a highlight of our trip. There was an exorbitant amount of tourists and the ruins weren't what we were expecting. They are small and there isn't much information about them. It also gets really hot up there, we went up to 41 degrees Celsius. There is not a lot to see, definitely wouldn't recommend it. It felt more like a tourist trap.

The My Son temple complex in my travel guide was described as expired ruins that rise out of the green jungle and an as one of the most attractive historic sites of the country (often compared to more famous Anngkor-what complex in Cambodia).We got the advice to visit the site early in the morning and to take a taxi or book one of the many tours because of the poor signpost on the way to My Son. We hired two scooters for the total sum of USD 15,00 a day and used Citymaps2Go (app with detailed maps from around the world, WITHOUT the need of an internet connection) to help us navigate to the site.We found the ride to be more interesting than the site itself. A Jurassic Park like train brings you to the complex where from you walk on a concrete pathway to visit the different sites. Most of it was in a poor condition and badly maintained. We were in and out within the hour and felt more impressed knowing that the My Lai massacre had taken place only kilometers from the very same complex.

Самый неинтересный объект, из посещенных мной, за 2 недели путешествий по Вьетнаму. По большому счету - малоинтересные руины, приконченные в 70-е годы американскими бомбардировщиками. Да древние, древнее Ангор Вата и шри-ланкийской Поланорувы. Но... скучно и неинтересно.  Самая интересная часть программы прогулка на электромобилях от кассы до начала экспозиции.Входной билет 150 тыс. донгов.

Taking the advice from the Trip Advisor community, we visited My Son Sanctuary in the morning. We got the hotel to arrange transport for us, and we left the hotel at 06:30am. Got our tickets and took the electric car (after the bridge) into the site. We then took the anti-clockwise direction to the sites. We were there at about 08:30am and the sun was scorching hot. The heat was pretty unbearable even in the morning. There was only one other couple with us hence we could wander around freely. However due to the heat, our parents were mainly just sitting under the shade the whole time, uninterested throughout. Having said it, the ruins were quite a sight. Probably not a good idea to visit with older folks or younger kids.
Tout est dit dans le titre. C'est pas terrible et ça ne donne pas envie de s'y intéresser. Bref des ruines hindoues en brique. Rien de majestueux. Attention à la chaleur...

Kennt man solche Anlagen schon aus Indonesien, Indien oder ähnlichen Ländern ist es nur ein Haufen langweiliger Steine. Auf keinen Fall mit einer teuren Tour machen, Eintritt 150000 dong (2016), besser mit motoroller oder eigenem Fahrer.

We were there with friends after reviews on TA and others. It's like 3-4 groups of ruins but no special view, nothing spectacular. Out 4th agree that if we have again choice we've not decided to go there again. Waist of time.

This again been highly recommended as a "MUST SEE" sights in DA NANG! The reason is probably there are not many sights in DA NANG, and both the TOURIST BOARD and local tour companies are at their wits end of finding Tourist Dollars for their livings! All the local tour agents arranged one day trip to this place including a simple lunch (of plain rice and few strands of vegetables and a bottle of water)! The site have not much to offer , and many renovation with the help of THAI experts are taking place.

Lo que queda aun real de viejas épocas está en muy mal estado, sin mantenimiento y con las plantas comiéndose todo a su paso. Lo que se puede ver "bien" es porque está reconstruído, lo cual hace perder la esencia del lugar. No hay nada explicado, si no contratás un guía no entendés nada. Así y todo no nos pareció la gran cosa y la entrada es bastante cara.

My Son is an interesting attraction. For me, visiting an archeological site, information is key. In this case we hired an official personal guide for 100000 dhong. She was supposed to speak English, but didn't succeed in making herself understandable. When we decided to then Google our way through the sites, we had an awfully hard and awkward time getting rid of her. In the end, when threatening to talk to her manager, she finally left and even gave us a refund. We then continued the tour by ourselves. Too bad the signs and displays providing information about the sites werent much more inspiring either.
Si vous avez vraiment du temps devant vous mais sinon moi j'ai été déçu de bloquer une journée pour ça ... Quelques monument mais rien exceptionnel et les rénovations sont affreuses
I was surprised what I saw and was not amazed.  Our tour guide was not speaking well English and didn't have good explanation about the temples neither the story! Disappointed

The journey is the destination is over used but certainly our easy riding with Henry for 2 hours each way thru the byways was the memory we will hold. The site itself is very unkempt & as many tours go there then some lipstick would be sensible. Maybe we are missing something but it was disappointing & a yawn. We are very pleased we were on the back as Zen & the motorcycle. Mind you the experience is a roller coaster & you mentally review your life insurance

This should not have been declared a World Heritage site.  The temples pale in comparison to Angor Wat in Cambodia.  Most of what is here has been reconstructed with new bricks.  Very few relics or carvings.

Before visiting the My Son archeological site, I had already Visiten Ankor Wat in Cambodia. So expectations were high. Unfortunately the temples at My Son had suffered so many damages during the war, so that they are hardly recognizable as temples. It looks more like piles of bricks.Unless you are interested in archeology, don't go!

Was with a friend when I visited. Quite a nice place though not really humongous as I have imagined, but something you can be proud of. Sadly, the authorities are not serious with their jobs their even maintenance and preservation. Can't believe some staff sleeping on hamoks infront of you. Can't beleive they are singing loud karaoke that can be heard around the sanctuary. Can't believe to see ladies practicing dance with Britney Spears' Hit me baby one more time....on stage....a disbelief! and all of us tourists who witnessed where so sad and badly surprised. Can't say anything more.

We booked a tour via our hotel and departed at 5am for the sunrise in My Son tour from Hoi An. The one way commute is roughly 1 hour. The sun was up in the air by the time we got to My son and we were the first one there.You will need a guide to understand the history and stories behind this Unesco site. Unfortunately, I still don't find this to be as interesting and beautiful as ankor wat. I'm kinda glad we did this really early in the morning coz the sun was very strong already at 9am.
הביקור בהריסות היה חביב.אם יש לכם דברים אחרים לעשות באזור הוי-אן, אפשר לוותר על הנסיעה ל my son, במיוחד אם ביקרתם באנגקור ואט שבקמבודיה.
Es una visita de un complejo de ruinas . Si tienes tiempo hazlo . Hace mucha calor despues de las 10.00 hs . A mi personalmente no me ha impresionado  mucho .
If you have a day to kill so maybe go visit my son. If not, don't bother to come. The structures aren't preserve very good, and most of them ruined. The bus to there was very uncomfortable too.

Đi một quảng đường rất xa đến đây, chúng tôi đi từ ngạc nhiên tới thất vọng. Qui mô và vẻ đẹp của khu thánh địa không có chút gì có thể thuyết phục chúng tôi về những mỹ từ mà bao người đã dành cho nó. Nói chung chỉ là tạm được, và tôi nghĩ nhiều tháp ở quanh vùng Cam Ranh, Ninh Thuận còn đẹp hơn nhiều.
まだまだ未整備の個所もありました。また説明書きがあったのかなかったのか？何の遺跡かもわからず、ブラブラしてました。貴重だと思われる遺物も、遺跡をちょっと奥に入ると散乱していて、目下研究中といったところです。お客さんは非常に多かったですが、これといっていいところはなかったです。

We booked a bus to get to My Son that turned out to be a guided tour. It seems to be the only way to visit My Son, so if like us you can't stand guided tours, do not go there.We also went quite late (11 AM) and the sight was overcrowded.About the sanctuary itself : it is well located but VERY small. There is not much to see and what can be seen is too busy. If you've already visited Angkor or Ayuthaya like we did, you'll be disappointed

If you have not visit Angkor Wat,My-son is a good place to visit for the cham culture. I had been to Angkor Wat triple times.So I have highly expectation of My-son holly land.I felt disappointed for its  worsening situation.Not conserve as well as Angkor Wat. It just only took us 1 hr to look around.The terrible  war damaged the kingdom and ruined almost everything.....I didn't see the apsara dance in time. There is 3 apsara dance show a day.If you want to see the dance show,check the show time before u depart.

Di tutto il viaggio in Vietnam, da nord a sud, questa è forse l'unica cosa ad averci deluso e anche parecchio. Non so dove siano stati quelli che lo descrivono come spettacolare e affascinante; a me è sembrato solo di perdere tempo e rimpiangevo amaramente di aver sottratto del tempo all'incantevole Hoi An per venire a visitare questo sito del tutto inutile, soprattutto se poi si visiteranno le meraviglie di pietra, quelle vere, della Cambogia. Secondo me non vale assolutamente la pena, mi ha lasciato del tutto indifferente e non mi ha per niente entusiasmato o fatto emozionare. Aggiungeteci il caldo micidiale e My Son è proprio da scartare, a mio modesto parere.

Als je bij Angkor Wat bent geweest en je komt hier, dan valt dit vies tegen. Een paar kleine hoopjes met steen en dat is het. Als je niet bij angkor wat bent geweest is het denk ik wel mooi om te bezoeken. Wij hebben een scooter gehuurd en vanaf hoi an naar my son gereden. Die tocht is prachtig en zeker een aanrader.
Het bijzondere aan dit tempelcomplex is dat het zo groot is, niet dat het zo mooi is, verre van. Veel ruïnes lijken op louter hoopjes stenen. Tegenvallend, want het was ons zo aangeraden...

We verbleven in Hoi An van hieruit een excursie geboekt met de bus naar my Son. We hadden een erg vervelende gids. Zeer slecht te verstaan en maakte continu flauwe grappen. Na 5 minuten hadden we dan ook de groep verlaten. Ik had het tempel complex groter verwacht. Met een uurtje heb je alles wel gezien. Eerder ben ik naar Angor wat geweest, vergeleken daarbij stelt dit niks voor.

no very very well kept. Similar of Ankor Wat in Cambodia, but smaller and very below average for information. People should know a bit more about this site, with more panels or professional tour guide if any.

Although semi interesting it is not worth the effort unless off course you are interested in this sort of history. They are ruins and you need a fair bit off imagination. Give them a miss and visit Ankor Wat in Cambodia. Having said all that I am sure there are people who like this kind of thing and if you are there and have a bit of time visit these ruins.
If you have recently been to Angkor Wat this is a big disappointment. We had a very annoying guide which didn't help.Beware it is a very long hot walk to the ruins from the car park.
Bof bof, une fois que vous avez vu les temples et ruines de la Thaïlande et les temples d'Angkor Wat au Cambodge cette visite n'est pas très intéressante mais plutôt décevante.

Sebbene sia in un bel contesto, sono rimasto un po' deluso da questo sito archeologico della civilta' Cham.  Templi mezzi diroccati e mal conservati che meriterebbero una maggior valorizzazione. Simpatiche le rappresentazioni che avvengono alle ore 9,30 10,30 e 14,30 della durata di circa una mezzoretta in un piccolo teatro aperto.
Lidt skuffende når man har set Ankor Vat i Cambodia. En meget lang gåtur gennem junglen. Tag nogle gode sko på og husk vand.

First off, the temples themselves are nice, though if you've already visited Angkor or Bagan I wouldn't bother.Problem is: the place is absolutely swamped with cheap bus-tours. Hordes of South Korean tourists armed with selfie-sticks and loads of huge groups tramping around.We actually made the mistake of booking this one through our hotel, and got stuck in one of the tourgroups because of that. That means 50 people to a guide, lots of waiting around combined with -strangely- a very rushed experience. If your even moderately interested in archeology, you need at least three hours. Oh, and forget about the (supposedly) very good museum near the entrance, with art and artifacts from the sites. Your tour won't go there.Finally, some tours offer a return by boat to Hoi An. Avoid this one, it's a complete farce. You''ll be bussed back to within 40 minutes boating from Hoi An. They'll happily oversell the tickets, so that the boat is dangerously crowded (not a floatation device in sight anywhere). And I do mean dangerously. At one point the guide came along asking people to spread out and not stand all on one side, since we were in danger of tipping over. Oh, and when it's full up, you'll just be made to sit on the roof in the blistering sun. The 'small lunch' that's included is plain filthy, and when you are finally in sight of Hoi An, and are hoping for the ordeal to end, you're forced to make a 'shopping-stop' on some island.This sort of thing is what gives Vietnam a bad name, and it's a totall rip-off.My advice: - Avoid the tours, rent your own car and guide. It's worth the extra money- Avoid the boat at all costs- Take a full day, and include the museum

I went here on a day trip from Hoi An. There's really not much to see. There are a few structures that are still intact and they don't have many interestings engravings or sculptures. The sculptures from the temples have been removed and are in the Cham Sculpture museum in Danang. The best part was the chance to learn a little about Cham Hinduism from the guide. The actual ruins are not interesting to look at.
It was a nice place called the holy ground. If u are not into history or tempels, is then notthing special.

sehr dürfig, was es hier zu sehen gibt: eine archäologische Anlage, die zunächst einiges verspricht, aber nicht erfüllt. Die Ausgrabungen bzw. Reste der Tempelanlagen sind sehr zerstört, man erahnt das es einmal ein schönes hinduistisch geprägtes Heiligtum war, nur wenn ich es auch nur ansatzweise mit hinduistischen Stätten in Indien vergleiche, ist es nicht der Mühe wert dort hinzufahren. Die Lage ist eingebettet in eine immergrüne Waldlanschaft, es könnte atmosphärisch ganz romantisch sein, wären da nicht die Massen an Gruppen.

We went to My Son Sanctuary from Hanoi after reading positive reviews about it and were actually dissapointed. I think if you are nearby then it is worth a look. But 2 hours ride and 100k fee for a 20-30 minuts walk in the ruins is too much. Anyway we enjoyed our trip there as a part of our "Exploring Vietnam" quest)))
We travelled to My Son from Danang and the drive took about 2 hours. At the end it wasn't that interesting. If you have been to Angkor in Cambodia then this will be quite boring.

I do not know the name of the tour i went on but it was booked through my hotel but i do not recommend it. The tour guide was informative and funny but do not go on the boat trip which is optional. This was a waste of time. The food was below average the boat did not offer and shade so we were all stuck downstairs where there was very little room. We were then taken to the local village and had to walk through the trinket stores which is the last thing on our minds in the heat. The actual ruins themselves are not very impressive and not much to see for the long drive up there.

If you've been to temples like Angkor Wat or Tical My Son will not impress you. Getting to the site is about an hour drive from Hoian and our tour guide didn't give us any great information that might have been worth the trek. Also, we signed up for the tour at sunrise thinking we could get great lighting for some pictures but the sunrise we saw was over a bridge and half an hour from our destination. For $12 (cost of transportation) plus a extra $5 to get into the park I would say save it and go to Hidden Beach in Hoian instead.

We took the group tour for $8, including lunch & boat. A little later, we found out that we should have paid $5. Some bad points:- The bus as well as the bus driver were probably the worst we've had in Vietnam;- The guide spoke very poor English, which turned My Son into a difficult to understand set of old rocks;- Foreigners have to pay almost double the entrance free that Vietnamese people pay;- That entrance fee was not included in the tour;- It was crowded and hot.However, the boat trip was nice and lunch was pretty okay. I would recommend to go earlier (really hot and crowded) and to listen to tour guides that walk around the place instead of taking the group tour.

If you have visited Angkor Wat, then this place is not to compare. If you really like history and archeology, avoid going in group tour and go there early in the morning. I arrived there almost at 11.00, the heat was so glaring and the carving on the relics was poor.

Ехали на мопедах с Хояна. Дорога заняла где то около 1 часа в одну сторону. Думали, что будут старинные храмы, а там старинные развалины, причем их очень мало, буквально несколько секций, которые мы обошли минут за 20. Ничего особо интересного не было, кроме дороги до храма и обратно. После путешествия остался загар мотоциклиста. Смотрелось очень прикольно.

Molto acclamato da tutti come meta principale di hoi an, ma sinceramente se uno ha viaggiato un po' il sud est asiatico, ha visitato luoghi molto più magici ed emozionanti!I siti sono piccoli, molti dei quali ricostruiti a causa delle bombe b52 americane!La storia del popolo che ci abitava é interessante, ma non abbastanza per farmici tornare!

I visited the my son with orginized trip from Hoi-an. Bus one way and the bus boat on the way back. Though it is realy cheap I takes more than a half a day. The My Son it self is very scenic but quite small and way to crowded. Tour guid will not forget to mention the american bombs every 5 min... You can drop from the group  when you arive at the parking and go your way makes it a bit better. On the way back we got stuffed in to too crowded boat and goot a very poor lunch. I think is better to choose bus-bus transportation

It's quite a way from anything and I don't think really worth the drive unless it's of specific interest. The site is quite nice but just has some small ruins, very hot and full of other tourists. They also do a short 'cultural show' but it all felt a bit tacky.

Место включено в список Юнеско, поэтому я и решил его посетить. Как оказалось, зря. Смотреть там совершенно нечего - развалины, заросшие травой. Конечно, проездом посетить его можно, но проблема как раз в том, что туда нужно ехать специально - это примерно 55 км от города Хой Ан. Кстати, для посещения советую не пользоваться экскурсионными бюро, а взять мопед и поехать самим. Это будет дешевле (за мопед мы заплатили 6 долларов за весь день, за топливо еще 8), да и впечатления от поездки на мопеде останутся надолго. Это в какой-то степени экстрим.

De vraies ruines ! Malheureusement la guerre est passée par là. Très peu de bâtiments sur pieds. Restauration mauvaise. Personnellement je regrette cette visite. Pas d intérêt. 2 h de voiture depuis Hoi an pour rien voir sur place.

Tip1: you should bring an umbrella (extremely hot and sunny). Maybe your hotel can provide you one. Tip2: Lonely Planet says that is impossible to visit it independently because there is no public transportation. But that is not true. You can do that by yourself. The (yellow - local) bus number 6 goes there and stops in the same place where the tour bus left us - at the site entrance. It should be less than $1. We paid $6 for the tour plus $5 for the site ticket. Our tour was awful, I would prefer to take the public bus.Tour: we booked the tour with our hotel Vaia (check my review), it was $6/each. They picked us in the hotel at 7.40 am. And then the bus went to several hotels in the city to pick up more people. It took more than 1h. And they forgot 1 person that took a scooter ride to get the bus (big discussion). The motorist drove like a maniac. And he stopped to pick up some supplies for a local restaurant (where they force us to stop and buy stuff). In the site the guy did lots of political propaganda. And he was also very vulgar. Instead of telling us more about the site he joked about dicks sizes and asked women in the tour if they already saw a giant one (no comments...). And encouraged guys to put the... in the Shiva's belly saying that would double the size... It was really disgusting!The guy didn't know anything about the site. More than 50% of what he said was in fact wrong. Lonely Planet has more reliable info about it.When we were returning the guy tried to force us to return by boat which is more expensive and it takes more time. Some of us refused and the guy told that they would leave us in the center and not in the hotel as they promised.In the center the left the bus and yelled at us to leave it too. No goodbye or thank you. It was very rude.Site: it's completely destroyed. It doesn't have much to see or do. They are recovering some temples but they don't look nice. If you have some time in Hoi An maybe you could go and check it. Otherwise enjoy the city and skip it.

Well the reception has nice building, but this sanctuary yes its old, nice ruins.. But if you compared to BOrobudur or prambanan in java indonesia is nothing, also if you just visit angkor wat before. I pay a lot to come here from danang. I think its not worthed for advanced traveller. Its just like that... Snd you need to walk a bit through the jungle. I think its good for archeologist.

I went here after visiting the mighty temples of Angkor in Cambodia and it kinda spoiled me so I wasn't impressed by these temples.  If it's your first ones you could enjoy it but not if you've seen the real deal in Angkor. 5 dollar entry fee is also a bit much for so few temples.  To each his own

First of all: don't make the same mistake like us: go either very early, or late afternoon as it is extremely crowded during morning time!! you can't get any feeling from the place.2nd: book a tour with an official tour guide or bring a very good guide book with you:Unfortunately, the site doesn't have anything to help the visitors to understand the sanctuary: how did it look before? what are the  very few remaining temple and them purpose?there's basically only the ruins ( and I mean ruins) and that's it!unless you are an historic nerds I wouldn't bother to go there if you have done Angkor Wat or any Cham towers( like in na trang).It's a shame as it could be a beautiful site if it was cared for.

Passez votre chemin, surtout si vous avez déjà visité Angkor !Quelques tas de ruines, le seul groupe vaguement conservé est celui des photos, 4 petits bâtiments de briques en mauvais état d'architecture très basique. Il faut être érudit, guidé, ou avoir une grande imagination pour y prendre du plaisir.
