On fait le tour en une heure, et franchement l'heure et demie de route pour y arriver n'a aucun intérêt, on perd la matinée (que les hôtels font payer presque une journée en voiture perso). La campagne est bien plus belle aux alentours de Hoi An où il vaut mieux prendre un vélo et faire quelques kilomètres. Nous avons regretté de ne pas l'avoir fait plus et zappé cette visite.Le site était en travaux en mars 2013 (accès handicapés) pour satisfaire sans doute aux exigences Unesco (très basiquement remplies pour l'instant). Le site est très simple d'accès depuis le parking (un quart d'heure de marche)

The ruins are not really impressive. It maybe an interesting place for someone who has not traveled to Siem Reap in Cambodia.We cycled 43 km one to My Son from Hoi An, which was a lovely trip through the Vietnamese countryside. My Son was just a place for a break from cycling. Food in the restaurant was tasty and staff was nice. After sightseeing the sitr and eating we cycled back to Hoi An. We recommed cycling trip to anyone who feels like cycling and can do 90 km per day. It took us altogether around 6 hours plus the sightseeing and eating time. Beautiful landscape and nice people on the way to My Son.

Not really much to see as it seems the ruins are getting worse over time and it is not so much temples but bomb craters and over grown shrubbery. Was expecting more from the temples, thought it would be similar to Angkor Wat but nothing like it. Give it a miss and spend your time going elsewhere

we only flew from saigon to da nang to visit my son because we were told it was really worth visitingWell...no it wasn't...not at allActually there were more bomb craters than temples...a few bricks laying around...half of it taken back by the nature and the other half under reconstrucion...

Dit was een grote tegenvaller. Na eerder in het Cham Sculpture Museum te zijn geweest wist ik gelukkig al het een en ander over deze plek en dat was maar goed ook, want ter plekke wordt weinig informatie verschaft. Daarnaast is er weinig over van de meeste "tempels". Wij zijn vanuit Hoi An op de scooter hierheen gegaan en die route was prachtig. Dus dat is wel een aanrader, maar verwacht niet teveel van My Son.
Only a few rocks and not much architecture and design unlike Angkor Wat.But it is not commercliased. So perhaps that is the upside of it.

We made a point to get out to My Son sanctuary by paying extra transportation.  If you had seen Angkor Wat, it would be disappointing seeing My Son.  Not much to see and very poor signage if you are traveling without a tour guide.  Nice walk-through the forest but totally not worth the 5 hr round trip.

実はホーチミンの旅行代理店で西欧人向けのツアーガイドとして働いているベトナム人の友人に「ミ―ソンは行くだけ時間の無駄！私なら絶対案内しない！だってただの廃墟」と散々言われたのにもかかわらずバス＆ボートツアー(US$7.00）に参加しました。結果的にはミ―ソンはまあに忠告のとうり・・・ボートの旅の方は良かったのですが・・・肝心のミ―ソンの遺跡はベトナム戦争中ここに北ベトナム軍が潜んでいたため徹底的に爆撃され遺跡内に大きなクレーターがボコボコ空いていて、煉瓦で造られた寺院もただの瓦礫の山、そうなんです煉瓦の瓦礫の山！んん・・・・それでも私は行って良かったとは思いますが～遺跡遺跡はしていません。本当に瓦礫なんです。あちこちで修復をしてましたが原形と留めて無いほど破壊されてるのでこれからが大変です。

世界遺産ということでそれなりに期待して行ったのですが、着いてみて・・・！！！？？？広々とした野原に荘厳な雰囲気で遺跡があるのかと思いきや、宅地造成前の草ボーボーの空き地にレンガが無造作に積み上げられているだけにしか見えない・・・。おそらく異国の人間には分からない歴史的な重要性があるのかと思うけど、がっかりポイントはかなり高め。現地発の英語ガイドのみのツアーのため料金も10ドルしなかったので(入場料60,000ドンは別料金)良しとしますが、個人的にはわざわざ何千円もだし、時間を割いて行く場所ではないかなと思います。なお、ベトナム戦争による爆撃と時の経過によりかなり崩壊が進んでいて、つい最近も一つ遺跡が倒れたとガイドさんが言っていましたので、遺跡や歴史が好きでそれでもなお見たいという方は早めに行っておいた方が良いかもしれません。

Overall I found the trip to My son rather disappointing.  Firstly, we had asked our hotel (Betel Garden) about early trips to My son.  From my research I understood it was a key element of the trip to be there early, before the tour buses arrive and the heat builds up.  We were told trips leave at 8am.  Too late for us so we hired a car through the hotel. (6.00 or 6.30am pick up - can't remember exactly).  Actually we did not make a complaint to the hotel, but we probably should have as this driver really did have a disgusting habit of picking his nose, constantly, as he drove.  And I mean the finger was right up the nose most of the time.  It made me feel squeamish. Then he would rush round to open our door for us.  Luckily, we had taken sanitiser!This man was just a driver, not a guide and we knew this, but I recommend that you do go with some sort of guide. We found it quite confusing there to find things.  Also it was most disappointing to discover that there were in fact small tour buses arriving at the same time called "early start tours."  Unfortunately we had not researched well enough and relied on our hotel to give us correct information, which they did not on this occasion.  I think we would have had a much better experience being with a small group and a guide.  All in all, I found My son fairly disappointing and we were happy to leave and go back to the hotel for breakfast.The only thing I did like was that some of the temples were picturesque and there was a nice museum place.  This had some interesting photographs of historic archeologists etc examining My son.

after reading reviews talking about how it was ok if you hadnt been places like angkor watt (i havent) that you'd enjoy it i was pretty disappointed. a few sites were closed off for construction so there wasnt much to see, and what there was wasnt that great. most of it has grass grown over it. alot of the statues have been displaced into small rooms where you can see them, and there must only be about 20 - 30 of those to see. was so disappointed with it.

I'll have to against the grain on this one and say that My Son is not at all impressive and is probably not worth the trip unless you are a history buff and/or trying to tick off UNESCO World Heritage Sites off your list (as I was). My Son is basically 3 little ruin sites in a semi-jungle (but not really). If you've been to Angkor Wat or Ayutthaya in Thailand, this will seem like child's play. If you haven't, My Son may be a good introduction to SE Asian ruins. Nonetheless, My Som is a historically important site so if you have extra time you dont know what to do with, it's worth checking out. There are early morning tours that leave at 5 AM and are back in Hoi An by 10 AM, as well as 8 AM tours that come back around 1 PM. The tour fee is $7 USD, and the park entrance fee is 60,000 dong ($3 USD).
not much interesting places to choose. journey to My Son is quite long too.

There is not much to see except for a few small temples & a crater where a bomb landed during the war.  You do however get to see  the countryside while getting there.  A boat trip with lunch is included .  The boat pulls up a Hoi An pier & you have to find your own way back to your hotel.

Die einzige geführte Tour die wir Vietnam gemacht hatten brachte uns zu My Son. Der Tourguide sprach ein sehr schlechtes englisch weshalb wir uns nach Ankunft von der Gruppe abgesetzt haben. Was als das Angkor Wat Vietnams angekündigt wird hat uns leider sehr enttäuscht. Die einzig wirklich gut erhaltene Gruppe ist die Erste leider tümmeln sich hier auch Scharen von Touristen man kann kaum einen Fuß vor den anderen setzen ohne gleich wieder neben jemanden zu stehen. Sehr enttäuschend war auch die 2. Gruppe die nicht vorhanden war.Wir wunderten uns zuerst warum wir nur 1 Stunde und 20 min. Zeit hatten das auf der Karte groß erscheindende Gelände anzuschauen waren dann aber nach 50 min. wieder am Bus. Vielleicht ist es besser wenn man am Nachmittag mit einem privaten Fahrzeug das Weltkulturerbe besucht. Eine Tour kann ich definitiv nicht empfehlen. Die anfahrt von Hoi An hat knappe ca. 1 3/4 Stunden gedauert, das war es am Ende leider wirklich nicht wert.

ホイアンの旧市街でミーソン遺跡の半日ツアーを取り扱ってる旅行会社がたくさんあったので、そこで予約しました。どこも安く、往復バスならば１０万ドン（入場料金別）はしなかったかと。帰りをボート＋ランチを付けたツアーも安かったです。ただ、残念なことに、ツアーではバス内で入場料金を徴収し、そのまま遺跡の中へ。入口近くにある博物館へは立ち寄りませんでした。博物館へ行きたいのなら、現地旅行会社の格安ツアーはやめたほうがよいと思います。肝心の遺跡ですが、かなり荒廃してました。地図の看板が何箇所かあるだけで、遺跡を説明した立て札等は全くないため、ガイドさんの説明に耳を傾けなければ、遺跡の内容は全くわかりません。草がぼーぼー生えた場所にただ石が積んであるようにしか見えません。アンコールワットのような素晴らしい彫刻もほんのわずかですし、あれほど細かいものはなかったです。ツアーは朝一の出発で午後には帰ってこれますので、ホイアンに何泊かする予定で半日くらい遠出しようかという方ならミーソン遺跡に行っても良いと思いますが、ホイアンの滞在日数が少ないのなら、あまりオススメしない場所です。

The Sanctuary itself is good, however beware of tours that claim to include the entry fee, and then ask for you to pay the entry fee again upon arrival at the Temple site.  We booked our My Son tour through Sunrise Travel in Hoi An, and were told that this tour included all transport and entry fees for our family.  A separate tour company (Blue Coral Travel) were the operators of the tour, and they then asked for the entry fee from us all upon arrival at the Temples, otherwise we wouldn't be allowed into the site.  It's not a lot of money, but no-one likes to be ripped off and everyone on the bus had been told the same thing, even though we had all booked through different tour providers / travel agencies.  Next time - ask them to write down all inclusions and exclusions.

We did a tour (7 USD) to My Son, it actually should have been a "sunrise tour". So we were picked up at around 5.00 am and drove for half an hour. Then we got a little breakfast (bread-egg-sandwich), after that we drove for another hour until we reached My Son. The entrance fee was not included in the tourprice. The story around My Son was really interesting, but unfortunately we have to say that almost everything was destroyed, there was not much to see anymore. It maybe depends how many temples or cultural heritage sites you already have visited, but My Son was definitly not our favourite. Oh yes and by the way, while we were driving the sun was raising, so there was nothing with sunrise tour over My Son :-)

The sanctuary is an exciting experience - but mostly sad, because 85% of what you could see 40 years ago is destroyed nowadays. They're trying to rebuild it, but at the moment there are only around 15-20 structures to see. If it's your first Khmer-Temple-structure, it's worth the trip - if you've been in cambodia, it's not.AND like my title says - don't book a cheap tour to go there. It will ruin your My son experience, if you visit vietnam for the culture and don't read boulevard-press.

If you get here after having visited other temples, especially in Cambodia, you are not very much impressed by the temples. What makes this experience different is that you still see the craters of the bombs and some temples ruined by the was 50 years ago

My Son is just shy of 40km from Hoi An's Old Town and makes for a nice day trip out by motorbike. It's a complex of ruins of Hindu temples and it's quite easy to get to.  There's little shade from the sun so make sure you cover up or sunscreen.  The damage to the temples is significant, due to bombing during the Vietnam War, but the lush green of the encroaching brush and the cat's teeth mountains behind makes for some nice views if you bring your camera.  It's about 100,000 dong to get in, and about 220,000 for bike hire and petrol.

Smaller version of ancient ruins if compared to the temples of angkor. it has a bit of a history by itself, a good walk in the humid hot jungle of vietnam. But if you've been to Angkor this is less magnificent and quite plain

We stayed a week in Hoi an and we decided to go by motorbike to my son. The road there was really fun and we were able to visit the cities next to Hoi an. If you decide to go by motorbike, it should take you about 1h20-30 mins. Just make sure you know where you're going, and at some point, you can start seeing directions towards my son.However, once arrived on site, it was a bit underwhelming. The statues/templeS are nice but that's about it. And plan on going in the morning or late afternoon because it can get really really hot in the afternoon. I would recommend doing going to see it if you have spare time, otherwise it's just not worth it.

5月の連休を利用して夫とホイアン旅行に行った際に、行きました。個人手配の旅行で行ったのですが、場所を考慮し、ミーソン遺跡だけは日本からツアーを手配してから行きました。ホイアンのホテルからはバスで1時間くらいかかったと思います。天気がとても良かったのですが、とにかく暑かったので、帽子とミネラルウォーターは必ず持っていく事をお勧めします。私達はガイドさんから話を聞くことが出来たのですが、ツアーに参加しないで行くと、遺跡の一つ一つに案内板があるわけでは無いので、良くわからなかったと思います。せっかくいくのであれば、ガイド付きツアーでないと楽しめないと思いますよ！

Was an absolutely steaming hot day here - much hotter than in Hoi An.  We travelled here by coach and to be honest it was a bit arduous.  Some of the temples have been damaged, some are under repair however with different colour bricks, I just wonder if better to leave them.  Wouldn't go back again, once more than enough
Es ist heutzutage nur eine Ruine mit nur ein wenig Tempeln der Cham Leute, aber es besteht aus zu vielen Architekturen und Skulpturen darin.
チャンパ遺跡が多く残っていますが、残念なのは保存状態があまりよくなく、またインフラ状態の整備がよくなく、観光地としてちょっと残念な状態です。ただ、このような遺跡に興味のある人にはいいかも？？カンボジヤの遺跡に比べると天と地ですが、これはこれなりに、寂しさや郷愁を感じさせる意味ではノスタルジックな観光ができます。

We enjoyed the day & the price is v reasonable but there were a few frustrations. We stopped at the worst toilets ever & then discovered that there were sparkling clean, brand new ones 5 minutes up the road. The boat trip at the end is v ho hum & the stop at the carpentry village a waste of time. Our guide was super enthusiastic though & the temples interesting (although not a patch on Siem Reap).

If you've been to the temples of Angkor Wat then you can give this place a pass. Unfortunately, with all of the bombings carried out in the past, there is nothing left of this place but ruins. What was once many temples that may have rivaled the might of the ones found in Siem Reap, now there is almost nothing. If you enjoy ruins, then by all means go because your tourism will help with the reconstructions. For me, this place just didn't do it.

We are older travellers ( in our 70's) so these comments may not necessarily apply to younger people.  This day tour was included with the great Vietnam tour we purchased online from Halong Tours.  We did not have much information about this other than the bit we had researched.  We were in a large group of about 40 people and travelled by a comfortable air conditioned coach.  My Son is only about an hour's drive from Hoian.  We stopped at the sanctuary's restaurant for toilet  break just prior to the commencement of our visit. To  use the non western, not very clean facilities cost 2000VND.We then had short walk to a mini bus which took us into the site.  We were a bit taken aback when our guide announced we would be walking 6 klms.  This wouldn't have been so bad if the terrain had been flat but the paths were uneven and in some parts quite steep.  We managed but a couple of other 'older' travellers found it hard going.There is no disputing the site is worth a visit but wear good walking shoes, sandals really aren't appropriate, and dress accordingly .  It was a hot day and we were happy we had brought with us a supply of water.   Once you are on the site there are no toilet facilities or anywhere to purchase water. Many of the paths especially those you use to return to your transport, are flag~stone type irregular paths.  For those who are unsteady they are not easy to walk on.  We took the boat back to Hoian.  We were given a very basic lunch on this.  Our boat was crowded.  It would have been a much pleasanter cruise home with fewer people on the boat.  We are not at all sorry we took this tour but we would like to have been advised of the amount of walking required.One last tip !  Don't forget the insect repellant and sun screen AND  an umbrella for the sun and the rain showers .,,

We arranged with our hotel, private drive with 3people.  Probably the best way.   Road construction to the sites son we Had to walk 1 kil in the heat to meet with transfer transportation for another kil.  This was not advertised so may walked the 2 kil in 40 degree heat.  Very little to see but the efforts are there.

Même si nous ne sommes pas sous la même empreinte culturelle, la comparaison avec les temples d'Anghkor est obligatoire et pas à l'avantage de My Son. Donc pour résumer , si vous avez passés 3 ou 5 jours au Cambodge pour visiter les temples Khmers vous serez déçu ici, sinon pour les autres c'est une destination incontournable .Mais une longue route pour une courte visite .

There are multiple sites situated close together, each representing a different period in Champa temple building.  The whole site has suffered from gratuitous bombing and vandalism by past masters and therefore there is far less original structure than say Ankor Wat.  However it is worth visiting the various period sites to see the evolution in the design.  They are putting in a proper road to the site but currently you have to walk 2-3km from the drop off point.  I would imagine they will finish the access route in the next few months so less walking in 40 degree heat!  I think you would benefit from a guide as the level of incompleteness makes it hard for a westerner to interprete the remains.  There is a rest area just before ruins that does a short singing and dancing show periodically.  Although the new road is nice and flat, wheelchair users would struggle with the high number of steps  required to access each section of the ruins once you get there.

Quand on voit les photos de ce site avant de s'y rendre, on imagine une espèce de "mini Angkor"... Force est de constater que ce site a beaucoup été détruit soit par la guerre soit par les pillages ou les approvisionnements de musées... Seuls un ou deux emplacements et vestiges valent le déplacement.

ホイアンから１日でトリップするとしたら、ここしかないのでみんなここにいきます。小さなアンコールワットという感じです。１－２時間あればすべて見れます。とりたてて凄いというわけではないのですが、この地域の王朝の遺跡として世界遺産になっています。早朝から行くツアーで、昼過ぎには帰ってこれるため、反日いくのには良いです。注意したいのは、真夏は（４－８月）はとんでもなく日差しが強いということです。遺跡を歩くだけで倒れそうになります。４０度近くなります。高齢者は避けたほうがいいのではとおもってしまうくらいです。熱射対策だけはしてください。

We took the my son bus+boat tour + lunch.  We were glad about the hotel pick up.  Really saved us time walking to the agency.  Taking the tour with the agency is cheaper than taking it from the hotel.  It was drizzling on and off the morning that we went.  So it was cool and a pleasant walk around.  The area is really in ruins by war and nature.  It's a must see although not as grand as other places such as Angkor.
Lots of brick structures that are pretty interesting at first sight. That being said there was no tour guide and after a few minutes you find yourself walking around wondering what you're looking at.

The Santctuary is very far from the city and with you don't have time to spare it's not worth. The temples are not that nice, there is not much signs and if your guide has a poor english (which is the usual) it won't add much.
Rien de plus à dire que ce sont de vieilles ruines, la route est longue pour s'y rendre, il faut arriver tôt pour le spectacle musical

It was not within our expectation as most of the sites in this sanctuary are just ruin. It is very hot and advised to bring enough drinking water. We joined a tour going by bus and coming back with boat. The bus was very poor and has very weak air-conditioning system. We felt very warm inside the bus after the walking. Before we heading to the site, the bus stopped at a rest stop very close to the entrance where we have to pay for toilet!! Instead, there are toilets provided just at the entrance! This is really bad. A local lady was standing besides the toilet of the rest stop and forced us to pay into a donation box. Instead, she had the money for her own and checked for the amount. Donation is just donation where you donate VOLUNTARILY but not by forcing! As well, the boat tour was disappointed with the facility and scenery. There was no great scenery, save for when it approached Hoi An town. The lunch provided (veggie) was very small portion and tasted bad. Before joining the tour, the hotel staff told us that we would be given a bottle of water. The tour guide sold soft drinks and beers when we get into the boat and only provided us a bottle of water when we left the boat. I would say this is a kind of cheat. We were disappointed with this tour guide.

Typical of old sites, you see what has been preserved, what is being preserved and a pile of rubble. That aside, it is interesting enough as much for the drive there but more so for the music and dance performance by the local people. Very enjoyable.

It's a nice activity and doesn't cost too much at all, but it was a bit underwhelming. I guess, if you have been to Angkor Wat, then you can skip this. But if you go, please try to go early in the morning to beat the crowds (we were the only people there) and read about it first to know what it's all about. It looks good, but it's a very small site.

Same comments as most of the other reviews. Go early in the am - as we were melting trying to walk around and check out the ruins late in the morning - I think because its a bit of a valley, there's not much breeze and the air is thick and hot. Ruins are tiny (incomparable to Bagan or Angkor Wat) and most have unfortunately been destroyed from the war, they are however rebuilding a few. We didn't get a tour guide, but probably would've been nice to. A nice trip to get away from Hoi An.

We had a personal guide. The ruins are really interesting BUT the visitor experience is really poor. You do have to use a lot of imagination even with a guide. They could do so much better with enhanced presentation and a decent visitor centre. To be honest after about an hour I was bored

O local tem uma bela história que conta muito de Shiva e das construções realizadas para adoração. As marcas da guerra são bem presentes neste local, mas a má conservação destrói a beleza e tira a mágica da história.
Worth a look at, we learnt about the Cham temples, and got to see the remains of these. We went by private taxi with a tour guide. Took about four hours from start to fining from da nang.

It's like visiting a smaller scale of the Angkor wat but only this one was a scatter collection of everything. The well kept ones are in the danang Cham museum, which was also not impressive. If you have  been to Angkor wat /Angkor thom you may want to give this a pass......

We bezochten deze tempelruïnes met een bustocht vanuit Hoi An. Het was er erg druk waardoor het lastig is mooie foto's te maken. We hadden ongeveer 2 uur en dat was voldoende om alle tempels, voornamelijk ruïnes, te zien.
Stopped here on part of my easy rider tour. Was a little bit disappointed at what there was to see (not much). I am happy I didn't pay for a hotel organised tour to My Son.

Our plan was to go to My Son by motorbike by later we changed the decision and went there by bus with a group of people. The problem is that most of such groups come there in the late morning and that is why this place is a bit overcrowded and lose its quiet mysterious atmosphere. This is unfortunatelly why I am giving average rating. I belive that if comming there later in the afternoon, my experience would be better.

Probably I expected a bit more - My Son is interesting but quite a small sight.  There are only a very few ruins, so if you don't have much time in Hoi An area or you're not an archeologist - consider to skip it.

This is a somber location where the wounds of war are laid bare.  There are numerous artifacts and photos relating to the event and the descriptions focus the blame.An interesting, recently-made video can be seen (at least in English, possibly Vietnamese and other languages) that attempts to make sense of the tragedy.  It is not an unbiased video, however.This area is not for the faint-of-heart, nor for children.

If you are only visiting Vietnam, then by all means visit My Son. If, however, you have already spent some time in other SE Asian countries, you may not be that impressed by My Son. To maximize your experience, either go on your own to avoid horrendous tourist crowds that flock this place, or do the following. Most groups come roughly at the same time, and there seems to be a kind of a break at around 11-11:30 am. Forget about yoru guide (he's likely just going to mumble incoherently about some rubbish), and head to the lesser visited parts first - check them out on your own, and when you start seeing the groups fleeing the main complex, head there and hopefully, you'll have 15-20 minutes of it relatively uncrowded. Until the next ten packs of tourists arrive, that is.

We had a much better experience in My Son because of our tour guide. The attraction itself was great to see as it was the only thing of its kind we did in Vietnam. It isn't big however and you need to make sure you see it when no one else is around. We went on a private tour with a lovely tour guide who was full of enthusiasm and made it much more interesting as there is no info available there.Most tor groups seem to arrive around 9am, but we went in the afternoon when there were only a few other people. There was a large coach of tourists but they rushed through it pretty quickly and as it got close to sunset we were the only ones there, which vastly improved the experience.We paid Mr Turtle ca. $25 each for the afternoon which we felt was good value. If you go on a group tour, and you have already been to somewhere like Angkor Wat, it's probably not worth it. We did hear that the sunrise tours that get you there a few hours before the main tour groups are also worth doing.

Few things remained, hard even to notice them it is really a site for which you absolutly need a guide...by the way I am not really into antic staff, so read my review like this...for me even the Acropolis was a big appointment nowadays.

Having seen the wonderful temples in Cambodia and Thailand, My Son is rather underwhelming.  It's also rather packed with visitors.  We went with a tour but took ourselves around to try to avoid the big crowds.The tour was cheap at $9 with a boat ride back, and we had a good day out, but don't worry if you don't get chance to see it

If you're going in the summer, or even if you're not, try to arrive when the area first opens in the morning.  During mid-day, there is no shade and the temperatures can get up there (in the summer).  So early is good here.  Also, the tour buses start piling in an hour or so after the site opens - so get there before they do.Unfortunately, this site was badly damaged during the Vietnam-American war, so, you need a guide to explain it to you.  Without a guide - you're looking at a pile of ancient bricks.Having said that, My Son awards you with a glimpse into an ancient world and life and is a very interesting experience.

An hour drive out of Hoi An and costing about 200k dong including entry lies this small temple complex. Mostly destroyed by the war and bombing there are a few intact buildings and some which are being restored.Tour guides make this place more interesting, however it is lacking the wow factor of Angkor Wat, so if you haven't been there in the previous weeks you may be more impressed. Some of the restoration is still brand new so hasn't aged enough to make it look like anything but a new build.
Very badly restored. They should make a better effort at restoring the monuments, using better craft / authentic workmanship and not plonk in "raw" bricks just to "prop up" the crumbling ruins

This place is amazing and you really get a feel for the Vietnam jungle that once hid this site from the 7th century BC up until the French discovered it in the 1800's.  Much of the site was destroyed in the war in the 1970's and evidence of bomb craters and bullet holes are everywhere.  There has been some restoration of the ruins but much of it remains as it was after the war.

After going to Ayutthaya in Thailand the previous year (see my review of Ayutthaya Historical Park), I was hoping for a similar type of experience at My Son. However, My Son is quite a bit smaller, which is understandable because of the havoc that took place during the Vietnam War... so if you are expecting something like Ayutthaya (or Angkor Wat) then you are likely to be a bit disappointed.  What I was also disappointed with was the way in which they have tried to preserve some of the ruins. In some places they have just done an awful job of trying to rebuild, to the extent that the bricks used are completely the wrong color! The one thing that made our trip decent was that our hotel (see my review of Essence Hoi An Hotel and Spa) set us up with a great guide who gave us some very insightful thoughts on the history of the Hoi An and My Son areas. He had clearly grown up very open-minded and seemed to have a pretty good grasp of history, especially compared to some of the more brainwashed guides that we saw with other groups. He also had great advice in getting us to My Son very early before the crowds hit. Once the crowds came in (between 9-10am) the place became a zoo. Thankfully we were leaving by the time this happened! If you are going to visit, I recommend getting to My Son very early to avoid the tourist buses and crowds!
Make sure you get a good tour guide - ours wasn't good and we only learnt info from other guides. We travelled for 1.5 hours to the area where the jungle woods are beautiful.

Det är sevärt om man gillar historia. Vi var där under lågsäsong så det var inte mycket turister där alls. På kartan såg området större ut. Det gick lätt & fort att ta sig till fots från område till område. Vi var där utan guide & grupp & kan konstatera att vill man få ut mer är ett tips att man läser på om My Son. De skyltar som finns där säger inte mycket om ruinerna.
Is like a small version of Cambodia Angkor wat. Walking into the place is quite easy not as difficult as what the tour guide said.

Ruins are mostly destroyed, but part of them are really nice. If you are in Hoi An, take a half day tour for few bucks and when visiting, don't stay with your group. It's the only chance to avoid the crowd, take nice photos without zilion people in the background and have a nice chill out there.
アンフーツアーと言う現地では老舗のツアー会社で行きました（英語ツアーのみ１８５０００ドン）　行きはバス帰りはボート、入場料は個人払い（１０万ドン）、軽食付き、英語ガイド付き。多少の登り坂はあるけれど　それより暑いので対策は必要です。ツアーだと見どころは案内してもらえるので良いと思います。

The temples appear to being restored - and the park seems to be managed well. E.g Paths to follow, clean and tidy, no rubbish around, a little stage show etc.. I tagged onto a tour group as I had cycled out there alone but I would have preferred some signage or plaques to guide me around the park. No written information at all beside some text on the ticket that I could see.However  I had to chuckle at the arrangement of placing 2 large missile shells inside one of the temples and as groups walk though the VN tour guides explained how the war was won..etc..  Never miss a good opportunity...

Ехать около 1 часа. По сравнению с Ангор-Ватом в Камбодже смотреть не на что. При этом, за последние 5-6 лет ничего не изменилось по восстановлению храмов - как все было, так и осталось. Деньги Юнеско уходят в другое место))) Если время есть и до этого не видели подобные руины - то съездить стоит. Если были в Камбодже - то время тратить не стоит

I have had chances to visit some Cham temple towers like Po Klong Garai, Po Sah Inu, etc and most of them are in good condition but My Son is totally different. My Son was the abandoned holy land of the Champa and it is not just a tower, it is an architectural complex. It contains a lot of temple towers, each of tower has it own role. Unluckily, all main towers were destroyed by wars. Now we only see the ruins, contemplate ancient embossments and listen to tour guides telling the tale of Cham temple tower construction.

Found it boring because i have been to ankhor watt. The boat trip was boring too and the lunch sub standard, drinks not included. Lunch was vegetarian, rice with tofu and a couple of veggies. Trip to the island was interesting seeing the craft people at work. Trip cost $6 100000 dong to get in.

my son heeft veel geleden onder de bombardementen van de Amerikanen: het meeste is kapotgeschoten en overwoekerd met gras en onkruid. Een groep, ik dacht groep C (de tempels van my son is verdeeld in groepen: groep A , B. C. etc) is nog redelijk intact zodat je toch een goed beeld krijgt hoe de tempels er vroeger hebben uitgezien. Groep G wordt ten dele gerestaureerd en ziet er ook wel aardig uit maar de rest ligt in puin. Dit is heel jammer aangezien de tempels in een prachtige vallei liggen en het zo mooi had kunnen zijn. Van groep E/F heeft men zojuist een tempel geheel gerestaureerd en die ziet er ook weer mooi uit, maar zoals gezegd: driekwart ligt in puin en kan waarschijnlijk nooit meer herbouwd worden . Als je Bagan in Birma en Angkor Wat in Cambodja hebt gezien valt dit zwaar tegen en je moet dan ook trachten iedere vergelijking te vermeiden hetgeen geen gemakkelijke opgave is. Is my Son de moeite waard ?. ondanks het feit dat ik me er meer van had voorgesteld en er veel in puin ligt vond ik van wel, omdat de tempels die er nog wel staan er mooi uitzien en een goed beeld geven van de pracht van weleer en ze  in een prachtige omgeving staan.
Las ruinas reflejan una preciosa ciudad levantada entre montañas y arroyos. Imprescindible la vuelta en barquito de madera, mientras se contemplan las numerosas aldeas de pescadores.
私は他の客がいなかったので、一人だけの専用車。なので２５ドルという高い金額になってしまった。時間がある人は地元んツアーで行かれると安いと思います。（料金不明）片道５０分位。遺跡はレンガ出積み上げられた遺跡群が敷地内に点在しているのを見て回る。ガイドはいないので、事前に勉強しておかないと、単なる廃墟を見ている感じ。

Many time I had visited here. It looks natural, old, and so quite. It was Cham's history from VN. Not tour guide but it is not important owing to this venue not too large, you can discover by yourself. However, should be booked with some Travel Agencies in Hoi An Ancient Town to let their tour guides can support you more.

I agreed with another reviewer when they said that if you try and compare this place with the likes of the ruins in Cambodia, you'll be disappointed. These ruins were not in very good condition, though some of them were being restored. We organized to see this place with a tour company as it's at least an hour from Da Nang, so you'll need transportation to get there and back. Also, when we were there, there didn't see to be much information which you could read by yourself. Our tour guide was okay. He was a nice guy, but English wasn't good. He kept mentioning that the state of the ruins were due to the Vietnam war and showed us many big craters where the bombs went. Not sure how much of this was true? I've been to Cambodia and if I knew the state of these ruins, I could have easily skipped it.

Une bonne marche matinale en pleine forêt avec une braise en plein milieu de montagne, conduisant à des ruines historiques malheureusement pas toutes restaurées faute de moyen, dans certaines on a plus l'impression de voir juste un stock de briques :(((( dommage que toute la beauté des lieux soient perdue au fil du temps, le site est à 1h de route de hoian, il vaut le détour mais avec un pincement au coeur de l'état actuel des lieux (comme pas mal de sites au vietnam)

If you try to compare this UNESCO site with others in Cambodia or elsewhere you are likely to be disappointed. If you are interested in seeing some ancient Cham ruins then this is an interesting place. It is about 2 hours away from Da Nang, and about an hour away from Hoi An. Once parked the walk to the ruins is approximately 15 minutes.Some of the structures have been rebuilt and others are in various stages of being rebuilt. Some of the guides are very knowledgeable about the site and provide great information about the buildings and artifacts. I suspect that without a guide or an understanding of the Cham culture and a little reading on the internet this place could just be a pile of bricks to some folks. But for those who enjoy exploring ancient ruins, taking picturesque photos...My Son is worth the visit.

Nothing much to see really. The whole tour I could only see bricks and listened to the tour guide not-so-funny jokes.I have been to Bagan (Myanmar), Yogyakarta (Indonesia), Siem Reap (Cambodia) and I can say that this place is not even worth the 8USD and your time.

A great UNESCO historical site. However it is best to opt for an early morning tour as later in the day it gets too hot to walk about and takes the joy out of seeing the place as you are hot and tired. The boat trip back was the perfect antidote to the heat we endured while walking around the ruins. Wear flat shoes. I saw some tourists in high heels finding great difficulty navigating the walk!

This sanctuary is a good place to discover different kinds of architecture from South East Asia. It's not a must do but it's a nice trip to discover the landscape around Hoi An, especially if you manage to avoid the main road and to go more through the countryside. Nevertheless it's not as good as the surroundings of Hue, (temples and forbidden city)
ダナン市内にある博物館に主だったものがあるためそこを見てから行くと少し良いです。遺跡自体はベトナム戦争で破壊されているため廃墟を見に行く感じです。遺跡目的ならお勧めですが、観光目的ならアンコールワットかボロブドゥール寺院などとは桁外れに壊れておりがっかりします。また遺跡まで駐車場から1KM位あるため運動靴をお勧めします。

Definitely interesting (albeit on a small scale) Unfortunately it took us around 2 hours to get there by taxi and we were expecting more as it had been billed as Vietnam's Angkor Watt. If I were travelling alone or with friends it would have been a much better experience. I wouldn't recommend it with young children.
Beautiful and historical but HOT! In the summer you should plan on finishing by noon. Great for photos but not much else around....

Sinceramente devo dire che se dovessi scegliere tra il sito di My Son e lo strepitoso Museo di Danang nn avrei dubbio alcuno: il museo. Piccoli templi nn ancora restaurati dopo i bombardamenti americani e nessun reperto dato che sono tutti nei musei. In mezzo alla foresta i tempietti migliori ma comunque niente di impressionante. Si può arrivare al sito in tanti modi ma è bene arrivare presto per evitare la folla.

Our guide, Spider, made this trip. His commentary was very good.However at the end of the day I just didn't find it that impressive, although I'm sure some people will love it.As the old saying goes, 'you've seen one ancient Vietnamese civilization temple, you've seen them all'

These ancient ruins would have been absolutely incredible to view prior to the Vietnamese war. What is left is still impressive ( if you haven't been to Angkor wat apparently). Make sure your have a guide who takes you through each part of what is left of these ruins a very interesting history and significance of the remaining ruins. Unfortunately not much is left and only the first site is still standing. Efforts to rebuild some ruins have begun but are not going to be nearly as impressive due to the lost ways of burning bricks to compress and force uneven brick together. Note: you must have a tour guide otherwise it will just seem like a bunch of bricks ruined by war.Another reason for an average rating is the lengthy bus trip it takes to get there ( similar to all attractions in Vietnam) if you have the money splurge on bike and tour guide to take you out early other wise your whole day seems a little wasted. Also  do not take the boat back. I repeat do not do it. There is very little scenery even in comparison to Mekong. And the food was poor for lunch. Waste of stomach space. They do take you through a village but if you have done some Vietnamese tours prior it's all a bit familiar . Interesting to see how they make boats but other than that waste of time spent in hoi an area.
We went here anticipating seeing some untouched ancient architechture, but there're not too many of it left. Most parts are being renewed so kinda disappointing.

If you have plenty of time in the area, it's good to visit My Son Sanctuary.  It's quite well managed and good for those who never visited Angkor.  We have been to Angkor before and therefore, find this site not attractive at all. And the scale/site is a bit small.

We enjoyed the drive from Hoi An through the small towns and the country side. But for me, the ruines of My Son were not worth the trip. If you have seen Angkor Wat, you will probably be disappointed.

My husband and I visited My Son today on an arranged tour ($8 for bus to My Son, guide, bus to a boat, lunch on boat, boat trip, short stop at some craft shops and then dropped by boat back to Hoi An - admission to the site is 100 000 dong on top of this).We echo the sentiments of previous travellers, who commented about the heat but also the beauty of the area. Basically, for us (and we love the sun and heat) this trip was way too hot (over 40 degrees), despite having an air con bus and a breezy boat trip. Neither of us are particularly interested in archaeology, and so were on the trip more for the natural beauty than anything else. The heat was so overwhelming at the site that we were pretty desperate to leave about 30 minutes after arriving. The boat trip was pleasant, lunch was very basic, and the stop at the craft shops was an unnecessary and a little frustrating (due to the dripping sweat) part of the trip.Overall we'd say if you have a particular interest in relics and history - then it's worth it, otherwise save your money.

Thanh dia My Son (My son sanctuary or ruins) and Hoi An are our next destinations in 2 days and 1 night journey. I called the taxi driver and agreed on the price of VND 900,000 for one way trip from Ba Na hill to My Son ruins (just need about 2 hours visit) and then Hoi An. Going like this with two persons are even cheaper than booking a tour and of course it is more comfortable and flexible in terms of times. There are lots of taxi waiting at the cable station of Ba Na hill. But because I already ordered the taxi from the previous day so we still waited for that taxi driver although other drivers even offer a little cheaper price. There our taxi driver came! he picked the luggages for us and we got in his car. On the way he pretended to call his call centre and saying that the price we agreed was too cheap. Now, I got angry because my kindness of waiting for this guy and ignoring other taxi drivers was abused . I told him that no other price was made, otherwise we would get out of his car. He said sorry immediately and didnt talk further about this.A lesson to learn is dont make any agreement in advance with any taxi driver, just pick a car you want and negotiate the price in advance.My son ruins are ok, just like what it is name 'ruins'; there are not much things to see except some nice photos if one wants to take as the souvenir. The ticket is VND 100,000/foreigner and VND 60,000 for Vietnamese. But they offer nice dances of the Cham ethnic group (the last performance is 2.30pm). It was so hot in My Son and I had to buy a Vietnamese traditional hat (non) for my husband. Although the quality of that hat was not good and quite expensive he liked it so much and we did have some funny photos! Later when coming back to Hanoi we bought 6 additional hats as gifts for friends and for our shelves to wear in the beach in Belgium (maybe it will be a new fashion trend in Europe this summer)!!

Ожидали немного большего (в прошлом году были в Барабудуре и Прамбанане).Храм/Развали находятся в часе езды от Хой Ана, если поедите с экскурсией (в все отели предлагают экскурсии), то скорее всего окажетесь в большой группе туристов с гидом, который плохо говорит на английском, повторяет каждую фразу три раза и немного достает. Поэтому если есть возможность - берите личного водителя. Сам храм находится в живописном месте, но был сильно порушен в том числе во время Вьетнамской войны. В результате большая часть храма восстановленная (местами вообще новые кирпичи лежат). Плюс там в принципе не так уж много зданий. Из-за достаточно большого количества туристов достаточно сложно делать нормальные фотографии =(Поэтому в целом, если специализируетесь/интересуетесь хинду, то непременно стоить съездить, если обычный путешественник, то скорее всего несколько разочаруетесь.

We were looking forward to our visit to My Son and it's just about worth a visit. It was about an hour away from our hotel and we were glad that we had a private transfer rather than a tour because once you've been there an hour or so, that's about enough. It was very very hot and humid, even in the dry season but the setting in the valley is very scenic and the bird/ animal noises really add to the atmosphere.The fee for entry was VND100k per person which is very reasonable. I almost wish it was more so that they could do a better job with the restoration.There are 5 areas. Each with a set of ruins. The first couple are really interesting but after that they were pretty disappointing and in particular, they were being restored with what looked like regular house bricks. On completion they were almost going to look like brand new buildings. That's really not the point of restoration.Take water. Take a private car or taxi, not a tour.

Après plus d'une heure de bus depuis Hoi An,on y arrive enfin. Quelques minutes de marché dans la forêt avant d'arriver sur le site proprement dit. Il y fait très très très chaud (on est dans une vallée entourée de montagnes):prévoir chapeau,crème solaire et eau. Il ne reste plus grand chose de ce "grand passé" qu'on nous vend. Il faut être bien passionné d'archéologie pour apprécier. Pas indispensable

If you've been to siem reap you could give my son a miss, however still a nice little trip, very pretty scenery, and a nice walk in the jungle either way. I went on a group tour with a boat ride back to hoi an, I found the boat trip back a waste of time but enjoyed my son even if it was an hour drive. If you're travelling to Cambodia or already been don't bother going as it's not as spectacular.

Nous avons visites my son avec un tour organisé depuis hoi an  départ en car et retour en bateau assez décevant beaucoup de restauration a faire Cependant un moment agréable de préférence visiter tot le matin il y a moins de monde et il fait moins chaud

Купили экскурсию на улице. Организована экскурсия была нормально.Забрали из отеля, привезли. В стоимость входил завтрак, так как ехали рано утром. По мне это сложно назвать завтраком (бутерброд: хлеб, яйцо и лист салата + бутылка воды). Желательно о завтраке позаботиться самим.Смотрели развалины, гид рассказывал о них. Походить, посмотреть - интересно. Но так как я не любитель таких мест, мне не понравилось. Но все-таки, это место стоит посещения. Там тихо, спокойно. Сидишь и наслаждаешься тишиной.Сам комплекс не очень большой, в основном месте сосредоточено 5-6 строений.После рассказов об этом месте, гид отправил нас просто погулять.Нашли еще одно строение, но оно было на реставрации, и смотреть там было нечего.Обратный путь предполагал два варианта возвращения: на автобусе или на лодке. У нас был второй вариант. Прокатили нас на лодке, завезли в поселение мастеров (делать там было нечего, два магазина, где торгуют сувенирами, изготовленными из дерева). Потом довезли нас до центра на лодке. И вроде как по программе экскурсии нас должны были доставить в отель, но нас никто не встретил. Вообще вьетнамцы грешат этим, могут опоздать на час при сборе или, как получилось у нас, не отвезти в отель.Но мы не расстроились, а погуляли по центу, сходили на местный рынок.Место, стоящее для посещения тем, кто интересуется развалинами. Те, кто любят другие достопримечательности, я бы не посоветовала ехать. Общее время на экскурсию: пол дня.

As a site, it didn't excite me much. It's OK. Definitely I wouldn't go out of my way to visit it.But overall it was a great experience. If you like to observe and see real life and the real world, instead of hoping from ancient site to ancient site, the way from Hoi An is a treat. Lot's of things to see. I visited with a cheap 5 USD tour and was very happy with my option. I read a bit while the bus was collecting other tourists and opened my eyes to the views as soon as we left the town. Great tour guide, although once on the site I left the group. We had about two hours on the location which is more than enough.

Sometimes I get a sinking feeling in my stomach when I've lost something of value. I had that feeling 1000 times over when visiting My Son and seeing what one week of bombing during the Vietnam War did to 1600 years of history. I felt ill.I'm not going to fall into a trap and start talking about good and bad in war, as there is no such thing, but what I will say is 600 pound bombs did what Father Time couldn't. I'd like to say I still enjoyed the monuments and that I felt like I was Indiana Jones. But I didn't do either. Especially when I spotted bits of brick in a creek 50 m from where a bomb had directly hit a temple and blown it into a million bits. Up until today we'd heard a bit about the 'War' but hadn't seen too much evidence - so it came as a bit of a shock.The traditional music and dancers were great, and brought some form of niceness to a place that had been subjected to the opposite of human traits. For that reason and the fact that My Son should stand testament for why war is a pointless exercise in futility I rated it 3 out of five.

We were told if you are going to Angkor Wat then don't go. It was pretty hot and to be fair our tour guide was not that good but lots of personality. We wandered off and listened to another one for about 1 minute and learnt more than 30 minutes with the other one. If you get a good guide it would make all the difference. Our tour included a boat tour and carpentry village which you could miss. Overall it was ok as it's part of Vietnamese history. It's a pity America bombed the hell out of it and destroyed 95% of which is tragic really.

Quite an anti-climax. It is not a large complex and all the structures are incompletely restored or are in ruins. Only the attempt by the guide to provide an interesting narrative about its history, etc made something out of the visit. Visit only if you are serious buff about ancient civilisations or you have no better way to spend your free time. The dance performance was satisfactory and was not made any better by a stuffy and airless open-sided hall despite the air blowers and fan.

There is ALOT of walking on this tour and unfortunately we came on an afternoon with scorching heat and humidity. It was a challenging walk if like us, you have weak knees or legs. If you're interested in this, i highly recommend the morning tours where its a little cooler. If you've seen other ancient sites in the region, you might find this slightly lacking. The dance performances at the site were impressive though. The boat ride back to Old Town was very enjoyable.

From the marble mountains I booked a grave (motorcycle; 410,000 dong including waiting and return). Admission to My Son costs 150,000 dong. The electric shuttle will take you towards the 1. Temple driven, but you still have to walk a bit. I had imagined My Son to be different. It is rather a small temple complex of Cham culture and little restored temple. The facility itself is well maintained. Due to the rain, however, I did not stay long. Maybe an hour and a half. I have read many times that the facility is comparable to Ankor Wat - but I cannot confirm that.

This was a walk around a valley which had a number of ruined temples.  It was peaceful and a beautiful area with some history but limited in its interest value for tourists.  It felt as though you had to go because you're in the area but not worth travelling too far if you aren't.
Local the interesting to meet. Archaeological site with several recovered points .We made the return with a boat tour at sunset
Disappointed  , there was a lot of hype around this but it failed to impress . Long way to go when could have spent time elsewhere. Not worth the time in my opinion

You really need a knowledgeable guide to visit this site. For a World Heritage site, it’s got remarkably little signage or explanations (there are a few signs with English translations). There are signs suggesting there’s a self-guided audio tour, although we didn’t see anyone using it. Regretfully, our guide was not especially knowledgeable, so we missed learning about this important monument. There also seem to be few print or podcast resources about it.

This is the remain of an ancient dynasty in Vietnam. Many of them have over 1000 years history. It is recommended to those who are interested in Vietnam history. A tour guide is needed to provide explanation and talk about the history, otherwise ordinary people don't know what they are looking at.
The entrance fee for My Son felt too high for the size of the site. It was interesting, but not enough to justify the cost in our opinion.

This is an hour's drive from Da Nang/Hoi An and is a fascinating historical site. It was a warm walk in March 2024, so bring water and good shoes.Whilst the history of the My Son Sanctuary is fascinating and incredibly old, it is not well preserved, and there does not appear to be much going on, and  a lot of damage has been done over the years.

I guess it’s worth visiting once. It’s a small site. Historians may want to see at least once. I believe no further restoration would be done and unsure whether existing protection of the stonework is enough.

It is very nice, particularly the drive up from Danang. I am very glad that these relics are being preserved. I give it a "3" is for two reasons. First, many of the relics are almost total reconstructions, based on the archaeologists interpretations. You can tell by the brick. They are probably close, but it still is not the same thing. Second, they are building all of this nice flower gardens around them, which seems more touristy than real.

It is located miles away from Hoi An and every further away from Danang. It took us a good 40mins from Hoi An to reach the temple and upon reaching, we were approached by some shopkeepers offering us free parking, but we wont fall to the trick. We know we will have to buy something. There is parking available for 5000 dong inside, sheltered and safe. The entrance fee is empty and we visited the museum first before we walk to the temple. From the entrance point, we took electric bus (free) for 2km and walked another 400m to reach the temple. It is a big ruin and when I say ruin, I mean it, just ruins. The area used to be a very massive religious complex but great restoration had been done. Most of the ruins were normal looking since I visited the mighty Angkor Wat complex. But we did spend a good 2 hours there. If you never visited Angkor Wat yet, this ruin is a must see. But if you visited Angkor, this can be just another boring ruins.

Hired a private driver to take us to My Son Sanctuary . We did not go with any organised  tour group . This was fine as our driver was excellent and very helpful  The first thing to note is that there is a lot of walking and climbing steps , so if you have bad knees  legs medical condition  it will be very  hard going  especially the long walk back to pick up after the last  site Second  depending on the time of year go very early to beat the heat The  buildings are mostly in ruins due to their age and bombing during the Vietnam war  A couple of sites have been restored but most are not and are somewhat overgrown . The guides who take u around  know there stuff and  their knowledge is good Overall due to the poor condition of the  monuments etc would not bother having now been there
Better, than nothing, but very few buildings and they are almost entirely ruined. If you visited temples in Cambodia, better don't waste your time

Sanctuary has very interesting history, being found many centuries ago and being bombed during Vietnam national war. We did self guided tour and discover many interesting information. I suggest everyone interested in history to visit the sanctuary.

This is a UNESCO heritage site but not as famous like Borobudur or Angkor. In fact till recently I did not hear about the Champa kingdom though i visit vietnam quite often. The trip takes around 1.5 hour from Danang perhaps nesrer from Hoi An not much crowd. We visited around 3pm and watched the dance performance which was ok. Ticket costs 150k vnd per person and includes an electric car ride which can be quite irregular in terms of where they will choose to end. In my case it was much before the expected scheduled point and with a young kid in a hot afternoon this was a bit dofficult. The ruins are under renovation with help from India, enjoyed the serenity but due to the unfortunate war very few relics exists to see now. Guided tour would have been useful ( 100k vnd) but was told to wait for 1.5 hour so had to proceed on my own. Due to the war
Not really my cup of tea. Lots of ruins, interesting for 20 minutes or so. It had been raining so the path was really slippery

We visited the ruins in August. It was quiet and easily accessible. There was a nice traditional music and dancing performance that was really nice but the ruins themselves aren’t spectacular compared to others around the world. It’s a nice to visit but if you are picking and choosing your activities and limited for time this isn’t an absolute must

For history lovers, back more than 15 hundred years and enjoy ancient city, more for adult than small kids or teenagers, bit of waking and sightseeing. Not so many similar places and worth to see when you are in Hoi An

Look I love Vietnam and give full credit for the infrastructure that has been built to protect and support this site, but reviews that compare this to Angkor Wat it anything in Laos are misleading.This is small and in poor condition. If you haven't been to other ruins then it's probably worth a visit but if you have, I wouldn't bother. Spend your time at all the other amazing things in the region.
It was nice to take the bus tour there, I though 2 hours was not enough but turns out the site is pretty small and 2 hours is good enough. A shame that it takes 1 h by bus to get there.

2020年冬のコロナシーズンに訪問。ダナン中心部からgrabで600kVNDくらい。中国韓国人客は少なく、白人ベトナム人日本人が殆どだった。週末だったが、さほど混雑もなし。最古は7～8世紀のものからあるようで、歴史は感じられる。また、周辺は自然も豊か。イノシシの他ウサギやヘビ等も見られる模様。花や蝶の種類も多く、HCMやHNではあまり見られない自然も感じられる。Hoi Anアウトもgrabで、そちらは700kVNDくらい。予算にもよるが、複数人であればgrabやタクシーでもいいかと。

The information on the Cham (Champa) people seems extremely limited. Did they really build all this only to use it just 1 day every year? I should add that the dance and music show was interesting and fun, lots of people enjoying this as well

Me gusto el santuario lo que pasa es que si has visto el de Angkor pues este se te queda bastante pequeño es como su hermano menor, hay muchos templos que estan destruidos pero los que estan bien son bonitos. Se ve en 2 o 3 horas y yo lo recomendaria.
The place is beautiful only we do not have enough time to walk around.  It looks like Anchors Watt in Cambodia.  They are even older than Cambodia but as compare they are smaller.
Voor de afwisseling wel even leuk om te bezoeken als het niet te warm is. Met een goede gids is het wel aardig om de geschiedenis te horen. Anders blijven het een hoop stenen.

I think it really depends on the weather and the interest of the group visiting. It is a large archeological site as other reviewers had mentioned.  The paths between the ruins is accessible by wheelchair but to fully appreciate the details of the ruins, one will need to get up close and personal which means climbing up and down among the ruins which is not so easy for the not so abled bodies.  Even for those who cannot raise the leg to cross the door thresholds will find it difficult. During my visit, as it has been raining, a number of places on the paths were flooded with water. As a result, all out socks were soaking wet after the visit.  If you are into archeology, it is worth visiting.  Do be prepared for a reasonable distance of walking.

Veel is er niet meer over en wat er staat is zwaar beschadigdAls je dat weet dan kun je hier nog wel sfeer proeven over lang vervlogen tijden Als je een goed Engels pratende gids hebt tenminste, anders kun je net zo goed op eigen gelegenheid hier naar toe
Grabタクシーで行きました。帰りのタクシーも、タクシーの運転手が同じ値段でいいよ、まっててあげると言ってくれて交通手段には困りませんでした
It's located in a valley hence very humid and warm. It's all about Champa history temple ruins. If you are interested in history maybe yes, else can skip this.

E' il complesso archeologico più importante del Vietnam. Chi ha già visto i siti cambogiani di Angkor rischia di rimanere deluso perchè il paragone è improponibile; le strutture rimaste in piedi sono un paio poi solo resti. Dalla biglietteria partono dei trenini ad orario, scesi dai quali si deve fare un bel tratto a piedi (evitare le ore più calde). In generale non ci ha entusiasmato forse perchè avevamo termini di paragone di siti meglio conservati visitati in Thailandia, Cambogia e Indonesia.

visited the site, very interesting though can be hot so take water! you an buy drinks there.walk around and admire the 10th century buildings, some have been destroyed by the American war but are being rebuilt to regional design. very quiet place for serenity.

My Son Is a cluster of abandoned and partially ruined Hindu temples in Vietnam, constructed between the 4th and the 14th century by the kings of Champa.Though described as Vietnam’s Angkor Wat, which is hardly appropriate.My Son contains fewer ruins and they are very much ruined. We went with lot of expectation to see My Son but were disappointed very much.The temples are dedicated to the worship of the god Shiva, known under various local names, the most important of which is Bhadreshwara.ASI from India is also assisting in restoration at My Son's temples. This is a UNESCO world heritage site.There is a museum at My Son which is impressive and has many statues from the site, as well as information about the carvings, statues and architecture, and how the temples were constructed. Cham culture, religion and way of life are also explained. There is also a dance programme at My Son which is very good.

It's a small site in comparison to Angkor Wat.  It's about 1 hour from Hoi An.  Entry fee is 150,000 dong.If you can, go before sunrise to avoid the crowds, it's not as hot and you can get good sunrise photos.  India has currently partnered up with Vietnam to restore the site.  Use a guide, otherwise you won't know what the buildings are.We finished the tour in 1.5 hours.

we as a family with teenager,  it seems not much things for her to see.we book a private car with guide for morning half day tour.it seems quite expensive for that tour. but you need guide to explain the old site.we don't like the restoration work, it is not matching to old site.we prefer to see how destroy the site and don't want to see some new bricks add on.

As per other reviews this is probably more suited to archaeological enthusiasts as it’s quite a journey from Hoi An (we got a private taxi that waited there for us for $38). The ruins themselves are interesting but I would advise getting a tour guide as the information there is limited (we didn’t get one). Also, it does get very hot there so bring plenty of water and a hat.

日本語ガイドのツアーに申し込みました。遺跡はかなり精巧に作られていて、日本語ガイドさんの説明も丁寧で、歴史好きな私としては、とても楽しめました。また、入口付近で開催されている、チャム民族の伝統舞踊もかなり良かったです。動きが洗練されていて美しく、思わず見とれてしまいました。ステージに上がられている女性は、細身で小柄でしたが、かなりの筋肉がついていて、動きは優雅だけれど、かなりハードな動きなのだと思います。伝統舞踊の後は、遺跡ツアー再開。少し離れたところにも遺跡がありましたが、遺跡のすぐ近くには、戦争で出来たという大きな穴が……その威力の大きさに、戦争の恐ろしさを感じました。色んなことを考えさせられる、良いツアーでした。ただ……日光を遮るものがほとんどなく、遺跡観光はかなり暑かったです。アンコール遺跡などと比べると、小さめの遺跡ですが、それでも1キロ以上は歩いたのではないでしょうか？観光に行かれる方は、暑さ対策を万全にされることをおすすめします。

The My Son ruins are situated in a beautiful valley. But you come for the last remains of the Cham temples. Unfortunately, Americans destroyed a great part of the temples. Restoration is a slow process and one has to like the way it is being done. This makes the visuals in my opinion less impressive, also because of the fairly small site. However, it adds to the your knowledge of Cham culture to be more complete. Don't forget to visit the museums. The cultural show is nice, but not necessary. Oh, and come early, say 8 AM, so you do not have to worry about all the tourgroups in your pictures, they arrive from 9 AM.

This site might have been impressive once, before it was bombed to ruins, but there isn’t that much left to look at now. It’s at least an hour’s drive out of Hoi An or Danang, so a half-day trip if you’re staying here. I’m not sure I’d bother with it. Nha Trang, awful as it is as a city, has smaller but more interesting ruins (they sit on a hill under shaded trees) if you want a look at Champa architecture but with less of a time commitment.

夫婦でのベトナム縦断旅行でホイアンへ。日帰りのツアーを申し込みミーソン遺跡へ足を延ばしました。ホイアンのホテルから２時間くらいでミーソン遺跡到着。少し歩いて電動カートで遺跡へ、とにかく暑くて大変だったのでカートは助かりました。遺跡そのものは思っていたよりずっと小規模で少しがっかりでした。ミーソン遺跡にいたのは１時間半くらいでした。帰りはバスで９０分くらいのところからトゥボン川をボートで下ってホイアンまで帰りました。日程に余裕があればハイキングがてらに行く程度の感覚でちょうどよいかと思います。

It was interesting to walk around and view what is remaining, but don’t set your expectations too high. It is difficult to read the posted signs in each area, and the restoration/ maintenance of this site is minimal in comparison to something like the Hue Citadel. I would still suggest it is worth a look.
観光地用に整備されていて回りやすかったですが、湿度も高くかなり暑かったです。遺跡のすぐそばを回るときには日陰もほとんど無いので、帽子やタオルなど必須です。今はほとんど残されていない遺跡ですが、全部回ると大変なので、1番の見どころ(一番多く残っている、復元されている)ところだけ見ると大体の雰囲気は感じ取れます。民族の踊りや音楽もショーがしてあり、なかなか楽しめました。暑かったです。

Památky jsou v poměrně špatném stavu, dost se jich opravuje. Vstupné 150 tisíc dongů. Prohlídka tak na 2 hod. Od brány vás asi 1 km odveze elektrický vozík a pak je asi 2 km okruh kolem zřícenin. Byly jsme tam v odpoledních hodinách a byli jsme tam jen naše výprava ( asi 20 lidí) a dalších 10 návštěvníků. Bylo hrozné horko a tak jsme si koupili vodu, která byla nejdražší na celé naše cestě po Vietnamu. Na závěr prohlídky byla ještě asi 10 min ukázka tanců a hudby. Bylo to docela hezké a zajímavé.

This temple is interesting to look around - I do like a good ruin, but it's a bit far away - about an hours drive. It will cost you around £30 in a private taxi to get there and back, plus another £4 each for tickets.If this attraction were in Hoi An where we were staying then I claims gove it 4 starsIf you're on a shoe string budget I wouldn't recommend coming here. But otherwise, if you have the time and money, its alright. There's not much information about the site and what boards there were, were all faded too.  I imagine with a guide it would be a bit better, but then that's going to cost you even more. There's a guy there who will take your picture and print it out for you there and then... at a cost of only 20,000VND... that's like 60p! So that's pretty good!
Heel veel oude gebouwen midden in de natuur van Hoi An. Moet je een keer gezien hebben en dat is genoeg

We’ve been to My Son on a ‘sunrise trip’ that we booked through our home stay in Hoi An. You don’t really get to see the sunrise, but leaving at 4.30am as we did is a great idea as it allows you to get there before the crowd of tourists and visit the Sanctuary in peace (and taking all the pictures you want). Also, around 9am when we were leaving, it started getting really hot so the earlier the better. The Sanctuary is divided into sites, some of them were completely destroyed by the American bombs during the war so there isn’t much to see, but some others are still in decent conditions (or have been restored). The best thing about the sanctuary is its location, as its immersed in a beautiful jungle. Definitely worth the visit.

I resti del santuario dei Champa sono sparsi in una valletta con fiumiciattolo e vegetazione ridondante. Resta in piedi quanto è stato graziato dal tempo e dalle bombe della guerra americana. Caldo opprimente.

If you have seen either Ayuthaya or Ankor Wat you could possibly skip this one. Unless your a history buff. The storyline is quite interesting. Definitely: - take a tour as there are several areas to look at and it is back roads most of the way. - carry some water with you as there is no water for sale while walking around the sanctuary.
Nous avions ajouté ce site car notre vol était en milieu de journée.Il ne présente pas vraiment d’intérêt, même si on n’a pas vu Angkor.

Onder 40 graden in de schaduw was deze excursie niet echt de moeite. Op zich wel, met extra verbeelding, een mooie site. Maar toeristisch enigszins opgeblazen tot iets dat het naar mijn gevoel niet was. Er is echt wel veel verwoest in de Vietnam oorlog nadat de Fransen ook al eerder beelden verminkten om het onder te brengen in het Louvre in Parijs. Toegegeven : we hadden een blaaskaak van een gids die weinig uitleg gaf...

We went here from Danang by bike. It took us about 1.25 hours to get there and it was a nice ride coming in. We left at 7:30 and reached around 9am The place itself is lovely due to the natural scene. Mountains, streams, trees. We even saw a snake slithering away to cross the road.. The ruins are quite basic but considering some of it is from the 7th and 8th century AD, it is worth looking at. The whole site is well taken care of. Only thing was the HEAT. 10 hours later i’m still suffering from a mild heatstroke. The ride back was really hard with the hot sun and wind. Had to stop and cool off every 20 mins. My suggestion is to use an air conditioned taxi especially useful when you return because even at 9am, we were struggling with the heat!

ホイアンのホテルから片道1時間要しました。「遺跡エリアは広いから散策には2時間必要」と聞いていたのでアンコールトムのような広大な敷地を想像したのですが、実際には「遺跡エリアは200メートル四方くらいしかないが(全然正確では無いので注意!)、そのエリアにたどり着くのに時間を要する」という感じでした。具体的には以下のようなプロセスを経ます。・チケットを購入して入口を入ると8分ほど歩いてトラム乗り場に到着・トラムに7分ほど乗車・トラムを降りてから徒歩15分ほどで遺跡エリアに到着。つまり遺跡周囲を巡る前に片道約30分要する訳です。遺跡群は4箇所ほどあり、それぞれ100メートルずつ離れている感じです。ガイドをつけると面白かった可能性がありますが、残念ながら敷地内で誰もガイドの売り込みをしてこなかったので(というか見かけなかった)、案内板に書かれていることを読むだけで回りました。アンコール遺跡やボロブドゥールに比べると規模も芸術性もかなりインパクトが落ちるので、印象に残った場所が無かったです。むしろベトナム戦争時(?)の地雷が爆発して出来た穴の方が面白かったです。ダナンかホイアンで半日時間がある方が訪れれば良いかと思います。

We were there before the ticketing office was opened (opens at 6am). The sun was already out by then. As the site is 2km from the entrance, you need to take a free tram ride to the main site. Once inside, you will see the temples but due to destruction, most of the structures are gone, and some restored with new bricks or layers. The site is not big and you can complete it within 2 hours if you do not intend to stay for the show. Although it is UNESCO site, it is not comparable to those in Angkor Wat. It is strongly encourage to be there early in the morning to avoid the heat and crowd.

From Hoi An this site around 1.5 hours by car. There are golf carts to get you from the car park to the site which is great in the heat. It’s not a massive site and makes for a pleasant walk. Many of the ruins were further damaged by the USA bombing during the Vietnam war which is a real shame. There is restoration underway but it’s a slow process. Personally if you have been to Ankor Wat as we had we found this site less interesting.

You will read many reviews comparing My Son to AngkorWat. Do not get confused. Angkorwat is way better. The temples of My Son are in ruins and widely dispersed. It gives you a good walk through of a historical site but manage your expectations.

We visited My Son from Hoi An, an archaeological site of ancient temples built by the Cham people. It was about a 1 Hour drive through the countryside. There really wasn’t that much to see at the site, a few reconstructed temples, some had nice carvings. Mostly there was still a lot more work to do in reconstructing the piles of red bricks, unfortunately damaged during the Vietnam War. There’s a very small museum at the site which gives a little overview about the building techniques in the Cham people. Once you arrive at the site there is a provided golf cart to drive you up the 2 km to the actual site. It’s uphill and you can walk if you choose, but it’s quite nice to be driven up to the site on hot days. Once you’re out the site, there is a little bit of walking to do but it’s on paved paths. All in all it was an OK half day trip, but it certainly isn’t as good as many other archaeological sites in the country.

Vestiges de la civilisation Cham ou les édifices ont souffert du temps et des bombardements US. L'environnement en revanche est superbe, navettes de bus électriques, spectacle de danseuses Cham. Pour les inconditionnels des vieilles pierres ou de l'art asiatique.

La visita a My Son è imperdibile se... non avete già visto Angkor Wat o se siete appassionati di archeologia o culture passate del sud-est asiatico.È un sito indubbiamente interessante che occuperà circa 2 ore del vostro tempo in un escursione di mezza giornata da Hoi An o da Da Nang.Si può effettuare con un tour organizzato o, piu economico, affittando un auto con conducente che potreste considerare anche per un ulteriore tappa a Marble Mountain sempre in giornata.Il sito ha diversi edifici in buono stato di conservazione anche se le decorazioni sono limitate a differenza invece dei resti parziali di altri edifici che abbondano. L'ottimo museo all'entrata (comprensivo del costo del biglietto di ingresso circa 2 euro) consiglio di visitarlo subito cosi da avere un infarinata sulla cultura Chang.L'incredibile lavoro di restauro portato avanti da squadre indiane, tedesche e italiane ha sicuramente migliorato il percepito del sito archeologico, ma quello che piu amareggia è sapere che quello che non ha fatto il tempo e l'abbandono, o i saccheggi nei secoli, l'hanno fatto i bombardamenti americani.Una buona visita a voi.

I was really looking forward to visiting My Son after reading about it in the travel books. After visiting Angkor Wat in Cambodia a few years ago I think my expectations of visiting here were a bit high. The trip from Hoi An is easy and relatively inexpensive in a car. You purchase your  tickets at the entrance to My Son and an electronic buggy transports you the mile or so into the valley where you then follow the map and walk around the site. You follow the pathway through areas labelled A to F. Each area contain the ruins of different temples. The area was extensively carpet bombed in the 1960's by the Americans in the Vietnam war and therefore many of the temples have been completely destroyed. As a result you can view the site in a little over an hour.  I am glad I visited, sorry I can't be a bit more enthusiastic about what was left.
캄보디아나 인도, 미얀마 등 힌두교권의 유적과 비교해 비교해 보면 공통점과 차이점을 발견하고 흥미로운 곳. 하지만 베트남 전쟁의 격전지였던 탓에, 유적지는 대부분 파괴되고, 제대로 복원되지도 못해서 아쉽다. 유적들과 함께 전쟁의 잔재들도 볼 수 있어서마음 아팠다. 수풀이 남아있는 사람들의 머리카락 뭉치들이 무섭고, 슬펐다.

Si vous aimes les pierres et les histoires...allez y, Si vous aimez marcher et admirer la verdure allez y...Prévoir m/m 1h30..... une petite navette nous emmène au centre du site, vous pouvez aussi y aller à pied.site très verdoyant

This is kind of a different experience. Many of the structures are almost ruins but you can appreciate the ones that still stand firm after thousands of years. It's best not to book a tour since most tours charge about $10 and don't include the 150,000 VND entry ticket. I rented a motorbike and parked for free at a cafe right by the entrance where all buses and vans park. One thing I would recommend, if you go here, is to take a guide. It costs 100,000 for 1 hour but if you don't take a guide you won't really know what's what. The descriptions at the sites don't offer a lot of info about the buildings.The journey from Hoi An to My Son takes about 1 hour. Use google maps for hassle free travel since signboards are a rarity in Vietnam!
There is frankly nothing much to see, if you are not much into history and religion. You can probably walk around a bit and see the ruins, but I didnt find it too interesting.

Interesting to see the ruins, but the area is not so big and as other people have wrote: if you have been to other more known ruins in Cambodia for example, My Son is less important to visit. However, the traditional dance performance was very impressive.

It is such a shame that so much of my son was destroyed by bombing. Only a fraction is left and many of the remaining buildings have collapsed, or have been extensively restored. The decorations are difficult to appreciate given the state of the buildings. Still, it did have a jungle, tropical appeal in the rain, and, at least it's not all gone. The performance of cham dancing was actually pretty good too.
Once it must have been a wonderful place to visit. Unfortunately a big chunk of it was destroyed by the war.
A place for those who love history and archeology. A short trip through remote ruins surrounded by mountains.

Booked a tour to My Son via our homestay (supposedly a government tour, bus there and boat back). Paid 10 usd for travel and 7 usd for entrance. Having been to the Angkor the week before, we were quite disappointed and admittedly also had too high expectations. The site is quite small and there are only a few temples to see which are in bad shape as the restoration efforts are nothing like at the Angkor Wat. If you haven’t been to the Angkor, you might enjoy it more than we did. Overall an okay experience but nothing extraordinary.
かつて栄えたチャンパ王国が作ったミーソン遺跡。信仰するヒンドゥー教の影響を強く受けている。ここからどんな彫刻がチャム彫刻博物館に移設されたか、という旨の説明を受けながら広大な遺跡をめぐることができます。このような説明をちゃんと聞けないと、ただの散歩になってしまうかもしれません。ガイドをつけて訪れることをお勧めします。

I got to My Son at about 8am and was one of maybe 5 tourists in the sanctuary (hardly saw them). It was incredible to have the place to myself and I would insist on getting there early if you’re going to go. By 9/9.30 there were bus loads of tourists arriving and their noise ruined the sanctity of the temples/ruins. The only reason I’m only giving three stars is because there is a tourist centre that plays music way too loud And it is with in earshot of the ruins. Again, ruining the sanctity of the experience.

Aus meiner Sicht eher nur für eingefleischte Geologen oder Historiker.Wir sind heute ca. 15:30 Uhr aus Hoi An mit Moped nach My Son Sanctuary gefahren, sodass wir ca. 16:15 Uhr da waren. Aufgrund der Zeit war es schon merklich ruhiger. Auf dem Parkplatz standen noch ca. 6-8 Busse, aber fast alle Insassen dieser sind uns am Shuttlestand kurz hinterm Eingang entgegen gekommen, sodass wir dann so gut wie alleine unterwegs waren. Alles in allem haben wir den Rundgang in ca. 45min geschafft, was u.a. auch daran lag, dass wir die Tafeln mit Erläuterungen nur flüchtig überflogen sind.Wie gesagt, aus unserer Sicht nicht unbedingt ein Muss.Der Sonnenuntergang auf dem Rückweg nach Hoi An war da für uns schon wesentlich interessanter.
サンダルで行きましたが舗装されていない所もちらほら。エントランスからはカートで途中まで登り、そこから大分歩きます！朝は混むとのことなので、お昼過ぎくらいに行くのがおススメ。

We boxed at local travel agent in Hoi an for a full day trip to My Son, which the guys were really keen to see. By now we were familiar with the format -lots of pickups before you head out into the country and a very controlled format. At My Son Mr Paul  (our guide ) insisted we stay with the group and him but we wandered away and checked out things rejoining the group regularly. The guide'so information was quite repetitive so this worked well. The ruins are interesting as different ages and materials, tour longer than needed. Air conditioning on bus broke down on the way back and became an oven within two minutes. We stood on the side of the road while the driver got busy with a wrench and five minutes later it was fixed. Added to the trip :-D
1人でのんびりと回りました。チケット売り場のトイレが綺麗でした。中に入って カートの乗り降りばのところにもあるトイレも綺麗でした。

There isn't much left in turns of ruins, but they are easily accessible. And there is a very informative museum right at the entrance, so visitors can get well-informed before starting their tour. Guides are also available for hire. To my, however, the most interesting about this visit was the motorcycle ride to/from. With frequent stops, I got a good feeling of "local life".

menarik untuk di kunjungi, tempat nya cukup nyaman fasilitas nya, hanya detail bangunan nya banyak yang masih di pugar dan tidak ada detail penjelasan yang di pajang , sehingga perlu tour guide untuk mengetahuinya.
