Very interesting showing the ruins of the Cham people of the 7th century. The country was once called Champa, which now makes up portion of Vietnam. If history is your interest then the Holy Land is worth viewing. Beware that light walking is required.

It's a fairly long drive from Da Nang and we drove here on scooters. Took us 1.5 hours to reach and when we did it started raining, which was a blessing considering that it was a hot day. Not much to see per say, specially if you're going from India and have seen better sites. We did not hire a guide and just walked around reading the information written. I wouldn't call it great and can be skipped.

To be honest this wasn't a place I would recommend as it was hot, alot of walking and alot of repetition of old ruins but if you like that sort of stuff then you may love it. The best thing was the Cultural dances at the end which I would have to say were fantastic but in hindsight I would have chosen another tour while we were in Hoi An

If you see the Cham museum first, you will have a better understanding of My Son. Many of the buildings are still relatively intact but some are in poor shape. Seeing the items in the Cham museum makes it easier to imagine what a vibrant place My Son once was.

Go in the early morning to avoid heat. Not worth if you already visited Angkor Wat. If you go with motorcycle, please bring along the parking card when enter the motorcycle parking lot, I put it in the motorcycle drawer was missing after visit the ruins, the lady ask me to pay 50K Dong, after some arguement with the old lady and her husband, i pay 5K.

Sito archeologico dalla storia importante, anche se ormai rimane poco di quello che è stato. Importante per la storia del Vietnam, ma per un italiano che ha visitato bene il proprio paese così come la Cambogia o la Thailandia questo sito a mio avviso non dice molto. Se potete evitare luglio e agosto per il grande caldo oppure andateci presto la mattina. Vale la pena pendere una guida.

This site is on a much smaller scale than Angkor Wat or Began. Much of the site was destroyed by bombing by the Americans during the Vietnam war or artifacts taken by the French. There are bomb craters throughout the site. There are only a few buildings still standing. There is a museum just past the entrance explaining what was found in the area. You have to catch an electric car from just inside the entrance which takes about 5 minutes otherwise it Is a long walk to the site.

Mỹ Sơn' quer dizer Montanha Bonita- Património mundial da UNESCO. Aqui temos um conjunto de templos hindus abandonados. Pertenceram ao reino de Champa e foi aqui que foram sepultados os reis da dinastia Cham, que dominaram o Vietname central do sec IV ao XIII. Na minha opinião deveria estar melhor cuidado apesar de estar em curso restauro de alguns templos.
A lot of it seemed to be closed off. But what we did see was beautiful. Car drives you half way there which is nice but seemed odd they didn’t go the whole way.

We booked on the sunrise tour for the My Son temples, however, a delay at one of the stops picking people up from a hotel meant we didn't actually make it for the sunrise. The tour guide was apologetic though. The place was filled with culture and very sacred however, considering we booked an English speaking tour we understood about 25% of what our tour guide said, but give him 100% for effort as he gave it his all, making him very likeable and the experience a lot better!Recommend booking the sunrise tour, you arrive there about 6/6:30 am meaning you miss all the tourists and the midday heat, was worth it to walk around comfortable!
ツアーで訪れました。ガイドの方より説明を聞いたほうがより理解を深めることが出来、興味が沸きます。いつの時代も戦争がありますが、歴史を知ることで戦争の悲惨さを繰り返してはいけないと痛感しました。動きやすい服装、暑さ対策を忘れずに。

The walk from the shuttle bus is way too long for the climate of Vietnam. We arrived completely soaked with sweat. The ruins themselves are nice and interesting, but not so special, compared to ruins in neighbouring countries. If you have a free day it is a nice day out, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit the place. The dance in the visitor center was really nice, and we enjoyed it.
If you have been to Borobudur in Yogyakarta, then My Son is considered amature! Nevertheless, it still has its interesting history and worth a visit, after all it's a World Heritage Site!

At first I was quite interested but after a while it becomes a case if seen one, seen them all and it gets a bit boring especially as most of them are falling down. Take plenty of water to stay hydrated and wear sensible shoes, so many people walki ng around in heels! At the end is a cultural dancing show kind of a cross between Indian dancing and belly dancing, I felt uncomfortable as the girls wear skimpy outfits and the men just ogle them, like a free strip show! Once it is over everyone leaves. Wr arrived at 9 and it was empty but it gets full very quickly and people are not considerate which ruins the photos so go early to avoid the crowds!

Site a 60km de Hoi An, tres abime par le temps et la guerre. Les crateres de bombe subsistent, a cote des monuments qu'elles ont souffles. Ils sont effondres ou penches.Un service de navettes permet de s'affranchir de 2 fois 2km de marche. Petit spectacle de danse Cham sur place.Il y a des ebauches de restauration de monuments, mais les moyens manquent visiblement.
Box ticker. a few hours here will be plenty. Interesting history but because of the age most are ruins in very poor shape. Interesting box ticker however. Opens at 7'ish. Go early.

Le site archéologique est intéressant mais sans plus. Le prix (150 000 VND) est assez cher pour la qualité du site mais il s’agit d’un site classé au patrimoine donc on se dit que c’est comme un don pour le protéger. Apparement c’est mieux avec un guide. Si vous avez déjà fait Angkor wat, la déception sera grande...

We booked a half day tour from the hotel. Everyone was so excited and the tour guide was so nice but we found that the historic temple was disappointing. It’s not really religious at all cause everyone can use shorts or something and the people not really care. And the site is not really nice for viewing and enjoying the history of a hidden dynasty. We went back by boat and they served awful lunch.

Well, if you'll been to places like Siem Reap, or Bagan in Myanmar, then you can probably give this place a miss. There are only a couple of temples to visit and honestly the scale is not as big as Siem Reap or Bagan. Some of the temples are close due to restoration works in progress.

This was a very interesting site to see in the jungle, however, do not do this as a tour. We got picked up at 8am and did not leave Hoi An until 9.15am. The tour itself was okay, but a lot of people and in the extreme jungle heat I would've preferred to get my own transport and see it quicker. The boat back provided by the trip was also disappointing as you could not sit outside and was very slow.Would say the sanctuary is worth a visit but not as part of a tour.

Went on this half day tour today. It was okay. Interesting to see such old ruins, but you can pretty much see the whole lot in about 20 minutes. We got there at about 9am, just before all the big crowds arrived which was ideal. The drive out to the ruins is beautiful with lovely scenery of the mountains and rice paddies. Our tour guide was very friendly and gave us lots of information about the ruins, his English was fantastic.

Good to see especially if you have a half day needing to kill time but don't go out of your way if you are compromising doing other things.Gets very very hot by 10am and you walk alot in order to see much of the same. Our tour guide discussed the 'bricks' and building of the structures so such intensity and on repeat that i now think im an expert. We even arrived back at the hotel an hr early as we were done.Side note- nothing against the site- however ensure your guide doesnt do that gross nasal phlegm hock back because ours did is constantly and at one point i was ready to telp him to drive us back. This is a pet hate of mine and one of very few drawbacks of travelling Asia. (My mum even offered him a cough losenger! Haha)

There isn't much to see in Hoi An.  We stop here en route to Hue. This is interesting given not much is known about the Chams.  Its a really small complex and would recommend coming in the morning when there are less visitors. Wouldn't recommend coming on a day trip from Hoi An, better to do it en route to Hai Van Pass then Hue.

Perhaps what made this experience memorable for us was the fact that we rode a motorcycle to the place.The site contains more ruins then buildings and you can see the craters left over from the bombing in the Vietnam war. If you have seen Angkor Wat in Siem Reap then you might be disappointed with this place

If  this were to be your first EVER visit to an archaelogical/historical  site then you'll probably come away with a fair impression. However to those who have seen or been to other venues (e.g. Borobudur,Angkor Wat, the Pyramids) you will  be disappointed.  In fairness, it must be born in mind that the damage and destruction of the venue during the war left little behind.The venue is however well maintained. All the paths to the site are easy to manage even for those with physical limitations . There is a buggy service that takes you halfway however upon justified request was open to take the respective to the last point.As part of the ticket there was a cultural dance show which was entertaining.The surroundings are beautiful as it is set n the mountains. It was cool and green and there is a lovely clean river that flows parallel to the site. I found it to be a refreshing change from Hoi An and it was quite insightful to have the opportunity to see the local life along the way there and back. I opted for a tour that facilitated a return boat transport to Hoi An. It was dusk at this point and although; due to the fading light, there wasn't much that could be seen in detail as we travelled, it added a relaxing change of pace to the journey. However at the point of approaching Hoi An it was lovely to note the pretty colours the lanterns casted  as we arrived.Generally ,I quite enjoyed this day trip .

Nous avons visité ce site en septembre 2017.  Site  intéressant, mais partiellement à restaurer.  Ayant été endommagé par les bombardements de la guerre du Vietnam  1959-1975.  Prévoir plus d’une heure de marche.

A significant historical sight recognised by UNESCO, this is a great attraction. However its condition is a sad reflection of what it was before being needlessly bombed by the USA. What's left is limited and crumbling, with poor restoration that clashes with the original and is crumbling faster than the original!

호텔에서 예약해서 다녀왔습니다. 저희 가족과 다른 한 팀을 제외하고는 모두 다른 나라 분들 이었습니다. 가이드분이 영어로 이야기를 하기 때문에, 저 말고 가족들은 그냥 사진만 찍다 왔습니다. 전 그나마 재밌게 들었지만, 유적지가 그리 크지 않고, 아직 복구 공사가 진행 중인데다, 날씨도 엄청나서 그리 추천하질 못하겠네요. 전쟁으로 인해 유적지의 상당 부분이 파괴된 것이 많이 아쉬운데다, 유적에 대한 연구도 많이 진행 안된 듯 하여, 가이드를 통해 들을 수 있는 내용도 그리 많지 않습니다. 가는 시간과 비용에 비해 많이 아쉬운 여행이었네요. 이거 오실 시간에 다른 여행 하세요. -0- 정말 궁금하신 분들만 오셔서 들으시고, 나중에 더 개발이 되면 그때 오시는게 더 좋을 듯 합니다.

Koster 150.000 dong at komme ind. Er et lille museum først og derefter bliver man kørt 2 km med el bil til runierne. Det meste blev ødelagte i Vietnam krigen, men de er ved at genopbygge det igen. Er derfor ikke så voldsomt meget at se. Er ingen informationer overhovedet, så man skal have en guide med for at få noget ud af det. Er et danse show der taget ca. 20 min, som er ok at se.

Interesting place to visit, in my opinion the price was high although, I think too expensive for what the tour was. The ruins were very nice and had a very interesting history, but there was no much more to see. There is a show of dancers very nice.

Die Überbleibsel sind schön, der Weg aus HoI An mit dem Roller ist super !! Leider ist man ohne Tourguides aufgeschmissen, weil keinerlei Erklärungen oder Beschriftungen vorhanden sind. Das Restaurant, welches nach dem Shuttleservice kommt, ist eine Katastrophe ! Die Küche ist dreckig, wir bekamen zwei Eiskaffee mit unterschiedlicher Menge und vorher wurde im Kaffee komisch eingewühlt, als wäre ein Haar hereingefallen, welches die unfreundliche Dame herausfischen möchte ..
アンコールワットに匹敵する価値あり、という話もあったので見に行きましたが、規模が小さく、ややガッカリしました。ただ、通常の建築の常識とは逆に、上になるほどレンガの壁の厚みが増していく方式は珍しいと思いました。ベトナム戦争時に米軍に空爆された跡（穴）もベトナムならではで珍しいといえば珍しかったです。

Some old temple ruins. If you have been to Cambodia and Angkor Wat, this is not worth seeing. But maybe if you never have seen anything like it. I booked a buss, but didn’t know I had to follow the guide around. So I just left the group and did my own thing. Maybe better if you go by yourself on a scooter. Overall - wouldn’t recommend people to go and see it.

Sadly there is not much left to see here and it was an anti-climax after Angkor Wat.  We stopped here en route from Hue 2.30-4.30 (closes @ 5) and it was very quiet - ticket price 150,000vnd.  A very peaceful area and lots of signs telling you what would have been here as well as restoration work on-going.

My Son, era un sito religioso importantissimo durante il regno cham (maleo-polinesiano) che dominò la parte centrale del Vietnam tra il IV e il XIII sec.Si trova a circa 45 min. da Hoi Han (40 Km).Questa località circondata da colline nel cuore della foresta tropicale è un insieme di templi che, purtroppo, presentano notevoli danni per la guerra (sono ancora visibili parecchi crateri di bombe), pertanto merita la visita solo se siete appassionati di storia o archeologia perché il livello di deterioramento è altissimo.
保存状態があまり良くなく、修復済みのものは「新築物件ですよね？」と言いたくなるくらい作り直している。

HISのオプションツアーで、ホイアンからマイクロバスで行きました。約1時間ぐらいでしょうか、途中のどかな田園風景も見れますよ。バスを降りると、電動自動車に乗り換えます、ゴルフ場のカートを大きくした感じの乗り物です。思ったよりスピードが出ます（笑）  そこから先は、徒歩で約10分ぐらいかな。アンコールワットに比べると規模は小さいですが、ガイドさんの説明を聞きながら見学。夏場は暑さ対策が必要です。必ず帽子や日傘、ペットボトルの水、女性は日焼け対策。遺跡の近くで、決まった時間に、民族衣装を着ての舞踊がありますが、必見です！思った以上に良かった！  見学のみなさんで手拍子して盛り上がる場面があります。これから見学される方の為に、ここでは書きません（笑）  とにかく暑さ対策をしっかりしてください。トイレは、チケット売り場からすぐの所にあるので、そこで利用すれば大丈夫！

We had already visited Ankor Wat so this complex was rather disappointing on the size especially given how far we had to drive to get to it.  I'm not sure it was worth the time, if you are time limited.
いくつかの王の墓がある遺跡群です。入口から少し歩くと遺跡群までの近くまでは電気自動車で移動しました。民族舞踊のショーも時間によっては見られます。気になったのは、遺跡ギリギリに大きな爆弾穴があった事です。

un sito che ne ha viste di tutti i colori. Residuo di tante battaglie in cui le voci degli uomini che si combattevano e le cicatrici delle violenze subite sono echi imperituri, rimasti tutti nelle fosse di quella vegetazione, tra quei mattoncini che, un tempo, formavano templi ormai quasi completamente distrutti ed impossibili da ricostruire con l'anastilosi. Emotivamente impressionante se si considera le crudeltà degli eventi che si sono svolti in questa area.

My Son was a huge city of the Champa Empire. It was still a huge city being restored until the end of the 60's. But then war happened and most of the buildings were completely destroyed. From the original 71 temples you will find less than 10 still up. There are some restoration going on now and hopefully My Son will revive its best days. But today is a 2 hour visit at most.
完全不能和吳哥或素可泰相比，只有磚砌的遺址，雕刻欠奉。位於山中樹林，雖有電平車但也要走一大段路，而可觀賞的地方很少，拍照也不算特別漂亮。和預期不符。在峴港的占婆博物館還可看到優美的建築構件文物，這裡只餘下磚頭。

Wir fanden die Anlage eher mäßig beeindruckend, zumal es außer ein paar Ruinen nicht viel zu sehen gibt. Das Museum am Eingang hatte leider auch nicht all zu viele Exponate ausgestellt... Somit verblieb dann nur noch der geschichtliche Hintergrund und die Infos des Guides zur Bauweise, Kultur und der Tempelstadt an sich. Für "wirklich" interessierte Leute ist es empfehlenswert.

El viaje de ida con el Bus y regreso con el bote lo organizamos desde nuestro hotel. Esta opcion cuesta 200 000 VND por persona, en comparacion con 550 000 VND en un Tour con Bus (al parecer con un grupo mas chico).Con las dos opciones hay que pagar la entrada al parque (150 000 VND).El grupo era de aprox. 30 personas el guia  simpatico, pero nosotros decidimos seguir por nuestra cuenta. Uno cuenta con poco mas de 2 h para visitar el complejo y luego es el regreso. Alrededor de 30 min en el Bus para llegar a la embarcacion en donde nos recibieron con poco de arroz con verduras (después de la caminata, cae muy bien). El viaje dura otros 60 min para llegar practicamente al centro de la ciudad. En comparacion con otras ruinas el complejo es pequeño, pero interesante.Importante llevar liquidos y algo para cubrirse la cabeza.

Hele middag hier geweest, maar was niet meer dan grote ruïne in een mooie omgeving.  We deden dit als een halve dag tocht met gids. Lange autoreis heen en een bootreis terug 2 uur daar in de hitte. We hadden liever aan het zwembad gelegen.

We arrived at My Son at about 10 in the morning and the place was already swarming with tourists. It was tough to enjoy the ruins to their fullest due to the large amount of people there. We left the ruins about 12 and the crowd had already thinned out. So if you have a desire to see My Son then go when it opens which is 7 a.m. or go later in the afternoon around 3 p.m..

Wij besloten niet met een groep’s excursie te gaan maar zelf op de brommer die kant op te rijden. Het is ruim 1 uur weg van Hoi An maar goed te doen. Daar aangekomen geen gids kunnen vinden dus zelf gaan lopen. De routen is prima te wandelen met goed aangelegde paden. Wat jammer was dat er weinig info bij de tempels stond. Ook waren de tempels die er nog stonden in slechte staat. Het was mooi om een keer te zien maar vinden het geen must. Als je ooit in Cambodja bent geweest kan je dit prima overslaan.

신투어에 신청해서 버스타고 다녀왔는데버스가 좀 낡아서 의자들이 심하게 삐걱거리는 것과 가는 길이 안좋아서 덜컹거립니다만 나름 가면서 구경할 것도 있고 베트남 경치 구경할 수 있구요. 투어비 인당 5천원가량 따로 입장료 따로인데 입장료가 15만동으로 생각보다 비쌉니다...그리고 덥습니다. 덥고 많이 훼손된 건물들이 대부분이예요. 앙코르와트를 다녀오신분은 굳이 안가셔도 됩니다. 조금 실망스럽더라구요. 빨리 복원이 되면 좋을 듯 해요.가고 오는길에 전기차 타는데 그거 시원하고 재밌습니다.

在一路怀疑是否走错路中，终于到达美山圣地，一个占婆族的圣地遗址，极富历史感，经历了十几个世纪的大自然和战火洗礼，剩下的每一块砖都是无价的宝藏，值得全人类好好保护。 这里是占婆王国时期留下的最古老最庞大的建筑群，要不是在几十年前的越战中遭美军轰炸，现在只能看到废墟和砖块，我们能看到的建筑会是现在保存的三倍有多。去过吴哥窟的人，这边是小巫见大巫而已，规模和完整性差很多！我们很高兴看到当地人为了防止遗址再遭到破坏做了保护工作。除了建筑，这里还有少量的文物展品，而另外的大部分已经转移至岘港的占族博物馆，我们决定隔天就追随去看看。还有一些则收藏于巴黎某博物馆内，例如那些“缺失”的头。

Ich bin mit dem Roller hingefahren (1,5h) und war letztendlich enttäuscht. Der Park ist zwar schön und gepflegt aber die Tempel sind entweder massiv zerstört oder werden restauriert. Kein Vergleich zu Angor Wat, wie es ab und an beworben wurde.Eintritt 150.000 DNG

かなり苔生していて残った部分も煉瓦を積んだだけの印象が強く、細かい彫刻とかはあまり発見できませんでした接着剤を使用しないで積み上げてあるとの事だったので凄い技術だと思いましたまだ発掘中との事で歩道はきれいに整備されています日焼け、暑さ、寒さ対策を忘れないで出掛けてください1日何回か、無料で観れるダンスと音楽が実施されていますカート乗り場にあるレストランぽい場所はシャッターが下りていました旧正月明けすぐだったからかもしれません

If you have been to Angkor Cambodia or Borobudur/Prambanan Indonesia, you could be disappointed.Essentially one of many historical sites of the Hindu civilisation, this had been badly damaged by time and the wars. There is not a single completely intact structrure and is quite a drive and walk, altho the sanctuary is serenely surrounded by hills.
現地の激安ツアーで行きました。1,200円くらいでしたが、エアコンバスでの送迎と帰りはホイアンまで川下りでした。とにかく暑くて41度あり、倒れるかと思いました。帽子やお水などの対策をして行かれた方がいいです。英語が分からないと、面白くないので日本語ツアーで検討される事をお勧めします。

The complex is quite large and the setting very tranquil. A lot of the temples were destroyed during the war and there are some large bomb craters left. What remains is interesting but there were a lot of tourists there so patience is needed. All up our tour to, from and around the temples took about 4hrs but our guide was moving quickly.
遺跡までとても遠くて…心配したら、お手軽カートが来た。運んでいただき、感激。遺跡近くでは、現地の民族踊りも披露された・遺跡は、ベトナム戦争で、あれて、たいそう傷んでいた。大事な遺産が、もったいないと感じた

Acknowledge Many of the project you couldn’t implementElectricityMunicipalityIdeas how to overcome these problemsProjects are not aligned with ICRC strategy (schools)Solar panels (be more detailed/elaborate on proposal)Document more the problems/the impactUnderstand better the impact/output of the projectsNot consistent motivation. Take time The time to take actionRented a bike in Danang, stayed overnight in Hoi an for new years and roadtripped to My son. We left at around 2 in the afternoon to avoid the touristic crowd from Hoi and reached there around 3. Not much scenery on the way.  The ticket costs 150 dong (around  8$).Another timing can be at sunrise. To make it short any easy for you if you ever visited Angor Aat then drop My Son unless you are person that just ticks off attractions.The ruins in themselves will not add to your experience or knowledge unless you have guide in front of you to explaining (there is at the entrance to hire)Wham impressed me was the location of the ruins surrounded by a jungle (honestly I considered it more a short hike than a sightseeing.)Last words: think twice before you go.
既然是遺址了，那肯定是比較破舊了，不過應該會和你的想像有點出入。如果去過吳哥窟的話，覺得可以忽略了這個景點吧，當然這是個人認為了，當然從歷史上面很有不同了。

If you have a knowledge of Hinduism, this might be for you. Unfortunately many of the Temples were damaged by US bombing. Peaceful, apart from the endless selfies. If you have been to India, then don't waste your time. The Museum is by far the most informative part of the visit.
その日は夜にホイアンに行きたかったので、ダナンからわりと近いミーソン遺跡に。行ってみたら以外に広くてびっくり。カートに乗って回るのかと思ったけど、最初の入り口だけで、その後は結構歩きます。
這美山遺址與想象中有點出入，就是遺址中大部分的建築都已損毀，破敗不堪，較為完整的只有一小部分, 算是有點小失望吧! 雖然如此，但若果未有看過類似的遺址，還是會推薦到來!
遺跡としては大変きれいな形で残っていて興味深いです。道が整備されていて、森の中を散策するように歩くのが気持ちがよいです。カートに乗ることが以外に案内されていなくて、歩いてしまっている人が大勢いました。
Very cool buildings and all but it would have been nice if there was a little more that was explained about there role in history. In the jungle so mosquitoes everywhere

This is a historically interesting site to visit, but if you've been to Angkor Wat and Angkor Thom in Siem Reap, it's difficult not to make the comparison, and My Son becomes "just okay". Having said that, it's certainly a site to visit if you are interested in ancient ruins. It's still amazing to think about how these temples were built so many centuries ago. I visited with a friend, using a private car and driver from Da Nang which cost about USD$65. Not a bad way to spend an afternoon strolling through the area.
This is a rather small "Angor Wat" - but it is worth a visit though and is a good place to have a strolling
VND 150,000 entrance fee was quite worth it. Nothing spectacular of course compared to Angkor Wat. Slightly overrun with tourists even at this time of the year.

Hätte mir etwas mehr erwartet. Gebäude sind schon beeindruckend, v.A. Die Landschaft drumherum und die Tatsache, wie die die Gebäude gebaut haben.Haben das ganze ohne Guide gebucht, vll lohnt sich einer, der einem mehr erzählt - nur aus dem Reiseführer lesen ist zu wenig.

Ни в коем случае не сравнивайте это место с Ангког Ватом, оно и близко с ним не сравнится.Мишон полностью разрушен. Вход 150 000 донгов (~8$)Ехать из Хойана на байке 1 час.Есть платная парковка, но можно оставить внутри комплекса за шлагбаумом бесплатно.Погуляли меньше часа, удовольствия практически не получили. Комплекс абсолютно разрушен.

Santuario caratteristico e segnato profondamente dalla guerra , non vi sono più particolari edifici ma fondamentalmente solo rovine. La location è particolare in quanto immersa nella foresta e il sito è tranquillamente raggiungibile in scooter da Hoi An con 1 ora 30’di tragitto...
The pictures make it look like more than what it is. Most of it is in ruins and there is very little information around to tell you about the history. Don’t rush to see this.
ダナンから1時間ちょっとでした。世界遺産ですが、こじんまりとした遺跡でした。歴史好きな人には楽しいのでしょうが、私には少し物足りなさを感じてしまいました
五行山からタクシーで一時間ぐらいはかかって到着。見学は工事などでDエリアまでしかできませんでした。修復中はまさにゲームの世界に入った気分です。

In the past this place was probably a beautiful sanctuary, while unfortunately right now only ruins and bricks can be seen.The sanctuary’s visit covers different spots but only one is satisfying and gives visitors a good idea of how the sanctuary’s architecture looked like before being bombarded.

La balade est agréable (prévoir casquette, eau et bonnes chaussures) car si le soleil est présent, les températures grimpent vite. Les temples sont en mauvais état (si vous connaissez déjà Angkor, vous serez déçu), mais c'est agréable de se balader au gré des ruines et de découvrir la culture Cham.

You'll use up a half day of your holiday visiting My Son if you are staying in Hoi an. The temples are impressive but in bad shape due to the American bombing and lack of maintenance. Nearly as impressive as the temples is  the overgrown remnant of a huge bomb crater as you approach the sanctuary. There are a lot of temples (around 20) on the main site and you can see they would have been impressive in their hey day but time and troubles have detracted from the specatcle. Its probably unfair to compare this site with Siem Reap's Angkor Thom but they don't stack up to say Bayon or Ta Phrom or Banteay Srei. But remember these temples are from the 4th/7th century onwards - significantly older than 12th century Siem Reap. The temples aren't all labelled and there is relatively little by way of information about the buildings. Worth it? - you'll have to go yourself to decide that.
This place is OK for a day trip, i wouldn't consider it the must thing to do in the area. We went on a really rainy day and it took away some of the places charm.

We were told if you are going to Angkor Wat then don't go. It was pretty hot and to be fair our tour guide was not that good but lots of personality. We wandered off and listened to another one for about 1 minute and learnt more than 30 minutes with the other one. If you get a good guide it would make all the difference. Our tour included a boat tour and carpentry village which you could miss. Overall it was ok as it's part of Vietnamese history. It's a pity America bombed the hell out of it and destroyed 95% of which is tragic really.

We participated in a tour to the My Son sanctuary. I loved the area, the history and the obvious peacefulness. I grew weary of hearing the high level of political rhetoric and believe the negativity contradicted the Hindu beliefs and spirit. Our guide may have been knowledgeable but his constant anti-western comments spoiled the trip. I would say this reflective one bad experience except I overhead equally negative comments from other guides. If I had a mulligan, I would still visit MySon, but not with a guide.

Via een plaatselijk reisbureautje ochtendexcursie geboekt. Helaas hebben we zelf weinig tijd gehad om rond te kijken, na 1,5 uur moesten we weer vertrekken! Als ik dat van te voren had geweten had ik het met een privechauffeur gedaan!
Pas désagréable, cela permet de faire une ballade en moto, mais si vous avez visité Angkor ou si vous projetez de le faire, ce n'est pas incontournable...

You could not spend extensive time here due to the small size of the ruins. The ruins are interesting and there is a small museum at the site.It's for the individual to weigh up whether it is worth travelling (most likely from far away) to visit here. For me it was not.

Mooie plek. Er is best nog wat te zien. We werden om 8.30 al getrakteerd op een dansvoorstelling. Leuk om met een persoonlijke gids door heen te wandelen. Let op dat je er niet midden overdag rondloopt! Het kan er verzengend heet zijn!

わたしが遺跡を見に行きたいということでツアーで申し込み、お昼ご飯抜きの5000円ほどのツアーで行きました！案内してくれるのは、日本語を話すベトナム人の方で、すごく分かりやすかったです！残念なところが、⚫︎雨が降っていて、全然雰囲気が違いました⚫︎ツアーで申し込むと、自由時間が15分しかないので、写真をゆっくりとれません。⚫︎観光客が多い良いところ⚫︎ベトナムの歴史を感じられる⚫︎ジャングルみたいで別世界ぜひゆっくりしたい方は、ツアーに比べてタクシーで行った方が安いのでないかと思います！その際はしっかり調べて言ってくださいね！必要な物虫除けスプレー日焼け止めウェットティッシュ多少のお金があればわたしは大丈夫でした！ガタガタの道が多いので酔いやすい方は酔い止めを！
Es muy bonito el entorno, aunque las ruinas son pequeñas, por eso si ya habéis visto otras como las de Camboya o las de Tailandia y tenéis poco tiempo, no son imprescindibles.
Ruinas y entorno bonito, si no has visto otras ruinas os guardara, si conocéis algunas ruinas podéis prescindir de visitarlas

My son bezocht. Complex is erg klein ten opzichte van het veel grotere Angkor wat in Cambodja. Helaas staat er ook nog maar weinig overeind. Wanneer je in de buurt bent dan zou ik het bezoeken. Wanneer er speciaal voor komt, dan valt het een beetje tegen. Bij Angkor wat valt My son in het niet.

Similar in design to the Angkor Wat buildings but much smaller compound. It was ungodly hot that day and we arrived at noon which made it worse. Thankfully it only took about 1.5 hrs to tour it all with plenty of stops for water and sweat breaks on my route. It's worth the drive if you have a few days in Hoi An or Da Nang. The buildings are interesting to see, unfortunately the bombing of the war has also destroyed several of them.
유네스코로 지정되어 특이한 볼거리가 있으나너무 땡볕이고 덥고 가실때 꼭 양산을 쓰시길...너무너무 더워서 지칠수있어요.
I been there twice, they are working hard to restore each building. Good experience . Must go with a tour guide to get historical information.

Sau nhiều lần trì hoãn, tôi đã quyết định một mình đến với Mỹ Sơn - Di sản văn hóa thế giới.Thời điểm này là tháng 5 nên cảm giác đầu tiên là rất nóng do khu di tích nằm trong thung lũng. Khách tham quan hầu hết là khách ngoại quốc, rất ít người Việt. Dù có xe điện trung chuyển miễn phí, xe chạy liên tục mỗi khi có khách nhưng du khách vẫn phải đi bộ quãng đường khá dài giữa các đền tháp nên nếu có trẻ em sẽ rất mệt.Kiến trúc rất đẹp. Đáng tiếc là hầu hết đã trở thành phế tích.Nếu muốn đến đây, các bạn nên chọn thời điểm mát trời một tý.
せっかく来たからと訪れました。世界遺産。確かに神秘的な感じはしますが、急ごしらえで観光地化されちゃった感じも。歴史に想いを馳せることはできますが、二度は行かない場所かな？訪問の際は、ツアーをオススメします。タクシーでも安く行けますが、待っていてもらわないと帰りの足がなくなります…お互いに母国語じゃないので、ここは安全策を。
比较小型，一个小时可以完全游览所有的地方。相对安静，从岘港过去坐大巴车需要一个半小时，如果没有去过柬埔寨的吴哥窟，又有多的时间，可以来看看
Можно съездить для разнообразия. НО жара, руины, толпы туристов - подумайте дважды, на мой взгляд - не самое приятное времяпрепровождение

The ruins at my son are not as good as many ruins of similar age in thailand or cambodia. There are courtesy carts which take you half the way from the gate to the ruins but you still walk another 2 km in a very pleasant valley but the walk was a chore in hot humid weather.The trip to my son from hoi an takes an hour each way. I would prefer a half day at the beach in future.

We drove motos from Hoi An to My Son heritage site which was a lot of fun through the little towns and through the roads. The heritage site is very touristic, compared to the other Champa sites there is not much left of it, due to destruction during the Vietnamese war. The landscape and hills are beautiful and we moved through the site pretty swiftly.We walked 4km up the hill from the car park to the site itself which was nice though there is a free shuttle bus which takes you to and from. I enjoyed the landscape and journey there more than the UNSECO site itself. The exhibition museum near the entrance gate is not particularly informative nor impressive.
J'ai apprécié le musée car sans guide cela est plus facile de comprendre le site, une partie est en restauration.
タイ、カンボジアの遺跡と同じ、やっぱり東アジア特有に石の積み上げ。どうしても隣国の遺跡を意識してしまいがちでした。もう少しミーソンについて知識を蓄えてから行くと面白さが倍増しそう。

My Son is tiny compared to Angkor Wat so you need to put this into perspective before you visit. Buses stop in a car park (avoid the dreadful toilets!), then you walk for a few minutes to get into golf buggies which drive you up to the site ruins. You can see all of it within an hour but it gets crowded though. It was 37 degrees when we went but there are places to buy drinks both at the start and close to the ruins themselves.

We visited My Son with Sinh Tourist Hoi An, we were picked up at the hotel and dropped at Hoi An ancient town. We have been to Borobudur, Angkor Wat and Bagan (found out and they are all related). It was hot and humid day in My Son, from parking location had to walk about 15 mins to electric car station then continued by electric car (about 10 mins ride) and another 20 mins walk to My Son ruins (20 mins walk in hot humid day is hard thing to do). Overall it is nice to see but unfortunately smaller than expected.

This was very interesting the information that we received and to see the temple ruins, but I expected more. Pity the places were so badly damaged with war, show was good. I would recommend if you have time

Some really nice scenery. Unfortunately our tour left later and it was really too hot to enjoy the tour. Especially when the guide decided to stand in the sun rather than the shade to describe what we were looking at. I found out later that some tours are available earlier in the morning 5AM so as to avoid the heat.

Overpriced for what it is. A small scale of the Angkor Wat in Cambodia.Beautiful decaying temples, but the whole site is so small, with really only one main area to look at.Beautiful mountains nearby. Would have been very peaceful back in the day.Can get around it within half an hour. There was a great short show with talented musicians and dancers in a sheltered area to watch.Hard to get a photo without 30 other tourists in it.Shame the US bombed it back in the war, remnants of bomb craters there and of course they blew up some of the temples... :(

Costruito tra il IV e il XIV secolo dopo Cristo, è stato il cuore politico e religioso del regno Champa, uno dei più importanti del sud est asiatico. E’ probabilmente il complesso di templi induisti più importanti al mondo ed era composto originariamente da 71 templi. Gli archeologi hanno suddiviso le costruzioni in 10 gruppi denominati con le lettere dell'alfabeto, dove i più importanti gruppi archeologici sono i gruppi B, C e D. Essendo stato costruito nel corso di circa dieci secoli, My Son presenta diversi stili architettonici, accomunati da una notevole perizia tecnologica e di progettazione. I templi sono interamente in mattoni con colonne in pietra e bassorilievi raffiguranti ambienti naturali della regione e icone della mitologia induista. Per questo motivo, il complesso è considerato uno straordinario esempio di interscambio tra cultura indigena e spiritualità indiana. Patrimonio dell’Umanità Unesco, My Son è stato scoperto all’inizio del ‘900 da archeologi francesi e riveste un ruolo fondamentale per comprendere a fondo la cultura e la società del sud est asiatico. Le distruzioni della guerra hanno però causato  gravi perdite ed oggi, purtroppo, è possibile ammirare i resti di solo 25 strutture.Questo sito di My Son non mi ha però entusiasmato, perché faccio dei confronti. L’anno scorso avevamo visitato lo spettacolare sito di Angor Wat in Cambogia (dove hanno ristrutturato molto), mentre qui i templi sono stati ridotti in briciole dai bombardamenti e non hanno avuto probabilmente la possibilità di fare opere di ripristino. Durante la guerra in Vietnam i Vietcong si nascosero infatti tra queste mura e alcuni templi, tra cui quello più grande e più bello, vennero distrutti dai bombardamenti da parte degli americani, dalla compagnia Charlie. Nel piccolo museo sono raccolte foto dei lavori di restauro effettuati, oltre a piccoli pezzi archeologici e  mascheroni.

Wir waren für 1 Woche in Hoi An und haben einen halben Tag genutzt um My Son zu besichtigen. Wir hatten einen privaten Fahrer, das war sehr angenehm. Wir sind absichtlich erst gegen 11Uhr am An Bang Beach gestartet und wie geplant kam uns der große Pulk entgegen und es war recht ruhig an den Tempelanlagen.Es muss ursprünglich eine wirklich großartige Tempelanlage gewesen sein, die leider im Vietnamkrieg zerstört wurde.Dennoch sind auch die Ruinen sehenswert und eine tolle Fotokulisse.Der Besuch war wirklich interessant und angenehm.Vom Eingang zu den Anlagen legt man einen gemütlichen Spaziergang zurück oder man lässt sich von einem Shuttle fahren (wir waren die einzigen zu Fuß).

My Son sanctuary is an incredible place with rich history. It was a bit too hot for me to have a spiritual experience but it is that kind of place. Stop at the Museum at the base before you proceed any further to read about the historical and religious significance. Better yet, read before you go. It is truly a remarkable ancient archaeological site.

Os desfalques dos franceses e as bombas americanas deixaram pouco a ser visto em My Son. Para mim, que viu antes Bagan, Myanmar, My Son, infelizmente, não impressionou tanto. O lugar está arrumado para visita. Ônibus elétricos levam você até o centro do santuário. O melhor da visita foi o guia, que era muito bem preparado e explicou bastante sobre os templos.
Is slightly far from the city. Best to go in the morning else it will be very hot in the afternoon. Better engaged tour guide to understand the history and layout of My Son.

If you are into ancient architecture and culture then this area is worth a visit. An hour out of Hoi An, this religious and cultural site was mostly destroyed during the American war. Pleasant location, some noisy groups, but efficiently operated. We really enjoyed the boat ride back into Hoi An which was offered by our tour.
在會安住宿時，住宿地方都可以報名美山的行程，蠻方便的。有分成兩種類型，一個是搭車去搭車回來(中午一兩點就會回到會安)。另一個是搭車去，搭船回來(會比較晚，有含午餐)。這邊晴天太陽很大，出發前或是到那都可以看到很多人買了大瓶裝水一直喝，記得要防曬，免得回去也曬傷了。這裡的規模真的沒很大，但是對這方面歷史遺跡有想了解的，也是種體驗。

It is a nice place, but we had already been to Ayutthaya and Angkor, so My Son was just very small and unimpressive. We didn't think it was worth spending 2 hours to get there and go back to Hoi An, and also the 500 dong we paid for the bus ride and the entrance fee.

If you've already seen places such as Angkor Wat, Bagan, Prambanan and Hampi then you may well be underwhelmed by the visit to My Son. There's not really much left of the various temples, in part due to the bombing by the US during the Vietnam War. Add to that the hordes of tourists who will probably be there at the same time as you and the whole experience isn't really that great.

Visited the site as we were told "have to". It was interesting to see how the Cham people managed to build like this with the tools I assume they had 1000 years ago. However, you need to be very interested in history and old cultures to get the best out of a visit like this. Sad to see the destruction the US bombing caused on the site.

Visited here today, driving there was hectic almost died like five times bear in mind we had a little child in car, also witnessed a crash. Views were lovely there and there was a shuttle bus to and from which was nice. Unfortunately torrential rain ruined it lol

Wir sind von Hoi An aus mit dem Scooter (ca. 1.5Stunden) nach My Son gefahren. Mann sollte unbedingt erst nach dem Mittag hinfahren um den Riesen Ansturm der Touristenbusse zu vermeiden. Wenn dann vor Ort ist, braucht man ungefähr 1Stunde um die Tempelanlage anzuschauen. Es ist ein Besuch wert wenn man genügend Zeit hat, ansonsten würde ich eher mehr Zeit in Hoi An verbringen.
ツアーで行きました。ガイドさんは流ちょうな日本語でした。途中で雨となりびしょぬれになりました。カッパと傘は必須ですね。靴も晴れてればいいですが雨の可能性があるならぬれてもいい靴で歩きやすいものを準備すべきです　カートに乗ったり、歩いたりでゆったり楽しめます。ツアーの場合は一緒のグループになる人たちとの相性も影響しますね。

We went there with a tourism company Phong Ve. It was the worst tour ever! The guide didn't speak proper English and the lunch was more like a snack. The nature and ruins are worth a visit if you would go there yourself and try to avoid tourists (go early in the morning or in the evening) cause it tends to get very crowded.

Said to be the most important temple site of the ancient Champa kingdom it is hard to imagine what it must have been before the Vietnam war. Most of the temples are reduced to rubble, less by the centuries since they were built but by the US bombs. Sad, but still worth a visit if you are at Hoi An.

Die Ruinen sind in einem eher desolaten Zustand. Die Anlage ansich ist interessant und die Aussicht auf die umliegenden Berge ist schön. Wer Angkor Wat in Kambodscha und die Ruinen in Ayutthaya Thailand gesehen hat, kann sich diesen Ausflug sparen.

My Son Sanctuary dates back to the 8th Century and provides an interesting look into the lives of the Champ people. The ruins are in rough shape however, many damaged by bombs from the Vietnamese/U.S. War. It's also a good 75-80 minutes drive outside of Hoi An. If you have limited time or if you're planning on seeing Ankor Wat in Cambodia, you might consider skipping this attraction. If you're not going to make Ankor, than this attraction will give you a taste of ancient temples.

Unfortunately the day wer visited it poured with rain making the walkways muddy and slippy.The temples are OK but just a few, the museum at the entrance was interesting and the dance performance was good.Worth a visit but don't expect too much and take good shoes, not flip flops

ホイアンに並んでいる旅行代理店で一番安いところを探して予約しました。行きバス帰り舟遊びつき　12万ドン生き帰りバス　9万ドン時間があったので舟遊びつきを選択。行きは大型バスでホテルまで迎えにきてくれ車内でミーソンの入場チケットも販売してくれます。ミーソンに入場すると博物館があり電動カートで奥の遺跡エリアへ。期待していましたが思いのほかこじんまりとした遺跡ですこしガッカリでした。帰りのバスに乗ってホイアンまで4kmぐらいの川辺まで来てから船に乗り換え。ランチ付きでごはんの上に野菜だけの中華飯風の飯とペットボトルの水がでました。ニョクマムが美味しいのかしょぼい割に味付けは良く美味しかったです。乗船時間は1時間ぐらいでナイトマーケットの川側に着けてくれます。

My Son è il sito archeologico più suggestivo del Vietnam, immerso in una verde vallata è anche una piacevole passeggiata.Purtroppo il tempo era brutto e quindi non abbiamo potuto goderne appieno.E' rimasto poco di quello che doveva essere un enorme complesso, la guerra ha fatto parecchi danni, ci sono anche dei crateri provocati dalle bombe a testimonianza.Alla fine del percorso si può anche assistere ad uno spettacolino di danze e musiche popolari molto piacevole.

Reminiscent of other ruins around the world My Son is set in a very developed and organized site.  It's historical significance is probably worth the visit.  We booked a private car through the hotel which made it an easy trip.

Worth every second. It’s a 2 KM walk through several sections of ruins, each unique and interesting. It is quite hot, but there are plenty of places to buy water or a cool drink. Don’t miss this place

Decided to grab a Grab to the site. Someone warned me Best to be there early as it gets very hot. Arrived at 8 am in the morning and it was already starting to get very warm.The beauty is we had the place almost to ourselves no buses or tours had yet turned up. Decided to rent a pushbike and now realise for the price. There was no real benefit, it was cheap. For those of you who like a little bit of danger in their lives I totally recommend buying the pushbike because the brakes don’t work.Got a walk around all see all the relics and ruins,it was very solemn and definitely worth a trip. only an hour out of Da nang, you have the option of having a Tour Guide or using the app for a guided tour. I didn’t do either and still thoroughly enjoyed the morning dress light and casual and wear some good shoes for walking.

We had a really good family experience at Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary. It was a hot and sweaty day with lots of walking, but the sites themselves were fascinating and well worth the effort. There were plenty of food and drink stops along the way, all reasonably priced, which made it easy to keep everyone happy.The souvenirs were a real bargain too – we picked up four hats for under £10! The cultural show was a highlight, with slick performances, excellent dancers, and talented musicians.Overall, a memorable visit and one I’d highly recommend to families looking for history, culture, and a good-value day out.

Amazing temple ruins made from bricks that we don’t understand today (apparently). The new bricks being out in for restoration get moss covered and age 100 timesFaster. Blows the mind that these temples were built over a 1000 year period. We hired a tour guide and at 100,000 VND it is well worth it. Intimate knowledge of the era and culture of the time. Such a shame much of the site was destroyed during the Vietnam war. There is a free mini show. Nice to watch but could easily miss it. Be warned, obviously it is all out doors with little cover. Be mindful of the weather. August 2025’was BRUTALLY hot.

Best to book a guide or a tour, easy to do once in Vietnam. There is an audio guide and some signs, but to learn the full history a guide is better! There is a traditional dance performance a few times a day, and plenty of shops to buy cold water! We went in the afternoon, which was great as it was very quiet and could wander around without any people there, but it was very hot!

The ancient Hindu temples are impressive, even in their ruined state. It was fascinating to see the history nestled in the quiet jungle. It's not as grand as Angkor Wat, but it's a beautiful and historically rich site worth visiting.
Visit My Son Sanctuary's ancient Hindu temples amidst jungle mountains for a tranquil, historical experience. A cultural must-see in central Vietnam.

The Shrine of My Son is an exceptional UNESCO World Heritage Site in Vietnam, offering fascinating insight into the history and culture of the ancient Champa civilization.The sanctuary originally included more than 70 temples and towers, organized into different groups. Today, about 20 structures remain, having withstood natural disasters and war damage.About 40 km from the city of Hoian Tariff for Foreigners: 150,000 VND/adult, 50,000 VND/child.Opening hours: 6:30 - 17:30 (Monday to Sunday).

We left our hotel in Hoi An at around 10am, saw the Mỹ Sơn ruins and got on the 4am flight to Da Nang airport. I took a shuttle car to and from the hotel airport in Hoi An. The tour time in Hoi An is about 3 hours. We had a lot of fun.

They are largely ruins of ancient temples of the Champa people. Much has been restored, but there is still much work to be done. Much was destroyed during the war. Interesting in itself to know more about the life of the Champas, who have now almost completely disappeared from Vietnam

A must to go with a guide so you understand more of what you are looking at. Lots of walking but worth it if you like history. Dance show included but try and get a front seat as people stand up taking pictures and filming etc. Ruins are fascinating.

This is a historic place, near the city of Hoi An, composed of a set of Hindu temples built between the 4th and 16th centuries by the Champagne kings and which was almost destroyed in Vietnam's war with the United States. Some countries have sponsored its reconstruction. The landscape is beautiful, in the middle of the jungle and with a warmth typical of these equatorial latitudes. There is a very enjoyable show, with native dances that tell a little the history of the place.

My Son is a well worth visiting, but would be uninspiring if you had previous visited Siem Reap on the same trip.  Visiting requires a fair amount of walking so mobility-challenged visitors will probably want to give it a pass.  The sights and sounds of the surrounding jungle are very nice.The Cham dancers were interesting but the viewing is impaired by the seating design.  The floor is flat and the stage is not particularly raised so it was difficult to see.  Our experience was made worse by a couple directly in front of us who spent most of the performance taking photos and videos.  The upside was that I was able to see the performance through their phone screen.

Beautiful area with many bird species and interesting and beautiful ruins. The walkways are well maintained and easy to walk along. During our visit there were not many people there so the atmosphere was calm and harmonious. Well worth a visit.

A good hour on a motorbike out of Da Nang, you'll find My Son.It's the relic of a Cham settlement and dates back to between the 4th and 13th centuries.  A fair bit remains. Even more would still be standing if it were not for American bombing in the Vietnamese War.It's an unesco site, the conservation of which has been 2funded, strangely, by India.The entrance fee is đ150,000.It's in an au natural state, which makes for a pleasant couple of hours.Motorbikes should disregard the beaconing from cafés as you enter the site, and pay đ5,000 at the entrance....

Absolutely stunning place. Wanted to explore for much longer & get to all the ruins, but was on a walking stick due to knee replacement & bad back. A electric cart takes you part of the way, but there is still a long walk before you get to the main ruins. I would suggest that they should offer a electric cart all the way to the ruins for people with poor mobility, so they can explore more. Even if they charged a small fee for the service. I was lucky to grab a lift back on a electric cart that was passing by. Wish I could have explored more.

We were staying at a hotel in Hoi An in August, which is our fault  - August is not the perfect time to visit Vietnam, but it was the only time we could go.  It was incredibly hot and humid, so we chose a tour that picked us up in Hoi An between 5:30 and 5:45 am. I thought it was definitely worth the trip - about an hour by van from Hoi An.  The site was amazing.  It was very uncrowded at that time, and the heat was almost bearable, but we did miss the folk art performance, which I understand was a pity to miss.  Our guide was a native but spoke English with an amazing Australian accent.  Our tour did not include the entry ticket (which we should have noticed beforehand but booked this tour only the night before), but I think the tickets were only about $6 per person.  Luckily we had some cash.  Our guide spent quite a bit of time talking about the different restoration efforts that had happened - the one led by a Vietnamese group (which our guide said - his words not mine - was a total disaster), the one led by an Indian group, which was meh, and the one led by the Italians, which he said was quite good.   I do like history, so this tour isn't for everyone, but I really enjoyed it. Our guide also spent some time talking about the French (who currently display a few of My Son's greatest treasures at the The Guimet Museum in France, which my guide would like to see be returned to Vietnam) and the Americans who bombed the site during the Vietnam War.
Historical tourist destinationTìm hiểu giá trị văn hoá lâu đời của người Chăm, vì sao gọi đây là vùng đất tốt, trù phú, nền văn minh của người Chăm

Amazing ancient history that has been painstakingly preserved, lots of information about the all the years of change and eventual destruction, the museum has plenty of fascinating information about it all

We took a tour from Hoi An. It included pick up (Central City only), guide, driver, Bahn mi, and boat ride at the end. We opted for the afternoon which was hot. We took umbrellas which were invaluable for keeping the sun off. Our friends went the day before us and had rain and told us to take the umbrellas for that as their tour didn’t supply them. I recommend taking them either way. The ruins don’t compare at all to those around Siem Reap in Cambodia. There are a handful of temples and associated buildings that have been partially restored and others that are being rebuilt at My Son. If you’ve been to Angkor and surrounding temples, don’t expect the same. It’s a far cry from, and inferior to, them. The grass around the temples was pretty long and it would have added to the experience if it had been cut back. I also understand that you used to be able to walk into Vietcong tunnels that are on site but which aren’t currently open. Again, having those open would have been a great addition to the day, but that was sadly missing. The entrance ticket was 150k dong for 16 year olds and up and 30k dong for those under (for the electric cart). That’s about right for what you get. Our guide was great and informative. I also recommend reading up on the sanctuary before you go. There was a 15 minute free dance show on when we were there. It was pretty good.
Ancient ruins in a well-maintained area. An hour away from Da Nang. Worth a visit.  Bring an umbrella during the summer since it’s hot.

This is Vietnam’s most significant ruin from the ancient Champa Kingdom, which prospered from the second to 15th centuries.  My Son was established as a religious center in the fourth century.  The name means Beautiful Mountain.We learn about the history of the complex and its surroundings during a guided tour, which gives us time to walk among the red-brick ruins and admire the delicate masonry. This area was a North Vietnam army staging area.  This location was just on the other side of the mountain where the Ho Che Men supply trail ends.  Air recognizance could not see the location for all the trees.  I may have previously mentioned that the US used a defoliant (Agent Orange), but we learned they also dropped acoustic monitoring devices that would detect voices and mechanical activity, and thus pinpoint where to bomb next, and did exactly that at this location.  Unknown to the US the area was full of these ancient temples.  Word got back to President Johnson that the bomb drops at MY Son were causing concussions that were causing the structures to collapse, so in that light Johnson ordered no more bombing in this area!!!!!  The area is pocked marked with bomb craters, and there are signs off the paths warning of unexploded ordinances.  Sadly, out of the 70 original brick structures here at My Son, only around 18 remain standing for Tom to study.What Tom did not like was the Chinees tour guide girls with the loud speaker, but what was worse is when the Chinese male tourist talked towards their family / friends as they yelled out loud like he was mad at them (We have found in our trips to China that is just the way they converse)!!

Beautiful sight with a history that has been honored. We travel from North to South and are sometimes shocked at how in Vietnam they can turn beautiful things into something very touristy and cheap. That is not the case here. We left our hotel in Hoi An at 7:30 am and that is a perfect time. Our tour guide was also very well versed in history. Regardless of its history, the Park itself is highly recommended. Definitely go.

An optional extra excursion as part of a guided tour.  What an amazing find out in the jungle!  Incredible that so many remain, and many in decent condition, despite the bombing from the war 50 years ago, with many bomb craters seen next to the temples.  A very extensive site which is being repaired and restored with assistance from other countries.  Would have been a sight to see when it was in it's heyday.  Lots and lots of walking, in extreme heat and humidity with little shade.  The theatre production of traditional Champa performances at the end was overcrowded, not helped by inconsiderate people who placed bags on chairs.  Thank heavens for the electric trolleys to ferry people from the entrance to the start of the temple walk.

It was a wonderful view! It's a place where you can feel the history.No matter where you take a photo, it's picturesque.There are a lot of tourists, so if you take a photo, you need to time it right so that people don't get in the picture.It's very humid and you'll sweat a lot, so make sure you have a drink...!
Exploring the ancient Cham temples at My Son Sanctuary was fascinating. It felt like stepping back in time and learning a piece of Vietnam's history.

We arranged a driver from our hotel in Hoi An to visit My Son and really enjoyed it.  A very peaceful place, with lots of old temples, though some had sadly been damaged over the years.  A really worthwhile trip out, there’s plenty to see.

My Son Sanctuary is one of the existing architectural complexes of the Cham Dynasty. Visitors were simply attracted to its high historical value as it was the most successful and prideful product of the Cham Dynasty.

Ticket 150,000 Dong. You take an electric shuttle and after 2km you arrive at the entrance. A 15 minute hike in the sun and you arrive at the temples of area B-C, those found intact. In March it was 32 degrees and very humid. There are so many tourists, it's hard to take photos. It's best to go at the opening to avoid the heat.
Small but nicely cared for archeological site. Unfortunately, so much was destroyed in the war, but there are structures in a reasonable state to get an idea of what it was like.

The memorial - the temple ruins are from about the 4th to the 12th century? - it is not certain when the facility will be completed. The wife and I were there on a tour for the first time already in 2007. Since this time, the sanctuary has been continuously restored and has become a real sight for anyone interested in cultural history. The Vietnamese have turned the place into a tourist attraction - not only for tourists from Hoi An but also for visitors from Danang. There are many different offers to experience the place; the offers depend on how much time and what type of means of transportation you want to use! We ourselves chose a minibus to the sanctuary and a tourist boat with lunch service back to the city. All this service made the trip very enjoyable, - and this for a reasonable amount. You should study the offers before you decide! I am attaching some pictures from 2007, so that you can see the progress; this time there were also still bomb craters between the temples and no proper roads, no fencing, no kiosk, no toilet.

This is a large site with lots of walking. There is also a short show with three numbers. Most of the sites are the temples in various stages of decay. They are reconstructing many of them.  Most of the statues and the good ruins are at the Cham Museum in Danang. Luckily we had been there so the site made more sense to us. It is interesting how our guide showed us the craters, bullet holes and damage caused by the American bombs g during the war.

A bit over crowded, so get here early if you can, and go straight to the site before the tours overwhelm it. The small museum adds context but can be seen afterwards as most groups seem to skip it. The short cultural shows are worth catching.Not many intact sculptures are left, due to time, collecting  and US bombing but well worth a look.The small museum of Cham art  in Hue as part of the Royal Antiquities museum is worth a look too and has good context and labelling.
Great hours in really beautiful surroundings. We drove there from Hoi an, took about 1 hour. Can be recommended, we had children aged 5 and 8 with us.

My Son is one of the most important historic sites in all Vietnam. It's located in the highlands, an hour drive inland from Da Nang.I suggest making your itinerary visiting first the Marble Mountains (read that review) and after that proceeding here. Both sanctuaries are on the same way and both belong to Champa people who ruled today's Vietnam from 4-17c. This would be a very interesting all day out excursion from Da Nang.We organized it with our Grab driver who we met on Day 1 in Da Nang. As he was a very charming and kind person, he became our private driver for the following 5 days making agreements for the following day at the end of the day.My Son was Champa's main sanctuary dedicated to Lord Shiva, as well a political center and burial ground for the Royalty. Any comparison with Cambodia's Angor Wat is inappropriate and misleading, just like calling every city with canals Venice. Angor was built by Khmer people in 10-12c, while My Son is older - first shrines dating back to 4th cent and being enlarged till 14 c by Champa people. The size and beauty is also incomparable in favor of Angor Wat, so if you've visited Angor before you might be disappointed with My Son.Nevertheless, if you lower your expectations you will be rewarded with terrific shrines and great time exploring this ancient civilization.After the ticket booth there's a small museum to give you a glimpse of the site and Champa people. After that, a  golf cart transports you further up where's the site. The area is pretty big, you need around 2 hours to complete the tour. We joined a terrific guided tour with detailed explanations. Originally there were around 70 shrines, but sadly more than half were demolished or heavily damaged during bombing in Vietnam war. Several bomb craters you can see walking the site.Today, My Son is recognized as a UNESCO world heritage site with ongoing excavations and improvements with local, Polish and Indian archeologists.By the ticket booth is a bar and restaurant where you can refresh or have lunch.
Amazing place to visit, really old temples in a spacious hidden forest, Plenty of shade and spots to have a break. Nice gardens and everything is well maintained.

Staying in Hoi An and took the taxi out to the site. My family loves to be out doors and walk (2 adults 3 children) so this was perfect for us (if you don’t like walking it’s not for you). Lots to learn, amazing photos to take and beautiful surroundings to be enjoyed. There is a cafe on site and we enjoyed some icy cold drinks after our walk. Would recommend not going on a super hot day.

An hour drive from our hotel in Da Nang. We booked our ticket in advance; we didn't take the package with the tour guide option regrettably. The sanctuary is huge, and visitors will be transported via an electric buggy or golf cart-type vehicle. The location can be extremely hot depending on the weather timing; fortunately, the weather was good when we visited. There are scheduled traditional shows and souvenir shops around. It was a good experience to explore this historical landmark in Vietnam.Tip: Be sure to bring an umbrella and sunscreen. Get hydrated, especially during a summer visit.

Although badly depleted through bomb damage it remains a fascinating place to visit. Our local guide Hai brought it very much to life with his knowledge and interesting stories.  The tourist show at the end was absolutely marvellous and was provide by really excellent entertainers

We needed to move from Hoi An to Hue so we organized a drive to stop by at My Son on the way by Hura Cars. Once you get to the entrance, you walk a bit and come to a mini bus terminal. Mini bus takes you to the real entrance where walking tour starts from. The mini bus seems to leave when some of the number of tourists gathered.This is one of the world heritage in Vietnam. This sanctuary tells you that Champa people lived here with beautiful cultures. The buildings were mostly ruined due to the time and wars. Vietnamese government is working with Indian authorities to preserve but the speed or enthusiasm didn’t look great. This place reminded me of Bagan in Myanmar.What we really liked here was Cham dance show. The dance and music was beautiful, lively, elegant but strong. The movement of dancers fingers were amazing. The lighting with too strong colors looked tacky though.T ry to arrange your trip to be in time for the dance, the performances take place 4 times a day.It would be very hot with strong heat and you walk a lot. Please pay attention to your condition.

The Sanctuary is well set out with a visitors centre and carpark well away from the ruins, with a shuttle to get you there. This meant that the area around the ruins was peaceful.They have a small performance by Cham dancers and orchestra. This was enjoyable and colourful although it could have done without the light show as it detracted from the performances. It was also the most crowded area.The ruins are a collection of 7 different temples from across the centuries. All were heavily bombed during the American War with some being restored, some under restoration and some little left. Still our guide provided background on the Cham people, the building techniques and religion.It is worth a visit but do come prepared for the heat. The ruins are in a valley surrounded by hills which seems to keep the heat in and any wind out. Definitely bring water, sunscreen and even a umbrella.
Entry costs 150,000 dong. It's definitely worth a look. There's a tour of the entire complex and at the end you can watch a show if you have time.
L是由占婆一眾國王在公元4世紀至14世紀期間建造的一系列印度教寺廟遺址。入場費150k vnd /人， 在內看到有印度政府資助資料紀念碑。有博物館可先了解一下資料，當然也可降温一下。此景區提供免費電屏車到遺址出發點，2公里，約5分鐘車程。有部份已完成修建，可欣賞整座建築物嘅外型及雕像，我們這些門外漢，也看得興致勃勃。未曾修建的就來想像一下模樣。繞了一圈約個多小時回到車站。

We loved this experience. We hired a private car to get us there and back (and wait for us). It ended up being a little more than if we went with a tour however would strongly recommend it- as it allowed flexibility and comfort.The Sanctuary was very well executed. Great roads, walkways and bridges, Cafes were always nearby as well bins.The actual ruins were amazing and you can't help but feel a bit of loss at the ruins and resentful of the bombings. The size and details of the ruins were pretty extraordinary. A major note- if you do not have good knees or legs- do not go.There is definitely a lot of walking and climbing involved. It was a great outdoor activity and we went during a heatwave.One thing I would consider to do differently- hire a guide to explain the ruins in more detail. We overhead some guides and the extra history and info sounded great.
These temples would have been amazing before the war, but a lot of the buildings were destroyed by the US troops. A bonus is the electric carts to get you most of the way to the top.

Très beau temple à visiter, c'est l'un des seuls temples indous au Vietnam.Il a toujours des restaurations et de l'archéologie sur ce site.Compter 1h30 pour la visite, des navettes électriques à l'entrée vous y emmènent.L'entrée est de 150k VND par personne
Zeer goede impressie over het Champa volk.  Zeer goed bewaard bouwwerk.  De wandeling door het volledige domein is een verpozing.  Ook optredens van het Champa volk in een overdekt theater

My Son, is about 1hr - 1hr 15min from Hoi An by car. The time is based on what seems the speed limit of 50km and navigating any free space you find on the road, whichever side of the road you find yourself on.  Just don’t stress about the driving… just think of a beep on the horn as, hi I’m here … think about forgiving and being forgiven … and all will be well.The ruins take about 2hrs from leaving the carpark to returning to the carpark (no queues in Feb). It involves buying your ticket at a kiosk before entering (vnd 150k each), showing it to the man at the entrance … walking 5 mins to the transfer station.  On this section you can choose to visit the museum on the right, which gives you a little more insight to the structures that you will come across… we found it better after visiting the ruins, but each to their own. The transfer shuttle takes you 2km to the start of the trail (5mins) where there is a souvenir shop, cafe and toilets (they were pretty clean, but wet as they wash them down).From here you can also hire a Guide for between vnd 100-200k (we were told), but none were available, so we could not confirm this, but we didn’t need one, between reading some info plaques and info gleaned from the museum (suggest ringing and booking a guide if you want one).  Its a further 10min walk to the ruins.  We walked around for about 1hr 15mins or so and then headed back.Not much shade except underneath the odd trees.Temperature was about 26deg C, i understand it gets hotter in the following months.Ground is firm and paved in various methods, but don’t think it is too friendly if you are disabled or struggle walking.I think tours start are around vnd 600k with boat ride and lunch, but if you sampled Hoi An / Vietnamese food and have been in a boat ride, a private car (if you don’t have transport) could be an option to get there and back quicker than an organised tour which could go around the hotels / accomodations picking up clients and dropping everyone off again.Private car was vnd 1.8 million return (they wait for you at My Son).The ruins were interesting to see some of vietnam’s past, however the ruins do not present any wow factor if thats what you are looking for.I would give it 3.75 stars if i could.

we had an English speaking guide from the Trails of Indochina so were well informed   If felt a bit like Indiana Jones in Vietnam, there was a team still excavating one of the temples  The 1 hour drive from our hotel was just as much fun, you drive thru countryside and little towns where people were drying rice right on the sidewalk  There is an open bus that takes you from the parking lot to the site of the temples

The place is surrounded by green trees and mountains. We went there on a day with nice cool breeze and not too strong sunlight so it was nice. Good to walk around and change atmosphere from Hoi An town.  However, it may be a lot nicer uf there's nice cafeteria with good food of reasonable price.  We rented a car for a private family trip and it wasn't easy to find a gokd place to have lunch there.

My day tour to My Son is provided by Vacation Indochina Travel ,HanoiYou guessed it it not only rained it was torrential and so much so that all cloths including shoes had to but through the dryer at hotelHaving said that and apart again from my disappointment based on weather and I may add restoration  work I did enjoy the few hours I spent at My Son exploring. Just wish I could have spent more time but based on weather and slippery conditions it just became unbearable unsafe.Restoration work is based on war damage but to me the letdown is that the light brick used for restoration just doesn't appeal and distracts from the original works. Personally I think it should have just been left as was as nature and war intended .Having said that it is amazing to see some carvings on the structures of the UNESCO world heritage site and to think that from the 7 - 13th century I am able to see part of it.Lunch is included in this tour at a local restaurant before being transported back to hotel at Hoi An

The My Son ruins are a one hour run in a car from Hoi An and in my view the trip is well worth the expense, even if you are hiring a driver and not opting for the alternative which is a tour group being brought to the site in a minivan. The entrance fee is 150,000 VND and there is some pressure to hire a guide if you do not already have one. I do not think that a guide is necessary; there is some good information on the internet and the whole site is well signposted and even the Lonely Planet guide book tells you enough to understand the Champa civilization and these ruins. I recommend starting with the exhibition near to the entrance before getting the free shuttle buggies to the various sites. There is a cultural show which is worth watching, as much for its value as a rest between walking to the various Group sites. It is heartening to see the ongoing work being done to restore some of the temples after the damage inflicted by the US bombing. There are places to stop and snack and importantly there is a free buggy ride back to the entrance at the end of your tour.

Petit tour tranquille sur le site de l ancien royaume de champa. 150 000 Dong l entrée. Le site est grand et agréable a visiter même si les monuments ne sont pas exceptionnel non plus. Pour avoir visiter des autres sites de ce genre. Mais ça permet de passer un bon moment dans la nature.

The temples are amazing. We paid for a tour guide at the front office. We were put off by our guide asking straight away that the fee we paid was only for the government and we needed to pay her separately. We were taken aback as we had paid an entry fee and for a guided tour. Through out the tour a Hindu man overhead the information our guide was giving us and came to correct the woman. This was slightly awkward.  We put it down to the translation from Vietnamese to English. The ruins and their history are amazing… the constant hinting for a tip was not so much. At the end of the tour, our guide asked for a tip. As in Australia tipping is not usual, I stated I would ask my husband and she stated she would come back with us to get her tip. I ended giving her a small tip and then she left. Ruins great, guide experience, not so much.

A wonderful place to visit if you have the time. The story behind its history is fascinating and I appreciated the multi-country effort currently underway to restore it.The "cultural" show was pretty terrible... and almost downright uncomfortable to watch which is why I removed a star. I felt like they took the history of the Cham People and then tried to Vegas it up in a tacky and inappropriate way. I'm pretty sure the ancient Cham didn't have disco lights and red sequins. The end of the show felt like a cheap belly dance strip tease because of the outrageous costumes and lighting.

Read up about the history before you go or have a decent guide, otherwise you will not gain much from the trip except a long walk under the sun. Take the shuttle from the main gate all the way in (there’s a refreshment stop midway, nothing there). There’s cultural show (of sorts), check the time schedule if interested.

This is accessible from Hoi An and Da Nang although closer to Da Nang.  My best advice is to get here as early as you can - it's really warm and humid and there is not a lot of shade.  They have a shuttle to take you from the main entrance up to the site but there is still a fair amount of walking to be done which is not difficult - just in the heat, it could be more challenging.  While nothing like Angkor, it is worth seeing!  We had a guide with us so we were able to learn about the history but without a guide, there isn't much information at the actual ruins but there is a very good visitor centre before you reach the ruins and that is where you will find a layout of the area and a history.  You only need a couple of hours here - that should be plenty of time to get to the visitor centre, and walk around the ruins.

My Son was built by the Champa people some 1,500 years ago and it shows their strong Buddhist beliefs : lots of Sanskrit inscriptions, statues and Shiva lingas. Apparently it was a large site with more than 70 temples, but a lot got destroyed by American bombs when the Vietcong hid in it. Only a couple now remain more or less intact. Restorations are ongoing with involvement of the Indian and Japanese governments.Obviously it bears no comparison with Angkor Wat, but I thoroughly enjoyed visiting the site. It's a pity the local Vietnamese seem to have very little interest in this important cultural heritage site (I was strongly discouraged by the locals to visit - they said it was only a pile of stones).

This was a nice day out for us. We travelled from our hotel. Anantara. With our guide, we visited the Champa Kingdom My Son. After we got there, you will go on a little shuttle to the site. You will definitely need a guide as you would not have a clue, what is what. We first watched a show of dancers for about 30 mins, which was entertaining. Then off on our tour. It was very hot, but we had a great time. Such a shame the site was deliberately targeted by the enemy all those years ago, and much was destroyed. Very informative tour.
ヒンドゥー教のシヴァ神を祀ったチャンパ王国の聖域。レンガに接着剤を一切使わずに建てられたという不思議な建築様式です。ベトナム戦争で多くの遺跡が破壊され、現在もなお発掘と修復作業が続いているそうです。遺跡を飾るアプサラ像や、美しいレリーフなどの装飾も見所。

WARNING! If you have already visited Angkor Wat, give this temple a miss, as it will be EXTREMELY underwhelming. Several people on our tour did just that and were disappointed with the experience. Luckily, I had not, and very much enjoyed this first step in temple-visiting!I booked a sunrise tour of My Son through Anh Khoa, which included a small breakfast, bus ride to and from the site, and a guide for the morning. I recommend taking the sunrise tour to avoid overcrowding which can occur during visits later in the day. Besides one or two other couple visiting with a private guide, our small group of 12 were the only people on site, besides some archaeologists working on restoration. We did miss the sunrise, but I blame that on poor weather conditions, and not on the tour itself. I would also recommend visiting this site WITH a guide, or with a good knowledge of the history of the area, as the information provided on site is either lacking, or TOO informative. One plaque described the specific placement of each of the buildings in group C in relation to each with SUCH DETAIL, but explains little of the history or function of the buildings. Not very helpful unless you are trying to build an exact replica!The buildings, in various states of disrepair and restoration, are slightly removed from the parking and entrance. An electric shuttle whisks you up the hill, where you can walk around the various areas, in and out of many of the buildings, and then walk down a very smooth walkway to a cafeteria/giftshop, where the same electric shuttle will whisk you all the way back down again. This is a good location for people with reduced mobility, but still able to walk short distances, on slightly uneven stone paths. The surrounding views of the mountains are also amazing.Note: the coffee at the cafeteria is bad, and doesn't even come close to Vietnamese standards. Don't waste your money.

Ruinen mitten im Dschungel, leider hat der Vietnamkrieg Spuren hinterlassen, trotzdem unbedingt besuchen. Man sollte gut zu Fuss sein, denn nur einen Teil der Strecke vom Parkplatz zu den Sehenswürdigkeiten kann mit einem ElektroShuttle bewältigt werden. Auf die "Tanzshow" im Eingangsbereich des Geländes kann ohne weiteres verzichtet werden.

We enjoyed visiting the ruins of this Khmer community. The restoration work is a bit incongruous with the original works. There's certainly no attempt to make it look original. However this location is pleasant and the buildings are very accessible. The performance at the end of our tour was a good way to end it off.
sehr sehenswert, schön wären  genauere Beschriftungen der einzelnen Gebäude und deren Verwendungszweck damals in mehreren Sprachen

If you decide to visit My Son just outside Hoi An, do it as a tour. Our tour included a boat trip back to Hoi An which was a nice way to end the trip. The guide was very informative with regard to history and the various construction types at the site.

Easily reached on motor scooter from Hoi An or on an organised trip. Impressive collection of ancient structures which make the most sense if you take the time to go in to the museum at the start of the trail. You get a sense of these buildings in a broader south east asia context. Not easily visited except on foot, although they run you up and back from the entrance gates to the start of the ruins (2kms).  If you do ride up there on scooters, there's a good guy who allows you to park your bike at his cafe rather than pay the VND5,000 parking fee; in the hope you'll have a beer or coffee when you return. He doesn't charge more than fair price for his drinks so worth supporting the local business

1999年に世界文化遺産に登録されたミーソン遺跡。６世紀から１３世紀頃まで栄えたヒンドゥー教のシヴァ神を祀ったチャンパ王国時代の聖地で、煉瓦造りの祠などの遺構が残っています。ベトナム戦争でほとんどが破壊されてしまって、今も修復が行われています。壁面に施された彫刻の数々は素晴らしく、チャンパ文字を見ることもできました。チャム族の伝統舞踊が１日数回行われていて、時間ぎりぎりだと席がなく後ろで立ち見になることがあるので、早めに行かれることをオススメします。

My Son Sanctuary located in Duy Xuyen district, distance Hoi An 25 km.Its so beautiful and you should visit it in afternoon to avoid the crowed and hot air.The ticket is 150.000 vnd and its also inclued bugy car.The information for someone want to discover by themselves

Mooi tempelcomplex dat we bezochten met een bus. Tijdens rondleiding ook nog een dansvoorstelling gehad. Surreëel zijn de bomkraters tussen de tempel ruines. Ik dacht altijd dat het verboden was cultureel erfgoed te bombarderen. Blijkbaar hield niet iedereen zich hieraan.
In ruins but a divine place for the deities.serene surroundings and intricate works in the temples. Mostly similar to each other but quite historic.

nice and calm and serene, with amazing history, ruins in the middle of a jungle, so much green, the walk through the jungle was nice and the weather was cooler than Danang and other part of VN.. very sad history though.There were some restoration going on while we were there, hopefully they will complete next time we come back.. there were also some local tales about ghosts haunting the place but no one really come fwd with anything too scary, would love to hear more about it

Although we left our hotel early and visited My Son as part of our tour, it was still very busy.
