There was a short walk - about 10 minutes, across a bridge, to the point where the electric vehicle (free of charge) took us up to the temple complex. I don't think you would want to walk this bit. It is a long way to walk in the heat. There were very few people. I wonder if people try to visit early to escape the heat. The walk towards the temples was amazing - there were really loud noises of birds and insects. The temples are extraordinary. The craters left by bombs have not been filled in. We were aware of the singing and dancing performances but did not watch. We had a coconut drink from the small booth on the way back. Very atmospheric day out.

My Son is pretty amazing.  We got a Grab driver to take us there and he waited to take us back while we walked around.  We chose to get the audio tour.  This was really good - a bit long winded - but definitely worth it to explain things.   It was VERY hot as My Son is mainly out in the open with little shade.  Recommend taking lots of water and an umbrella.  There is a little shop at the start of the ruins walk (the shuttle bus drops you here) where you can get ice creams and cold drinks.  Overall we really enjoyed My Son and it took us about 2hrs to wander around and watch the show.  Well worth it to get out of the city and see the countryside.

It takes about an hour to ride here from downtown Hoi An. The entrance fee is 150,000 dong for foreigners and 100,000 dong for Vietnamese. After entering the park, go to the museum to read the introduction, then walk a short distance to the shuttle bus station to take the free shuttle bus into the ruins area, and then walk around the ruins. There is a performance venue at the end of the ruins with performances at fixed times every day. , the last show is at 3:30 pm, after watching the show, take the shuttle bus back to the starting point

It's incredible to see the ruins of multiple Cham Pa people temples (Hindu temples) in the middle of such a lush jungle.Make sure to go early to avoid the crowds, otherwise the amount of people is unbearable. Going early also helps on hotter months as it will be a little bit fresher.

Truly a discovery that we would not have thought of! Magnificent archaeological site classified, like Angkor in Cambodia and Ayutthaya in Thailand, as a UNESCO world heritage site.Some very beautiful complexes in this valley surrounded by mountains built between the 4th and 15th centuries by the Cham people (of Malayo-Polynesian origin) of whom it was the capital of a kingdom (that of Champa) stuck between the Mongols to the north and the Viets, and to the South by the Khmers.Small electric vehicles will transport you from the parking lot to the entrance to the site itself. The fact remains that you have to walk again, in the heat that can quickly become overwhelming (you should definitely bring enough to drink, even if there is a restaurant area on the site).

My husband and I visited this splendid archaeological site. Our added value was the guide we had who showed us the site and the various buildings.The cars are left in a car park and you then take an electric car before continuing on foot to the site. The walk certainly wasn't pleasant in August, but it was worth it without a doubt. At times there were waves of tourists, but I still managed to take nice photographs while waiting for the right moment.
Amazing experience. Go very early to avoid the crowds. Watch the sunrise n wander through the ruins. Worth the early morning wake up call to see these monuments.

A stunning place, rich in history and hidden in the forests of Central Vietnam. I recommend travelers to visit very early in the morning to avoid the crowds of tourists coming in tours. I arrived at 6.00 am and there was no one else, it felt very peaceful.

Really glad we went to this. We hired a driver to take us from Hoi An.  Spent just short of 2 hours wandering about.  You get a buggy to the main bit and we didn’t have to wait long for it.   We got the audio but it was difficult to hear on our phone and it was too hot to stand around for long listening to it.   Some fab sights to see.

Very interesting. Not frowded, so we got to see everything and get good photos.  The audio guide tells stories but thin on detail.Well worth the trip if you're into history. Plenty of places to eat & drink within the park, and they're not a tourist ripoff. Recommended!

These ancient temples are seriously impressive - all grandiose and majestic. But half the fun was finding them hidden in the middle of the jungle. It felt like we were Indiana Jones minus the booby traps (thankfully). History's cool, but it doesn't have to be stuffy. Our guide was great – super knowledgeable, but also cracked a few jokes about the monkeys who think they own the place.

My Son is a monumental complex of temples of the ancient Champa civilization, dating back to the 7th century AD.It is in a remote valley in the middle of the mountains about seventy km away. from Da Nang. From the entrance, you can get there a little on foot and mostly with an electric shuttle.Bombed for a long time by the Americans, because the Viet Cong were hiding there, it is still being renovated.Three restoration techniques: one Vietnamese, one Indian and one Italian. Guess, according to our Vietnamese guide, which one was better…Numerous craters still present, caused by bombs.All fascinating.

If you are interested in culture, history and landscape, this is the right place.It is a large complex (we only had 2 hours and therefore couldn't look at everything in peace) with many temple ruins. The audio guide is very comprehensive and informative. The landscape is also great: jungle all around and lots of dragonflies, butterflies and lizards. I also saw larger spiders (I don't like spiders), but they were off the paths between the trees and didn't bother me.The cultural show that takes place at certain times is great! There are two traditional dances and a music show (together about 15 minutes) that are very nice to watch. It's free and I highly recommend it!Of course, the temple complex is not in perfect condition, as it was largely destroyed by the Americans during the Vietnam War. Nevertheless, we found the ruins very impressive and beautiful!

Book a very early morning tour (it opens at 6am) and arrive before the masses to enjoy the peaceful surroundings with the birds chatting away!We booked a 5.30am collect from Hoi an arrived by 6.45am with no one else in the car park!Our guide lead us around the site and we had plenty of time to relax and take in the surroundings and wildlife.Interesting site dates back to the 4th century but also scarred by the recent war with craters and bullet holes!We left as the large amount of others tourists were arriving.. well worth getting up for highly recommend

Even if you feel "templed out" after visiting Angkor Wot & Angkor Thom, please make time to visit My Son if touring Cambodia & Vietnam.We organised this tour from our hotel in Hoi An. It takes just over an hour each way and cost $35. We were escorted by a very knowledgable guide who spoke excellent english.The site is of an manageable site and was not too crowded when we visited in mid February 2024. Tip: There was a musical performance whilst we were there and we took advantage of this diversion to get our photos when others were distracted.What a shame that such an historic site, that survived for well over a thousand years, should be so badly damaged by bombing in the Vietnam War. Some restoration has had to be undertaken to preserve the remaining structures. One temple has been reduced to a pile of rubble and the custodians are yet to decide what course of action would be appropriate.We worth a visit.

There are some temples here but sadly some destroyed in the war. However, definitely worth going to see the site and those there. There is some walking to do, although some terrain covered by small transport but if it is a hot day you will feel it with the walking required.

My Son (pronounced “Me Son”) is a former Champa historic site that was severely damaged during the 1960s and early 1970s. What’s left is nicely presented. For those with phone service, there is an audio tour that will talk you through the place. While not as impressive as Angor Wat, it is an amazing cultural site. Recommendation: If possible, visit the Cham Museum in DaNang before going to the site. All the major pieces are located there and found visiting helped us understand it better.

My son is a UNESCO heritage site, but quite secondary in group tourist circuits, so we opted to get there by scooter from Hoi an (about 1 hour), at the time when the groups finish their first visit (around 12 ). Entrance fee 15 thousand VD, immersed in the greenery of the jungle among flowers and trees, it is certainly a place where you can breathe peace. There is a lot of walking to do and the first stretch (2 km) can be done comfortably with an electric buggy which circulates every 2/3 minutes. Unfortunately the temples were destroyed and damaged by the bombings of the war. Sacred and mystical place.

The most important archaeological center of the Cham civilization in central Vietnam. There are ancient Hindu temples ruined by American bombing (some bomb craters can still be seen). With the help of international organizations and some states, recovery work has begun.Despite everything, very beautiful, thick with vegetation, very hot and high humidity, not very crowded. To be recommended

A great historical site to visit. This tour was made more interesting by our tour guide who has a very good communication skills (i mean for a vietnamese speaking english -- he is top notch.) and was able to fully understand the tour very clearly. He is Bary -- As he would like to be called. He is very accommodating and knows his job pretty well. As for the itineraries, it was well planned and the the transfers were very convenient and comfortable. I highly recommend this tour and take a glimpse of both the natural wonders of Hoi An and the its Old ancient town.

This morning I visited the My Songrace sanctuary at the Hoi An Express agency, with the help of an audioguide. The experience was transformative. The guide presented a rich historical context, bringing the ancient ruins to life with stories of the Champa kingdom. The significance of each temple was told clearly, enhancing my appreciation of this UNESCO World Heritage Site.Overall, the visit to My Son, combined with the audio guide, was a highlight of my trip to Vietnam. It's a must-see for history buffs and travelers alike.

We visited My Son as part of a cycling tour from Hoi An with Grasshopper Bike Tours and were blown away by the vast extent of the Sanctuary and all the history and recovered relics including the bombing of it during the Vietnam War (you can still see the bomb craters!) There are clean toilets at the entry/exit as well as in a few spots in the sanctuary but make sure you visit the loo at the entry and when you see one  along the way as they're few and far between.  Take water - it will take about 2 hours to get through the sanctuary and on a hot day you sweat a lot! Was super hot when we were there and we were walking in the sun for the most part (very few places to hide from it).If you can, go with a guide.  We learnt so much from our guide! Definitely a must see when visiting Hoi An!
Was a Good trip guide told a lot of information lunch was included and nice free water on the bus it was a good day

If you have interest in history and culture, do visit this sanctuary. Though the temples are in ruins, the technology that they used for making these brick temples as hard as stone, is still a mystery. Our guide Sunny was very well informed. Besides the temple history he told us stories about Vietnam war. I salute Vietnamese people, who haven't forgotten but forgiven and look towards future and not past.The journey inside the sanctuary was very beautiful  within the forest.

We visited on a rainy afternoon in December. It is a fascinating place. Well worth a visit - good for kids too. The buildings are interesting. We benefited from having a guide. It would be challenging to make sense of the site without a guide or reading up first.We enjoyed the dance show -well worth seeing. There are good facilities / cafes etc on site.
An amazing experience! I'm amazed that this temple survived the war.  Beautiful architecture and art. A perfect day trip from Hoi An.
A great world heritage site, about an hour out of Ho An.  There are a number of old temples going back to the times of the Romans (by European time line) set in the jungle of Vietnam.

The structures reminded me of Angkor Wat and while they are not quite as impressive they were lovely and we enjoyed touring them. What we really loved was the short song and dance show at the ruins. Great overall experience but it is a bit out of town (a little over an hour each way).

We are so glad we decided to go to My Son. Great  morning out ! We had a private guide and started the walk at the smaller monuments and moved clockwise finishing with the main structures . I would recommend this. Didn’t wait for the bus was an easy 2.5 km walk on made road . History of the area is facinating highly recommend .
I don't want to know about the tour to My Son.There is a lot of history to be said and it is fascinating how they built back then. And all without any major equipment.

It's about an 80-minute drive from Da Nang city.We chartered a Grab for 6 hours to get there.(6-hour charter: 888,000 VND, 5,383 JPY)The entrance fee was 150,000 VND (about 900 JPY).The ruins are scattered in various places, so it took a little over 2 hours to explore.There was a lot to see, so we looked around even though it was hot.You can also see the Cham ethnic dance, although there is a set time.At the end, there is a river covered with lotus flowers, which is a great photo spot.

The site can be visited with a guide booked on site or on your own.Impressive site with very pretty colors in good weather. We had a little traditional dance show, very pretty.Preferably early in the morning because the temperature quickly becomes difficult to bear.

My Son Sanctuary is an impressive and well-preserved monument of history. Its majestic and intricate architecture is a testament to the past, and its beauty is unparalleled. The complex is made up of numerous temples and statues that are both awe-inspiring and humbling.hly recommend a visit to this majestic and monumental sanctuary. It is a must-see for anyone interested in history and culture.

Construído entre os séculos IV e XIII, o local era a capital política e religiosa do reino Cham que se estendia pela costa do Vietnã (o templo Ponagar em Nha Trang também é desse período). Os franceses descobriram o sítio de My Son no século XIX e fundaram um museu em Da Nang dedicado à cultura Cham. Nesse museu é possível como eram as edificações em My Son antes dos bombardeios norte americanos as transformarem em ruínas.
Had a great time with many different nationalities on this tour. Our tour guide was fun and had good English. The Weather was hot but good. I would recommend this tour.
My Son makes an incerible impression, because of its history and all the temples, many of them are now beautifully reconstructed

Bijzondere plek om te bezoeken.Na het kopen van een entree ticket kun je gebruik maken van een shuttle service welke je een stukje dichterbij de mooie tempels brengt.Er is ook een dansshow, leuk om een stukje te kijken. Ook het museum vlakbij de ingang is interresant om wat meer informatie over de site te krijgen.Er zijn voldoende mogelijk om wat te eten, drinken of ijsje te kopen. Natuurlijk wel iets duurder dan buiten deze locatie.Er is weinig schaduw, dus een pet of UV paraplu is misschien een goede tip om mee te nemen.

We had a guide pick us up from Hoi An and take us in the afternoon.  It was super hot but not so many people there.  A guide made a big difference to us so that we had some substance to what we were seeing.  It was fascinating and quite a bit to see.  We must have walked around for a couple of hours taking photos.  There are a couple of places back at the car park to get a cold drink or ice cream, which was most needed.  Got to watch a champa dance too.  Worth the stop.
As a tourist whom likes to do things at his own pace and timetable, visited My Son one morning giving myself two hours to wander and enjoy the old history that makes modern Vietnam what it is today.
Ly was a wonderful tour guide. Knowledgeable and funny . He did a great job of leading us through My Son on a very hot day - he found all the shady spots to share information with us.

Beautiful place... we loved Indian exploring SHIVA LINGA, NANDI and many more. Surprised see rich Champa dynasty by Indian Kings. Glad to see Indian Government Modiji's effort to save, preserve and develop the heritage! We were amazed and proud to see Indian flag in the heritage centre!!

To be honest before I arrived to Vietnam, I didn’t know anything about Champa Kingdom. This is not as famous as Ancor Wat in Cambodia. However the site is unique as this ancient nation mastered carvings in the bricks. The site is beautiful and located in a wonderful jungle area full of flowers, butterflies and birds! The only sad part was to see one of the ruins crumbling into pieces. There was a tree growing in the middle and it was treated with chemicals. As the result all surrounding area was affected and now left to rot before it will be rebuilt as new based on evidence of photos. Some other parts were damaged during the war and there is even a big bomb created left after one of the bombs exploded damaging nearby buildings.

Very informative, relaxed tour. Got to see the monument at a time when it was not busy or too hot. Enjoyed a coffee on the way there and an excellent breakfast on the way back, all provided by the host's family.Back in time for lunch and an afternoon of more things to see and do in Hoi An.
Fantasy trip to Hindu temple in the Southern, near Hoi An old. Get a lot knowledges about heritage in Viet Nam. Contact whatsapp +84 944337018 mr Eric

We arrived 7:30am, no one there and not extremely hot yet. Ruins are amazing - who knew Hindu ruins in Vietnam. Restoration taking place post destruction from previous wars. The jungle like surroundings are beautiful.

Did not know what to expect after being driven there by car for an hour from Hoi An. The entrance fee was steeper than any other attractions the we encountered in Vietnam so far. An easy hike on a paved road to Group H did not disappoint. The placards provide plenty of explanation but we were able supplement the information with some internet capability.  The subsequent groups were also impressive. Gets warm so getting there early in the high season of Vietnam’s travel season would not be a bad idea. Wish we could have stayed for the show that repeats several times a day, but no midday times while we were there. A fantastic sight and trip. A highlight to our trip to Vietnam. A must do. A guide would have even added more but they do not have guides on-site for hire.

We visited My Son Sanctuary on an organised early morning tour, which was really great, as there were not so many people by then.Taking a guided tour will enable you to enjoy the sanctuary at most, in order to distinguish the different periods when the temple was built and have great explanation about the restauration works.A place I would absolutely recommend when in the region of Hoi An.

Poco battuto dal turismo ma indubbiamente da visitare se vi trovate ad Hoi An , dista circa mezz’ora Ciò che resta dell’antica civiltà Champa Una delle migliori testimonianze Induiste presenti in Vietnam Resta poco perché ha subito diversi bombardamenti dagli AmericaniDal 1999 patrimonio mondiale dell’umanità

It's great that we were able to do a half day tour to My Son Sanctuary . Got a drive from Hoian Town about one hour, then transfer an electric car, you will be able to enjoy the great temples of Cham Erain the forests of central Vietnam.

n outstanding experience on the Cham era (11th century Vietnam) there is a museum at the entrance.You are taken by electric carts to the site. They will also bring you back.in a few minutes walk you will see the ruins. You can walk in some areas of them.In one of the buildings there are 2 bombs. A 500 and 1,000 pounds. It was bombed by the US Air Force. You can still see the indentations on the ground, the large and deep holes. I will put photos up. A photo = 1.000 words.Don’t miss it
So glad I was able to make a trip to see the ancient ruins of My Son. Located in the forests of central Vietnam are the temples of the Cham people. A UNESCO world heritage site.

Fascinating history of the Cham people and how this site relates to similar style temples in the region, such as Pagan in Myanmar. We were fortunate to have a guide and to have arrived just after sunrise. The guide (Fletcher, Tours by Locals) made the experience so much more interesting than if we’d been on our own. Also wonderful to be there early, have the sites to ourselves and nobody photobombing. Well worth a visit. Tragic that so much of the site was destroyed by American bombs during the war.

If you’re into ruins like me, My Son is worth visiting. This is a recognized UNESCO site. In a nutshell, this is a cluster of Hindu temples built between the 4th and 14th century.  The temples were erected in honor of the god, Shiva.  There are headless gods/goddesses at this time as the French took the heads and are now displayed at the Louvre Museum in France.  If you do see some heads on these gods/goddesses, they are not the original ones.  It’s better to have a tour guide like Eric from Beyourfeet.com tour company, as he has information about this place. He will explain to you the history of this place. Also, there is one location in this site where the US dropped a bomb, creating a big crater on the ground.  The grounds is full of beautiful trees and plants. A nearby brook with a small bridge and a backdrop of a mountain with hanging low clouds (go early in the morning to witness this).  I enjoyed visiting this place because of its history. Some people reviewed this place as “not worth it; a disappointment, etc” .  I say, it’s better to have a knowledgeable guide as this place will come alive as you imagine what it’s like to live during that time. Also, don’t compare this to any other ruins, as this place has its own uniquenesses.

A place with historical and natural landscapes. Take a buggy ride up and hike up the paths to the ancient  aka Angkor Wat of Vietnam. Then sit down to enjoy cultural dances and music at the theatrical stage. A great experience

My Son is about 50 minutes from Hoi An, accessible by taxi or tour.  The site is remarkable, for natural beauty, history and the remains of an ancient civilization.  My Son is isolated from all commercial activity, and very peaceful.  It also shows signs of the destruction of warfare, as a reminder of the tragedy experienced by Vietnam.

Impressive structures and was good to learn about the Champa kingdom and links to the Indian history. The Apsara dance is beautiful and not to be missed. Make sure to check the times for the dance before you book your tour!

My Son Sanctuary was on our "must do" list during our visit to Central Vietnam so we booked the Sunrise tour via Viator. Our guide, Hoang Quan, and the driver picked us up promptly at 5am from our resort in Hoi An. The drive took about an hour so we got to know each other, and Quan shared about daily life in the villages we went through. The time passed quickly as we learned that several loud speaker announcements are made daily beginning with a wake-up call at 4:30am, the fish and fresh produce markets start at 5am, school begins at 6:45am Monday-Saturday, and other facts that Quan shared.Upon arriving, Quan covered the map of the site to brief us on the layout and what we would see. He also provided a brief history of the Cham people, and the spiritual origins of the temples built to Hindu divinities: Krishna, Vishu, and primarily Shiva. The Champa Kingdom existed between the 4th and 13th centuries CE. A good reference site for more details is whc.unesco.org/en/list/949. The entrance is a lovely arched bridge and after crossing it, we boarded a cool electric golf cart that whizzed us up and around to the disembarkation point with a marvelous view of the Cat Tooth Mountain. We walked up a short path, and came upon the first ruin! And a few steps away was Group B - the oldest temples at the site. Quan taught us that the Cham temples were built in 3's - the Water temple, the Main temple that only the King could enter to reflect and pray to Shiva - the divinity of destruction and recreation, and the Fire temple. Group B contains 3 sets of temples for a total of 9. Many of the pictures are from this group. Quan was a wealth of information throughout the tour, and we learned so much! The next group is slightly elevated as it is set on a small hill. These were built a century later so Quan asked us to identify 4-6 differences in this group compared to Group B. This kept us engaged and observant versus him just telling us. Compare the pictures and see if you can find differences. We moved on to a small area where artifacts are kept, and then to an area that was heavily bombed in the Vietnam War. Some of the ruins in this area are overrun with vegetation - just as the ruins were for the 400 years they were abandoned. There is one ruin they are currently working to restore that looks like a pile of rubble. Quan shared that all the ruins looked like that before restoration. This gave us perspective on all the amazing work that has been completed to preserve this religious and historical site. We finished up at the site viewing the final ruin, and a nice walk back to the electric golf cart for the ride back to the bridge. During the return drive, Quan taught us about the agriculture and weather impacts as we passed by many rice fields, and discussed his parents farm located in the mountains. The tour was educational, informative, thought-provoking, stimulating, enjoyable, inspiring, and very economical (private tour for 2). Book it if you love history, religion, and ancient cultures!
It was very worth doing…. They say it is the Ankor Wat of Vietnam. Having not been to the actual Ankor Wat, I still imagine they are correct.

Tolles Erlebnis morgens, ca. 8:00/8:30 bevor die großen Gruppen kamen und es noch nicht so warm war. Phantastische Ruinen, traumhafte Farben. Haben einen Fahrer gebucht und sind nur mit unserem Buch durchgelaufen. Uns hat es so gereicht, wir waren froh, einfach Ruhe und Eindrücklichkeit des Ortes zu genießen! Auf jeden Fall eine Reise wert

A good place to unwind and find out more about the history of the Champa. Good to have a tour guide to explain so that you are not lost about what is happening or what the ruins represent.Will be more meaningful that way
We were here on a lovely sunny day, which definitely enhanced the experience. Scenery is beautiful and the ruins are interesting. A tranquil place to spend a couple of hours

Chúng tôi khởi hành từ Hội An lúc 8 :00,và mất khoảng 1 tiếng để đến thánh địa Mỹ Sơn.Có khá nhiều du khách đến đây,nên tôi khuyên bạn nên khởi hành sớm hơn để tránh đông đúc và thời tiết mát hơn.Mỹ Sơn thật đẹp

Well worth the visit and so cheap for a personal guide. A place to contemplate history and appreciate it. Was very hot. The dance show was lovely too! Helps to know about Hinduism a little to appreciate the experience fully.

My Son is an enormous temple complex. It was the religious center of the Cham Dynasty.  Sadly, the temples were bombed during the Vietnam War.  Today, it is a UNESCO site and a must-see attraction when visiting the Hoi An area.If you visit on a hot day, as we did, be sure to take advantage of shuttles that are available to and from certain points on the property.  There is also a cultural performance given throughout the day.  It is quite well done and worth seeing.  The problem is that as soon as the show ends, everyone immediately takes the nearby path to the ruins, and then it becomes crowded.  If you are able to wait until after your visit to the ruins to see the show, I would opt for that.Take your time wandering the ruins and absorbing the structures from every angle.  They are really spectacular.  It's about 1.5 hours' drive from Hoi An, so do not rush your visit.  I will say there were some photo shoots/Instagrammers taking pictures which interfered with the serenity and enjoyment of our time there.Nevertheless, this is a must-see destination and I hope you can get there.

Très belle visite. Le site est grand et niché dans la forêt et entouré par des montagnes verdoyantes. Peut aussi compter pour réserve naturelle : papillons, oiseaux, libellules, écureuils. A environ 1 heure de voiture de Hoi An.Prévoyez une bonne paire de chaussures (baskets suffisantes) car pas mal de marche. La montée peut se faire par un service de navette gratuite. Entrée : 150.000 VND (env.6€)Pour y aller vous avez deux options si vous n’êtes pas véhiculés : via une excursion (moins de 30€/pers) ou avec chauffeur privé (voiture entière coûte env. 35€). Les prix sont pour l’aller et le retour depuis votre hôtel.
If you ever travel to Da Nang, you must definitely visit My Son Sanctuary to visit. It is a sacred place, a cultural heritage of the World, with a long history from the 8th to 12th   century

Do hinduistického chrámového komplexu My Son, který svým stylem architektury připomíná chrámy v kambodžském Angkoru v menším měřítku, jsme se moc těšili.  Vydali jsme se sem z Hoi An, vzdáleného přibližně 45 kilometrů, kde jsme trávili dovolenou. Cesta taxíkem nám zabrala něco přes hodinu. My Son je krásné místo se smutnou historií, umístěné uprostřed džungle, kde okamžitě pocítíte vysokou vlhkost vzduchu.Od vchodu jsme k ruinám přejeli elektrickým vozíkem, který postupně nabírá skupinky turistů.  Dále jsme pokračovali po pěšině s úžasnou zelení a nádherným výhledem na okolní kopce. I když bylo zataženo, bylo velmi horko a dusno a po cestě nás provázely všudypřítomné zvuky přírody, což působilo velmi relaxačně. Ruiny se rozkládají na velké ploše, ale nebyl to žádný problém si postupně projít celý areál, i když jsme neměli průvodce. Plánky rozmístění památek jsou srozumitelné. Tento hinduistický chrámový komplex patřil království Čampa - vietnamsky Cham Pa, a byl vybudován mezi 4. a 12. stoletím, kdy bylo toto místo důležitým centrem země. V historii  zde bylo v různých etapách vybudováno až 80 chrámů. V roce 1890 komplex objevili Francouzi a podařilo se jim zachránit 15 chrámů. Část chrámů pak byla úplně zničena při americkém bombardování v průběhu války ve Vietnamu a dodnes jsou zde na některých místech patrné krátery od bomb.Chrámy jsou postavené z červených cihel, slepených pouze kaučukem, díky čemuž  krásně vynikají  v okolní svěže zelené přírodě. U některých staveb probíhala rekonstrukce, stále však existuje dost zachovalých chrámů s množstvím krásných soch a ornamentů, které můžete obdivovat. Vlhké klima a zub času, zároveň s trsy prorůstající zeleně dodávají památkám zvláštní kouzlo a půvab.

My wife and I visited Hoi An as part of a 17day trip through Vietnam and Cambodia, where we were travelling with 10 other people. The tour was with G-Adventures and so the visit to My Son was prearranged by them, including a local guide for to give us a high level of specific history and knowledge. As with most places of interest, a local guide helps you to get the most out of the experience. If you are travelling under your own arrangements, there are companies in Hoi An offering guided tours of My Son.The grounds are now a UNESCO World Heritage Site and it is often compared to Angkor Wat in Cambodia. However, or trip ended in Siem Reap just 8 days later and, in my opinion, there is no contest as, impressive as My Son may be, Angkor Wat is in a completely different league. Our trip included all entrance fees, but I was able to find out that people turning up of their own accord were charged just over $6 (USA), which I felt was very reasonable for such an experience.The complex is a series of Hindu temples, which were originally built, over ten decades, as the religious capital of the Champa Kingdom. As construction ended in the 14th century, the buildings obviously fell into disrepair, until they were restored by French archaeologists in the early 1900s. Sadly, they were later targeted by American bombers when the Viet Cong hid there in the misguided belief that the Americans would not bomb a religious site, but there are still enough buildings surviving to merit the visit.I would definitely recommend visiting My Son, especially if you are spending any time in either Hoi An or Da Nang, but would emphasise the need for a local guide to get the best from the experience.

My Son Sanctuary ruins are absolutely unmissable to all those traveling around Huê, Dà Nãng and Hoi An. Before going, it is important to know that the ruins are located at only 10 kms from "Trà Kiêu" (aka "Dông Duong"), the ancient capital city of the "Kingdom of Champa", the Indianized Kingdom of the Cham people. "My Son Sanctuary" was its religious and cultural center, as well as a burial place for the royalty and for national heroes, with over 70 temples and tombs. The "Champa Kingdom" ruled Central and Southern Vietnam from the 3rd century up to its defeat, in 1832, by the "Kingdom of Dai Viêt". After the defeat, the temples and tombs of "My Son Sanctuary", dedicated to the worship of Hindu God Shiva, were abandoned. The lush nature of Cat's Tooth Mountain and Thu Bon River captured them. Years later, the site was again severely damaged by bombings during the Vietnam War. The ruins, although much smaller, are often compared with other Hindu complexes, as "Angkor Wat" in Cambodia and, in 1999, were appointed by Unesco as a "World Heritage Site"! Unmissable! Enjoy!

Around 30km from Da Nang city, lies the ruins of ancient Shiva temples, believed to be made in the 4th century. Constructed by the kingdom of Champa, these ruins were declared a UNESCO wolrd heritage site in 1999. The relics are fascinating, that have witnesses decades passing and lot of destruction by the US army. Indian Government had collaborated for the excavation as a part of preserving the ancient heritage. The Shiva lingas, the scuptures of Gods, carved pillars and stones - its stunnning to watch these elements in the premises that have been reserved by the Vietnam Govt with great care. The structures after being partially destroyed during Vietnam war have been renovated and the process continues for few structures. The greenery around makes it quite pleasing. Do avail the free shuttle ride from the ticket counter to the site. Entry fee- 150,000 VD (roughly 470 INR)

visited here when i travelled to Hoi An last year is September.i joined a morning tour organized by the sinhtourists.i have to wake up early,but this is not the sunrise tour,just go there in the early morning,then go back Hoi An before lunch time,cuz i have other activity in the afternoon,so i chose this morning tour.My son santuary is a relics of the ancient Champa civilization. Champa was an independent state before it was occupied by Kinh Vietnam.The are many stone sculptures, temples and towers here.besides,it was also a political centre and a royal burial ground.heard that if you already been to Ang kor wat,then you may disappointed with this place,cuz not big.but for me it is the first time,so quite enjoy.there is a souvenir store around the  electric cars station.and also a simple museum near the entrance gate,make sure you dont miss them.the museum though simple,but you can get some info about champa culture.heard that other session,like afternoon,there is a champa dance performance here,so may come again next time.

Visite très matinale, ce qui nous a permis d'être au calme sans trop de touristes, au milieu d'une forêt/jungle, on se serait presque cru dans un film du style Indiana Jones.Visite par petit groupe avec un guide, se qui permet de bien entendre et ne pas être les uns sur les autres au moment de la visite. Pendant 5min le guide va vous expliquer le lieu, l'histoire, et va ensuite vous laisser 5min de libre pour pouvoir faire des photos.Le sanctuaire est vraiment magnifique, ils font en sorte de bien l'entretenir, le préserver, malgré le temps qui passe. Vous trouverez même des cratères de bombes américaines, de la guerre du Vietnam.Et pour bien finir la visite, petit arrêt pour boire un café, avant de reprendre la route 😊Je vous le conseille fortement !!

子供を連れて自力で行くのが難しそうだったので、事前にツアーを申し込んで訪問しました。ホイアンの町からバスに乗って約1時間。それほど遠くは感じませんでした。ゲートから遺跡までは、少し歩いて行きます。こちらの遺跡、ベトナム戦争でほとんど破壊されて修復されたものですが、そういった印象は無く、建設当時の雰囲気を残しているような感じがします。建物や彫刻が印象的な遺跡で、歴史好きの方は必見のスポットだと思います。私達は、ツアーだったので滞在時間が限られていましたが、全体をしっかり見るのには半日以上掛かります。

Ungefähr 50 km von Hoi An in Richtung Inland entfernt liegen diese alten Tempelruinen aus dem 2. oder 3 Jahrhundert, die aus der Cham-Kultur hervorgingen. Wirklich sehenswerte Anlage, teils sehr stark zerstört, wobei die kunstvollen Bildhauereien noch sehr gut zu erkennen sind. Obwohl Experten am Wiederaufbau arbeiten, ist dies sehr schwierig, da die Cham eine sehr ausgeklügelte Bauweise nutzten, die sehr schwer nachzuvollziehen ist (fugenlos, außerdem eine besondere Mischung für die Steine, die ihre rötliche Färbung bewahren).Im hinteren Teil ist noch ein Bombenkrater aus dem Vietnamkrieg sichtbar.Man kann diesen Ausflug sehr gut mit einem Abstecher in ein Dorf verbinden, um etwas über den Alltag und traditionelle Herstellungweisen von z.B. Reispapier zu erfahren. Des weiteren gibt es die Möglichkeit, mit dem Boot nach Hoi An zurückzufahren.

Very interesting ruins of temples constructed about 1500 years ago without cement.  Very interesting technique. Partly restored following damage during Vietnam war. Theatre shows dancers and musicians who recreate Cham entertainment.

We enjoyed this World Heritage ancient Cham site - towers, temples, and beautiful statuary. The various towers are built from red brick. The sanctuary was bombed during the American War in Vietnam. Many sections of the sanctuary are still in tact. If you visit on a sunny day take water and sunscreen.

Nous avons visité ce site par une chaleur accablante (37 deg au moins, exceptionnel début mars) et en pleine crise du Covid-19. Nous n’étions qu’une dizaine de touristes et avons pu profiter pleinement de la beauté des ruines et du charme du site, loin des routes et de la foule.Un bus électrique vous amène à proximité du premier monument et ensuite vous circulez à pied au milieu des arbres, des cratères de bombes américaines et bien sûr des monuments plus ou mois restaurés. À la fin nous avons assisté au spectacle de danses Cham. Alors que nous nous attendions à une forme de piège à touristes nous sommes tombés sous le charme des danseuses et des figures qu’elles exécutent avec leurs mains

An amazing place that is not fully understood, but an honor to walk the grounds to try and capture the Champa history and beliefs.  Great carvings and stunning how the bricks (no mortar) are holding for centuries.  Interspersed are the bomb craters and rubble from the bombing as US forces tried to suss out the Vietcong hiding in the ancient ruins - a testament to the horrible consequences of war.We took approx 3 hour walk through the trails to arrive.  Variety of past and present remnants to stumble through.
ドラクエの世界と言われていますが、ゼルダの伝説の世界(神々のトライフォースとか)の方が近い感じでした。ガイドなしですが、他のツアーのガイドが話してるのが聞こえてくるのでなんとなく分かります。建築物も彫刻も、見応えがあります。色々写真を撮って、見て回れるグループは全て見ました。だいたい1時間半～2時間でちょうどよく見てまわれます。必ず、水分が必要です！暑さ対策も万全に！

Learning about the history of the Champa people and understanding how clever they were in construction and since was a real treat.The site is just big enough and the musical culture show was a decent appetiser. Do go with a personal guide if possible and then you'll get the most information and best insight into the overall history of the site, including its wartime role.

One of the most beautiful historic places in Vietnam! I really loved exploring this ancient village and learning more about the Champa people. The architecture is still well intact and great for pictures

Went here as part of a tour to see the ruins. It was an interesting place as our guide provide detailed explanation of the history of this temple. There is around 20mins walk into the actual grounds. There are also craters left behind by the bomb in Vietnam War. I would recommend coming to this place!

I had the benefit of having Tu of Local Buddy Tours to provide me with numerous facts about the history of My Son. The visit would not have been anywhere near as interesting if I had gone by myself. We also set off in a jeep at 5.30am to beat the crowds and it was only about a 50 minute ride in a jeep from Hoi An. There was at the most only about 15 people at the site and sometimes we had no other people around us whilst we were exploring the site. You can easily spend 1.5 - 2 hours here and I would highly recommend getting there early to avoid the tour buses. It cannot be compared to Angkor Wat but is still well worth a visit.
園區不算很大，只因為各遺址分開比較遠。園區內可選擇步行或乘坐免費車上山，車程10分鐘內。遺址上有很多修復痕跡。需要影相的話，需要等待，因為有部份人影很長時間。

Nous avons adoré visiter cet ensemble de temples Chams car nous y sommes allés à 6h30 du matin pour l’ouverture donc nous avions le site pour nous tout seul. Situé au cœur d’une nature luxuriante, c’était fantastique. Les groupes de touristes arrivent vers 8h30/9h...
途中まで無料のシャトルバスが運行しています。その後は、10分程歩くと遺跡に到着します。中にも入れます。レンガや石段があるので、歩きやすい靴がおすすめです。

Visited here 31 January. Guided tour with a Government Guide. Very historical relating to Cham people who are Vietnamese and Indian descent. Not dissimilar to Siam Rep, Cambodia. Brilliant souvenir shops after the tour with great prices. Recommend a visit if staying nr DaNang or HouAn

Enjoyed my half day tour to My Son Sanctuary from Hoi An. The wisdom of the ancient architecture is fully displayed here. This place is supposedly much older than Angkor Wat! But a large part of the site was destroyed during the war. Nevertheless, still well worth the trip!

Username: An Bang Sea Adventure Homestay Hoi An
Comment: chúng tôi thực hiện chuyến thăm quan bằng xe máy . nó khá xa so với thành phố tôi sống . no khoát lên vẻ cổ kính và đầy bí ẩn. hy vọng mọi người sẽ vẫn mãi giữ nét cổ truyền nơi đây

We had the fortune of booking a private guide and leaving Hoi An at 5:30am and were one of the first ones there.  This gave us the opportunity to be there virtually by ourselves and roam around and take our time.  We also got the chance to see the sunrise over the mountain and see the sun light hit a certain hole in a building and light up one of the temples.  We only looked at Group BC&D as they were the most intact.  It is fascinating to see the architecture and carvings.  It is sad to see the rubble from the Vietnam war and the crumbling of a once proud culture.  Well worth the trip, but go early.
オプショナルツアーで行きました。日本語を話せるガイドさんが説明してくださったおかげで、なぜ世界遺産になったのか、よーくわかりました。ツアーだったので全ては回れませんでしたが、見る価値のある遺跡をダイレクトでまわれたのでよかったです。
Exceptional examples of religious masonry. Awe inspiring indeed. We arrived early, so we had exceptional viewing of all the ruins.
Highlight de notre séjour à Hoi An. Nous y sommes allés vers 16h en fin de journée, endroit extrêmement authentique et agréable.

Lättillgänglig aktivitet och väl anpassat för alla. Lika gamla tempel som i Anchor Vat som är under ständig restaurering. Det är några tempel som är klara som man får besöka. En fin dansuppvisning at Cham-folket ingår. Lät att gå runt i området. Rekommenderar att ha en guide med. Vi hade Djom som guide och han var superduktig.
Die Anlage ist sehr toll und wunderschön angelegt. Diese Tempelstadt muss man unbedingt gesehen haben, denn nicht nur die Anlage, sondern ebenfalls die Landschaft und Umgebung ist Traumhaft.
A fascinating insight into Champa history and culture through the ruins of My son. Best to go to the Champa museum in Da Nang beforehand to fully appreciate the site.

每次來來去去峴港，都沒有機會和動力來參觀。為了讓小孩增廣見聞,才特地安排這次的行程。我們到這剛好正中午，可能來的季節是冬季，個人覺得整個行程走下來，還蠻涼爽舒適的。這被列爲世界文化遺產剛好滿20週年，歷史很悠久，可遡回公元4世紀到14世紀，印度教寺廟遺址，崇拜印度教神-濕婆。其中有大部份因越戰而被損壞，有點可惜。目前只保留下20座古塔和廟寺。建築的雕刻和搭建技術另人連連稱奇。如果事先能做公課，加上如果英、越語不錯的話，建議可請導覽來介紹，會有更深的體會認識。

Посетить стоит обязательно. Очень бы хотелось это сделать не с толпой китайских туристов - но при автобусной экскурсии это невозможно.Если Вам скажут, что это то же самое, что Ангкор Ват - не верьте. Мишон совсем другой - маленький, по-своему очаровательный, очень жалко что американские бомбардировки разрушили эту красоту.Если Вы закажете автобусную экскурсию - не надо возвращаться на лодке - на реке смотреть нечего.

It’s a pity that quite a lot of damaged has been caused due to the war but I think it adds to the mystique and history of the place. The ruins are amazing and you can only imagine what it would’ve been like pre the war. Watch out for the bomb craters dotted around the place.It’s just over an hour from Da Nang and we hired a driver for 1,265,000 VND. We spent a couple of hours within the ruins.

We didn't know about My Son Sanctuary before,And we were recomment by local people.And Its actually very nice and worth to discover when you came to Hoi An.But Im afraid that it will be broken by time since its very old

We enjoyed watching the traditional dancing. Very skilled dancers. We had a private tour guide who explained the significance of the site to us. We went late in the afternoon and we were lucky to see beautiful sun light shining on the ancient stones. I saw interesting insects and spiders on the way, which added to my enjoyment of the trip! I was hoping to see monkeys and squirrels, but nothing!

This is a site that you have to visit in Vietnam. It is easy enough to reach with a guided tour from Hoi An, and probably best that way to have some explanations on site of the different monuments. We found the place beautiful and enjoyed the Cham art a lot. Clearly best to go as early as you can as it gets crowded from the morning onwards. Enjoy!

This is the most complete Cham site in Vietnam and its setting in a lush jungle valley below Cats Tooth mountain is wonderful. The remains have been badly damaged, particularly in the American War, but still evoke the civilisation they represent. There are ongoing archeological excavations and conservation  underway. Try to arrive either early, around lunchtime or late to miss the tour buses, which can be a noisy distraction. There is an audio guide available, however you need to leave a deposit. Try to see the small Cham sculpture museum in Danang to, as it has an absolutley superb collection, some from My Son and elsewhere in the Cham world.

Username: あいじゅんあいじゅん
Comment: ベトナム中部奥地の、ミーソン遺跡 。ヒンズー教シヴァ派の古代チャンパ王国の遺跡。7〜13世紀のもので、世界文化遺産。元の侵攻を受け南部へ敗走する際に、シヴァ神の顔だけ持って逃げたり、盗掘を受けたりしたため、完全な姿のものは少ない。当時使われていたサンスクリット文字が、彫られた石柱が印象的。

We went there with a private car (7 seats, 700000d). We wanted to be free to roam around and not to be bound to a tour. We stayed there for 3 hours, but we could have easily spent another hour or maybe more. Unfortunately the driver was available only for 5 hours (1hour each way), so we highly recommend to go with a rented motorbike to take your time. The traffic is way better than in town and the roads are decent.The site is stunning, we loved it and it is a must see. Get there around 12 and you will avoid the tours and enjoy the place in peace. There is also a free service of mini buses to take you from the entrance to the site. There is not a lot of shade, so be prepared if you go there on a sunny day.Entrance fee is 150000dong, and there are some places where to buy water or sandwiches. There are also toilets at the entrance and close to the last site.Overall a must, not to be missed.

We headed off from HoiAn at 6.30am with our guide Ken (Tommy Dao Tours, HoiAn) & our lovely driver (who’s name escapes me, sorry). During the drive to MySon Ken delivered the most comprehensive and delightfully informative run down on the history of Vietnam and set the stage for a fabulous tour of the sanctuary. His knowledge of all the temples and attention to detail made our experience a day we will never forget. Ken’s amazing photography tips are guaranteed to please. 100% would recommend Tommy Dao Tours & Ken, thank you.
ベトナム戦争で米軍によって破壊され、ミラノ大学などの協力で一部が復元されていますが、莫大な費用がかかり、全部はできていません。復元で新しく補填された煉瓦との比較も。

We made the best choice for booking a tour of a small group very early in the morning. We started from our hotel in hoi an almost 6 am. Although we were a little bit sleepy the tour worth the time and the money as the time we arrived we were the only group of guests in the area. That was great cause we had plenty space and time to see all these amazing structures which takes you back on the 3rd century.We could take several beautiful photos and relaxed as our excellent tour guide (which now i dont remember the name) gave us all the information.The sightseing worth to visit if you are in the area but early in the morning. Not only you see amazing things which travels you through history and time but also you see the buildings that were damaged from bombing in the war of Vietnam.A strong recommendation to everyone.
A one hour and half trip from Hoi An will bring you to My Son Sanctuary, where you will get to discover ancient ruins of the Cham empire. A great place to visit for the history buff!

About an hour's drive from Hoi An, the ruins of the ancient Hindu religious center at My Son have a melancholy beauty that was further ruined by American bombs during the Vietnam war because the Viet Cong were using the site as a headquarters.  Bomb craters filled with water dot the site.  Do not expect Anchor Wat but if you have a little extra time, you will find My Sin well worth visiting.  The site itself is beautiful and jungly and backed by mountains that form the border with Laos.
Very impressive temple complex where it is easy to spend half a day. A lot has been damaged in the war unfortunately, still very much worth a visit.

The temple ruins are an open air experience similar to Angkor Wat. From the entry gate there is a free shuttle bus to the starting point. The paths are paved and generally easy to use for strollers. Good walking shoes are recommended. While it is mainly shady, I imagine it would get very hot in summer. You walk from 1 site to another and can get up close and personal to the ruins. I recommend reading the background prior to visiting so you have a better understanding of what you are seeing. Guides can be hired and seemed very good but we didn’t use one. There are bomb craters present which caused great excitement amongst the kids! There is a toilet at the entry point and again at the furthest point. These are clean and well maintained. There is also a shop selling snacks and drinks inside the park. It took my group about 2.5 hours to explore thoroughly. We took a taxi from Danang and had him wait and take us back. Entry was 150,000 VND per adultHighly recommend this place.
ツアーで参加しました。ベトナムの方々の祖先ともいえるチャム族のルーツです。保存状態は良くないですがそれがまた味があってよいです！解説があった方がより楽しめます！
I took private tour since it was 6 of us. Definitely we can enjoy it more since there are no english speaking tour available onsite. You can combine this with hoi an trip.

A must place to visit and the temples have very intricate design. If you love historical places, this is a place to visit. It is nice to see that the temples are still standing despite being attacked by the war. It was also my first time seeing a bomb crator too. No regets and the cool weather made our visit a great one. My Son (pronounced as Mi Son) also reminded me of Angkor Wat. Another beautiful UNESCO site.

I took my family there for a day tour and believe me it amazingly  eye open experience. It's so beautiful as you can see all the old buildings that is still standing and non standing. If your in Danang have to visit this place.

Impresionante a pesar de que esta muy destruido, y eso se lo tenemos que agradecer a los continuos bombardeos por parte de los yanquis en la guerra. Completamente rodeado de montañas , ríos y muchísima vegetación, arboles, me recuerda mucho a loa templos de angkor.  Un lugar muy bonito y visita obligada, nos estuvo lloviendo mucho a nuestra llegada a este sitio, pero tuvimos la gran suerte que el momento de la visita, lo dejo, aun un así con  lluvia, tiene su encanto.

This is a must to visit. Our tour guide isn't very proficient in English but we got our history lesson that day about Champa & Hinduism centuries ago. It had drizzled earlier before we arrived so the paths were slippery, muddy & paddles of water. So wear proper shoes & attire. There were old ruins left behind after the bombing & newly constructed ones. The traditional folk dance was entertaining too. Crowds of tourists in the late mornings & the wait for the buggy ride was long. About 2+ hrs of touring

We have been to Angkor Wat but thought My Son was also worth the visit. Luckily the weather was cooler and nice and there were no crowds, most everyone cleared out by lunch. We hired the tour guide at the entrance and she was excellent, the tour lasted over an hour and we learned a lot. Afterwards we went to the different relic areas and explored. The museum was good too. Not sure if this would be as good in brutally hot weather or with crowds but we loved it.

A 2h de Hoi An, ce sanctuaire vaut tout de même le détour. Il a appartenu à l'ancienne ethnie Cham, on y voit une grande citée avec de beaux bâtiments (site en rénovation continue), et un spectacle de dance traditionnelles très agréable
Nothing compared to Angkor Wat, but this place is amazing in its own right too! Here you will learn a lot about the Champa people, and the place where Hinduism first arrived in Southeast Asia.
sau hơn 70km từ đà nẵng vào mỹ sơn thật sự mình rất choáng ngợt trước vẻ đẹp của nơi này,  các bạn cũng nên ghé thăm để tìm hiểu nhé

Wären da nicht hingegangen wenn es nicht in unserer Tour mit drin gewesen wäre, was ein feheler gewesen wäre. Die Anlage ist auf jeden Fall sehenswert, unbedingt mit Guide eine tour machen. Die Gartenanlage und die Berge drum herum sind wunderschön. Lohnt sich auf jeden Fall

This site is about an 1 hour drive from the ancient town.Initially our hotel wanted to charge us $1.4Mil VND p.p. for a guided tour, but we opted for a taxi service that cost $45USD both way. If you shop around for operators in the town, you can probably find even cheaper deals.I have been told My Son is inferior to Ankor Wat in Cambodia, but I have never been there and thought this was very good. This site involves some walking and 2 hours would cover all of the sites if you are a fast walker. You can see bomb craters on the ground in the site - done by our American friends during WWII. Not sure why they would bomb this place but I'm guessing the Vietcon forces were using this place to hide.Lots of history - worth going.
Khoác lên mình vẻ đẹp cổ kính đầy bí ẩn Thánh địa Mỹ Sơn đây là vùng đất đáng để ghé thăm một lần khi đến Quảng Nam

Grate historical place. It was a large religion complex of ancient people of Vietnam professing Hinduism. Unfortunately it was ruined during the Vietnam war. Although it still impressive. We visit complex with excursion from Hoi An with English speaking guide. He tell us a lot of interesting things about Vietnam history. We looked at the country with fresh eyes. Highly recommended to visit.

Rovinato purtroppo dai bombardamenti USA, il sito non è in condizioni ottimali. Gli edifici sono però estremamente suggestivi per lo scenario in cui sono posti:la foresta magari con una nebbiolina, come è capitato a me, fa sembrare di essere in un film. Carino il piccolo museo allestito un uno degli edifici principali. Il valore archeologico del complesso è comunque altissimo.

We have chance to visit Hoi An in arround 3 days.And My Son Sanctuary make us feel an amazing.Just distance Hoi An ancient town 25km,and you should visit My Son in the afternoon to have chance to look sunset.We loved My Son Sanctuary

We took a blue bus arranged by our AirBnB to My Son (not sure of the company name - My Son Sanctuary something). It picked us up at the door, and the cost of $5US included bus there and back and a tour guide (although we ended up ditching him and wandering around on our own). In addition we paid the entry fee of 150,000 dong. English signage at the site was good,  the ruins are amazing and atmospheric, and we loved the experience of being in a jungle valley. Great value considering we heard many hotels charge in the range of $50US for a My Son tour.
덥다덥다. 차를빌려 가족이 아침먹고 이동하여 오전10시정도에 도착햇는데 엄청 더웟습니다 참파왕국의 유적인데 여전히 복원중에 잇습니다 옛날 영화로웟던 왕국을 상상하며 걸어보면.  덥습니다.  매점에서 아이스크림과 시원한 음료는 필수.

Februárban voltam, és mégegyszer elmennék. Reggel indultunk motorral, így láttuk a reggeli ködben a napfelkeltét, ezt sosem fogom elfelejteni! Reggel érdemes menni, mint miden ilyen látványossághoz:) Ankor Wat - hoz hasonló, érdemes megnézni, gyönyörű öröksége a múltnak!

If you are into ancient history then this is a must-see during any visit to Hoi An or Da Nang. Recommend that you get a private driver using 'Grab'. Can highly recommend Le Hoang Thien +84 905 988 112 as a reliable and honest driver. He will take you there and wait for you all for crazy reasonable prices.The site is a world heritage listed site. We recommend you pay the $100,000 Dong to get a guide (for us that was around $7 NZ) and it added to the experience. Sadly, many of the temples were damaged by bombing in 1969 during the Vietnam war but are being restored as there are photos of them in their former glory taken in the early 20th century by French archaeologists. Thoroughly worth a visit.

Well worthy of a UNESCO Heritage site.  This was an ancient religious site of the Cham people.  I would recommend you get a good guide who will explain the history of the Cham people, the religious significance and also the construction methods of each structure. Also, start early as it can get quite hot and crowded.Really sad to see a large part of it destroyed during the wars.

These ancient Hindu temples worth a visit. Smaller in scale and size in comparing to the others in South East Asia but they have their own attractions. Highly recommend to come early in the day with a small group for the best photos.
Absolutely fascinating site - incredible building feat. Even the dance show beforehand was fun to watch. Get there early to beat the crowds.

Went on the my son tour with urban adventures, have been on many tours with urban adventures and this one has been by far the best. Our tour guide Nam Pham was very friendly and knowledgeable. He was very educational about not just the temples but also the city. He spoke amazing English he was very easy to understand and made the tour so enjoyable. The driver was very friendly as well with a immaculate kept van.

These ruins are about an hour ride from Hoi An, and well worth the trip. They are on a continuum with the Angkor ruins and UNESCO has done a great deal of work here in partnership with Vietnam and India. You need about 90 minutes to 2 hours of walking over uneven terrain and as always here it will be hot, but you can go at your own pace and there are benches to rest when you need.

I loved my experience not his My Son tour. It started off wonderful with an on time pick up and very friendly tour guide and driver. I loved the organisation and the english of our tour guide was amazing. We could ask questions and we could understand all the facts about these amazing ruins. Our guide helped us to take photos for our memories and we felt the half day tour was just perfect. Thank you for the experience we will never forget.

My partner and I trekked out there from Hội An. We rented a moto from our hotel and drove there. It was a little less than a half day trip. We were away from Hội An for about 4 hours total. We spent about 1.5-2 hours there and went around 12 noon. We lucked out and there were only a few other visitors at this hour. Like others have said, bring a hat and water. It is handicap accessible with paved paths to each site, and total walking distance was ~1 mile. The photos we took and history we got to experience was really, really rewarding and I would absolutely recommend a short day trip to My Son.

We booked a local tour that picked us up at 4:30am. By 6am we were at My Son entrance and went in, just in time for the sunrise. There was hardly anyone in the park and at the sanctuary. Totally worth the early start!The site itself is very impressive. It was a first of a kind for me and I was amazed. The experience was definitely made better by the early time of the visit (less hot, hardly any tourists, peace and quiet).Our tour was a bit rushed and so I would recommend to rent a bike and drive there instead. It’s not far from Hoi An (1,5h max) and not a difficult drive. And definitely go early (park opens at 6am).

My Son was the first attraction site I visited in Vietnam. I found it fascinating. We had a tour guide who spoke English with a very strong accent, but I was able to figure out everything he was saying. Our tour included a dance and music show, as well as a boat trip back to Hoi An and dinner.  All in all, it was a great experience.  I loved learning about the history and culture of the Vietnamese in this area.

Had not heard of My Son Sanctuary on a previous visit to Hoi An despite it being listed by UNESCO as a heritage site, and so very surprised to learn it was once the Imperial City. A lot of restoration under way. Even with mini train transport up to the start of the village, there is still quite a lot of walking to get to the actual site. Very hot this visit so ensure you take water. Very interesting history of the Cham people and their singular devotion to procreation. Origins in Hinduism and then a mixture of Hinduism and Islam developed. At the end of the visit a group performed traditional dances.

Although I have been to Mauritius but there Hindus are due to Britishers but MySon was  first place for me to see Historical ruins of Hindu Dynasty out of India.To our surprise it dates back to 12th Century and the ruins were so intreging and interesting that we felt that we are some where in India only.Frankly we never heard or read about Cham Dynasty and it was a pleasant surprise and we were the only 2 Hindus in group so were getting some good attention also :) which we didn't mind.This place is only for people who likes history and can walk good miles. We walked almost 2-3 KM and on sunny day it can be hectic.We had lot of fun here as it connected us to our roots back in India. I would recommend this place as its part of human history and if you are interested then its good place to be

No recomiendo el sitio. Además, como sucede en muchos sitios en Vietnam, no permite comprar las entradas con antelación por Internet, y allí en las taquillas no permite pagar con tarjeta de crédito. Leider wurde bereits am Eingang versucht uns zu betrügen, da wir keine Dong hatten, sondern mit Dollar zahlen mussten. Aus 12 Dollar für 2 Personen nach aktuellem Wechselkurs wurden auf dem Taschenrechner des Kassierers ganz schnell 22 Dollar. Jegliche Versuche ihm den Wechselkurs nahezubringen ignorierte er und ließ uns keine Karten zum regulären Preis kaufen. Die umliegenden Shops wechseln nicht. Uns haben dann andere Touristen mit Dong ausgeholfen. Auch die Fahrer der kleinen Shuttles waren unfreundlich und warteten unnötig lange bis zur Fahrt. Mình là khách du lịch từ xa tới lần đầu, nghĩ là leo đồi núi nên mình mặc áo thun và quần short jeans đi giày thể thao. Nếu không vừa ý thì nhắc nhở du khách, hoặc đưa ra quy định luôn để du khách biết, đàng này không nói không rằng ông hướng dẫn chỉ vô mình nói là lên thánh địa không được mặc quần short rồi cầm cây quất thẳng vô chân mình luôn trước mặt cả đoàn.
Mình rất bất ngờ với hành động đó và cảm thấy tức giận nữa, nhưng quyết định dằn lòng xuống để giữ chút tôn trọng cho ổng trước mặt cả đoàn vì ổng cũng lớn tuổi, và nghe nói cũng hướng dẫn nhiều thạc sĩ, tiến sĩ nghiên cứu về khu di tích này. Sau khi quất vô chân mình thì ổng vẫn điềm nhiên hướng dẫn tiếp, nhưng mình không còn nghe lọt tai câu nào nữa. THÁNH ĐỊA MỸ SƠN - QUẢNG NAM

Thánh địa Mỹ Sơn thuộc xã Duy Phú - Duy
Xuyên - Quảng Nam, gần thành cổ Trà Kiệu, bao gồm nhiều đền đài Chăm Pa, trong một thung lũng đường kính khoảng 2 km. Đây từng là nơi tổ chức cúng tế của vương triều Chăm Pa cũng như là lăng mộ của các vị vua Chăm Pa hay hoàng thân, quốc thích. Là một trong những trung tâm đền đài chính của Ấn Độ giáo ở khu vực Đông Nam Á và là di sản duy nhất của thể loại này tại Việt Nam.
Mỹ Sơn có lẽ được bắt đầu xây dựng vào thế kỷ IV.
Là khu di tích chính của văn hóa Chămpa tại Việt Nam.
Từ năm 1999, Thánh địa Mỹ Sơn đã được UNESCO chọn là một trong các di sản thế giới tân thời và hiện đại.
Hiện nay, nơi đây đã được đưa vào danh sách xếp hạng 23 di tích quốc gia đặc biệt quan trọng.
Trong chuyến tham quan đợt này đoàn chúng tôi được anh Tiến là hướng dẫn viên (trong anh còn mang dòng máu của người Chăm) đã thuyết minh những điều mà lâu nay chưa biết đến. Anh còn hứa nếu tham quan đợt sau thì sẽ đưa chúng tôi đi hướng khác sẽ thưởng ngoạn và nghe những thông tin mới hơn về đền tháp Mỹ Sơn.

꽝남에 위치한 참파 왕국의 유적지입니다. 다낭 시내에서 60Km 정도 떨어진 거리에 위치해 있습니다.
불교가 대세인 베트남에서 특이하게도 힌두교의 영향이 물씬 느껴지는 유적지라고 합니다. 프랑스 식민지 시절에 프랑스인에 의해 발견되어 지금은 유네스코 문화 유적으로 지정된 곳이라고 합니다. 꽤 넓은 부지에 위치해 있어서 입구에서 매표를 하면 카트를 타고 유적지로 이동해야 합니다. 크게 몇 군데의 그룹으로 나뉘어져 있어서 다 돌아보려면 시간이 꽤 필요합니다.
방문한 시기인 2020년 10월 18일 경에는 태풍이 지나간 뒤인데다가 코로나로 관광객이 없어 조금 을씨년스러운 모습이었습니다. 관광객이 아무도 없어서 박물관, 식당,  기념품점 등이 모두 문을 닫은 상태였고, 도로 곳곳이 진흙탕인데다 나무도 쓰러져 있곤 했네요. 하루 두 번인가 공연도 한다는데 여러 가지로 아쉬웠습니다.
고고학에 관심이 있거나 베트남 역사와 문화를 더 알고 싶은 분들은 시간 내서 방문할 만한 곳이라고 생각합니다.

A fascinating temple complex dating back hundreds of years. Unfortunately a lot of the temples are in ruins and the huge numbers of tourists walking through the ruins probably do not help in keeping this place up. I went there twice, once in 2014 and in 2019 and one of the temple ruins still had the same scaffolding around it. Some restoration does take place but it seems like the priority is to squeeze as much money as possible out of tickets and the money does not go into restoration, but that is just my impression. It is still an impressive place to visit, and if you come here very early in the morning you may enjoy an hour without too many other tourists around (especially large groups with microphones and flags, those are so annoying....). As far as I remember, the entry fee is 150k VND, and you get a ride from the entry to the actual ruins in an electric car.

2024.03.31到訪。
因為熱到快昏倒，所以給兩顆星，這天氣、這熱度，真的令人受不了。
歷史博物館本身很有文化，導遊也講解的很好，但是這麼熱的天氣，這麼好的古蹟地點，為什麼沒有冷氣？！
如果有冷氣，至少可以讓人更有動力往下走下去；下午兩場的文化表演也很好看，但在這麼熱的天氣裡，一堆人坐在一起，即便是在通風的空曠空間，我真的還是覺得熱到不行。
類小吳哥窟的古蹟確實令人感到以前的人也太勵害了，為什麼現代人維修的地方會因爲潮濕長苔蘚，但古人修建的完全沒有，太神奇了。
Un espacio cultural, arqueológico y patrimonial que hoy parece una parque de diversiones bastante falso y desordenado. Una pena.

As always, your experience largely depends on your expectations. I see some people mention Angkor here... well if you just have been there and you expect somethink like that here, you gonna be dissapointed. Although if you do not expect much, it can make for a pleasant half-day trip, but you really should go on a dry day.We visited the site with an organised tour in order to learn more about the history. Note that the tour doesn't include the entrance ticket that cost 150.000 dong per pers and which I find overpriced compared to other attractions in Vietnam. The company that runs the tour is called Camel. We were told that the pick-up time was 7.30 am but the bus arrived a whole hour later... not a good start. After more people have been picked up around 9.00 am, we had to change to a slightly bigger bus that was already full so my boyfriend had to sit an hour with his legs up. We had a fun tour guide, he was Ok with fairly understandable English. What I didn't like about him is that he asked us for giving him tips. We spent around 2 hours at the site, most of the time walking. There were some muddy parts so walking shoes are recommended, especially in the rainy season. This applies umbrellas/raincoats, which you also can buy at the entrance (15-20.000 dong for a poncho, 80-100.000 for an umbrella). The area is really beautiful, a lush green site with a small river surrounded by mountains. But the ruins (as little as remains after the war) aren't very impressive. Some are being restored but I didn't like the way they do that. However I have to admit that the main site is quite nice and we took some very nice pictures during our visit. Another plus is that the area is being kept clean, a rarity in Vietnam.There were two options to get back to Hoi An, by bus or by boat. We did the latter that costs a little bit more but we thought that would be nicer. It also includes a very light vegetarian meal (rice with some tofu and vegetables - tasty but not really enough to call it a lunch). Note that it is not boat all the way, first you drive 40 minutes back by bus and after that you change to a small boat, and this trip takes you another 40 minutes. We imagined the boat trip would be longer as we were told we would be back around 3 pm with the boat option;our guide said 2.30 pm; finally we arrived in Hoi An at 2.00 pm. So the tour started an hour later and finished an hour earlier than we were told... All in all, it was ok, I do not regret it but it is not really worth the time and money.

About an hours drive out of Hoi An, the sanctuary was very average. Maybe because I automatically compare it to Angkor Wat or Borobudur. Most of it is in ruins or has been restored, which doesn't give it the same effect that I can imagine that it once did. The bomb craters around the site were the most interesting bit. And SO many people there at once, it was hard to get photos or walk through the structures.

If you have been to Angkor or Bagan this will not compare by any measure.  The setting is nice but the temples are in very poor repair (thanks in part to US bombs).  The rainforest and hilly surroundings are however atmospheric.Main disadvantage and why only 3 stars - on an organised tour from Hoi An (pretty much your only choice unless you have a private car (it is too far for a motorbike)) it was a long 6 hour effort there and back with lots of hotel stops, for a ruin that frankly can be seen in 30 minutes if you didn't have to follow the pace of your enormous tour group.Tip: hire a private car and local guide - more expensive but more comfortable and time-efficient.

We turned up one rainy December day to find we were the only visitors...at 10am!! Turns out the tour coaches couldn't get through the flooded roads, unlike our expert driver. Great we thought - no crowds. However, not only could the coaches not get there, but the guides you book on site couldn't get to work either. So no guide. Oh well we said.  So went in anyway. We quickly found there's little information on site or even sign posts pointing towards the exits! We were the only 3 people in what was basically a monsoon and kept getting lost. Very confusing!!So, all in all a spectacular place but ruined by the lack of a guide or signage. Think we just got unlucky - will try again in the future.
