Die Anreise mit einem geliehenen Roller von Hoi An klappt problemlos und ist auch bezahlbar, im Vergleich dazu ist der Eintritt mit 150.000 Dong schon richtig teuer. My Son ist als Ausflug, wenn man nichts besseres zu tun hat, ganz nett. Möchte man allerdings beeindruckende Tempelanlagen sehen in Asien, so führt an Ankor Wat kein Weg vorbei.

Wir sind mit dem Roller von Hoi An allein hingefahren (etwa 80Min Fahrtzeit). Die Fahrt war sehr anstrengend, da die Straßen staubig sind und viele LKWs fahren (schlechte Luft). Wer nicht so auf das Geld achtet, sollte ein Shuttle oder Taxi nehmen.Der Eintritt war 120k oder 150k.Der Parkplatz für den Roller 5k, obwohl er uns erst 10k abnehmen wollte (man sollte überall in Vietnam handeln oder man zahlt halt mehr als den tatsächlichen Preis)Die Anlage an sich ist ganz schön aber meiner Meinung nach nix besonderes. Nicht vergleichbar mit Ankor Wat.Es war keine Zeitverschwendung, jedoch ist es kein Muss.

My Son Sanctuary is about 45minutes to an hour away from Hoi An which is not far. Upon reaching the site, you have to buy the entrance ticket which is 150k viet dong. Then walk a short distance where you will be able to take a free shuttle buggy up to the hill where the ruins are. There is a map which you can follow and walk along to visit te ruins. If you would like to hire a guide, you can do that as well through the guide office next to the stop of the shuttle buggy.The ruins are pretty much in bad shaped compared to Angkor Wat. The authorities are trying to preserve but they used the wrong method of doing so, as pointed out by the guide. For example, if you look carefully at the walls which they tried to repair and construct with modern methods, are full of algae. And those walls that were built in the past did not have such growth.But overall, it is still a nice place to visit.

Crumbling site of temples dating back to the Champa people. Heavily bombed during the war as it was used as a military base by the VC. Looted by the French during colonial reign. Good guide, informative, but a little anti American, made me a little  uncomfortable. Site overall had a bit of a sad vibe.

Scenic temple ruins to be accessed by tourbus. The ride is a little long (1hr) and it might be better to hire a private guide on site or travel in a smaller group. Very beautiful mountains, ruins and nature in general. Interesting cultural history and very visitor friendly for walking around. Depending on the tour operator, you may be unlucky to be forced to visit some kind of craft village or something like that, which we find annoying. We can't recommend the 'return by river' option, as the boat is loud, smells of exhaust and our boat actually broke down.

It is a similar site to Angkor Wat of Cambodia, but of a way smaller scale. For me, it does not seem to worth a 1.5 hour ride from Hoi An. Anyway, if you are a lover of Hindu Sanctuary or Ancient Temples, you may still like this site very much.

We rented a scooter for the day and drove out to the ruins. Parking was 10k. Entry fee 150k each. They had a nice shuttle taking you within walking distance of the ruins. Pretty neat site. Very run down from the bombings but they are trying to rebuild and preserve. Not as magnificent as Angkor or Borobudur but if you're in Hoi An it's worth the drive.

К сожалению, во время войны большая часть комплекса была разрушена. На сегодня доступны к осмотру несколько руин древнего города. Пара небольших объектов восстановлены для представления, как это было раньше.  Не сравним с храмовым комплексом Ангкор-Ват в Камбоджи, но если Вы там не были и у Вас есть половина свободного дня, то обязательно посетите. Входной билет стоит 150 000 донгов, платная парковка для байков. На территории бесплатный шаттл к началу осмотра достопримечательностей.

If you've been to Siem Reap you are likely to find this site disappointing in it's scale & condition. The site is interesting in it's own right however & the right tour guide can bring it to life. The US bombings did the site no favours. If you've not been to Siem Reap this may whet your appetite to go. It is reasonably accessible with shuttle buses reducing the walking distance and the butterflies and flowers in bloom added to it's charm.

We took one of the cheap package deals, about $30 per person. Includes pick up from hotel, 2 hrs at the site and boat trip back. Don't expect anything more than the basics for this price. Minivan was cramped and stuffy, boat was basic and the included meal was no more than steamed rice and some fried veg. Not a whole lot to see along the river but fun to do all the same. Be warned, they don't take you straight back. Instead they stop at a village across from hoi an where they sell woodcarving products before dropping you back. Also the guides don't take you to the small site museum located at the entrance which is stupid because there are useful maps of the site and diagrams of temples. This seems a much better place to introduce the site than standing in the baking hot Sun!!
ベトナム戦争で破壊されてしまったようで、保存状態があまり良くなかった。全体的に希望はあまり大きくなく、アンコールワットの方が美しかった。土産物屋や飲食店もあまりない。監視員が常にどこかにいて、遺跡に登ろうとする人を見かけると笛を鳴らして注意していた。広い敷地内は、バス？で移動できるのは、ありがたかった。
是中越古占婆王朝所建印度教廟宇,因越戰而殘破不堪,破敗不已,不及吳哥窟千分之一,而地雷處處,危機四覆,切記勿脫隊亂跑.以免葬身異地.

We went on a tour from Hoi An, and found the experience rather rushed. The ruins were so busy that it was hard to get any sense of the atmosphere and we felt rather rushed around. I wish we'd made our own way there and come in the late afternoon, it would have been a better experience. The ruins are badly damaged, but still interesting.

Si has estado en Camboya o Tailandia... Puedes prescindir de esta visita.150.000 Dongs por persona + 5000 Dongs de aparcar la moto.Dispone de transporte interno gratis entre los templos.El calor en esta zona es infernal.Si vas en moto echale 1h a buen ritmo....y sin perdernos.

A mon avis juste génie pour les passionnés d'archéologie !! Aucun doute! Pour nous qui n'y connaissent rien et pour qui il est difficile de comprendre toutes les explications réalisé en anglais non avons trouvé le prix excessif, certainement du a la présence de hoi an à côté ! D autres visites réalisées par la suite deux a trois fois moins chère nous on d'autant plus marqué tels que les tunnel de vinh moc et les grotte de phong nha!!! jolie certes mais...

Ik vond My Son zeker wel de moeite om te bezoeken, maar ik had er veel meer van verwacht. Eigenlijk is er maar 1 gedeelte echt de moeite om te bezichtigen (zie foto's). Het museum vond ik niet zo interessant. Op de paden kun je een beetje schaduw vangen, maar anders kan het er heel heet worden.

Vanuit Hoi An met een scooter naar My Son gereden. Leuke route en prima te doen qua drukte. Wij waren er rond 12:30 en het was er rustig! De drukte kwam rond 14:30 weer op gang. Op eigen gelegenheid in my son rondgelopen. Ruïnes zijn zwaar beschadigd, wel interessant om te zien maar de ruïnes in Cambodja en Thailand zijn veel mooier. 30-60 min rondlopen is echt voldoende.

Ce site d'un grand intérêt historique est hélas bien abîmé . Les ruines restantes sont restaurées , pour certaines, pas toujours de la meilleure façon . Allez y tôt , car il y a beaucoup de monde. Facilité de circulation sur le site grâce à une navette électrique.

旅行会社の昼食付き半日観光で参加しました。ミーソン遺跡までは片道1時間半程度で、トイレ休憩はありませんでした。半年前にアユタヤ遺跡を見たので、正直、規模が小さく物足りない感じがしました。ですが、遺跡、彫刻が綺麗に残っているものもあったので、行って損はない感じがしました。ただし、往復3時間程度かかる一方、滞在が1時間程度だったのが、残念でした。夏に行ったので、メチャクチャ暑かったです。防止等の暑さ対策をしていって下さい。

Nous avons bien aimé la visite de ces ruines, celles encore debout sont belles et bien différentes de ce qu'on a pu voir dans le reste du Vietnam, mais le tarif d'entrée est bien trop cher pour ce qu'il y a à voir selon nous ...

We booked the standard tour through our hotel. We were picked op at 4:15 AM! Since we were the first on the small bus. Breakfast consisted of two slices of bread, two slices of egg and some lattuce and tomato. I suggest you bring your own snack. At 6:00 we arrived together with a larger bus. Some 40 people shared this guide that we didn't join. It was much nicer to walk around and discover the temples on your own ( do some reading yourself before you go). Much has been destroyed by US but the atmosphere is really nice. We also did some birdwatching. On the way back we opted for the boatride (last part of the way back). This was very noisy and views not really interisting. The village visit was also not interesting, I suggest to take thd boat back.

Si vous avez déjà visité des sites Cham, en particulier au Cambodge, cette visite n'aura pas de réel intérêt pour vous... A faire, si vous aimez les ruines... Entrée à 150 000 VND et pour le trajet en bus aller-retour comptez 6$. Vous poubez ausi faire le trajet aller en bus et le retour en bateau pour 8$, qui inclut une assiette de riz et légumes, et un arrêt au Kim Bong Carpentry village.

Tour vanuit Hoi An naar de My Son temple gedaan. Aardige busrit van ongeveer een uur over slechte wegen. De tempels zijn indrukwekkend om te zien mits je genoeg inbeeldingsvermogen hebt om te bedenken hoe het eruit heeft gezien. Tip ga vroeg in de ochtend met een sunrise trip dan is het nog te doen met de hitte.
Booked a US10 local tour to my son at hotel front desk, excluding the VND 1,500K  entrance ticket. Hard to compare it with Angkor but surprised by the destructive effect  of Vietnam war

If you have been to Angkor Wat in Cambodia, do not waste your time coming here as you will find this a disappointment. There is nothing much to see, most of the ruins were in poor condition plus the scorching sun were major minus points for this place.

Most of what's left is really ruins. There are only 2-3 places in good enough condition to enter, and they have been rebuilt. The site is fairly interesting at first glance, but you'll struggle to spend an hour there. Even if you use the thoughtful shuttle bus, there is still a LOT of walking to see a not particularly impressive site.

I booked the sunrise tour of My Son sanctuary via my homestay, and was a little disappointed that we arrived at My Son well after sunrise. For me the draw was seeing the sun actually rise over the temples and mountains. There's a fair amount of walking at the sanctuary, which can feel overbearing in the humid jungle, but there are shuttles between gates. There are 4 main sites to walk around, and a few stray dogs to play with. I can't imagine going without a tour group, as you wouldn't learn much. The signs are few and far between with very little information on them. Overall, I'd only recommend staying here for 1 hour if you have a lot of time in Hoi An to kill.

If you've been to Angkor Wat or Borubudur you will be totally disappointed. Nothing much to see though our tour guide has a lot of information. It was very hot and humid. Bring water, umbrella, and hat. It's not appropriate for small children.

A bit of a disappointment, I must say even when I had prepared myself not to compare it with Angkor Wat.  Unless you are a huge fan of similar types of ruins, this place would not impress you if you have been to Angkor Wat as it is much smaller and preservation works are somewhat lacking.

By the passing of time, long lost invasions, but certainly helped along by 20th century wars, very little is left of what looks like it could have been a vibrant place of imbibing the divine. The obvious traces of yogic and Hindu influence are very interesting to see.I am not sure it was worth the visit though (maybe the scorching sun was just a tad too much when I visited)
Perhaps I've visited the Angkor temples. This is comparatively less impressive, but still worth a visit to learn about the history of the Cham people.

It is a famous visit attraction in Danang, but if you don't have a tour guide to introduce the background information, your visit will be boring. The entry fee is fair, not expensive. However, I will not visit again shortly because maintenance is under processing.

Bien que passablement détruites lors de la guerre le site contient encore de belles ruines. Le site est aménagé, facile en famille donc. Cependant cela enlevé du naturel à l'endroit et il y pas mal de touristes (allez y en semaine). Il faut 1h30 voir 2 max si vous prenez du temps pour faire le tour. Le prix est de 150k dongs.

Para quem gosta de histórias e ruínas é muito bom. Um pouco longe cansativo até chegar por lá. Interessante e místico esse lugar por estar no meio das montanhas e florestas. Acesso fácil e muito boa estrutura para o turista. Limpo e organizado.

Als je tijd hebt kun je dit zeker even doen en dan het beste als het bewolkt is anders is het echt te warm. En mocht je zelf scooter rijden lukt dat makkelijk vanaf Hoi An... Een tourtje hiervoor boeken is zonde van je geld.
Very cool history. Lovely and peaceful area to walk around. Beware of entrance fee (around 6 USD). Worth a visit

Dernière visite culturelle que l'on ait fait au Vietnam : il ne reste plus grand-chose de ce site hindou detruit par les bombes américaines. Nous n'avons pas eu de chance notre guide était inintéressant. Il n'y a quasiment aucune indication sur place pour comprendre. A voir uniquement si on a du temps.

Ce site est présenté comme le Angkor Vat vietnamien...c'est sans doute exagéré et il vaut mieux ne pas avoir vu le site cambodgien avant de découvrir My Son. Le site en lui même est peu étendu et certains vestiges , suite aux bombardements  de la guerre du Vietnam sont en très mauvais état ( des restaurations sont en cours ). A découvrir pour les " vrais "amateurs de vieilles pierres . Les autres risqueront d'être déçus..
Not much left from the old city. Not much to see,but nature  is beautiful and it's good place to escape from the town. If you don't have time you can skip this place.

We went with a tour, which stopped at a shop before the entrance to my son for 'drink and toilet break'.  The toilets were filthy,  had no locks on the doors, no toilet paper in sight and to top it all off you had to pay to use them.When you get to entrance to my son there are much nicer and free toilets. So don't use the first ones if you can help it. The ruins themselves we enjoyed, it was a terribly hot day so we found it a little hard,but our tour guide was informative and learnt a lot.

ホイアンから長いことバスに揺られて着いた、「ミーソン遺跡」＝「世界文化遺産」です。山の中にレンガ作りのお寺です。いくつかの時代ごとの区域があり、判りにくい案内板を頼りに行くわけですが、見学前にショーを見てから見学です。レンガ作りのため崩れかかっているものがあり、一部には徐々に修復されてるようです。日本語ガイド付きオプション50ドル前後で6～8時間かけていくほどのものではないと思いますが、ダナンに行ったら行かないのはもったいない。半～日以上

O tour é interessante e tem bastante detalhe pra ver. Se você tem pouco tempo em Hoi An, sugiro priorizar outras atividades pois o santuário foi bem destruído na guerra, então não se vê edifícios inteiros.

Si vous êtes plusieurs jours sur Hoi An, pourquoi pas ...Certains endroits sont en rénovation.Ça reste un site sympa quand même. Vous faites une grande boucle pour visiter ce site. Prévoir de l'eau avec soi car il fait très chaud !!! Possibilité d'en acheter au milieu de la visite.
Interesting to visit and probably would have been great before it was damaged, but the long trip out there was probably not worth it. Having been to Angor Watt, there is no comparison.
Morning myson一早來myson看日出……雖沒有吳哥美，最大的竟然也越戰被炸毀了，東南亞五大王朝的美名，比吳哥早500年的歷史，世界文化遺產的名頭，人生還是不能有遺憾是吧^^

It's OK but a bit underwhelming. We found the site hard to put into context and not well preserved although restoration work is ongoing. Signage was minimal but our guide was helpful. It was very hot in late March.

The place/site is good. I just don't understand why the entrance fee (150k VND) isn't included in the $6-8 we paid at the booking agency. Also and more importantly, the tour guides SERIOUSLY need to improve their English comm skills and sense of humour.

It took over 2 hours on a bus to get to the Holy Son site and then a 20 minute uphill walk. Our guide spent 20 minutes telling us a brief history of the site and then we had another 20minutes to take photos and look around, then back on the bus. I think there were better ways to spend a day but that's just me.

Nos esperábamos unas ruinas impresionantes y no es nada de eso. Es un lugar bonito y con encanto, más por el paisaje que por las ruinas en si, además nos toco un guía local que hablaba mal ingles y nos enterábamos de la mitad. Es mejor ir lo más pronto posible ya que a partir de las 10 se llena de turistas y pierde bastante

L'endroit n'est pas loin de Hoi Han prévoir une bonne matinée car le chemin n'est pas une autoroute surtout vers la fin .À l'entrée du site ,il y a des navettes electriques vertes toutes neuves qui vous transportent jusqu' aux ruines qui ressemblent à un micro Angkor  du moins ce que les bombardements US ont épargné .La visite se fait rapidement car le secteur est peu étendu , à noter la construction étayée menaçant de s 'écrouler à proximité d 'un cratère de bombe ,ainsi qu'une sculpture représentant la fertilité masculine  dans tous ses états... Un spectacle de danse s'y déroule aux  pieds  des ruines de ce qui fut un refuge pour les maquisards .

We stayed at Danang and booked a day trip to My Son.  Maybe because I recently went to Siem Reap, and basically My Son is a mini version of Lady Temple in Siem Reap, so I wasn't too impressed.  If you enjoy temples and have time for it, then I guess it would be okay.  It was a bit of a drive too from Danang.

Nice temples in pretty surroundings. Not worth a visit if you've seen other temples. Don't do the $6 or $12 My Son tour sold in Hoi an - the price of entering the temples is conviently not included (the brocgurr mentioned that it excludes 'insurancr and personal fees', whatever this means), the village tour is a souvenir shop - overall, it is a utter scam!
A very nice place to visit for half a day, a nice wonder round what basically is a very very small ankor watt.
캄보디아를 가보지 않은분들에게는 괜찮습니다.캄보디아 앙코르 와트를 다녀오신 분들에게는 규모등 여러모로 시시하게 느껴질 수 있습니다.

Username: グローブ・タロッター
Comment: ミーソン遺跡ツアーをホテルにて予約しました。代金は7US$もしくは150,000VNDです。ホテルまでピックアップがあり、帰りは船で簡単な食事付きのコースです。遺跡までの道中は、ガイドさんの諸注意や遺跡にまつわるうんちく話で、なかなか楽しい時間を過ごすことが出来ました。遺跡到着後は少しの距離を徒歩で、途中からは電動カートで移動するという、ある意味エコな雰囲気ですが、ここだけ何処かの国や企業の援助を受けて完成したという感じが否めません。遺跡到着後は、ガイドの指示によりツアーが開始されるのですが、この荒廃した遺跡を良しとするべきなのか否とするべきなのかは判定の分かれるところです。私自身は、ベトナム戦争などの影響を受けたことなどの背景を理解しても、カンボジアにあるアンコールや東南アジアに多くある仏教・ヒンドゥ遺跡に軍配を上げたいと思います。勿論遺跡の保存状態や修復などは、比較するレベルにも達していません。また、ガイドにもよるのでしょうが、ベトナム戦争の話やリンガ(男性崇拝)の文言があまりにも多く登場して、少しばかり食傷気味でもありました。遺跡観光の起点には食堂などがあり、ひとときの安息が訪れますが、水や日よけの道具は必ず持参するべきだと思います。帰路の途中、河に遭遇し船にてホイアン中央市場付近の船着き場に帰ってきます。その途中に簡単な食事が提供されます。評価は、各人の期待度や歴史観や嗜好によって大きく変わると思いますが、一度は訪問されても良いのではないでしょうか。

Went on a tour organised by our homestay.The ruins themselves are ok to see, but if you've been to Angkor, Ayuthaya or Sukhothai they're not too impressive. My partner and I found the tragic effects on the area the most interesting-huge craters still in situ as reminder of the destruction. If you do the tour with the boat ride back its  an ok day trip for the scenery but personally wouldnt go back to My Son by itself.

I canceled online booking and booked tour through a hotel, actually it was much much cheaper!This tour actually need to prepare for it. Need to walk very long distance, very hot, recommend to bring comfy shoes, big bottle of water, sunnies, hat,if you have a towel, would be perfect! I was expected arriving exactly My son but bus arrived very far from My son, walk to electric car port, ride 2km then walk around my son. so many other tourist complained about awareness of this tour, hotness, and place.I loved this place, but I don't think I will be back.

The temples are ok and interesting all though for older people the walk in the heat and sun could be a little much. If your based in Hoi An don`t bother doing the boat ride back the journey is an hour with nothing to see, then you get to wood making island for 20 minutes then more boating back to Hoi An . I did not find that part worth the extra dong and I couldn`t buy wooden things to bring back through Aus customs anyway.
Maybe a bit commercialized, in the sense there were so many tour groups at one time that you missed a lot because of all the hustle and bustle.
Sanctuary is well maintained n serviced but the explanation n remains of temples is not properly done.
ホテルで予約。往復ともバスは6USD、入場料150,000VNDは別。帰りが小型船でランチ付きはもっと高い。フランスやベトナム戦争でアメリカに破壊されたり、持ち去られた云々と英語ガイドが言っていた。レンガつくりや首のない像が印象的。

베트남 전쟁 때 폭격 맞아 거의 무너져서 폐허상태입니다. 그래서 큰 기대하시면 실망하고요. 호이안에서 한 시간 반 정도 버스 타고 갔나 그랬어요. 전기자동차 타고 좀 갔다 내려서 걸어야 하는데 보행이 많이 불편하신 분 아님 애들이나 어르신들도 걸으실 만 할 거예요. 저흰 2월 말이라 20도 정도라서 덥지도 춥지도 않고 적당했는데 건기에 오시면 많이 더울 거 같았어요. 돌아오는 길은 버스 타고 오는 거랑 배타면서 점심 먹고 도자기 마을 갔다 오는 게 있는데 저는 그냥 버스 타고 왔어요. 호텔에서 보트투어 따로 해 준다해서요. 버스타고 오는 게 돈은 더 쌉니다.

i joined the tour by The Sinh Tourist. It says from the web that they offer pick up from hotel at 4:30am, coffee and breakfast will be provided at my son so we could enjoy sunrise there. But no one tried to email or call to ask where to pick up. So i had to confirm in person when i arrived hoi an, and they said the pick up time would be 5am. Eventually the car picked us up on time but stopped in the middle of nowhere, still in city, to wait for no one for more than 10 minutes. so we arrived my son a lot later than sunrise. No coffee provided and the tour guide was making joke of that instead of telling me where i can get a coffee. For my son, it was just okay, not surprising. Some original details of my son was worth paying attention, but the restoration was done without details, which was quite distracting.

Our 4th visit to Hoi An and we felt it was time to visit My Son. Private car $35USD picked us up at 7.00pm, we enjoyed the drive through the countryside. Took approx 1 hour, arrived and the car park was empty, bought our tickets 150000VND each and quickly passed through the ticket booth walked a couple of hundred metres over a bridge to where the shuttle cabs were waiting. 2km later and we were at the top, we hired a guide for 100000VND and headed off to see the ruins. Like many reviewers have previously said they certainly cannot be compared to Angkor Wat, a very very small sanctuary in comparison. The guide was with us for approx 1 hour explaining when the temples were built etc She had been a guide for 12 years and personally I think she needed a change, no enthusiasm or obvious pride or passion in what she was doing. When not explaining she talked on her phone and played games. At 9.30am we watched a Cham dance demonstration, I thought it was very well done. We then walked back to catch the shuttle cab back and OMG the place was crowded, car park was filled and there was a long line at the ticket entrance. Called into the museum on our way out looked around for a couple of minutes and then jumped into our air conditioned car and left the crowds.

The site itself is not big, compared to Angkor Wat or Ayutthaya. Some of the ruins are restored recently, making some of them look a little too new. We went there with a tour operating company which costed us $7 including a tour guide and transportation roundtrip to/from Hoi An. We arrived at the site around 10am and it was really really hot. The guide's English wasn't good and he couldn't explain much about the history of the place. It'd have been nicer if we go earlier when the weather is cooler and I'd recommend that you hire a professional guide otherwise, you wouldn't understand much what you were seeing.

Took a day trip from Hoi An went by bus returned by boat. If you've visited the temples at Ankhor Wat you'll find My Son underwhelming. Returned trip by boat wasn't really worth it. Boat traveled very slowly served entre sized portion of boiled rice and luke warm vegetables, unappetizing. All goods at Artisans village were extremely overpriced. Although their craftmanship is amazing.
Het is mooi om gezien te hebben, maar niet in vergelijking met de borobodur op Java. Toch heb ik erg genoten van mijn bezoek. Niet alleen om de tempels, maar ook de omgeving doet veel.

Onze gids vond vooral zichzelf heel erg leuk. Zelfstandig rondlopen was er dan ook niet bij. Eén gedeelte is goed bewaard/gerestaureerd; de rest viel me zwaar tegen. Kan me voorstellen dat het voor Vietnam iets bijzonders is, en het feit dat de Amerikanen het in de oorlog bijna helemaal plat gebombardeerd hebben (zoals onze gids -tig keer vermeldde) maakt ook indruk. Ik zou er niet meer heen gaan.

Išli sme sem organizovane z hotela z Hoi an. Cesta trvala približne hodinu. Chrámy sú krásne a staré, samozrejme v lesoch vo Vietname. Ak sa ale chystáte do Angkoru, tak povenujte čas radšej iným miestam v tejto oblasti. Ale ak sa tam už vydáte, určite nebudete ľutovať. Je to rozdelené na cca 4 časti, prvé sú väčšie s viac chrámami, ostané sú už menšie s 1-2 malými chrámami. Všetko sú to ale menšie chrámy, nie je to ani zďaleka ako Agkor. Celú časť aj s výkladom obehnete za 2-3hod.

We took a guided tour to the site in the late morning which was very crowded.We found ourselves unable to move around freely as there were so many people taking photos. I would suggest taking an early morning tour or making your own way there by motorbike as I feel the crowds took away from the significance of the site.If you have limited time in Hoi An I wouldn't make this your top priority.

I took the dawn tour of My Son, leaving Hoi An at 5am. It was a cool January day, so that wasn't really necessary, but I'm glad there weren't many people there. We had a tour guide who explained a lot of the history and masonry, which was by far the best part of the trip. The temples are so old and reflect a nearly forgotten history that has little resemblance to modern Vietnam. As a spectacle and photo op, the site is only marginally  cool - it's no Angkor Wat or Bagan. It's also quite small. If you have more than 2 days in Hoi An, I'd definitely venture out there, but if you have les time, don't feel bad about skipping.
c'est un endroit joli mais pas mon lieux favoris.Cela permet de sortir de Hoi An pendant une bonne matinée.La route pour se rendre sur le site est très belle

Ancient temple relics worth visiting if you are into culture and history. Rather long drive from Hoi An. Not very sure it was worth the visit. Personally I think the countryside you get to see on your way there is better. Maybe if I had more time in Hoi An it would have looked better...

Alles is middelmatig, wel paar mooie tempels maar snel uitgekeken. Wat vervelender was is dat onze gids die we daar ter plekke boekten, zeer slecht engels sprak en ze liep met het tempo van een slak. Geen intentie om iets meer op ons tempo te lopen. Al met al een matig dagje

My Son was smaller than we expected and in hindsight, since we were only 3 days in beautiful Hoi An, might not have been worth it to spend the whole morning going there (1 hour trip in bus). The fact that only a few structures still remain standing due to the Americans bombs during the Vietnam war makes it less interesting than what site seems to have been.The most interesting things about this Charm culture site is that has such a big influences from so many different religions, etc. but this can only be grasped if you have a good guide with good English, we did not and he seemed to try and tell us many interesting things but we could understand only 40%.

If you are in Hoi Ann only 3-4 days I am not sure if I would spend my time again in travlling there half a day unless I was sure I had a great guide which would explain a lot of the Charm culture and the history of the site.Visually there is only a few buildings left intact, a lot was destroyed by American bobs during the Vietnam war, there are a few intresting buidings and a lot of little sculptures around the site.We had a guide we arranged through the Hotel as a group tour and we understood 50% of his english, although he seemed to be telling some intresting things, specially intresting that the site has mix of so many cultures.
Một địa điểm thú vị đẻ tìm hiểu thêm về lịch sử. Tuy nhiên hơi thất vọng về việc bảo tồn các hiện vật, nhìn các tháp được trét lại bằng xi-măng thật không đẹp mắt chút nào.

I found these ruins to be very unimpressive. They were just a heap over over grown rubble and buildings falling down. The drive there was more interesting.  There was lots of walking and climbing so it's not suitable for anyone who struggles to climb hills.
ベトナム戦争の名残と文明遺跡が一緒に共存する場所。タイのアユタヤ遺跡、カンボジアのアンコールワット遺跡とはまた違った遺跡を見学出来るかと。。。ダナン空港まで行ったら足を伸ばしてみてください。

Since we are Indians ...we didn't find it great shakes .considering the wealth and treasure we have back home..this was just a tip of iceberg of our legacy back home .It was nice nevertheless .and one feels sad for the Vietnamese xoz they lost it all in all early invasions and attacks ..leaving them bereft and with little to show the world .The complex is well maintained . Structures are mostly ruined save a few that made it for our visit.Our guide was cute with his broken English and Vietnamese accented pronunciations .he made it funny for us while he kept emphasizing on the phallus symbol.The ride back was in a boat .this trip cost us 5dollars each hence we didn't expect much.we carried our lunch and we're glad ..coz they offered us boiled veggies with miniscule rice . They promised a visit to the carpentry village which was a sham..they knew they weren't taking us .it was too much to ask for in 5 dollars .We booked through am agency called dive in. We felt we would have been better off in a spa basking in the ancient town of hoian instead of coming here all the way .Hue would have been way better but we didn't have time .Avoidable.
Very historical place with loads of history and culture of Cham and Hinduism - unfortunately mostly destroyed during the Vietnam war

Wir haben es gegen 12 erreicht. Sie Sonne und die Temperatur von 30 grad (Winter) waren schwer ertragbar. Erst als es zugezogen ist, ist es angenehmer geworden.-bei kühlen Temperaturen besuchen Vieles ist zerstört aber man erahnt die unglaublichen Dimension der Anlage. Ich bin nicht sehr interessiert in Architektur, aber in Geschichte. Ein Führer ist nötig, wenn man viel erfahrne will, da es nur wenig Schilder gibt. Für Historiker und Archäologen ein muss! since there was bad wheather in hoi an I decided to visit my son with one of the early trips. I did the combination with a boat trip and visiting a wharf when returning. the pick up worked very well, the guide was lovely, very competent and friendly and his english was very good. the included breakfast was not what it was promised as it consisted just of an egg sandwich and water, no coffee. but fine, that's not important!my son has a great atmosphere and is situated in the middle of the jungle. you feel like you just discovered it when you go there in the early morning with no other tourist at the site. there was a mysterious fog in the jungle which made the scenery look unreal and very adventurous. however, the site is very small. on the map it seems like there's many different groups of temples but there's only two left and all the others where destroyed in the war. of the two ones left one is just a ruin without any decoration, but it's situated on a hill which is very nice. the other one is decorated slightly with reliefs and some of the buildings were restored very badly because there was all information of its former decoration missing. I've been to angkor wat before which is of course the most impressive pagoda in a very good state and it's not comparable to my son. however, my son is very ancient being built in the 4th century which is, of course, very interesting. going back by boat was a loss of time cause there's nothing to see on the river and also the short visit of the kam island is not very interesting. there's some carpenters doing great sculptures bit if you're interested you'd rather go by ferry from hoi an. the price for the trip was 15$ including the bus, the guide, the breakfast and the boat, but NOT including the ticket to my son which is another 7$. so I think it's not really cheap and you could also rent a motorbike for 4$ the day before and go there very early. so, summing up, if you didn't visit angkor wat you should take the few hours and go there!

Contratamos la excursion desde Hoi An y volvimos en barco. NO LO ACONSEJO! El grupo era enorme, perdimos mucho tiempo. Yo vendría en un grupo mas pequeño, eso si, recomiendo guía en My Son, ya que el sitio esta muy afectado y sin explicación te quedas indiferente.

Arrangements made in town to do $11 return bus tour including entrance. Overall the bus trip was enjoyable but a little chaotic and arrived back later than expected. The My Son ruins are just that and cover a small area and would recommend a guide to get the most from the attraction. Watch the weather as misty rains in the hills may hamper your photography. The place overrun by tourists so I would suggest private tour or get your own bike so that you arrive late morning after the first set of buses leave and control your own timetable.Thanks for the vote if you found this helpful.

Mon avis est certainement biaisé car ayant déjà visité les temples d'Angkor, j'ai été relativement déçue par ce site. La guerre étant passée par là, il ne reste quasiment rien et le peu qui reste est parfois entièrement recouvert de végétation, si bien que l'on passe à côté de montagnes sans se rendre compte qu'il s'agit d'une partie du site. Aufgrund des schlechten Wetters in Hoi An entschlossen wir uns zu einem Ausflug nach My Son. Der Trip war ganz ok, aber eigtl lohnt es sich nicht dafür 3/4 eines Tages zu opfern. ガイドがいれば別ですが、ダナンの博物館を訪れてから行く方がいいと思います。歴史に興味があれば、行くといいと思います。ホイアン市内から車で1時間くらいでした。ホテルでカーチャーターを頼みました。半日のコースで1000000ドンでした。見学を終えて車に戻ると、冷たいおしぼりとミネラルウォーターを出してくれました。

Did a half day tour to My Son with a return boat trip. Easy to explore but my advice would be to go with a guide as there are very little guidance boards to provide explanations. 1 to 2 hours is all the time you'll need for My Son - it is really small with the structures clustered in 5 groups. Signages are poor and you only get to read a brief background of the conservation work that is being done to each structure. There's nothing substantial about the Champa civilisation that built the buildings.Entry costs VND 100,000 per person. Children get in free and their definition of what constitutes "children" is very relaxed so I'm happy for that as my 10 year old got in free.We paid USD 30 for a private car to take us in. It's an hour's drive there, 2 hours there and an hour back. Good for a half day tour.

My Son is located 40km outside of Hoi An. You can go by bus (which I understand is quite long, by scooter or by car (it took us a little over an hour and cost us $30 excluding entry fee which is priced 100k VND). Mỹ Sơn is an interesting site if you’re into history and have some basic understanding of Hindu culture. The complex is large enough to spend a few hours exploring, and you don’t really need to hire a bike — I walked the whole site comfortably.

I enjoyed the visit more because I researched the area as I went, which helped me understand the significance of the different buildings and objects. Without that context, a lot of the site could feel underwhelming. I was told the audio guide isn’t very good, as it focuses more on dimensions and measurements rather than historical or cultural context.

Some parts of the site are reconstructed rather than original, which you can notice by differences in brick age and construction. The original temples used very specific brick-bonding techniques that stand out once you know what to look for.

Overall, it’s a pleasant and interesting place, but unless you’re a history buff, it may not feel as impressive as some other heritage sites. I enjoyed it personally, but I’ve seen more engaging ruins elsewhere. Still worth visiting if you have time — just do a bit of research beforehand to really appreciate it.

Dans un site d' une telle ampleur , ces deux touristes aurait dû être dégagé manu militari .

Vous vous devez de protéger ce site magnifique.

公元四世紀到十四世紀,美山山谷是〔占婆」歷任國王舉行宗教儀式的場所,越戰期間遭到美軍一星期的地毯式轟炸,70座寺廟及古塔中,只有20座倖免於難保存下來,1999年聯合教科文組織將美山寺廟列入世界遺產名錄。
進入園區步行約500m到電瓶車站（下山&上山都可以搭電瓶車）,下車後有一處開放式的表演廳,定時有[歌舞表演】有看沒有懂,再步行一小段路開始欣賞紅磚建築的〔美山寺廟〕-世界文化遺產。

入場費150000越南盾，（約台幣195元），進入園區走約5分鐘，即可到免費接駁車站，搭車可省10-15分鐘路程，在炎熱的美山，是一大福音。

ミーソン遺跡（Mỹ Sơn Sanctuary）は、ベトナム中部、クアンナム省に位置する古代チャンパ王国のヒンドゥー教の聖域です。
​主な特徴は以下の通りです。
​チャンパ王国の宗教的中心地:
​紀元4世紀から13世紀にかけて、ベトナム中南部に栄えたチャンパ王国の歴代の王たちが、ヒンドゥー教の神々、特にシヴァ神を祀るために神殿や塔を築きました。
​かつては70棟以上の寺院建築があったとされ、チャンパ文化の輝きを今に伝える貴重な遺跡群です。
​建築様式:
​赤レンガを用いた独特な建築様式が特徴で、接着剤を使わずにレンガを積み上げるという高度な技術が用いられています。
​ヒンドゥー教の神々や伝説上の生き物をモチーフにした**美しいレリーフ（浮き彫り）**が残されています。
​世界遺産:
​その歴史的、文化的、芸術的価値が認められ、1999年にユネスコの世界文化遺産に登録されました。
​登録基準は(ii)（インドからのヒンドゥー教の芸術や建築物が残り、文化交流を示す）と(iii)（東南アジアの交易で栄えたチャンパ王国の足跡が見られる）です。
​現状:
​ベトナム戦争中の爆撃などにより、多くの建造物が破壊されましたが、現在もいくつかの塔や祠堂が残り、当時の歴史と文化を伝えています。
​周辺はジャングルに囲まれ、神秘的な雰囲気が漂っています。
​ホイアンから南西へ約40km、ダナンからもアクセスが可能です。
​ミーソン遺跡は、東南アジアでも重要な古代宗教遺跡の一つとして知られています。

とても素晴らしく、行って良かったです。ホイアンの町からは車でしか行けないので、半日の日本語ガイド付きのツアーで行きました。建物が沢山有って、半日では全て見ることは不可能ですが、十二分に満足しました。ただ、日陰が殆どなくて、遺跡の中はほぼ無風なので、とても暑いです。帽子、サングラスに、冷却シートなどの暑さ対策と、水分補給が出来るように、水筒やペットボトル飲料などが必須です。ハンディ扇風機は熱風しか来ないので、余り役に立たなかったです。

El yacimiento es una pasada, y tiene la opción de audio guía. Dicho esto, las explicaciones de la audio guía son bastante pobres, solo se limitan a describir lo que hay pero proporcionan poco contexto histórico. Aun así el sitio merece la pena, pero recomiendo ir o muy temprano o tarde, antes de que cierren, ya que el calor puede hacerse insoportable.

Se puede coger si antelación las entradas en la caseta del recinto. Pero no aceptan tarjeta. A fecha actual siguen siendo 150.000 dongs. Aceptan dólares y euros también. Si vas por tu cuenta en moto hay parking en el recinto por 5000dongs. Hay aseos disponibles. Te acercan a las ruinas en un mini carrito eléctrico. La verdad que como están metidas en la selva-montaña hay bastante humedad. Son ruinas más o menos conservadas, podrían estar mejor, pero hay una zona tipo museo donde relatan que cayeron bombas en el terreno destrozando elementos. No son tan impresionantes como las ruinas de angkor ni las de indonesia, pero te llevas una buena impresión del estilo arquitectónico y obviamente no hay construcción semejante en Vietnam. A 40km aprox de 1h-40min según tú conducción temeraria, y también sales muchos tours desde hoi an. Recomendable si estás por Vietnam y quieres conocer el imperio de angkor.

Merece la pena acercarse si estás varios días en HOI AN. Los templos se conservan relativamente bien. No es ankor wat, ni por asomo, pero te sirve para hacerte una idea. Eso sí, prepárate para sudar, vas a flipar. Te apetecería darte un chapuzón sin no fuera por lo limpios que van los ríos. En moto desde HOI AN 55min, si vas como valentino rossi, 40min 100%. En una hora está visto.

Mình không biết nên gửi xe bên ngoài quán cà phê gần thánh địa Mỹ Sơn, vì họ ghi gửi xe miễn phí, nhưng sau khi lấy xe họ kêu uống nước, mình gọi 1 trái dừa với giá 30k. Ai đi thánh địa, nhớ chạy thẳng vào bên trong không gửi xe bên ngoài, mắc công lắm. Giá vé tham quan 100k, có xe điện nên đi khá thoải mái, rồi dạo vòng quanh khuôn viên cũng mát mẻ dễ chịu. Khi mua vé vào sẽ thấy một nhà lớn bên tay phải. Chịu khó đọc kĩ về lịch sử hình thành, và chiều dài văn hoá của khu Thánh địa, để khi vào khuôn viên xem khỏi bỡ ngỡ. Mình chỉ lỡ tiếc mục âm nhạc thôi vì không có thời gian cập nhật bên ngoài để biết mà tham gia cùng. Mỗi ngày hình như có 6 suất diễn theo giờ. Nếu được hỏi nhân viên phòng vé để được biết nha.
Khu vực cho bạn nào ưa khám phá lịch sử, với nhiều điểm di tích của dân tộc Chăm, nhiều hơn các điểm di tích Chăm khác trên cả nước...

Review & Travel Guide to My Son Sanctuary – A UNESCO World Heritage Site in Quang Nam, Vietnam

1. General Introduction:
My Son Sanctuary is an ancient complex of Champa temples nestled in a narrow valley, located about 40 km from Hoi An and 70 km from Da Nang. Recognized by UNESCO as a World Cultural Heritage Site in 1999, My Son is a must-visit destination for history lovers and those fascinated by ancient architecture and culture

2. Entrance Fees and Costs

Adult ticket
(Vietnamese & foreign)	  150,000	      Includes entrance fee and museum access
Student ticket 	                100,000	               Must present a valid
student ID

(to temple area)	                     Free	              Included in the ticket
price

Local tour guide         300,000 – 500,000       Negotiable or can split                             / group	                     cost in a shared group

💡 Money-saving tip: Go in a group to share the cost of hiring a guide, or use the free audio guide in the museum.

3. How to Get to My Son
✅ From Da Nang / Hoi An:

Motorbike rental: Around 120,000 – 150,000 VND/day. Travel time: 1.5 – 2 hours.

Taxi / Grab: Approximately 500,000 – 800,000 VND round trip.

Join-in tour: From 400,000 – 700,000 VND/person, usually includes transportation, entrance ticket, and tour guide.

✅ Road conditions:

Scenic countryside views.

Note: Some winding roads – caution is advised if riding a motorbike

4. Service Review at My Son Sanctuary
✅ Pros:

Well-preserved: Main temple clusters are clearly labeled and maintained.

Knowledgeable guides: Many speak good English and provide historical context.

Convenient shuttle: Easy access from entrance to the temple area.

Modern museum: Air-conditioned, informative exhibits and displays.

❌ Cons:

Limited food options: Only a few small stalls with basic drinks and snacks.

Few shaded rest spots: Hot and sunny during summer, limited places to rest.

No personal audio guide system: Not yet widely available for self-guided tours

5.Conclusion: Is My Son Worth Visiting?Where to Eat Near My Son

Ba Be My Son Eatery: Small local spot near the gate. Serves dishes like Mi Quang noodles, beef noodle soup, chicken rice – priced from 40,000 – 70,000 VND.

My Son View Café: Open-air café offering sugarcane juice, coffee, and bottled water.

👉 Tip: Bring your own water bottle, sun hat, and sunscreen if visiting during hot months (April – August).

6.Travel Tips for Visiting My Son

Best time to visit: Early morning (7:30 – 9:00 AM) or late afternoon (after 3 PM) to avoid harsh sunlight.

Dress code: Wear comfortable, respectful clothing and walking shoes.

What to bring: Hat, sunscreen, water, camera or phone with full battery.

Respect the site: Do not touch or climb on ancient structures.

𝐓𝐡𝐚́𝐧𝐡 đ𝐢̣𝐚 𝐌𝐲̃ 𝐒𝐨̛𝐧 – 𝐧𝐡𝐮̛̃𝐧𝐠 𝐤𝐲́ 𝐮̛́𝐜 𝐛𝐢̣ 𝐥𝐚̃𝐧𝐠 𝐪𝐮𝐞̂𝐧

Được UNESCO công nhận là Di sản Văn hóa Thế giới năm 1999, thánh địa Mỹ Sơn ẩn mình giữa núi rừng Quảng Nam. Không lộng lẫy, không ồn ào, nơi đây lặng lẽ gợi nhớ một thời vàng son đã lùi xa của vương quốc Chăm Pa.

🪬Từ thế kỷ IV, vua Bhadravarman cho dựng ngôi đền đầu tiên thờ thần Shiva. Từ đó, Mỹ Sơn trở thành trung tâm tâm linh của cả một đế chế. Với người Chăm Pa, Shiva là đấng tối cao – vừa hủy diệt vừa tái sinh, hiện thân cho vòng luân hồi bất tận của vũ trụ. Mọi tháp chính đều dành cho ngài, với biểu tượng Linga – Yoni tượng trưng cho sự sinh sôi và tái tạo.

🧱Những ngọn tháp ở Mỹ Sơn được xây bằng gạch nung đỏ au, ghép khít mà không cần vôi vữa – một bí thuật đến nay vẫn là điều bí ẩn. Nhưng khi đế chế Chăm Pa sụp đổ, những công trình ấy cũng dần ngã xuống. Người ta nhận ra: thần linh có thể bất tử trong niềm tin, nhưng di sản con người chỉ sống mãi nếu được gìn giữ bằng trái tim trân trọng.

Ngày nay, bước chân vào Mỹ Sơn là bước vào miền ký ức. Giữa rừng già tĩnh lặng, ta thấy bóng dáng một vương triều từng kiêu hãnh, nhưng cũng thấy sự mong manh của những điều tưởng chừng vĩnh cửu. Có những cái tên, chỉ cần nhắc đến là ký ức lùa về. Mỹ Sơn cũng vậy — một miền ký ức vỡ vụn, nhưng vẫn ở đó để nhắc ta rằng: không có đế chế nào bất tử, và những gì ta 𝐧𝐠𝐨̛̃ 𝐦𝐚̃𝐢 𝐦𝐚̃𝐢 𝐫𝐨̂̀𝐢 𝐜𝐮̃𝐧𝐠 𝐜𝐡𝐢̉ 𝐜𝐨̀𝐧 𝐭𝐫𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐡𝐨̂̀𝐢 𝐮̛́𝐜.

建议骑摩托车来，世界文化遗产：美山圣地（Thánh địa Mỹ Sơn），修建于公元500年，该地区是曾经统治越南中南部地区长达14个世纪的印度教占婆王国的心脏地区，所有的建筑都是由红砖瓦或石头堆砌而成，独特的砌筑工艺，红砖间缝隙极小，并保持千年不损坏，据说工艺已失传。

I visited My Son Sanctuary with my son and it was a truly memorable experience. The ancient ruins are surrounded by lush jungle, creating a magical and peaceful atmosphere.

Even though some parts are damaged due to time and war, you can still feel the history and cultural importance of the place. My son was fascinated by the old brick towers and carvings, and it was a great opportunity to learn about Cham history and architecture.

We recommend going early in the morning to avoid the heat and crowds. Don’t forget sunscreen, water, and good walking shoes — some paths can be uneven. Definitely worth a visit if you're staying in Hoi An or Da Nang!

少し遠出したくて、「ミーソン遺跡」か「バナヒルズ」で悩みまくった。
ぶっちゃけ両方行きたい。
なんなら、1日で2箇所行こうか。
とかまぁいろいろダナンについてから考えて、
気温や体力（私の）を考えると1箇所だなと。

「バナヒルズ」はテーマパークみたいな感じで結構人も多いし、しんどそうだし、遠いという理由で、「ミーソン遺跡」へ！！

早朝じゃないと、暑いし、人（ツアー客とか）が多くなるらしいので、AM05:30ごろホテル出発。

grabで約70分くらい。
朝の通勤ラッシュ帯。バイクえぐい。
だが、運転はとても上手くて、全く酔わない。神。

半分の確率でgrabのおぢに営業されるが、今回はミーソン遺跡付近にgrabがいないかもしれないからと、念のためLINE交換。（夫が）

ミーソン遺跡、思った以上に迫力あり。
大自然。
そして、不思議な遺跡たち。
何より、空気が綺麗。
久々に肺の中の空気入れ替え作業。

もちろん、毛虫、アリの大行列、なぞの虫、カラフルなトカゲ。

いちいち叫んでたらキリがないので、受け入れる。

日陰がないとう事前情報から、完全防備で来るも、サウナみたいになってくる。
エアリズム、意味なし。
脱ぐと涼しい〜。でも、直射日光痛い〜。

とにかく、スケールがでかいっていうのは、私が映ってる写真を見たらわかるだろう。

滞在時間は約1時間30分くらいかな。

行き帰り、ゴルフカートみたいなのに乗るんだけど、
4、5人スタッフさんいるのに、ずっとチルってて一向に動かない。
うん、めちゃくちゃ楽しそう。こんな職場いいね。

そして、海外あるある。
急に前触れもなく動く。結構なスピードで。

最後に入口付近の展示室見て、行きのgrabのおぢに LINE。
思った通り、おぢ「現金支払い」を要求。

私「こういう交渉、英語でできんのんだが」
夫「まかせろ、慣れてるから」
私「…慣れてるとは？」

その後を要約すると、
現金ない！を主張し、ホテルのATMでおろしたらええやんっていうおぢに、できない！を主張し、そしたら、おぢが折れて、grabでなんやかんやして、無事にことなきを得た。

車内での会話。
夫「だいたいキレられるけど、今回はブチギレられんかったわ」
私「…？」

意外とミーソン遺跡の近くにも何台かgrabあったので、 LINE交換しなくても行けそうなので、参考までに。笑

ホテルに着くのが、まだギリギリ朝食のやってる時間帯なので、ダッシュで向かう。

ひと仕事終えた後のスイカジュース、最高だぜ！！！！

My Son is definitely worth the trip, just expect to be very hot! It was 29 C when we went and the humidity made for a very sweaty day. We brought sun umbrellas, water, and fans but it was still hard to beat the heat. There is an electric cart to take you into the park but once you're there it's still a decent walk to get to the scattered sites.

Only about 25% of the original complex is still standing. However, it's still worth the experience to see some 1000 year old temples and their carvings. Ironically, the parts of the temple that look the oldest are actually the newest. The brick and mortar support reconstructions trap water and moss grows on them. The original bricks don't use mortar and are water tight, creating a clean surface. (Probably also painted with an oil resin for water resistance, or so they think).

There is a 30 minute dance performance that is in the shade with some A/C fans blowing. It's a performance of the Cham people and is well done. Be sure not to miss it!

Archeologists are working on uncovering more temple ruins so the site will be growing over time.

I gave it 4 stars because the paths could use improving (gaps and uneven stones that my kids were falling down from tripping on the edges). The signage explaining each site could use improving (we had a guide which was very helpful). The electric carts only come to pick you up one by one, therefore a long line grew to get back to the parking area. That said, we still had a great time and felt the cultural experience added to our overall trip. I think it will for you as well!

Took a taxi up to My Son. From the car park to the site is a shuttle and a 500m or so walk which surprised some less mobile people who gave up.

My Son è uno dei siti archeologici più affascinanti del Vietnam centrale, un complesso di templi in rovina immerso nella giungla che testimonia la grandezza della civiltà Champa. Patrimonio UNESCO,  My Son risale al IV-XIII secolo e fu il centro religioso del regno Champa, dedicato a divinità indù come Shiva. I templi, costruiti con mattoni rossi senza malta grazie a una tecnica unica Cham, subiscono gravi danni durante la guerra del Vietnam, con crateri di bombe ancora visibil.

Mỹ Sơn: Cuộc Trở Về Sau Hai Thập Kỷ
​Gần 20 năm, thung lũng thần linh Mỹ Sơn vẫn nằm gọn trong vòng tay núi Chúa, nhưng lớp rêu phong trên những viên gạch nung dường như đã dày thêm ký ức. Trở lại lần này, tôi không nhìn bằng sự tò mò của tuổi trẻ, mà bằng cái chạm tay của một người bạn cũ – trầm mặc và thấu hiểu.
​Màu gạch Chăm vẫn đỏ rực kiêu hãnh giữa đại ngàn xanh ngắt, một minh chứng cho kỹ thuật xây dựng từ thế kỷ thứ 4 vẫn là ẩn đố quyến rũ. Tiếng kèn Saranai và vũ điệu Apsara vang lên giữa lòng tháp cổ, tạo sợi dây liên kết vô hình giữa quá khứ huy hoàng và hiện tại sống động.
​Dưới góc nhìn của một người làm về sinh thái và nhân quả, tôi trân trọng sự "trần trụi" của di tích. Mỹ Sơn đẹp nhất khi không bị tô vẽ hay thương mại hóa quá đà. Những vết sứt sẹo của thời gian và chiến tranh vẫn còn đó, nhưng đã được xoa dịu bằng sự bảo tồn tử tế.
​Vạn vật đổi thay, nhưng giá trị tâm linh và nghệ thuật đích thực thì bất biến. Mỹ Sơn sau 20 năm không già đi, mà chỉ sâu sắc hơn, nhắc nhở ta rằng: Mọi sự tồn tại, nếu được chăm sóc bằng sự hiểu biết, đều sẽ kể những câu chuyện xuyên thiên niên kỷ.

My Son is a marvellous ancient ruins. It used to be Lord Siva temple. It’s a UNESCO world heritage site. It’s a good place to know the history for the country. A Hauntingly Beautiful Journey into the Heart of Ancient Champa Civilization

My Son Sanctuary is not just another archaeological site; it is a powerful, emotional portal to a lost kingdom. Located in a lush green valley surrounded by jungled hills, this UNESCO World Heritage site is Vietnam's most significant complex of Cham heritage.

Walking among the weathered brick towers, you can almost hear the echoes of ancient Sanskrit prayers. The architecture is breathtaking – the brickwork was laid without any visible mortar, yet it has withstood centuries of both time and conflict. The intricate carvings of deities, mythical beasts, and dancers on the walls tell silent stories of spiritual devotion, artistic mastery, and a deep connection to Hindu cosmology.

It’s crucial to understand the history here: this was a active religious and political capital of the Champa Kingdom for over a thousand years. While the ruins bear scars of war, this only adds to their profound resonance. They stand as resilient witnesses to history's layers—glory, decline, and rediscovery.

Practical tip: Go early in the morning to avoid the heat and crowds. Hiring a knowledgeable local guide is highly recommended; they bring the stones to life, explaining the symbolism of each tower dedicated to different gods (like Shiva, the protector-king). Combine your visit with the excellent Champa Sculpture Museum in Da Nang to see the most exquisite carvings up close.

My Son is a humbling and essential experience. It’s less preserved than Angkor Wat but feels more intimate and contemplative. It’s a must-visit for anyone seeking to understand the deep historical and cultural tapestry of Central Vietnam, beyond its beautiful beaches.

Rating: ★★★★★ (5/5)

На русском языке:
Завораживающее путешествие в сердце древней цивилизации Тямов

Святилище Май Сон (My Son) — это не просто еще одна археологическая зона. Это мощный, эмоциональный портал в потерянное королевство. Расположенный в зеленой долине, окруженной поросшими джунглями холмами, этот объект Всемирного наследия ЮНЕСКО является самым значительным комплексом наследия тямов во Вьетнаме.

Бродя среди древних кирпичных башен, обветренных временем, почти физически слышишь отзвуки санскритских молитв. Архитектура захватывает дух — кирпичи были уложены без видимого раствора, и тем не менее, башни выстояли под натиском веков и конфликтов. Искусная резьба по камню, изображающая божеств, мифических животных и небесных танцовщиц апсар, рассказывает безмолвные истории о духовной преданности, художественном мастерстве и глубокой связи с индуистской космологией.

Очень важно осознавать исторический контекст: это была действующая религиозная и политическая столица Тямского царства на протяжении более тысячи лет. Хотя руины несут на себе шрамы войны, это лишь усиливает их глубокое звучание. Они стоят как несгибаемые свидетели разных слоев истории — величия, упадка и нового открытия.

Практический совет: Приезжайте рано утром, чтобы избежать жары и толп. Настоятельно рекомендую нанять местного гида — они оживят камни, объясняя символику каждой башни, посвященной разным богам (например, Шиве, царю-защитнику). Совместите посещение с отличным Музеем скульптуры тямов в Дананге, чтобы рассмотреть самые изысканные резные детали вблизи.

Май Сон — это смиряющий и совершенно необходимый опыт. Он менее отреставрирован, чем Ангкор-Ват, но ощущается более интимным и располагал к созерцанию. Это обязательное к посещению место для любого, кто хочет понять глубокую историческую и культурную ткань Центрального Вьетнама, выходящую за рамки его прекрасных пляжей.

My Son Sanctuary, a Hindu holy site and Cham ruins – architecture and ancient civilization. This is a group of partially destroyed temples, once thriving religious sites for the early Cham people, but long abandoned.

This holy site is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, featuring stone carvings of Hindu deities and towers nestled in the tropical jungle.

Mỹ Sơn is a place that is absolutely worth visiting — one of the most fascinating archaeological sites in Vietnam and a true journey back in time. The ancient Cham temple complex, hidden in a lush green valley, makes a strong impression with its atmosphere, history, and the surrounding nature.
🏛️ Incredible history and architecture
Walking among centuries‑old temples is an unforgettable experience. Although part of the complex was destroyed, the remaining structures still impress with their details and unique character. Here you can truly feel the rich heritage and architectural mastery of the Champa civilization.
🌿 Beautiful natural surroundings
Mỹ Sơn enchants not only with its ruins but also with the scenery. The mist-covered hills, the sounds of nature, and the peaceful valley create a magical atmosphere unlike anywhere else. It’s the perfect place to slow down, connect with nature, and take a break from the hustle and bustle of the city.
🧭 Well prepared for visitors
The paths are well marked, the area is maintained, and informational signs help visitors understand the history of each temple group. The staff are friendly and helpful, contributing to a calm and pleasant experience throughout the visit.
⭐ Summary
Mỹ Sơn is a must‑see for anyone interested in history, culture, or simply looking for a unique and memorable excursion. Its magical atmosphere, beautiful setting, and impressive ruins make it a place that stays with you long after the visit. Highly recommended!

Понравились руины Мишон, однозначно советую к посещению, чтобы познакомиться с культурой цивилизации Чампа.

Брали утреннюю экскурсию через гидйоргайд с возвращением на лодке и обедом — хороший вариант, рекомендую. Организация удобная, всё по времени.

Посещали руины в конце января: было не холодно, скорее даже душновато. Лично мне было очень жарко, потому что много ходили. Тем не менее советую взять с собой лёгкую кофту — если возвращаться на лодке, может быть прохладно из-за ветра.

Людей много, особенно туристических групп, в том числе довольно шумных. Будьте готовы много ходить — как к самим руинам, так и внутри комплекса.

Хороший совет для фото: делать снимки во время шоу — в этот момент большинство людей уходит, и появляется шанс сфотографировать руины почти без людей.

Обязательно удобная обувь (кроссовки). Видел женщину в каблуках — ей было реально тяжело передвигаться.

К комплексу подвозят электрокары, но не прямо к руинам — от конечной точки всё равно нужно идти пешком около 10 минут.

В целом впечатления очень хорошие, место атмосферное и точно стоит потраченного времени.

En la aldea de Son, comuna de Thu Bo, ciudad de Da Nang, en el valle de Son, aún se conserva un importante complejo de torres de templos hindúes, símbolo del arte champa, que se desarrolló continuamente desde el siglo IV hasta el siglo XIII.
Los antiguos champa fueron criados para ser maestros del arte de la construcción en ladrillo, esculturas y tallados de gran belleza, inspirados en imágenes de deidades, sacerdotes, danzas, plantas, animales y sacrificios. DEBIDO A SU IMPORTANCIA COMO EJEMPLO DE INTERCAMBIO CULTURAL E INTEGRACIÓN DE CULTURAS EXTERNAS, ESPECIALMENTE EL ARTE Y LA ARQUITECTURA DEL HINDUISMO CON LA CULTURA NATIVA, COMBINADO CON UN VIVO REFLEJO DE LA HISTORIA CULTURAL DEL REINO DE CHAMPA EN EL SUDESTE ASIÁTICO, EN DICIEMBRE DE 1999, LA UNESCO LO INSCRIBIÓ EN LA DECLARACIÓN MUNDIAL

Great ruins from the 8th to 13th century and some of the few left greater building complexes from the Cham people. A lot was destroyed, obviously, by US bombs during the Vietnam war, but the whole atmosphere of the complex is still unique. The different sites are now called complexes A to K, of which groups A and especially B are the most significant (some you can even enter), but they're all worth a look. Close to nature: we even spotted a monkey in the trees close to complex A.

My Son Sanctuary is truly a must-see destination for anyone interested in history and culture. The ancient Hindu temples, nestled in a lush green valley, create a peaceful and mystical atmosphere that is deeply moving. We were amazed by the intricate brickwork and the enduring spirit of the Champa ruins, which have stood the test of time for centuries.
​Walking through the site felt like stepping into another era. The surrounding mountains and the quiet beauty of the landscape made the experience very special. It is a place of great historical significance, and seeing the preservation efforts firsthand was impressive. Whether you are a history buff or just looking for a serene escape into nature, My Son offers a unique and unforgettable experience. It was definitely one of the most meaningful parts of our journey.

You want my advice? Do not waste your time visiting this site.In itself, the place is very important to understand history and to ask questions about what terrible was done by the war.But the thing that leaves me disgusted is how it's handled.People taking pictures in hatches smiling, guides who (although they should be the first to convey seriousness) invite customers to take it all as if it were a game.The shooting range then? Pathetic. There was a war in that place and what are you doing? You're putting up a range?Honestly, if I were a veteran of that war, I would feel outraged.You want my advice? There's so much better to see, don't waste your time.

Thousands of civilians have died in these places and for years a massacre has been fought that has marked history. Amazing how a place of war was turned into a circus. Really a shame because it would also be an interested visit in itself. Getting into the tunnels is very special. But other than that the site has been transformed into a kind of crowded amusement park. People taking pictures and laughing inside holes in the ground where poor soldiers were hiding. As you walk through this place in the distance, the gunshots from the shooting range are heard. They give you the chance to have fun with a rifle used by soldiers. Very seedy and disappointing. Just a waste of time. Zero respect.

Unfortunately the classic tourist trapAll in all overcrowded, insufficient framing of the action, constant shooting noise, since you can shoot with an AK. From Saigon round trip took 6 hours - the time is not worth it.

We visited today with a lovely guide .The day was going well and everyone was enjoying the day . Our 2 boys wanted to shoot the AK47 and the M16 and we paid for 30 rounds for each .The man at the shooting area was just the rudest man we have met in a very long time and obviously unhappy with his job I watched him load approx half the rounds into the magazines and both boys thought he was role playing as he pushed them around . We have spent 3 weeks in Vietnam and the people have been amazing so I naturally expected a second lot of ammo.This did not come .Our guide and ourselves spent the next 30 mins arguing with the supervisor . They told us they had cameras so we ask them to check but they refused and eventually gave us the extra rounds .The AK 47 the worlds most reliable assault  rifle  jammed every third round  I suspect the guns are very poorly maintained , perhaps not fit for purpose . This ruined the day .

Very sad experience, if I can make a suggestion I would avoid it in every way.I state that the site is invaded by dozens of tourist torpedoes that roam like ants between the various installations of the site, and therefore make it impossible to observe serenely the experience of this war that, I recall, claimed three million victims among the Vietnamese and 60 thousand among American soldiers.It is precisely this war that has turned it into a kind of playground of horror, with even a delusional final attraction, which provides that the tourist can, for a fee, fire 20 bullets with a war rifle, as if we were on the set of Platoon.All stuffed with black and white propaganda videos, in the Mussolini style of the videos of the light institute in Italy.To be honest, how the Vietnamese, a courageous and dignified people, have been able to turn a profound opportunity to reflect on the absurdity of this war into a freak show, truly astonishes me!If I may give you some advice, go and see the War Remnant Museum in Saigon-This is a good document of what this war represented for the Vietnamese and for all the generations who in those years lived the horrors reflected

Very disappointed in the customer service of the tour. We only had one day to spend in Ho Chi Minh and were very excited to do the tunnels. We booked online and specified our hotel for pick up as required. We were told we would be picked up from the hotel at 7:30am so woke up early to be ready. At 7:25am we messaged the company on WhatsApp and via Tripadvisor to check we were getting picked up. No reply but we waited. It got to 7:50am and we were getting worried so messaged again and found a number to call. We were told the tour had already left! The driver claims he came to the hotel and no one was there. No attempt was made to call us or message us. We were sat waiting for almost an hour in the reception that had glass doors with a clear view of the street outside and nobody came or called reception to say they were here. If we had not of called we would of been left waiting and waiting. To make matters worse we were only given half of our money back when we didn’t even get to go on the tour by no fault of our own! We explained the situation over the phone and received no help being told there’s nothing we can do. Also buchten wir eine Tour über unser Hotel (540 TD pro Person, davon 110 TD Eintritt). Nach längere Fahrt kamen wir an und wurden binnen 1 1/2 Stunden durch die Anlage geleitet. Die Informationen waren eher eintönig. Einzig die Begehung der Tunnel Anlage war etwas interessanter. „Höhe Punkt“ die Möglichkeit nach Erwerb von Munition (min. 10 Schuss; teilbar auf maximal 2 Personen a 5 Schuss zu insgesamt rd 600 TD) mit einem automatischen Gewehr oder einer Pistole zu schießen (ob das an einem Ort, der an den Krieg erinnern soll, in dem mehr als 3 Millionen Menschen starben sein muss, soll jeder selbst entscheiden). Insgesamt absolut nicht zu empfehlen.

I’m assuming people who rate this as a five star experience do so because of its historical significance.  From the gleeful presentation of the various killing devices to the references of caught soldiers as “Saigon Souvenirs” or “barbecue”, this tour held none of the sober reflection that it deserves.  Our tour guide was laughing at these jokes and even compared his junk to the tuber size tapioca root.  I’m all for middle school humour, this tour was not that place.The background noise of the perpetual gunfire explosion (which we assumed was a background soundtrack to create authenticity) was actually a live shooting range where it was recommended that we too shoot an assault rifle.  It was not only extremely loud, but disturbing; it’s inclusion felt disgusting.Tourists posed with VC mannequin soldiers for photos, and the atmosphere felt light and careless as we stood dumbfounded.Days before, at the S21 and Killing Field Memorial in Cambodia, opportunity was created to reflect on the tragic losses.  There was nothing in this experience that created space for reflection for the significant loss of life.  It was created as propaganda for the “winning” side.... with a little capitalism sprinkled in.We spent 5 minutes in the tunnel.Please read other reviews for alternative historical viewing.

I understand the relevance of having somewhere like this to educate people and tourists on the history of the area and the resilience of the people, but this just felt like a big tourist trap.  It was impossible to get near 90% of the exhibits due to the sheer number of people; and we were pretty much rushed through.The safety aspect of the tunnel crawl needs to be addressed - my partner did a 20m section in a group; and the guide disappeared up ahead and did not stay back to ensure the rest of the group were ok.  There was a fork in the tunnel, with sections going off in different directions and no instruction as to which way to go.  Luckily my partner chose the right one and found his way to an exit; by which time he was completely panicked.The shooting range felt inappropriate on a site of so much harrowing history; and I felt that there were much more respectful ways to honour the land and it's people.  For those who wanted to shoot at the range, it was also ridiculously overpriced.  Minimum of 10 bullets to be purchased at a price of around $5AUD each bullet. The souvenir shop was also very overpriced, with standard fans and bric a brac that were available everywhere else for a fraction of the price.It is a shame that so many tickets are sold daily here (our guide told us around 5000 people attend each day); and maybe the numbers should be limited.  If you could actually get near any of the exhibits and hear the stories, this could be a very important and thought provoking experience..... however the sheer number of people and tour groups completely limits and prevents this.  Very disappointing!

We visited the Cu Chi Tunnels based on a number of reviews stating it’s a ‘must see’ when you are in Ho Chi Minh City. Have to admit I wish I had researched it better before we went. It’s essentially a place where over 50.000 people were killed. There is little to reflect this at the sight. Instead, torture is glorified, people are encouraged to climb on and have a selfie next to a tank and the worst part was the optional activity to fire an AK47 (using real bullets!!) at the firing zone. The noise in this area was deafening and it felt sick that people were laughing and joking about how many shots they had got on target. It felt more like an amusement park than an opportunity to see part of the history that changed Vietnam. Just think again ....

While Going down of the Tunnel we had to crawl on our knees where probably some starved to death. And One of Child was terribly afraid of Lizards she didnt want to go down until we all went down without her, She was in the middle of the line and kept looking around while crawling and then she screamed and pushed almost all of us to move aside but it was too narrow of a space we wondered why she had screamed and then we saw there was 6 lizards around the walls and one of them getting cutted by the tail accidentally by my daughter and it was still moving so all of my Children just ran somewhere and we had to look for them cause they were absolutely not able to be found! Until we found them at the "Shooting range" still screaming and running around cause one of the lizards was on her back and the sound of the shooting,we saw most of our children having redness on they're legs and we saw that it was scorpion bites. It was a worrying situation about the bites, I do not reccomend this to anyone reading.

Ce monument est considéré comme une chose à faire si vous êtes à Saïgon. N’y aller pas, c’est vraiment nul, apart des trous il n’y a rien à voir. Je suis pourtant passionné d’histoire, mais cette visite n’a aucun intérêt. Sans oublier le guide qui fait de la propagande. Cela ne vaut clairement pas l’heure et demi de car.

We organised a trip through Vietnam Adventure tours. Our host Phu was very informative although he did talk ALOT. He has a way of telling the same thing 5-6 times over. He handed out cards to most people on the bus home to fill in trip advisor reports and interestingly ignored us and another family who had the same feeling as us so you might want to keep that in mind when you read the reviews.If you are going by bus try to organise a Sunday. It was still busy but I think it would add another hour to the trip each way on another day. We stopped at the customary souvenir shop on the way. Ok fine.Ok, the Cu Chi tunnels. It is fascinating and the ingenuity is something to behold however my wife and I had this uncomfortable feeling all day. It seemed like a Ripley’s Believe it or Not, crossed with a Madam Tussaud  and throw in a shooting range which all created to get this tour that is the Cu Chi tunnel.The shooting range in particular horrified us. People pay 600 000 dong to shoot bullets into the hill. Interestingly the gun shots in the background had great effect as you walked through the jungle. The gun range was the “rest stop” where you can have a drink and a bite to eat. The sound is deafening and to be honest we couldn’t wait to get out of there. One tip, you might want to take your own food as you only get one bottle of water for the day. I think you could pare this back, have some guides at the site that perhaps lived in the tunnels or had family that did.60 000 died here, it was relentlessly bombed with agent orange  and to have people standing in tanks doing the selfies and shooting off machine guns did not feel at all right. The gallery of traps and impaling devices was next level. By the way, if you are going to be sensitive to the communist propaganda this is not for you. Phu’s family was from the south so he was able to provide some balance which would have been missing otherwise. Our advice, hire a driver for the day, buy your own ticket and perhaps organise a personal guide although the whole thing is pretty self explanatory. It really is an exhibition of ingenuity and demonstration of how horrible humans can be to each other be it interesting if done differently.

Obligated to share that I encountered a black scorpion while touring a "second" tunnel, or what seemed to be a bonus tunnel. This forced 10 of us to backtrack out of the tunnel, including a family of four with two boys. This should have been reported to the tunnel management, so that specific tunnel with an aggravated scorpion could be shut down immediately.Tunnel management should make safety first by ensuring a clear tunnel crawling safety protocol is established and followed, with safety warnings, and pre-screening and with close coordination with the tour operators. For example, a safety sweep should be done before any visitors enter any tunnel.
I felt that a shooting range at a place where 55,000 died in pretty horrific circumstances was a wee bit insensitive and inappropriate
Spennende å besøke Cu Chi-tunellene, men stemningen ble helt ødelagt av heftig skyting. Dette burde være et minnesmerke for ettertanke og refleksjoner, og ikke en lekeplass for skytegale personer.
This is the place if you are into medieval methods of maiming and killing adversaries or shooting machine guns.

I am an anti Vietnam War baby boomer who wanted to visit the tunnels, as I understood that they were quite remarkable.  I also understood that there likely would be more than lingering anger at the role of the U.S. in the war.  Leaving aside the genius of those who constructed the tunnels, the opening anti-US screed in the movie which greets visitors was overwhelming.  Later on in the tour, a "docent" proudly displayed a series of jungle traps, designed to impale our soldiers in various parts of their bodies.  I was appalled and nauseated.  There was nothing redeeming about this site.
No war feeling. To crowded. You feel like cattle. If you are up north. Check the winh mokh tunnels instead. Less people and bigger system.
היה חם מאוד והמדריך שלנו היה לא רציני ולא נתן כמעט מידע, רק התבדח על דברים. לנו היתה חוויה לא טובה ולא הייתי ממליצה לאחרים על המקום
This is overrated. First, much of what you will see is a replica. Next, its crowded.  Its also far from Saigon which would be ok if the site was actually worth seeing.

The shooting range at the tunnels is very loud. My ears felt blocked afterwards and I’ve developed tinnitus, ringing in the ears which I’ve not been able to shift since going there. BE CAREFUL!!!! Please flag so more people can read this review.
You may simply join any group around the hotel you stays at 20% to 30% of the price. The different only being private or group tour.

I can’t understand why this is the most popular attraction in Saigon. It’s interesting, yes. But is it worth battling traffic for 4 hours (2 hours one way!), absolutely not. I thought the War Remnants museum was much more interesting. If you have nothing to do it’s not a bad way to burn a day but definitely don’t do this if you’re stretched for time.

Hmmmmmm , we all know about the Vietnam war etc etc very bad time in many people's lives,  but I'm afraid the cu chi tunnels is just a money maker now most of it is mock up and unbelievably packed with tourists . Shame as its just a cash cow

We took a small group tour (12 people)for $29US per person each which included 1 bottle of water and the $5US entrance fee. The tour company was Asiana Link TravelWe waited 40 mins for a pick up then ran around from corner to corner with the guide trying to coordinate where the bus would pick us up. It was 2 hour + ride out to the tunnels with a unnecessary ‘bathroom’ stop at a local handicraft spot. Once we arrived it was crowded with many other groups of tourists, some very large groups whose guide used a speaker that was deafening. You move from spot to spot to see various life size dioramas of Cu Chi Guerrilla’s encampments, some bamboo traps, a kitchen, examples of tunnel openings but most (or all) have been destroyed. A walk past the snack bar and a gun range where you pay extra to shoot a AK-47. Then you get to crawl through a network of tunnels with 6 different exits. The tunnels have been doubled in size for tourists but it is supposed to give you the idea. A quick tea stop with a sampling of fresh tapioca root then off to see the 30 min Viet Cong propaganda video that was of some interest to see what they were thinking but a poor production so hard to hear and too long. Finally a two hour drive back to Ho Chi Minh City. It has basically been rebuilt so nothing original (if there ever was after the war) and what is left has been trampled by tourists. If you felt you must go, book a private car, pay your own admission and roam the site at your own leisure. It is pretty self explanatory and you could find gaps to see things between tour groups. Or go later and have the place to yourself.

We did a half day tour with Panda company which was very bad by itself (bad transportation, guide without proper English...)The place itself was also so poor! Half of the attractions are just for you to pose and picture without really something behind it. Nothing important is being transferred and you don't really learn a lot.The shooting range is meters from the stop which makes such a loud noise.And the movie- from 1967 just saying over and over how the garilla was heroes.Not recommend!!

If I had known beforehand that this place was marketed to suit the western dollar and not to act as a way of remembering the dead I would not have come. The entire place has way too many tourist groups and at every station our tour guide would say "ok hurry up take picture the next group is coming" some people in our group just wanted to look at things for a while longer without being pushed to the next station. It was so rushed and incredibly inauthentic. I absolutely hated the tank and guns. Firstly, why should we be commodifying this war? Its absolutely disgusting. People should not be allowed to climb all over a tank and take selfies on something that has killed thousands of people. And the guns? Disgraceful. Why on earth someone would want to PAY to shoot an AK47 and M16 that was used to kill thousands on that same ground is very taboo! Our tour guide would not stop talking and praising the act even though nobody in our group was interested. The whole place is so sad and depressing. I imagined walking through an eerie but tranquil place at my own pace looking and imaging the life of the vietnamese during this sad time. Instead we were pushed through an amusement park making a mockery out of what the vietnamese sacrificed during this time. Then it ended with an extremely uncomfortable propaganda video that pretty much said "kill all Americans" 200 times. Dont go here!!!!!!!!

Det skulle vi prøve. Men så skuffet blev vi. Vores guide fortalte en masse historie og det var også spændende, men jeg er så nysgerrig, at jeg blev ved med at spørge om hvordan man konserverende disse ældgamle byggematerialer, og kunne ikke få et svar. Det viste sig da også, at kradsede man i overfladen, kunne man se at materialerne var af nyere dato og kunne umulig stamme fra krigstiden. 1999 var en af de prægninger jeg fandt tilfældigt. Jeg snakkede med en kollega som havde boet i Saigon i 8 år, og fik bekræftet at dette sted udelukkende var bygget til turismen....!
Nicht empfehlenswert....Massenabfertigung und peinliche Kaffeefahrt, die nur auf das Verkaufen von Touristenware ausgelegt war. Durch die Orte selber wurde man durchgejagt ohne wirklich was zu sehen.
Good place.very recommended and good for knowledge and new experience. We get good tour guide during trip

I came to this after being advised it was one of the most important places to visit in Vietnam so I had hoped for what I'd hope to be quite a harrowing account of a terrible war. Unfortunately, there were few positives to take from it. Firstly, it was presented in a very entertaining manner where guest were encouraged to take photos in a tank, and shoots guns. I found the whole manner incredibly distateful and quite insulting to the memories of all those affected by the war. Now, I would say that there was another guide taking a small group at the same time as us and clearly his group was captivated and realising this wasn't an adventure playground. If everyone was like that guide, this place would be a lot better. Our guide just made terrible jokes and kept talking about ak-47s. It's a good idea but it's become horribly distorted. I don't go to a museum like this to enjoy myself, i go to realise the atrocities and attempt to understand how terrible it was. I have been to a lot of locations similar to this in Asia and they were all tastefully designed for tourists - such as the killing Fields in Cambodia which was harrowing to walk around in. This should have been harrowing but everyone was having fun. Our guide kept talking about the guns numerous times throughout the tour and who could wait to use them. I don't understand why people want to use guns here unless you want to put yourselves in the shoes of those fighting - which is not for fun! Very strange atmosphere, and one I wouldn't recommend for anyone who wants an authentic experience. If you like to have a laugh at these sights, go for it. On the positive side, the tunnels were an experience to really put yourselves in the shoes of those fighting in the war.
