Also, the movie at the end was interesting as you saw the other side propaganda. We are always used to seeing the side of the west so it's always good to see both sides. If they changed the way the site was designed for, this would be transformed into a great experience.

There is more than 1000+ better things to do in HCMC than this! I really wanted to visit the tunnel system, unfortunatelly the whole business built on Cu-Chi is a tourist rip-off! Basically all tourist agencies offering Cu-Chi tours are just reselling internet well known crappy agencies, which forces you to visit some kind of shady shops (because they get commissions). The Cu-Chi tunnel system park is also a tourist trap by itself, even without annoying guide with language skills of a 2 year old...
Absolute Zeitverschwendung. Billig, schlechtes Englisch und absolut überfüllt. Sie bieten auf einem Kriegsgebiet eine shootingrange an. Absolut geschmacklos.
Not my kind of thing. .... a little redundant but it is history and you must be a history buff to really enjoy this.

Zu Beginn fährt man 60min um dann einen Halt bei den Touristenfallen zu machen. Diese Touristenfallen sind immer gleich aufgebaut. Irgendwelche Behinderten arbeiten dort nicht ganz freiwillig für praktisch keinen Lohn und verkaufen handgefertigte Dinge. Egal ob Hanoi oder Ho chi minh diese „Raststätten“ hatten wir bei jeder Tour in Vietnam. Leider ist der Halt auch immer 30min. Die Tourguides verdienen dabei natürlich eine Provision. Nach nochmals 30min kommt man bei den Tunnels an. Als erstes wurde uns ein kurzer Propagandafilm gezeigt über die blöden Amerikaner und die tapferen und heldenhaften Vietcongs die sich gewehrt hatten und die grosse Revolution der Kommunisten angetrieben. Auch dies ist leider in ganz Vietnam Standard. Danach sahen wir ein paar Puppen und Fallen. Der nächste Stopp war nochmals eine Raststätte, dieses mal konnte aber für 60$ noch auf einer Shooting range mit einer AK47 geballert werden. Der Disneyland Eindruck war damit komplett. Wir krochen durch einen 20m Tunnel und assen eine Okkerwurzel welche das Hauptnahrungsmittel der Vietcongs war. Das wars auch schon und wir fuhren zurück. Schaut besser die Bilder auf Google an und spart euch die Zeit. Vermutlich sind die Tunnels dort noch nicht mal aus dem Vietnamkrieg sondern wurden nachträglich als Touri Attraktion gebaut. Wer glaubt dort Kilometerweite Tunnels zu sehen wird enttäuscht. Ein Abgang ist abgesperrt und man darf nicht reinschauen, da es anscheinend Schlangen dort hat. Wir glauben eher das dort noch nicht mal ein Tunnel war. Der andere Abgang ist der erwähnte 20m Tunnel zum durchkriechen.

We were told this was a must see thing to do and were very disappointed. It was all a bit distasteful. Get down this hole and pose for a comedy photo! Shoot some AK47s! I would recommend going to the war remnants museum instead - we just didn’t like this place at all.
슬픈 역사를 담고 있는 현장입니다.  덥고 습하며 슬프고 마음이 아픕니다.   다시는 이런 일이 없어야 겠지요.  밥 못먹었어요  ㅠㅠ
In het bos staan hoofdzakelijk wat poppen opgesteld. Tevens een aantal tunnels waar een toerist door heen kan kruipen. Verder kun je zelf schieten? Zonde van de tijd (en geld)

While the history of Cu Chi is certainly fascinating, what has been done to this area is heartbreaking. Many soldiers lost their lives here, and what should be a solemn ground commemorating the atrocities of a brutal war where, as our guide aptly put it, “both sides lost,” this site is really just a low-grade attraction. The sounds of gunshots (from the shooting range, where for 550,000 VND you can shoot an AK47 or M16) were insanely inappropriate. I wish we did not spend money on this. An example of the government capitalizing on the fight for communism.
At least recognize that it's a solemn place and not an attraction like that at Disneyland or Universal Studios.
Alleen oppervlakkige en mensen die niets van de geschiedenis weten kunnen dit waarderen. Dit is te goedkoop. Dat schieten!!! Oorlog als vermaak. Walgelijk.

Wir haben eine Ganztagestour von hcmc aus gebucht und haben uns sehr auf den tag gefreut, da wir schon von einigen Leuten gehört hatten, wie interessant und spannend das Tunnelsystem sein soll.Ich glaube am Ende hatten wir einfach den falschen Anbieter gewählt. Unser guide von ytc (youth tourist joint-stock company) war unglaublich schlecht zu verstehen - für alle und auch bei mehrfachem nachfragen. Er hat uns den ganzen tag wie wild durch die Gegend gescheucht. Nirgendwo hatten wir zeit etwas nachzulesen,  stehen zu bleiben oder fotos zu machen. Es war also ein stressiger tag ohne viel information. Fazit: ich denke die Tunnel lohnen sich aber bitte tut euch einen gefallen und bucht bei einer anderen Gesellschaft. So macht es einfach keinen Spaß und am ende ist man enttäuscht einen Tag vergeudet zu haben.

Not going to lie, I actually really liked this tour and thought it was very interesting UNTIL you realize you are listening to gunfire the whole time and coaxed into paying for 10 bullets to shoot so that you can shoot the guns that were used in the war. (which I did not because I found it very disturbing). That is 100% disrespectful and extremely terrifying. We are basically standing on a huge war memorial and then told we can go purchase bullets. I think the tour was completely downhill from there. They should get rid of this aspect and then just charge an extra 50,000 VNDong if they feel the need to make more money.

I thought the tunnels would be an educational experience but i hated every moment of it, except the 3 minute tunnel crawl. First it takes more than 2 hours to get  there, because of the traffic, even though it is only some 30 miles away from the city. When you get there you are first shown a propaganda film, which looks like it was made in the 50s, but yes, it was made after the war. It is a black and white poor quality VHS film, which is supposed to tell you about the tunnels and their makers, but it mostly depicts how Vietnamese heroes killed americans and got medalls for killing americans and were good at killing americans.. Many tourists left the show, others laughed out loud. At the end our guide explained that they are friends with Americans now, but maybe they should make  a new propaganda film. The worst part is the shooting range which is in the middle of it all. I always wanted to shoot a gun, but got so appalled at the site, that I just couldn't. Tourists flocking to shoot AK 47s and simmilar guns looked like it was just a fair where you shoot balls to win prizes. It is also loud as hell. The guards who show you the booby traps and how the people used them are either bored out of their minds or smile, like they are enjoying it.  The tunnel crawl was interesting although my legs hurt for 2 days after. The tour lasts less than an hour and you see groups of tourists being rushed through the site, but with the drive there and back it takes up most of the day. For me it was at the same time a waste of time, but it also gave me an insight into how one sided the Vietnamese show their history.

The history content of this tour and the site is very interesting. However, we were extremely shocked to see a shooting range and the option of paintball there. This shows no respect for the millions of lives that were lost and the people that are still affected by the war today, no matter what side they were on. We were looking forward to this tour more than anything else in Vietnam but left with a sickening feeling from listening to gun shots the entire time and the lack of respect shown.
There is not left from history. It was all built in the 80s for tourists. Nothing really historical. Just propaganda

For me, I found the visit to be irreverent and thoroughly disappointing. The loud noise of tourists firing AK43s fill the air from the beginning of the visit and become almost deafening at the cafe pit stop of the tour. Videos played showcasing the victories of "American killing hereos" where young Vietnamese girls and boys would be celebrated for killing the most people. Guides brag about booby traps and how well they were used to spear and murder the US.I love visiting historical sites but I found the tone quite disturbing, not focusing on the fight for freedom but rather encouraging tourists to take selfies with war parafanalia.I found the selling of soldiers uniforms to be tacky. I have great respect for the past and cannot fathom the atrocities that occurred.  But respectfuly not an enjoyable visit. Would have preferred Freedom as the tone rather than killing.

Don't bother going there if you only staying a day or two, or even two and half day in Ho Chi Minh City, traffic was bad both way, 2 hours there, 2 hours back, and maybe 2 hours maximum in cu chi tunnel. Nothing impress me and all my friends at all. Hot day, sweating and with lots mosquito around you. I rather have a sleep in for the day and hanging around in the city. The entrance ticket is $90.000VDN plus $20.000VDN for Ben Dinh. Apparently Ben Douc is $90.000VND.

This could and should have been so much better and actually provided interesting and valid information on a war taking place just 50 years ago ......instead it left a very bad taste in the mouth. Beginning with a propaganda film, taken round by a very, very poor guide, inviting us to take “funny” photos in front of booby traps & midway through, a shooting range where you could have a go at firing an AK 47. Tasteless and disrespectful to all those who died, regardless of nationality.
wer gern mit Militärwaffen umgeht und ein Urlaubsbild auf einem Panzer brauch und als Top einen alten DDR Film zur Verherrlichung des Wiederstands sehen möchte  sollte hier hin gehen.
Just not worth the trip - 3hrs getting there and 2 hrs back to hcmc. Spent 1.5 hrs on site. Found it really setup for tourists and not much to see. Read a book instead - time better spent in hcmc.

I was appalled by my visit to this site and cannot recommend it.  It started with a vile propaganda film which we left after a few minutes.  The tunnels had laughing tourists having their photographs taken standing in a tiny space.  I don't think there was anything funny about the Vietnam War.  The traps that the V.C. impaled soldiers on bamboo spikes left me in tears.  The shooting range is far too close to the tourist area (why it is there at all, I have no idea) and we had to rush through quickly to avoid hearing damage.  We made a mistake of going to the War Remnants museum afterwards.  More propaganda about how the Communists were treated, but no mention of what the Communists did (Hue anyone!?).

We travelled there by motorbike and it is a very long and busy 2 hour drive from HCMC. Once arriving, the tout started with a 20 minute propaganda movie that has very little historical content e.g. how many tunnels and troops were there and the movie was also dated and poor sound quality. Following that, we had the opportunity to go into 4 short tunnels which were somewhat interesting but here also, not any historical content. And that was the end of the tour after about just 1 hour.There were no other displays that explained just what happened there over the course of the Vietnam War. So as compared to the War Remnants Museum in HCMC which would rank 10 out of 10, this sire would rank only 1 out of 10. In addition, the War Remnants Museum doesn't tale 4 hours travel back and forth.

This place is incredible. Unbelievable feat of engineering steeped in history and sadness. Unfortunately this was not portrayed whatsoever in the tour. Huge tourist trap and very expensive for what you get. Couldn't believe it when we were told the tour was finished and I assumed we still had the actual tunnels to see!! The tour involved us being rushed through bad demonstrations, bad jokes by the tour guide with a very "Quick take picture!! Amazing! Hurrr hurry!!" attitude, and some hard to follow videos with questionable facts. I feel the place deserved a more sombre approach and some recognition of the many people who died and fought for their lives here. Found it very strange to see tourists climbing on tanks for pictures and posing with the strange manequin soldiers. For me having a shooting range as part of the 'attraction' was just crazy and distasteful. We were rushed through the tour and spent a total of no more than 5 minutes crawling through 1 tunnel. Very disappointing!

The tunnels are an immensely important site to visit in Ho Chi Minh City and unfortunately our guide paid very little respect to this fact. We were in a very large group (fine) in the morning on March 9, 2018. Our guide gave us almost zero information about the tunnels, the war, or anything. We skipped the film, he led us quickly through the exhibits skipping half of them. Our entire group tried to eavesdrop on other groups to try to gain a glimmer of information. My friend and I watched a YouTube documentary upon our return to try to learn something about the tunnels. I’m sure the other guides are good with this company but this guide was truly the worst I’ve had in any trip I’ve taken.

Very disappointed. 2 hours or more driving each way so we could walk the entirety of the “tunnels”. Actually just one small portion of a single tunnel. They talk about tunnels being hundreds of km. So I dressed for a day underground. 5 minutes. That’s all. And that was because some guy blocked us so he could get my guide to take some photos. Plus going again... mmm. I thought it would be an adventure tour but it was less than an hour to eat ice cream and listen to a bit of history and 5 minutes for the actual tunnel. Could possibly be extended into two hours if you count the rifle range and have a huge tour group.  5 minutes of tunnel for 2.5 hours driving.
Awesome experience. No wonder the Vietnamese couldn't be beaten. They extraordinary lengths they went to was mind boggling.
Das Programm besteht im Prinzip darin, alles schnell anzuschauen und möglichst zügig wieder das Gelände zu verlassen. Sicher eine interessante Anlage aber die geführte Tour war ein Reinfall.

This is a somber and important monument to the 22,000 men and women who died in an around these tunnels. But I think it’s a disgrace that the government allows a rifle range on site for tourists. Imagine this happening at Gallipoli!!

Einfach nur schlecht gemacht. Ein paar Löcher im Boden. Es wird nichts richtig vermittelt. Größte Attraktion wo man auch gezwungenermaßen Pause machen muss ist ein Schießstand was in Anbetracht der Geschichte makaber ist. Lieber das Museum in saigon besuchen.

Tour di un ora dove vedi qualche trappola finta..un carrarmato.. e un tunnel dove entri e fa un percorso di 2 minutiLa cosa più brutta e stato prima di arrivare ai tunnel fermarsi senza nessun avviso ad un negozio per 20 minuti e poi a metà percorso della visita aspettare le persone che spendono i soldi per sparare..Nn lo consiglio

When I first time visited this site was in 1998 and through it will change when time and memory turns but to my surprised the mentality has not change at all except physically they (whoever run this place) enlarge most if not all of the tunnels to accompany large Westerner. Look at the fact, I do not imagine any war to be a wonderful thing but at least have respect for those opposite and understand, they just like you try to do their job sets out by their leader at that time. The reason I go again, not because I like or its well kept but my friend ask to, after I give the same warning and they agree with me, after they visited the site. My warning, Do not supports these kind of establishment. I would say God already judge the losers and HE will judged the winer too.

We booked a day trip which included the Cao Dai temple in the morning and the Cu Chi Tunnels (Ben Dinh) in the afternoon. The tour to the tunnels was horrible. Our guide didn’t show any respect for the humans killed here during this horrible war. (It was informative yes, about how clever the VC’s were...) It was like going to Disneyland (shooting possibility next to the bar...) I was expecting something very different. The other site (Ben Duoc) based on some comments might be different. Don’t go to this site, don’t support this kind of places!
DisapointedToo turisticexpecting more historyVery expensive for what we gotLong Journey by bus to get there

Awful company to deal with. I booked the tour from Bangkok the day before I arrived and also booked a centrally located hotel just outside area 1Then they email and tell me they won’t pick up from hotel as it’s too far out??? Just outside district one. So they expect me to take a cab in rush hour to their office at 8am for pick up between 8-8.30No guarantee I would make that anyway due to traffic.I email them and ask for refund and they start showing the fine print....what a joke. How is a tourist on a first time visit to HMC supposed to know every area ?? Hotels just say they are centrally located but it shows the customer service of this company that they couldn’t even pick me up, So I had to book another trip via the hotel and at the last minute, they say they will come and pick me up..What a joke, I hope you enjoy spending my hard earned money....”a travel mate deluxe travel group”I don’t think you know too much about being a mate or a travel companyGood luck, but I think you are terrible

If you think it's hot in HCMC wait until you get into the jungle the tunnels are in after you've driven 2+ hours. Everyone's clothes was soaked with sweat and we were all swatting bugs away. I'm not sure we saw anything that wasn't a remake for tourists. The story of the tunnels is interesting but I'm not sure I learned anything for the time and sweat I sacrificed that I couldn't have gotten in 15 minutes reading about it.

not worth the time to drive out and visit. our tour guide was rushed because they arrived late. have chance to go through one tunnel and it's not even that fun. watched a black & white film that was waste of time (could have been shared before to watch if really interested). save your time.

Left with uncomfortable feelings. This place is not authentic which maybe would have been fine if the tour was informative. Our guide though was more busy telling jokes. But that was nothing compared to the end of the tour where they decided to put a shooting range for some reason (next to the gift shop, of course). As someone who served in the military I don’t find it ‘cool’ and I wish people would be aware that a gun not a toy or an attraction. Disrespectful in my opinion. There are less crowded REAL tunnels two hours on local bus from Saigon. I truly recommend to go there instead. unfortunately didn’t have time but people told me it’s such a different experience.

A tragic remembrance of historical significance turned "Disney World" with a semi-automatic shooting range and creepy Vietcong and American soldier mannequins located throughout the grounds (I saw many people posing for pictures with them...what?!?!).  This place leaves a bad taste in your mouth and a heaviness in your heart.  Avoid the waste of your time.

My friend helped me book a tour with this company but the salesman didn't explain clearly for us that we're booking a private tour or a combination tour with another guest and of course, we're a western people so we don't understand full of information that they were telling my friend and my friend even don't understand too. I got sick and my friend took me to the hospital at midnight and didn't call them back to cancel (she thought we'll go on a group and we didn't pay yet so if we won't go with them it's not a big problem and of course she doesn't want to wake anyone up at midnight to tell them that we can't go with the tour). The salesman booked private tour for us by themselves without our agreement so she called my friend in the morning and try to make my friend pay for the private tour that we CANNOT go. Finally, after my friend discussed with the salesman and they cancelled without payment. I know that we are trust to each other and we don't need to pay first but I'm in a emergency situation so how can I go and pay for a private tour that we were never ever wanted to go private!? I like to go in the group more than private btw. I'm not a VIP to take a private tour! I and my wife so upset that we can't go to Cu Chi because this is my first time we're traveling to Vietnam but we're very angry with what they said and do!

An early morning tour of Cu Chi Tunnels(Ben Dinh)..such an important site...felt like a theme park. Over crowded with groups of pushing, shoving, loudly laughing, disrespectful people. Some local guides looked stressed as they saw people climbing on exhibits for that all important FB/Insta shot. The optional shooting range is downright bad taste...a nasty novelty in a place where so many fought, and died,  for their beliefs. We were amazed at how this 'Tourist Attraction' has been allowed to develop. The film documentary  on show is certainly original, & is interesting as such, but is poorly presented with distorted  sound and extreme racial overtones which will certainly upset some! We won't visit again. It is not worth the 6 hours required unless this all important site is managed differently, with crowd control in place & some respect & dignity for the site & people involved.

The trip was made by the tour guide who was informed and humorous. A warm humid walk in the jungle looking at all the ways the VC could think off to maim the foreigners. A chance to buy some bullets from the Vietnam Army and win one of their old caps if you hit the targets and then the opportunity to crawl through a tunnel for 100 metres.It was an interesting place with history but two and a half hours away from Saigon (including a stopover at an artefact factory) was a bit too long.

Mit einem ehemaligen Soldaten haben wir die Tour gemacht. Erster Stopp Souvenir Shop ... danach ging es zu den Tunneln, leider erinnert es mehr an eine „Touristenattraktion“ als an eine „Andachtsstätte“ sehr schade. Zum makaberen Abschluss gibt es noch einen Schiessstand. Schade.

Vieje desde Argentina pensando en visitar los túneles que construyó el Viet congreso y me encontré con un parque de diversiones. En un lugar donde murió muchísima gente debería tenerse más respeto y más silencio. Había  miles de turistas sacando fotos a maniquíes de soldados. La recorrida por los túneles duró exactamente 2 minutos.Además se tardarán 2 hs para ir y volver. No lo recomiendo en absoluto.

Vor Ort erwartete uns einfach nur eine Massenabfertigung von Touristen.Dieser Ort sollte eigentlich dazu da sein um die damaligen Gegebenheiten näher zu erläutern jedoch steht der Massentourismus und das "Vergnügen" am Schießstand im Vordergrund. Einzig allein der Gang durch den Tunnel war beeindruckend und zeigte wie eng und beklemmend es damals gewesen sein musste.
As an war veteran I felt very uncomfortable with the displays and demonstrations of the booby traps used during the war.

Yes, the VC put a lot of work into digging tunnels in these Hills.But story told is quaint cold war propaganda, nearly humorous in the modern world.Sadly HCMC in general has no soul.What history it does have is derelict or met the wrecking ball in the rush to be a modern city.

Where do I start. The biased propaganda video. The bragging about how clever the Vietcong were killing allied troops. The proud way they showed off their killing traps and methods. How about the constant sound of machine gun fire from the shooting range. I didnt expect such a glorification of such a horrible war. Im not saying they shouldnt display how they fought in the war as they were very clever but this was tourism gone very wrong. I left in tears.
I always heard of the Vietnam war when coming here then i get to see whats happening during war era...
I took a Grab ride - one the way to Cu Chi Tunnel - my handbag got snatched - they cut the handbag handle with a scissor - my passport is gone - I am in a terrible condition

The tunnels themselves are great but unfortunately we booked the impress travel. On their website impress travel stated that the travel time to the tunnels was c.1hr however when we got onto the bus our guide told us that the drive was actually 1hr and 40mins with a stop on route at a gift site. In fact the journey took 2.5 hours each way, I find it totally unacceptable to lie to your customers in this way. We had a international evening flight and as a result of this delay almost missed our flight. Our guide and driver were also very rude when we asked if they could drop us off on route back to HCMC so we could get a taxi to our hotel (as it was outside the centre) but they completely ignored us. They then said that they would drop us by the post office to get a taxi but actually dumped us on the side of the road a 15min walk away from there. The entire experience ruined the last day of our holiday and the tunnels for us.

We visited on a coach organised by Hai Travel - whom I wouldn't use again (no AC on bus and maniac driver)It is a very simple set up, follow a guide (in a group of 40+) try and hear what he's saying and then look at a few holes in the ground. Really uninspired - they know tourists will come so really no effort is made.Trip took one and a quarter hours altogether (2 hours each way to get there) and I didn't learn anything more, a quick look on Wikipedia and YouTube would give you a better understanding. There's a shooting range which means you definitely can't hear the guide for a portion of the tour.Groups were far too big, no time to look around on your own.

En mi opinión es una excursión que se ha tomado como obligatoria en la que existe una masificacion de turistas impresionante donde vas corriendo de un sitio a otro para poder ver las diferentes zonas.Zonas que son muy simples, recreaciones de las trampas, zona donde destruyeron un tanque, una montaña falsa de termitas, y unos túneles por donde puedes pasar que simulan los que ellos usaban,(túneles que se hacen claustrofobicos debido a que meten grupos de 20 turistas juntos, entre que me hago la Foto que ahora grabó con la cámara y otra cuando salga, estas dentro del túnel arrodillado esperando a que todo el mundo termine de hacer su reportaje)Zona de tiro donde te cobran por disparar una m16 o un ak47Vietnam tiene mucha historia bélica y es mejor un buen guía que te la cuente que no está visita.

Had travelled via TNK Travel and the tour guide spoke quite ok english. However, the Cuchi tunnels have been resized and there wasn't the actual sized one there anymore. They should have retained the original sized one there and build another bigger sized one to fit the tourists. There is not much informaton and knowledge-sharing so i left that place with no new knowledge about the place, sadly as I am a fan of history.

No se trata de un museo para recordar la guerra, es una especie de parque de atracciones sobre artilugios de la guerra y los túneles como disculpa a todo lo demás, hecho con bastante mal gusto.Se comienza la visita viendo un vídeo antiguo de propaganda de guerra en el que se refieren a los héroes de guerra como combatiente valiente aniquiladora de yankis.A lo largo del recorrido hay tiendas de souvenirs, sitios para tomar el té (en la que el grupo que nos precedía tuvo a bien deleitarnos con unas canciones) y hasta un campo de tiro para disparar unas balas, como actividades complementarias a la visita de los túneles.Cada grupo entra con su propio guía sin ningún tipo de orden. Algunos de los guías llevan altavoces para que les pueda seguir su grupo sin importarles molestar a los demás (y sin que nadie se lo prohíba). Hay grupos de más de 30 personas que son capaces de colapsar el paso y que impiden que otros visitantes puedan hacer su visita con normalidad.La visita consiste en un recorrido por el exterior donde te muestran trampas, armas, impactos de las bombas, como hacían la ropa y las sandalias. Muchos de ellos con maniquíes vestidos con ropa del ejercito con los que los visitantes posan para hacerse fotografías. El recorrido interno consiste en pasar por un túnel del tamaño de una persona baja y recorrer 20, 40, 80 y hasta 200 metros detrás de un guía con linterna que no tiene ningún interés. Las salas como el hospital de campaña, la sala de reuniones, el comedor o la cocina son accesibles desde los túneles pero también desde el exterior y tampoco aportan nada.En resumen, no me ha gustado nada y está muy mal gestionado y organizado. He estado en otros escenarios realizados para recodar el dolor de las guerras y nunca había estado en un lugar tan frívolo e irrespetuoso. Aconsejo leer y ver fotografías sobre los túneles de Cu Chi pero creo que es innecesario venir a este siniestro lugar.
Es wird ein furchtbarer Film gezeigt, sonst gibt's noch einen Schießstand???? Also ich würde diesen Ausflug auf keinen Fall empfehlen.

We booked a tour from an agency on Bui Vien Street and the agencies all get together and put you on bus groups together. We ended up with 40 to 50 other people.  There are loads of groups the same size walking around the site where you get to see some replica traps and some entrances to tunnels.To be fair the entrance to a tunnel just looks like a hole in the ground and unless you have a great tour guide to explain everything I would say it's a pretty poor excursion.We felt it was a waste of 3/4 of a day when we could have enjoyed some of the other great sights of HCMC.I just wish someone had warned me first.

Como dice mi titulo, me esperaba mas. El problema fue que nosotros, mi pareja y yo vimos un documental de los túneles de Cu Chi, era del año 2000, claro! En aquel entonces podías entrar en los túneles y ver las viviendas, donde dormían, cocinaban...etc. Ahora ya no!También podías disparar AK-47 y una M60, actualmente solo puedes disparar una pistola. La visita es muy corta de una hora y media aproximadamente... y 45 minutos te los pasas viendo un vídeo explicativo.

Nous avons pris une excursion à la demi journée par une agence et notre impression globale est mitigée : on dirait un gros attrape touristes bien organisé. Nous avons pris le bus à 13h puis apres 1h de route nous faisons une pause dans un atelier pour handicapés où ils vendent des tableaux tres chers. Visite obligatoire de l'atelier sans etre au courant. Arrivés aux tunnels, l'entrée payante (110.000vnd), nous nous promenons de reconstitutions en reconstitutions, commentées par notre guide en carton. Ils ont mis des mannequins pour illustrer la vie pendant la guerre mais rien d'authentique. Au final nous traversons 2 tunnels qui ont sans doute était construit uniquement pour les touristes... Nous avons regardé un documentaire en anglais nous expliquant l'origine des tunnels, à 50 devant une toute petite tv, difficile de tout entendre. En bref, depuis Ho Chi Minh, le déplacement ne vaut pas le coup, à notre avis.

No he ido a muchas atracciones como estas pero mi opinión es que no vale la pena, a no ser que estés interesadisimo por asuntos de la guerra de Vietnam o por estrategias militares de bajo presupuesto. Además opino que los precios de las balas están muy caros y algunos empleados son unos maleducados con los turistas; gruñidos, gestos y murmuraciones poco amables cuando les preguntas. Por supuesto no hablan inglés, sólo han aprendido cuatro frases. Los túneles bajo tierra están bien, sólo nos dejaron pasar por uno y... no es obligatorio. Si lo prefieres puedes esperar fuera a que salga todo el mundo sudando y lleno de barro.Te da que pensar, si, como todas las guerras o museos militares.Hay otras cosas que hacer en Saigon.

Terrible. Tour guide never showed up. No email. No text. No call. 30 minutes and two phone calls from me later, we were picked up for a tour worth less than half the price I paid in advance. The replacement was without a speedboat. Took 3 extra hours. The replacement tour guide was excellent but I am still very upset with Viator and the company providing the tour. No attempt from Vietnam Travel Group to contact me or refund the difference on cost. Buyer beware!

Una excursión a dos horas de Saigon que vale demasiado para lo que ofrece. El interés de la visita se centra en gatear por un túnel de 40 metros y disparar un arma a precio de oro (2$ la bala). Un par de curiosidades más y en una hora te ves fuera con 1 millón de dongs menos en el bolsillo. Sólo lo recomendaría si realmente no tienes NADA mejor que hacer en Vietnam y te sobra un día.
Very educational for both adults n children. Fyi, only age above 18yrs is allowed to play paint ball shooting.

es un teatro para los turista que viajan a Vietnam, logicamente la guerra y los túneles estaban alli construidos de origen pero actualmente lo unico que ves es teatro de lo que algún dia fue uno de los sitios estratégicos de la guerra de Vietnam, la verdad para lo lejos que esta y lo que realmente ves y lo que cuesta no es nada recomendable, hay sitios mas bonitos en la propia ciudad de saigon.  lo unico que te pierdes ese ver 4 agujeros un túnel de 5 metros y 3 tanques destrozados en medio de la selva.
Une reconstitution sans vraiment d'intérêt. Un historique plus que douteux. Un piège à touriste. Des installations tristes.
So much potential, but was really boring and a time waste. Could have been good if they just didn't think about the money... Shooting range with 10 bullets for 30USD.... Come on guys

I had the pleasure of visiting several tunnels around the Cu Chi area during my tour there.  I read extensively about the tunnels of Cu Chi before my return there in Nov. 2016.  I read about the tunnels being over one hundred miles long and housing up to 17,000 soldiers.  The tunnels I was shown was some of the exact tunnels we discovered in 1966.  They did consist of a cookhouse, a hospital, an ammunitions room etc. and they were interconnected.  When I asked for someone to show me the tunnels that ran to the river and ran for over a hundred miles, they replied that was classified.  The trip is very cheap, but it does make a lot of money for the Government of Vietnam today.  For someone who never served in the Infantry in Vietnam, it is a good show.  For me it was a farce.

Took 2-3 hours to get there as we stopped off at a small craft shop which was hugely over priced. No one bought anything as it was like 100$ for a wee plastic plate.Get to the place and we do a half assed tour, got to go in a tunnel eventually. It's all just a money spinner. It was 500000 dong to shoot 10 bullets from an Ak47 which is deafening to the other visitors.All in all, waste of time and money. Just google it.

If you're going to Cu Chi Tunnels be sure to go by boat up the Saigon River, as it's the best part of the trip.   Super-crowded with tourist groups to the point it's difficult to move.   The tour starts with a 1968 propaganda film and continues in that vein throughout, so,if you want an accurate historical outing this is not the place. .  Also, if you are bothered by tourists  shooting M16's and machine guns for $2 per bullet in a place where so much Vietnamese and American blood was spilled, don't make the trip.

From an American perspective, be prepared to be horrified and disgusted. Tour guide seemed giddy to demonstrate how Vietcong traps eviscerated American soldiers. I loved visiting the beautiful country and meeting the Vietnamese people, but this exhibit was definitely not for the faint of heart. Not recommended for children.

I have read the other reviews rated 'terrible' and they have also clearly captured how our tour was of the Cu Chi Tunnels. I went as part of a pre-booked small tour group and was interested in how people survived underground and the ingenious methods used such as how to diffuse cooking smoke and maintain a life hidden from enemies.  Unfortunately this tourist trap is just a money spinner that rejoices in the various ways and methods Americans were killed.  I found it offensive and unnecessary. I have traveled the world visiting countries and I am fascinated by the history of places including their struggles and their joys but this was not one of those places.

You, too, can shoot a rifle (for a price) at the conveniently located range near the tunnels. You, too, can climb through the tunnels that people built for survival and warfare to the sound of gunfire. See and hear the booby traps that were used to kill people described like menu items. It's all available in a carnival like atmosphere so you can have your photo taken. Surreal. Disturbing.

You get more knowledge reading the article on Wikipedia and looking at pictures on googleImages than going there. At peak times they are pushing though 4000 tourists and you feel like ants in the rain forest! You can barely hear your guide because of the constant AK-47... Just stupid!

Holes in the ground with no context or accompanying information or guidance. If I didn't read extensively beforehand it would have been even less interesting. The grade 2 science project automation of human figures at the end was it for me. Also the inappropriate shooting range at the end was quite bizarre. You can spend your time better elsewhere.

We went with Hai Travel and found it to be one of the worst tours we've been on. Instead of taking us on the highway, we wove our way tooting and fishtailing all over the road (the bus was not in good condition) for ages and had to make a 'rest stop' at a place where disabled people make goods for tourists to buy. There was no food there.Finally we got to Cu Chi tunnels with all 32 people in our group and amalgamated with another group to bring our group to well over 60 people (we were told it was a smaller tour). I think because there were 2 guides they counted it as a smaller tour? One guide did most of the talking, coughing and snorting into his microphone and so of course we couldn't hear or understand what he was saying or what he was referring to. At the tunnels you learn about the different traps (if you can get a spot closer to the guide), you walk through the tunnels and you can fire an AK if you pay extra but it was all really average. The large number of security guards lying about the show too usher you through rudely like sheep. There is also no food at the tunnels unless Pringles count.So average.

They try to sell everything that has no  value,  it seems that they are arrogant.  The tunnel you can see,  just 3 m below ground,  and you have option how long you want to go,  20 m,  40 m,  60 m or 120 m,  but you will not find the storage room underground,  no food room,  no second layer tunnel or even the third layer tunnel.  They just cheat your money.
Long hot drive to something that is overrated, unless you have nothing to do.To fire the rifles there is a cost of 400,000 dong. No one on our tour did it.

Let me tell you what happens. You take the bus, it takes about two and half hours to get there.  Your there for an hour and a half, and then you drive back, but by now it's dark so it takes three hours to get back.  During the trip there they play a terrible overexposed black and white propoganda film.  You go into the tunnels where you can barely breathe (seriously, they tell you if your not healthy don't enter the tunnels and the hospital is far away), getting your clothes muddy. And my luck, we got caught in the pouring rain the whole time.  Interesting?  Definitely!  but worth the money, not for me.

CuChi Tunnels were interesting, however the entire site is an overrated tourist attraction with mega bus loads of people. Mass assembly line of people through the different sections. We did not enjoy waiting so long for a few individuals to fire AK47s at end of CuChi.
This tourist attraction is one big propaganda site, just a waste of money but most of all a big waste of time.
Although the tour gives you some idea about history, it is not authentic and I thought it was a waste of time.
Wat nen dikke tegenvaller. Precies in het Vietnamese Bokrijk terecht gekomen maar dan veel duurder! Echt zijn geld niet waard!"

Honestly, spend your sightseeing dollars on something else. This place was super packed and very contrived. They have taken an area with some interesting tunnels and built a giant tourist trap around it, complete with plastic soldiers.  Very disappointing. There were so many people there you could barely see anything and all the various group guides shouting into bullhorns made it almost impossible to hear as well.  Very sorry we wasted our money here.

This tour was very disappointing. The guide was right off the wall. The tour was very superficial there just wasn't enough focus on the tunnels. I would not recommend. The War Remnants Museum is a lot better value for money.

A must do but maybe not here.This is too touristic. I'm sure there are other places to check the tunnels... Anyhow you must see the tunnels, very interesting.The shooting range is good businnes, 1USD for a bullet, smart!

These tunnels were built strictly for tourist money, they are not even in the actual original location !!!  they are made of concrete and have electric lights....they are not even close to what the tunnels looked like in the 60's and 70's

Die Fahrt von ca. 1. Stunde ist es Wert. Dort einmal zu sehen, was man ja nur aus Filmen oder Fokus her kennt, ist überwältigend. Die Feuchtigkeit, Hitze und die unglaublichen Tunnel, in die man auch hinein krabbeln kann, lassen einen nun fühlen. Und der Schiesstand ist wichtig. Man erfährt was es heisst - zu schiessen. Es ist laut. Man erschreckt. Mein Fazit: Ja, dass sollte man gesehen und gefühlt haben.

Trauriger "Hohepunkt" der Tour war der Schiessstand, wo Touristen ihr Geld verballern können. Ich finde dies unpassend. Generell steht der Massentourismus im Vordergrund und nicht das traurige Schicksal der Soldaten (beider Seiten) und der Zivilbevölkeung. Zu oberflächlich organisiert das Ganze nach meinem Geschmack.

Одно из самых больших разочарований поездки во Вьетнам. Впечатлить может только тех, кто никогда из мегаполиса не выезжал и не знает, что в войну людей, оказывается, убивали. Гиды рассказывают абсолютно скучные истории (не только наш гид, подслушивали на других языках - там тоже самое), потому что рассказать толком нечего. Поездка туда-обратно занимает очень много времени. На месте устаревшие "экспонаты" и проход по туристическому туннелю длиной в 100м, который преодолевается минуты за 2-3. Жаль потраченных денег и времени.

Attraction pour touristes.Lors de notre visites nous étions un groupe de 20 personnes.Impossible de voir quoique ce soit.Je vous recommande donc de faire cette visite en guide privé si vous souhaitez absolument voir ces tunnels. Ou alors il a d'autres tunnels dans le centre du Vietnam.

Leider wird hier nicht auf angemessene Art der Krieg vermittelt, sondern lediglich Geld mit Massen von Touristen gemacht. Trauriger Höhepunkt ist der Schießplatz, wo Touristen echte Waffen abfeuern können. Geschmacklos und wirklich uninteressant. Das Geld und die Zeit kann man in HCMC besser investieren.

The story of the Vietnamese struggle for independence and the determination exhibited in the construction of the tunnel system is truly impressive. Unfortunately the Cu Chi Tunnel tour manages to convey little of the story. Save yourself the misery of their Disney-esque side show and read a book or watch a documentary.  If you insist on going at least avoid the exorbitant fee and crappy lunch provided  by Les Rives.  Big thumbs down.

Massentourismus und kommunistische Gehirnwäsche inklusive Schießstand. Die an sich sehr bewegende und interessante Geschichte des Ortes wird sehr einseitig und pietätlos erläutert. Der Guide konnte lediglich auswendig gelernte Sätze von sich geben, und hat nicht zum erlebnis der tour beigetragen. Der Schießstand ist das letzte!

I was charged for this tour more than 25 USD with (supposedly) the entrance fee inclouding. I paid the tour at graceful saigon hotel. By my surprise on 27th at 1 pm i was picked up in a small bus with just 15 people and the person over there took my receipt that proved i had paid my tour. After 10 minutes ride she drop me up in the middle of nowhere to wait a second bus (just me had to do that) i didn't argue with her.one hour later i had to join a big bus with 40 people and with other company call YTC. When i was in the bus i was requesting to pay again my entrence fee even knowing i had paid for this tour 5 times more than the other people who were sahring the bus with me. But the worst part  is here. A very rude and impolite Vietnamese travel guide call JOHN WAYNE gave me the worst treatment i have ever receive during my 3 years trip around the world. Basically he started to make fun about me in front of the other 39 people at the bus and calling me indirectly stupid because i didn't want to pay again a tour already over cost me a lot and because i didnt know how to contact the person who sold me the tour. Not happy with that, this person JOHN WAYNE threatened me to leave me in the middle of nowhere at 2 hours of Ho Chi Min if i didn't give him the entrance fee again. Also threatened me saying i would have to wait inside the bus if won't give him the money. But all of this in a very impolite and rude way. During all the tour this tourist guide JOHN WAYNE just laugh about me in front of other tourist and make jokes about my situation.Hope you never have to meet this person in your life, and avoid the companies call YTC, TNK travel. and keep your receipts with you all the time, if not they will scam youThank you

They are not real. Been enlarged for tourists and not very informative. They had a bunch of traps to show us but seems very put on and an act. You can learn more on google. Dont bother with this one. Unless youve got too many days booked in ho chi minh.  They do however have a shooting range which for a lot of money you can shpot 10 bullets in whatever gun you want. But its all just $$$$ for tourists. I mean what has a shooting range got to do with making tunnels to survive enemies hunting you down.

Very daunting venue to visit. Thank god I was not involved in this conflict. I had a top tour guide who gave great depth to what we were viewing. I took many photos of the traps, tunnel entrances and other experiences on display.
I found the Cu Chi Tunnels to be very disturbing. Too many bad memories from the war! I would not recommend for anyone who was remotely associated with the Vietnam war.
On se croie dans un vieux parc d attraction sans aucun intérêt. Beaucoup de transport depuis saigon, 4 h de bus, pour 1h de visite seulement.

Tourist trap, all you need can do there is take photos with mannequins and a few holes in the ground.The shooting range is terrible, I don't understand how weapons can be used for entertainment in a country that has suffered so much from war machines.

Autant l'histoire du lieu est super intéressante et il est important de découvrir ces lieux, mais là pour le coup la réalisation est selon moi désastreuse.Une visite d'un lieu chargé d'une telle histoire ne devrait pas se transformer en une sorte de Disneyland où les gens se succèdent dans ces tunnels en gloussant et en rigolant tout en admirant des faux mannequins en plastiques tenant des fausses armes.Mais là où selon moi on atteint des sommets c'est le stand de tir... On propose aux touristes en mal de sensation de venir tirer avec des vraies armes d'époque sur des murs en terre. Alors certes je conçois que certains aiment tirer et avec des armes d'époque, mais par pitié pas dans un lieu aussi chargé en histoire que celui-ci! Bref pour moi ce lieu est à éviter à tout prix!
Para nada recomendable la visita, plagado de turistas sin control, muy mal organizado, todo muy rápido. Muchas agencias cada uno con un guía distinto. No volvería no aporta nada

I don't get the allure of this site and do not suggest anyone spend their time or money visiting the tunnels. The narration as well as figuring animation was poor and borderline cheesy. Think of this like a Vietnamese Disneyland. Did not shed light on anything I didn't know about the Vietnam war.

We've just returned from a really disappointing tour of the Cu Chi tunnels with TNK travel which we booked due to the mostly favorable reviews. The tour guide was unprofessional and his tone was completely wrong given the serious nature of what we were experiencing. Multiple and repeated 'jokes' about his girlfriends, people being blown up and the weight of some of the guests on the tour was just not on. The prodding of a larger guy's stomach and suggesting that he eat less was just plain cruel (the guy looked really upset). We also felt as though we didn't learn anything at all and we were rushed around the tunnels but spent hours on the coach due to multiple guest pickups which seemed unplanned - we drove down a few streets multiple times seemingly picking guests up at random.

This is not a historical site, just a presentation of propaganda, our tour guide was massively racist and it was almost like going to some political rally. Everyone on our bus thought had been told admission price was included only to find out it was not and is an extra $5, aboit the same price for the whole tour. The 'highlight' is the actual tunnels which is fine if you like queueing on your hands and knees. You are shoved in with around 50-60 people and yelled out to rush through, no time to enjoy or experience it at all. The day ended and I felt I hadn't learnt a thing and had just been robbed, I hear there are other tunnels less than 5 miles away. Look into that and skip cu chi, it's woeful!

J'ai toujours été intrigué par les foules se rendant à Cu chi... Apres 1 an à Saigon je m'y suis rendu à l'occasion de la visite d'un ami.Et voila un exemple sublime de ce que peux donner la propagande vietnamienne sauce communiste.On y apprends tout et n'importe quoi jusqu'au fameux français qui mangeaient les enfants... Bref jolie ballade pour ce rendre dans cette sublime plaine mais visite abrutissante..

Tour: Cu Chi Tunnels small adventure tour from Ho Chi Minh City (booked through Viator)We we're really looking forward to going to the tunnels but unfortunately the tour guide left without us. We were at the pickup point (general post office) on time which was a good distance from our hotel but we made sure to give ourselves plenty of time. However after 20 mins of waiting we rang the tour company and found out they had already left. We never even received a phonecall!! We can't understand how we "missed" it. So just ensure if you are booking with this company to ring them the minute you arrive at the pickup point! In fairness to the company they offered us the same tour for the following day but unfortunately this was our last day in Ho Chi Minh.

For a 6 hour outing that comprises 5 hours sitting in a bus mired in traffic it is hard to imagine any attraction worth that pain. The site of the underworld defense network is interesting but you can learn more on-line, with a better delivery and save your bottom for more rewarding experiences. This was one of the most painful and unfulfilling tourist experience; it was hard labour.  Traffic will only get worse and more congested.
現地で少人数で申し込みました。一人25ドルとかなり高いですが、少人数だしいいかと思いましたが、間違え。ガイドが全く英語が話せません。ただ、見るだけでは、つまらない場所。銃が撃てるので、それがこのツアーのハイライトになってしまいました。大人数のツアーで行くのをおすすめします。

The best way to waste half a day and 500,000 dong is to visit the cu chi tunnels. Imagine a forest with a few holes in the mud and there you have it. If you've seen a photo of the tunnels then you've already seen all there is to see, except you didn't have to travel 4 hours to see it. Best to avoid unless you really can't imagine what a hole in the ground looks like.

This was pretty brutal. Apart from the actual 2 minutes you get to go through an already- westernised version of a tunnel passage itself, this is almost as outdated and pitiful an experience as the Dublin wax museum and that is saying something!!!! Perhaps it was also because we had a tour guide that was also impossible to understand, I'd recommend going to the simulated tunnel experience closer to the city and not waste the journey out. Old models, shoddy displays, impossible to hear audio, and doesn't do justice to what could be a great tourist attraction.

It's a huge tourist trap, the place is overcrowded so you can't wander at your own pace.  It's hard to take in what happened here, but it feels like a huge disrespect in how tourists are encouraged to take hero pictures on a tank and pay to fire real bullets.  It might depend on the tour, ours was awful (TNK Travel) Huge group, incomprehensible and arrogant guide, hours on the bus picking up and dropping off etc.  I think you would be better off going to the War Museum.

Let me start by saying that there is an actual firing range in the middle of this "museum" where they try to get you to purchase bullets and then shoot them. In a museum that was created to display just how hard people had to work to stay alive during the war...I think this is just wrong. I am told there are other tunnels that are not quite as touristy so I would skip chu chi and try those instead

It is far -at least it feels like it is far from Ho Chi Minh City (only 50km)but because of trafic and crap car we had made it in 2 hours-very long journey. If you go then invest in a good English speaking guide-ours a research first-ours didn't care and was wondering off without explaining anything -we got absolutely ripped  off on this trip-the tunels are the tunels nothing amazing about this place-interavtive video at the end was horrible-boring not informative and badly presented. THE WORSE PLACE I HAVE BEEN IN MY WHOLE TRAVELING EXPERIENCE.

essentially holes in the ground, doesn't appear to be a lot of original holes in the ground, tour guide didn't offer a lot as he couldn't speak english (most of his ramblings were pre orchestrated) and not a place to go if you want to see authentic and insightful historic tour of the vietnam war. the tour guide kicked leaves over the people who went into the spider hole when they were trying to take pictures and did not appreciate getting dirt and leaves. made for an awkward experience as the joke stopped being funny the first 4 times.the gun range was a money grabbing farce. i feel sorry for the souvenir shop workers who were placed right next to the loud gun range. hearing shooting for 8 hours a day without ear plugs is surely damaging to these people? a run through the 'tunnels' was staged and opened up for bigger people to fit through.absolute tourist trap and not recommended.

Seeing the tunnels might be OK except for the propaganda.The Vietnamese are lovely people but the ruin with this type of thing.It was so disappointing. Stay wherever you are, go shopping and buy a nice pair of shoes for the cost of the trip. Of course there is the wasted time which you will never get back.You will not regret it. The shoes I mean.

We booked through our hotel "Ngoc Minh" and went on a half day city dour/half day tunnel tour. Our city tour guide was awesome. Very funny and nice. But it all went down hill when we got on the bus to the tunnels with our new guide "binh" he was 66 years old and was in the war. This made us think that the trip would be better considering he had Lived through all of this. But no. He spent the whole trip talking about his personal life saying how he works for the CIA? But is a tour guide? My girlfriend and I stopped listening to him 10 minutes into the bus trip as he had started to bad mouth almost every country but Vietnam. He called us all fat and told us how bad we would be at fighting... Anyway this went on for the whole day and when we were at the tunnels all he did was tell us how his friend Steve died at this place in particular. He must of had a lot of friends named Steve or maybe he died a couple of times because he couldn't make up his mind on what the location was. All in all the tunnels itself were good and definitely worth going but if you get onto a bus and find him. Stop. Get off the bus. Don't even bother trying to get your money back. Go and book a different tour. My partner and I feel that we missed out on a lot because of him talking crap all day

I went to the tunnels with a tour group and was really disturbed by the presentation. I expected a somber experience knowing the history of the area. Unfortunately, the tour is an interactive experience that glosses over the history and makes light of the tools used during the war. There was also an opportunity to buy bullets to shoot so all of the visitors walked through the area with the sound of gunfire in the distance. It was a troubling experience and I could not leave fast enough.

Thought it was only 20,000 dong for the ticket to enter but you have to pay an extra 70,000 dong to get into the tunnels. Don't rent a bike to here if you don't have any prior experience to riding especially in really really busy roads

Understanding of the war field and respect the importance of peace are essential for us to recognize how silly things that war brings.  Cu Chi Tunnels are the places where people can learn what happened.  As a first time Japanese visitor to Cu Chi Tunnels I felt a little puzzled when we were taken to the shooting range.  My wife and I saw people having fun firing guns at the shooting range with such a big noise that can reminiscent of war.  What is the implication of this place trying to teach us?  Having fun like an amusement park in such a place where a lot of people died?
This was not nearly a realistic representation of the tunnels as how they were during the war. And its flooded by hordes of tourists, don't understand it because it's not worth your time and money.

Es una descripción de como los vietnamitas peleaban con los americanos en esta zona, mediante túneles y viviendo bajo tierra.Cuidado al entrar, 20 m se te hacen eternos y acabas con las piernas doloridas.

This was very, very disappointing - the tunnels themselves are really worth the visit, however the small group adventure tour is not!1. When booking it states pick up in district 1 & backpack area - no! We had to meet at their office, then walked to the park, waited 20 mins then walked back past the office to be placed on a BIG bus (thinking this was just to get to our smaller bus) there was no communication!!2. Drive around the city for 40 mins as a passenger was not at their hotel for pick up!3. This BIG bus then broke down - no air conditioning-and we needed to wait for another bus, I do understand that this was out of their control however we are still in a bus of 40 people - this is not a small group!!!!4. Continue in group of 40!! It was noted that everyone on the bus had been sold different packages and told different times. Overall, very unprofessional!! This was not an enjoyable experience! PS.  our guide tried so hard to appease us, he was doing his jobNOT RECOMMENDED!!

Having read many reviews talking about the journey there being a nightmare of 2 and half hours of which the first hour is picking people up from they're hotels we decided to hire a driver at a cost of 1 million vd $45 usd approximately. This was for us the best option having a toddler but when we got there I saw lots and lots of coaches and minibuses which I thought we'd of been able to avoid due to leaving Ho Chi Minh bang on 8am but I was wrong. Swarms of people in groups of up to 50 people all around one attraction made it near to impossible to hear our guide. Personally I wouldn't recommend but maybe find out if the afternoon is quieter but obviously would be more hot?
Rondleiding erg dominant, lacherig bij meest vreselijke methoden om mensen te doden. Nog steeds veel schoten te horen omdat je op de schietbaan mag schieten met van alles en nog wat.

We took the half day tour and the bus should pick us up at 7:30am. It arrived around 8 and we continued picking up people for 1:30h. Finally we started moving to Cu Chi with a group of 31 and a "guide" that could barely speak english and that resumed the history of Vietnam in the 2 sentences he knew. Before arriving at the camp we stopped at a souvenir shop in the middle of nowhere and for no reason. Finally we arrive at the promised site at 11:30am. First we see a hole in the ground that was supposed to be a tunnel and we stopped there so everyone could take a picture one by one while the guide explained absolutely nothing and kept saying: nevermind, we have time. Then we saw some traps and a canon, again stopped for pictures and everything around just seems tourist scenary, it's like being in Disney World for pictures with Mickey Mouse. No history at all. Then he asks if someone wants to fire some shots (and to pay for it), so the whole group stopped waiting for 2 guys that went to do it. While waiting, the sound of the shots is almost unbearable. Finally we get to the tunnels! A 40 meters passage underground for the ones who fit. It is now 1pm and we're done... Again: no information at all about the war or the people living in there or anything! Two more hours to get back in town. Basically you pay 11$ for a 1 minute tunnel and 5:30h in a bus. Wikipedia is by far more authentic and interesting. Do yourself a favor and skip it.

Try to find another way to see the tunnels. Of course you have to see them in a way, if you are in vietnam and it is the easiest way from Saigon, but if you have more time there are propably nicer places to explore them.
The tunnels were recreated for tourists and makes a mockery of warfare.  It turns a serious event into a theme park event.  Save your time and avoid.
Definitely worth the trip. Left Saigon at 8am and back at 1pm. Highly recommend escorted tour including mini bus as guide provides local knowledge.

We were on a tour with a group of 70 people. The information given by the tour guide was insufficient and his English was rather poor. I do not recommend the sinh tourist as a company.But the tunnels and the site itself is worth to see. Bring your own guidebook or get own guide.

Afschuwelijke Tour. Heel toeristisch.! Je wordt in rijen door het complex heen geholpen. Snel wat verteld waarna de volgende groep alweer klaarstaat om te luisteren naar hun gids. Echt tijd om foto's te maken en dingen op je in te laten werken is er niet. Na een route over het terrein even pauze om met echte kogels en geweren op vlaggetjes in de heuvels te gaan schieten. Waarom? Er is onwijs veel ellende in de wereld, we zijn hier bij iets wat in de oorlog erg belangrijk was en wij gaan schieten voor de lol? Gaat me echt te ver.Daarna even kort door de tunnels gekropen, stuk aardappel gekregen en weer de bus in.Erg jammer dat het zo druk en toeristisch is waardoor je niet echt de tijd en rust hebt dingen in te laten werken. De gruwelijke dingen die daar gebeurd zijn. Erg jammer.Het oorlogsmuseum zelf is dan wel zeer de moeite waard!

I wouldn't recommend the cu chi tunnels. Huge numbers of tourists flock here and we ended up with one tour guide for 2 bus loads of tourists, and the guide Mr Binh would not stop spouting fictitious stories of his past. Plenty of people do seem to be taken in by his stories, but the book he claims to have written was actually written by a woman, about her travels in vietnam in 1944 (before Mr Binh was even born so it's not like he could have even been an inspiration in it!). He claimed to have fought in the US army as his father is American (his English, although good, I'd imagine would be better with an American father and living and working in America since he was 18). He claimed he wasl mates with various Hollywood actors and US politicians, and our particular favourite - him flying a helicopter in the war and while describing this his actions were that of driving a car with a steering wheel - pretty sure helicopters don't have steering wheels. Basically we heard more fictitious crap than actual history and the size of the group and Mr Binh's constant talking meant the tour took ages to see and learn not much.

visiter les tunnels de Cu Chi afire ou pas? je travaille dans une agence de voyage depuis 2008 au Vietnam et on se demande souvent si nous devons proposer ce circuit si demander par les touristes. je ne comprends toujours pas pourquoi les gens veulent absolument faire les tunnels de Cu Chi. Tout d'abord il faut le savoir, les tunnels de Cu Chi sopnt seulement une representation et non le lieu exacte de tunnels srvant durant la guerre. voila tout est dit, ceci est un Fake. donc se deplacer pour voir un tel endroit et proposant juste comment les gens vivaient dans des petits tunnels durant la guerre, tres peu pour moi. je pense que le Vietnam possede tellement d'autres choses a faire, surtout que les touristes ne possedent pas bcp de temps. Donc n'allez pas a Cu Chi, visitez le Vietnam le Vrai.

This was probably the low point of my entire trip. Riding for 2.5 hours in a crowded bus with no AC to the tunnels seemed miserable at the time, but was worsened by the fact that the tour guide was completely drowned out by the several  other groups right next to us.It was impossible to hear anything over the cacophony of everyone, and the little bits you could actually hear were obvious facts about what we were seeing (e.g. Here is a soldier, he has a gun) rather than any pertinent information about the importance of the tunnels or facts about the war.I asked a few questions about Vietnam in the present day (like whether the farms were still state owned/operated) and he gave me answers that directly conflicted with every other source I checked. He seemed to just be guessing at virtually everything.I can't emphasize how obnoxious the crowding was; you couldn't hear or see anything during the entire tour. You get the opportunity to go through some of the tunnels, but since you're packed in shoulder-to-shoulder and everyone is screaming their heads off your ears are ringing badly by the time you get out.

do not waste your time on this tourist trap . This is made for naive tourist . Nothing authentic here . The young and the elderly will find this place boring very quickly . The tranquility of the country side is broken by noisy gunfire from the firing range which is the money making machine for the park operator . Best advice is stay away from this place

We were staying in the centre of Saigon. This was a bus trip out to the tunnels which was about 1.5 hours long with ok scenary - scooters, shops, rice paddies, tombs and the odd animal roaming the streets. Lots of people here and we had our own private guide. Many tunnels but only one to go through 20 metres, 40 metres or 100 metres. In our party of nine only three did the 20 metres. Gun range as well - need to shot ten or more bullets and wait of 30 minutes - too noisy so moved on. Video about tunnels and tourist shop.

You get a tour guide upon buying a ticket. I was always fascinated about the Vn war, they show you all the booby traps they used that goes back to the medieval days. Also you get to go into the tunnels and shoot (machine)guns at Ben dinh, we took 2 kids one that can walk and one in the pram it is a very bumpy ride for the kid so if you got carriers use them but only thing is you won't be able to go in the tunnel. It was definitely worth visiting. Bullets are very expensive tho and just go so fast. But worth the experience.

Ok couple essential tips and thoughts. 1, there's two versions of the tunnels. One that tourists get to see and another for the Vietnamese. Your tour thus consists of walking around the tunnels (on top), looking at traps, smoke holes, entrances etc. the tour only allow you to go into the tunnels very briefly. To my knowledge, wether you paid like 600 000 dong or 100 000 it's all the same, just that with the more expensive tour there's prob hotel pickup and a smaller group. 2, there's a million people there and it all feels really really artificial and fake. I'm impressed by the tunnels but hated the tour. Couldn't see anything cuz it was so busy. Just wanted to get out of there. There much better ways of spending your time

For us it was very disappointing ! We was thinking we will known better history about war in Vietnam, but this place dont told you antythyng new ! Ouer guide He joked all the time :/ and he dont said any interesting information about this place 😑 you can there shoot from a gun and eat ICE cream and drink beer... Come on is historical place about a war not a playground !

Long and hot bus ride for a whizz in a group of 40+ through different exhibits of deadly traps, manufacturing caves, a crappy and ancient propaganda video and finally 100 meters of tunnel. Of course the system of tunnels the Vietnamese created was amazing, but there were so many large groups of people it is hard to get more than a cursory glimpse of anything. The general interpretation is lousy and the guide we had (from Vietnam Discover Travel) was terrible. More time is spent in two rest areas than anything else. I guess we had 45 mins of actually doing something for a 7 hour trip! The guide even left us behind at the rest area without noticing there were 7 people missing from the group. At the 'rest' area you also have to endure the ear-splitting sound of all the wankers who like to fire the machine guns at $5 a bullet. They say a million tourists a year visit here per year - way too many for them to cope well.  I will not contribute to the growth by coming back! Everything is probably growing too fast in this country so the quality can suffer. It certainly does here.... Not recommended!

We had a really bad guide but it felt like the entire thing was a tourist scam, they made new tunnels and placed puppets to show how it looked like. There is no substance to the visit and very few details about the place. They show a 15 minute propaganda documentary from the 60`s in black and white, i mean seariously?!There`s also a fire range where you can buy bullets and shoot different types of rifles, but keep in mind that you have to buy minimum 10 bullets when each bullet costs 40,000 VND.

Creo que no vi casi nada autentico. Todo fueron puras recreaciones. Si que entras en un tunel donde puedes salir cada 20 mn pero es puramnet un tunel. No ves absolutamnete nada de las habitaciones donde vivian. Por eso me parece que ese tunel era solo una burda recreacion,

We booked our tour via a Tripadvisor Winner 2015 hotel. We had done it that way before and it never disappointed us. Guess it had to happen once! The company An Travel (not on Trip Advisor) took us in a minibus without air conditioning to the tunnel site. No biggie in the morning, but several people in the bus were complaining about it on the way back. The tour guide was disinterested, sloppy and worst of all: incomprehensible, because of his bad pronunciation of English. Questions seemed to annoy him and he did not know the answer half the time. The site itself is nice if you are into theme park museums with dressed up mannequin guerrilla dolls. This could still be interesting though, with the right tour guide. Unfortunately, we were rushed past all different sites without a full explanation of what we were looking at or a sense of historical perspective.At the shooting range, those who are not interested in shooting are supposed to enjoy shopping for war and tourist paraphernalia and having a beer, while listening to the incredibly loud and obnoxious sound of gunfire. For half an hour. So bring ear plugs if that annoys you!All in all a very disappointing experience.

We booked with Sunrise Travel Group and were quite surprised as the bus transport only cost €4,- Now we know why. First of all, the bus was an hour late, due to traffic but "no problem". It took us 3 hours to get there with a short stop at a factory, usual sales stuff. Entrance fee was around €5,- and when we got there it was going dark so we were rushed round, quick quick take a photo and within 30 mins we were back on the bus! Someone wanted to know about firing the gun, excuse was that it was being repaired but the truth was, they'd already packed up and they wanted us out. 3 hours back to Ho Chi Minh as the traffic is apparently always bad. We were told 1 to 1 1/2 hours there depending on the traffic so don't let them mislead you. If you really want to go I suggest you take the morning trip at least you'll get there and back before dark. A totally wasted day but at least the other guests on the bus took it well and tried to see the funny side of it.

Having heard much about Cu Chi tunnel, we decided to see it for ourselves. Few were there, tour guide was friendly but talks too fast to comprehend. Much of what he said were propaganda and you have no way to verify it. Few tunnels with opening to see, the rest are houses reconstructed to typify a  Vietnamese village with its daily activities. The trip was boring, only the tunnels were interesting to walk through but with much physical difficulties especially for Westerners. You would have to stoop down and fumble in the dark with bats flying past by. But this was only a small part of the entire trip. I would advise to skip it.

Mais uma opção de tour pega-turista em Vietnã. Mas, se vc fez a besteira de ter um dia livre em Saigon e acha legal  entrar em um buraco feito no chão por 2 minutos ou menos e esperar uns 20 turistas fazer o mesmo, ok.

The whole place is just a propaganda shietshow. Crowded as fuack. Speaking of american devils and glorifying vc terror attacks. Tunnes are not for you if you are claustrophobic. Once in you cant decide  for yourself when u want to get out again, because the whole system is plugged with obese ppl.
Tunnels were very interesting. Hard to believe how these soldiers lived and hid. I couldn't go down there because of my knees, but everyone that did said it was great.
They are over rated, and far from Saigon.  Not worth a trip, a visit, and if you are claustrophobic, do NOT go in.
It was packed, exceptionally hot and the tour guides had to fight for space.  Without warning they stopped at a lacquer factory and we felt intimidated because we did not buy anything
Graphic and insensitive. Seeing how US soldiers were impaled with bamboo sticks while guides smiled was sick.  Propaganda (expected) but not to this degree)

Completely over rated.  If you are some sort of VN war addict you might want to go.  But you find out a lot more about VN/American war history at the museums in HCMC, and most of all by READING good histories of VN.

Tour was good. Nice guide, interesting facts and even bought some bullets to shoot a Garang.But let's speak of the start of the tour. You sir in a small room with 40 people watching a pro-vietnam anti-american film. They refer to them as "...swarm of devils"... just for one instance. It's disgusting. Take that propaganda out and you've got a great day!
Could not go to see the tunnels, because we are very tall people and would not fit into the tunnels, but I think it can be worth a visit.

I have been to Cu Chi many years ago and found it fascinating! Was looking forward to a return visit with my partner and friends. Our guide TAI was absolutely hopeless! His grasp of English was limited to the script he had practice and was unable to answer any questions. He rushed us around Cu Chi in about an hour and we ended up hanging with our groups to try and get some information. The tour company KIM tours, did end up giving us a discount after we complained but was just really disappointed that this TAI disrespected an important part of his countries history. This tour company was recommended by our hotel.

If you are curious how the North Vietnamese Army (NVA) used to hide and operate during the Vietnam War, then you'll be amazed how people could live in series of tunnels like this.  But the Communists gov't brags and lies so much that you would leave this place thinking "why the heII I spend my afternoon and money to come here?!"

Gebucht haben wir eine Tour von 08.30 - 15.00 Uhr, außerdem eine Gruppengröße von ca. 20 Leuten.Was wir bekommen haben: Bis wir im Bus saßen war es ca. 9 Uhr, unsere Gruppe bestand aus mehr als 30 Leuten.Die Führung über das Gelände hat höchstens 1 Stunde gedauert. Dabei haben wir viele Stops ausgelassen "don't worry, we will come here later." - kamen wir nicht.Bei der Gruppengröße hat man den Guide oft kaum verstanden, gesehen hat man bei den Stops dann meistens auch nichts und dann ging es immer Zack Zack weiter. Der längste Stop war bei der Schießanlage, an welcher man nach Aufpreis verschiedene Gewehre ausprobieren kann. Wir wollten nicht schießen, also saßen wir 20 Minuten in einem ohrenbetäubenden Lärm herum.Auf dem Gelände kann man auch Dokumentarfilme anschauen, wir wurden jedoch wieder in den Bus verfrachtet, den Film würde man uns dort zeigen. Auf der Rückfahrt gab es also den Film, von welchem man weder etwas gesehen (zu hell oder man saß zu weit weg), noch etwas verstanden hat (dauerndes Gehupe im Straßenverkehr und zu laut).In der Stadt waren wir wieder um 13.30 Uhr.Ich würde diese Tour so nie wieder buchen und rate jedem davon ab.
Goed om het eens te hebben gezien, maar heeft weinig om het lijf. Hets is zeker geen must do or see. Het is prijzig, je kan beter je tijd en geld geven aan andere zaken

Really disappointed with this trip, the main issue is the utter overcrowding, there seems to be no limit to the amount of people on site at any one time. Also from an educational standpoint it's also a big letdown, it feels more like a low rent theme park, there is a rifle range where you can fire a AK47 for an extra charge, who thought it a good idea to put the cafe right next to the rifle range, needs their head examing! A word of warning if you are tempted to order a Banh Mi from the cafe, it's without a doubt the worst food we have had in Vietnam by a long way! All in all well worth avoiding.

Very ordinary.SOME interesting things but not much to see or do Tunnel made for tourist's  Gun range a highlight very loud brings home how scary it would hav been during the war .Not worth the trip from Ho Chi Minh City.
Highly over-rated and mobbed with tourists including hoards of school children on group outings. Rather limited number of remodeled tunnels and very poor quality informational video.

The traffic is literal hell to get out there and back. The area has so much potential and the war such an important part of the history of this country that this site does not do it justice. They need massive investment to improve infrastructure and make the place much more interesting.  Too many people there when I went and  I left with the sensation that I wasted my day.
A lot of tourist here. And not much exciting area.  2 hour than trip after can arrive. All tour 9 hour (4-5 trip) . i think noot need visit here

We loved everything about Vietnam, including the time we spent exploring the Cu Chi area, but if there was one thing we would have cut, it would have been this. It's important to visit for historical reasons -- it's impossible not to admire the tenacity and drive of the Viet Cong -- but the drive to the site is long and it's overwhelmed with tourists who just want to take silly pictures.

I can’t believe that this is rated as the top tour near Ho Chi Minh City.  The reason why I rate this tour so poorly is that, if you are coming from Ho Chi Minh City, it’s a 6 hour investment of time for a completely contrived experience.  You could learn everything that you learn in the Cu Chi Tunnel tour in a 5 minute video or by a 5 minute read on Wikipedia.  If you are looking for an authentic immersive experience, don’t bother with this one.  If you’re already in this area and you’re looking for a few cute/contrived photos, then this is fine.
