Cu Chi Tunnels, very disappointing! Sad that they have developed this to its full potential. Given the number of visitors, and the history, there should be a lot more to see. Luckily the Mekong Delta part of the tour made up fir it in some respect. My view, skip the tunnels and do a full day Mekong Delta

I was really looking forward to this, but it was by some way the worst tour we had from our SE Asian cruise.  Yes, there are some fascinating insights about how the Viet Kong fought, lived, and emerged victorious against far superior American weaponry, but it could and should be done so much better.  It wasn't helped that we had an awful guide, but the the displays they had were very amateurish.  The place was also utterly ruined by a firing range where visitors were able to fire some of the guns used in the war.  How tasteless is that?  It is also exceptionally loud and is deafening when in the shop / cafe area.  No doubt the guides get a cut from those who want to play with the guns that caused so much suffering.  It is touristy when it should be a sobering experience.  And tunnels?  you get to see about 10 metres.

We visited this site as it was included in an agency tour.I must admit that I was very disappointed with the visit, let me explain, this is a place that has entered the legend of the history of Vietnam, a place where there was fierce fighting and where many people suffered and died. From my point of view it should be a place to be visited with respect, almost a shrine to honor his memory. Instead it has been transformed into a sort of amusement park where tourists of all kinds and nationalities seem to want to vent their war instincts in a sort of children's war game, it has even been set up a shooting range to complete the picture where you shoot at targets in uniform with all due respect to peace.

For those with good knowledge of the Vietnam war, this is just a one sided Viet Cong propaganda day camp for kids. The presentation is simplistic and outdated. All of it is fake and could have been setup 15 minutes from Saigon instead of requiring a 4 hour road trip in the middle of nowhere and wasting the whole day.

Situated supposedly on top of a real Viet Cong tunnel complex but not impressive - a few replica buildings, some tunnel entrances showing how tight a fit they were, which could have been real or replica, some airholes, which could have been real or replica, some mantraps, which could have been real or replica, and a short stretch of tunnel which was supposedly real but enlarged for tourists. The Viet Cong tunnel system was / is one of the wonders of the modern world but this site does not do it justice.

This is a long trip from Saigon.  There is a lot of anti American propaganda to endure to visit a collection of holes, booby traps and a short crawl through a tunnel. Displays and audiovisuals are primitive. When trying to get a drink or walk around gift shop, you have to endure loud and persistent automatic gunfire from the adjacent gun range. Then you have to take your bus 2 hours back to Saigon and the day is shot. I encourage to ask some Vietnamese who speak English how they feel about communist party rule.
This was a mandatory propaganda tour on our visit to Ho Chi Minh. Experience the Tunnel life and how the VietCong made traps to mutilate soliders.

A tour of the Cu Chi Tunnels was a walk in the bush and seeing how the North Vietnamese fought during the Vietnam war. Displays showed how they hid underground and the different booby traps they used. Very sobering and not for me.

This is not great the area is made for tourists the tunnels themselves are great. The tour we went on was informative  but we were told to leave half was through the video as it was to late, no time to look at the gift shop. Lots of people on different tours do it was hard to hear and concentrate.

Cu chi tunnels was great experience the best though, mekong river tour was boring regardless where your from etc not worth it though… you have you travel 2 hour journey from district one to cu chi tunnels then 2hours again to the mekong river, then a 45min slow taxi boat to these little islands called “devil island and coconut something” then once that’s done from the mekong river 2 hours back on the bus/limo,  so majority of your package is just on the bus… which depending on your tour guide, you’ll be irritated and can’t wait to get back to your hotel after the mekong… so basically just do the cu chi tunnels… and we all felt the same all 7 of us on the coach…. And fyi if you go the shooting range in the cu chi tunnels don’t do it, or if you do, don’t do the ak47 jams every shot, cost me 1.2million dong basically 10bulltets In each…….

Saigonista matkaan menee noin 1.5-2h per suunta riippuen liikenteestä. Ei ole sen arvoinen. Muutama tunneli jossa voi vähän ryömiä. Muuten ei mitään kiinnostavaa. Muovisia sotilas ukkeleita ja muuta turhaa.
ホーチミンから時間をかけて行くには内容が乏しいです。トンネルの中も入りましたが、25メートルくらい。ほんの一部。確かに米兵は入れないような、狭いトンネルをよく作ったとは思いました。最初に短い映画を見せられますが、内容はベトコンは頭の良い戦いをして勝ちました的な感じ。色々な方向から事実を見る重要性を感じました。あのような、戦火の場所で射撃をさせて商売するのはどうかと思いますね。

The tunnels are worth visiting and superb in educating us how guerilla warfare took place in Vietcong.However, please do not buy any metal miniatures of warfare equipment as all are made from bullet powder.Especially foreigners land up surrendering them at security check at the airport as these are banned items. The name of the shop selling these is”Dia dao CU CHI near the landing jetty. These are expensive items and it hurts to give them up in a jiffy.
This viewing was a mess. Absolutely touristy - the guides were totally unmotivated.We also went to the War Museum in Saigon - it's really worth it.

2-3 hours of driving for 2 hours of info is not something I waited. It was nice to see the traps and walk for 1 minute inside a tunnel but that was pretty much it. Expensive shooting range and coffee shop was way too near it, so your hearing is affected. Wouldnt mind that if they offered somekind of hearing protection - but no...

We visited  tvings that was not abounded in advance. The guide was more like a Stan up comedian. The place was very crowded.so it was difficult to hear what your guide said because there were others talking every where around and some had loudspeakers. Pity because this is very interesting Ang sad modern history.

The tunnels were a pivotal strategy in the Vietnam War, and therefore an important part of history.  The tunnels that the tourists have access to have been widen an enlarged and are really a tourist item.  The overall feeling was more party-llike and seemed to disregard the tens of thousands that lost their lives defending their country, or trying to widen America's domination in a poorly conceived war that Australia should never of been involved in.One area that needs IMMEDIATE change is the heavy weapon firing range located next to the cafe rest area.  These heavy weapons that are available for shooting (for a minimal price) are large bore and capable of quickly causing significant hearing loss ... just move the range or the cafe, please.

Booked this tour but we were sadly misled as the unscheduled stop at the factory for the disabled did not allow enough time for the tunnels. Altho it was a moving experience at the 'factory' and we did make a financial contribution to the care of these unfortunate folks it did mean we were rushed at the tunnels which is what we had booked to see. I found the guide more than a little patronising and I'm unsure if he was quite as well informed as he tried to portray.Maybe the history books in the west are at odds with the events experienced by the Vietnamese. I think our guide had an agenda as he was at pains to let us know his personal family history which must have been traumatic. Overall this trip was not as advertised and we felt rushed when we did arrive at the tunnels. Photo opps were therefore limited and some of our group were constantly being left behind as a result. Maybe this trip requires more than 'half a day'. 2* only I'm afraid.

Fuimos a cu chi tunnels con un grupo. A pesar de que no nos gustan mucho los tours, en este caso era mucho mas facil y rapido que hacerlo por nuestra cuenta. En la mayoria de las agencias de turismo en Saigon ofrecen un tour economico por unos 10USD y te llevan junto a otras 40 personas en un autobus. En el camino paramos en un lugar de artesanias malisimo y lleno de turistas. Cuando llegamos a cu chi tunnels estaba lleno de gente y a nuestro guia no se le entendia nada de lo que decia porque su ingles era malisimo asi que sugiero que gasten un poquito mas en el tour y se aseguren un buen guia. En cuanto a las instalaciones del lugar nos pareció todo un poco desorganizado. Si vas a la mañana esta lleno de gente y no se puede ni caminar y para todo tenes que esperar. Por otro lado, hay un campo de tiro al lado del patio de comidas, asi que si queres comer algo ahi es imposible.Si tuviera que volver, eligiria ir por la tarde pero con un buen guia para aprovechar mejor el lugar!
We expected to actually some of the tunnel network but we were treated to "holes in the ground" and diagrams of how it was.

I was disappointed in the lack of thorough and detailed information for the Chu Chi Tunnels.  The 2 hour ride each way could have been used for detailed information about this historical site.  None of the guides were very good.

My wife and I booked this place with a tour company in Ho Chi Minh which was a fantastic company. Upon arriving at the tunnels we did a variety of activities including the tunnels. Great day out except for the staff that worked in the shop near the firing range. They were rude and very ignorant to both my wife and I. Would have given a better rating if the staff would have been more polite.

Unfortunately I left my tour feeling rather disappointed. Our tour guide rushed us through the various stops ordering us to 'take a photo, go on take a photo and leave', when really I just wanted to take a good look and learn something new. I am interested in history and left feeling like I learnt nothing today, perhaps this was just our tour guide and I was unlucky. I also found it highly inappropriate that there is a shooting range where tourists can pay to shoot guns?! Why anyone would find it exciting and cool to shoot a gun for fun on the site where thousands of soldiers were killed is beyond me, yet clearly many do as there was a big queue to pay for this. If you haven't got a lot of time in Ho Chi Minh I suggest skipping this trip.

We traveled 2 hours from central Saigon to get there and even though mid October is low season the place was packed with tours making it actually difficult to see let alone experience the different exhibits.The tunnels are not for the faint hearted as not only small but hot and humid.The short video is a Russian propaganda film from 1967 and shockingly anti American Whilst this could be seen as an important educational site the lack of context( no references to France, Japan or the legitimate south Vietnamese government) only the devils from America is far from measured and very one sided They actually encourage tourists to the fire old weapons which I felt to be beyond distasteful.Ask yourself why you are going as I left feeling repulsed at the celebration of ferocious cruelty
This place where so many died during the war in Vietnam is now something akin to Disneyland. You can stroll amidst the tunnels and view human torture devices as well as relics of the war.

集合場所の説明が非常にわかりづらく、ヒヤヒヤしました。集合場所にホテルが指定されていたのでロビーに行っても追い出されるし、前に立っていてもそれらしき人はいないし。集合時間も過ぎて焦り、誰かに聞こうと歩いたら、2つ先に旅行会社がありました。説明に「〇〇ホテルの並びにある当社」みたいに書いてくれてたらよかったのに。ツアーガイドは男性でいい人だったんですが、「二日酔いで・・・」なんて言ってて、途中バス酔いして吐いてました。プロ意識に欠けます…

Activity is too expensive...Nothing looking good but the activity charges are expensiveThe time available for the tour is short...But the time required for transportation is not short.Not very worth seeing

A tour announced from 8:30 to 14:30 became a tip from 8:00 to 15:30. Asian interpretation of time. For just under 12-14$ we left with 20 people, who mostly had to be collected in the city. Our guide spoke such difficult to understand and often simply repetitive English that many things were not understood or the information content was low. On the way out we went to a disabled workshop to buy something. And that at completely overpriced prices compared to the market. In addition, one does not necessarily have the space for a wall relief or a vase. The construction of the tunnels is heavily frequented by tourists. Group after group walks through the facility and often you only have a few minutes to see something or hardly any space to photograph something in detail. Everybody wants to see something. Here, however, we noticed the difference from other guides who spoke better English and took their time. You should therefore really pick up a little more money when choosing the tour and ask how much time you have on site. The shooting range is a matter of taste. Everyone can, nobody has to. The film is interesting, but not reflective. Hold war propaganda. On the way back was stopped for lunch. Overall, it is interesting to see the facility, but we wanted more depth. Tourism also has its downsides.

After a long drive from HCM City, we arrived at the tunnels. It was very crowded, which made seeing exhibits difficult. The exhibits are very contrived and offer little education. Too many gimmicks. The tunnel you are permitted to go through is clearly not original. Too many shopping opportunities. Very overrated and disappointing. Not worth the time.

My husband and I visited Central and South Vietnam just the two of us with a self-organised tour. Since we had a spare morning at the end of our stay, we decided to visit the Cu Chi Tunnels, but we wouldn't do it again. We were excited to see the tunnels and learn more about the Vietcong tactics and strategies during the American war, but we left the site quite disappointed. This open air museum is very touristic and doesn't offer a real taste of what it must have been actually in the past. Apart from the tunnels themselves, which have been enlarged for touristic purposes, there's nothing that's really worth the visit. My husband would have fancied trying to shoot but the cost was insanely high (around 20 dollars per 10 bullets), so he changed his mind.If you are keen to learn more about the American war, I'd rather suggest visiting the war museum in HCMC.
Interesting visit (the less tourist one), guide very passionate about his country. A little too gratuitous re the war for us with opportunity to shoot guns at end.

First, by way of background, we are an American couple who remembers the Vietnamese War well.  So when the propaganda movie came on about how terrible we were, it wasn't pleasant to watch.  Nor was it fun to see the speared traps that were laid for them.  The tunnels, which were originally built to fight the French, were very small and seeing a few of them was plenty.  Also we took an hour boat ride back and forth.  As we were on a short time schedule in Ho Chi Minh city, this took at least 1/2 day and for us, at least, a big waste of time.

have to be seen - but then we got there; we were rushed around after a tour guide, the tunnels have been tourist amended to not even close. The weapons on offer were affixed in a way that prevented even the weight & feel to be felt. Gift shop was interested in selling only - were not happy I insisted on a clean shirt purchase :(  This site represents so many struggles, heartache & mayhem a true representation of trauma, blood, sweat & tears.
Returned to Cu Chi Tunnels after 15 years and fined this place very commercial. Our tour guide rushed thru and fed us with useless information all the way over and over.

We went there with YMC tour (booked through our hotel). It’s a 2h drive from HCHM (bus stopped on the way at a workshop where victims of agent orange where making stuff: you’re supposed to buy something of course). Our group was 45 people! The site with the tunnels was..crowded. There were some replica traps used by Vietnamese, a tank, some mannequins..and the tunnels..also replica (tourists wouldn’t fit in the original ones). You can also shoot some guns next to the coffeehouse/sitting area..and believe my..it’s noisy there, so bring some earplugs. I wouldn’t recommend this propaganda..

Tunnels & supporting information is as expected - the trip under the ground is worth two stars on it's own, but as with other Vietnamese attractions, the heavy handed treatment of events leaves a bad taste.

This is so over rated. If you only have a couple of days in Ho Chi Minh DO NOT waste one going here (it is quite a travel from the city - about 2 hrs). There are so many better things to do. The Le Rives Mekong Delta tour is so much better.
Really jno atmosphere and bus driver stops off at friends to get you to buy stuff .. old tank and some small tunnels to squeeze through .., like a rabbit in a trap .. a tourist one

Our tour guide was not good and as a result the tour was painful and I could not wait to get off the tour. What also did not help was that the day before we had a full day tour with another operator and the tour guide was brilliant and had set the benchmark

Cu Chi tunnels is one of the most important places linked to the Vietnam-American war. We did the standard one day trip with a relatively large group of about 20 people from Ho Chi Minh city but I was disappointed. Way too many tourists where sometimes you could not even see the attraction. Very few original spots were shown and almost nothing underground. I guess people will keep visiting Cu Chi tunnels despite my bad review but I am sure Vietnam can invest on infrastructure and improve this exhibition with more stuff shown on original underground spots

Mit hat der Ausflug nicht gefallen. Es ist für mich leider nicht sensationell , in ein Loch zu steigen und den Deckel über den Kopf zu schließen, oder bei großer Hitze in einen engen Tunnel zu steigen. Ich fand das Ganze dröge. Mein Highlight war ein 40 cm langer Riesen-Regenwurm im Wald ...

Kann man machen wenn, muss man aber nicht... zu Beginn läuft ein richtig schlechter Propagandafilm welcher garantiert schon einige Jahre auf dem Buckel hat. Total überlaufen mit Reisegruppen. Man ist bei ca. 2 Stunden Anfahrt gerade mal 1 Stunde vor Ort inkl Pause auf der Shootingrange/ im Restaurant Alles für Touristen ziemlich bescheuert nachgestellt. Da kann man besser ins Kriegsrestemuseum gehen um etwas über die Kriege hier zu erfahren.
If you wish to sea how people were killed , and badly injured , go see this Wax Museum of Death.I can see this on You Tube anytime. There are a lot more pleasant things to do in VN

This was an included part of a package tour - otherwise I would not have gone. I only got half way through the tour before the sound of gunfire and the sight of the booby traps sent me back to the waiting area. I found the ‘propaganda’ film actually quite interesting to see the ingenuity of the vc and to see the war from another perspective- but I do not understand the attraction in climbing on top of a captured tank to take selfies or paying money to fire a gun used at the time. It somehow seems disrespectful to those who lived through the war. More like a war theme park than an explanation of history.

Nous sommes partis dans l idee de passer dans les galleries qui on servit pour la guerre .. au final nous avons eu le droit a une video explicative d epoque puis un bref passage dans des tunnels fait a mon avis pour les touriste bref nous avons du parcourir 4 petit bout de tunnel qui ensemble ne devait pas exceder les 50 m, vous pouvez ensuite moyennant finances tenter au tir a l arme de guerre (ak47).Bref beaucoup d heure pour pas grand choses, si vous voulez le faire prenez le temps de voir les differentes agences qui proposent a partir de 100 000d le voyage et a votre charge l entree de 110 000 d ce qui vous coutera moins cher que les 600 000 d demander par certaines agence avec visites de pseudo marché..

Incredible. Amazing. Creative and cruel. Great experience which is so very hard to believe - but yet a historical fact. Brought my kids. They childishly loved it, had a lot of fun but also valuable information.Extremely bad signings to the shooting site (we walked!). Even more extremely expensive to shoot once you get there at last. A genui price trap :-(

Mogelijk waren mijn verwachtingen te hoog bij het bezoek van deze toch wel historisch te noemen Cu Chi Tunnels. Ik kwam er via een georganiseerde halve dag tour van de Sinh Tourist (budget tour operator). Verre weg de goedkoopste (120.000 VND voor bus en 120.000 VND toegang). Het wordt druk bezocht door locals en toeristen! Ik vond het een soort openlucht museum waarbij ik mij toch afvroeg in hoeverre dit niet was “gemaakt”. De tunnel(s) zijn aangepast (groter gemaakt) voor de bezoekers. Er is een schietbaan. Er staat een verwoeste tank en je krijgt een “maaltijd” aangeboden (agave). De snelheid waarmee alles wordt bezocht is opvallend. De Sinh Tourist is daarbij niet de enige want alle groepen werden er in een sneltrein vaart doorheen geloosd. Toch jammer want het is historische grond. Helaas tegenvallend! Must see? Ik weet het eerlijk gezegd niet.

Poorly done tourist trap. Virtually nothing authentic. Even the propaganda film was terrible. Didn’t give you any idea of how the tunnels really worked and all the ingenuity of the Viet Cong. Save a wasted trip and just read the book “Tunnels of Chu Chi”.Was really looking forward to seeing this and it was a wash out. Only good bit is crawling in the tunnels

Firstly listening to the guide was great and very interactive and the stop of tour to the factory on the way there Informative.The problem is I think the awful history is lost in a tourist attraction where you can pay to fire numerous weapons and have a laugh with no thought for the numerous casualties here. I may sound a little miserable but the human cost seems to be lost a litlle so we can all have a little adventure War Remnants Museum is a must
Terrible company. English of de guide is very very bad. They make you stop at two other places for souvenirs and food without telling.

Too crowded to enjoy. Even if you go on your own or on a private tour, every exhibit is surrounded by 30 or 40 package tour people. You end up spending most of your time looking at the backs of people’s heads, or listening to a guide blare over his loudspeaker, or trying to peer around the woman in front of you taking pictures rapid fire with her 12.9” iPad Pro. Crawling through the tunnels is an experience, but again it’s ruined by people yelling and joking and cramming everything up. Firing range is a nice touch but expensive. 1 mil dong for 15 M16 bullets. With hindsight I would skip the whole thing, at least this time of year.

It was 4 hours round trip to drive there because of the Saigon traffic.  The place itself is like a war theme park.  Very commercialized.  While it was interesting to see and hear about what happened there in the war, the whole thing was very "Disneyfied", and there is a shooting range attached which was very loud and kind of creepy.

We chose this when planning the trip with our agency. First, be prepared for a rather long drive outside Ho Chi Minh City that provides a nice look at the countryside. If you are American, you may be a little disturbed. In addition to the tunnels, there are examples of booby traps and the remains of US tank that was blown up there. Yes, I knew these things happened, but I signed up to see the tunnels, not those particular details. Had I known, we would not have done this

I’d read a book about the tunnels and heard about them from my son. He visited 12 years ago. The tunnels provided shelter for local people but also enabled the Viet Cong forces to ambush American troops. I thought the place would be solemn. It was not. Although our group heard an introduction from a retired VC soldier, the rest of the place was like a little tourist trap village. There was even a photographer saying, “Smile,” and snapping a photo he’d try to sell me later as I exited one of the tunnels. Buy six bullets and get a free ride to a shooting range. Really. And walk through an extensive set of shops on the way out. Quite a sad exploitation of a site that should be thought-provoking.    It was a two hour drive from Saigon. Spend your time at the War Remnants Museum instead.
The tour guide was really nice and made up for some of the things this venue lacked. There is basically nothing there, other than a 100m long tunnel to crawl through.

This attraction is 2 hours outside Ho Chi Minh center.  The tunnels are dark and small.  Brings back too many memories from the stories of the tunnels when the war was going on.  It is interesting but in retrospect I would skip this one.If this review is helpful then please indicate so below.

Cu Chi Tunnels were a 2 hour drive from Saigon. I initially was surprised to learn that there were 2 different tunnel tours, the other place being for Vietnamese. The tour is very busy there being many coach tours. I felt that the whole location was very artificial an attraction made for tourists. The historic film at the end gave a far better understanding of how the Vietnamese people lived and survived during the war.

The tunnel system itself is very interesting, but...Traffic is very bad. Takes about 5 hours for both ways. You will need at least until 3 pm to be back in the city (tour started at 8) Be careful what tour you're on. The big bus stopps at a place where handicapped people produce useless art. The way you visit that place is very degrading in my opinion.

I think seeing this was a good thing to do to get a better insight into the history here. The intro films were not watchable for me. I know the tunnels are a representation and not the actual ones. It was interesting to see how the traps worked. They do allow you do shoot an AK-47, but I find that a weird thing to do. On the other hand, when people are shooting, it does give you a feel of the sounds for the people that were fighting.

We expected a much more authentic a d atmospheric experience that showed what took place and the history.Instead we found it to be a commercialised tourist factory where tour groups and herded around from photo opportunity to photo opportunity. The only slight insight into life in the tunnels for the soldiers came at the very end with a crawl through 100m of tunnel. It's such a shame that a place with such remarkable stories to tell has become trivialised for the sake of tourist money.
Disappoiment for meNot so much to see and it's a long way to get there in a traffic.Too many people.

90 mins drive from the centre of HCM. If you are yet to visit, it’s still a must-do on most Saigon visit guides, but here’s the deal- you need to love holes in the ground, crouching through tunnel networks under a now, fully regrown, forest, watching rebel archive film footage and then enjoy posing for photos with your choice of Vietnamese rebel fighter plastic re-enactment models or sitting astride a US tank to justify 3-4 hours out of your day if you’re only in Saigon visiting the city for a couple of days.The sight of American millennial tourists smiling and joking whilst selfie posing on top of a US tank was no less ironic than seeing people lining up to take shots in a rifle range there on site.Two decades ago, with a fraction of todays’ daily tourist numbers and traffic, a visit to CCT was kind-of justifiable, but with so much going on in HCM these days, perhaps a visit to the superior War Museum in town will inform, and shock, and provide much more insight into the miraculous recovery of the great Vietnamese people more than a trip to the forest.Maybe I’m out of step, but in 10 years time, I wonder how many times people will refer back to photos of holes in the ground in s forest in Vietnam and wonder.. why did I take a photo of a mound of soil with air holes!

I thought the tunnels were a let down - the entire place is very busy and feels very superficial. The intro video and welcome by the guide were impossible to understand, and the actual part where we went in the cave was very short and only a walkway, so its not as though we saw the underground bunkers or kitchens or anything like that - we were hunched over in a row for a couple minutes of walking. Also, there is a shooting range on site, right next to the rest spot, which is extremely loud, irritating, and feels almost offensive to the history. Overall, I'd say skip it - its a long way from HCMC, and there are far better ways to spend your time.

Die Touren sind auf Touristen ohne wirkliches Interesse an der Geschichte ausgelegt. Dies mussten wir nicht nur durch unsere Mitreisenden feststellen, die sich am meisten für den Schießstand und ein Selfie mit amerikanischem Panzer begeistern konnten, sondern vor allem durch unseren enttäuschenden Guide, „JOHN WAYNE“, der auf dem Weg hin zwar ein paar Witze riss, dann jedoch wirkliche Informationen verweigerte und auf Nachfragen sehr zickig und fies antwortete. „don’t you trust the viatnamese?“ Anstatt richtiger Informationen erklärte er lediglich Verschwörungstheorien, erzählte uns er könne hier offen nicht sprechen wegen der Kameras in den Bäumen und die Viatnamesen wären in Wirklichkeit ein armes, dummes Volk ohne Bildung, was die Ursachen des Krieges oder auch die Folgen von Agent Orange nicht verstünden. Die Tunnel sind es natürlich wert, obwohl sie mittlerweile so touristisch sind. So schade, was damit gemacht wird und wie schlecht die Geschichte erzählt wird. Wenn ihr eine Tour mit Guide buchen wollt, stellt vorher sicher nicht mit Chung Lee aka John Wayne unterwegs zu sein. Für die Tour selbst 0/5, aber die Tunnel sind es tatsächlich wert.

There is so much history here an how much more to improve the visual displays. We expected a bit more informational content given the importance of this site but with all the war sites we visited on our trip, the government still has a lot of improvement potential to inform the visiting tourists. It is a must if you visit Saigon but set your expectations.

Super turisted. Der var ikke særlig meget respekt for de gamle tunneller og man måtte kravle rundt på en original gammel tank. Derudover var der en skydebane ind inde på grunden. Generelt respektløst. Det eneste der gjorde det ok, var de opførte tunneller man kunne prøve at kravle i for at få en fornemmelse af hvordan det har været. De er større end de originale, så der også er plads til turister.

1. 조금 실망. 사이공의 대표 관광지 중 하나인데, 정말 별거 없다. 물론 사이공 왔으면 반드시 와봐야하는 곳이라서, 온 것을 후회하지는 않지만 그저 아쉬울 따름2. 터널에 직접 들어가서 기어가는 게 하이라이트인데, 오직 1분동안 걸어다니는게 전부. 물론 워낙 좁은 공간이라서 오래 있을 순 없지만, 그래도 터널 안을 좀 더 탐색하고 싶었음. 산속을 계속 걸어다니면서 부비트랩 이런거 몇개 보고 끝이다.

How this is the best thing to do in Ho Chi Minh I will never know, probably the worst thing we’ve done in Vietnam. You get rushed around all these model things barely getting any information on them. Don’t understand why they try to glorify their war like this.Made to watch a “short documentary” at the end which is just a propaganda video which is rather odd.Not to add the tourist trap stops on the way to and from the tunnels! The war museum in the city is far more interesting and authentic. Didn’t enjoy it at all, and we ain’t even Americans.

I did not enjoy the Cu Chi Tunnel experience, and may have had an unrealistic expectation for the site. I wasn’t anticipating just how many tourists would be there, as it ended up being buses and buses of people shuffling through the area at once. The crowds were in addition to the 90+% humidity and constant sound of AK-47 and M-16 gunfire, so just keep those factors in mind. Although shooters are given ear protection to wear, the range is so close to the gift shop area where non-shooters wait, the noise is pretty deafening. I think my biggest disappointment was that there didn’t seem to be much in the way of well-organized education about the exhibits; I feel that almost everything I know about the Cu Chi Tunnels, I learned from a travel website video that I watched before my trip. There were rangers on hand to demonstrate the various traps used by the Vietnamese army, however, the traps really were pretty self-explanatory. Maybe more signage would be helpful? It’s probably difficult to rely on written education with so many crowds passing through and not wanting to further bottleneck the exhibitions. For you photo-conscious travelers, regarding the pictures you see of people popping up out of a tunnel entrance with a door over their heads—at the time of my visit there constantly was a crowd of at least a dozen or more people closed in around, and you could barely get a photo without someone’s feet and legs in it! Also, if you have any lower body joint issues, you may not even be able to walk in the tunnels as the clearance is extremely low at least in some areas. The tunnel our group members entered was only about 4 feet high at the beginning. Even though the Cu Chi tunnels are very important historically, I can’t recommend an in-person visit to this site. If there were some way to do an early-hours private tour option before all the crowds arrive, that might be a better experience.
Everything from the tunnels to the traps are fake, made for tourists. Only the tanks are real.They rush you in the visit as it's crowded.
뭔가 지하벙커를 보고싶었던 나로서는 심심하고 더운 관광이였음 .기대없이 와야할곳 .물은 필수 손수건도 필수품목입니다.

We went for a half day trip and found this to be way too fast and full of tourists. The shooting range was a bizarre addition. I think this whole place could be much better with a more serious atmosphere and watching the documentary films upfront, with more detailed explanation signs throughout. Unfortunately it had the feeling of a theme park. I would not recommend... (Or if you go then go on your own so you can take your time walking around and watching the videos.)

Went early on a Friday morning from Saigon ~ 7:30am but place was still very crowded and with the heat and humidity, the tour was not very interesting or pleasant.  The short 4’ high tunnel trek was stifling.  Just reading and looking at internet pictures would have been a better choice for me.  Visiting the site wasn’t worth either the cost or long travel time. Place got even more crowded by the time our bus left at 10:30am. Would not recommend unless you want to see some old war relics.

My friend and I spent a hot Thursday visiting the Cu Chi tunnels in Viet Nam with our own private tour guide. It was a 2 hr drive outside Ho Chi Minh City - or longer depending on the traffic. How I wish I had read the reviews posted by others beforehand so I could have been better prepared for what we would see. Comments posted are accurate in my view so I won’t repeat what has been said. although the suggestions by a few to participate in the gun range shotgun firing was too much for us. It was bad enough hearing shots being fired throughout the whole 3 1/2 hrs of our visit. So be prepared for the noise.Our tour guide was a young local lady who seemingly had been trained to be entertaining and funny for the tourists (us).Besides viewing the interesting numerous artifacts and tunnels throughout the large area preserved from the VietNam war (or the American war as the locals call it), we were quite taken aback (shocked actually) that our tour guide made jokes abt the fatalities and torture done to the Americans. She laughed at one of the deadly ground traps encased by hidden sharpened bamboo sticks to kill the American enemies that they were ‘skewered’ like supper  when the soldiers fell through the trap. Also she cheerfully said at a site where the Americans were burned alive that they were barbecued. She proudly told stories of the Vietnamese heroes who were honoured for killing the most Americans. We are not Americans - but felt this insensitive commentary and joking was inappropriate and should have been talked about much more respectfully and sensitively - as there were many fatalities on both sides. Perspectives from both sides could possibly have been shared. War is tragic for all.  I talked later with others who had visited the tunnels as well and the majority of them did not have this same experience with their tour guides - thankfully.  It might have been an anomaly or poor training; hope so.  Just wanted to warn others in case this same happens to them. We should have told our guide that we wanted to hear a more respectful, sensitive and somber commentary when speaking about the horrors of this war, regardless of which side she was talking about.

My friend and I did the same trip. She got an amazing experience and compared to her mine was less than mediocre. Even so I would defenitly recommend you to go and see the tunnels. The tourgide I had just wasnt good. We didnt get to see much of the tunnels and he didnt seem so happy with his job.

We were pretty excited to see the tunnels, however at the end we were just dissapointed. We had a guy with a 4yo English who had now information about the place. If anyone asked question, he didnt know the answer. We expected to learn about VC and the war but we ended up walking around randomly placed artificial atractions.

The tunnels themselves are not too expensive, but traveling to them from Ho Chi Minh City is expensive. The tunnel tour at Ben Duoc (the less touristy, more northern tunnels) was provided in English but involved little information. Basically you got to step through three short tunnels, look at a few traps, and watch an incredibly uninformative video.

Wir hatten uns einen Fahrer organisiert, der uns von Ho Chi Min City zu den Cu Chi Tunnel gefahren hat, da wir diese in Ruhe besichtigen wollten. Leider wurde uns erst nach der Kontrolle der Eintrittskarten klar gemacht das dies nicht möglich sei.Also fügten wir uns in eine Gruppe ein und es geschah genau das was wir vermeiden wollten. Wir wurden von einem Punkt zum anderen getrieben, ohne Zeit sich mal genauer mit den Besichtigungspunkten beschäftigen zu können.Bizarrerweise war der einzige Platz an dem wir uns länger aufhalten konnten, die Shooting Range. Wer darauf steht, kann dort mit echter Munition und M16 Gewehren herumballern... Für mich persönlich ein absolutes NoGo an dieser Stelle und Örtlichkeit.Anschließend ging es dann imEiltempo weiter bis zum Schluss.

If you feel the need to experience this go but be warned it is a slow wander with a tour guide who will repeat the imperial invader story many times. It seems very fake with the Disney type models. Taller people can scramble the tunnels, but having to go by the shooting range and having AK 47 noises was horrible, but perhaps that is the point!

Teniendo en cuenta lo que había oído hablar de ellos, me decepcionó bastante. No obstante, la sensación que se tiene al estar metido en uno de los túneles es indescriptible y te hace pensar cómo tuvieron que vivir en ellos.

The tour hour drive from district 1 in Saigon was interesting, seeing how so many people live away from city centre.The tunnels were disappointing, guide was uninspiring and it was somewhat commercialised. We wouldn't recommend it. The War museum was far more interesting

Joined a local tour to Cu Chi Tunnels, our guide was an old guy with bad temper. The whole tour he kept telling us he was a spy during WWII. He was so proud when he showed us the dead traps, to make the experience worst was that he would pointed at group and said that was how they killed "us" during war. It was a mistake to join the tour, there is an eerie vibe around the whole place.

Wij komen net van de Cu Chi tunnels af, na 2,5 reizen vanaf Saigon. Binnen op het terrein lopen er mensen op die een groepje maken en voor je het weet sta je binnen het uur weer buiten, dit omdat we in een razend tempo alles gedaan hebben is het wat mij betreft niet de moeite waard. De gids sprak wel Engels maar had niet zo’n zin vandaag. We moesten voor een tv gaan zitten en zouden uitleg krijg over de geschiedenis van de tunnels maar daar had meneer niet zo’n trek in. Het eten werd overgeslagen en na 3 tunnels die wel indrukwekkend zijn om te zien en kruipen, ben je al bij de uitgang. Mensen met claustrofobie kunnen dit beter overslaan, kun je nagaan dat er ook nog vleermuizen over je vliegen terwijl je aan het kruipen bent in het donker 😊

On one hand, the Cu Chi tunnels are really well done, on the other, there are 2 things which really spoilt the trip. 1. The “educational video” is actually a Viet Cong propaganda video. Now, this would be perfectly fine if it was labeled as such.. but it’s not. They say it’s educational. It’s about as historically accurate as watching those old 1950s American cigarettes commercials. It’s great to be proud of your countries victory over an invading force, but this video is really lies and macabre. 2. The firing range. It certainly adds to an authentic experience walking around in the jungle listening to guns.. however, in my view, it’s very very poor taste to have it here. This is a place to learn about the wars, the atrocities and about how the Viet Cong operated during those times. To pick up an AK47 and fire off some rounds for fun seems completely counterintuitive to that aim. I can only strongly recommend that if you are a veteran of any war. Do not come here. Imagine what this would do for someone with PTSD. Very poor taste. If you must have a firing range, why not put it 15kms away.. on the bus route to the site. Somewhere out of ear shot. Otherwise, the tunnels are an amazing experience and the way they are preserved is really well done. Having a guide helps fill in any gaps. Small groups are better. Take your time. Don’t be pushed.

This is obviously a difficult place to visit for obvious reasons.  However the firing range is completely distasteful and the majority of our group were horrified that having just learnt about the atrocious war they would offer you the chance to practice shooting.  Awful.

As someone who is interested in the history of what the Vietnamese call the 'American War', this was the thing in HCMC that I was most looking forward to. However, our guide knew no more about the history that can be derived from a half page of a guide book. The place is crowded with tourists and is an unsatisfactory experience, especially if you go on an organised tour. If you are genuinely interested in the history of the American war then I would recommend getting a taxi from HCMC and walking around by yourself.

je krijgt een replica van de tunnels te zien, de echte worden geheim gehouden voor mogelijk een conflict. Je krijgt wel een goed beeld van wat de vietcong allemaal heeft gedaan. Ook krijg je de mogelijkheid om met  een AK45 te schieten. Helaas vonden wij dit niets, omdat dit zoveel lawaai maakt, dat je gehoorbeschadiging kan oplopen en waarom dit in stand houden en geld aan verdienen, terwijl men daar ook ellende mee heeft gehad.

We booked a full day tour that included the Cu Chi tunnels and the Mekong Delta. Our guide took us through the entire site, explaining how the tunnels were used and when. He showed us the traps used by local fighters to main and kill American soldiers, the makeshift clinic, the kitchen smoke vent, and the sandals made from old tires that faced backwards to confuse anyone following the tracks. We also crawled through a short length of tunnel. We didn’t buy bullets to fire machine guns, but we could hear the constant rapid fire in the background. Lastly, we watched part of a black and white propaganda film made in 1967 that describes how local fighters were celebrated and rewarded for killing US soldiers. A strange film to show tourists, but a fascinating historical document just the same. Like the War Remnants Museum in Saigon, we fully expected to see only one side of the story here, and that’s what we got.

The "English speaking GUIDE" was in fact just an English translator. Although he was a nice person, he limited to translate what a Vietnamese ranger of the Park explained him. The level of English wasn't great to be honest, and I'm pretty sure he missed several info "lost in translation".This tour is cheap but you get what you pay for.

I took a half day visit to the tunnels via my hotelcollected in a small bus with local guide young chap in his 20's I seem to remember his name was Koi .The tunnels are approx 1 hour ride out of the city on the way the your stopped of at a charity workshop who suffer from the after effects of the defolient Agent Orange.Quit sobering, they make all sorts of things by hand, labour intensive, the sale of these things helps maintain the charity.Your told that the products are not available anywhere else only at this venuethey are quite expensive, but a worthy cause. The exact same things are available at the other gift shops too.Our young guide was enthusiastic however his delivery of the history of the conflict should be delivered in the 'third person' because his delivery was a little condescending as he always referred to the communist Viet soldiers as 'our soldiers' which could be uncomfortable especially if the visitors were American.I had to pull him up a couple of times during the tour as I felt it was becoming very partisan, and that Communism is not everyone's preferd type of government.We had 15 minutes on the bus journey back to the hotel where he asked each one of the tour to guess his age ?I did ask what has this got to do with the tour, it was an attempt to 'warm the audience for   a gratuity 😊He should stick to the very sobering events and be mindful of visitors emotions   and talk a lot less about himself.

I was really looking forward to seeing the tunnels but at the end of the day I was disappointed.  Based on mediocre reviews from other travellers we decided to just book a taxi and do it on our own. I am glad we did. The driver waited for us and we just paid the admission fee at the office and went in. The whole thing is very cheesy with fake grass, statues etc. They show you some tunnels that are not original, the booby traps etc but that is about it. As you get closer to the gun range the gunfire gets very loud. Overall I learned one or two things I didn’t know and would have been better of watching a documentary on the tunnels and doing something else. I am glad we didn’t spend 80 plus dollars on a tour. The taxi way cost us less than 40 dollars as a couple

This being a to thing on the Vietnam trail, was very disappointed with the tour. After a longish drive to the tunnels, you are met with a load of tour buses and a throng of people. I don't like saying this due to what happened here and to be fair some of it was fascinating and you can shoot machine guns... if you're on a time sensitive trip, go to the Mekong and watch a doco about this place.

Do not recommend if you are afraid of dark confining spaces. Tunnels a small & narrow, if you are a large person, will have difficulty navigating the tunnels. Glad i had a flash light with me.  I found the tunnels too confining for me.  Unless you happen to be a tunnel rat — soldiers during the war who cleared these tunnels with only a flash light & a .45 pistol.

A must visit fake-historic site you must visit commented by the fresh guide from the party.The most interesting thing about this site is not the endless cave, movies or speech well-prepared to demonstrate who's the good and the shooting range of course but the fact it 'd been built after the war on the 23rd regiment's station.

This place is always presented as a must see and for those interested in the history of the Vietnam war it may still be worth seeing. However when you go it is very much a tourist trap and a lot of the detail is lost. My advice is go to the war remnants museum instead.

Very interesting and informative place to visit, but don't buy the souvenirs made from bullet casings. They can't leave the country. My son bought 2 little planes made from empty bullet shells, for about $10 CAD each. He was able to bring on 2 domestic flights to/from Phu Quoc Island but when we flew home to Canada they were confiscated from his CHECKED luggage at security in Vietnam. The casings were empty and clearly a toy, but the officer didn't care. We pleaded our case but to no avail. The officer knew they were from the Cu Chi tunnels, but still said it was ammunition and a weapon. He said they were fine on a domestic flight but not internationally. What??! What's the difference?It's irresponsible of Cu Chi tunnels to not advise, or at least have signage stating these souvenirs cannot be taken out of the country. I imagine the majority of their clientele are foreigners. This was highly disappointing for my son. It was a waste of money and a upsetting way to end our otherwise wonderful 3 week trip through Vietnam.

As an American, I can say this was very slanted against America.  The film was nothing more than propaganda.  Somehow they believe America did not know they lived in tunnels!  Guess they do not realize we had the Geneva Treaty.  hot, humid, bugs

Interesting but not quite as I expected. Interesting to walk around and learn about the Viet Cong. Suggest you read information before attending, as not all is as you would be led to believe I have since discovered.

Super druk en toeristisch. Het begint met een onverstaanbare en onbegrijpelijke propagandafilm die stijf staat van de subjectiviteit. Geen enkele basis introductie m.b.t. de oorlog, het lokale verleden en/of idealen. Door de zinderende hitte met een chagrijnige gids door het oerwoud, met oorlog als vermaak voor toeristen. Ooh ja, je mag zelf een geweer afvuren als je wil. Onsmakelijk wat mij betreft. Cultureel een moetje, de enige reden waarom ik ben gegaan, maar ik adviseer U over te slaan.
It is a 2 hour drive to the tunnels. It is a long time to return travel for approx 1.5 hour visit to the tunnels. Not sure that it was worth it. Suggest go to the War Museum instead.

I'd been looking forward to seeing the Cu Chi Tunnels since I booked my trip to Vietnam almost a year ago.  The first half of the tour I was fine.  Hearing gunfire off in the distance really "added" (which seems like the wrong word to use) to the experience - being able to somewhat imagine what it must have been/sounded like for the soldiers.  That is until the midpoint of the tour, which is where the shooting range is.  The sound there was deafening and I'm not sure why but I started crying uncontrollably and could not stop.  All I could think to do was get away from it as soon as possible.  Even after moving on I couldn't stop crying - especially when someone would rapid fire a gun.  I honestly can't imagine why someone would want to fire one of those guns, I just don't get it.  Plus, as an American, I kept thinking of the kids in our schools who have heard something similar to this - and how they shouldn't ever have to.  It also really made me wonder how anyone can come out of serving during wartime and not be changed in some way.  I'm shocked everyone doesn't come out of it with PTSD.I didn't expect to react the way I did, my reaction completely shocked me (and still does).  I wish the shooting range wasn't in the middle of the tour because after being there I had to skip the rest of the tour (and going into the tunnels is after that).  I had to get as far away from the sound as I possibly could. If you go (and I think it is worth going) I hope you don't react the way I did.

I was hoping to be able to experience to Cu Chi tunnels and leave feeling more enlightened and educated with knowledge.However the Cu Chi tunnels is over run with tourists everywhere you look and you hardly have time to look at an exhibit bridge being shoved out of the way by a tour group, local guide or group of tourists. The shooting range in the midst of the tunnels also contributed to my poor experience. The shooting range is a short stones throw from the gift shop / amenities. The sound of the guns firing is piercing and I imagined would be quite traumatic for anyone who has experienced any trauma related to guns. Our tour guide described the tunnels and traps to be similar to the ‘Home Alone’ movies... I tuned out after this poor comparison.

This place doesn't have much to offer. There are some poorly done displays of booby traps and some European looking mannequins (not Asian looking at all) which don't really do anything informative. Overall, the only thing there with any authenticity at all is, supposedly, a short section of tunnel you can walk through. I think it's strange that of more than 100km of tunnels you can only actually see like 50 meters of one of them and no rooms. Maybe I missed something.If you want to learn about the American War, it's much better to go to the War Remnants museum, which actually has some content to offer. Not recommended - save yourself the drive.

Mijn tip is om de Cu Chi Tunnels over te slaan. Er zijn mooiere en interessantere plaatsen in Vietnam te zien. En als u zo graag wilt schieten dan kunt u dat goedkoper in Nederland of Vlaanderen doen.
Negen jaar geleden waren de openingen en de tunnels nog smaller. Nu is alles aangepast aan de toeristen. Destijds was het schieten nOg inclusief,  nu extra betalen. Volgens mij echt geen aanrader.

abbiamo visitato il secondo sito alternativo (Ben Duoc). E' più piccolo e meno interessante di quello accanto (Ben Dinh)infatti il biglietto d'ingresso è inferiore. E' completamente asservito al richiamo turistico. Di fianco pare esista un campo giochi o villaggio turistico il cui animatore microfonato urla imperterrito disturbando la calma e la meditzioe di un luogo comunque degno di profonda riflessione per quanto ivi  accaduto.

While I don’t think it was anything to do with our guide or travel company, Cu chi tunnels did not meet my expectations in the slightest. The whole thing was somewhat of a spectacle built for tourists and didn’t seem authentic. There was AstroTurf everywhere, replicas of the traps and weapons, tunnels that were obviously widened for westerners and a firing range. The documentary was of really poor quality and if I had my time over I probably wouldn’t go again.

I can't say I enjoyed the trip when the shadow of a malignant war was lingering all over the place . I was saddened , angry sometimes but  felt hopeless when I remember what human is capable of . Cu chi itself  was/is ?! Lovely . I just wished I visited the place for another reason ... alas , if only history can be rewritten !

Really long day (8 hours) for not much history and about 5 minutes in the tunnels. If you really want to spend 5 minutes in a tunnel and 3-5 minutes shooting a gun with your whole day this one is for you.

Was interesting to see some of the techniques used by the viet cong but it is heavily one-sided. Our guide said that the viet cong could outsmart American soldiers because they were always drunk and that they were slow because they ate too many burgers and kfc. They didnt mention that the vietnamese lost nearly every conflict and the death ratio was 1:13 US solider to Vietcong.Also have to watch a ridiculous video that describes Americans as violent devils killing everything in their path (and cite that they shot chickens). The video goes on to explain how the viet cong drove the Americans out of the country and won the war, which again is completely inaccurate as the Americans left due to a political defeat back home.
Lange rit vanuit Saigon.  Interssant om te zien maar is zo mega druk dat je de beleving niet echt meekrijgt. Van alle excursies die we in Vietnam hebben gedaan is dit veruit de minste .

Как-то вообще не очень, мы ожидали большего. Сложно добираться, короткий-тоннель, человек который нам должен был давать информацию о месте говорил по-английски очень плохо. Вывод: сами больше не поедем, и вам не советуем

Took the long 2 hour drive from HCMC to see one of the must see tourist sights outside of the city.We were a group of 4 and had a young enthusiastic guide who tried hard to make it seem like the tunnels were the most important part of the war.Everything is new. The original tunnels have been destroyed or a small part is available for Vietnam citizens only.The fake exhibits, manakins, hype and exaggerations were over the top .No country can be proud of the war but to being proud of torture is also wrong.

The intricacy and egineering the tunnels is fascinating. However this is lost on the presentation and the tone set by the tour. Instead of presenting the site as a place highlighting the fortitude of the people, repsecting the dead (on both sides), and exploring the sombre reasons behind the development of the tunnels one is presented with a carnival like atmosphere with jokes about bad tourists being sent to Cambodia or tortured in booby traps. This led to witnessing young people dance and joke on a tank (where apparently soldiers died) and pretend to perfom fellatio on the tanks gun. Call me a buddy-duddy but I found this disturbing. I have always wanted to fire a machine gun but could not bring myself to do it at this place as it just seemed too out-of-place, too irreverent.  Too bad this could be such a meaningful and educational experience.
Visitamos los túneles (que en realidad se trata de un trocito de túnel) y, aunque la experiencia resulte interesante, la visita en sí resulta decepcionante.
Это просто смешно, три ямки и два манекена, тоннелей в реальности нет, даже по пути, это потерянное время
Might have been more interesting for nationals of countries that were involved in the Vietnam War. Pretty much designed for American tourists.

Honestly I’m glad I’ve done it however I wouldn’t go again. It’s a couple hour bus ride and a quick walk around. It’s crazy to see what the tunnels were like. There’s just not enough to see for the long bus ride.

This is a "must do" and some of the actual tour and chat was good. It is however very dumbed down and groups are tripping all over each other.Go if you must or passing - the downside is the 2 hours each way (via a factory) from HCMC - when you are there it's OK, although not authentic. The gun range is way overpriced and too noisy - right next to the cafe which is a shame.Our guide flew through the whole place - do the Mekong tour instead maybe, also touristy but much more fun!TIPS ON PRICING - the actual entrance cost is 110,000, often included in your tour. We nearly did the speedboat version for 1m each but settled on 500,000 each with small bus and water - less pickups and all good.

So much publicity, yet so little to see. The highlight of this trip is shooting the gun and walking about 5 minutes through the tunnels. And I really mean it when I say tunnel. You walk through no room or other space than a tunnel (not much to see, but is a strange experience to walk through that little hot space). You can also choose a gun to shoot at the end of the tour - although a little expensive (20 usd for 10 bullets). The sad thing is that they don't tell you anything about how to shoot or keep your posture right... I think there were much nicer tours, even for those interested in learning about history (for example, can gio island shows much more, as you can see how Vietnamese people lived in the mangrove forest. Plus, you get to see lots of monkeys there). I don't think it is worth the trip to the tunnels, as you lose 1 day (about 4 to 5 hours the round trip from HCM, and 2 hours at the tunnels).

Perhaps all tours are different, but with two unadvertised stops on our booked trip, the journey out and back was lengthy and uninteresting. The actual tunnels visit was very interesting, but the place is poorly managed to the point that the various independent tour groups frequently collided, often making it difficult to hear what out guide was saying and sometimes having to bustle with others through various points of interest. I’m pleased I did the tour but I won’t be rushing back for a second time.

Well politically aim, you would have no difficulty to figure out who's the bad and the ugly. A tour full of historic bias date's facts. You'll hear why you're not perfect and decadent and how to correct yourself. Visit of different station and tunnels. You can try yourself at a shooting range trying to remodel history.  Enjoy corrupted imperialist!

I had heard nothing but praise about the tunnels, but for me it was a huge disappointment. Our guide spoke so fast and with a heavy accent that we couldn't understand very much, not even the American (native English speaker) in our group. The tour also seemed extremely rushed. The guide ushered us on very quickly, and there were a lot of groups before and after us, so the path felt very crowded. I had no time to really soak it in.When my group spoke about it afterwards we all agreed that it could have been so much better. It was cool to see the tunnels, but we felt like we didn't really learn much. Also - I feel that the shooting range next to the tunnels felt really distasteful. Shooting can be fun, sure, but at a war museum it just feels wrong..
Meeting a former VC was interesting as were the tunnels, but the heavy-handed propaganda detracted from what could have been a healing moment for veterans.
Cam giac chi la la lam. Khong co gi dac biet , cac duong ham thi chat choi, am thap. Khong dang de den tham quan
except play, normal people no like stay here, that war yr,poor people be die .....they kill the pity poor children.

This place was nice to look at but it is extremely touristy, we struggled to have a look at everything due to the large tour groups that take up all the room.The tunnels they offer aren't even the originals. It was interesting to see everything but I've heard the other tunnels are better (where the locals go).

Very touristy in not necessarily a great way. Personally found it fairly sterile and didn’t give a good view on the tunnels or war as does the War Remnants museum. Found the shooting range quite distasteful and glamourises war. Wouldn’t bother in hindsight.

В описаниях все выглядит круто, на деле это небольшие (судя по всему сделанные для туристов) тоннели метров по 20-30. Экскурися длится около часа Зато в окрестностях большой парк с разными активностями, в целом довольно уныло

It’s a 2 hour drive each way much of it in traffic jams, and 1 1/2 hours there.The quality of Cu Chi Tunnels are very run down, and the exhibits are low quality.Many of the mannequins used in the displays are broken, missing or just poor quality.I would have given this a miss if I’d have known.
We couldn't understand our tour guide, very long ride to the tunnels and very little time spent there. Tourist trap!!

Beware ..They have allowed too many tourists at same time visiting which means with 4 hrs travel time you have wiped out most of your day . If you have limited time may not be worth it . Our tour company infinity included lunch which didn’t happen so take food . Tunnels were interesting and stories good but spoilt by travel and too many people .
Interesting, very far out, not worth the trip. Tunnels are very small, need to go on hand and knees, if claustrophobia this is definitely a no no.

We booked in one of the cheap tours being advertised in the shops of  bie vieu street (bagpackers district). The same tour can be sold in the rangs of 4-7 $ depending on your luck and the.this tour is the cheapest option it includes transportation by an,airconditioned bus, guide and a big number group. It excluded the 110 dong entrance to the tunnels. If budget is not your concern you can buy the smaller group option for around 30-25$. The tour was scheduled at 1 we left at 1:45 after picking up all the travellers from their hotels. We came back at around 7:30 after being stuck in trafiic several time. 6 hrs 30 mins was the whole time spent to attend a 1 hr tour (which I do reccommend). My tip will be dont skip it. Its is interestinf but do it yourself. Rent a bike amd follow the map.if you didnt go by my advise and decided to book a tour, better to ask other travellers around for recommendations as the quality of the guide will me make the whole experience different

I was so excited about this tour and it turned out to be a total waste of a day. Firstly it took us nearly 2.5 hours to reach the tunnels and about 2 hours to return to city center. A 4.5 hour round trip would be a stretch for a great tour. This tour was a farce. I was extremely uncomfortable with the photos opportunities our tour guide encouraged. Posing with fake VietCong plastic mannequins or with tanks that devastated a nation is wildly inappropriate. It felt really wrong. During the majority of the tour you can also hear loud gun fire in the background as a result of a firing range. I have to imagine that would be a massive trigger for anybody who has actually served in any armed conflict. My husband and I both enjoy shooting guns so we did try this tourist trap. I’ll admit it was interesting to shoot guns that I’ve never had access to in the US but it was insanely overpriced and very rushed like the rest of the tour. Our tour guide - Gingko Tours - was very difficult to understand and literally said things like “come look here - this is a flashlight so they could see in the dark.” We didn’t pay $200 USD and take 7 hours of out of our trip to have a guide point to objections and state their name - here is a canteen, here is a hole ... yeaaaa got it. What I was looking forward to but heard none of was the importance of the tunnels in the Tet Offensive, the daily life of a person living in the tunnels, etc. There was almost a void of genuine history during this tour. I was surprised to find out that almost none of what we saw was remaining from the actual Cu Chi tunnels. The recreations of the tunnels are lame. They lack authenticity. The entire tour lacked authenticity. I think they could easily make this site more representative of the incredible history that took place here by adding more history, removing the gimmicky mannequins and photo opportunities, and giving tourists a better sense of what life was like during this terrible event in world history.

They have many of these sort of tours in other heavily visited Asian cities like Hong Kong and Bali.. they go on for way too long and although your paying for the 6 hour tour only 1.5 hours of the trip is actually visiting the tunnels. The rest is travel time. And to be honest I could of watched a documentary at home and learnt more about the tunnels then I did on my tour. Save your time and money and avoid !

I had so many expectations for this tour but I turned out to be very disappointed. As someone that really likes history, the tour added nothing to me. It was like the pictures, nothing interesting or fun. Our tour guide wasn’t good either (he was lazy and we could barely understand his English) so that made me like even less the place. There is a shooting place and it was incredibly loud and annoying for me. If you really want to go, go for the experience and to say you went there but don’t create any expectations.

We felt frustrated and exhausted after a long day at Cu Chi. We stopped at a "handicapped craft factory" which tugged our heartstrings but wasn't well integrated into the tour, we were quickly herded into a shockingly overpriced store to buy goods and were sold water at a ridiculous price of 30, 000 per 250mL. At this stage I was not bothered. We donated to the factory and kept going.We had been picked up at 8am from our hostel, and had managed to get to a cafe for breakfast, but for the ensuing six and a half hours there was NOWHERE tobuy lunch. We were exhausted,and there was no acknowledgement of this by our tour guide. How you could take a packed lunch, while travelling, I'm not sure!! The tunnels themselves are interesting, but the place ispacked with "fillers", including a "documentary" which is impossible to hear for half an hour, and various instruments of war, including a shooting range, which seemed like an offcolour addition. Plenty of places to pose with US tanks and artillery, but if this isn't what you enjoy, it will smack of an amusement park. If you are still keen to go, beware cheaper tours that stop for treats, but are just hustling sales and then berating you for a five star review.Not a great day, we asked to get off the minivan after they gave two vegetarians weasel coffee without disclosing what it was. By the end, we were hungry and ethically compromised.

This was advertised as a half-day trip to me but it took three hours to reach the tunnels, which are 70km away, because of traffic. Throw in the fact that you only spend 90 minutes actually there if you’re on a tour - most of which is just photo opportunities with modern remakes of traps etc - and the whole experience is a bit of a waste.Only bother going if you like attractions swarming with tourists or you want to spend a small fortune to fire a rifle for a few seconds.

Wenn man die Fotos im Internet gesehen hat, braucht es keinen Besuch mehr. Man kann nur einen ganz kurzen Teil der Tunnels begehen. Es gibt keine differenzierte Auseinandersetzung mit dem geschichtlichen Hintergrund. Die Fahrt dahin hat sich für mich nicht gelohnt.Wir waren am Nachmittag und es hatte mittelviele Leute.

This was top of our list but I left disappointed. I did find it interesting but I couldn’t help but feel that it was all a little stage managed and prefabricated to cater for increased tourism into the country and to be a revenue spinner. I fully acknowledge that the comments may be controversial and due to the interest in the subject matter, that  there is clearly a significant health and safety matter to address to help and satisfy obvious public curiosity.The venue is well laid out, clean and well supported by friendly and efficient staff. No complaints there, but it just did not feel genuine or authentic. Even down to the ability to shoot live bullets on a range which was part of the exhibition if you were happy to pay for that experience.

We love to learn about the culture and history of a country. That's why we booked a tour for the Cu Chi tunnels. Our experience is that this is not a good idea! The Cu Chi tunnels are interesting dont get me wrong. But the touriat groups are awful. It was just like: 'Okay here we have a hole, here is this and here is that. Okay we have to move on for the next group.' There was no time to let it sink in. They try to make a show out of it, while it is not something considered lightly. It would be better if you could go on your own and have an audio tour.

In terms of how important this place were for the war, I and my crew expected much more. Old installations and less explanations. The bullet to try a shot is also very expensive (almost USD20 for a peck of 10).

Tog oss 2.5 + 3.0 timmar att transportera oss fram och tillbaks på grund av trafiken i staden. Hopppas detta ständiga problem ändrar sig när metron står klar. Annars var det ett besök som varade i 45 minuter. Största attraktionen var att klättra ner i en tunnel. Önskar mer och större sevärdhet i samband med platsen.

We took a bus trip from Saigon which cost 185k VND and didn't include tickets. The Company (called "calm and peace" or so) packed us in a bus with 40 other tourists. What was good about the tunnels: - clean, made for tourists- the tunnels themselves What was bad? - long ride from Saigon (3h+) - big bus, many people, only one guide- only 120m of tunnels, the rest ist corny open air sites! - puppets in military uniformes everywhere so you can take corny pictures with themAll in allege only good thing about it were the crawl through 120m of tunnel which gave you a hint about how it must have felt down there. Everything else was horribly touristy. I would go to another complex of tunnels the next time...

The place is some 55km from the center but takes 2-3h one way depending on the traffic - so the whole day spent.In addition all guides admit that all proper artifacts like guns, tanks or bombs were sold to Russia and China so that they could replicate the technology.So you are left with less than bare minimum of the worst samples. The tunnels are 250km but tourists are only allowed to view 100m!!!You can shoot 10 bullets from old AK47 or similar for some 12$2* only because of a good guide we had.Not recommended

It was not expensive but i wish I had taken a moto taxi to the Cu Chi Tunnels.  The bus must have had 40+ tourists on it.  We exited the bus at the destination at which time if you were not in the front of the pack you really could not see or hear what the heck the tour guide was talking about.  Then all were encouraged to enter a tunnel in a tight formation and it was very hot and sweaty down there.  I was not into it after having to chase down the tour guide with 49 other people.  The tour guide tried but seemed to go thru the motions and really didn’t know or care if all were present and accounted for. I just wanted to get the heck out the area and would like to return alone and relax and take my time.

We went to the cu Chi tunnels nearby Saigon but it wasn't what we expected. The whole area was full with other tourist, everything was expensive. We wanted to shoot a few bullets with a AK-47, but $20 was a little bit too much. The tunnels we went in, were only 100 mtrs. Not worth to do it.
Learned a lot about the War but the tunnels was done in a few minutes ....not what we expected ...underground living rooms are not included in our tour So sad :(

Very touristy. I felt like was on a conveyor belt squashed between two large groups of very noise Chinese teenagers. Staff  understandably looked bored to tears. I gained no more knowledge of the Vietnamese /US conflict than I ready know from reading or watching a few television programmes . If you do decide to go, go with a private guide that way you may learn something. Which ever way you go take mosquitos spray we certainly needed it

Tunnelsystem interessant jedoch keine Infos über Kriegsverlauf und Hintergründe. Propagandavideo (Böse Amis die friedliche Bauern angriffen) Unnötige Stopps auf An- und Abreise (Agent Orange Workshop und verordnetes Mittagessen)

I visited with a guided tour group. This is quite a trek outside Ho Chi Minh. It's very touristy. Every time you get to a station, you're struggling to hear your tour guide over the other tour guides. The perspective is very much that of Vietnam, rather than an unbiased point of view (I realize this can be difficult with war, but this was pretty extreme). The shooting range area is very loud, even if you're just in the shop waiting for others to shoot. It is interesting to see how the VC lived and fought. I certainly wouldn't go again, but it is a piece of history.

A bit hard to describe, but the tone of the sights at Cu Chi is not as somber as it deserves to be. I agree with the other review describing it as "Disney-fied". We were guided through several exhibits showing truly awful things: living conditions within the caves, a variety of traps used to capture soldiers, a collection of the weaponry used throughout, etc., and each was presented as if it were part of a game. Our tourguide grinned throughout, with a "isn't this cool?!" attitude about the "clever" ways in which lives were taken. While I definitely appreciate this piece of history and I'm glad to have learned about it, I couldn't help feeling uncomfortable with how callously the tragedy was discussed.
Beautiful Place but Got Robbed ..My Friend had her handbag on her and someone just flicked it and Ran, She Lost her money and Passport

I booked a fairly cheap tour of the tunnels in HCM which is what most people tend to do when they're visiting. A guide is essential really as I didn't see any information signs about the exhibits that would explain what you can see. I won't comment about the guide and transport though as this isn't organised by the Cu Chi Tunnels.Overall the place is just okay. You're shown a few of the holes that the Vietnam soldiers used as well as the booby traps and the stories are impressive however there was still a part of me thinking that I was just looking at a hole in the ground. The gun range is far to expensive for what it is and is bizarrely located right next to the sort of refreshment area meaning anybody that doesn't want to shoot the guns has to put up with the deafening noise. The refreshment stop half way through the tour only has a few things to offer and nothing that would really fill you up that much.The highlight really is the tunnels at the end which is 100m long although there are a few points you can get out at if you suddenly decide you don't want to go further. It's actually really hard work which I didn't because your constantly in this tight squat position and shuffling along so everyone was covered in sweat by the time they got to the end.In summary I would say that this isn't really worth the 2 hour trip from HCM and if you're interested in the Vietnam war you're better off going to one of the museum's in the city.

We took a half day trip to Cu Chi trip and it was really not worth it. The tunnels themselves are of course interesting, but at Cu Chi you only get a small glance at it and are rushed through the "parcours-like" exhibition. The shooting ranch is a really unnecessary addition to this touristic place which adds constant gun shot background noise to this unpleasant experience.If you have a chance visit Vinh Moc tunnels more up in the North of Vietnam. This is a much better experience: less tourists and you walk through several tunnels.All in all booking a guided tour from HCMC to Cu Chi is an overpriced trip with tourist rip off stops in between (art shop and restaurant). You can spend your time and money better in Vietnam!

It was interesting to see the tunnels. If you keep an open mind it's interesting to see the way the government portrays the war from its perspective. I'm sure all our governments lie to is about war but the propaganda was slightly obvious. Between that and the translations, it was awkward sitting through a video talking often about people who were "American Killer Hero's". If you keep that mind open as an observer of humans and governments, it's fascinating to find that the government teaches (in schools) the war was between Vietnam and America... no word of south Vietnam.... except in regard to the north winning because many spies from the south acted as allies. I like to take an outsider's view and learn about humans and the things we do, say, and believe. Still, interesting to see a bit of it. I am too young to be very emotionally affected by the war, even though my father fought in it. Those with more direct ties and personal experiences my have much stronger reactions.

We hadden deze tunneltrip icm met de Cao Dai Temple gedaan. Conclusie: je reist echt veel (5u+) en bent relatief heel kort bij de sites. Beide zijn niet noemenswaardig mooi of interessant, de foto van de tunnel laat 80 procent van Cu Chi Tunnel attractie zien, alleen de verschillende boobytraps zijn daarnaast nog enigzins de moeite waard. Ik zou mijn tijd in Saigon de volgende keer toch echt anders benutten.

An sich sehr interessant. Man erhält durchaus Einblicke in das Leben der Guerilla. Teilweise erscheint alles etwas propagandistisch. Was zu erwarten war, sind die Massen an Touristen, die durch das Museumsdorf ziehen.Was mich sehr abgeschreckt hat, war das Schießen. Daran stoße ich mich. Touristen können just 4 fun mit echten Waffen abfeuern. Dies wird von den Guides auch noch beworben. Ob solch eine Aktivität an einem Ort wie diesem sein muss, bezweifle ich. Meins ist es jedenfalls nicht und ich würde es nicht wieder tun.
