We booked a half day tour of Cu Chi tunnels through our hotel and the tour was run by Hai Travel.We left our hotel at 7:45am and returned at 4pm - not a half day.The coach was full and had no air con. The journey took over 2 hours each way and it was unbearable. We asked the tour guide to turn up the air con and we were told it was broken but there was no apology.Upon arrival at Cu Chi, we were behind all the other coaches as we were running late. We had a separate guide for Cu Chi and he took round a group of SIXTY people. We could not hear a thing.Cu Chi itself is interesting and it was fun going through the tunnels but we only did 40m instead of 100m and there was no explanation by our guide. It is a tourist trap - so busy you can't see or hear much. There is a very loud shooting range near where there are things to look at but you can't hear the guide.My advice is to pay more to go in a small group on a bus with air con. Then we might have enjoyed Cu Chi!

I know this 'attraction' is a 'must see' in Vietnam but personally I was underwhelmed by the experience. I expected to see the extent of tunnels but only saw a hideout, 1 tunnel and some boobie traps. You only got an idea of the vast tunnel network by viewing a poorly built, small cross section model before enduring a cheesy, propaganda video at the conclusion of the tour, which for me was the last straw. This 'epic' just went on for too long.......exhausting. The best part of this tour for me was the boat ride down the river to and from Ho Chi Minh and the provided lunch upon conclusion. A lot more work to do before this is an attraction worth visiting.

Good to visit and learn about..but the actual place and experience is very tourist-oriented..almost to child-level. Ironic, as the place is not young-child friendly..very hot, bugs, rifle range closed...etc. won't be back!

REG SHOOTING RANGE: the shooting range was closed and apparently has been closed for months due to them having no bullets. They will not tell you this as the range is at the very end of the tour.The whole tour ,while interesting is incredibly bias when it comes to history. Be Prepared to watch a 20 minute propaganda  video about the "American killer" who killed a bunch of soldiers. They glorify the violence to enemy nations while condemning violence to their own. You would never see this kind of blatant disregard for foreign sensitivities in any other country . We don't have videos about glorifying  "jap killers " in Australia, and never will.

Simply not worth the faff. Our tour guy was nice enough. But the place itself is just not impressive. You can go into a few tunnels...but that's about it. If you know ANYTHING about the Vietnam War you'll find the 'history' side of the whole thing primary-level and dull. Though if you have the need to hop down into a tunnel and have a photo taken of you with the lid above your head - as many people seem to want to do - then go for it...
I think it is not good enought for paying what they make you pay!If I would have to choose between going there or going to The war museum. I would choose The war museum!
La visita nos decepciono, todo muy comercial, lleno de maniquies y maquetas. Lo unico que se salva es pasar por el tunel y si eres claustrofobico ni eso...

Just be warned it is so packed! Ultimate tourist trap and takes 1hr30 mins to get to!!!! So if you are not really into the History and just going to say you have been, then don't go because it is frustrating when you are there and I'm a History teacher!

A very touristy trip.People taking selfies, smiling, on a broken american tank.Fun memory? Shooting with AK, in Vietnam?No problem, you can do that on this tour.Time changes and people forget.Ok that the local  people getting money from the terrible things that happened, now so long ago that nobody on the tour didn´t seem to care.Rather disgusting.The good thing: Nice seeing the area a bit out of Saigon.

We combined the cooking class and tunnels. I woumd recommend just to see the tunnels by itself. The guide / cook clearly didn't like her job and was a bit rude at times (and probably some cultural misunderstandings). Tunnels where very interesting though, but It was all a bit rushed. I recommend going there with a company specialising in that specific tour.

A pretty disappointing tour. The museum itself was interesting and a few good things to see. We booked a tour with The Sinh Tourist. The whole organisation of the tour groups was a disaster. We started our tour along with 4 other groups and our guide had to fight for a spot to gather the group to tell us the information. It was hard to hear your tour guide because you can constantly hear other group discussions. We missed out a couple of stops on the tour as they were busy with other tour groups and we couldn't get in to see. We got to the restaurant and the guide said we were stopping for lunch then bizarrely two seconds later he had whisked us away to watch a documentary then straight after we were taken to the bus back to Ho Chi Minh. There was no point watching the documentary as it was far too quiet. A women did turn it up but a little boy in our group was constantly speaking with his dad encouraging him that I couldn't hear anything anyway. There needs to be a better system with tour groups as the whole experience is lost trying to find your group and hear what your guide is saying because of all the distractions. I'd rather have waited for 10/15 mins at the start so as we could have a better experience of the tour in our group.

Our tour guide was excellent. He was very funny. Had brilliant English and tried to get to know us individually and learn a little about our slang words. It was a very short tour however for the length of the bus ride and the video we watched at the start did not explain the history very well. I had to ask our guide questions (which he answered very well) and google about it after.

Just way too overtouristed, could barely hear our guide over all the other guides. Probably only good if you want to shoot guns. That said, the guide makes it, and ours wasn't really the most interesting fella

Interesting visit but a tourist trap and propaganda outlet. They show you some traps and some scenes with mannequins and you can crawl through a prepared tunnel. I don't recommend the shooting   range, hearing protection is of poor quality, there's no door so kids were able to enter the shooting area and the  noise is terrible. In  short, only  go if you have time to spare.

se ve en una hora, y porque te van dando el conazo en cada agujero, siempre hay gente que ve un palo en el agua y le parece . un lugar magico y marvilloso que para eso se han gastado la pasta en ir tan lejos, pero alla cada cual con su escala de valores. lo unico que tiene diferente es que por 20 euros puedes pegar unos tiros con un AK47

Did not enjoy at all and wish I hadn't gone. It was a gruesome display of the traps used and the description of the slow deaths of many young soldiers spoken of with great pride. War is not pretty and it's a horrible reminder of how we should avoid sinking to mutilating our fellow human beings.

The only good thing about this was the bus ride there because you saw some countryside.It wasn't informative enough, and make sure you eat before you go because there's no actual food along the way besides ice cream.Just nope nope nope
It's very crazy to know what Vietnamese people did in that area. But I think would be better to read a book about it...

War museum was much better. Think it is over priced and tour guides kind of just want to get you through. Our group only got 1 tunnel. Getting to use the guns was easily the best part.  We also took a speedboat back thank god!
C'est pas exceptionnel, voir les tunnels de la guerre ok mais bon le guide parle que vietnamien on ne comprend pas une miette de ce qu'il raconte.

Took a tour bus both directions. Long long ride. Some tours offer speed boats to the tunnels. I suggest you take a boat in one direct at least. We went in the afternoon, which is said to be less crowded. The tour of the tunnels was rather depressing. There are many more interesting things to do in the Saigon area.

Wir sind aus Saigon mit einer kleinen Gruppe angereist und waren sehr enttäuscht. Dies lag aber nicht an dem Ort, dieser ist unglaublich sehenswert und nur zu empfehlen. Es ist einfach verrückt, wie die da damals Krieg geführt haben. Das ganz große Problem war, dass wir alles andere als allein waren. Hunderte, wenn nicht sogar tausende Menschen schlängeln sich da die kleinen Pfade entlang. Etwas fülligere Touristen probieren allen ernstes in die kleinen Löcher im Boden zu verschwinden, den die kleinen vietnamesischen Kämpfer damals benutzt haben... und bleiben stecken!!! Drei Männer mussten die Frau da raus ziehen.Wenn es irgendwie geht dann sollte man mitten in der Woche, weit vor Tagesbeginn aufbrechen um dort zu sein sobald es öffnet und somit weit vor den ankommenden Touribussen. Am besten an einem regnerischen Tag :D Wirklich! Wenn man da halbwegs ungestört durchlaufen kann dann ist es der Hammer.

non  mi ha entusiasmato, oltre a verificare lo stato della foresta in cui si trovavano i combattenti, con caldo tremendo ed aria umida, i tunnel che fanno praticare ai turisti non dicono nulla, per me si puo' comodamente evitare. Patetica la possibilita' che danno ai turisti di sparare con fucili veri, a pagamento si intende. No creare una specie di Disneiland su la guerra non mi piace.

Over hyped tourist trap. So many people here and not much info. The guides are not very helpful and people are everywhere so you can't even get a good picture. More information is provided at the War Remnant museum and at least that is air conditioned and you can go through it at your own pace. Guides are also very hard to understand. Save your money

I think it is good that a country deals with ist history and the victory from North Vietnam is here very visible. Who wants a critical contention with this topic shouldn't go here. And there are fields for shooting and playing paintball - I'm sorry but at a Setting of war this is fulsome! There were also some puppets placed like figures in a theme parc.Going through the tunnes was the only interesting part of it !

I think the Cu Chi tunnels are something on balance that most people will want to visit to explore part of Vietnam's history. However our visit was very disappointing as they are a very overcrowded tourist action with groups herded around quickly! On balance we would not recommend going.

La parte delle trappole è stata molto interessante, ma tutto questo entusiasmo per questo posto io non l'ho provato. Un posto dove hanno perso la vita tantissime persone, ridotto a una "trappola" per turisti. Il tutto corredato con un poligono di tiro dove chiunque può sparare! Non mi sembra una cosa rispettosa per gli avvenimenti successi non molti anni fa. Avrei preferito mezza giornata in più per Hanoi.

I was very much looking forward to this but felt like bit of let down.The bus trip was horrible and glad when I got off but the guide on bus was fantastic.There really wasn't much to see and the one tunnel you were allowed in was very restricted to size and weight.This seemed to have massive mosquito clan there too!

Leider kann man die Tunnels nicht alleine besichtigen, sondern nur in der Gruppe mit Führer. Da ich in einer 4er Gruppe war, mussten wir uns beeilen, da die nächste Gruppe schon direkt hinter uns war. Daher wurden wir regelrecht durch die Tunnels gescheucht. In Ruhe ansehen war daher nicht möglich. Ich empfehle daher die Vinh Moc Tunnels an der DMZ, die sind viel interessanter, größer und man kann sich dort so lange aufhalten, wie man möchte!

We were looking forward to this, but were disappointed and a little disconcerted that people seemed to think it was ok to treat burnt out tanks in which people lost their lives as props for a photo op. It was interesting to learn about some of the challenges the guerrillas faced and the ingenuity they employed but I really wouldn't recommend a visit if you have any sensitivity to those who lost their lives in the conflict, whichever side they were on.

Besökte tunnlarna en halvdag. Det mest spännande vi fick se var en stridsvagn som faktiskt var på riktigt. Resten av området var helt fejk, allt från tunnlar till vägarna ovan jord mellan dom. Att vi dessutom hade en guide som var helt knäpp förbättrade inte situationen. Absolut inget jag skulle göra igen med facit i handen.

Ich war in verschiedenen Tunnelanlagen. Klaustrophobisch. Als Europäer passt man da nicht rein und kommt dreckig wieder raus. Es ist historisch und als Gründungsmythos  sicher wichtig (wie the Alamo) aber auch  42 Jahre her. Ein schöner Urlaub sieht nun mal anders aus. Und es gibt viel zu sehen!
We expected a bit more than a quick rush through.  Not much to see.the movie at the end is maybe interesting but the tour is boring as

We booked a half day afternoon tour on viator and were very disappointed. The tours usually take you to the shooting range nearby and you have to wait there for other people to shoot before they take you to the actual tunnels. We had a big tour bus and it took us 3.5h to get there instead of the usual 1.5h.The tunnels itself are great and quite impressive. Make sure you get more than an hour there (which is what we got after to long travel). It felt like we had to run through them after spending so much time on a bus.I was told you can book a private taxi and can go there without a scheduled tour group which is what I'd recommend.

Somewhat entertaining and informative. The main reason for our visit was to shoot the M-60 machine gun. When we in the part of the tour where you can shoot the guns we were informed that they ran out of bullets for the M-60 (we were there before noon). The only gun that they had bullets for was a carbine which would have been my last choice but that's all that they had.We paid for the ammo and were directed to the firing range. Apparently they don't maintain their guns very well, it jammed five times while going through one clip.If the main reason for your visit is to shoot the guns, I would advise against it.

The guides were knowledgeable and explained everything clearly, including how the traps worked and how people lived underground. The small museum and video at the beginning set the context well. I also appreciated the opportunity to try crawling through a short section of the tunnel.One star off just because it felt a bit crowded and touristy at times, and the shooting range nearby was quite loud and a bit unsettling. But overall, it's a must-see if you're interested in history and want to understand a different perspective of the war.

Everything’s was good. Our guide Tinh was very friendly,knowledgeable,their pick up timing also perfect, only things we found uncomfortable is their van, not comfortable for 12 people, particularly for 4 hours in busy traffic, not enough space in van, very small seats for 3 adults. Felt but tired after 4 hours drive.

I think it’s a must this visit. You do get a sense of how the war was fought. The tunnels themselves are an experience, but I think they could elevate the experience more. It was pretty basic. Oh and bring some food with you.

Interesting visit to understand the difficulties of the war and the ability of the Vietnamese to react.  Do not buy anything created with bullets because at the airport they seize everything: necklaces, key chains, planes, tanks

I originally wanted to take the bus, but considering the traffic in HCMC I booked a grave rider (motor scooter - 1,000,000) who was also my guide. The journey took 1.5h - back a little faster. Admission costs 125,000 dong. The facility was crowded - it was hard to take photos. All this time, all I heard was "balling." The system seemed a bit inauthentic. I'd rather have seen the Ben Duoc nearby than the Cu Chi tunnels. However, my driver was fired up that I should shoot. Honestly - I don't visit a theatre of war and shoot there. There is also food - by the way, next to the shooting range. Overall, nevertheless interesting to see the tunnels (you can also walk/crawl through) and traps.

Definitely worth visiting but like most popular tourist attractions in Vietnam, it was overly crowded with too many tour groups arrving at the same time. I was struggling to hear what my tour guide was saying as there another tour group very close to us and their guide was speaking too loudly. The cost was 500,000 VND (about USD $20) per person with Vietnam Adventure Tours. We did consider going there on our own by Grab but I am glad we didn't because it would have cost a lot more. (The ticket price for the tunnels  alone is 125,000 VND)It took about 2 hours to get to the tunnels from Ho Chi Minh. There was a 30 minute stop along the way.

A historical place that needs to be visited out of resepct for the country - sad when young people pose On tanks making duckfaces for best photos …..And the endless sound of shoothing that accompanies the trail - which Looks almost like a movie display. Had an entusiatic guide, a bit confusing since he had to understate his siding With the Americans in the war and showing VietCons strugle at the same time. Also, be aware traffic both ways, 2 hours plus each way from HCM city.

The Cu Chi tunnels are definitely worth a visit, very interesting and brings life to some of what we had seen in the war remnants museum. We went on the afternoon tour and were collected around 12:20 in the afternoon and returned around 8pm so it is quite a long day. Our guide Tuan was knowledgeable as he fought in the Vietnam war himself so shared some personal experiences. However, he was very aggressive and it honestly spoiled the experience for us. We stopped at a rest room on the way and a lone female member of our group was lost outside looking for the bus and we were starting to drive off. I pointed this out to Tuan and he screamed at me in front of the whole bus and told me to mind my own business and we would leave her behind (luckily she spotted the bus and ran over). Such a shame as this was our last day in Vietnam and on the whole the tour guides have been lovely. An interesting tour but I wouldn’t go again with this guide.

We visited the tunnels and battle ground site in the morning. This visit was interesting, specifically the crawl through the tunnels themselves. You coukd also go to a shooting range to fire a weapon if you choose to do so. If not on an organized tour, you could spend 2 hours here. The afternoon visit was to the Mekong Delta. This visit was less enganging and more focused on walking through local markets and stalls.

Cu Chi TunnelUnfortunately only the tourist complex. There are two tunnels that can be visited. We stayed at the Ben Dinh facility. The complex has been recreated for tourists. One also gets an impression of the hard life of the partisans and peasants and their struggle, but is just for the passage of touri masses. With souvenir shops and finally a shooting ranch where you can shoot with sharp weapons! There is a clenching that the pipes glow. You can hear the noise well before you get there. We do not think that such a facility, which is actually there to commemorate, is appropriate. If you have a choice, then visit the facility 20 km further north – Ben Duoc-. There are the original facilities, which are mostly visited by the locals. No Touri crowds! Without shooting ranch and such commerce. And if you want to learn more about the war, you should go to the War Reminant Museum in Saigon. Impressive!
It’s very far from the city. Not worth to visit so far for this reason when you have a less time. Better to explore other parts of the city.

Interesting to see some of what is left of the tunnels but the actual tunnel access is very limited - for small and fit folks.  The strangest thing was that the firing range was right next to the snack area and you had to endure excessive noise whilst eating.
Crawling through the Cu Chi Tunnels was not for us. Small spaces aren't our thing, and it felt too dark and stuffy down there.

Sadly what should have been one of the highlights of our tour was spoiled by the sheer volume of tourists in the place. 10 min waits at each of the exhibits in 38 degree heat in the jungle is not fun.

I joined a Group Tour that stated in Ho Chi Minh City. The Guide started talking about Vietnamese women, particularly those that drive their motor cycles. I did not find this funny!  Also 4 passengers in the bus kept talking loud and laughing all the way to the Cu Chin Tunnels.The tour began with the video presentation of the tunnels, very informative. After we visited several places where they show different types of tunnels, we went to the Shooting Range. Several men in our Group did the shooting, 600 dong per bullet,  minimum requirement to buy 10 bullets for 600,000 dong. Visited more places , then we all had to go inside the tunnel. it was too narrow and dark inside. Midway, I decided to ask for help and came back outside. For those with mobility issues, not recommended to go inside the narrow tunnel.  Recommend to visit? Maybe NOT, better to join the Mekong Delta Tour.

Disappointed as stopped off at disabled centre to get tourists to buy goods. Guide then rushed through Cu Chi tunnels. We didn't see the 3D film as advertised. The tunnels themselves were good but Kim Travel guide was not. 2 hours travelling each way . be  aware  we didn't get dropped back off at hotel... this tour was booked through booking.com with Kim Travel.

The journey to Cu Chi took much longer than expected, as we stopped for 1/2 an hour (not on the itinerary) at a craft workshop for the disabled and associated gift shop and cafe stalls for 25 minutes.  And did not get to see paddy fields/countryside which were on the sales leaflet. Disappointed.  I would advise anyone visiting the tunnels to read up on what to expect - they are really interesting but gruesome (examples of horrific traps for enemy soldiers I.e. Americans and their allies). I did go down to crawl through the narrow examples of smaller tunnels and did not care if they were‘genuine’ or reproduction as they gave a real sense of claustrophobia and how impossibly difficult life down there must have been.  I knew about but wasn’t enamoured of the shooting range which, annoyingly was next to the cafe(!).  I think you can get a lot from this trip, just manage expectations and be informed.

We had excellent guide who spoke good English and was very friendly. It was interesting to learn more about the history of the war.  You can go down a short section of tunnel, unfortunately someone ahead of us started to panic and we came to a standstill, as I was at end I turned and went back out as I was starting to feel claustrophobic.They have area with examples of the rather horrific booby traps they used.Felt some people were a bit disrespectful, considering that people fought and lost their life here.  Clambering about the tanks, this was adults, shouting and cheering!  You can also buy ammunition and fire guns at a shooting range again to lots of cheering, felt this spoiled it for us.There is a shop and place for snacks, unfortunately rather close to shooting range.In all a bit disappointed, could have been so much better if people just showed some respect.

The tunnels etc are an incredible thing to see & deserve their place as a bona-fide tourist site. Unfortunately, the area is treated with huge disrespect. The firing range with the AK47's & machine guns & the whooping & cheering of some visitors climbing on tanks etc was totally inapproprite. Honestly (& sorry to say)  it felt like a theme park for the loud & uneducated for much of my time here.

We visited this landmark on a cruise ship tour (Princess cruises), and although filled with history and given a chance to climb into a similar tunnel and traverse underground for a short bit, it was really touristy and campy to say the least. Imagine a Pirates of the Caribbean ride at Disney (without any animatronics) and calling it a history of naval piracy tour…that’s what it kind of felt like. I do know this tour (be it on a cruise ship or from any tour company) is going to remain immensely popular for the area, but I’m thinking I would of rather spent a day in Ho Chi Minh City exploring than going on this tour.

it is interesting, you can see commercial activities, brick factories, processing, cosmetics and sweets coming from coconut... more boat explorations of tributaries of the Mekong, a nice meal in a family restaurant with local fish from the river. Pleasant.

This was a difficult tour for me considering it was the tunnels the Viet Cong used to live in and launch surprise attacks. Several people enjoyed exploring the tight quarters on our tour. It was buggy and humid so dress accordingly. We had a couple of veterans in our group that were also triggered. You should be in good physical shape to squeeze through some of these tunnels.

This was a half day tour which took most of the day due to traffic. We left our hotel at 12 o’clock and got to the tunnels at 3.30pm. The tunnels themselves were interesting but our guide needed to give us more information. Locating the gun firing station next to the gift/food area is a mistake as it is so noisy!! We did this as a small group tour but the group was too big. I would highly recommend a private tour here as they seem to get through quicker as a lot of waiting around. A disappointing tour unfortunately!
Very touristy! Important to learn the history of Vietnam but also so sad & confronting.  Rifle range must pump through hundreds of thousands of dollars a day!

Visiting the Cu Chi Tunnels is one of the highlights when you are in HCMC. The journey from the city takes around 1.5 hours. Personally, I didn't find the whole tour anything special. Knowing that people have lived more or less in the tunnels for years is certainly impressive. Visiting the tunnels was OK, but nothing more. Visiting the tunnels (some of which are made larger on purpose) is certainly fun, but ultimately you see relatively little of the existing tunnels. But that is certainly an individual opinion.

Somehow a "must-see", but boring. The shooting is completely overpriced. Otherwise, without a stop at an art "stall", the event is over pretty quickly and you spend more time sitting in the car than anything else. You have to do it, I guess, but somehow we expected more.

Informative and properly organized tour. The problem is the bus going and coming back to Ho Chi Minh City. The bus aircon was so low you could feel it was only on fun mode. We were sweating because it felt like we were inside an oven. There were no windows you could open. The seats of the bus is also very tight and small. Word of advice, do not do the shooting part because it is very expensive. It is a total rip off. Bring your own snacks and water. The stores inside are very expensive as well. Bonus experience, we met 2 real former VC rebels during the tour.
If you are a claustrophobic person, do not try to enter the tunnel. Despite being only approximately 20 meters, crawling may be pretty intimidating. It's pitch black in the tube.

Not sure if I have the correct tour operator. Pleased I went to cu chi but no point in going back again,  highlights some of the horrors of the war,  in a very pro communist way.  Lunch was good,  the mekong was interesting,  would go there again. Taken to several places where we were almost expected to pay for various foods and entertainment,  ( being asked for donations)I  would go again but on an all day mekong tour.

Chu chi tunnels trip is good, but don’t bother with the Mekong Delta, its just a sales trip, I usually travel independently and avoid this kind if rubbish that people seem to think that the tourists like.

In my humble opinion, the experience of going to the Cu Chi tunnels and then continuing towards the Mekong delta was one of the most overrated things you could do when you're at Ho Chi Minh.  The tour I went with also didn't really help.I remember we were picked up from our hotel and were crammed in a small minivan and had to wait long for the other tourists to arrive, which obviously wasn't the tour's fault but was definitely an inconvenience. We then went to the second hotel to pick up another group but they weren't there so we left without them, only for us having to return and pick them back up whilst we were already 5 minutes away. It also did not help that our driver was incapable of pressing on the brakes. Anyways, we had a pretty fun guide to be honest. His name was Locky and he was upbeat and told us facts and information about Vietnam's history. We, along with other tour buses, first stopped at a building in a small rural area halfway down the road to see a workshop where victims of agent orange sold their artwork. It was a nice place to grab souvenirs and appreciate their work before leaving and heading to our first stop, the Cu Chi tunnels.The Cu Chi tunnels were certainly interesting, but to be honest, it's not worth the hype. Yeah you can explore the tunnels the Viet Cong once crawled through to hide from the Americans and you can take cool pictures at the tunnel entrance, but other than that, that's it. Surrounding you is just a standard forest and the area around it is underdeveloped, much like the many rural areas I see back home in Indonesia. It's also a couple hours away from Ho Chi Minh in a crammed bus. There was also a shooting range there that was sooooo loud because a dozen people at a time would all be blasting at the same time. You could hear the sounds from the very beginning of the tour. I thought they were playing gunshots on the speaker to mimic a war setting but I thought wrong. Finally after eating lunch, we left for the Mekong Delta.Oh gosh, where do I even begin. It was a long 2 hour drive to the Mekong and once we arrived we entered a port where we could go on a boat towards the other side of the river. This other side was very uninteresting. It was just a small rural village area where they showed you how they made and sold very local products, visiting areas that are just generally uninteresting and quite unnecessary. They had no architectural or historical significance and was just a really standard village that I could find very similarly in my home country. I say "very" local products because it's literally just handmade products that are probably only sold in that area of Vietnam. The tour obviously only took us there to support the local businesses and the people. I understand that this is how these people make their living. Eventually we went on the boat ride through the "Mekong" like those tourists do with the traditional hats and stuff. Turns out, it was just a small stream that ran through the middle of the village that was crammed, dirty and brown and had nothing special going for it. Once we reached the end we would be pushed back out to the sea where we'd reunite with our boat and head back to the main land. This was probably the best part of my trip because it was me and my family calmly sailing through the Mekong while the sun set behind us.Finally, it was time to go back to our hotel after another +-2-hour bus ride. We came home exhausted but oh well. To conclude this review, I'm not saying you shouldn't go, I'm just not recommending it. Saigon is a beautiful city and honestly there's no reason to go out of it unless you got an extra day to spare.

I am glad I did the tour, just not what I was expecting. First we went to a workshop and were shown how the community made pictures vases, etc, out of shells.Then on to the tunnels, It felt abit rushed, we were shown around the  environment and did go down one tunnel. Then onto the documentary. Which I watched  however some how it didn't feel right. It didn't help me to understand the reason for the war, seemed like we were being told what to think.
luogo in mezzo alla foresta dove i vietcong avevano scavato tunnel sotterranei a tre livelli che in parte si possono visitare;molto impressionante.

Cu Chi Tunnel was a good place. But with advance booking as well the tour started late by more than 1 hour. The Bus was good and comfortable. Water was served instantly once we settled down.The guide was not that good with English and seeing the other guides with other groups at Cu Chi Tunnel they were more expressive and informative. Do not book with trip advisor rather I would suggest to check with some other platform.

The site is well-managed and one gets to understand what life was like there.  However, the introductory video that visitors are compelled to watch is pure propaganda.  Because of this presentation, it is nearly impossible to believe any of the other information presented.  The administration would serve themselves better by providing more accurate information, from which visitors could have a better understanding of the multitude of factors and players in the area.

The tunnels and displays are interesting, but I must admit it didn't live up to my expectations after what I had been told by friends who had been there before me.It was good to see how they were built and how people lived in them during the war.Also, the types of booby traps, how they were built and deployed. There were lots of tour groups there and as such some displays can get crowded.There is a live firing range, where you can fire the weapons used during the war, at a cost of US$3 a bullet, minimum of 10 bullets. The range should be further away from the rest of the displays, as the noise from the range tends to drown out the guides explanations of some displays.There is plenty of shade from the trees on a hot sunny day and Mosquito repellant is a must.

Had it not been for this part of the experience, I would have rated Cuchi tunnels higher. The transportation, facilities on site and our tour guide from Kim were very good - close to excellent.Then we went into the tunnels with a tunnel “guide” who quickly went ahead into the tunnels, very fast, way ahead of us and then exited. He didn’t bother to check if everyone was behind him, or leave any instructions as to where to turn. I got into a dead end and luckily there was someone behind me who told me to back out and turn right to get out. At this point it was no longer interesting or “fun”.    I suggest the guides go a lot slower and leave some  tine for people to catch up.  The experience ended up being needlessly stressful and a bit embarrassing. I would think twice before encouraging other people, especially older people to go into the tunnels.
Historically interesting but not an essential place to visit. Much propaganda. Good place to fire loud semi automatic weapons if that is your jam.

É uma experiência que tem que fazer em Ho Chi Minh, afinal a região tem muita história. Uma coisa muito legal foi aprendem como fazer o papel de arroz, totalmente inesperado! Sobre o lugar em si, acho que deveriam acabar com as atividades de tiro (mesmo sendo opcional), não mostra o devido respeito ao lugar, fora que é super perturbador escutar os tiros.
You can learne a lot aboout Vietnamese war and got to go through the narrow tunnels. Nice place to visit for half day from Ho Chi Minh. You can combine with Ho Chi Minh city tour

If you are in Ho Chi Minh this is a sight to do.  With 45 minutes from the center you are at the tunnels. Good option is to do this with private driver. Guided tour by him with sufficient explanation. Whether or not to crawl through piece of tunnel. Is a tourist attraction though. Short video at the end of the tour. Interesting to see images. You'll be done with an hour and a half.
We are a senior couple late 70 and we feel it is not worth to go to the tunnels  cannot get into them

We were somewhat disappointed by our visit here. We came without our own guide, and were told the place provides their own English guide but this was messy: we were told to wait somewhere for 15 minutes, then were told to wait another 10, and then they made us move to different locations until the guide arrives. The place tells the tragic history of the war, but it has become a mixture of propaganda and fun place (what's so fun about encouraging people to take pictures of themselves in traps that killed American soldiers? How appropriate is it to encourage people to shoot with real weapons (for extra payment, of course) in a place like this ?

Our half day tour was with Urban Adventures. You have an early start and stop for a comfort break at a ceramics workshop. However, in order to get back by say 1.30pm or 2pm (to make it a half day) you are rushed around with no time to pause and reflect and enjoy each exhibit. The whole place deserves a longer duration. It is the number 1 visitor attraction so that means the place is always packed out with people. In fact in front of some exhibits you are "moved on" so that the next group can approach. It spoilt the experience for me. At the end of the route there is a shooting gallery and the noise is extremely loud you cannot enjoy sitting in the cafe area.

We did this tour with KIM travel - To start, you get picked up and taken to this workshops a hour or so out of town run by people who have been affected by agent orange. You can buy the products they are making and they are all working very hard, the guides boast that they don’t use any machines to make these products and it all feels a bit weird as it’s all just tourists here.There’s also restrooms and a coffee place.After another hour you get to the tunnels and you are directed to a room to watch ‘old footage’ from the war of people in the tunnels but it’s very obvious it’s a reenactment. The voiceover talks a lot about how horrible the Americans are and what great jobs the Vietnamese did in killing them… it again, felt a bit strange to watch.You are shown some traps with spikes for Americans and the guides talk and joke about how people die and die again and it feels very insulting to be speaking and joking in this way… he even pointed a stick shaped as a gun at people and joked we were the Americans… very strange!And then the cafe! The worst part as it’s connected to a shooting range and you are told if you don’t want to shoot you can relax and enjoy some ice cream and food - that is until the shooting starts and terrifies everybody as it is incredibly loud and hurts your ears but you have no where else to go so you just have towait for your group to finish shooting! It’s really not a nice experience so bring noise cancelling headphones or earbuds! Gave me a migraine after Finally, after being shown more uniforms etc you get to the tunnels! This was the best part as it’s crazy how small they are! There is exits every 20m inside as it’s so claustrophobic and it gets quite dark at some points - don’t wear a backpack as you are crawling the whole way and the bag will scrape mud onto you from the ceiling! Also if you have knee issues, I wouldn’t go in! Then we finish with a snack of what they would’ve eaten in the war which was fineThe half day tour lasted from 8.30 pickup to 3.45pm drop off so bring snacks! It’s a long day!Get a private tour if you can, as there’s a lot of waiting for other people that really takes a while! Also the tour guide came down the bus and wrote everyone’s reviews for you on your phone and checked everyone’s phone for proof of a good review which is such a pet peeve for me as they don’t want you to say your honest experience!It was a decent trip and deffo interesting to experience but I’m not sure I would recommend this particular tour/ group

It was an eye opener seeing the tunnels and hearing how the Viet Cong operated during the war. I would give the tour 4 or 5 stars except that I found the guide Kim a bit strident. On the bus, she reeled off a long list of statistics about how Vietnam had suffered under the Americans, eg how many bombs the Americans had dropped, how many Vietnamese had suffered from Agent Orange etc. I'm sure all of it was true but it made the tour sound like an anti-American bashing campaign. We were later shown a series of grisly booby traps with spikes etc used by the Viet Cong to trap American soldiers, and she described the effects of each trap with great relish. Some of her jokes were a bit crude too, eg after falling into the booby trap, "no need to have children any more, have to go Thailand", and also a joke about farting in someone else's face when crawling through the tunnel because you bend over so much. It spoiled what would have been an emotional and profoundly moving tour. I think that any American whose loved ones died during the Vietnam war would have found this tour quite difficult. I deeply sympathise with the Vietnamese who suffered during the war, but nothing is black and white. There are many in Southeast Asia who were grateful that the spread of Communism was halted by the war, and there were also many young American soldiers and their families who suffered and wished they need not have gone to war.

A little overrated and it takes almost a full day. We arrived at our tour agency (vietnam adventure tours) at 8am as instructed and the bus left 5 minutes later. We spent the next 45 minutes driving around and picking up more passengers, making about 10 stops. And these stops were all within 5-10 minute walking distance to agency. After 45 minutes, we headed north out of town, driving right past where we started. It was Saturday but there was still traffic heading out of town. We stopped for about 20 minutes at a rest stop which was really aimed for us to buy souvenirs. We finally arrived at the tunnels 2:40 minutes after I first boarded the bus. The tour itself was ok but being in a large group (24 people) made the tour less enjoyable and slow. We spent 2.5 hours there,  including wasting 15 minutes on a horrible film. In a small group, the tour could probably only take 1-1.5 hours. The drive back was another 1.5 hours. In total, I spent 4.5 hours on bus and 2.5 hours at the site, which really should’ve been only 1.5 hours max.

We had booked the tour through a local travel agent. The bus full of tourists left from Disttrict 1 traffic signal with our energetic tour guide Ben narrating stories of Vietnam. On the way they stop at a workshop where handicapped atrisans make paintings, pots and nick-knacks. They are little expensive    but the horrific war stories that left them handicapped will urge you to pick up something, even if small. In the tunnel premises, the tour guide will walk you through the mine forests where you also get to experience life inside those tiny tunnels. Traps and deadly weapons occupy a major part of the exhibit. The tour ends with a visit to the museum and a shooting range where you can practice shooting for a price. Its expensive! Also, it not advisable to buy bullets as souvenirs for two reasons- -1- They are ages old. Hence risky. 2- They bullets are not allowed during custom clearance, even if you show them your purchase receipt. Carrying arms/bullets is illegal on flights. It was an exhausting half day trip. Though there was a lot of history to soak in, we didn't like the place much. May be it was because of the soaring temperature or perhaps were not prepared for the location.

Really eye opening experience visiting the tunnels. We were guided throughout and the tour guide was informative, though I feel that we spent too much time in certain areas (Like the video at the start) and then not enough time in other areas, including getting a refreshment in the really hot weather. That said, the tunnels were very claustrophobic but interesting to go in and see. I would recommend shooting the AK47 as well.
For me personally I wouldn't visit again but was an eye opener.   So sad and interesting.  We are indeed lucky.

The Kuchi tourist spot, which has a historical background and various mechanisms hidden away, is often like, "Heh!" That's what I'll say. If you are a good guide, you will be very satisfied with the many explanations. I don't personally like gunshots, so I kind of hate them.

Se hicieron kilómetros de túneles durante la guerra, sería interesante reabrir más de ellos, eso sí, son sumamente angostos e incómodos. Supongo que lo ideal sería hacer un túnel paralelo al auténtico adaptado para poder caminar erguido y desde el cual puedas ver el interior, especialmente las salas que sirvieron de hospitales, cuarteles, pozos, etc. Nos dieron la oportunidad de recorrer un túnel (el único en apariencia que podía recorrerse). Hay que ir de rodillas a no ser que estés en buena forma o seas un niñ@, lleva rodilleras, no cargues con mochilas (no caben bien) y ten agua para cuando termine el viaje porque vas a tener sed, de hecho era el lugar más húmedo que recuerdo de Vietnam. Me costó, pero pude terminarlo, (tiene salidas intermedias por si te cansas) para mi sorpresa era solo un túnel, no había nada y terminaba en una fortificación al aire libre.Restos de un antiguo carro de combate, algún agujero de las bombas arrojadas por los estadounidenses, una demostración de las tácticas del Vietcong y la oportunidad de disparar armas auténticas (yo usé un M-60), aunque a buen precio (a dólar la bala, máximo 10). Recuerden disparar tiro a tiro para evitar terminar la experiencia en unos segundos. Las armas están fijas, solo puedes girarla levemente arriba y abajo y muy unos treinta grados a los lados de manera que la atracción resulta segura. Vimos también en la misma excursión el templo Cao Dai, bonito, aunque algo pobre en comparación con las grandes pagodas.

Nous avons eu une visite un peu rapide, notre guide accompagnateur n'a pas pris le temps de nous expliquer les panneaux explicatifs des tunnels que l'on ne visite pas. Mais nous avons quand même apprécié l'expérience.

A very interesting tour, from Hcmc very long way. The guides speak very good English and really explain what life was like at the time of the American War, what the Vietnamese call it. It is really exciting to crawl through the caves yourself and imagine that someone dug these holes and could live at up to 8m depth. The guides explain very objectively how cleverly the Vietnamese have adapted and fought for their freedom. Great tour.
this place is very importance to visit for understanding the situation of the Viet-Kong persons \ soldiers at the war.
Man bekommt einen Einblick in den Vietnamkrieg. Interessant ist es in einen Tunnel hineinzugehen. ....
Yes, it is interesting to see the tunnels and hear stories of how they were lived in. But as a tourist attraction it wasn't really my thing! That doesn't mean it wouldn't be yours!

Mieliśmy szczęście trafić na polskich przewodników , naprawdę było ciekawie .Podróż około 90 min , wycieczka na 90 i powrót na 120 min - także trzeba liczyć pół dnia . Ciekawie opowiedziane z informacjami istotnymi dla Europejczyka .

One and half hours to get there from SaigonDid not come up to expectations Thought we would go into all three levels but only down the tunnel for about 5 minutes so disappointed The trip we were on also mentioned a forest walk but that was around the traps they had made for tourists to seeFilm at the start of the tour was informative
Afternoon visit, very busy, crowded and felt rushed. Hard to take it all in. Interesting properganda movie
Afternoon visit, very busy, crowded and felt rushed. Hard to take it all in. Interesting properganda movie
.... is hiding the truth or better, telling how it was from a highly curated point of view. Interesting to see VNAM struggle with its own identity based on a full review of its history

Grundsätzlich sehr interessant um die Geschichte des Krieges besser zu verstehen. Allerdings hat das Ganze etwas von Disneyworld. Vor allem wenn die Besucher auf dem erbeuteten amerikanischen Panzer herumlaufen und Fotos schießen. Und der integrierte Schießplatz ist schon sehr grenzwertig.

Avant d'arriver aux tunnels, on vous amènera dans une fabrique de polissage où des produits hors de prix vous seront offerts. Au début, vous serez tenté d'acheter, car vous aurez l'impression que c'est réellement fait par les gens de la place. Pourtant, quand nous sommes allés au marché extérieur de Ho Chi Minh dans le District 1, nous avons trouvé les articles identiques pour le 5e du prix. Nous avons été soulagés de ne finalement pas avoir acheté à la fabrique où on nous amène lors de l'excursion qu'est le Cu Chi Tunnels.Autrement, l'excursion est intéressante à visiter pour 2 à 3 heures. Il faut s'attendre à beaucoup de touristes. Cela en fait perdre légèrement l'attrait du lieu. L'excursion VIP peut être intéressante pour ne pas être trop coincés lors de la visite, mais non nécessaire à mon avis.Un 6/10 pour moi.
Look at tunnels, traps, eat and loose your hearing at the shooting range. Ok way to spend a day at Saigon.

I’m not sure what I was expecting, but this is a very average experience. As fascinating as the tunnels are, the whole thing is very poorly presented, with low quality films and models.Came away feeling we wish we hadn’t bothered.
I do not like it so much. Because it not very specially than i thought. The services in here need to improve than now

Les guides sont super, plein d’humour et de reparti, heureusement nous y sommes allé dans une période creuse donc on était un groupe de 7 Max sans autre groupe autour de nous. Fin de visite, on se dirige vers le stand de tir. Très décevant, des tarifs hors de prix un garde qui te charge les balles dans le mag et puis tu te demerdes, les armes sont déjà attachés, bref aucune expérience. Juste du tir pour du tir, très décevant.

Il luogo è sicuramentenda tra i siti da vedere a Ho chi min .I 3 pallini perché all'ansia, alla tristezza ed al dolore che si provano a visitare il posto, allo scendere in quei tunnel ( attenzione a chi soffre di claustrofobia e se si è troppo alti ), alla sola idea di come abbiano potuto vivere in quelle condizioni persone come noi, si contrappone il valzer del turismo esagerato   con gente che sgomita per salire su un carro armato o peggio spara al poligono di tiro presente nel circuito della visita. Tanta violenza poteva avere un epilogo più rispettoso. Comunque sito da vedere ...con occhi e pensieri rivolti a chi lì ci è stato costretto a vivere.
good visit however it's very touristic and there is not a lot left. too bad we couldn't connect with the elders who could tell the story from the past
We did this as part of a day tour package, it's ok, my husband really liked it.  Be prepared for a 2 hour drive there and back if your hotel is the center of the city.

We were really looking forward to the tour of the tunnels, and perhaps we were naive, but the experience was pretty mediocre. We thought we would be able to tour extensively underground, however, we were limited to a 20 m crawl through part of a tunnel. The historical aspect of the site was fascinating, but we were really put off by our guide. She had been witty and charming on the bus, but once we were there, she really toed the party line and gloated repeatedly about how stupid the American soldiers had been, and how clever the VC were.

unfassbar. unbedingt anschauen. ist auch mal nett, aus dem Großstadt jungel in den echten zu kommen...propaganda ist auch etwas, was das Land und seine Kultur beschreibt, das wird einem schnell klar, wenn man den infofilm sieht. super interessant, einzigartig in der Welt

We did a half day afternoon tour and we're not very impressed. While the tunnels itself and being inside the tunnels is a great experience, we lacked the history and knowledge behind it. The tour guide, a veteran, was not comfortable talking about the war or much of the history of the tunnels. Instead, we spent most of the tour waiting around in souvenir shops which felt like a waste. The actual reason to come, the tunnels, was only times for like 10 minutes.If possible, I could highly recommend to get a private tansfer and make a day trip of it as I am sure there is tons to learn d see

fascinating insight to the Vietnam war from the Viet Cong point of view. Obviously set up for tourists, so the real tunnels are inaccessible to us, but we are able to get a glimpse of how life was lived during this turbulent time. Did not feel overly crowded,  even though the tourist buses kept coming

I wasnt too keen on this but went along anyway as it was included in the tour. Besides all the displays of weapons, tunnels, torture weapons etc there is also a firing range where you can shoot a machine gun/AK47 if you wish though its not cheap. You can have a go at crawling in one of the tunnels if you fancy. But be warned it is a frightening experience. We tried the 20m tunnels and it scared us to death; we are not very good in enclosed spaces.

Cu Chi tunnels is a museum. You get to see all you expect and may even try to fire a kalasjnikov. Bundles of tourist make the Place overcrowded. If you wanna see the real tunnels 15 km away order a private tour.
The tour is very canned, but it is interesting to see the area and learn about the history of the tunnels.

It's basically a war museum. They offer visitors a sneak-peek at the underground life of Vietnamese soldiers during the World War. They also have a shooting range and visitor have to pay for this activity. Visitor can choose weapon type such as M16 with minimum of 10 bullets.

On balance I think this is worth doing. It's very crowded with tourists and the tours move you through pretty quickly. We had about 1hr15m on site for about 4+ hours of travel. Stumbling through the tunnels is a good experience though.
I expected a little bit more of this trip. I mean it was good to see and get some facts. But our guide was a bit crazy and refused to talk about the war even though we were eager to learn more.

As with many of the visitor experiences in Cambodia and Vietnam it looks like nobody spent much money on the place for years.Gets very busy. Worth visiting just to understand what combat troops were up against.Be aware that the shooting range where you can fire live ammo is right next to the visitors shop and cafe!
細長いトンネルを、身体を、屈めて進んで行きますが、何せ暑いので、熱中症みたいな感じで、途中でリタイアしました、近くにある実弾を、撃てる射撃場で、拳銃を、撃って来ましたが、楽しいですよ。まあ、近くの的さえ当たりませんが😰😰😰

Quite brief visit of the tunnels with introduction of when and how were tunnels used, part of a visit is a shooting range where you can buy bullets (10 is minimum) to shot from various weapons.Visit in a delta with lunch and small boat ride in the delta with visit of coconut sweets factory, and traditional sing.

Sehr gut gemacht und dargestellt. Man kann sich danach in etwa vorstellen, wie es damals war.Fürchterlich finde ich die Möglichkeit sinnlos mit echter Munition in der Gegend rumzuballern, was man auf dem ganzen Gelände permanent hört. Was das soll erschließt sich mir überhaupt nicht. Ist dort nicht genug geschossen worden?

Testimonianza di un periodo tragico di guerra e sofferenza. Non indicato per persone sensibili. I cunicoli sono stretti e  impressionanti non adatti a persone di corporatura robusta e o che soffrono di claustrofobia.

This was interesting enough but not great.  Perhaps it didn't live up to my expectations because of the hype.First off this is a POPULAR spot.  Get here early or be part of a massive crowd.The tunnels are interesting in a historical sense but they have been changed to accommodate western tourists (you can read that as fat Americans).   So yes - you can go into them but you're not actually experiencing what the Viet Cong did.Nonetheless there are some interesting displays that are worth visiting and I'm glad we went.
book a tour that leaves before 7am. Bring snacks and water. If you go later in the day the tunnels will be VERY hot.
I could have done without this stop on our tour.It was extremely crowded which made it difficult to hear our tour leader and there mobs around each exhibit.

We came late morning in this place. We bought our tickets from the counter. We wanted to roamed freely and tour ourselves around. While planning which place to go first, the tour guide appeared. He offered to guide us all. Well, good enough we went around. Happy that tour guide can speak English. We had a great time learning.
It’s an interesting place to visit if one is in the area. I wouldn’t come to Saigon for just this. It’s an iconic place. I’m glad I visited. I wasn’t overwhelmed.

Onze veelbelovende verwachtingen werden slechts deels ingelost.Tijdens het bezoek kon ik mij niet van de indruk ontdoen dat alles nogal "gemaakt" overkwam.De opgezette locaties met de uitgebeelde figuren en tentoongestelde voorwerpen die af en toe elektrisch met een druk op de knop in werking gezet werden was misschien iets te veel gekunsteld.Het echte oorlogsverleden was uiteindelijk zoveel meer ingrijpender geweest, zonder al te veel artificieel gedoe errond.

Did a tour of,the Cu Chi Tunnels and were very disappointed. This tour seemed like a DisneyLand ride than the very serious subject it deals with. Was expecting a highlight of our trip, but instead it was very forgettable.

From Ho Chi Minh City, there are a lot of recommendations for selling this trip in the vicinity, if you are not in English and strongly recommend that you can participate in a Chinese tour is more suitable, but the Chinese tour fee is much higher than in English, but without Chinese, all the explanations will lose meaning, the trip will end with potatoes to taste, and a drink to welcome the local tea has a special aroma, and I also think that the only advantage is

Much if these tunnels have either been destroyed or aren't open. There is a small stretch you can walk through but if you are taller than 5 feet, you will need to have good cardio. It's not a long tunnel, however you will need to squat and bend you back (think duck walk) to walk through if you are average Western height. If you are around 5ft, you can walk normally with your legs but just bend your back so it's slightly easier

I am giving it an average rating because it is a nice place but the guides just are so rushed and take you from place to place so fast you do not enjoy anything.  The tunnels were tight due to the fact Vietnamese people have a small frame so some people in our group could not go through the tunnels.

Took a private tour, we did not stay long. The tunnels are very small/narrow. Im 5’7 and slim built and I did not feel comfortable going through the tunnels so that cut out a huge chunk of the tour. Its an interesting place with lots of history. Was not a fan of the shooting range on site, it made for nice sound effects while touring the property but thats about it. It was a long-ish drive from where we were staying in HCM so if not on your bucket list, think twice.

Visited this without any expectations and that was a good thing. This is a popular attraction so it will be busy with tourists and as such there will be several gift shops. Located in a small forest near the river bug spray is a must. Displays are rather boring and un-engaging, but if nothing else it is worth visiting for the small forest and a glimpse into everyday life outside of the cbd.

The half day trip was interesting but honestly it was a rush . Perhaps 4 or 5 hours would have been better to experience the tour better. What was also bad was the number of tour groups getting into each other's way such that the guide had to rush and move on to another site in the tour. But overall, there's so much to understand about the complexity of the Vietnam War.

We visited here today and although the place is well preserved, there is no information available to read and the place is packed full of tour groups. We heard gun shots from arriving and assumed it was a tape playing to set the scene. There is actually a shooting range on the site and it is real gunshots you can hear. I thought this was very inappropriate and distasteful. The actual tunnel was also so packed so not only are you crawling but you cannot breathe because there are so many people in there.

Just realize that you will be informed of the war from the Vietnamese side; which can be a bit difficult to accept the suffering our US troops went through with horrible booby traps, etc...Glad that this history has been preserved though as people of both sides need to see how devastating war is for everyone.

side trip for this visit to the tunnel, a bit far and long drive. Guide was good but the story of this tunnel is somewhat heart breaking.  i dont recommend it to peoeple with claustrophobia as i myself didnt want to enter.  it was ok but not great though.

Interesting tour but a long way to travel too see.  The guide was very knowledgeable and some volunteers even got to enter the tunnels. Ensure you were enclosed footwear as there are biting ants that can cause a reaction.There is a cafe onsite so plenty of refreshments.

Ya que vas vietnam que menos, un poco de cultura. Por menos de 4€ te llevan desde saigon en autobus a lo turista y te ponen un video y un recorrido por diferentes actividades en los tuneles trampas etc.

We weren’t blown away with the experience but it is very interesting from a war history perspective and the guide was friendly enough if not slightly patronising in his demeanour.My girlfriend went through the tunnels but it’s scary I have to say and most thought so, my girlfriend is very small and fearless and she said even she panicked in parts that were pitch black but nobody makes you go through I guess. We shot the Ak47 and the M16 which kept getting dud rounds taking a while to flick through them until we eventual would hit a live round. None of the people at the gun range part speak English.I would recommend if your a history buff but don’t get to excited, you see a couple of tunnels, some cool traps and then making sandals. You have to pay extra to fire guns and it’s not cheap.
Very much tuned to the tourist rather than a true life experience. Reading about the tunnels, seeing the onsite video is better than the experience itself.
Die Anfahrt aus Ho CHI Minh Stadt ist etwa 2 Stunden lang. Dafür gibt es nicht allzu viel zu sehen. Ich würde alternativ eher eine Stadtführung empfehlen.
Das Tunnelsystem des Vietcong ist mitlerweile ziemlich bekannt. In wieweit die Darstellungen reell o. eher publikumswirksam inszeniert kann ich nicht beurteilen. Ein Besuch lont sich allemal.
Went there to break a full week of meetings with colleagues, did the intent. Went with a boat, was a bit long, however the tour was pleasant.

Nice information leaflet and some good info panels BUT this was pretty crowded in early in the morning and it was difficult to se some of the exhibits. Worth a visit if you have the time but reconciliation palace and walking in Saigon are  better options
Leider gab es von unserem Tourguide wenig Geschichte. Rein die einzelnen Orte wurden präsentiert und kurz etwas dazu gesagt. Vor Ort kann man schießen- wenn man möchte.

ホーチミンの市内から少し離れた場所にあるベトナム戦争時にベトナム人によって作られたトンネルで、いろいろな場所につながっているため、戦争時に欧米兵にとってはどこからベトナム兵が出てきて攻撃されるか分からず、苦戦したようです。またトンネル自体がベトナム人サイズで掘られているため、欧米兵が中に侵入したとしても、非常に動きずらかったようです。私もトンネル内を移動しましたが、閉所恐怖症の人にはおすすめできない箇所がありました。
Cu Chi tunnels give insight into the way Viet Cong survived bombs and attacks during the war.  Very one sided account of what happened so many years ago.

I went there with some friends and although the historical aspect is very interesting and we learnt a lot about how people lived in the tunnels during the American war, some of us didn’t enjoy the visit if the actual tunnels. They are extremely narrow and that’s after they’ve been widened for tourists!There’s a shooting range that I found of poor taste.The local guides are lovely and knowledgeable though, it’s worth tipping them.

Recomiendo coger la excursión compartida con el delta (rio). No creáis que vais a estar horas por los túneles, solo unos minutos, por lo que para que rellenen tiempo con vídeos , mejor aprovechar y coger la excursión de medio día o compartida con algo mas.

Through the full-day tour of the <구치 터널 및 메콩 델타>, I wanted to experience the Gucci Tunnel, which was the base of Vietnam’s military activity during the Vietnam War, and see the living nature of the Mekong Delta of the Mekong River that flows through Myanmar, Thailand, Laos, Cambodia and Vietnam.   The Gucci Tunnel helped to understand the realities of the time, where the Mekong Delta was a five-minute narrow waterway on a small boat and most of the course was a visit to the store for sale.  Showing tourists the peculiarities of the Mekong Delta in Vietnam, thinking of tourists as merely a target for money, is an act of blurring the tourist image of Vietnam.

We made in 1 whole day the visit to the tunnels and the river and the truth is that it is plenty. In the tunnels you can see enough to get to the idea, I don't think it's so extensive to throw the day.Watch out for the excursion you hire, because in all of them they make stops for you to buy. Tell you how many stops there will be.

As a keen Vietnam war historian I was a tad disappointed at the lack of things to see and do at the site.Everything seemed commercial and rushed.I got more out of visiting the war museum in Ho Chi Minh.Shooting the m16s and Ak47s extremely expensive at nearly $4 AU per bullet Minimum 10 rounds
Was underwhelmed.  Long rise to get there and not a great deal to see upon arriving.  If you are not familiar with the use of tunnels by the VC it may be of some interest.
It is interesting to see this place but not a must. I especially liked going down the tunnels but you need to be a bit small to get in

Nice adventure, good time and a lot of information about the war time and Vietnam struggle during the war and the massacre which happened there. WAR is just a business for the businessman and the looser is only the poor soldiers  and the society, STOP the war ALL over the WORLD. peace and love should be spread

We were able to understand many things about the vietnam war and the strategies used by them.One point to improve,  We would have enjoyed more if we had more time. Apart from time constraint  it was an very good experience

The scale and extent of the tunnels during the war is impressive and how the war was fought was interesting. However, the tunnels itself are just holes in the ground. Definately join a guide to get in depth details and history. The site it self will take you less than 20 minutes to see everything by yourself.

It was an eye opening experience. There were a couple of tunnels that you can through, which is sufficient as it can be very claustrophobic. The place gives you an overview of how the Vietnamese used their ingenuity to somehow tricked the American military. The tunnels and hiding spots were so tailor made to Vietnamese body type that an average American man will not be able to fit in. While the displays of booby traps are all sanitized, it leaves you an imagination of how gruesome and barbaric the results were.

Was here a few days ago and had a great guide Tanh, tunnels were surprisingly immaculate. They have a gun range near to the cafe and market stalls, optional to go. Unfortunately the gun range was right next to the cafe and made me jump after a shot was fired. Too loud and close to a busy tourist location.

If you're a history buff you'll love it. Half day tour is ideal for people who aren't into the whole history thing but just want to see it to check it off the list. I was very claustrophobic in the tunnel so if you're not one to like close spaces, stay out of the tunnel!

We had a funny guide, Johnny who took us to a Handicapped Handicrafts factory that wasn’t on our itinerary. Was he doing this for a slice of the profits from our purchases? Was it a tourist trap? Or does the money really go to the disabled. We were told about agent orange and made to feel guilty before we got the the factory then we bought loads of products (which we found at the local markets in Ho Chi Minh City for 10X less!). This is fine as long as the government or people running the factory are not corrupt. Next we were made to watch communist propaganda/a video on how the Viet Cong won the “American War”. Really it’s a war between the north and south of Vietnam that was aided by international allies.Cu Chi Tunnels were good but the live ammunition that tourists are allowed to buy for $28US is terrifying and very much upset me. They have a gift shop where the rifle range is in case you feel like shopping to the sounds of live rounds of ammo ringing in your ears. In some ways having the ammo really makes the war seem more real and present moment and what it may have felt like to be there. So, it was positive and negative.

Cu chi tunnellerne er en destination man ikke må gå glip at, da de er en vigtig del af vietnamkrigen.Men der er ikke vildt meget at se, og der kan være mange grupper på samme tid, så det kan også blive temmelig proppet ved nogle af seværdighederne ved cu chi tunnellerne.

The tour guide spoke English clearly understandable to all of us. He was quite helpful pointing interesting places and the historical context. The vehicle was quite modern and clean. Pick up and drop off was on time.

We were a bit disappointed, couldn’t really get in and have a good look around because of all the tourists. Also it’s a bit corny with the fake Viet cong mannequins and bogans shooting guns at a ridiculously high price. We visited Vinh Moc tunnels up north and they were 100% better, not so crowded and you can stand up in those.
the tunnels were very tight i could barely fit through but im not a small viet, i need to have 100 characters
Its okay if you want some guy to tell you how many westerners they killed and how...Its crowded , kids would love it , I didn't

Tons of interesting information but the actual tunnels that you get to check out are super limited. We got a private tour guide which was nice.  Lots of the information would have been missed if we went on our own,

This place is worth visiting once, but I must say it felt like the most touristy place I visited.Also, they REALLY need to update the audio naration on their documentary video, s I could hardly understand it.

Whilst we were in awe of the extent of the tunnels and the way they assisted in the Vietnam war we were a little put off by the propaganda continually thrust upon us by the guide. Amazing how people lived and worked in these conditions and surroundings. The area has been managed well so that visitors can gain some understanding of living in the tunnels whilst keeping the integrity of how it must have been during the war.

ne vale la pena fare 6 ore di traffico intenso per vedere questo sito turistico? avete voglia di vedere come combattevano questi soldati, vi piacerebbe sparare nel loro poligono? se vi piace andateci e non rimarrete delusi
Tour guide was a lot of fun and knowledgeable.  Overall good trip, interesting to see and go through tunnels
Es una visita(obligada) si estas en Ho Chi Minh, es medio día de tour.Se tarda mas en ir y volver que lo que estas en los túneles, pero es historia.
Had a look at this during a hot July - but was not sold on narrowing and hot tunnels so was happy to pass. Probably better to do this very early before the temperature starts to climb.
Not worth travelling especially to see, unless already in the area. You can crawl through 100m of enlarged tunnels which was interesting and gives you idea of how it is inside real tunnels.

Embora seja a visita mais típica para quem vai a Ho Chi Minh, a visita aos túneis só vale a pena se você tem tempo sobrando em Ho Chi Minh (3 dias de turismo na cidade ou mais) ou se está disposto a gastar um bom dinheiro (600.000 vnd) para atirar 10 balas com uma M16, Ak47, ou outras. Se esse não é seu caso: não vá. A visita de menos de 2h não compensa as 5h de deslocamento. Os tuneis não tem nada demais e as explicações podem ser encontradas de forma mais completa no museu da guerra

Where I highly rate our tour guide and her knowledge of the displays, the tunnel experience is only average. For all the time we spent here, I expected more than a 20m tunnel experience that was nothing more than walking through a dark nothing. Above ground you will find an American tank stripped if everything worth anything. So much potential here. A real underground working scenario whole or cutaway, showing traps, kitchens, living, medical etc even to European scale would encourage me to go back, and with the number of tourists going through its got to be doable. Highly under utilised as it stands. BTW if you want to use the bathroom...... Don't! 😂
과거 전쟁에 쓰였던 땅굴을 구경하고 비디오감상하고 여러구경하면서 와닿는것은 굉장히 많았습니다. 다만, 한국에서 군에 갔다온 남성분들이라면 한번씩 봤던 것들이 여서 1~2시간 왕복 4시간 버스를 타면서 가볼만한 곳은 아니었습니다. 거기서 ak47쏴본 경험은 재밌었습니다.
Interesting to see tunnels and arrtifacts are displayed.It is quite a  distance - about two hours - from Ho Chi Minh City.

The tour had me just a bit engaged and interested.  Be ready to endure the heat and humidity of the area.  Peter, our guide, had a pretty heavy accent which made it hard for me to grasp the historical information regarding the tunnels.  Oh...the mosquitoes too, be sure to wear repellent.

So you can’t to go Saigon without saying you been here. Is what they have created mind blowing, yes. What you get to see is a small fraction. We took the boat there to avoid traffic, but kore expensive, but nice trip. I honestly didn’t think it was that much of an experience.

Great when appreciating the ingenuity of the Vietnamese people. Was a great insight into their struggles and their way of life during the war. However felt the shooting range was touristic and distasteful

Krátká prohlídka , zastaralé expozice a video na začátku prohlídky. Určitě ale stojí za navštívení a pripomenuti si války ve Vietnamu. Doporučujeme příjezd lokál bus em za 20 Kč na jednoho . Počítejte s cestou ze Saigonu okolo 3 hodin.

Mycket intressant och lärorikt och få se den andra sidan av Vietnamkriget/Amerikanska kriget. Dock är infinner sig inte den genuina känslan när man besöker anläggningen, då mycket är uppbygg och anpassat enkom för massturismen.
Los túneles son muy cortos, esperaba más, me llevé una decepción. Aparte de que fuimos con una excursión que compramos que fue un timo 2.30 horas para llegar, para solo estar 10 min en los túneles.

Visited Cu Chi tunnels with Vietnam adventure tours. The tunnels were fascinating and I would highly recommend visiting them. However, there is a questionable stop along the way which I do not agree with. Also, while our tour guide Mr Cung was good, at the end of the tour he came around to every person, asked them to log on to trip advisor and waited while they wrote him a 5 star review. He then checked that they had been posted. I think this is unacceptable - trip advisor is meant to be an unbiased opinion but if you are requesting 5 star reviews from people how does this work? I was not impressed by this aspect of the tour.

Disappointed about the Cu Chi Tunnels, we were herded from one display to the next without every really getting a true sense of it's history.  The tunnels themselves are really small and worth crawling into, we only made it to the 20 metre mark but glad we made an attempt.
ben duoc is original cu chi tunnels, used at the time of war. but they have made tunnels for tourists at ben dinh, these were not used for war and tourists are fooled by visiting here.

Had to visit the tunnels, but it is quite a drive, 1 hr 30 mins from downtown and then purpose built and small. Not sure what I was expecting, but the travel time made it not really worth the trip, for me.

A must visit location in Ho Chi Minh City. Ideally go in a small group with a guide as otherwise it could be quite difficult to explore the area. An amazing experience to know the history of Vietnam war.

Tip: Stay away from the tourists who might fart in the tunnel...and please refrain from eating the free potatoes too. You may end up farting so bad in the tunnel.Dont try the trap as i see people getting stuck in the demonstration.
Something to learn about Vietnam war.  The tunnels are very narrow, but have to admire the guts and courage these people took  to fight their cause.
Went there on a planned trip for half a day and it could have been done in half that. Interesting but too drawn out and a very biased version of events
For me it was pilgrimage. My uncle was a French soldier in Vietnam and I have seen were a part of his life was spent.
I especially liked the place. The idea of what happened here 50 years ago. The tunnels themselves have been adapted for tourists and no longer feel authentic. Go early, there are many tourists.
ツアーで行った際に実際にトンネル内に入ることができます。たしか3つの長さを選べて最長で120mのものもありますがおすすめしません。最短でも狭く暑く息苦しいので出たあと皆汗だくでバテてます笑
Worth the price but quite far from the city so make sure you haggle a good price with the taxi driver. We managed to get a return trip for 800k VND

Transportation was good and our guide Huy was fun. The tunnels themselves has been enlarged and coated with cement - so it was not a true experience of the war. The demos and areas outside the tunnels were interesting. The shooting range was expensive at $3 a bullet (minimum of 10)

We took this tour to the Cu Chi tunnels with Les Rives. Speedboat from Saigon and got there quite early.Our guide was concerned with beating the crowds, so felt like he was hurrying us along quite a bit.It was an interesting site to see and we got to crawl through one of the tunnels, but the propaganda video was pretty lame.The guide was knowledgeable and informative but felt you really didn’t get to experience anything of what it would’ve been like.Followed by the obligatory lunch (with too much food) before hopping on the boat back to Saigon.Your choice.

There are two sites for the Cu Chi tunnels. One more interesting than the other. If you have the time take the longest. Otherwise interesting discovery but soulless. The stupid sounds of the shooting range do not participate in a nice discovery. To do with a guide otherwise risks quite a lot to have the feeling of wasting his time.
ngày xưa đi chiu cái đường hầm nghe nó rộng rãi, sau mấy chục đi lại thì chiu vào cứ như là chui vào cái bao nilon vậy, vừa chật, vừa nghẹt như là đi tra tấn vậy, chỉ 1 từ sợ thôi :(
ベトナムの歴史に触れられる貴重な場所。歴史に触れられる場所は刺激の強すぎる場所もありますが、こちらは低刺激です。ツアーで行った方がいろいろ便利だと思います。

If you are looking for something fun to do in 1 day during your short stay in HCMc, check out this historical spot for experience. Amazed to see and hear about how people could survive in such condition. Not much to say about story as it is controversial.60 kilometers North of Ben Thanh market D1. You can get there by motorbike, bus or taxi in around an hour.
戦争の歴史的なものを感じる良いスポットだと思います。有料ですが射撃場でライフルを打てます。3000円で10発だったと思います。耳栓があると現地にある消音のヘッドホンみたいなものにピッタリです。わたしはAK15をチョイスしましたが他にも種類があります。硝煙があるので綺麗すぎる服は避けた方が良いと思います。9月でしたが死ぬほど暑かった。
Interesting but as over reviews firing of the guns seems to be taking over. Not good for people with mobility problems not many seats around the site.

Huge tourist attraction..even offer opportunity to fire bullets at one point   for a small fee. Provides a visual of the size of the holes they managed to survive in and the squalor conditions. Also you view the man made contraptions they used to trap and and kill soldiers. Each person will interpret all of this very differently.

A interesting trip to see the tunnels and experience the first hand what the conditions would have been like. The key to this was the knowledge, humour and personality of Vu our guide from Indochina.The actual site and presentations could do with a brush up, the rifle range too loud and the options for food and drink was somewhat plain.Also the time of year we visited was very hot and humid.
