A long trip to get there but interesting tourism site. Displays the amazing initiative determination and resilience of the Vietnamese
It's a great place even if it is a tourist trap, but like many other people, I didn't enjoy my tour either. My tour guide was talking so fast and we were moving so fast that it was hard to enjoy it.

Whilst the tunnels are impressive, I was disappointed that there wasn’t more to read on the actual life of the people who lived there. Even a video re-enacting life in the tunnels would give visitors to this place a fairly good indication of how the people lived. Everything seemed a little disjointed. I would recommend that people visit the tunnels but please go with a reputable tour company or take private tour guide who can share more historic facts and other bits of information that you won’t find in history books.
Turisti paikka. Mutta siitä huolimatta käynnin arvoinen. Lopuksi pääsee kömpimään tunnerihin jos uskaltas ja pystyy.
Touristy but interesting, really just a history of the tunnels and a few examples and re-enactments.

My husband enjoyed this more than I did he did serve as a National Serviceman in Australia but he would not go again as he felt it had been altered to make it more commercial but this is just our opinion. In truth, I thought it was quite well done if you are interested and it did give an insight into how the war was won!
I'm not crazy about the whole experience.. It's a sad story how people kill etch other..  how they survive under difficult circumstances etc.. and a bit propaganda..
Site intéressant pour ceux qui aiment les vestiges de la guerre.  Ils montrent bien la détermination des gens à se protéger

Me dio la sensación de estar viviendo una recreación desde el principio.Se entra por un túnel que parece la entrada del metro.Nada más llegar nos pusieron un vídeo de propaganda Comunista de la época con un vocabulario políticamente incorrecto como establece a los soldados del Viet Cong como soldados valientes aniquiladores de Yankis. Me pregunto si a los turistas estadounidenses se lo ponen igual.Es interesante pero, no dejas de tener la sensación de ser una recreación, Un pasatiempo para turistas.

We thought the Cu Chi (koo-chi) tunnels would be better. Don't get us wrong - urban adventures and our guide Linda were 5 star fantastic and its only because of their extra experiences that we feel this trip is worth it. The current tunnels aren't the original and you can tell they have been perfectly constructed. The video was hard to understand and was extremely biased toward how many americans were killed by vietnamese heroes. The traps were interesting and confronting (they wouldnt tickle) and to climb through the tunnels was an experience but we didn't get any sense of atmosphere. This was the region where a war occurred and we couldn't visualise it at all. Note: of course you have to go and experience it, no way would we avoid it but don't get your hopes up like we did for a life changing experience.

I flound this experience to be extremely confronting and the commerntary was totally biased. Definitely a case of history is written by the winners. The Video had poor sound and vision and was very difficult to catch in the outdoor setting. The commentary showed no respect for the enemy. The live speaker at the video was also softly spoken and difficult to understand. The tunnels demonstrated clearly the amazing ingenuity of the local people to the challenge they faced. However, the samples of the traps used to ensnare enemy soldiers were brutal and showed that we do not want war in our lives anymore, particularly guerrila warfare. I found the provision of the rifle range to be  totally inappropriate in the solemnity of the area.
It was interesting and fascinating on how they built the tunnels. The ventilation system..cooking sleeping..booby traps

Having seen various Vietnam war themed films and documentaries, often from the point of view of the US, it has been quite educational seeing the war from the side of the North. The tunnels were always a fascinating and ingenious element and the the horrific booby traps very disturbing. This site allows you to get some taste of what it was like. Admittedly the tunnels are for the most part holes now and a walkabout involves seeing various examples. The section detailing the horrific traps set for the US soldiers was very uncomfortable and to see them demonstrated added to the disturbing atmosphere.After this there is a chance to go through a small section of a tunnel. I avoided it being claustrophobic but others said it wasn’t too bad. Apparently the tunnel is larger than it was for the smaller Vietnamese soldiers of the time.There are a few areas showing a hospital, kitchen and command centre for meetings which are covered by a roof made of dried leaves and dotted about there are weapons on display and other exhibits. A shooting area which allows people to fire actual weapons from the period for an additional cost is close by, as well as the usual gift and food areas. The jungle areas with the holes isn’t that engaging but there is enough to read and hear from your guide to make it interesting and the other elements including rice paper and wine making add value.

Actually....it was waste of time....at least for me that I am 190 cm tall....so I just move some steps down...if someone is not over 170 cm...and not clistofobic...go and see it...after all that was the way that the appear everywhere in the jungle. ...
This was an interesting sightseeing tour, took around one hour to reach from our hotel. Take bug spray as got a few bites.

Es un buen rato de coche o autobús desde Ho Chi Min City (unas dos horas dependiendo del tráfico). Hay cosas interesantes relacionadas con actividad del Vietcong en esta zona pero está sobreexplotado, muuuuchos turistas por todos los lados. Lo mejor el guía que nos tocó (Luc) excelente.

Our tour of the Cu Chi Tunnels was part of our tour. I can’t say that I would have gone out of my way to visit the site on my own. Too many individuals died there. The displays were informative and interesting for anyone interested in military displays. Don’t forget your bug spray and wear a hat.
Interesting and worth a visit but don’t expect to go in the tunnels unless you are fit and flexible.

Hatten über Deluxe Tours gebucht. Anfahrt dauerte ca. 1 1/2 Std. Vor Ort hatte man die komplette Geschichte an diesem Ort kennenlernen dürfen. Mit jeweils kleinen Stationen, wie z. B. ein kleiner Panzer (schon bisschen zerfallen), ein Schießstand (fanden wir jetzt nicht so gut), einzelne Häuschen/Bunker und natürlich die Tunnel. Diese haben wir uns spektakulärer vorgestellt. Ein kleiner, wirklich kleiner enger Tunnel ... Platzangst sollte man nicht haben! Es gibt drei Ausgänge, einmal in 40m, 70m und 100m. Man muss gebückt in der Hocke „durchlaufen“. Ist kein muss! Wir dachten es geht weiter in die Tiefe und größer das Tunnelkomplex. Aber das wars schon.

We did went to the Cu Chi tunnels in a group tour ( please see my other review in regards to tour company) We found the Tunnels to be very interesting historically. Our guide made it into a real history lesson and told us all there was to know about the location, people and terrain. ​The atmosphere at the tunnels is very cattle car, but this is due to it being so popular. ​If you take the time to read the boards and listen to the talks you will learn lot.​​Going into the tunnel isn't for everyone, but is an experience , there are exits all along so you can get out quickly if you need to. The tunnels have been widened and lights installed for the bigger tourists, still a tight squeeze.​​When you go near the firing range it is loud so be prepared. My husband and i chose to fire some weapons, it is expensive (1usd a bullet) but it's a once off experience. ​​Overall the Cu Chi tunnels is an important part of Vietnamese history. If you visits please be respectful..

The tunnels were very interesting to see and the staff were friendly and helpful. However it was a long drive from Saigon city, also there was a short video about the war which was very old and not clear

I felt that approx. 2 hours of bus drive (one way) is a bit too long for a 90 minutes stay at the museum like sight. The information shared about the tunnels by our tour guide was valueable and interesting. However not nearly as exciting as other day trips we booked in Saigon City. At the end it’s holes in the ground, with a bunch of traps and a - to me - unnecessary shooting range. Would not go again, time can be better spent elsewhere.
ホーチミンシティから半日ツアーを利用して行きました。バスで片道約2時間。現地ではビデオをみて当時の模様を知ることもできました。ベトナム戦争を知る良いきっかけにもなります。

The experience on a whole was very interesting. The history behind these tunnels and area was a real eye opener. The tunnels themselves a bit hard to do if you have a bad back as you are bent over the whole time. BEWARE THOUGH OF SOME OF THE SOUVENIRS THEY SELL IN THE SHOP AS YOU CANNOT LEAVE THE COUNTRY WITH THEM. I bought some empty bullet shells made into keyring (no primer or anything dangerous in them) and they were printed down the side with cu Chi tunnels and price tag on them and damn Ho Chi Minh Airport took off me

Before you book this tour you need to know what to expect. Ok so I thought it would be educational for me to bring my two high school aged girls to see this to maybe aid in their understanding of the Vietnam war. After arriving at the tunnels location we were seated to watch 20 minute video that was made in 1967. It was a propaganda video about the Viet Cong efforts during the war. That was to be expected. Unfortunately the entire 20 minutes was about the horrible Americans and the saintly locals. Yes I know that I should expect some of this but it was VERY offensive to me and my family. No mention of the civil war, no mention of all the Vietnamese killed, just glorification of killing Americans and the many ways they would do it. Also if you are at all claustrophobic do do this. Just a fair warning.
Sehr interessante Geschichte über den Wiederstand und das Überleben in den Tunneln. Was nicht so gut war, die Shooting Ranch im Gelände wo mit scharfer Munition geschossen werden kann.

Sehr beeindruckend wie die Vietcong dieses Tunnelsystem von 200km Länge und fast 10m Tiefe gebaut haben und damit ihren Kriegsgegnern erhebliche Verluste beigebracht haben. Unter der Erde haben sie gelebt und ihre Kinder zur Welt gebracht, aber auch viele Tote beklagen müssen. Deshalb ist eine Shooting Range auf diesem Gelände nicht angebracht , wo der amerikanische Tourist für 1 $ pro Schuss mit der Kalaschnikow oder dem M30 nochmal den verlorenen Krieg nacherleben kann. Sehr respektlos den toten Vietnamesen gegenüber.

I spent a really interesting and quite sobering morning at the Cu Chi Tunnels. Quite hard to imagine the people who were living and fighting in this area not so long ago. The site has been restored well to give a fair representation of what took place without over glamorising or playing down the horror. I am glad I went down into the tunnel to get the insight of how small and narrow they were. You do have to crouch down or pull yourself along the floor, though there are opportunities to leave every minute or 2. Well worth experiencing
This is the historic tunnel's site. It is a must visit when in the area.  IN eality nothing outstanding but just part of history.
Was visualized how tunnels were built, and how the Vietnamese who had little had to resort to ways to fight the enemy.

Cu Chi Tunnels experience was great, BUT be careful at the gift shops around the area where the tour guides up sell the gifts saying that the money is going to people in need. When making a purchase do not let our item out f site especially when it comes to the staff packing your gifts, as they will do the old switcheroo and pack a lesser quality item of the same look and design.

The Cu Chi Tunnels were absolutely worth the visit—especially for anyone interested in war history. Exploring the underground tunnels provides eye-opening context to the Vietnam War, and for an additional cost, the on-site shooting range even allows you to try Vietnam War–era weaponry, adding an immersive (and adrenaline-filled) element to the tour.  We opted for the private driver and tour guide for the day for our group with pick-up and drop-off from our hotel in Ho Chi Minh City.

When were were in Ho Chi Min City, we visited the Cu Chi Tunnels. This was an interesting place where you could view artifacts related to the Viet Nam War in which America was involved. There is the opportunity to go into tunnels, see a variety of traps used by the Viet Kong and learn about the history of this war.

Visited with a tour guide from the city, the drive there was an hour or two, depending on traffic, we then spent about 90 minutes there. It was very interesting and my first stop on my holiday in Vietnam. Great to see inside the tunnels and walk through them, maybe not so good if you don't like small spaces! A must see on a first trip to Vietnam. I followed this by visiting the War Remnant Museum.
Booked a tour of the tunnels through Intrepid Travel, who were once again excellent. The site itself is well worth seeing but a major tourist destination and was extremely busy on the day we visited.

You have to take the cu chi tour with a pinch of salt: expect a lot of pro Vietnam propaganda, not making everything look as it was. The tunnels are extremely small, the soldiers are depicted as larger than they should be, making it a very biased experience.Nontheless very nice to do...
Quite crowded, even on a weekday morning. Recommend sunscreen and a hat. Need at least 2 hours. More if you intend to do some live firing at the range.
Best tour ever .  Do it.  Learned so much , enjoyed the views.  We brought 7 people along of all ages and all of us were blown away .

Odalar temizdi yemekler gayet iyiydi ozellikle mutfak şefine ve ekibine tesekurler yemekler taze ve guzeldi calisanlar nazik iyi insanlar animasyon ekibine de tesekurler ozellikle gamze hanim ve tolga beye ayri ayri tesekur ederim kisacasi memmun kaldik  biz eskisehirden geldik ismim ilhan kartal

Enjoyable and interesting day out but if I did this again I would not go on an organised tour. After getting on the coach was told about a compulsory stop on the way at an ‘art’ workshop. When we got to Cu Chi, the tour guide couldn’t get us off the coach quick enough and almost ran us round the tour. Although very interesting I am sure we missed a lot due to being rushed from one bit to the next. Had almost no time to actually get involved at some of the interactive points.There is a lot to see and do, crawl through tunnels, hide in a hole in the ground, go into some underground bunkers (kitchen, etc), go on the shooting range, look at the abandoned American tank and weapons, look at the range of deadly traps created by the Vietnamese fighters and more just wish we could have spent more time at some of these.

It's best to book this trip through a local tour or trip app. My six friends and I booked a group tour for 40 people through a travel app, but the tour operator provided us with a private tour due to our large group size. We were joined by tourists from Taiwan, Singapore, the Czech Republic, and England. Throughout the journey, we were accompanied by a tour guide who was fluent in English and explained the history of North and South Vietnam, including their reunification. We learned many new things, including about Vietnamese history and the architecture of bunkers and tunnels that were built with a well-planned, effective, and controlled system. Try it out and discover new knowledge.
Very interesting history.  Video in the initial visitor hut is very old and could use updating.  Long drive to get to these tunnels.  All appear to be reconstructions.  Bugs were not so bad.

I couldn't imagine there's a place like this on EARTH. I got in and tried to picture what the Vietcong had gone through down here inside these tunnels. I could only move inside the tunnel for 25meters - it's 25 METERS people, and I really had to get out, I couldn't take it anymore. And The Vietcong had been here for what? - decade!

The Guide takes you around the site and gives you a brief history of tunnels. You can try going in one of the many escape hatches and a chance to experience the tunnels themselves.The cafe half way round serves cold drinks and snacks but its a tad noisy as its next to the firing range!

We went to visit the Cu Chi Tunnels as part of an organised tour. All travel agents appear to recommend the visit as an essential element of a trip to Saigon / Ho Chi Minh City. The tunnels are an important feature of the history of the Vietnam War (or the American War as it is known here). The ingenuity and resiliance of the Vietnamese people is astonishing. However, I am not certain that the tour itself is a first rate destination. There are a lot of reconstructions, with models of guerrilla fighters. Tunnels have been recreated for tourists. There is not a huge amount of original material. The sound on some of the videos is of poor quality and there is a fair amount of propaganda in the content. I do not wish to deny the courage and suffering of the people of the time but in my opinion the tour is overrated. It was not clear to us that the site is on hour and a half's drive away from the city. It was very busy. The tour itself is too long. And the opportunity to try shooting is distasteful.

Củ Chi Tunnels trên Google thường được đánh giá rất cao bởi du khách trong và ngoài nước. Nhiều người ấn tượng với hệ thống đường hầm lịch sử độc đáo, mang đậm dấu ấn chiến tranh Việt Nam. Khách tham quan khen ngợi trải nghiệm khám phá hầm, bắn súng thể thao và hướng dẫn viên nhiệt tình. Một số ý kiến cho rằng địa điểm có thể đông khách vào cuối tuần và giá cả các dịch vụ phụ hơi cao. Tổng thể, đây là điểm đến đáng thử khi tìm hiểu về lịch sử Việt Nam.
Visited today an it is a must see attraction but they really should spend some money on updating the AV aids and get really good fans in the enclosed areas. Very cool to be able to shoot an AK47
Di tích lịch sử đáng trải nghiệm, thể hiện sự thông minh và nỗ lực của ông cha trong công cuộc bảo vệ dân tộc.Các hầm khá nhỏ, trai nghiệm khá thú vị, ngoài ra hình như có 1 số hoạt động khác
It is amazing to see, how Vietnamese people made tunnels to fight strong American army. Traps for enemy and other details were shown and described by the guide.

If you are interested in the Vietnam war history, this place is of interest. If not, don’t go. It took almost 2 hours to get there due to HCM traffic which is horrific on a good day. Our private tour guide who was not part of the site helped us understand the Viet Cong usage of the area. It was an interesting visit.

A full day filled with history and experiences. It is a lot of sitting in a car, but that's because of the distances between the attractions and to Ho Chi Minh City.By the tunells there were many things to see, not just tunells. Various traps and objects from the war, Vietcong kitchen and a shooting range (a bit too touristy and noicy for us, but they sold ice cream 😄). We got to walk in one tunnel. The one we walked in was quite "easy" and my 5 year old could walk upright. I wish we'd had time to walk in others too.We had a lovely lunch in a restaurant nearby, then 2 more hours in the car to the Mekong Delta.On the Mekong Delta, a large, traditional motorboat took us to Unicorn Island were we watched a honey farm, fruits and a coconut candy factory. Last, we were in a small row boat on a narrow part of the river. Very relaxing.

We took this tour through our ship the Celebrity Solstice. It was listed as being 9 hours long. However, it took 4 hours to get there and 3 hours to get back! This left us approximately one hour of rushing around the compound and Tunnels, and about 40 minutes for a fast lunch. Traffic was bad in the morning and our restroom stop wasn’t till 3 hours after we left the port area! We did not stop anywhere in Ho Chi Minh City (where the locals still call it Saigon). But, what we saw from the bus, this city was run down in so many areas. Motorcycles were speeding everywhere and cutting close to the bus.The Cu Chi Tunnels themselves were very cramped but interesting. They demonstrated how people moved about during the war, how they cooked and breathed in there without being detected. The guides also demonstrated how they set many deadly traps. This forested, jungle area has been well used by all the tourists that visit daily. At some information stops there were way too many groups trying to hear their guide or see what they were talking about. There is a shooting range where for a few dollars you can shoot an AK47. The constant loud shots actually sounded like a war was going on. On the property is also a big gift shop and a place where you can buy some food and drinks. Restrooms were not clean.If you have mobility issues or don’t like sitting in a bus for hours, this type of tour (from the cruise port) might not be worth it. But, from a historical aspect it was interesting to see the area, learn about the tunnels, and way of life during the war.

We squeezed through these tiny tunnels like amateur contortionists! While it was a bit claustrophobic, we couldn't help but be amazed by the ingenuity. Our guide's stories were fascinating, though the war remnants were sobering. Not for the faint-hearted or claustrophobic couples, but definitely eye-opening.

Each tourist must visit this place! In the beginning there is a tour showing different traps and hidden underground passages. You are welcomed to click the famous photo of going inside or coming out of the tunnel. A word of caution for those with weak upper body strength- I struggled to lift myself up from this tunnel. :P Then they take you to the main tunnel. Its not for those who feel claustrophobic. As a parent of kids between 7-10 year old it was a little difficult as my kids started running in different directions inside the tunnel. We came out at the first exit. Kids were fine but we were scared in the tunnel!! :P

The tunnels at Củ Chi (Địa đạo Củ Chi) are a complex underground network. It was used by the Viet Cong. One of their operations was the Tết Offensive in 1968.It had everything, from kitchens to bedrooms to hospitals. It also had happy rooms where 6,000+ babies were made and born.We left hotel at 8am, spent two plus hours on site. The traffic is heavy both ways, two plus hours to and from the site, getting back to the hotel at 2:30pm. We could have returned a little early but some mates enjoyed the shooting of AK47, we all waited.The site is shaded, part of the paths are slightly uneven.The first exhibit is a bobbie trap: 11% of Americans, about 7,000 were killed by the bobbie traps during the war.A park range demonstrates how to get into a tunnel.

We sqeezed through those tiny Cu Chi tunnels - claustrophobic much?! Seriously impressive ingenuity, though.  It's a sobering experience, seeing how life was.  Definitely not for the faint of heart (or broad of shoulder!).  We learned so much, and emerged blinking into the light, slightly dusty, but wiser.  A must-do, if you're up for a bit of a squeeze!

But I wasn't as impressed as I expected. The inside of the tunnel was quite narrow and dark, so I felt uncomfortable in the hot weather. Especially the hospital district was interesting, but it was too crowded with tourists that it was difficult to see properly. Overall, the historical significance is great, but I thought I wish I could explore it more freely.

We had a full two hours here,it war 33centegrade,so cover up,despite lots of tree cover,it was hot.We followed a guide,who showed us the tunnels,well hidden and camoflagged,ytraps in the jungle,this is a forest maze of survival,or die,that many endured in the vietnam war.you can entre,a walk,crawl through tunnel,also lots booby traps,man traps,horrendous spiked pits.This is what life was like ,you need to see it to realise why America lost this war,tactical,warfare.unknown before.

Fascinating! Things to learn for all family members. I couldn’t recommend it more. Be prepared! It’s hot there. You can buy refreshments. You can fire weapons at the range they have there for a price. Bathrooms available and clean.  The tunnels and artifacts are fascinating. You can enter and crawl in small tunnel sections. Don’t miss this experience.
This is a fascinating place to visit, a real documented history of the tunnels, life in the tunnels and the ingenuity of the people and what they went through. Lunch was provided and was ok as well

We booked a full day tour using Klook app which cost us 5k INR per person and it included both Cu Chi Tunnels and Mekong Delta. The tour started at around 6 AM in morning when we were picked up from our hotel. The first stop was a small breakfast pit stop and then we stopped at a workshop/factory where Vietnamese War handicapped people work and make egg shell related arts. Then we made our way to Cu Chi Tunnels. Entry fee was included in the tour and the guide told us about all the history. These tunnels were used by Viet Cong soldiers to hide from USA army during the war. The living conditions were harsh. These tunnels were quite narrow and inside it used to be warm and moist. They used to setup death traps. Overall the tour was quite informative and you get some experience of going inside the tunnels. The tour ended with some tea and snacks.
Good place to visit, specially for kidslittle far from the city, but worth going there as there are not much activities to do in Ho Chi Mien,

a mandatory place to understand Vietnamese problemsVery well recreated with staff explaining the placesI don't like the shooting range at all that for someCoins, you can use the guns.If you want to set an example of peace, it is no place for so much rumble.2 hours drive, worth it

A great reminder of the resilience..self sacrifice and perseverance of Vietnamese people.Various historical sites including tunnels to the river...air hole small hillock.. demonstrations..film presentation was amazing..we tried crawling in the tunnels too.excellent gift shop too..

We explored miles of underground tunnels, discovering bunkers and ingenious traps. An adventurous and unforgettable experience for us. The adrenaline felt in the air as we imagined life during the war.

The Tunnels are not necessarily connected but scattered over a large area outside Saigon and the Makon Delt.  We visited just one cluster at Cu Chi.  The tunnel storage / staging areas were close to the end of the HO Che Men trail and allowed quick access for ground attack against Saigon and the US military bases.  We are on a 24 day OAT tour of all of Vietnam.The Bus ride out of the city took us about to hours to go the 50 miles to this location.The tunnels are tight and dark.  One of the defensive positions had small camouflaged access door, so small that Tom could not get his shoulders through it.  Most of the tunnels are only 2 ft wide and three ft tall so Tom made It ok-ish through all, but at the end of one tunnel into the command room he needed help straitening up after shuffling along so long all crotched down.

Amazing to see the ingenuity the locals had fighting US troops. Just sad that one of the highlights I was looking forward to was the shooting but the only guns available now was AK47 and another Russian machine gun.

Exploring the Cu Chi Tunnels was amazing! We crawled through tight passageways and saw clever Viet Cong booby traps up close. It's incredible to see how they lived and fought. A must-visit for couples who love history and adventure!!!!
I came here. That's really great. The Vietnamese are really so good, so smart.....................

Very informative and well set out to move visitors through the area to see the buildings, tunnels and the ingenious solutions to the challenges of jungle warfare.  There was an option to shoot some of the guns and the sounds of gunfire through the jungle really added to the experience.  We also had a thunderstorm and rain so it was full on.  The ground gets very muddy so wear appropriate footwear!  The bathrooms were clean and well maintained.  The tunnels are very tiny and I found them super claustrophobic and hot so only went a few metres in.  Husband did a longer stretch and came out in one piece, also a little red from effort!

One of the most interesting place were war between Vietnam and America happened and with the  Gorilla  strategies Vietnam defeated America. There are various types of tunnels made by vietnamese, great strategies were made by Vietnamese to cambat the war. The war was continued for 20 long years,but ultimately due to persistent of vietnamese soldiers they could win the war. Aniruddha Kshire India
It is really exciting and fun.I have never seen anything like this. So it is worth to explore.I had so much adventures and want to bring back my family to go with.

My first visit to Cu Chi Tunnels was an informative experience. I got to know firsthand the history hidden in the tunnel and how cramped the tunnel really was. The only downside was that the guide was a bit blunt with me. Maybe it was because I was the only Korean among the tour tourists and we couldn't talk to each other on the phone.

The tour guide Nelson speaks with terrible accent but He explains everything,  knows a lot about The VietCong,  politics and The tour is very interesting thanks to him Without The guide There is no point going by yourselves, you gonna see few holes in The ground and Without explanation of what you looking at its not worth it. - We went to The meeting point and got on The bus but others in The tour got picked up at their hotels which took almost an extra Hour before We could go which was annoying.
very interesting and historically very relevant site. it is well organized though full beyond reason and its making the visit a lot less impressive

Very interesting to delve into the history of this terrible war with all the strategies and traps of the Vietnamese resistance fighters. A small downside, however, this excursion does not really reflect the advertising that is made, namely that you cannot enter the tunnels that are too low 70cm, too narrow and without ventilation. Another strategy with photos on leaflets to attract tourists...

The actual tunnels and tour guide our group had was worth the trip. Although please be aware that at the end of the tour next to the cafe are people firing high powered firearms with real ammunition. The thought of that is off putting to say the least let alone the noise. Those that are firing shots get ear protection and those who are a couple of metres away in the cafe have no hearing protection offered. Also I see it being a high possibility for people suffering from PTSD to be triggered, ie ex defence force vets or police officers. I can’t believe there is no forewarning of this.

Impressive network of underground galleries of around 200 km at different heights dug by hand by the Vietcom to take refuge from the American war machine.They show you the different traps they used to hunt American soldiers when they entered the jungle area.There is a 100 meter tunnel that they have dug to European size so that tourists can enter and feel what it was like to live underground. There is no room for Europeans or Americans in the Vietnamese galleries due to size.They have a shooting range where machine guns can be heard throughout the forest and you can shoot different weapons from the time.You can watch an informative Vietcom propaganda video

Cu Chi Tunnel is a complex where visitors can experience the real on the ground situation at the time of Vietnam War with USA.Very impressive & a must visit place.It has real war time tanks, helicopters & other war time weapons on display, one can also try hand at shooting on payment of extra money.Very intersting place & a MUST VISIT

Our guide Vin was incredibly knowledgeable and spoke very good English.The tour started late due to people not being ready for their pickup times. This unfortunately left us no time to complete everything that was scheduled I.e., did not visit paddy fields or stop and see people working which was very disappointing.

I pre-booked the Cu Chi Tunnels half-day tour through the Klook app.It's been about an hour since we met on the morning of the reservation date and moved to the Cu Chi Tunnel on the outskirts of Ho Chi Minh City.The rectangular shape around the hole where my body entered in the Gucci Tunnel looks small, but I tried to see if my slightly chubby body could fit in, and I was able to get out without a hitch. I passed through the tunnel and came out from the hole close to where I came out.

Then air was really nice, a different way to see the countryside. The Barry and the guid was good, refreshments and fruit on the way. The tunnels are quite unique- so small as the local people were small. It is quite claustrophobic to get inside, for those who have this problem.

super cool thing to learn about and experience. sadly, it is a bit Disneyfied but I suppose they had to do that to make it more interesting for visitors. the film at the end is horrible, you can't understand the lady and it doesn't give you any information. the shooting range is extremely loud and right next to the gift shop and snack area - horrible. could have been so much better with good signage and a film that explains the situation. the traffic out of the city was a nightmare and we got home over an hour later than promised. even with all the negatives it was still worth visiting due to its uniqueness and relevancy.

These underground tunnels, an integral part of Vietnamese history, offer a vivid glimpse into the era of French colonialism.Our group had an enthralling experience, immersing ourselves in the dark, narrow passageways. Imagine crawling through these remarkably preserved tunnels – it felt like an exhilarating game of hide-and-seek with the past.The educational aspect of the visit added an extra layer of significance. Our knowledgeable guide passionately explained the ingenious strategies employed by the Viet Cong during the Vietnam War. It was truly eye-opening to learn how these tunnels served as hidden havens for soldiers and villagers alike.

I visited the Cu Chi Tunnels as part of a cruise ship excursion. I must admit I didn't know an awful lot about the finer details of the Vietnam War in advance of my visit so found the tour by our guide really fascinating, although it's clearly a story in history tinged with much sadness and human loss.Thousands of miles of tunnels were built during the Vietnam War by the communist guerrilla troops (Viet Cong) including a large complex of tunnels here at Cu Chi, located northwest of Saigon / Ho Chi Minh City. We had around an hour here but could easily have spent longer. I found it interesting to see the demonstrations of the various booby traps that were built, and it was certainly a weird experience to crawl through a short section of the tunnel complex and imagine what it must have been like in the war. It can be claustrophobic in the tunnels (for obvious reasons) and you need to be able to crawl or bend low on your knees to navigate them, but one 20m section was enough of a flavour for me.There is a firing range at the site which made it feel all the more authentic as you could hear constant gunfire as you wandered around the various exhibits.My tour was a little rushed at times...I would have liked longer to get video footage and photos of the various traps and sampled a bit more of the various exhibits around the site, but overall I thought it was well worth a visit.

Cu Chi Tunnels complex is a unique worldwide attraction in terms of warfare.It can be called a masterpiece of primitive engineering, which turned out to be a big success.They're located some 1,5-2 hours drive northwest from Saigon.It's a complex consisting of more than 200 km underground tunnels dug by southern communist guerilla which was under the command of North Vietnam.It had all the necessary infrastructure: underground kitchens, ambulance and hospital, storage rooms, ventilation, shoes and ammo making facilities,....At the same time, on the ground, they built many unique traps for enemies.The tour is very interesting and you can visit only the tunnels on a half day tour from Saigon, or combine them with Mekong delta for a full day tour. Having a good and easy to understand guide is essential and the quality of your tour pretty much depends on that, however you cannot influence on that.This was my second visit to Cu Chi tunnels - first one was 7 years ago in 2017, and this year's one was far less enjoyable.The tour became extremely commercialized with dozens of tour buses on site. Interesting spots throughout the woods are overcrowded with tourists and tour guides explanations mixing. In the background is a constant noise of a gun shooting range - a new money making attraction which wasn't existent in 2017, which makes it even more difficult understanding your guide.However, despite these odds I deduct one star for, it's really a must do attraction in Saigon I strongly advise as you cannot see it anywhere else

We organised a private driver and guide to take us to Cu Chi Tunnels and it was amazing! It's hard to even imagine living underground for weeks and months like they did!  Fascinating!We requested not to go to the section where the tour buses and most tourists go (Ben Dinh) but instead to the lesser known site at Ben Duoc.  It is a bit further out and a long drive, but we were able to do a side-trip to the village of "Napalm Girl" KimPhuc in Trang Bang, meet her family and see the church where they hid when the bombs were dropped. A very humbling and touching experience. We went through the various tunnels and in and out of secret entry/exit points and quite a few very narrow, very dark bits. There were many displays and lots of information inside the tunnels and also in the surrounding area. No tourists in sight! Fantastic! The day was complete with a traditional Vietnamese meal at a rural little restaurant with plastic chairs and a few lazy dogs.Worth the extra money for a completely unique and private experience.

This really is a very interesting experience. We had a private tour and my  guide gave a very detailed tour.The tunnels you can experience are good but obviously tight if you are tall or big.Refreshments and toilets available.The only thing I did not like is the constant gun fire you could hear. I don’t agree with promoting guns / target practice in a place that is trying to explain the importance of peace.
Visited here on the afternoon of Dec-27,2023 and saw the war zone, traps, weapons, bomb crater sites, the tunnels & animatronics on display.
Lucky i did a half day tour its enough. overall it was informative and good knowledge shared. its ok for a one time visit

This is a must see place when one visits Saigon . The place is about 1.5 hours from the district 1 area of Saigon . I highly recommend going by an organised tour as a guide is essential to visit the place and understand the history and significance of all the spots. There is a handicrafts place that the tours stop enroute where one can buy egg shell crafts and other souvenirs. It is expensive though.  The tunnels are narrow and only my teenage son dated to crawl into them . Don’t miss the shooting range where one can fire rounds on real AK 47 and M16 rifles. It is not very cheap but worth  the spend. Overall a very important visit to understand the war and how it was fought.

We did a half day tour to Cu Chi Tunnels and our tour took us first to a place called Saigon Handicapped Handicrafts where people impacted by Agent Orange in US-Vietnam War is employed & make beautiful painting & other stuff using Sea shells, Egg shells & sand etc. You can see the process and later can buy some stuff & contribute in a way. After this, we headed to Cu Chi Tunnels, this place gave us an insight of on US-Vietnam war, how Vietnamese soldiers fought, the incredible tunnels they had built, different strategies they used during the war etc. You can even find tankers and bombs on the display and also experience few tunnels crawling inside them. The place also has a shooting range too and you can try your hands at AK-47, Machine Gun etc. which can be an exciting activity for many. The place is hot and humid and as it's a forrest area so you might want to carry a mosquito repellent. We also tried Boiled cassava with Tea at the end of the tour. Overall, it would be a 2-3 kms walk and the place is worth the hype.

At first I was a bit skeptical about whether I wanted to do it. Then I booked a small group tour and found it really cool and impressive. There were 9 of us and we weren't stressed and didn't see many other tourists. That meant we could look at everything closely and take our time in the tunnels. It was really very impressive.
Educational and well-preserved excursion at a reasonable distance from Ho Chi Minh about the Vietnam War. Very touristy

Leaving cold Japan, I stayed in Ho Chi Minh City aimlessly.I thought I would go to the Cu Chi Tunnels, but the tour I initially looked for left in the morning, which was too early. When I got there, I saw that there was an afternoon tour, so I joined it. A cheap English small-group tour was about 2,000 yen including admission, so I joined it.I'm glad I went.I tried the cassava, which I thought was simple and delicious, but it was like steamed potatoes with almost no flavor. It would be better with mayonnaise or a stir-fry with a lot of soup.As the hotel was not included in the shuttle service, I took a Grab to the office at 12:45 and the bus left immediately. The bus was about the size of a Japanese route bus, with about 38 seats, and was spacious from front to back, and could recline.There were about 13 passengers, the guide and the driver.There was air conditioning, but no seat belts.We departed after touring the hotel for just under an hour, and my tour was around the city center, so I was glad to be able to see the city from the window.We took a 25-minute break at 2:30 and arrived at 3:30.The afternoon tour is probably less hot when exploring the local area.In the morning, it starts at 10:00 and lasts for just under two hours, but in the afternoon, you can explore after the sun has set.Shooting is optional, but it costs 60,000 VND per shot, for a minimum of 600,000.The Singaporean couple asked if we could share, as only the man wanted to do it, but 10 shots was too many, and we readily agreed.We left at 5:30 PM.On the way back, there was heavy traffic about 17km from the end.Looking at Google Maps, we saw that it was a 10-minute walk from our hotel, so we were dropped off there. It was 8 PM by that point.We probably didn't get to the last hotel until close to 9 PM.
We had visited this place before, but this time we felt a bit rushed. It is a very interesting place and well worth the journey.
Cu Chi: A captivating blend of history and ingenuity. The tunnels reveal a remarkable chapter, leaving visitors in awe of Vietnam's resilience and innovation.
We enjoyed our private tour to visit the Cuchi tunnels. Good to learn about Vietnam War and how Vietnamese could win over USA.

Good tour guide who explained the whole setup well. Bomb holes, man traps, tunnels etc all well displayed. Gunshots were a bit hard on the ears - would be better if the waiting area was further away. Good meal at the end.

If you want to understand the war of Vietnam, this is the place to go. You can see the how people struggle for the war, you can understand how much they suffer and you can even try to go inside the tunnels below the ground. Amazing experience that makes us not forget one of the most cruel time in history.

The tour was a lot of fun.Only the person at the shooting range was very rude and did not understand what an AK47 was.When we told him it wasn't an AK47 he got very angry and started shouting.Very unfortunate, I was looking forward to it.

Very interesting and rewarding excursion to the Cu Chi Tunnels. Although much is arranged for tourists, we still got a good insight into what the Vietnam War could have looked like for the warring parties, with a lot of cruelty and great suffering for the civilian population.Our guide did an excellent job of bringing the history to life in excellent English, it is easier to get the most out of the trip with a guide than to visit on your own.For the slightly more shooting enthusiast, there is the opportunity to test shoot the AK47, M16 and M60. However, quite expensive with a price of 2 USD per shot. The weapons are also worn and fastened in such a way that people with a shooting habit hardly get anything out of this.
During the tour we had a good and enthousiastic guide, Xem. The cu chi tunnels are worth the visit if you don't mind sitting on a bus for 3.5-4 hours in total. Well organised by Xem.

I followed the Vietnam War on the news when I was young. I was very curious to know the place where the Viet Cong hid to resist attacks from the United States. The installations and creativity of the guerrillas are impressive. It was very worth the visit.

A fun and informative half a day - worth the short trip out of Saigon centre to experience this - our guide was a hoot - despite professing to hold no animosity to any nation he clearly carried quite a grudge against Americans and became quite animated at times!!!

The way the tunnels were built and how they are connected is incredible. The opportunity to imagine what it must have been like during the conflict is provided by the tour through the region. There are many trees and not a lot of noise.

We took a special tour with a reserved guide for visiting Cu Chi tunnel tour. The guide was good and knew the place well. The tour shows the intricacy of tunnels in the war with US and how it was made, what purpose it served. One has to watch a movie and looks more of a propaganda of the current govt against the US. A lunch with local cuisine, tapioca tasting and green tea are a part of the tour. Most of the tunnels have been made up for the tourists to enter and take pictures.
Interest experience, saying the tunnels from the inside gives a bit of an idea of what happened in the past and see it first-hand

We did a private tour with small group through UniqueTours. Our guide, Hieu, was very informative. It was a highlight of our trip. An underground city that is well-preserved . Booby traps, secret passageways, kitchens, hospital, trap doors, and underground bunkers - it's hard to imagine people lived there for 15 years! If you like history, come to this place.My husband got to shoot 50 rounds of a M60 machine gun. Was expensive, but he was thrilled with the experience.Only downside, don't buy the souvenir 30mm cannon shell as this was confiscated at airport on departure.

We went as part of a half day tour from Saigon.  It was a fascinating place where we learned a lot about how the Vietnamese people fought against the Americans.  There were several tunnels you could travel through that were made for small sized people, so if you're claustrophobic, you might want to skip.  The only thing I found odd was the gun range, where you could pay to shoot an AK 47, which seemed a little macabre, given that the area was the site of immense death and destruction.  You'll probably want to bring along some bug spray, since the whole area is in a forest.

This is a fascinating place which gives a real insight into the war from the VC perspective. There are some gruesome exhibits and viewing these and the accompanying murals is not for the faint-hearted. It is worth braving the tunnels (even if you prefer just to tackle the short one, which only takes 2-3 minutes to negotiate) to get a sense of what it must have been like. My chief criticism is that the shooting range is located much too close to the rest of the site; the sound of gunfire was continuous, and when you are close to it (i.e. where the giftshop and cafe are) quite deafening. If people want to fire off bullets, then fine, but people who don't want to should not have to put up with this level of noise - they could easily move the shooting range even half a kilometre away to muffle the racket somewhat. Apart from that, this is an absorbing and sobering place to visit.

There are many ways to get here to visit, using a travel agent or personal transportation. Alternatively, you can take an e bus to Cu Chi tunnels. Despite receiving a lot of foreign guests, the public toilet system is not clean and comfortable. The internal paths inside do not have guide boards, the quality of the road surface is not good, so guests using wheelchairs cannot move. The entrance and exit of the tunnel area are far from each other, which is very inconvenient for guests on rainy days.
Our guide Nancy was great, she explained everything in English. Interesting to see the conditions & how they survived.
That's creative for the first time to go to such interesting places as it. should try it once . should try steamed tapioca, it's very delicious

A tour to Cu Chi Tunnels is certainly not for everyone. It is steep in war history which may appeal only to select segments, but I personally find it very informative as it is a pivotal part of Southeast Asian history.However, most important to note is that the tunnel itself requires you to bend down to enter the dark tunnels, which may not be suitable for everyone. It was really testing my knees and some section of the tunnels can be quite long.Nonetheless, you get to relive history and imagine how life must have been like if you were in the war zone. It's a unique experience, for sure.

We got there with tour guide Nim who was really knowledgeable, has good English and told us many interesting information during the tour, especially historical ones.Area is not large, contains many tunnel entrances, shooting range and enlarged tunnel which anyone can crawl through :-)
Great experience and learning about history. Highly recommended to people who will visit HCM. Nothing else to follow.

They are a fascinating reminder of the French colonial era and the struggle for independence that followed. The tunnels were ingeniously designed to offer hidden entrances and exits, and were equipped with booby traps to protect the Viet Cong from their enemies. Visiting the tunnels is an unforgettable experience. You can explore the tunnels and learn about the ingenious methods used by the Viet Cong to survive the war. You will be able to see firsthand the booby traps that were used to protect the tunnels, as well as the secret entrances and exits that were used to protect the residents of the tunnels. You can also take part in a range of activities, such as firing an AK-47 rifle or learning how to make a traditional Vietnamese meal.

Jimmy was very informative and was very easy to talk to, easy to understand, and very accommodating for me overall. He did have a tendency to disappear when it was time for the shooting range and lunch, which led to us staying in one place longer than necessary. Overall tho it was a very enjoyable trip.

Awesome day, educational and fun...Toan our guide was very informative with very good English our driver Hung was very safe and competent.  The food we had for lunch was fresh and tasty, I recommend this to anyone who wants to do something more than just hang in HMC, the tunnels where fascinating the Meekong delta enjoyable and fun

You see some really cool things and some horrific things as well. They show you an old propaganda film that is so devoid of facts it’s hilarious.  Our tour guide was great, but had no idea what was fact or fiction. We learnt that North Vietnam and America were the only warring parties, the South wasn’t involved. Also, it wasn’t a civil war, which became obvious when you consider South Vietnam wasn’t in it. It was a great tour despite the fictional information provided.

We booked the cu chi minh tunnel tour with Kim travel. We were picked up on time, and our tour guide Laughing shared his knowledge withbusus throughout the journey, thus enabling us to visualise what was going through during the war.

Cu Chi Tunnels are evidence of the sheer power of grit & determination.Knowing some history about the Vietnam war will provide a better context for your visit and help you understand, appreciate and enjoy your visit better.Although pretty much in wooded areas, getting here by 10 will help you beat the heat.The whole tour takes about 2 hours. If you are not part of a guided group, there is a local guide available arranged by the Cu Chi Tunnel Authority.Carry a face towel and a bottle of water for each person and dress lightly. Shorts & shoes are highly recommended.If you're a history buff and/or fascinated by stories of psychological warfare, this place is a must-visit.For me personally, one of the reasons for picking Vietnam as a holiday destination was what I had learnt about this network of underground tunnels.

Our family decided to visit the Ben Duoc Cu Chi tunnel complex as it is much large in area and more authentic and much smaller tunnels but longer. You can get there by bus but it takes a long time, but if you are on a budget, it is worth looking into.  We missed out on the large Ben Duoc memorial which looks incredible due to time.  Didnt really want to watch the movie as the crickets were so loud you couldnt hear much, but our guide disappeared for a walk, so we had to wait for him.  He was a veteran from rangers for Saigon and had a lot of great information to share.  My husband and sons really enjoyed his stories.  We even tried the tapioca at the end and it wasnt that bad to be honest.  Great place to learn some history.

An opportunity to visit an historical place and get more knowledgeIt’s very turistic attraction but still with taking the time to go thereGood organization and guides can be arranged available to enrich the experience

Went here on a private morning tour. Very informative and a good insight. A nice drive out and a goid tour if you beat the rush around 10 am.  The wife enjoyed shooting the AK47 also. It's definitely worth the trip.

Cu chi tunnels 1/2 day tour (pickup starting at 8.00 am/local time), finish around 2.00 pmFrom downtown to the cu chi side area takes about 2 hours ...Around 5-6000 tourist visit this side area during morning and afternoon ...This is history/memorable of saigon war versus usa during year 1970's ...Visit underground tunnels which been built by cu chi people before the war with usa around 50 km lenght, and during the war the people and military built up to 500 km ...The tunnels itself to do guellia war, people stay fir hiding the war ...STOP THE WAR !!!WAR MAKING PEOPLE SUFFERING ....!!!

We visited the tunnels 12 years ago and were interested how it had developed. The answer we found was much more of the same. There had always been several areas of interest in the so called ‘Jungle’ but now there were many more which meant that more tour groups could see everything without getting in each others way. Still very much well worth visiting.
Thực sự rất ấn tượng với những gì đã thấy. Rất đặc biệt và đẹp nữa đầy thú vị hãy trải nghiệm nhé....
super experience and a very worth it experience to do when you come to HCMC!  hope you are not clostrophobic through!

Very interesting trip to this place about 1 hour and 15 minutes away by road from Ho Chi Minh city. Went with friends and had a thoroughly interesting experience. The fact that the Viet  Cong built these tunnels over 20 years between 1954 to 1975 and used them as a form of guerrilla warfare against the French & Americans makes for fascinating history. When we crawled through the tunnels we realised how difficult it was and we were told by our guide that over 16,000 people had inhabited these tunnels! The various nasty booby traps used to maim and kill enemy soldiers were on display along with the underground kitchens, meeting places and bunkers and showed a very hardy and adept population that combatted its enemy occupiers. I am not sure what the reaction would be of an average American tourist to see documentaries of Viet Cong heroes being given medals for being American killers! Interestingly the place had a firing range where one could buy live ammunition and fire with AK47 & M16 weapons! That was an amazing and sobering experience when we used these lethal weapons on the range and realised their immense power and capacity to inflict harm on a human being. Still we had a great experience at the place and worth recommending to any first time tourist to Vietnam.

We toured the tunnels as part of a half day HCM/Saigon tour. We found the tunnels and artefacts fascinating… especially the bit where the man disappears into the tunnel and covers up the lid with leaves. I felt some bits could be updated: the information film at the start seemed outdated and propaganda-like, and maybe real actors instead of models would add to the effectiveness of the experience. Some updating is probably needed, but still a must-see if you go to this country.
Quite an interesting if long tour that is really overcooked with trips to many tourist traps. Too much time is wasted on the stops to the shops.
Discover the exceptional guerrilla techniques the Vietcongs used to trap the American army

Expérience sympas, à faire si vous avez un peu de temps à Saigon, c'est à 4h (aller/retour) de Saigon.Vous y trouverez un village avec toutes les traditions vietnamiennes ainsi que les fameux tunnel de cu chi.Dès que vous aller entré dans la zone des tunnels, un guide vous sera attitré.Je vous conseil de prendre une voiture privé avec chauffeur, et non un tour, vous serez plus libre d'aller où vous voulez et surtout un gain de temps fous.
The tour itself was excellent (just under 2 hours) and our guide (Lam) was great BUT be aware it was around 2-3 hours on a coach EACH WAY if your time in HCM is limited.
Spændende og informativt lavet. Personalet deltager rimeligt anonymt i demonstrationen. En form for krigsførelse man hører om, men sjældent set.

Åkte dit en eftermiddag, vi var en blandad grupp på åtta personer. Guiden som vi hade berättade om hur de levt och krigat i tunnlarna, vi fick också prova på att klättra nerbind gömma oss samt gå i tunnlarna. Intressant!
Il ne faut pas être trop grand, mais c'est bien, au pire des cas vous vous baissez un petit peu ça va bien se passer
It was an interesting experience to see. They have all of the traps on display and allow you to crawl through one of the tunnels. They also have a live fire range.

Got a coach from Saigon to visit tunnels- 1/2 day tour with guide who was very knowledgeable. Tunnels are fascinating and guide took time to explain in detail the various points of interest. Tunnels are small. I am over 1.82 m and could only manage first section on hands and knees. Can get crowded and very hot in tunnels

It is about a 2.5 hour drive from HCM city to get here, and this is usually packaged in with a Mekong Delta tour with is another 2.5 hours there and 2.5 back. The tunnels and history is amazing and it pays to have a good guide, the actual tunnels only end up being about 15-30 minutes of looking and about 5 minutes inside them. The shooting range here also is not included you can expect to pay 20 USD for 10 bullets. The tunnels themselves are great though.

There were several of us in a guided tour. Our guide was knowledgeable. I squeezed into the tunnel. Tight fit. Some of the folks in our group chose not to go into the tunnel, maybe for physical limitations or because of claustrophobia.

I enjoyed the most the introduction, looking at the maps and listening to the history of how people survived there and were organized. We had a chance t try to move through some tunnels but have not seen anything like on the picture. We were on an afternoon trip and there was not much to eat there so advice to take some snack with you.
What an experience! how terrible the war for the Vietnamese here. Come to see and no more war at any parts of our world

Vietcong tunnels were an important part of Vietnam history. As tourist highlight spots are somehow rare in Vietnam, visiting the tunnels is one of such highlights. Everything there is well organized but lacks a bit of intellectual substance. Operating a shooting range there fits somehow into the image of lack of style.
The entrance was not very clear/easy to find, but overall a good experience. We were lucky to have a really good guide as well.

This was an opportunity to see how the Vietnamese combated their enemies, firstly the French then the US and allies. The chance to go through a tunnel (which is actually made for us westerners, it’s larger) was an experience that I was eager for. Although I don’t consider myself too concerned with tight spaces, I was very happy to see the end of the 20m crawl. While you don’t crawl, you duck waddle and if you have bad knees or don’t like cramped spaces maybe this isn’t for you, and it was hot. The tunnels are approx 1.5hrs drive from Ho Chi Minh City centre through the bustling traffic. If you like guns there is an opportunity to fire automatic rifles, the sound adds to the experience and at first I thought this was just sound effects. It is worth the time to see the trap doors and how they lived and survived during the conflicts.
Fun place with family and friend.The historical information is interestingI would recommend my friends come here

Our accomodation was not in the list of pick-up points, so we got on the tour bus at the meeting point at 7.30am. Bus took some time to pick everyone else up. Tour Guide Phu was friendly and informative, shared his knowledge with us during the bus ride. First stop at a souvenir shop to support the handicapped, then to the Cu Chi Tunnels. Total cost includes ticket price and transport to and fro. To add 55-60k per bullet (available in 5s or 10s) for the gun shooting section (note that guns are mounted on stands). We reached back to the meeting point in HCMC around 1.30pm. Overall useful trip to Cu Chi Tunnels, but not particularly interesting.

The drive was a few hours out of town, and when we arrived you received quick access to the camps. It was very busy with tourists swarming around the various hatches, traps, dummy holes, and tunnels. Be warned that if you are sensitive to sound, there is a shooting range on site and the guns are awfully loud. Otherwise we recommend getting there early.

It was very good. The guide Kevin (from Vietnam Adventure Tours) gave a good historical background of the tunnel and the war. This generation Vietnamese forgives the past and tries to build a bright future country. We stopped at an art factory of which the workers are the offsprings of the generation affected by the Agent Orange. Kevin explained everything in details clearly. We crawled through a section of the tunnel. What a wonderful experience!The tour had 26 people. It took a little bit longer to pick up people from different locations. However, Kevin looked after us well.

If you know the Vietnam War History its more interesting , if you don't know the history you must hire a Guide. you can do Guided tour in VIP limmo from HCM . Its fun to watch their tricks to fight Americans but most important is you can experience REAL M16 , M60 and AK47 at their shooting range, we did fire 10 round of M16 and we had fun experience, the gun was tied up from the front, so the thrust back was not the real one but still we faced the thrust from the Gun fire. Overall good experience.
Half day tour with My as guide was really great. She is knowledgeable, charming and entertaining. The Cu Chi tunnels are a must see. Do not miss it.
Pickup was on time at the hotel. The guide was entertaining and encouraged people to experience "crawling" the tunnels. The shooting range in the end was a fun input.
With our own travel guide the cu chi tunnels viewed. It was more touristy than I thought, but very interesting! We were happy at a quiet time in September!
When you go in the tunnel, stick to the 20-meter version. I barely made it out of there, yet some guys did the 100 meter version! Incredible ingenuity of the Vietnamese to fight colonialism.
We did the afternoon tour - it was well organised.  Our guide was friendly and helpful.  The tunnels was great and worth visiting.  The shooting range was a great experience.   I would recommend
Definitely worth a visit. We did for half day tour.  Our guide was great, very informative and friendly. Cu Chi is a must for anyone to know about Vietnam war

A beautiful discovery with our guide, who explained the history of this region and its inhabitants.A story with a lot of emotions and courage.Very interesting place with its secrets.Narrow tunnels but can be made ( 1.83m )A very good half day very rewarding, I recommend.

If you come to Vietnam you need to come to Cu Chi to tick the box, it is a short run from Saigon and there are many trip options based on your budget, get a good one to miss the stops at shops selling tourist tut. The tunnel histories are interesting, but not for those of a large dimension (height or weight) - you can also go to the firing range and shoot AK-47's / M-16's and other military weapons used during the conflict, but its an expensive activity - but for those that will never come into contact with these types of weapons, you can tick the box, half a day is plenty, once you have wandered through one dark tunnel you have pretty much gotten the idea - just watch for snakes and scorpions sunbathing around the area, we saw a number on the day of our visit.

Tunnels are well worth a look, they are an amazing piece of engineering and as you learn more you can see how much thought went in to them. My tour guide was excellent. One thing to note if you stop at a place where disabled people are making decorations you will be brought into a shop DO NOT buy anything it is a monster rip off. Same goods are a fraction of the cost in Saigon in fact even when you get to gun range they are cheaper. Gun range part of trip is cool too I fired off an m16 - it jammed twice which they were known to do.

We were interested in visiting the Cu Chi Tunnels due to their historical importance.  It is a very interesting site.  You have the opportunity to crawl through a short stretch of actual tunnel, which give you a good sense of the size and what must have been endured underground.  This is where guerrilla came into being, and you can see displays and examples of the types of traps used, as well as view a video that provides the Vietnam perspective of the war.  There is a refreshment area and be forewarned that there is a shooting range nearby, so you will hear that during your visit.Allow at least 1.5 hours.
Our tour guide Lan led us around the tunnels for the day. It was a great day out, being collected at our hostel was also very convenient

Booked my tour through Hang at Beauty Travel in Hanoi (used on my previous trip to Tam Coc whole saying in  Hanoi ).Excellent communication,  all the way through the booking.Queenie, our tour guide (TNK Travel)  arrived on time, pleasant, friendly in attitude. Excellent knowledge about the tour and the war, explaining everything about the tunnels, traps etc. Very brave and courageous people during the war.Enjoyed the 1/2 day tour, well worth a visit.
Một không gian kiên cố dưới lòng đât. Thực sự rất khâm phục và ấn tượng với những gì người chiến sỹ cộng sản đã làm
Clear pick up instructions and fast communication. Nice excursion with a very nice guide. Would totally recommend. Prepare for some traffic jams on your way though.

The advantage of visiting Vietnam only 3 weeks after it opened to foreigners is we only saw 6 other people on our tour. The tunnels are a 90 minute drive from Saigon centre. The tour takes you around  the site shaded by trees for about2 hours. You get to go into one of the tunnels which are very low and narrow. At each stop there is something of interest to be seen.  You definitely need a guide as there are no boards of information. A very good trip.

Great trip to the Cu Chi Tunnels. Although this was called a "Half day" trip, we left at 8:30am and returned after 4pm, so it was really more of a full day, in case you're planning another activity after.There are two sets of restored tunnels and I went to the standard ones - I didn't elect these but I hear the other set has improved accessibility. The tunnels themselves were very interesting. Of course, effort has gone into the restoration/construction of the site and it's a bit of a conveyor belt but the whole point is to experience what it was like and learn more about the history and this you certainly get to do.  Our guide was friendly and informative. There was a stop at a factory making crafts, this dragged the day out longer than necessary, but overall it was a good day out and an essential part of any Saigon itinerary.

(2014年に行きました)ベトナム戦争時代、ベトコンが人力で建設したトンネルで、付随する射撃(実弾)や展示も体験できる場所。トンネルは2コース(長/短)があり、短いコースでも閉所恐怖症の人には相当辛いと思う。まず入ると、パンフレットをもらいVTRを観るが、銃声もあってか殆ど聞こえない。その次には、当時使われたと見られる罠が展示されており、ベトコンの残忍さが分かる。外国人は銃を打ちまくって楽しんでいた。近くの売店に、ビールの空き缶で作られたジープなどを買うことが出来る。
This is a place to experience first-hand the guerrilla tactics during the Vietnam War. Visiting with a travel guide was worth it.

We went there with friends and arranged our own transportation which I can only recommend. Tours are often filled with unnecessary stops and touristy stuff to stretch the trip...In total we stayed around 1h there (don't need much more time) and it was highly interesting to learn and see about the circumstances and life during that time. The tunnels are narrow and hot so better be not claustrophobic if you really want to see them.

A vast network of tunnels used by the Viet Cong  during the Vietnamese War. The tunnels are narrow and very, very claustrophobic even the ones that have been widened for the more corpulent western visitors to crawl through. Astounding to think that not only were the tunnels used  during combat but also used as living quarters and to house supplies of food and weapons. Within the War Memorial Park  you can also view some rather chilling traps and weapons of war and the craters left from bombing missions. A real insight into guerilla warfare. Note: the terrain is very uneven and probably not suitable for anyone with severe walking disability
Được tìm hiểu về quá trình kháng chiến của đất nước. Được rửa mặt bằng nước suối mát và thưởng thức cơm nắm, củ mì và nước trà rất thú vị
Nice and cheap experience. We would not fit into a lot of the parts though because the tunnel were very small.
Quite impressive to see and visit this sight. Can higly recommend that go get a small impression about that history.

Wanted to see the Cu Chi Tunnels and was not disappointed. Our guide was knowledgeable and make our visit more interesting. The gentleman who showed use how to travel in a tunnel was remarkable as the tunnel was very small and well disguised. the only disappointment was that the tunnels we were allowed in wasn't very long.A great visit
Our guide was very biased against the Americans and cut off any discussion of facts.  it’s best to know some history before you go.

Very well presented insight into the struggles and also awfulness of the Vietnam war. The resourcefulness of the Viet Cong when confronted with colonialism after which the potential of the U.S. forces, and the terrible circumstances they'd to endure, and the horrors experienced by the tunnel rats routed to battle them, is actually beyond belief. Quite moving.

Είμασταν 4 ταξιδιώτες, και αγοράσαμε το καλό πακέτο που ήταν για μέχρι 10 άτομα στο γκρουπ, σε κλιματιζόμενο minibus, με φαγητό και νερό, όταν φτάσαμε την πρώτη μέρα στο Ho chi minh. 2 μέρη υπάρχουν που ονομάζουν cu chi tunnels, εμείς ζητήσαμε και πήγαμε στα γνήσια τούνελ που είναι στην περιοχή Địa đạo Bến Dược, βρίσκονται 70 χιλιόμετρα βορειοδυτικά της Σαϊγκόν κοντά στα σύνορα με Καμπότζη. Η εκδρομή στοίχισε 500.000 vnd (19,50 euros το άτομο). αν δεν είστε κλειστοφοβικοί ή πολύ γεροδεμένοι περπατήστε τα, να φοράτε πολύ ελαφρά ρούχα και σίγουρα αντικουνουπικό. Κάποια στιγμή σε βγάζουν φώτο βγαίνοντας από ένα τούνελ και έπειτα σου ζητούν στην έξοδο 200.000vnd (8 ευρώ), εκεί παίρνει παζάρια, πλήρωσα 50.000 vnd (2 ευρώ). Μην αγοράσετε μπρελόκ σφαίρες γιατί θα έχετε πρόβλημα στα αεροδρόμια. Μην δοκιμάσετε να πάτε να ρίξετε σφαίρες στο σκοπευτήριο γιατί στοιχίζουν 10$ η μία σφαίρα !!!
Interessante Geschichtsstunde, wenn auch z.T. ideologisch gefärbt. Anfahrt von Saigon etwas lang. Selbst in einen Tunnel zu gehen, erhöht den Eindruck sehr.

We were picked-up at our hotel by a 7 seater van with aircon and a private guide. The trip to the tunnels took us almost two hours and there was not much to see along the way. But the tunnels made the visit very worthwhile. If you crawl through the narrow tunnels, you realize what the war has meant for many Vietnamese. I barely fit into the manhole and when the lid was put on it got terribly dark. It was a scary experience. The explanation by our guide of the many torture practices also caused goosebumps. We were happy to be able to go back to the safety of our hotel.
This place really makes you understand how hard life was during the war. A highly informative and educational experience.

Well worth the visit.  Guide was informative along the 2 hour drive. Excellent English, knowledge and a sense of humour.  Seeing the tunnels and environment provide a  realistic perspective to perceptions from books and movies.

Fomos visitar em grupo a área de túneis em área vizinha a Saigon. Lá é possível conhecer um pouco a versão vietnamita da guerra. Os túneis são impressionantes quanto ao tamanho e utilidade que tiveram. Além disso, há um stand de tiro pago para os visitantes. Boas lojas de lembranças. Recomendo.

Visiting the Cu Chi tunnels is a very popular daytrip from Ho Chi Minh city. We booked our trip through Tam Global  Travel at our Hotel in Bui Vienstreet. We were picked up at our hotel and on the two hour trip to the tunnels (with a stop at a handicraft store), our guide Rio told a lot about the Vietnamese war. The tunnels were very inventive and very narrow. You can also see some simple but effective traps and you can shoot with real weapons.

If you return the opposite, is it like saying OK once you go?Just over an hour from Ho Chi Minh City. Depends on the time of day.Now there are more activities and I don't know which is the main thing.However, the tunnel is fun to dive in.I want to wear something that I can exercise in.I get quite sweaty when I go through them all.There are plenty of souvenir shops to enjoy.

The bravery and tenacity of the Vietnamese people who fought and died in and around these tunnels is impossible to express. The U.S."tunnel rats" who fought there were also incredibly brave and I feel awful that they were conned into fighting by a misguided government that didn't recognize what was really going on. A highly informative and educational experience.
A sight of the US war in Vietnam at first hand with displays of living & working methods of the VC, the tunnels themselves which you can enter & some of the horrific booby traps.
Ovviamente se andate in vietnam sono assolutamente da vedere. Ben organizzati si riesce ad avere la percezione della tragica guerra...ahime
Had a half day tour of the Cu Chi tunnels. Managed to go through the full 100m of tunnel that was open to visitors. The guide was very knowledgable.

Great value, good courier, read about the history before going to maximise the experience. Wear or take long pants if your are over five feet tall as you will have to crawl on your knees in the tunnels

This was a tough plan to visit as an American due to the history, but it’s helpful to hear and see the other side of the ill-advised conflict. It was very interesting to see the underground infrastructure they north had...

We booked this through our hotel and our tour guide was Nim from Luxury tours. He was an excellent guide and we had a very interesting chat with him. He talked to us about his experience growing up in the Mekong Delta and how much Vietnam has changed in the last 25 years, as well as a lot of information about the tunnels, the area and the war. Definitely recommend this to visit.

Excellent way to understand how the Americans were out smarted by people who have been in conflicts and wars for over 2000 years. Very innovative in taking the fight to the Americans and developing and modifying their tactics to become victors.
Tour company and Van were great but the Cu Chi area itself is not as interesting because they have developed the “park” into a tourist attraction of sorts.

A very enjoyable trip. Fascinating history. However - not too impressed with the cafe next to the shooting range!! Firstly the choice of coffee was not good. Secondly, we needed hearing protection from the shooting range!

Very enjoyable and educational trip round the tunnels. We traveled by speedboat up the Mekong from HCMC to avoid the traffic and were guided round the history and practicality of life in the tunnels.Try them for size!!

We started the tour by watching an old propaganda movie from the early 1960s. Hard to understand the narrator but very interesting to watch. Our guide walked us through many attractions above ground. If you have a good back and knees you can also walk underground.

From a historical perspective, it is a great place to visit. In Vietnam, they call the war the ‘American War’. It was the war to ‘reunify the country’. In the 20 minute video at the beginning, they are very proud of how these people survived and held off the Americans, never mentioning the fact that the people of South Vietnam fought with the Americans to repel the North Vietnamese army. You do get to craw through some of the tunnels. If you are tall, you have to go in your hands and knees. After 20 meters you can come up, or you can continue for up to 100 meters. Give your camera to the park guide leading you for some good photos.

Wir haben die Tunnel von Cu Chi auf einer privat geführten Rundreise besucht und waren an dem weiter entfernten Teil. So waren wir fast alleine mit dem Reiseleiter. Es war nicht so touristisch aufgemacht wie ich mir das vorgestellt hatte und so war ich angenehm überrascht. Man konnte sich gut vorstellen, wie es zu Zeiten des Vietnamkrieges gewesen ist.
Amazing history and war remnants. The perseverance of the people and their stories make it even more realistic.. a must..

Amazing feat of engineering.  Don’t know how they managed to live in such small spaces.  Felt very claustrophobic and uncomfortable.  Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and held our interest throughout.  The sounds of the guns on the firing range gave the place an unreal and quite scary feel.  Would recommend a visit.
Interessant mal gesehen zu haben. Die geachichten dazu sind toll. Lustig mal selber hinein gehen zu dürfen. Die schiesserei ist sinnlos umd laut

It was a great opportunity to know the history of Vietnam. The Cu Chi exposition center is well organized and intend to get the turist to the horror of the war with an unexpected calm. The Mekong delta boat trip was nice and the sealler was a worrior waman that conducted us throughout the river with a glorious smile. I recomend a guide.

Ett spännande och intressant utflyktsmål om man är i Ho Chi Minh som man inte får missa. Man har möjlighet att gå ner i ett antal olika tunnlar av olika bredd och längd.
