Där blir väldigt trångt och varmt i den som var längst men det var värt det tycker jag. Som avslutning har man möjlighet att skjuta olika vapen. Man köper ett minimum på 5 kulor per person som kostar från 40.000 VND/kula. Så jag som sköt AK47, 5 kulor kostade det 300.000 VND

We did this attraction in around 2 hours.  It’s reasonably priced as part of a whole day excursion including a trip to the Mekong Delta.  £35 for the whole day booked at our hotel, including bus, water and lunch.  The propaganda film at the start is worth a miss.  Tunnels are interesting and nowhere near as claustrophobic as some reviews might suggest.  The shooting range, whilst a very expensive extra, was a good laugh.  Probably not great for small kids but older teenagers will enjoy. Reasonably priced food and drink available on site.  All in all well worth a visit.

Second visit here its a very interesting trip with lots of history about the Vietnam/USA war. The hardships that the Vietcong endured living in tiny little tunnels scattered allover the countryside the y lived slept cooked had medical facilities everything all hidden underground. Man traps set in lots of area to deter being found. My hubby went down purpose built tunnel made slightly larger for visitors to try out after just 60M he came out with scuffed knees and sweating somewhat.

The tunnels were interesting to see due to their use in war times.  It is a long drive from Ho Chi Minh and be prepared for the heat. I'm glad we took the opportunity to experience the Cu Chi Tunnels.

We booked a morning tour with deluxe group tours. We were some who were picked up at the hotel in a minibus. Our guide Will told us a lot on the long trip there. When we arrived we walked around and got a lot of information about how it happened during the war. Our guide Will was proficient in English and gave and much information and could answer everyone's questions. It's hot so bring water!

It was what I expected, a very one sided tour with lots of propaganda mixed in with it, the guide we had was pretty good tho, but found people in HCMC the rudest people I have ever seen....will never go back.
Visited the Ci Chi Tunnels. Guide gove a good review of what went on in Vietnam war. Cover up arms and legs if you are prone to  insect bites. Found half day tour was ample time.
An eye opening experience.  I did think you'd be able to see more when actually down in the tunnels but overall it is a must see to try and have a understanding of the time.
The tunnels are an experience not to be missed. You can also pay extra and go to the shooting range which is next door.
Estuvimos con la guía Ni, fue una experiencia educativa e interesante sobre varios aspectos de la guerra en los túneles en Vietnam. Ni explicó muy bien todos los pormenores.

A must do visit. I found it a little over stated but still extremely interesting. Our tour seemed a little to long for me to endure. Many escape tunnels to go back out and sit have a drink at many places. It would appear the tunnels have been widened for the influx of tourists but you can easily get the feeling of just what it was like. A great visit one of the must do things while in the area. Tours are not expensive and the guides are very knowledgable people.
ベトナム戦争の跡地としても有名なクチトンネル。トンネルを実際に通る事ができます。狭いながらも色々な事を考えさせられます。

Very interesting although too many people at some point and become too tourist oriented to push for buy extra stuffs . Prefer to go with  the speedboat tour rather than the bus . For a few dollars more , feel more relaxing and freshening

Fica a 90 minutos da cidade de Ho Chi Minh e mostra a vida do dia-a-dia dos Vietcongues. Há demonstrações de como costuravam os uniformes e como faziam as sandalias usadas pelos soldados. Usando pouca tecnologia puderam causar grandes estragos.

Upon arrival, we saw several postwar warplanes. I slowly saw a lot of used bullets, and the war was really terrible. There will be a short video showing how to fight the war at the time, and then you can try to enter the hole, the passage is really narrow and dark.
this location is a must in order to begin to understand the vietnamese resistance. Very good didactical stands, good touristic part of the enlarged tunnels
Our group enjoyed a lot at this place . In the Firing range it was great experience to learn how to fireAK 47 , M16 and many more

A short afternoon tour to this place in the forests where the Vietnamese built the tunnel network to face the US armies is a must do if you're in HCMC. You get to shoot some of the best military grade guns at the shooting range here - you've to pay for the bullets, ofcourse.

Đúng là phải đi 1 lần cho đáng 1 lần. Ai từng đi, phải cố gắng chui xuống từng khu để cảm nhận không khí, không gian ra sao. Rất mát, thông khí chứ không ngột ngạt quá đâu, nhưng cần người dáng thon gọn, chứ tạng người to mập quá là không nên xuống, dễ bị kẹt.

Did 3 tours in all with this company and couldn't fault any of them or the guides. The city tour I had a mini bus, driver and guide to myself. Didn,t disappoint. Went all over the city explaining everything. Took to a restaurant for lunch then several more joined to go to the tunnels. Being old enough to remember this happening made it even more interesting. How on earth did they do it and survive in those tunnels! Brilliant long day out.

Nous avons réservé un tour VIP, ça ne vaut pas les 60 usd que nous avons investi pour une visite de 2h passant par un attrape touriste (atelier de polissage ou les pierres sont vendu 3 à 5 fois le prix de la grande ville) Donc à faire mais prenez une excursion moins honéreuse.
We did a full day tour by speedboat. The tunnels are fascinating and a scary insight to the Vietnam war. Very interactive with plenty to see and do. Well worth a visit.

Virkelig spændende oplevelse. Vi havde Niem som guide og han var super god. Han var vildt dygtig til engelsk og kunne hele historien om krigen. Han kunne svare på alle vores spørgsmål og var super venlig. Vi var nede i tunnellerne og så området omkring dem samt Viet Kongs fælder og metoder. Vil klart anbefale til besøgende i Ho Chi Minh.

We did a full day tour stating with the tunnels . I amClaustrophobic so IDidn’t want to enter the tunnels themselves but still found it very interesting and informativeWe then went t to the Mekong delta and did an awesome tour of unicorn island - excellent

To fully understand the Vietnam War, you should visit the Cu Chi Tunnels to see what American forces were up against.  Visiting this place provides an up-close explanation of how a peasant army was able to defeat the world's greatest military in their war for independence.  This is a fascinating look at Vietnamese war tactics used to achieve victory.
A day trip to Ho Chi Minh City is a must. To test how they got through these narrow tunnels. How they lived underground and survived

Kyle, our guide from Saigon Happy Tour,  was like having a local friend give you a tour of his favorite place in Vietnam (Cu Chi tunnels). He was passionate in explaining the history of the Vietnam war and the crucial role of these tunnels. He was also articulate in sharing the current  Vietnamese perspective of the war, Viet culture and their hopes for the future. Excellent tour!

I can feel firsthand that there was a war going on here. The experience of going through a narrow tunnel and into a narrow hole was very valuable. However, if you are claustrophobic, please don't force yourself into it.The tunnel is really crouched down, and it is difficult to turn back once you get in because people keep coming from behind. Please be aware that some places require you to walk for a while through narrow tunnels.
Really important place to visit for anyone visiting Vietnam. It is a little bit of a drive from Ho Chi Min City, but the history and understanding makes this a worth while trip.

No trip to Ho Chi Minh City could ever be complete without visiting all or most of the attractions  associated with the struggle for, and eventual unification of Vietnam. In particular, that associated with the American attempt to prevent this from happening.In stating this, a “must see” is the Cu Chi Tunnels. A visit here demonstrated the incredible ingenuity, resources and sheer patriotism of those Vietnamese wishing to achieve the goal of national unification, and the counter-productive intentions of those opposed, as well as how each set out to achieve their goals. This attraction offers incredible factual and historical insight of a conflict which has frequently been distorted by insurgents’ propaganda. Visitors wishing to know the historical truths of this historical time, need to visit this site with an open mindedness, and allow the reality of what actually happened to be revealed. This site allows one to see and experience a revelation truth. Let this not however be the only place of such relevance be visited though.

The tour was great, we were picked up on time offered water and had a little history lesson before we arrived. It’s about an hour and half drive from the city. There were 4+ tunnels we got to experience and they were all great fun. Also had the option to shoot some of the guns from the war too at your own expense. Would definitely recommend doing the tunnels tour if you are in Saigon / Ho Chi Minh.

I learned a lot and the driving was really nice and engaging. The tunnel site is particularly instructive if you participate in the practical activities proposed by the guide. Inside the tunnels are warm and have variable widths and heights. It's a must-see to see Vietnam War movies with a new perspective. I recommend arriving as soon as it opens to avoid the crowds.

These Viet Cong subterranean hideouts are between Saigon and Cambodia.  The visitor gets a very clear idea of the daily life underground and how American soldiers could not possibly expect to be victorious in the fight with the Viet Cong that were supported by the area farmers.  One can enter and experience crawling through the tunnels.  An important site.
Great way to learn about the passion of resistance fighters to protect their homeland by taking an insightful look into the tunnel system that won them a war

Come here and let yourself be transported to the past! The place is full of underground tunnels that keep centuries of history, and touring them is an absolutely fascinating experience — mysterious, enveloping and full of emotion at every step. A historical adventure that you will not forget!

5, lockie 👍 nice tour around Cu Chi tunnel. Very energetic performance on the bus, telling Vietnam history and singing. Lockie also guided us through the tunnels, traps, and boat ride in the afternoon.

The history and the story around the tunnels is heart wrenching. It gives perspective of the human mind and it's ability to take and give pain.The tunnels are sign of human creativity and fight for survival.This place is highly recommend and only once to get into the tunnels you can appreciate its significance.
The leaders of the Vietnamese army were really smart and agile. They thought of and designed them with rudimentary tools. But it helped Vietnam avoid the enemy and avoid bombs well. Really admirable.

An amazing tour that provides insight into the Vietnam war and the challenges faced by both the Vietnamese and the Americans.  The ingenuity on the part of the Vietnamese, who had very limited resources in a conflict that many regret,  was a history lesson onto itself.

If you are going to Saigon (Ho Chi Min) it is a must. It impresses how they were organized and how they lived underground.It is striking how narrow the tunnels are, even though they are enlarged for the use of Westerners, since the Vietnamese have a very thin physique.Very interesting. Highly recommended
Our tour guide Poh was great! Fun day out. Interesting to see the tunnels and learn more about the Freedom War of the Vietnamese.

It's been a Fantastic and out of the world experience and that moment lives for lifetime for sure. It has a rich history (especially war) and heritage involved which has been showcased in a beautiful and interesting manner. The entire area is in a forest and has been used as war ground long long ago but now it has been a great experience to all the tourists who comes and visits the tunnel. Main motive is to do adventure by entering inside tunnel with lots of enjoyment, fun & thrills. Everyone visits that place with same motive which makes it more interesting. They make us even comfortable with the activity by first getting used to it with small tunnel, then little bigger etc. In the process, they make comfortable that you can very well enjoy the thrills inside. Along with so many visitors from different parts of the world makes it a real wonder. It was a full day activity and provided us with steamed special food for lunch.
The guide Nik was really good and comprehensive in telling the story of the country.Impressive to see up close how they lived in the tunnels To recommend.
Un very nice and educational experience with the guide James. A lot of insight into the impact of war on a human level, since he is the grandson of an FNL soldier
Mia from Intrepid Tours was amazing! She went back with me when I exited the tunnels by accident and guided me the full way on her own

Even if you are not a history buff, you would enjoy this.  I was a history buff, and I had grown up during the Vietnam War, so for me this was a particularly memorable experience.  This is a representative set of tunnels and other exhibits, to show how the Viet Cong hid from the US and South Vietnamese forces during Vietnam War.  You could actually go into one of the tunnels.  It is only a short one, but it is good enough for you to get a feel for what it was like.  A bit claustrophobic, but most people would have no problems, if you don't mind crawling on your hands and knees for a few metres.  Whatever your political persuasion, you cannot but admire the plucky Vietnamese, and their courage and determination in fighting against forces much stronger than them.  Now, we went as it was on the itinerary for our conducted group tour of Vietnam (with an Australian tour company,  Holiday Max), but if you are on your own, you could easily arrange a tour through your hotel in Ho Chi Minh City.
Gia đình tôi đi tham quan địa đạo Củ Chi. Thật ấn tượng khi nghe bạn hướng dẫn kể chuyện. Cũng tham quan dưới đường hầm 1 đoạn ngắn. 1 di tích tuyệt vời.

We explored all the usual tour options but in the end decided to do it all ourselves, and we could not be happier. You honestly do not need to pay for a ‘luxury’ tour and then put up with the unexpected stops at honey factories and other venues that the tour operators get commission for taking you to. Don’t accept this and arrange it yourself, it is so easy whilst saving you time and money.Here’s what you need to do:1. Get the Grab app. This is a must for anyone travelling in Vietnam.2. Book a taxi on Grab to take you to the tunnels.3. Ask the driver if he will wait for you, they will say yes in exchange for a cash ride back (you can also tip if you want to although this is not expected).4. Pay for entrance to the tunnels and for a guide provided by the tunnels site, a fantastic person chosen for their knowledge of history, communication and English skills.5. Enjoy the tunnels and see those big tour groups not getting as much one-on-one time with their guides.6. Enjoy your ride home without the additional stops, thank goodness.You can do all this for 1/2 of what you might pay on a tour and avoid all the nonsensical add-ons. You will be in charge of your own day and you can tip your driver as a way of compensating them for waiting you while you were in the tunnels. We had such an enjoyable day and were ecstatic we had done it all ourselves.

Our tour guide Jen was excellent and contacted us the evening before to confirm time and collection from hotel which she did at precisely 10am. Our driver was Lucky who did a great job managing the early morning Ho Chi Min traffic. Jen was great and very well informed regarding the Cu Chi Tunnel next work and answered all our questions . The journey was very comfortable and we were well stocked with water.The tunnels were amazing to see however we decided not to go through them however we did visit the underground workshop and hospital. We decided against participating at the firing range as it was very busy on that particular day .I would recommend wearing walking shoes or good quality trainers as the ground is very uneven and expect to get dusty/ dirty if you decide to enter the tunnels .Thanks again to Jen for a very enjoyable and informative trip

Such a fun experience! Going through the tunnels was a highlight. The tour guide was very nice and accommodating and they also gave us snacks and let us try local delicacies. Funny and tiring experience!

The Cu Chi tunnels are a must see if you’re staying in the area. Cost at time of writing is 125,000 VND per adult. You’ll probably spend no more than 2 hours here. It is without doubt best to book via an official tour company and have someone guide you. Do wear decent footwear and take water as it does get hot. We arrived by 9:30am during mid October so it wasn’t that busy, but it does get busier as the day goes on. There are information leaflets in English should you want one. If using a guide they will deal with tickets and entry. Furthermore they will provide all the information and take you around the site and through a couple of tunnels. The tunnels you go through are larger than the original but you get a real sense of what it must have been like. It’s ok, probably no more than 20 metres of tunnels but you don’t have to go underground. There is a live firing range onsite, it’s very noisy and you buy bullets for which ever weapon you choose to fire.Toilets are onsite, you can purchase cold drinks and there is a gift shop. It is an amazing place, full of history and demonstrates how the people lived and fought underground.
A very  well organised small group tour . Our guide James was very friendly and knowlegable . Thoroughly enjoyed this tour .Marisa and George

This was an experience in itself.  As we walked on the guided paths we could only imagine what it would have been like to be walking through the dense jungle with daily torrential rains and not knowing what was ahead.  Subterranean tunnel systems used by the Viet Cong to plan campaigns such as the Tet Offensive.  Underground living quarters and dining halls.  All built to hide out and attack American forces.  You can also pay to shoot different era guns if you'd like.

Went in the morning (7am pick-up from central Saigon) with Les Rives. Travelled there by speed boat up the Saigon river. Journey took just over the hour. Excellent trip, don't even think about going by road. Crawled through tunnels and saw lots of exhibits on tunnel warfare. Fascinating insights into this part of the war. Reconstructed tunnels are quite a bit bigger than the originals (which we saw), so are quite easy to get through. Firing range not included in overall price (which I knew in advance). Cost was 68k VND per bullet, with a minimum of 10, so overall cost 680K or near enough £20. I chose the AK47, which was on semi-automatic (single shot). It was OK but kept jamming. Never hit the target, but they wouldn't let me fire left-handed (that's my excuse anyway). With hindsight, the fully automatic M60 might have been the sounder choice.Overall, this was a great trip. A must-do when in Saigon. Morning was probably the right time to go, as the whole place was starting to get pretty crowded by the time we were leaving at about noon. Had some pretty good food at the restaurant (incl in price). Would recommend Les Rives.

Visiting the Cu Chi Tunnels was a fascinating experience. It’s incredible to see how people lived and fought underground during the war. The tunnels are narrow but well-preserved, and the guide explained everything clearly. It was both educational and memorable — definitely worth a visit if you want to understand more about Vietnam’s history.

Our family found the Cu Chi Tunnels both eye-opening and unforgettable. Walking through history, we learned so much together—kids included. The underground maze amazed everyone, though crawling through a section was tricky but fun! A powerful, educational experience that brought Vietnam’s past to life in the most real way.
Very cool place if you’re looking for some of the Vietnam history. We went inside and crawled in some of the tunnels they got there. A little tight but a good experience

Excellent trip out from the hotel , 13th sept 2025. The bus ride was about two hours, the tour did have a bit of walking but it’s manageable. Our tour guide Tuan Cong was fabulous, an ex army officer (aged 79) had us in stitches from the start and was very very knowledgable. It was great to have a guide who had actually been in the tunnels and knew everything. No question was unanswered, he was very open and honest. We called at a little factory set up for the people who are affected from the wars where they create the most beautiful handicrafts, the next stop was the tunnels … how the soldiers lived like that is mind blowing. There is quite a bit of uneven ground and the trail is hot and humid. There are a lot of groups but it is well managed with each group getting plenty of time for demonstrations and questions at each station. The gift shop at the exit is reasonably priced for mementos.On the way back we stopped at a lovely little cafe/ restaurant …Bamboo the food was hot and tasty and unbelievably cheap, service fast all food cooked fresh.A big thanks to Tuan he made the trip really great.

Great information provided. Great set up to show all the traps and workings of Vietnam cong. Learned so much. Great to be able to go through the tunnels. Son and husband got to shoot ak47 which they were excited about.

Exploring the tunnels and learning about Vietnamese culture is incredibly fascinating. However, if you're not physically fit, moving through the narrow, low passages can be exhausting. Make sure to bring plenty of water to stay hydrated throughout the experience.

The Củ Chi Tunnels were built during the French war (1940s) and expanded in the American war, stretching over 250 km. For nearly 20 years, Viet Cong soldiers lived and fought underground with kitchens, hospitals, and secret trapdoors. Despite US bombing and attacks, the tunnels helped them survive and defend Vietnam — a symbol of resilience after a thousand years under China and a century under French rule.Today, visitors can explore the tunnels, see hidden traps, and learn how the Vietnamese prepared and resisted one of the most powerful armies in the world.
The scale of the tunnel network is astounding. A word of warning: it's incredibly humid and the tunnels are very tight—definitely hold your partner's hand if you squeeze through!

I went with Intrepid Travel with Nina as our delightful, and highly informative tour guide.I highly recommend this tour. Whilst I have a good knowledge of Australia's involvement in the Vietnam War, this tour helped with adding some local perspective, leading to an improved understanding of my own knowledge of this terrible war.As I mentioned, Nina was fantastic and her excellent English language skills made the tour so easy to follow. Nina is a real professional.It was also nice to see that the site has not been over commercialised, which is a blight on some places of historical significance around the world.
Very nice tour. Our guide, Nic, was excellent -- very informative and fantastic sense of humour. The tunnels themselves are quite the experience -- narrow, hot and claustrophobic. Very interesting!

A very interesting and exotic visit. The guide (English speaking) was very friendly and fun, he gave us many details about life in the tunnels. The descent into the bowels of the hill is impressive. It should be noted that the tour is necessarily guided for non-Vietnamese (and therefore a little more expensive) and begins with a projection of the North Vietnamese account of the war.

During the war, Vietnam defended itself using guerilla warfare, relying on stealth and sabotage to fight a superior military power. To support this, the Vietcong had a vast network of tunnels that lay beneath much of the country. Here you can see a part of the tunnel network they used.To get to Ben Duoc, the site that hosted the tunnels, you can get a bus from Ho Chi Minh City. The public buses run past the site, so it's not hard to get there.Ben Duoc is more than just the tunnels. It's a whole tourist destination and museum, with other activities you can try, including a shooting range where you can fire various weapons.The site hosts war memorials, honouring those that sacrificed their lives to liberate the country. Near the entrance is a temple, a place where people can pay their respects.There are many displays of weapons in Ben Duoc, giving an impression of the amount of munitions used in the war. Many spent bomb cases and shells, howitzers, and disabled rifles bring you into the world of Vietnam at war, when guns were everywhere.Scattered about the site are several destroyed and damaged vehicles recovered during and after the war. Old tanks, jeeps, helicopters, and even bomber planes are on display.  They're impressive to see even in their ruined state.The tunnels themselves provide a guided tour so you get an explanation of everything as you go through the exhibition. Before being shown the tunnels, you are shown a brief movie that gives an overview of the tunnels, how they played a part in the war, and how they were used to help plan the Tet Offensive. Our guide was good at both being entertaining and informative, teaching us history and encouraging us to interact and explore the tunnels. We were allowed to crawl in and actually see and feel what it was like down there. The tunnels here have been widened and have lights installed, so they're a little easier to navigate than they were during the war, but you still get a feel for it. You will also be shown the kind of traps that the VC used during the war, as well as several mock scenes of what life was like in the tunnels.At the end of the tour we got to enjoy some local Vietnamese food while we talked about our experience. It was a educational experience, and one I would recommend to anyone interested in the history of the Vietnam War.

Undoubtedly, an essential visit in Vietnam to understand its history. It is easily reached from Ho Chi Minh in about 90 minutes.The visit lasts an hour and a guide or audio guide is highly recommended to understand everything, although there are information panels. You can try a secret trapdoor and walk through a stretch of the original tunnels.A gun range is available on the premises for an additional cost (approximately 65,000 dongs per bullet, minimum 10). This activity can extend the visit if you go in a group.

Undoubtedly, a must for any trip to Vietnam to learn about its history and an excellent option to spend the morning while staying in Ho Chi Minh, as it is possible to reach them by different means in just over an hour and a half.The visit itself lasts about an hour, and it is highly recommended to have a guide or an audio guide to understand everything, although there are several information plates that give context. It is possible to try the entrance and exit through one of the secret trap doors and also to travel part of the original tunnels, in sections of 20, 100 or 200 meters with the directions of a local guide.Within the compound is also a shooting range where it is possible to shoot with different weapons for an additional cost of around 65,000 Vietnamese dongs per bullet, having to buy a minimum of ten per person. If you are going in a group you must bear in mind that this extra will delay the whole visit.

After all has been said, and after you‘ve seen your share of Vietnam war movies…this is the place to visit to see the real thing. One has to see and experience a little bit (go through the longer tunnels….) how the population had to protect themselves and fight back against the Americans.  They had so little and paid such a high price. People who built this and all the others complexes, earn our full respect for the their tenacity and sacrifices they made.

Chu Chi tunnels in my opinion is one that you have to experience at least once if you ever get a chance. I went through the tunnels which were small and very confined. Mind you, some parts of the tunnels have been enlarged to facilitate bigger tourists, but not that much bigger in my opinion. Definitely an interesting one to do.The bus was really clean and comfortable. We went on the smaller luxury tour group and it was good value for money. The shooting range experience, as much as it was fun, was rather expensive. VND 65,000 per bullet and min. 10 bullets purchase for each person and the trigger on the gun was very sensitive so before you know it 10 bullets went flying. Expensive for less than a 2-minute experience   Linda our tour guide was very nice and friendly. The lunch was ok with basic Vietnamese/Asian food that wasn't included in the tour price. All in all, a very good tour. 👍

Great experience, lots of information on the tunnels and how the Vietnamese lived in them. Managed to fit inside the tunnels but it was said that these tunnels have been made wider for visitors. Lots of toilet locations, it does get a bit busy so go in a morning if you can. Glad we had a guide or else we wouldn’t have learnt as much as we did. The rifle range is well worth the money if you’re into shooting guns.The Mekong delta was not as good as I had hoped but it was still a worth while experience.

We crawled through secret entrances and exits like giggling spies—tight, dusty, and utterly thrilling. This historic maze stunned us with its quiet courage. One minute we’re ducking, the next we’re awed by stories of resilience.

Visiting the Cu Chi Tunnels was an unforgettable and deeply moving experience that offered us a greatl glimpse into Vietnam’s wartime resilience and ingenuity. The site is meticulously preserved and thoughtfully presented, allowing visitors to walk through narrow underground passages, see original booby traps, and explore living quarters that housed fighters during the Vietnam War. The guides are incredibly knowledgeable, sharing vivid stories that bring the history to life with respect and clarity. A fascinating part of the visit is the screening of an old North Vietnamese propaganda documentary, shown in a small theater on-site—it’s a remarkable piece of history in itself, offering a raw and unapologetic portrayal of the conflict from the Vietnamese perspective. The visit not only highlights the incredible resourcefulness of the Vietnamese people but also encourages reflection on the broader human cost of conflict. A must-see for anyone interested in history, perseverance, and the spirit of survival. We took the speedboat from Saigon to get there - and then back to Saigon.

We visited the Cu Chi Tunnels as a day trip from Ho Chi Minh City. Our tour guide took our group around and explained different points as we stopped along the way. As an Australian and knowing we had troops involved in the Vietnam war I found the stories very confronting. The Tunnels were small and there were many you could go into of varying lengths, I wouldn't recommend if you are in any way claustrophobic.  There is an option to shoot a rifle at an additional cost (must purchase all 10 rounds). As you wander through this area, having the sound of rifle shots in the background is a very surreal experience. I found this experience very intetesting and would recommend a visit to the tunnels as a half or full day tour when staying in HCMC.

This was a very interesting and informative tour. Our guide Binh was super helpful and very engaging. He provided thorough insight into the tunnels and the conditions of the war. It was very interesting and I would highly recommend.

I was worried we had added Saigon to our tour just for the Cu Chi tunnels, but after today I’m pleased to say it was worth it.  Ho Chi is quite a cosmopolitan city in comparison to Hanoi, Hue, and Hoi An.  There is plenty to do and see over a few extra days. The tunnels were interesting, extras we liked were the firing an AK 47 (we don’t have guns on our country, so this was novel) and also the forest tea and eating tapioca with a nut and sugar mix. We loved the crawling through tunnels - it wasn’t as hard or as narrow as we were lead to believe. We learnt much.  Good half day there and then back for a rest before on to more activities. Go early is my tip!!
This is a great informative tour learning about the resilience of the Vietnamese people Our guide Lee was great and really brought the tunnels to life.

definitely the most interesting excursion to do in Ho Chi Min City. It is a park full of artifacts of war, tunnels and various traps that demonstrate the extraordinary resilience of the Vietnamese people to the American invader. Moving in some parts and always interesting. We came there very early in the morning on the advice of our guide. It was a great idea both for the heavy muggy heat and because there were relatively few people at the time of our arrival. Incomprehensible to me the shooting range where some choose to shoot with real guns and spend money to go boom boom boom and play war.

The trip was very pleasant, very informative and very delightful Mr Tan answer all of the questions was asked, and after I would recommend this trip to anyone and everyone it is a eye-opener about the condition and the events of the war that people may not have heard about

our guid was called tuan he was in the war and gave us first hand account of what happened and was able to answer all our questions honestly one of the most amazing people and places to go see it has opened up my eyes i would come back again just to see tuan

Nestled beneath the tranquil countryside of southern Vietnam lies an extraordinary network of underground passageways that echoes with stories of resilience, ingenuity, and unyielding patriotism — the legendary Củ Chi Tunnels. For any traveler seeking not just a destination but a profound encounter with the spirit of a nation, the Củ Chi Tunnels offer an unforgettable journey into the very soul of the Vietnamese people.XemSoMenh.com had the privilege to explore this remarkable historical site, and what we discovered was far more than a military relic. The tunnels are a testament to the heroic determination of a people who, despite facing overwhelming odds, stood firm in their love for the motherland. During the Vietnam War, Củ Chi became a symbol of national resistance — where ordinary farmers, students, and villagers transformed into extraordinary warriors of the land.These subterranean chambers span over 250 kilometers, with hidden trapdoors, sleeping quarters, kitchens, command centers, and even underground hospitals. It is awe-inspiring to witness how the people of Củ Chi turned the red soil into a labyrinth of hope and survival. Venturing inside, one cannot help but feel humbled by the sheer willpower it must have taken to live, fight, and survive in such confined darkness, surrounded by danger on all sides.But beyond courage, the Củ Chi Tunnels also reveal the boundless creativity of the Vietnamese spirit. Every inch of the tunnels reflects intelligent design and adaptive thinking. From smoke-dispersing chimneys that kept cooking fires invisible to the enemy, to the cleverly concealed booby traps that utilized simple materials for complex defense, each element tells a story of a people who used wisdom as their shield.Walking through the narrow passages, we at XemSoMenh.com felt the powerful energy of ancestral strength — an energy rooted not just in military might but in deep spiritual conviction. The tunnels are not merely a war memorial; they are sacred ground, a reminder that true power lies in unity, courage, and the love of one's homeland.Visitors from all around the world come here to marvel at the engineering feat, but they leave with something deeper — a reverence for the Vietnamese people’s endurance and resourcefulness. The Củ Chi Tunnels are living proof that even in the darkest of times, the light of determination and wisdom can lead a nation through.As XemSoMenh.com continues to explore and share the cultural and spiritual treasures of Vietnam, we urge every soul with a curious heart to visit Củ Chi — not just to see, but to feel. To feel what it means to stand where heroes once stood, and to understand that Vietnam’s greatness is not only in her history, but in her people.

It was quite scary to see what people were going through during the war, in what conditions they lived, what threats the nature exposed on them. It is definitely worth visiting, especially to commemorate all those lost lives. Our guide Jens from Saigon Adventures was incredible and to make our experience more 'real' she has offered us to try casava with cane sugar which people had to eat during the time of starvation. It tasted awful and nearly made me sick. Those were nasty, horrible times. By the way, apparently every morning they check tunnels for cobras and scorpios as often they'd hide there from the heat.
It's not too far from HCMC and you should not miss if interested in history of this beaufiful country...................

You get to see booby traps, tunnel system , weapons , tanks and so on . You can even try different guns here . The food stall serves amazing food . Their fried rice and French fries were delicious. Definitely recommended if you’re around Ho Chi Minh City
Amazing time with Tuan! Super insightful and so nice. If you want the best tour with the best information go with Tuan!! Highly recommend!!!
Had a great time with Lockie. Great and entertaining tour guide. Brought us around and shared with us the history of the city.

A visit to Saigon, Vietnam, feels incomplete without experiencing the Cu Chi Tunnels, a famous battleground from the Vietnam War. If you don't suffer from claustrophobia, actually crawling through the tunnels is highly recommended. I did it myself, and it felt like entering a completely different world deep underground – an experience I will never forget. I also developed a deep sense of respect for the soldiers who fought from that location during the conflict.

It's truly difficult to grasp the reality of life within those tunnels based on descriptions alone. Experiencing it directly, ideally by actually walking through the tunnel network yourself, is highly recommended for a real understanding.

This is a highly recommended destination for anyone who wishes to gain a more profound and nuanced understanding of the daily realities and significant challenges faced by individuals during wartime. The exhibits and information available here offer compelling insights into their experiences.

Very interesting tour run by Saigon Adventure. Tour guide, Ken was very informed with loads of stories and the venue is really hands-on, you get to experience what it was like plus all tge "tricks" the Vietnamese had to out fox the Americans. Started at 7.30am from Ho Chi Minh city to avoid the heat and traffic (it still takes around 2 hours anyway). There weren't loads of crowds as it's low season, back at our hotel by 1.30pm

What an eye opener, television can portray things in a very one-sided way. To see the determination of the Vietnam people does give you a very different perspective. A must visit attraction for all how visit Vietnam.

Lần đầu đến Địa đạo Củ Chi và thật sự rất ấn tượng! Không gian được quy hoạch tốt, sạch sẽ, dễ tham quan. Thật thú vị khi tự mình trải nghiệm chui xuống địa đạo – cảm giác vừa hồi hộp vừa khâm phục tinh thần của người xưa. Nhân viên hướng dẫn nhiệt tình, sẵn sàng giải đáp mọi câu hỏi.

My friends and I completed this tour in May 2025. We had an amazing time thanks to our tour guide, Tuan. Tuan is a war veteran and provided us with amazing insight into the war, he was so open to questions and ensured everyone got involved.

Rất đáng để đi chơi, đi tham quan, đặc biệt có thể dẫn theo trẻ em, tìm hiểu về cuộc sống chiến đấu ngày xưa. Ngoài ra ở đây còn có những ngôi nhà xưa, có lũy tre rất to. Và mình cực kỳ ấn tượng về khung cảnh làng quê ngày xưa.

Oh my goodness, this was such an interesting tour. You get to explore inside the tunnels, see the traps that were made, you can also try your hand at shooting. We had a lovely lunch as part of the tour. It does get busy, however we were one of the earlier ones, so try to get an earlier trip to avoid the crowds. Definitely worth visiting.

Great day out. Having the chance to enter the tunnels, fire pretty much any gun you like. Seeing how the viet con fought silently, with deadly force during the war. A good insight into their lives during the conflict.

This is an outstanding place to delve into the rich and fascinating history of the Vietnam War, offering visitors a unique opportunity to learn about the events that shaped the nation. Beyond the historical insights, you can also have an unforgettable hands-on experience with the iconic AK-47, adding a thrilling and interactive dimension to your visit. Whether you're a history enthusiast or simply looking for an extraordinary activity, this destination is a must-see for anyone traveling to Saigon.

It’s definitely worth visiting to understand the experiences of the Vietnamese people as they struggled for their independence.Congratulations to the Vietnamese people on the 50th anniversary of the liberation of South Vietnam. 🩷🩷🩷🩷🩷🩷🩷

A place to learn about the horrific events of the war and how the tunnels saved the lives of the Vietnamese. A completely new experience immersing yourself in Vietnamese culture, where people are striving for survival.

This is an experience to be had absolutely because this visit allows us to better understand how Vietnamese soldiers experienced the war. You can also see the ingenuity of the latter to make life hard for the aggressors. How to imagine the construction of 250 km of tunnels in this region of Vietnam.

If you really want to understand what went on in the Vietnam War then you need to do this trip.  Incredible history lesson but also so grounding.  To imagine them living in those tunnels for years at a time.  I couldn’t even go underground for 20 metres! A must do if visiting Saigon.
Completely awesome,  such a valuable piece of history,  the gun fire really added to the atmosphere Really worth a visit!

The guide provides extensive information about the war, addresses every question, and shares his narrative with great enthusiasm. A unique and heroic historical site. An experience you cannot miss when visiting Ho Chi Minh City.

Ours was a half day tour with Kim Travel. I’m interested in the Vietnam War (or American war as it is called locally) and found it fascinating. We were given a good background and shown  diagrams of bunkers and various gory booby traps used by the Viet Cong. We were able to try getting in and out with the wooden ‘lid’ into a tunnel covered with leaves (great photo opportunity). We were able to go into a section of the tunnels (wider for tourists which narrows as it progresses - but regular options to exit) it was really warm underground. Also info on food, making footwear, Agent Orange etc. Very interesting. The sounds of gunfire from the shooting range added to the experience. Separate from the tour I was able to fire an AK 47 (10 rounds 500,000) about £15. A very interesting place to visit and understand how the tunnels were used by the VC.
We had a great tour guide with Jacky from Kim tours through the Chu CHI tunnel landscapes, you absolutely have to do them. Dieter

Try out the shooting range. Not cheap bcos 1bullet costs 60k. They told us these are live bullets. We tried out the AK. But don't expect to have kill shots or bull eye shots bcos the gun is fixed so that you can swing a limited way right and left but not really up or down.  Have some idea of where the bullets hit by the flying debris or flying soil behind the target. We ended up paying 3.6mil just for the experience

If you are in Vietnam, this is a must-see. The Vietnamese were so ingenious in creating these tunnels. They thought if everything to protect their  country. These tunnels show life underground during the war.Our private guide was a little disappointing as he presented as either not knowledgeable or couldn't be bothered. To learn, we piggy backed and listened to other tour guides talking to their groups. Our guide rushed us, which I didn't appreciate as this place needs time to take in the history and appreciate the thought the Vietnamese put into these tunnels.

A heartfelt experience!! Very moving and a must to do. Our guide was very informative through the whole day .Another great intrepid experience. Going into the tunnels is a must even if it's only 20 meters .Gives an insight into how the Vietcong lived and survived
Had an amazing trip to the tunnels. Our guide Kai was really welcoming, friendly and knowledgeable. Would highly recommend taking the opportunity to visit them.

The Cu Chi tunnel experience is set up for tourists to get some idea of what its about.  Crawling through the tunnels was a little tight but okay if reasonable agile.  Didn't want to shoot guns at the firing range but plenty of people were.  Overall a good experience

Our visit was with a pre-arranged tour guide organised before we left the UK and I don't see an option to record that.Our guide walked us through what we believe is a standard route for visitors, explaining the significance of each exhibit. Despite the crowds the visit worked well and we managed to see all the exhibits as we walked through the forest. Our tour started with a video explaining the tunnel structure. This was of relatively poor quality and with a poor soundtrack which was difficult to understand - certainly needs to be re-done.Age, poor backs, hips and knees prevented us from going down the tunnels but the visit gave us an fascinating insight into the Viet Cong fought the war and their many clever tactics. Despite our guide, we still haven't quite grasped why these tunnels and the fighting was specifically in this area - some research needed on our part!The visit is well organised and apart from the challenge of the heat and humidity was well worth the effort, enabling us to gain further insight into the history of Vietnam. We are glad we went.

Great experience.  Our guide, Tam, was brilliant.  Very knowledgeable and full of stories. Fired a fed of the weapons at the range (not cheap but it was something I wanted to do). Had a nice meal at the restaurant.  Prices (apart from the range) were very reasonable. Something very worth while doing.

Amazing adventure, really exposed you to the difficult conditions experienced by those living in the tunnels.  Also gave an insight into just how small and hot it is down there.  Not for the faint hearted.
Trip was good. Guide Hai Yen was very good and skillful managing the tour. Recommended. Everything managed perfect from history of vietnam, shooting cu chi tunnel
We took a guided tour, it was interesting, and it makes you understand the skill and cunning that the Vietnamese had during the war.
We did this VIP tour with Voyage Aventure Tour, our Vinh guide knew the history of the Cu Chi tunnels very well, which made the tour very interesting and rewarding. Very comfortable vehicle.
Interesting and fun, shooting AK47 and M16, exploring history and Vietnam war history , must visit if you got time , new experiences

Join one day tour going to Cu Chi Tunnel, very exciting and fun. The guide tell the story about vietnam so good, i can see many traps here and try to enter the tunnel, really fun! Try tapioca roots at the end the tunnel. This place also sell souvenirs.

Một địa điểm lịch sử ấn tượng, giúp hiểu rõ hơn về chiến thuật du kích trong chiến tranh Việt Nam. Trải nghiệm chui hầm rất thú vị nhưng hơi chật hẹp. Hướng dẫn viên nhiệt tình, thông tin bổ ích. Rất đáng để khám phá

Địa đạo Củ Chi là một điểm đến lịch sử ấn tượng, giúp du khách hiểu rõ hơn về cuộc kháng chiến của Việt Nam. Hệ thống hầm ngầm độc đáo, trải nghiệm chui hầm thực tế rất thú vị. Visiting the Cu Chi Tunnels with my family was an unforgettable experience that provided us with a fascinating insight into the ingenuity and resilience of the Vietnamese people during the Vietnam War. The tunnels, which stretch over 200 kilometers, were used by the Viet Cong as a base for operations, a hideout, and a place to store supplies. It was incredible to see how the network of tunnels was constructed and used in such challenging conditions.Our tour began with a brief introduction about the history of the Cu Chi Tunnels, explaining how they were built by hand, using basic tools, and without any modern technology. The guide shared stories about how the tunnels were designed with various chambers for living, cooking, and even medical treatment, showing how resourceful the Viet Cong were. It was fascinating to learn about the different traps that were set up around the tunnels to protect against invaders, many of which were still on display.As we ventured deeper into the tunnels, we were able to crawl through a section of the tunnel that had been expanded for visitors. It was a tight and claustrophobic experience, but it gave us a real sense of what it must have been like to live and fight in those tunnels. My family and I marveled at how such a small and confined space could serve as a home and a battleground for so many.One of the highlights was seeing the display of various weapons and tools used by the Viet Cong. We also had the opportunity to try some traditional Vietnamese snacks that were prepared the same way they would have been during the war.The tour was both educational and eye-opening, especially for the kids, who learned firsthand about the hardships faced by those who lived through the war. It was a somber reminder of the realities of war, but it also showcased the incredible strength and determination of those who fought for their freedom.Overall, the Cu Chi Tunnels is a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Ho Chi Minh City. It's an experience that brings history to life in a way that is engaging and thought-provoking for visitors of all ages.

This tour provides a very sobering and fascinating insight into the history, construction, layout and use of the tunnels. Thank you Thanh for helping us to understand  and imagine what life was like in the tunnels.

It was well worth the 2.5-hour trip out of Ho Chi Min City. The traffic in this city is easily the worst I have seen. The innovative nature of their guerrilla war eventually wore down the USA with the help of the Anti war movement in USA (which does not get a mention).

Great place to visit. Amazing to see how well the locals build the tunnels and how they survived in them despite the hardships. Very impressed. Keep aside a full day. Better to start early morning. Kids really enjoyed the experience especially going through the tunnels. Also keep cash handy. If you forgot you can get money out from the restaurant near the entrance. We did not take any tour. Went by ourselves. You can book tours online as well.

So fascinating to go here and hear about what really happened in the Vietnam war. The ingenuity of the Viet Cong can not be underestimated. Quote hard at times seeing the traps they set but completely understandable. Well worth a visit.

A must see see trip.Best to have a tour to fully understand how this place operated.Enemy (brutal)  traps and 200 km of hidden underground passages. You can go into a few tunnels but one is very narrow.Unbelievable how this place stayed hidden for years and 3000 Congo fighters lived here.Great trip worth doing.

I absolutely loved visiting Cu Chi tunnels. It offered a great insight into the consequences of the war and how the tunnels and terrain were used during the war. This is a must place to visit for any history lovers. Our guide, Bao, was fantastic!! He is so passionate about Vietnam and the history of the war, it was fascinating to listen to him

You can’t say that you’ve been to Saigon if you’ve never crawled through the Cuchi Tunnels. One of the most famous battlegrounds of the Vietnam War, this is the one sight apart from the War Remnants Museum that will leave the longest lasting impact on you even after you’re safely back home in your own beds.  At times, it was hard to believe that these small, dark spaces were once home to entire communities, hidden deep underground from the chaos above. These tunnels were once used as living quarters, kitchens, hospitals, and even storage for weapons. It was incredible to think that such an intricate network was constructed with little more than basic tools and immense determination.Highly recommended unless you're claustrophobic. Wear comfortable shoes because you’ll be doing a fair amount of walking, crawling- and the tunnels can be tricky to navigate. Be prepared for tight spaces, especially if you want to crawl through the smaller sections of the tunnels. The heat can be intense, so carry a bottle of water and take your time.I’d also recommend going with a guide if possible. Their personal stories and deep knowledge of the tunnels give a whole new dimension to the visit. Without the guide’s context, I don't think I would have left with such a deep understanding of how the Cu Chi Tunnels were a symbol of survival and resilience.

If you’re looking for an unforgettable half-day adventure, TNK Tours’ Chu Chi Tunnels trip is the ultimate combo of history, action, and hilarity! From the moment our guide shared mind-blowing facts about the tunnels (did you know they even had underground kitchens?) to the heart-pounding thrill of firing a machine gun and or a handgun every moment was pure gold.Crawling through the tunnels was like squeezing through a history book—tight, dark, and surprisingly exciting! Bonus points to our guide for teaching us the art of “tunnel squatting,” which I’m convinced is Vietnam’s secret fitness program.The shooting range? Let’s just say I channeled my inner action hero while awkwardly holding a gun for the first time. Loud? Yes. Adrenaline rush? 100%!TNK Tours nailed it with a perfect balance of education and entertainment. If you’re ready to laugh, learn, and maybe question your upper body strength, this tour is a must-do. Five stars and a fistful of tunnel dirt for authenticity!Thanks to our guide Peter and driver Duin who both nailed it!!!

This is a must see – fascinating visit to a small portion of the tunnels used in the Vietnam War, which although was almost 50 years ago, is still quite raw, with many of the tour guides using emotional language, especially against the Americans. Their view of the conflict provides an interesting perspective and differs from the western interpretation. However, it is the physical and practical exhibits that make this visit so worthwhile,  If you wish, you can go into two real tunnels – and for me, this made it very real as the tunnels are narrow and low – in fact so low that if you are more than 5.9’ you will need to crawl. I doubt those over 14st (196 lbs) could fit.  The main tunnel is 50m long and has exits at 5, 10 and 15 metres. Only one of our group completed it. The second tunnel at 30m is narrower, lower and darker.  That is only for the brave, however the experience is memorable and made me appreciate how effective the tunnels were in guerrilla warfare.The one downside is that this is super popular – it’s a well-oiled commercial machine, and rightly on every visitor’s “to do” list – but it does feel crowded and I felt a little “processed” – nevertheless, it’s a highlight.

Crawling through these narrow underpasses made us realize the extreme living conditions of the soldiers. Explanations of military strategies were exciting. It is a unique site that has left a profound impression on us. A must-visit for history buffs, offering an authentic and poignant insight into this period. We highly recommend!

Walking through these narrow passages, we were impressed by the ingenuity and resilience of Vietnamese fighters. Each step offered a lesson in military history and strategy. This immersion gave us a better understanding of the importance of this place in the context of the war.

We tried the Cu Chi tunnels experiment, and we can tell you that we are not about to forget! Imagine: miles of underground galleries, secret entrances and exits camouflaged in the jungle... We felt projected in the middle of the Vietnam War, claustrophobic version guaranteed!  It's impressive how they could live and fight in these conditions.  An unusual and memorable experience, even if we were very happy to find the daylight!

Wow! Our visit to the Cu Chi Tunnels was an absolute eye-opener! We were blown away by the intricate underground tunnels used during the Resistance war. Crawling through the narrow passages gave us a heart-pounding glimpse into the resilience of the Vietnamese people. The exhibits and demonstrations were fascinating, and we couldn't help but feel a deep respect for the ingenuity and bravery displayed here. It's a must-visit for history buffs and adventure seekers alike! We left feeling both humbled and exhilarated by this incredible testament to human perseverance.

Like the War Remnant Museum, this place is overwhelming, the air that you breathe in this tunnels, thinking how they were living and fighting…. Meet people still affected from the Agent Orange toxicity…. Wow…. What a lesson!!!

Beautiful experience, educative and very intense. Having the opportunity to see how they were living during the war has been emotionally intense. Our guide Vinh has been amazing, thank you Igor sharing part of your country’s history with us.

We were guided by Felix from TNK and he was quite knowledgeable and passionate about his country. He explained the Vietnam war background and the guerrilla warfare beautifully. Stooping down to traverse the tunnel gave us a real experience of some of the hardships suffered by the poor victims of war. Once again Kudos to Felix for his passion, care, and knowledge.

Cu Chi is best known for its historic Cu Chi Tunnels, an intricate underground network used by the Viet Cong during the Vietnam War. Visitors can explore the tunnels, learn about their significance, and experience a glimpse of life during wartime. The tunnels are surrounded by lush forests, making it both an educational and scenic destination.After a visit to the Cu Chi Tunnels, head to Viet India Restaurant, located nearby at Ban Dành. The restaurant offers a delightful fusion of Indian vegetarian flavors, prepared with pure vegetable oil, homemade ghee, and fresh yogurt and paneer. It’s the perfect place to relax and enjoy a flavorful meal after a day of exploring Cu Chi’s historical landmarks.

Amazing Tour Experience..!!  Our Tour Guide for the Cu Chi Tunnels Small Group Tour by KIM Travels was Ms. Thuy [Energetic/Enthusiastic/Informative]. A Mini-Bus Picked me up at my Hotel on Time and Co-ordination/guidance with customer was perfect. The Bus was good and comfortable. Tour guide was providing constant information about the History of Vietnam war/revolution all along the way. We had a stop in the middle to visit the factory where disabled families of Operation Orange victims were working. After that we reached Cu-Chi Tunnels. Initially we were shown Brief Video Documentary and later we have visited all around leisurely and had Kasava in the end which was delicious. Even though it was drizzling a bit , but we had a nice tour. During our return we were given light snacks and a wet tissue. Overall very good experience. Recommend it.

The trip was truly eye-opening, offering deep insights into how Vietnam fought and triumphed over the U.S. The underground tunnel network is remarkably sophisticated and a testament to the resilience and ingenuity of the Vietnamese people. Our guide was exceptional—he provided a thorough and engaging explanation of the history and significance of the war in Vietnam. I highly recommend this experience to anyone interested in understanding the powerful history and enduring spirit of Vietnam

Talk about a tight squeeze! Our visit to the Cu Chi Tunnels was a barrel of laughs... and a few panicked "I'm stuck!" moments. We crawled through tiny passages, giggling like kids in a playground. The highlight? Trying to spot fake grass hiding booby traps - turns out we'd make terrible soldiers! The underground hospital had us joking about "worst places for a check-up." Despite the serious history, we couldn't help but find humor in our clumsy attempts to navigate this wartime maze.

The cu chi tunnels tour was very mediocre. It felt so rushed, overcrowded, and just a lack of reading material combined with the quiet and unenthusiastic talking from our guide, it wasn’t a great experience. There was a film room, but we didn’t have time to watch it. I hate paying for tours and then getting led around like a puppy. Also the shooting range was right beside a walkway to the cafe and bathrooms, which was such a bad sight and it was so so loud and just disturbing to hear machine guns shooting 50 metres away from us. We were only there for 1.5 hours, but even if I had 2 or more hours there, I think I’d still leave dissapointed. It was just meh, and with no real reading material or depth to the tour.

To me, it felt disrespectful for the visitors to climb the tanks, have the tunnels widened for the chubby, and just have a lack of history and emotional connection to a place of war. But at the same time, the venue is more-so made for Instagram pictures, and no real history of the Vietnam war or learning to be had.

Would not recommend
I

An diesem Ort wurde jahrelang erbittert gekämpft, es gab unzählige grauenhaft Verletzte und tausende Tote. Heute ist es ein Kriegs-Disney-Land: es wurden Tunnel so nachgebaut, dass auch dicke Touristen reinpassen, Originales gibt es nichts zu sehen. Dass dort mit Kriegswaffen herumgeschossen werden kann ist verstörend und respektlos.

Don't waste your time. Took the 8 am morning tour from HCMC. Take ages to get there, partly because of the mandatory tourist stop where you get the hard sell to buy crap artefacts. The tunnel site has been Disneyfied, complete with a shooting range. It's rubbish. Visit the War Remnants museum instead.
Nhân viên bán hàng đồ ăn khó chịu với khách hàng . Không nhiệt tình chảnh choẹ

Waste of time and money

We wasted an entire day on this trip — 2.5 hours to get there and 2.5 hours back — for a tour that lasted less than 45 minutes.

The group had around 30 people, which made everything rushed and unpleasant. First, they make you watch a 10-minute video that doesn’t even explain anything properly. Then they basically just let you go into 5–6 tunnels with almost no explanation at all.

The tour guide barely explained anything and there was no real storytelling or history like there used to be.

We came here 8 years ago and it was fantastic — informative, engaging, and well organised. Now it’s just a tourist factory trying to push people through as fast as possible.

Don’t waste your money or your time. We had a horrible day and completely regret coming here.

It’s an interesting thing to see, gives you all the claustrophobic feeling and mad respect for people from the past.

The thing that we didn’t get was you pay for the entrance (expected), people infront of us got tickets just for the entrance fee and went while when we asked we were told it’s a set price all together with a guide. She didn’t let us get only the entrance so we paying 125k instead of 30-40k. But it’s okay, at least we get a guide that will tell us more about it and we can learn something new today (ha ha no).

After that we were not even told where to go or where to get a guide nothing and lady didn’t really looked like she wanted to help so we just walked up the road. While getting to the entrance to the tunnels we were stoped and asked where’s our guide we said we didn’t have anyone, so he told us to hurry up ( literally said run ahead to catch up) ahead to join another group.

That group was good 30-40 people, we had no backstory, couldn’t hear shit , and best of all 90% people went there only to take a selfie, comments made by the guide were just ‘funny’ comments ( example we are staying next to death trap and he asks a lady why the guy next to him can’t go there first - because lady’s comes first - ha ha ha, no).
It’s disappointing as we wanted to learn more about the place and the history but all we got is a funny comments and way too many people rushing to get a selfie.

For the money we paid to get a guide and the rest we just feel like we got scammed big time and it wasn’t worth the trip there.

No ir con CIVITATIS. Tuvimos la mala suerte de que nos tocara Thuan como guía.
A las 6am nos envía un mensaje diciendo que pasará a recogernos 8.15h cuando el horario era 7.45h y terminó siendo 8.30h. Entiendo que sea temporada alta pero lo podría haber avisado la noche anterior así nos organizábamos mejor. Una vez arriba del bus (con aire acondicionado defectuoso) tuvimos que recoger más pasajeros por media hora. Sería más sencillo si nos encontráramos todos en un punto. Luego, paramos otra media hora para comprar souvenirs.
En el sitio hay muchísima gente, no limitan los tours y se hace muy difícil recorrer el espacio.
Nuestro guía no explicaba bien y lo que es peor, nos apuraba en todo momento. Cada vez que hacías una pregunta, te miraba con mala cara. Sobretodo cuando le dices que no quieres pagar 30€ por disparar las armas. Si decides no disparar, tienes que esperar 45 minutos que el resto lo haga (solamente 4 personas lo hicieron de las 25 que éramos). No nos sentimos seguras cuando teníamos que experimentar dentro de los túneles porque no te explicaba nada. El conductor muy bien, muy amable.
Más allá del guía, la excursión es una estafa para turistas. No lo recomiendo, no vale la pena y menos con Civitatis, otras empresas que estaban allí se veían mejores.

The Cu chi tunnel shopping complex executives falsely claimed that bullet plane decor item will be allowed through checked in luggage. At hanoi airport it was confiscated and wasn't allowed to take on flight any way. A waste of 280VND.

Guides should inform this to tourist.
Si vas por tu cuenta no te dejan entrar. Tienes que con un tour. Si quieres ir solo tienes que ir con grab y que tu conductor te acerque hasta la ventanilla de los tiquets. Sino no podrás entrar
Vé tham quan ko đẹp

Trải nghiệm không tốt, đã tốn vé ngoài cổng. Mà còn yêu cầu khách nước ngoài trả thêm 90k nếu muốn tham quan địa đạo.
Cái gì cũng mất phí, trừ việc xem phim tài liệu.
Không mong muốn giới thiệu thêm địa điểm này cho các bạn nước ngoài.
Mị dân
Pretty much a tourist destination with little takeaways to walk out with. Would recommend visiting some of the other less-touristy tunnels around HCMC instead.

The experience was incredible. Only reason I'm giving this a 1 star is that the tourist shops there are a trap and scam if you buy anything bullet shaped like a necklace or lighter it WILL get confiscated at security in the airport. DO NOT BUY THEM! I genuinely believe they sell them to tourists to get money knowing you can't take it through security at the airport and they confiscate it there and recycle the same products back to the souvenir shops as a tourist trap to generate money. It is terrible. Apart from that the general experience at the tunnels were amazing would recommend.
歴史を知りたければYouTubeで充分だと思った。色々見たがYouTubeで見た情報しか無かった。

This place was horrible and i strongly recommend that you NEVER come here as all I received was bad food, poor accommodation and terrible service. this was the biggest waste of time and my hard earned money in my whole life.

Man sieht Tunnel und Fallen. Jedoch ist alles auf Massentourismus ausgelegt. Die Anlage ist selbst in der Nebensaison absolut mit Touristen überlaufen. Unsere Führung erzählte eine rein vietnamesische Lesart der Geschehnisse. Vom grossen Ganzen erfuhr man nichts. Zusätzlich ist ein Besuch der Shooting-Range, wo man absolut überteuert mit einem MG schiessen kann, Teil der Führung. Direkt daneben (ultralaut) befindet sich das dadurch sehr ungemütliche Restaurant.

STAY OUT! Do not go into the  Cu Chi Tunnel complex as it carries contagious diseases. People have become extremely ill after going into them and had to be hospitalized. They are hot, humid, and prime grounds for disease. Thousands go through them yearly from various countries and spread germs and disease by coughing, touching the walls and other means. Various animals also go into them carrying other diseases (bats, rats, mice)  The tunnels are not disinfected -excellent place if you want to catch COVID). People hold-up the underground line by posing for photos at the exit; so you may be stuck in them not moving for 30 minutes at a time. Please heed this advice.
ベトナム戦争を知れるがあまりオススメしない？
Tôi đi rồi bị đột quỵ
A waste of time and money. Nothing to see, is a sad mini park for children.

Honestly, I'd recommend skipping the Cu Chi Tunnels. My experience was that it was incredibly crowded, making it difficult to truly appreciate the historical significance or even move comfortably. Beyond the congestion, I personally found it quite boring. The explanations felt rushed due to the sheer number of people, and the overall experience didn't resonate with me.
Instead of spending time in the overrated and crowded Ho Chi Minh City, I wholeheartedly suggest heading straight to Phu Quoc Island. This gem offers stunning beaches, crystal-clear waters, delicious seafood, and a much more relaxed and enjoyable atmosphere. You can actually unwind and experience the beauty of Vietnam there. Skip the tunnels and skip Ho Chi Minh City.

Giá vé thu không rõ ràng, 120k/1 ng. (Nhà mình 5ng)Bạn mình là tây lấy ck Việt bị phụ thu thêm 90k( nghĩa là con bé ốm nhách mang mặt tây bị thu 210k mà không ghi rõ ràng là khách nc ngoài giá bn, chỉ nói miệng.Tụi mình toàn trên 60t, mà hỏi có đc giảm ko, thì trả lời KHÔNG. Trong khi mình du lịch khắp nơi đều đc giảm,( thậm chí ra nc ngoài,trên 60t cũng đc giảm giá.) Cách phục vụ thì thờ ơ, để tụi mình tự đi, gần cuối mới gặp 1 em trai nhiệt tình chỉ dẫn. Khoai mì ko dẻo như xưa,mà khô khốc.Đa số là Khách du lịch nc ngoài mà nhà vệ sinh đóng bợn bốc mùi( 5 điểm). Em bán vé giải thích hời hợt 5 ng chúng tôi đều ko phục.

Mucha gente y mal organizado. Ningún control del aforo. Comprar la excursión en Vietnam, en España cuesta entre 84 y 95€, en Vietnam a partir de 15€.

Trước tết, mình dẫn con đi địa đạo Củ Chi với mục tiêu cho con học lịch sử.
Tuy nhiên, tiếc thay: hướng dẫn viên chỉ dẫn đi qua loa vài điểm. Vào hầm thì chỉ cho đi vào 5m là ra luôn.
Cho nên, đi xong con cũng chả biết gì về địa đạo.
Vé thì bán mà làm ăn dối như vậy thì bảo sao dân ta không biết sử ta?!
Phim thì cũ, mờ không còn thấy gì mà vẫn cứ chiếu! :(
