왜감?

After witnessing the horrors of the war at the museum, the "shooting range" component of the Cu Chi Tunnels feels very disrespectful. I hope anyone going to the tunnels does not partake, as well as expresses that this should not be part of historical tourism.

I tunnel molto belli
Posto interessantissimo ma c è un problema....sono una guida turistica e ho portato il mio gruppo che ha comprato nel negozio.
Tra i prodotti in vemdita ci sono modellini di aerei e carriarmati fatti con bossoli di proiettili....tutte le persone del gruppo che hamno comprato questo articolo se lo sono visto sequestrare in aereoporto....!!!!circa 15 pezzi!!!!
La direzione dovrebbe avvertire i turisti che questo articolo nn si puo portare in aereo....NON ACQUISTATE CARROARMATO O L AEREO FATTO CON I BOSSOLI DEI PROIETTILI

The tourist area is worth to experiencing but the staff was unhelpful, This is a relic area that many foreign visitors come to pay tribute to the country... hope it will be changed for the better... the tour guide just done his job and quickly disappeared very poorly directions for visitors.The shooting range and ticketing are so ridiculous after we bought tickets for shooting range I have to wait at the tickets station for almost 60 minutes WAITING before someone can drive us in it was quiet no tourist, and the person who’s guiding for shooting also poor reputation, he just quickly charged the bullets then tell to shoot no explanation no guiding, another words is they just did their jobs for the day and that’s was it out.

C'est vraiment une usine à touristes, ou tu n'apprends pas grand chose et c'est fait pour que tu consommes. C'est sur, ça doit dépendre du guide. Nous avons eu un guide de chez Kim Travel. Nous avons traversé le site au pas de course on s'est arrêté à 4 endroits et pas les stratégiques, max deux minutes. De plus, il a été très agressif avec 3 papis qui voulaient prendre le temps de faire une photo. Une honte. Nous étions 25 personnes dans le groupe, on a tous été écœuré par l'attitude du guide. Quand au stand de tir, en plus d'être catastrophique pour la faune local et extrêmement désagréable. Je trouve cela sincèrement déplacé de  "jouer" à la guerre avec des armes au même endroit où beaucoup d'être humains sont mort.
Je n'ai vraiment pas aimé cette visite. Il y a tellement d'autres lieux intéressants à visiter pour apprendre sur le sujet.

Je déconseille, aucun respect de la mémoire et des visiteurs.

NO MERECEN LA PENA. Trampa para turistas. Los túneles en sí están bien. Pero … la verdad que NO merece la pena. Si vienes con excursión, son 2 horas venir y 2 horas volver desde Ho Chi Min. Si vas en bus por tu cuenta, son 3 horas ir y otras tantas volver. Todo ese tiempo para llegar, pagar y que la visita a los túneles dure unos 20 minutos. No más. Entras en 2 tuneles y recorres a penas un 20 metros, con una duración dentro de menos de 2 minutos. Cuando te das cuenta te dicen que ya has acabado (el resto del tiempo te dan un paseo por los caminos y te enseñan 2 agujeros en el suelo). El resto del recinto? 4 vehículos de guerra que puedes ver en el museo de la guerra de Ho Chi Min y el resto de zonas no están incluidas con la entrada. Resumen: entre 4 y 6 horas de transporte para una visita que dura 10 minutos. No merece la pena. Si estuviera en Ho Chi Min, por pasar el rato, pues bien. Me quedé muy defraudado por que sinceramente pensé que te dejarían más tiempo en los túneles, recorrer algo más de ellos y poder hacerte alguna foto que ni nos dio tiempo.
Además, me parece extremadamente caro para lo que es la visita (son 5€, pero es que la visita en sí es tan sumamente corta que no merece la pena). Y, por supuesto, si eres grande, ancho, etc (es decir, mas grande lo que son los vietnamitas que son pequeños), ni te pienses el venir, por qué lo más curioso es entrar en los túneles y NO vas a poder, por qué no hay espacio físico para entrar.
Y en respuesta a tus indicaciones. No es necesario 6 horas de viaje para apreciar el entorno rural. No, no es verdad que está hecho para preservar los túneles que son históricos. Los túneles tienen más de 150km … apenas se enseñan 20 metros … y el resto son recreaciones, no los túneles en sí mismos. Las visitas están creadas para que duren lo mínimo posible. La visita es ir a ver los túneles, si alguien no puede o no quiere entrar por el motivo que sea, que no vaya a verlos o que no entre una vez está allí. Pero el resto que si queremos y si podemos no tenemos por qué no hacerlo, por qué el resto no quiera o no pueda. Hay muchas formas de cuidar la seguridad. Obviamente la forma más sencilla es impidiendo hacer la actividad, de esa forma te aseguras que no pase nunca nada. Además, que escusa tan absurda! Hablas de seguridad en un país en el que la seguridad no existe! 5 personas en una misma moto, semáforos y pasos de peatones que no se respetan, cables eléctricos colgando y sueltos por todos los lados, peligro de que se te caigan cientos de cosas según andas por la calle, y un largo etc. y no se puede entrar en unos túneles en los que vivieron cientos de personas durante al menos una década? Repito, escusa absurda. Se pretende sacar dinero sin más, ya que la visita NO ofrece una visión real y no es, ni mucho menos, merecida. Ni si quiera se deberían llamar túneles. Se debería llamar recreación, que es lo que es. Y para hacer lo que habéis hecho allí (la recreación de los túneles), lo podríais hacer en Ho Chi Min, ahorráis a la gente 6 horas perdidas para nada.

Es una estafa para turistas, solo te permiten acceder con guía y este te limita el acceso a prácticamente todo, solo te dejan visitar 2 trocitos de túnel pequeños. Los túneles están en un recinto grande que dispone de distintas zonas pero NO ESTAN INCLUIDAS EN LA ENTRADA, se tiene que pagar todo por separado. Para la distancia que tienes que recorrer para venir hasta aquí y lo que te enseñan NO MERECE LA PENA. Mucho más recomendable ir al museo de Ho Chi Minh donde puedes estar el tiempo que quieras por un precio razonable y te explican muchísimo más. Además si eres talla M o superior no entras en algunos túneles, yo peso menos de 50kg y entraba justilla. No repetiría.

Если бы знала, что меня там ждет - не поехала бы. Это не те самые тоннели, а просто демонстрация для туристов. Выкопали каких то ямок, сделали из них имитацию комнат. Несколько тоннелей - опять же для туристов. Мы прошли 15 метров - очень узко, дышать нечем, и хоть у меня нет клаустрофобии - было очень некомфортно и хотелось побыстрее выйти. Показывают фильм про войну, но очень плохой перевод, почти ничего не слышно и не понятно. Можно пострелять из оружия, 1 патрон стоит 60 000 донгов. Учитывая что мы потратили пол дня на эту экскурсию - я бы предпочла по другому провести это время . Оно того не стоит.
Do not buy any of the bullet souvenirs... You will lose it in customs...

This visit was terrible. You just walk around “stops” where the guide shows you a bunch of traps and reconstitutions of tunnels with little to no explanation on how and why it was used. We learned basically nothing about the war.
The whole place just feels like a tourist trap where people smile and take photos in front of tanks with no respects of the dead’s.
To top it all, you can hear shotguns during the whole tour because there is a shooting range in the middle, where people can shoot AK47… even if you don’t do it, you will have to wait for the others of your group just besides the range.
Definitely not recommend.

What a waste of time.
It’s better to come with a tour than going by yourself. Introductions of cuchi tunnels were only in Vietnamese, only a small video in English, which is not enough information.
We did by our own and we got lost because there were no signs. There were some places that looked like abandoned tourist park.
Terrible experience.

Très décevant.
Nous n’avons pas compris pourquoi cet endroit était si bien noté.
Il n’y aucun intérêt à venir visiter ce lieu très peu authentique. Une visite qui n’en vaut pas le détour.
Seul point positif , le guide souriant et sympa.
Sik lengekn bawi

La banalización del mal más absoluta. El enfoque es turístico, no de recuperación de memoria histórica ( cómo sería deseable). Mete miedo que esto sea patrocinado por un partido comunista y por el mismo pueblo que sufrió estos horrores. Horrible,es la palabra para definir esta tontería de tour de los horrores,lleno de muñecos y merchandising

Más que un tour histórico, nos ha parecido un parque temático para entretener a los turistas. En nuestro caso, el guía apenas nos ha explicado la historia ni ha dado datos históricos, se centraba en hacer bromas para hacernos reir... Una pena ya que es un lugra muy interesante. En la mitad del itinerario encontramos una tienda con souvenirs cutres. Si tienes poco tiempo, no lo pierdas en ver Cu Chi.

La pire arnaque du vietnam !!!
Je passe sur la difficulté pour si rendre si vous ne prenez pas des excursions toutes faites et sur le prix de la nourriture sur place . Une étoile revient uniquement au souterrain qui sont corrects.

La grosse arnaque est pour le stand de tir où l'on vous propose une navette gratuite si vous prenez 5 balles minimum à 60 000vnd unité.
Si vous n'avez pas envie de payer cette sommes vous êtes bon pour 30mn à pieds si seulement vous arrivez à trouver le bon chemin du premier coup .
Une fois arrivé on vous annonce que c'est 10 balles minimum soit 600 000vnd .
Après un échange peu courtois j'arrive à négocier pour tirer 5 balles soit 300 000vnd. Aucune explication vous tirer en 10sec et ciao.
Et pour courroner le tout on nous refuse la navette au retour.

1h de marche, 5 balles, 10 secondes de tir.
Merci !
Terrible. Tan masificado que es imposible verlo bien. Un campo de tiro súper molesto en un área en la que te paran a hacer un descanso.

Worthless place. All you do is walk through the earthen tunnels like a child.

The guide in the picture had no intention of explaining anything and didn't even show us half of the Cu Chi Tunnel ruins.

He has a set salary, so he tries to slack off as much as possible.

When I asked him to show me the last place we didn't see, he suddenly lost his temper, went crazy, and refused while yelling abuse.

I think it's a shame that Vietnamese people don't value Vietnamese history.

There is a place where you can see the same thing for free. It's the Southeast Military Museum "Bảo tàng Lực lượng Vũ trang Miền Đông Nam Bộ". It's near Saigon Airport.

Visite très décevante.  Après 2 heures de voiture depuis Saigon il faut savoir que la visite ne dure que 30 minutes sur un petit parcours de 3 tunnels plus ou moins reconstitués. Le début de la visite commence par un vieux film de propagande de 1967 à la gloire du communisme... Bof Bof je m étais fie au guide du Routard et je me suis bien planté  !!
Rất tệ. Very bad !!!
I don’t recommend spending a whole day here. You don’t even see the actual tunnels or traps, they just say drive you 2 hours out of the city to see some replicas.
一般。对西方人来说可能有点新鲜感。没必要去跑一趟。
Có ai đi 2 lần 1 lần đi theo đoàn Việt Nam, 1 lần đi theo đoàn nước ngoài không nhỉ 😁😁😁. Hãy thử so sánh và cảm nghiệm đi nhé, bất ngờ lắm đấy.

Comment: Thái độ nhân viên. Nói chuyện với khách vô giáo dục. Nhân viên làm bánh tráng.

Disturbing experience to be honest. I was a bit blinded by the good reviews and indeed it is fascinating to see how clever the vietnamese managed to fight and which trickery they used. However with the masses of tourists this place has become more of a theme park for people that just come to shoot big guns and take pictures of tanks. I couldn't believe the amount of people rushing through the site and behaving like this is some funny video game, insane.

In case you come with a group tour from HCMC it is even worse. Nearly 4 hours of bus driving for spending half of the time at the shooting range. And in case you don't want to spend lots of money to fire an AK-47 you will have to wait for the others right next to it, listening to the heavy noise of constant gun fire.

If you are not a war fanatic mass tourist and want to learn something about the history of the Vietnam war, do yourself a favor and go to the war museum instead. Or watch a documentary. This one is not worth your time.

We were very excited to tour a critical warfare element for this area, but were gravely disappointed. Firstly, the cost of the tour did not include the entrance fee to the tunnels, which doubled the price of the outing. We've been very pleased with the level of information and knowledge the guides posses in the past, but that element seemed to be lacking in this tour.In general, the overall tone was an emphatic applaud to the killing and death of American soldiers. We anticipated some of this undertone, but it was over the top. The "informative" video was purely a piece of communist propaganda. Detailing how the "American Devils" were murdering the local innocent women and children. The horrors of war should be instilled in everyone, but not with a one-sided view.The historical content was low, the glorification of killing was a turn off, and the shooting range (which was a huge draw for many tourists from around the world) was overpriced and unsafe. I've advised other tourists to spend their limited vacation time elsewhere.

I have spent the last hour or so rereading reviews and wondering if I went somewhere different. What should be a hugely interesting, evocative and probably emotional experience is actually on the tacky side of a Theme Park. If you want to spend two hours travelling each way to pose pretending to pop out of one of the hiding places, to fire a few live rounds or to be able to say that you have crawled through the Cu Chi Tunnels (widened and sanitised for the Western Tourist), then this is for you. If you actually want to understand the history of one of the most amazing David v Goliath battles then you'd be better spending your money and time on a good documentary. Such a shame, I'd looked forward to this for a long while.

Chu Chi tunnels divided to 2 tunnels (different area) which are Ben Dinh (nearer and more tourist) and Ben Duoc (further away and least tourist).But either one tunnel is enough to cover the Vietnam War history, because both tunnels will give the same experience. We were brought to both tunnels and paid twice for the entrance fee.The time when we went to Ben Duoc (which has least tourist) all the staffs are not there especially English Guide Speaking. We were asked to wait 10 minutes for the guide (this is my 1st time experience that the guest need to wait for the guide, terrible!). Then, after I was complaining when 10 minutes waiting, the Vietnam speaking guide lead us in but again asked us to watch video (for introduction-which is the same video with the Ben Dinh ) which takes us another 10 minutes, just because the English Speaking Guide was not yet there. Then after waiting, finally he came, with a very bad English pronunciation, I barely understand what he was saying. He brought us with 'super-speed' tour, less than 15 minutes our tour in Ben Duoc finished. Which means the waiting time for the guide is more compare to our tour time. Very disappointing.Be prepare for insects repellent at Ben Duoc tunnels as you be having problems with 'hungry' mosquitoes, and i mean LOTS and LOTS of mosquitoes. They just won't stop.But compare to the other tunnel, Ben Duoc has larger tunnels which you can see table and chairs,  General Mohawks, etc. inside the tunnels. But just be prepared with the repellent. After the tour, while passing the souvenir shop, the staffs were caught sleeping under the tables, and lights were also switch off, and funny thing is it was just 12:00 p.m.I never seen a tourist spot that has no staffs working, or taking a break in a BIG GROUPS in the middle of visiting hours. Very unorganized!I really hope they will improved.

My wife and I went and could not wait to get out of ther

Honnestly I usually do not give Terrible review, but here you should be warned of what you can expect. If you are passionate about the Vietnam war, then go to Cu Chi Tunnels. If not you will get bored, see some holes you will find surprisin that people could have fit in but not mor ethan that and you will have teh unique chance to shoot with a gun. Visiting the tunnels was one of the main reasons we went to south Vietnam. They were very interesting to see and eye opening to hear about the war from the Vietnamese perspective. HOWEVER, it is very apparent that they hate Americans and will take every opportunity to prove it to you. The first thing they do is sit you down and show you a 15 minute propaganda type film focused on how horrible Americans are. I can understand some of this from the history of the war, but the entire movie was ridiculously anti American. It made me nervous just being there. The tour guide was extra cold toward our group, even made of show of us in front of everyone because we had backpacks on and apparently you aren't supposed to bring them into the park (we didn't hear anything about this and no one would let us leave them anywhere). The tunnels were cool although the guides continued to hassle our group, making us hustle through the tunnels (extremely hard because they are only 2 feet high). I have a bad leg from an recent accident (broke my femur a few months ago and am recovering from surgery) so this was very frustrating. After the tunnels we decided to go to the shooting range to shoot fully automatic machine guns. It is about 1.5km from the tunnels. In the heat of the day and with a bad leg this is a long ways to walk. We asked a lady with a golf cart shuttle to take us there. She refused even when we offered her money. So we began the long walk down the road. About five minutes later the same lady come cruising past us with an empty cart clearly heading to the range. We tried to flag her down and she shook her head at us. She drove past us 3 more times during the walk, each time refusing to pull over and even talk to us. We saw her laughing with another Vietnamese person when I came limping around the corner dripping in sweat. We made it to the shooting range and it was cool, although quite expensive (about $1.50 per bullet for the M60). All in all, if you are American, expect to be treated like a rat in a tunnel.

So with three weeks to travel Vietnam my girlfriend and I started from Hanoi going South. Ending in Saigon we thought the Tunnels a good day out. All we found was a western style tour of recreations and boring fakes whilst being paraded around like cattle through the uninspiring attractions. We opted for the half day tour which begins at 8 and ends at 3. With such time spent doing activity we were amazed to learn that there was no lunch included or even offered. There was many a shop and stall to purchase snacks but when you need food after not eating since breakfast Pringles don't quite cut it. The only real highlight of the trip was the history but unfortunately it was presented like a war propaganda film with the most bias I have ever witnessed! A true upset as the history is of some interest to me. Overall a terrible, commercial tour and a waste of our time. I would reccommed getting out of Saigon and experiencing the rest of the wonders this country has to offer. We love Vietnam and this has been a large waste of valuable time.Harry and Charlie

The tour here started us off on the wrong foot with extreme anti-American war propaganda that leaves you feeling uneasy. In the first 5 minutes we were left feeling unsure why we paid money to be exposed to this kind of bias and frankly, hatred of Americans. The tunnels and their history could be made interesting but the anti-American sentiment they have embraced at this tourist trap is overpowering and leaves you with a bitter taste in your mouth. Skip this waste of an attraction and find something better to do. Let's improve Vietnam-American relations, not worsen them!

Fuimos en coche privado para ir un poco más rápido y aún así fueron más de dos horas de viaje lara llegar a, parque de atracciones cutre que han montado y en el que en menos de 5 minutos te enseñan los dos únicos trozos de túneles que tienen abiertos y encima súper cortos ..creo que no merece la pena el paseo que hay que darse para llegar hasta allí y ver lo poco que te enseñan .te ponen un vídeo rodado hace 50 años en ingles inentendible y con imágenes y sonidos de la época ,te explican muy poco de su construcción y de la guerra . En mi opinión no gastaría el tiempo en ir hacia allí .espero ayudar a indecisos .

It is a personal thing, but I found this to be very upsetting. It felt like a theme park, yes, including a firing range where visitors can buy live ammunition and shoot guns. Propaganda-ist with its wax mannequins, propped up with sticks wearing dusty worn out outfits, grim reproductions of gruesome man traps, to name a few of the off-putting features.Too many people on all sides lost their lives, let them and their battle rest in peace.Would not recommend, sorry if I upset any reader.

1. Es wird kein Vietkong Tunnel gezeigt, sondern sie haben einen Touristen Tunnel gemacht vor ein paar Jahren! Der Tunnel ist etwa 50 Meter land und groß genug dass auch dicke Menschen problemlos in genockter Haltung durch gehen können. 2. Ein volles Magazin (50 Patronen) kostet 80 Euro am Schießstand! Touristenabzocke pur. 3. Der Propaganda Film zu Beginn wurde eigens für die Touristen gemacht es ist kein authentisches Material. Es ist zwar ein schwarz-weiß Film und es flimmert aber der Film wurde erst vor ein paar Jahren gemacht. 4. Ein paar Puppen von Vietkong, jede Menge Souvenir Shops und ein paar lahme Geschichten von einem schreienden Tour Guide dessen Aussprache man kaum versteht runden das Ereignis zu einer langweiligen Sache ab.Ich möchte nochmals betonen dass man dort keinen Vietkong Bunker, keinen Vietkong Tunnel und nichts dergleichen zu Gesicht bekommt. Es wurde diese Anlage nur für Touristen errichtet, das hat nichts mit der Geschichte zum Vietnamkrieg zu tun. Ich musste übrigens ca. 6 Mal den Abzug betätigen pro Schuss da meine AK 47 am Schießstand dauernd Ladehemmung hatte, außerdem sind die Waffen dort fix montiert, man kann nur abdrücken und fühlt nicht mal den Rückstoß. Geht ins Museum für amerikanische Kriegsverbrechen bzw. Für Opfer aus dem indochinakrieg, das ist echt ein toller und informativer Ausflug!
