I had been looking forward to the tunnels tour for years.People talk about Cu Chi Tunnels being just outside of Ho Chi Minh City. That is very wrong. It took nearly two hours to get there!  For some reason we were given a compulsory stop at some overpriced art factory. That didn't go over well with everyone.My experience in the tunnels at the end of my long exhausting day was 5 or 6 metres. What a waste of time, money and effort. My tour guide seemed totally reluctant to talk, and was most surprised if asked a question.This was disappointing.

ホーチミンからローカルバス乗り継ぎ（片道100円程度）で行けます。実際に行ってみると、なんでここが有名スポットになっているのかわからない。爆弾でできた穴や実際の地下トンネルがあるが、5分で飽きます。炎天下の中戦時のトンネルを再現した箇所を回る感じです。ベトナムは基本12時前後に32～35℃になります。私は熱中症のようになり頭痛がひどかったです。戦争博物館のような気味の悪い展示はありませんが、霊感が強い人は注意して下さい。私は誘われても二度と行きません。暇でしょうがなく付き合いでごちそうしてもらえる場合は射撃場のみ行きます。また実弾射撃も可能ですが、1つの銃は5発まで。AK47は5発で35,000ドン、200円くらいです。しかしながら銃は台座に固定されており、らしく構えてトリガーを引くだけになります。発砲体験が初めての人は衝撃や音等で感動できるかもしれませんが私はグアムの屋外射撃場で本格的な射撃を経験済みですので、私のような方はお遊び程度にしか感じないと思います。

호치민 시내에서 2시간 떨어져있는 터널. 보통 투어에 조인해서 찾아오는데 7달러 정도 냈음. 처음 도착하면 똥같이 오래된 선전 영화를 틀어주고, 여러 전시물과 가짜 터널을 관람한 뒤, 사격장으로 데려 감. 솔질히 얼마나 볼게 없고 할게 없으면 사격장을 만들어서 운영할까 싶었는데, 터널도 진짜 별로 였음. 사격장을 만들어서 삥 뜯을게 아니라, 터널에 관련된 박물관이나 만들었음 재미있었을 텐데

Had high expectations of this visit, but 'wax work' vietcong soldiers where you could pose for pictures was like a bad taste Madam Tussauds.  A group of mechanical munitions makers in a pit were tawdry and not at all enlightening.  Found the whole place superficial and underwhelming.

Don't bother going get a good documentary programme you'll see more and learn more from that than this experience, mannequins dressed as soldiers making bombs like a bad Santa's grotto!  Really go somewhere else you'll only regret visiting.

Great history museum with lots of real exhibitions and insights - I should give it a five out of five. I was left unsure of my thoughts about this attraction. I don't like guns or warfare. I found the story of the vietnamese people's struggle in major conflict with the French,  Japanese and finally a corrupt regime propped up by the US very interesting and fascinating - exhibites are very good. Climbing into the fox holes, crawling through the tunnels, and seeing how people lived was a good experience. However the gun range on site was a step too far for me. I didn't enjoy having to sit and wait 20 feet from people firing guns. The majority of our group did not use the gun range and some had there hands over there ears. Only one person used the gun range. For a more enjoyable experience for the majority of people either move your shop/sitting area away from gun range or move the gun range.

We went on a tour with Kim travel. Cost 300000 Dong but we found out later we had to pay an extra 90000D to enter! So basically you pay 300000D for a bus trip and a guide. The tunnels were just an area full of tourists that did not display the true size of the tunnels. I would not suggest going. Give the money to someone on the street.

We went on this trip after visiting the museum which we thoroughly enjoyed. The review that states "blink and you miss it" is absolutely correct. You are rushed along, learn nothing and the guide "John Wayne" has absolutely no interest in informing or educating. Get you through as quickly as possible and back to town to eat in his friends restaurant. Thank god we were not subjected to the "shopping" experiences, I think that would have finished us off. this place is an absolute tourist trap and what it really signifies is completely lost. Awful.

It's a theme park. These aren't the original tunnels. There's astro turf. They wont let you walk around  without a guide, and force you to sit thru the movie. The highlight is the tapioca. Save yourself a day and a long drive and do something else.

I had high expectations of Cu Chi Tunnels, but my girlfriend had read some bad reviews which I hadn't, so she was prepared. We only went because I wanted to go there.It took 3 hours to get there from Ho Chi Minh, and we stopped off at a 'Disabled Art Gallery', but there didn't seem to be anyone there that was disabled displaying their work. It just looked like a weird shed selling artefacts.When we got there, we were rushed around and I expected a bit of a history lesson, but we didn't get much to be honest. We only stopped at a few of the 'displays' for about 3 minutes each time, and there wasn't much information on hand.We then stopped at the shooting range, where people could have a go at the guns. Great for people who want to spend their money shooting, but pointless for people like me.We stood around for 30 minutes waiting for people to have a go. The tunnel itself was good, it was interesting going through it, but that was about it.All in all, a pretty disappointing couple of hours.

Quien le iba a decir a los Vietnamitas que construyeron los tuneles en el circo que se iba a convertir. El documental que te ponen....Ni me lo creia, en blanco y negro,y no entendia ni la mitad de lo que decian. Mi consejo es que con las fotos que se pueden encontrar en internet es maws que suficiente.

Don't waste your time. This is a totally overrated attraction. The only one we could write a negative review on our entire one month trip to Vietnam. Mr. Binh was a good guide, but the place didn't have anything interesting to offer. Tourist trap.
Defiantly not great for kids. It's not a good representation of Vietnam.  The gun fire is obnoxious. Don't waste your money.

Visiting itself isn't so bad, but it's actually pretty far from the city.  We booked with a tour company, they picked us up and dropped us off centrally.  Overall, not bad in that regard.  However, they make you stop at random shops.  Not good in that regard.  So, if you do go...go with a private driver or something.Additionally, the tunnels themselves were just not worth visiting in my opinion.  I'd happily choose another day in the city over it.  The make you watch propaganda videos, and were generally quite offensive throughout much of the tour.  So, if you go..keep in mind you're learning about the war from a very different perspective.  I'm well traveled, and I have no problems with that.  But, when showing kill traps and such, the staff almost seems smug about it.  It was a little disturbing to me in that regard.

You don't get to see the real tunnels at all. The whole experience is a made up affair with pretend tunnels dug by the locals. At the beginning you watch a communist positive video aimed at the vietnamese of the time. Talking about how many "enemy" Americans they had killed during the "American War". Did not enjoy. You walk about a predefined track with your guide. His story telling ability was terrible. "this is a tunnel. Do you want to go in?" or "this is a kitchen do you want to go in". Even the pits where they had the "kitchens" or "meeting rooms" were completely dug just for tourists and decorated with animated manequins. They try to upsell you to make money from the shooting range as everyone is forced to walk into it on the trail. Barely anyone else walking about and I now know the reason why. A waste of money this experience, I actually learnt more just from reading the Lonely Planet Vietnam book and was telling the tour guide more information than he seemed to know about the tunnels.

I was so excited to see the Cu Chi Tunnels, but it turns out these are not the real tunnels, rather all replicas. Even the replicas of the traps used in the war are poorly done concrete and astroturf versions of the real thing. Complete with creepy mechanical dummies performing irrelevant tasks like cutting rope, this place is a strange, fake world that I didn't need to drive two hours to see.The tunnels themselves have been rebuilt to fit big western tourists, and claustrophobic as they are, they are not the size nor the material of the originals.The place is jammed with tour groups and cluttered with opportunities to sell things (soda, souvenirs, shooting range, etc.). It didn't help that our guide spoke terrible English and kept making really bad jokes, and doing demonstrations when half of the group hadn't caught up to him yet.I wish I hadn't wasted my day here. It felt like such a tourist trap.
The most hostile country i ever been to. Full of locals trying to take advantage of tourist. Over rated tourist destination.

Without a doubt the worst tour I've ever been on.   The main entrance point were you pay didn't have credit card facilities, that was surprising, they didn't have multi-language directional signage, they didn't speak any english, which I can understand as it is Vietnam and it's their Country. However, without the signage we were left not knowing were to go, eventually we found our own way to the tunnel and found a guide who looked completely disinterested. He took us through the tunnel and then at the end just walked off. Once again we were lost and spent half an hour trying to find our way back to the bus. Unbelievable, I would not recommend this tour.

While this is advertised as a tunnel tour, you barely spend an hour of the whole day at the tunnels!  Take out 6hrs travelling to/from Saigon (not very great distances, just damn slow traffic), an hour for a very ordinary lunch (at an expensive restaurant) and 1.5 hrs to visit the Cao Dai church doesn't leave much time for tunnels. There is quite a bit to see (which we didn't as no time on tour) at the tunnels complex.  The highlight is to go into a tunnel but the best they offer is a significantly enlarged 40m of tunnel that you (foreign tourists) can walk thru bent over.  No insight into the size of the real tunnels. You can drop into a pit through a typical tunnel 'trap door' which is quite small but surprisingly all who tried it did fit!  You had to sit thru a 15 min propaganda film about how the locals beat the mighty USA by hiding in tunnels (ironically these locals now all have iPhones and use Google). A quick look at some human traps set to kill and maim soldiers. Quick look at some pits that replicate the kitchens, meeting rooms, weapons workshops, etc that existed in the tunnel complex (hard to imagine what they would have been like underground).  Half way stop at a shop for drinks, souvenirs and if inclined to buy 10 M16 or AK47 bullets for $20 and shoot them off from mounted guns (you just have to pull the trigger).  If possible do the trip by private tour and you will see and do a lot more (compared to the bus-load of 40 people we travelled with).

This is a perfect example of a tourist trap. Everything felt fake (I'm sure because it was) and just    wrong.The gun range (aka cash cow) at this location felt disrespectful to me considering how many thousands died at this location.  Having to wait for others in your tour group to fire guns before you can continue the tour mid way through is ridiculous. (I like shooting guns but not in this context)I expected to learn a lot about the significance and history of the location but that was glossed over.  I learned more information on the wikipedia site, but that might be the fault of my tour guide.  The main reason I wanted to go on this tour was to check out the tunnels but that was a major disappointment.  The tour guide said the tunnels zig zag to confuse 'tunnel rats' but the ones you go in are for the most part straight and you can't go very far."Get your camera ready" guide turns on animatronic Viet Cong soldiers. Guide activates an example of a booby trap "Who wants to try? Hahaha." Ugh.  So tacky, so disrespectful.  How is this attraction is so highly rated on Trip Advisor is beyond me. Do yourself a favour and skip this tour and spend some time at the War Remnants Museum.The only positive was it was relatively cheap as we paid $13 US/275K Dong at a tour reseller by our hotel which included bus, English speaking guide and entrance fee.  The choice is yoursa) do this tourb) skip this tour and use that 275K Dong towards about 8 local beers, 2 bowls of Pho, 6 Banh Mi sandwiches and 6 hours of your life not wasted.
Funny its look like a different location as was back in 1993,  surely Im not the only one who seen  this ?   clearly fake,  but why Tunnels are real, just not the real ones that was in use,

I hate giving such an important part of history such a low rating but I was left horribly disappointed and frustrated by the whole day. With limited time in HCMC, I had to prioritize my sightseeing and this was top of the list.What a waste of a day. Our guide, as they all seem to be, was a loud and crass man who went by the nickname 'John Wayne'. He made offensive and inappropriate comments the whole day, until I eventually walked away just to escape the grate of his voice. The drive to the tunnels is about two hours and was made even longer by an unscheduled stop at an arts and crafts workshop, where victims of Agent Orange craft and sell the wares. While I appreciate the cause the visit was not on the itinerary, was supposed to be twenty minutes and ended up being an hour and the sales people in the attached shop were horribly aggressive. The actual tunnels were an incredibly short visit considering the entire outing took seven hours. We saw some booby traps (accompanied by more awful jokes), sat covering our ears next to the random and expensive shooting range and then crawled through ten meters tunnel. The entire 'tour' was wrapped up by a grainy propaganda video. It was a boring day and an expansive one, at $22 a person NOT including entrance. Plus our group was about thirty people, so getting close to any of the displays was more hassle than it was worthwhile.

Our tour of the tunnels was with a group of about fifty other people (two large buses full) and we had only one guide. As a result of this crowd, we couldn't see properly or hear anything the guide was telling us, so it was a waste of time. All of the tunnels we saw were reconstructions anyway, not the actual tunnels used by the Viet Cong. Coupled with the propaganda film we were made to watch and the unscheduled stop at an arts and crafts shop on the way there, it made for a very disappointing day. Would not recommend.

Abbiamo speso una mezza gionata per visitare le gallerie dei Vietcong...totalmente scongliato, siamo rimasti veramente delusi. Sembra di and are in un parco giochi in cui e tutto ricostruito; dei tunnel origin air non c'e' praticamente traccia. Durante il percorso sono stati ricostruiti alcuni esempi della vita dell'epoca (la fabbrica di armi, quella di scarpe, esempi di trappole, esempi di tunnel,...). La parte più interessante  riguarda la mostra di armi e quella con le foto/ video dell'epoca. La parte peggiore e' il campo al termine della visita dovi si possono provare le armi..
It's not true, these all are new tunnels. All the tunnels had been destruction during the war 1972 & 1974.

Gisteren via de receptie in ons hotel een tripje naar de welbekende Cu Chi Tunnels geboekt voor 10 dollar pp. Let op: dit is exclusief de toegang tot het tunnelcomplex. Toegang kost 90.000 VND. Touroperator: AntourOm 7.30 uur zaten we klaar in de lobby van ons hotel. Paar minuten voor 8.00 uur werden we door de reisleider opgehaald en gingen we in de bus. Vervolgens een hele ronde door de stad met diverse pickups om nogmaals bij ons hotel twee mensen op te pikken. Vreemde ervaring. Met een volle bus vervolgens op pad, begint onze reisleider zn verhaal. Slecht Engels begint ie een zielig verhaal dat zn vader heeft gestreden in de oorlog voor de Republic waardoor hij nu niet voor de overheid kan werken en dus maar gids is geworden... Op de heenreis die slechts een uur zou duren werd mij drie keer gevraagd naar de wijze waarop we hadden geboekt en hoe we betaalden. Ik vond 1 keer wel welletjes dus heb m twee keer genegeerd alsof ik Vietnamees doof was. Omstreeks 11.00 uur kwamen we aan bij een sociale werkplaats waar slachtoffers van Orange door overheidsgeld een soort van bezigheidstherapie krijgen in de vorm van kunst bedrijven. Ligt absoluut heel mooie kunst bij, maar de prijs staat niet in verhouding tot het werk. Een jaarsalaris in Vietnamees geld voor een potje voor op de schouw gaat toch wel wat ver... Na een half uur door met de bus naar datgene waar we voor mee waren gegaan: de tunnels. Opnieuw een vervelende ervaring met onze reisleider. Toegang tot de tunnels kost 90.000 pp. Dus we zouden 180.000 moeten betalen. Ik had alleen Amerikaanse dollar bij me. Omgerekend zou ik zo'n 8.50 dollar moeten betalen. Wil die snotaap me 20.000 Vietnamese roebels terug geven. Boefje. Dus de hele bus werd voorzien van kaartjes, maar ik moest dan maar even zelf een kaartje fixen. Geen probleem, want ik spreek gewoon Engels en daar kun je doorgaans een eind mee komen. Dus kaartjes gekocht en gewoon netjes wisselgeld teruggekregen zoals dat hoort. Eenmaal binnen in het 'attractiepark' wordt je als soort kudde vee voort gedreven waarbij inderdaad een soort van slechte propaganda wordt gemaakt voor het communisme. Schandalig slechte film met een soort van documentaire over de tunnels. Bij mekaar geraapt zooitje. Vervolgens ga je met de hele groep een rondje lopen waarbij het tempo er goed in zit. Je krijgt een opgejaagd gevoel en de verhalen die worden verteld slaan echt helemaal nergens op. Het lijkt een doel aan zich om je zo snel mogelijk te frustreren waardoor je flink wat geld wil uitgeven om je even uit te leven op de schietbaan. Daar betaal je een slordige 40.000 VND per kogel en minimale afname is 10 stuks. Het ware leven in de tunnels blijft zwaar onderbelicht. Even als de tactische gedachte achter de tunnels en hoe dit heeft kunnen functioneren en hoe dit is gebouwd. Helemaal niks geen achtergronden... Na de schietbaan mag je even de tunnel in... 20m 40m 60m wat je wil. Geen ruk aan. Tip: bezoek lekker het oorlogsmuseum in Ho Chi Minh (3e kwadrant). Daar kun je naar binnen voor 15.000 VND en dit is zeer de moeite waard. Daar wordt wel het verhaal verteld en daar spreken foto's en word je meegezogen in de trieste verhalen van slachtoffers. Wij wilden uiteraard net als iedereen ook de tunnels van nabij zien. Achteraf een desillusie: slechtste excursie die wij hebben meegemaakt. Jammer.

Von den Tunneln habe ich mir einiges erwartet. Doch die Realität war sterbens langweilig. Wir haben eine Tour gebucht, ich beschreibe folgend den Ablauf unseres Tages.8.15 Uhr - Wir werden früher als verabredet abgeholt9.00 Uhr - Endlich sind alle Tourteilnehmer im Bus, 27 Leute.9.05 Uhr - Der Guide fängt an alles zu erzählen was er weiß.  Alle sind genervt. Der Guide versucht auf witzige Art die Stimmung zu verbessern,  es misslingt.9.45 Uhr - von der anstrengenden, Achtung Ironie, Dreiviertelstunde erholen wir uns 30 Minuten an einer Raststätte und werden gebeten überteuerte Fliesen zu kaufen10.15 Uhr - obwohl alle Touristen um 9.55 Uhr wieder im Bus saßen fahren wir erst um 10.15 Uhr weiter11.00 Uhr - Wir erreichen die Cu Chi Tunnel, fast!11.15 Uhr - wir betreten das Gelände und sehen uns zunächst eine vietnamesische Reportage an. Das friedliche Leben vor den Angriffen wird dargestellt, ehe der Ton rauer wird und die Bomber kommen.11.35 Uhr - Uns wird ein Loch gezeigt und alle 27 Touristen können ein Foto von sich machen lassen, nachdem die Gruppe vor uns damit fertig wird.12.00 Uhr - Wir bekommen 3 Puppen gezeigt die unter einer Plane hocken. 12.10 Uhr - Wir bekomme  verschiedene Fallen gezeigt. Der Guide versucht witzig zu sein, es misslingt.12.30 Uhr - Wir stehen vor einem auf 120m Länge vergrößertem Stück eines Tunnels und können uns hindurch zwingen. Der Enge war beeindruckend. Ich als 2m Mensch konnte teilweise nicht im Entengang vorankommen und musste mich wie eine Spinne fortbewegen. Man bekommt einen guten Eindruck, wie bedrückend die Zeit gewesen sein muss.12.35 Uhr - Wir können an den Schießstand. Meiner Meinung nach ziemlich harter Stoff, dass dort zu veranstalten.12.45 Uhr - Wir bekommen ein Stück Süßkartoffel und einen Schluck Tee.12.50 Uhr - Wir bekommen 10 Minuten Zeit Souvenirs zu kaufen.13.00 Uhr - Abfahrt!13.05 Uhr - Der Guide macht ne Ansage, er merkt, dass alle tierisch enttäuscht sind und verzichtet auf einen Witz. 1. Es hat sich nicht gelohnt, die eigene Fantasie und oder eine Reportage geben einen besseren Eindruck als die Tunnel.2. Einen Guide kann man sich sparen. Die Anlage ist so gestaltet, dass jeder Mensch mit mindestens drei Erbsen im Hirn eine Führung geben könnte.3. Der gesamte Pfad ist mit Touristen überfüllt.4. Wenn ihr noch nen Tag überhabt, tut was gutes, lasst euch massieren oder geht essen. Aber ein Besuch der Tunnel verschlechtert nur die Laune!

Cu Chi tunnels themselves are an interesting 3/4 day trip from HCMC, we booked a tour through our hotel as we didn't have time to do research on a good company.We went with An Travel on a huge bus. At crowded tourist sites we often walk past huge groups of tourists with a loud, unfunny, rude guide on a megaphone and shake our heads. This time unfortunately, it was us.Our guide was unbelievably inconsiderate, obnoxious and disrespectful to the point of awkwardness for the entire group. To start the tour off he apologised for being late and gave the reason as "I always have such a small breakfast, we are too tired - I bet you all had a big breakfast didn't you?..." Only went downhill from there. He insisted on screaming bullet and explosion sounds through the loudspeaker for minutes at a time, even combining the odd scream of american soldiers or the dying whimpers of an army dog. Then proceded to laugh at his "jokes" while 40 people just stared at him.As other reviewers have stated, the guides then laugh as they explain how the booby traps mutilated and killed the americans, even turning them into "lady boys" before inviting us to step into a pit full of spikes, Really was a charming guy...The final straw was when we had to sit right beside ear shattering gunfire for 20 minutes while Rambo hotheads shot the hell out of a pile of dirt for $1/bullet. We just walked off... Why they think having a shooting range is appropriate at a place which suffered so badly  is unknown to us.With a mentally balanced guide and a bit more sensitivity shown (not asking for them to give both sides of the story, that obviously isn't going to happen) this could be a really worthwhile tour. At the moment, we definitely wouldn't reccomend it. And as for the documentary at the start - just laughable. Get rid of the disgusting shooting range!!

Although we were very interested in the history, we had come to actually spend some time in the tunnels to get the real feel of what had happened there. We were really disappointed that we only ended up being able to actually spend a few minutes in them. Apart from that, the tour was taken up showing displays of mines on plastic grass, a captured American tank and the opportunity to shoot an AK47 (extra charge).  While the history of the place was interesting, I got exactly the same info reading the one page leaflet. Waste of an afternoon.

This should be a must-visit place but sadly the tours to the place offer very little information to the tunnels and deliver it in such a shoddy way that it is off putting. I didn't meet a single person who thought the tours put on to the tunnels were worth a go.The tour starts by taking you to rest stop on the way to the tunnels - it is a 90 min drive, we don't need to stop off anywhere. Plus the stop off point is not on the way. The coach drives you out there and then drives some way back before heading to the tunnels. Once at the place you are herded round like cattle. We did meet one group who's guide was in the war and had experienced the tunnels first hand - he offered some great insight and stories. Our guide wasn't a veteran and seemingly only had 3 points about the tunnels he repeated over and over again. May be if you go on your own to the place, or even visit the sight at the other end of the tunnels where I've heard it is less touristy then the visit could be interesting. But at the moment the ship them in, ship them out approach of the tours from Ho Chi Minh are a waste of money. Asone person said to me, "watch a documentary instead, you'll learn more!"

Double pricing in Vietnam is already something not reasonable and not tourist friendly at all but in Cu Chi tunnel, it's much more than double pricing!!! Local needs to pay 20K for entrance ticket and foreign tourists pay 90K = 20K ticket + 70K service fee. The ticket seller could not explain which kind of services they offer to foreign tourists she just says if you are VNese then you do not have to pay for that service fee, but if you are foreigner,  you have to pay. I am a Vietnamese, and really I feel frustrated about this kind of ridiculous pricing.

While the history that this location embodies is significant, its presentation is terrible! The entire thing is one silly propaganda, lacking in heart or thought, completely touristy, and really really tacky! The fact that visitors can shoot AK47s for a buck a shot mocks the history of loss and death that occurred here. What a shameful way to honor or remember a war that took the lives of so many. The place seems like a cheap tourist trap and certainly not worth the time and effort it takes to get there and back.

Echt nicht sehenswert! Komplett durch formatierter Massentourismus. Der einzige Moment indem der Besuch gerechtfertigt ist, ist das gekrieche durch den Tunnel. Das ist aber auch das einzige das an diesen Ort noch einen Eindruck von den Chu Chi Tunnels gewährt.

Vi besøgte Cu Chi tunnels - og for at være ærlig så er det slet ikke turen der ud værd. Stedet er fuldstændig overfyldt med turister, og der bliver lukket alt for mange mennesker ind. Det er nedslidt og de tusindvis af mennesker gør at det kun bliver værre. Stedets medie center er en almindelig fladskærm - hvor man sidder op mod 200 mennesker på rækker og kigger på den. Man kan intet ses. Alle de steder der er noget udstillet er overfyldte og når de skriver at man har mulighed for at gå/kravle i nogen af tunnelerne så er det 20 meter i rumpetten på den næste turist.

No doubt, the Cu Chi tunnels are a great attraction ! Unfortunately you just spent about 5 minutes in or near the tunnels, the rest of the trip was an anti-american and pro-communist propaganda. The visit starts with a 15 min movie of propaganda that says that Cu Chi was a "peasful place" with a lot of peaceful people that were invaded by the evil americans and that the communists from the North came to rescue the country.It was really pathetic. Our guide told us that it was a fight between Justice (the democratic North) against money (the non-communist South).

After visiting the Cu Chi tunnels here in Vietnam, I found it necessary to write this review.I really do NOT think it is worth a visit. It costs 90000 Dong, almost 5 USD, which is for S-E Asia quite a lot and the 'tour' is just about 30 min. Apparently, this money includes a guide but my guide spoke hardly any English and if another tourist wouldn't have told me to join their group, I probably would still be waiting now.Furthermore, they put you in front of a cheesy propaganda movie that takes 15 min, and after that you climb on hands and knees through 3 different, but very short parts of the tunnels. Then they give you a taste of a certain root that the villages used to eat there and that's that.Coming with the local bus from HCM, it took me 2,5 hrs to get there and again 2.5 hrs back. And it was certainly NOT worth all this effort.... wish somebody else had written this review and I would not have lost my time there. Maybe the legend is bigger than the actual site, but to me it was a joke.

I felt 30 years back in time, being brainwashed by pure communist propaganda. Of course, there has been something like tunnels to hide during attacks, but definitly not to the extent they want us to believe. It is simple not possible, so this is a tourist trap of the highest degree!
Alles wat je er ziet is nep (inclusief de tunnels), hordes toeristen en de film is zeer zeker niet objectief.

Vanuit Ho Chi Minh City ben je een dag kwijt voor een kleine 2 uur bij de Cu Chi Tunnels.Bij aankomst krijg je een filmpje te zien dat wel interessant is echte dit zou je ook thuis op je laptop kunnen bekijken. De volledige informatie is niet objectief en eenzijdig. Alles en dan ook alles is op het toerisme gericht en Niets is origineel!!!Wat ons betreft niet doen!

This was by far my worst experience in Vietnam. It was a waste of 6 hours that I will never get back. I went with Authentic River Cruises. The tour company was professional except our guide. They provided food and drink on the boat which was good. The bad thing is that there is nothing authentic about the river cruise. It is a little over an hour each way on the boat. Cu Chi tunnel experience was awful. It was filled with propaganda and not much real history. I got a much better education on the internet after visiting. It is nothing more than a tourist trap with a lot of poorly done exhibits.Save your valuable travel time and money and see Ho Chi Minh City. If you still go after ready this I will tell you "I told you so"Please do not go!

WARNING. PLEASE DO NOT GO INSIDE THE BEN DUOC CU CHI TUNNELS.We want to make everyone aware that the Ben Duoc cu chi tunnels are incredibly dangerous. We visited recently and went down a tunnel where our friend was attacked by a scorpion and died in the tunnel. We want to make this so clear to everyone that going in these tunnels is not safe in the slightest. There was no warning as to whether there was anything lethal in the tunnels and the guide did not inform us. The scorpion simply attacked our friend as he was retreating away from it. Our friend neither provoked it nor was attacked by being ignorant of the danger.For the sake of not going through what we did please do not go to Ben Duoc cu chi tunnels. Please flag this to stay at the top so that future visitors are still highly aware of the dangers.

What a load of bat guano.  From the visit to the cheesy  tourist trap on the way to the opening film strip filled with hatred, lies an pure propoganda to a tour guide more interested in making poorly worded jokes about killing your spouse/girl friend or double entendre's, this tour was a farce.  I came to see the military history of a valient and noble soldier and people who fought overwhelming force with little more than picks and shovels and antiquited guns (I am retired military).  I have the greatest of respect for these people and what they did -- but what did we get -- an opportunity lost by the Vietnamese people who run this shabby faux Disneyland.  Propoganda film from the 1960's vile and hateful, misrepresentation of both sides, cheesy faux tunnels built for the westerners fat butts, and poorly done representations of the battle site.  These people should be ashamed - it is an opportunity lost to honor those patriots who fought here.  Read a book in the newly opened Starbucks, Burger King or KFC in Saigon (what were these people fighting for again?) and understand the history before going.  I hesitate to recommed this but it is like a bus wreck - you just can't look away.

I cannot for the life of me understand how anyone thinks the Cu Chi tunnel park manufactured for tourists is a highlight of Vietnam/Saigon. The history of the tunnels is clearly fascinating, but the tourist site fails in almost every way to capitalize on its potential. You begin with a shabby propaganda film and a brief overview of the tunnel system. It's downhill from there as you're led through a series of intertwining paths with tunnel 'exhibits' and decrepit mannequins dotted throughout. All of the tunnels you will be shown have been created specifically for tourists using mostly concrete and have nothing to do with the actual tunnels. While I understand that the real tunnels are far too small and dangerous to allow tourists to enter, the recreations are lazy and depressing. At the mid-point of the tour there's a shooting range where you can pop off a few AK-47 rounds (US$17 for 10 bullets) but if you have no interest in shooting (or you enjoy shooting but have no desire to pay the extortionate price to do so) then you have to sit for 15 minutes by a souvenir counter while incredibly loud gunshots rattle your brain. Buy a book about the tunnels, find a nice coffee shop in Saigon, and read for a few hours. You'll learn a lot more and enjoy yourself more.

I completely don't understand why people think this is a good place to go. You're better off reading an article on Wikipedia about the tunnels and skip the tour. The bus made an unscheduled stop at a souvenir craft factory first and the place was filled with bewildered tourists waiting to get back on their buses. The tunnels themselves are a very small part of the tour and we were constantly rushed along by our guide giving noone time to really look at anything and also losing a few people along the way who eventually managed to find us right at the end. The guide had no interest in checking to see where they were and just said they could join another tour, astounding. The 15 minutes allocated for shooting guns leaves those who aren't interested in that to sit in another souvenir shop while trying not to lose their hearing over the loud repetitive fire. A definite low point of my SE Asia trip.

We booked a half day tour through the hotel with "seat-in-coach" tours who are run by Saigontourist Travel Services. Firstly the price in the brochure was 525,000 Dong person but the hotel told us the price had gone up 5%.  We travelled to the tunnels in a very nice air-conditioned mini-bus and oure guide Son was quite informative on the way there but as soon as we got to the site he spent all his time telling us to hurry up and saying we did not have time to look at certain things.  One particular exhibit he said not to look at as we had "already seen a body trap".  In fact we all went to look and it was an exhibit showing how the tunnels were built.  He said it must have been changed recently which was clearly not the case.  We were the only English people on the tour, the other 3 were not primary English language speakers and he got very annoyed when they asked questions which he said "he had already told them".  His English was quite good but even we had difficulty understanding him some of the time so it would have been much more difficult for the others.  He kept saying "I have already told you that" and "are you not listening".  He was so rude it really ended up as being quite funny.  Other groups with guides seemed to be spending a lot more time explaining things and showing other exhibits.  He said the company did nopt allow enough time for it but that was not our fault.  We left the hotel at 8.20 am and coulod easily have left earlier. In short the actual tunnels were very interesting but this particular tour company and guide were awful and expensive.  My advice is to do the Tunnel tour but not with this coimpany

Don't waste your time.  It will take you half a day for nothing.  Fake, not well maintained, probably government-operated given the poor attitude of the staff.  Awful quality & propaganda video at the beginning.Avoid!!!!!

If your thing is to celebrate ingenuity then Cu Chi Tunnels is the place for you.  Beware its an hour and half to two hours outside of town. Nice but rather long drive.  I was expecting a self guided walk though the tunnels.  The entire trip consists of 5 minutes in the tunnels which if you are a westerner will be just about all you can stand.  Keep In mind the tunnels have been "widened" for us...and they are still tiny, very enclosed and dark.  The remainder of the hour or so, the tour takes you through the various activity rooms; kitchen, uniform making, gun and bomb making, shoe making room.  And the sick part of the tour, examples of how we killed our enemies.

Rip off tourists. Entrance fee : VN dong 20000Observation tunnel system: VN dong 20000Video: VN dong 20000 (not needed, the place has no air conditioning, it was hot like hell.Guide:  VN dong 30000 (not needed)Total of VN dong 90,000.00 If you chose all, but you have no choice, because you can't read and speak Vietnamese. They took advantage of it and charge you VN dong 90,000 It is very common in Vietnam, that vietnames took advantage of tourists. Video is not necessary-Documentary Channel does much better job than this old and scratchy video was held in a hot, humid shed.The tunnel is designed for small people. For those that weigh 65 kilo or more, you go there to see the hole from the ground. It is hot, humid, dirty and dark. Boogie traps that is no different than the one in movie.Most tour guides were terrible, and cheat. My tour guide collected entrance fee of VN dong 90000/person and said that it would be cheaper and easier for him to buy in group.  Everyone in tourism business want a piece of it, it is unorganized. Itinerary seems fun and interested, but it was a fool. There is very few side walk for pedestrians, most of these areas are occupied by motorbikes, stands that sell goods and food.There are very few traffic lights. You may get killed by motorbike when crossing street.There is no such thing that driver and biker yield pedestrian.This is Vietnam, toilet is filthy, most places has no toilet paper.There is no such thing is priority for wheel chair folks or children.There is no such thing is wheelchair access.You always worry about safety, pick pocket, and bargain. They raise the price twice, some even three times more than normal price, because they can tell that you are tourist.It is not honest business at all. They eventually hang themselves. Because I wouldn't recommend my friends and families visiting Vietnam . I left Vietnam without buying anything. I don't care about the souvenir because I don't want to support this kind of business.I can list 100 things that make you feel sick in your stomach.Overall, I had terrible experience in Vietnam. I wasted of my money.

We went there because of the good reviews at TripAdvisor and I was interested in the Vietnam War. We went with a half day tour group (very well known tourist company) and we couldn't be more disappointed. The bus trip took almost an hour (view was great though), and upon arrival we saw nothing but tourists. We were expecting some "hundreds of kilometers" of war tunnels that remained to this date what they looked like in the war. What we got was worse than a film set. Everything is touristy. We sat through 15 mins of badly made video about the tunnels. A freshly painted tank model had 30 tourists waiting to take photos standing next to it - The War Museum would be a much better place to be if you wanted to take photos with tanks. A "tunnel entry" had a nicely paved road leading to it with some sign and again, 50 tourists trying to get in there to take photos. You get my drift. The whole experience was ruined by the way they presented it and the sheer number of tourists around.The tour guide spoke broken English and took us around the place that took about an hour, but most of the time was we had to wait for the other people to finish taking photos, shooting weapons, etc. Then the bus back to the hotel - another hour wasted. We spent the next day cancelling all similar day trips, but wandered in the local districts and streets of Ho Chi Minh city, that turned out to be a much better experience.

This was the worst day of my holiday and such a waste of time. It is incredibly touristy and feels like a strange theme park rather than a place of historical significance. Our guide knew very little about the tunnels. The gun range is so disrespectful and ALL the group have to stop here to wait for those who want to fire a gun - it is very unsafe and loud. In Saigon we also visited the war memorial museum which is so much better and explains the impact of the US war on the Vietnamese rather than making it into a 'fun' attraction.

A few holes in the ground of highly dubious originality, if you want to do this without 50,000 others get a cab and get here very early. Although close to Saigon takes well over an hour due to it almost being a suburb. Some of it is interesting but the movie and most of the boards are largely propaganda and horrendously translated (this was actually the highlight, my favourite part "small cute peasant farm girl American killer hero "). Vinh Moc are real tunnels (DMZ), if you want to get the shirt and say you've done it go along, otherwise don't bother.

We found a visit to the tunnels a waste of time and money. You wander round a woodland for half hour looking at dummies dressed like Vietnam Cong and get to crawl through a tunnel for 100 metres.  It takes half a day, which we felt was a long amount of time for a third rate attraction. You get no sense of the history of the place because it is clouded my the constant "kill Americans" chant.

The tour started badly with an un-advertised trip to "Handicapped Handicrafts" for some emotional blackmail and extortion.  The place itself, whilst having what might have been some of the genuine set-up from the war (though probably wasn't) was for the most part historically inaccurate and clearly set up to harness the tourist dollar.

L'attraction locale ce ne sont pas les tunnels de Cu chi mais plutôt les hordes de touristes qui ne font que dénaturer le paysage en hurlant à tout va, en se coinçant dans le trou à photo parce que trop gras... Très pathétique tout ça, les guides eux mêmes sont complètement désabusés ...J'y suis arrivé avec mon amie ... on commence par une video sur une télé et on se suit à la queue leuleu pour voir les différents pièges utilisés par le Bon Vietcong contre les méchants Américains ... pour une fois, on plaint ces derniers tellement les pièges sont cruels et fait, non pas pour tuer, mais pour faire souffrir... bien entendu, la propagande du site occulte cet aspect. Certaines choses sont intéressantes comme l'ingéniosité des combattants à masquer la fumée s'échappant des cuisines des tunnels. Enfin, l'attraction principale, qui dure moins de 5 minutes, est la visite d'un tunnel ... on est très déçu car ce n'est qu'un bout ridicule situé à moins de 1 m sous terre et long d'une cinquantaine de mètres ... il est clair que ce n'est que pour les touristes et que ce n'est pas un tunnel d'origine !Ce site est à éviter, certains tunnels près de la DMZ dans le centre sont parait-il bien plus intéressants !

Like the War Remnant Museum, this place is overwhelming, the air that you breathe in this tunnels, thinking how they were living and fighting…. Meet people still affected from the Agent Orange toxicity…. Wow…. What a lesson!!!

Beautiful experience, educative and very intense. Having the opportunity to see how they were living during the war has been emotionally intense. Our guide Vinh has been amazing, thank you Igor sharing part of your country’s history with us.

We were guided by Felix from TNK and he was quite knowledgeable and passionate about his country. He explained the Vietnam war background and the guerrilla warfare beautifully. Stooping down to traverse the tunnel gave us a real experience of some of the hardships suffered by the poor victims of war. Once again Kudos to Felix for his passion, care, and knowledge.

Cu Chi is best known for its historic Cu Chi Tunnels, an intricate underground network used by the Viet Cong during the Vietnam War. Visitors can explore the tunnels, learn about their significance, and experience a glimpse of life during wartime. The tunnels are surrounded by lush forests, making it both an educational and scenic destination.After a visit to the Cu Chi Tunnels, head to Viet India Restaurant, located nearby at Ban Dành. The restaurant offers a delightful fusion of Indian vegetarian flavors, prepared with pure vegetable oil, homemade ghee, and fresh yogurt and paneer. It’s the perfect place to relax and enjoy a flavorful meal after a day of exploring Cu Chi’s historical landmarks.

Amazing Tour Experience..!!  Our Tour Guide for the Cu Chi Tunnels Small Group Tour by KIM Travels was Ms. Thuy [Energetic/Enthusiastic/Informative]. A Mini-Bus Picked me up at my Hotel on Time and Co-ordination/guidance with customer was perfect. The Bus was good and comfortable. Tour guide was providing constant information about the History of Vietnam war/revolution all along the way. We had a stop in the middle to visit the factory where disabled families of Operation Orange victims were working. After that we reached Cu-Chi Tunnels. Initially we were shown Brief Video Documentary and later we have visited all around leisurely and had Kasava in the end which was delicious. Even though it was drizzling a bit , but we had a nice tour. During our return we were given light snacks and a wet tissue. Overall very good experience. Recommend it.

The trip was truly eye-opening, offering deep insights into how Vietnam fought and triumphed over the U.S. The underground tunnel network is remarkably sophisticated and a testament to the resilience and ingenuity of the Vietnamese people. Our guide was exceptional—he provided a thorough and engaging explanation of the history and significance of the war in Vietnam. I highly recommend this experience to anyone interested in understanding the powerful history and enduring spirit of Vietnam

Talk about a tight squeeze! Our visit to the Cu Chi Tunnels was a barrel of laughs... and a few panicked "I'm stuck!" moments. We crawled through tiny passages, giggling like kids in a playground. The highlight? Trying to spot fake grass hiding booby traps - turns out we'd make terrible soldiers! The underground hospital had us joking about "worst places for a check-up." Despite the serious history, we couldn't help but find humor in our clumsy attempts to navigate this wartime maze.

It was a unique glimpse into Vietnam's history and the ingenuity of the Viet Cong. Our guide's insightful commentary brought the experience to life. A fascinating and humbling experience that's a must-do in Vietnam!
It was an interesting hike with my family. We were accompanied by a very knowledgeable Guide who made the trip interesting. Route to do with the Guide possibly...

I drove there with a scooter, Google Map might show you the backside of that sp you should ask the locals around,it was fun when we passed the robber plantation and not too challenging drive.It was amazing to see how the Vietcong got so creative to fight against American and South Vietnamese army,the staffs were helpful and lovely,you bought the tickets and there would be a guide working there show a big group around so it's totally easy to do the tour by your own without any tour agency

Drove from Ho Chi Minh City with an individual tour and a Russian-speaking guide. With two kids - an adult daughter and a teenage boy. Everyone was wondering, in general the place is certainly done now as an entertainment complex. So it's very important to listen to the history of the Vietnamese guerrilla struggle, just to go and see without a guide makes no sense
Cu chi tunnels are really worth a visit. We got a lot of information about the war. The sailing on the Mekong River was really an experience

The trip to the Cu Chi Tunnels were great. Our tour guide, Long, was very friendly and told us a lot about the history of the place. The communication and pick-up was also well organised. Highly recommended!

Crawling through the Cu Chi tunnels was wild! Seeing the ingenious traps for enemies firsthand was incredible, though we're glad we visited during peacetime. Let's just say we're grateful for our spacious above-ground lives!

The Cu Chi Tunnels is more than just tunnels - it is a large tourist attraction. To get the most out of your visit it would be best to go with a tour guide. This where, during the Vietnam-American war, Vietnamese hid from the Americans and from where they ambushed the Americans. You can go through the tunnels themselves. Its not too taxing, you can go for 20, 40, 60 80 or 100 meters. I would describe going through the tunnels as a stooped walk rather than a crawl. You can also see how the Vietnamese lived underground, the traps they set for the Americans, how they camouflaged the tunnels, etc. There is a film at the start that provides some context. There is also a firing range where you can fire M16s or AK47s. You have to buy the bullets. I would say that this is a must see experience if you are staying in the nearby Ho Chi Minh City.
The communication system was impressive, those traps for enemies? Yikes! We laughed, we ducked, and we marveled at the ingenuity.A must-visit for history buffs and thrill-seekers alike!

Visited the Cu Chi tunnels with this company and had the best, most memorable time. Our tour guide Viet was so charismatic & passionate in telling us the history of the war in Vietnam & gave us detailed accounts of how the Vietnamese people utilised the tunnels during the war. He showed us all of the hidden gems. Thank you so much!

An amazing trip, especially down to our tour guide Viet. His charisma and enthusiasm made the trip from start to finish. He told us about the history during our way there using real life examples and explained everything in a really interesting way during the tour. The best guide ever!!!!
Những cái bẫy booby traps và bẫy cho kẻ thù khiến chúng tôi vừa sợ vừa cười. Đi cùng người yêu, chúng tôi đã có những khoảnh khắc vừa hồi hộp vừa thú vị.

We were blown away by the sheer scale and ingenuity of this sprawling network. Crawling through secret entrances and exits, imagining life underground during the war, was both humbling and exhilarating.  The authenticity of the site truly brought history to life. A must-do when visiting Vietnam!

We explored the Cu Chi Tunnels, a testament to the ingenuity of the Viet Cong’s guerrilla warfare against French colonialism. The narrow tunnels and hidden charmers revealed the harsh realities of war.A must-visit for history enthusiasts, offering a glimpse into the region’s complex past.

Crawling through those narrow passages brought the reality of Vietnam War and the resilience of the Vietnamese people to life. It’s a powerful reminder of their strength and determination. A must visit for anyone interested in war history!

The secret network of tunnels, used for guerrilla warfare, was both intriguing and educational. We were amazed by the underground hospital and living quarters. This visit offered a unique glimpse into history and resilience.

Talk about an adrenaline rush! Exploring the Cu Chi Tunnels was like stepping into a time capsule of the Resistance War. Crawling through the underground tunnels, we were amazed by the ingenuity and resilience of the Vietnamese people. Secret entrances, hidden bunkers - this place is a must-see for history buffs and adventure seekers alike!
Awesome experience!!!! Definitely a must see when in Vietnam. Going down into the tunnels, we can see how miserable the ancestors were in the past.

A visit to the Cu Chi tunnels is a real eye opener. It is an amazing experience. The tunnels were dug up by the Vietcong soldiers to keep themselves hidden from the enemy and also to launch surprise attacks. Hundreds or thousands of Vietcong actually lived deep inside the tunnels which acted as living quarters, sometimes for a month or more, and those inside could not surface as it was too dangerous. Just think of the logistics living in there! And they managed it. There are many tunnel holes throughout the area which are barely big enough to fit a very slim person through!  Also there are examples of many types of deadly traps designed to catch unsuspecting enemies. There are also various types of workshops and the like. Quite a learning experience!

Great day out to visit the historical cu chi tunnel. The actual tunnel you could do a 100m walk within it, a bit small. Not recommended if you are tall. Bring mosquitoes repellent as you would be in a rainforest’.

I cannot express my feelings and was amazed to see how  Vietnamese survived during the war . These tunnels speaks volumes about the struggle period how they battled both with survival as well as war . It shows each and every living Vietnamese contributed their part during the war . It was truly a great learning experience

Extremely busy, they sell way too many tickets. Its starts with an old film of horrible picture and sound quality. After you look at some some tacky looking replicas before heading to the shooting range area. $25 USD for 10 rounds. They have a another souvenir area and a small snack bar serving low quality food. After we saw the tunnels that were dug around 10 years after the war, they are just more replicas. If your don't like small space or have back/knee issues, don't go in them. Tourist trap kind of a place....

Ожидали немного больше. Сама экскурсия не очень интересная, предварительно посмотрели документальный фильм, гид рассказывал тоже самое, нового ничего не узнали. Единственное что понравилось: сам тоннель, можно прополсти его, но он не длинный. Сувенирный магазин, обед, можно пострелять, но только из 1 оружия, манекены. Мы дольше ехали, жаль, что потратили время на эту экскурсию. Ехать целенаправленно сюда не рекомендую.

Purtroppo con cosi tante persone non si riesce a vedere e apprezzare nulla!
Me lo aspettavo molto meglio
Centinaia di bus arrivano continuamente!
Aspettare 15/20 min per vedere ogni singolo reperto o tunnel per via delle tante persone
Spari di fucili e mitra assordanti
Penso che sia la loro fonte più alta di guadagno visto che chiedevano 40usd per sparare
Brutta esperienza

Tolle Einblicke in die Tunnel, aber man sollte wirklich sehr klein sein.
Wirklich krank ist, dass man raus kommt und erstmal gegen Geld schießen kann...

I encountered a guide who was very doctrinaire. A few holes in the ground, a few bomb remnants, and he kept repeating that HCM had given them freedom. I understand the atrocities of this war and I am a staunch opponent of it and the US, but you can't be so doctrinaire.

Fui con get your guide y es lo que hay. Trampas de la guerra de América y como eran los túneles de la guerrilla. El restaurante no vayan, pedi Una pizza 100.000D y me dieron una mini pizza, ni en un plato sino en la caja donde viene del super, cruda y los ingredientes fríos. Luego unas papas fritas que se le olvidó mandar a pedir y acabaron trayendo 5 papas contadas. Horrible
Sehr touristisch und von den originalen Anlagen sieht man nichts. Fahrzeit lohnt sich nicht

Von Ho-Chi-Min-City fahrt ihr einfach 2,5 Stunden mit dem Bus. Grund für die lange Fahrtdauer ist der Dauerstau auf den Aus- und Einfallstrassen. Dafür könnt ihr dann ca.2 Stunden durch das Gelände laufen. Ich würde die Tour nicht wieder machen. Die Dauer der Busfahrt rechtfertigt den Besuch in keinster Weise.

Triste que un lugar con tanto valor histórico esté tan sobreexplotado por el turismo masivo.
Si realmente te gusta la historia y quieres disfrutar de una visita tranquila, este no es el sitio adecuado.

Aunque en internet parezca que se ofrecen visitas guiadas personalizadas y en grupos reducidos, eso es mentira. Pagas por una experiencia “exclusiva” de unas 10 personas, pero en realidad dentro del recinto te cruzas constantemente con grupos de 30 o más. Es imposible disfrutar de las casas, los túneles o cualquier explicación sin sentir agobio o prisa.

Además, da la sensación de que gran parte del turismo que visita Cu Chi lo hace simplemente porque aparece como sitio recomendado en las guías de viaje, no por un verdadero interés histórico. Si eres un apasionado de este tema, probablemente te sentirás fuera de lugar: la mayoría de la gente parece estar allí solo para tachar el sitio de su lista, no para comprender lo que realmente significan los túneles.

Mi consejo, para quienes de verdad quieran conocer y entender los Cu Chi Tunnels, es claro: no vayas en grupo.
No pagues por una visita guiada rápida en la que apenas puedes ver nada por la cantidad de gente.

Ve por tu cuenta, sin guía, tómate tu tiempo y, si lo consideras necesario, paga por el audioguía.
Reitero: si te apasiona la historia y buscas una experiencia auténtica y tranquila, evita las visitas guiadas masivas y las falsas promesas de tours “personalizados”.

Aun así, no quiero que nadie deje de ir por este comentario.
El sitio en sí es impresionante, de un valor histórico incalculable y totalmente digno de visitar.
Solo quiero que la gente sepa qué se va a encontrar realmente y que valore bien cómo organizar su visita para poder disfrutarlo de la mejor manera posible.

Leider viel zu viele Menschen. Trotz gutem Guide konnten wir nicht viel mitnehmen. Geschmackssache ist auch der Schießstand, an dem jeder Besucher gegen Bezahlung mit Waffen aus dem Vietnamkrieg auf Atrappen schießen kann. Das nimmt der tragischen Geschichte etwas die Ernsthaftigkeit.
Dennoch beeindruckend bzw. bedrückend: die Tunnelsysteme. Wir waren nur für 20 m im Untergrund und konnten fühlen wie die Vietkong vor nur 50 Jahren dort unten fühlen mussten.
Du lịch quá tệ

È tutto molto turistico e le guide che ci sono non spiegano bene. Inoltre la foresta che esisteva prima fu distrutta ed in seguito l'hanno ripiantata per cui anche la foresta non è più quella di una volta che doveva essere molto più fitta. Interessanti le trappole che venivano usate e una parte di tunnel per capire le dimensioni anche se sembra siano stati allargati per permettere ai turisti di entrarci dentro.

This place is interesting and I could spend there all day. We also had a nice guide.

BUT!
It was so crowded! People everywhere, you couldn't even take a picture of something without people in background or around. Everything was rushed, because people are queuing and wanting for you to get out. It absolutely killed the vibe of such an interesting place.

Pay extra and try the other tour where they promising non tourists trip. Because it was terrible experience.
Also, I know it's hot in Vietnam but go for the afternoon tour. Our tour guide told us in a peak season up to 4k/day people will come for morning tour and only 1000/day people for an afternoon, that's a massive difference!!

That's what I would recommend to my friends and also to you!!

There is no one to guide you properly..
They just sell the tickets at the counter and after that they have no responsibility.
Also you do not have the option not to pay for the guide.
You have to compulsory pay for the guide.
Guide price is almost 3 times the ticket itself

Not worth the journey here and back to HMC. The whole park can be summarized as weird mannequins and one tunnel you crawl through for 5 minutes.

Just go to the war remnants museum for a way better look at the war.
Sad to see how they have turned a war site into an amusement park for tourists to shoot guns. Found it inappropriate.
Vreselijk toeristisch, op paar authentieke details na nep. Gidsen halen je over te poseren voor tanks en te schieten met een AK. Niet heengaan

Hơi thất vọng so với tưởng tượng.địa đạo có vài m.đi 1 mình nên thiệt thòi chút.ngày thường ít khách mà mấy bạn nv có vẻ căng thẳng,hối thúc như chạy giặc.tham quan xong không còn tâm trạng chơi nữa về lun

The attractions/sights were stunning to view, and the tunnels were extremely fun with people enjoying themselves whilst going in to experience them.

Unfortunately, many of the employees working in the shooting range seemed very uptight, and unwilling to work. The shooting range was empty with no people at the time we arrived, the hostility and rushing felt very unwelcoming.
khu di tích lịch sử rất là đáng để trải nghiệm nhưng thái độ dịch vụ của chuyên viên rất khó chịu khi du khách có việc cần cách nói chuyện rất không thoải mái với du khách

Die Geschichte der Cu Chi Tunneln ist natürlich sehr interessant und erstaunlich wozu Menschen in der Lage sind. Es ist ein kleiner Teil für die Besucher offen und man kann es sich in etwa vorstellen wie heiß und eng es ist, die haben sogar Schulen und Krankenhäuser dort gehabt wie Städte.

Großer Nachteil, viel zuviele Leute. Es freut mich für Vietnam das der Tourismus einen Aufschwung erlebt aber das hat alles leider den Beigeschmack einer Massenabfertigung und verliert somit den ganzen Charme.

Bedenkenswert finde ich es das nach all der Geschichte die ein Land erlebt hat und das ist noch gar nicht allzulange her das die dort doch beinhart einen Schießstand haben, das wäre wie (und ich bin Österreicher) wir bei den Gedenkstätten (und unsere Geschichte ist länger her, und nein ich will nicht damit aussagen welche Geschichte tragischer ist) einen Schießstand aufbauen am besten gleich im KZ!

Aber ok wo keine Nachfrage da kein Angebot welches ja eher ein Armutszeugnis der Gesellschaft ist, finde ich es wäre wichtiger sowas mit mehr Respekt zu behandeln, aber ja jeder hat so seine Meinung

Expected more.
Very touristic place , you see and walk a bit where are the tunnels and inside and thats it.
If you want to shoot it’s really expensive.
If i knew I wouldn’t come.

Nichts für Leute mit Klaustrophobie

Man muss sich pflichtmäßig ein 15min Propaganda Video ansehen, wie böse die Amis waren und wie toll die Sowjetunion
Also sehr prokommunistisches Gedöns
Aber an sich interessant, das mal zu sehen

Ciekawe ale i straszne miejsce - okropności wojny opowiadane z uśmiechem i pewnoscią siebie zwycięzców. Do tego dziwna "świątynia" z cho cho minhem zamiast Buddy. Ale przede wszystkim, pod zadnym pozorem, nie bierzcie poczestunku, oferowanego za darmo w przerwie trasy - ten kawalek gotowanego manioku, wziety z ciekawosci, kosztował WSZYSTKICH zwiedzajacych z mojej grupy o ostry rozstrój kiszek. I bylo to bakteryjne - pomogł dopiero lek antybakteryjny, a wczesniej była ostra biegunka, gorączka, a ja nawet zaliczylem w samolocie utratę przytomnosci w drodze do wc... Maniok ma ciekawy smak, chetnie sprobowałem, ale zdecydowanie lepiej bylo znaleźc go na jednym ze streetfoodów, a nie "za darmo", w panstwowym muzeum...
Nesplnilo ocekavani... kdyz clovek navsteve vynecha tak neudela chybu...
A tourist trap!

Sheer waste of time. More than 1000 tunnel which makes no sense. Small hole in the ground with no strategic importance as of today is showed with such charm as if great thing happened there. Old times they had war so it was important but now does not make sense. Can be subjective but see before you visit.

Die Tunnel von Cu Chi zu sehen, war sehr interessant. Man konnte sich ein Bild davon machen, wie beschwerlich und entbehrlich der Kampf gegen die Amerikaner war. Wir hatten zu fünft eine Privatführung. Andere hatte nicht so viel Glück. Denn: Leider war das Freilichtmuseum sehr überlaufen und es kam mir teilweise nicht sehr authentisch vor. Im Hintergrund hörte man dauerhaft lautstarke Maschinengewehrgeräusche, weil sich hier auch eine Shooting Range befindet…das ist für mich sehr unpassend. Wo durch den Krieg doch viele Menschen sterben mussten.

Assez déçu, c’est très touristique et cadré.
La possibilité de tirer (avec de vrais armes) sur cet ancien lieu de guerre est très étranges surtout après avoir fait le musée de la guerre et quand on sait les atrocités qu’il y a eu ici.
Cependant guide sympas mais sans guide il n’y a aucune explication.

It was a good experience to know about how the Vietnamese soldiers… but please don’t buy any souvenir related to bullets as Inspite of confirming with the shopkeepers the souvenir keychain which had bullet replica was taken back at the security check at Saigon airport … unfortunately we had bought couple of them and lost the money for it . So please be mindful of the souvenirs you buy from the shops there .

As a Malasyian who is traveling to Vietnam, this is a amazing place to visit in HCMC. As you can learn a lot about the Vietnamese war, we managed to see different type of traps used by the Vietnamese to attack their enemies we got to crawl down the tunnels and learn different methods the Vietnamese used during the war. That is just some of the places you can visit at this place however, in the suvernior shop we bought a plane and tank made with bullets but it was taken by the immigration at the airport and we feel like they should not sell this to the tourist as most people who buy the suvernior would bring it back to their home country.

Selvom det er vigtigt at forstå krigen fra guerillasoldaternes sider og deres pinsler, er dette ikke et sted jeg vil anbefale at besøge.
Det er en lang tur derud og der er for mange turister. Det eneste bemærkelsesværdige var at man kan kravle i tunnellerne brugt dengang.
Man kan vælge at skyde med våben, men det koster 60000 dong pr skud, og man skal købe mindst 10, så det løber hurtigt op.
Hvis ikke man vælger det, skal man stadig vente på andre fra gruppen, der skyder. Man venter lige ved siden af skydebanen, så man er ved at blive døv af at stå der. Eneste gode er at man kan købe drikkevarer til lave priser.

Is a weird place, at the beginning you watch a 20 minutes history about the tunnels where bearly you can heard what the commentator says (sound is terrible), later you see some old tunnels entrances and try to get in one trap to take some pictures. The tour continue showing different info about how people used to live in this area about feed themselves, live underground and clothes they worn. At the end there is the stranger thing, a shooting target field, very crippy to be honest and annoy at the same time, because there is a souvenir shop, toilets and a rest zone just beside the field where people shooting old weapons as if the war still there(?). If you are sensible to the loud sounds, need wear the safety headphones they provide and keep walking till the entrance of the 100m tunnel, which is not for everyone but was not to bad.
By the way, there are plenty of guides who only talk is about propaganda of Vietnam and zero facts about the war.

Ticket price is 35000 VND for tunnels & 90000 for local guides. Total 125000 VND per person.
If you wanna try shooting with real guns like AK47, pistols, etc, it's 60000 VND per bullet.
This place over hyped. Just a couple of tunnels you can crawl in. Yes the history is rich but nothing great as a tourist spot. Just a few tunnels are opened. A souvenir shop, Cafe & that's it.
We drove 55 kms from HCM City & the parking is also a about a km away from the entrance.
Not a very great place to go to... 5/10
Lịch sử hào hùng của dân tộc Việt Nam, của đất thép và con người Củ Chi.

nice but there is not much to do, not for non athletic people, my limbs hurt for weeks after

Franchement ça ne vaut pas le coup. Premièrement c'est loin de Saigon, la route est périlleuse, 2h aller, 2h retour et vous avez déjà perdu 4h. Le parc en soit est bof. On vous fait payer l'entrée à part, les tunnels à part et le village de réunification à part.
Entre la distance du parking/entrée et le début des tunnels est au moins à 15 minutes de marche sportive.
Franchement si vous êtes à Ho-Chi-Minh pour maximum 2 jours, ne perdez pas votre temps à aller là-bas.
Allez plutôt au musée de la guerre dans la ville qui est vraiment intéressant.
Tourist trap. Just 50-100m of tunnels made for tourists. It's ok if you want to take picture-two and that's it
It is very hypes.nothing much is there and ticket price is really high as compared to what they are offering so far from HCM city

Was okay to have seen it once. Walking through the tunnels and seeing how it was back then is impressive. But there is not much around it.
The price, by the way, is 125,000 dong, tour guide is included but with moderate English and not much info, just leads you through the tunnels and explains every few meters what tigers or no idea.
On the rest of the plant speaks as good as no one English. Caution at the shooting range, you may not hold the gun itself, is mounted in front, which takes the whole fun.
Even with Google translate you do not get on here. A bit of a shame but well finally everyone must decide for themselves whether it is worth a full day with bus rides and in tunnel sightseeing.

After the tunnel tour, you should explore the area yourself. There are far more places to see, but people usually wave it off and think that there are only the tunnels.

Đường vào thì xa, mà ko có bản chỉ đường rõ ràng gì cả, đi nhầm mấy lần mới tới lối vào. Vào rồi phải ngồi xem phim tư liệu giới thiệu cả buổi mới có hướng dẫn viên lại dẫn đi tham quan.

See a veeery little part of the tunnels, like 40m. a cheap 70's propaganda video, and 5 or 6 traps for the troops.

A fascinating insight into the 'American War'.  The conditions these people lived under were incredibly tough but they managed to survive. The fighting spirit shown and the cunning tactics and knowledge of the landscape was what made them so succesful in combat. If you want to get a better understanding of what happened during the war you must visit the tunnels.
Mặc dù đã xem ảnh và video nhiều nhưng lần đầu thăm quan trực tiếp không khỏi xúc động và ấn tượng với những gì người chiến sỹ giải phóng đã làm.

We’re a family of 5 with 16, 14 & 11 yr old kids. This was a great tour of Cu Chi tunnels. We went via speedboat which made the 1.5hr trip there more interesting. Our tour guide was fantastic with good English, funny and a good pace for kids. He gave us all the info we needed at a relatively fast pace then left time at the end for questions so we weren’t bogged down by info at every stop. His name was Mr Chi - I’ve included a photo of his card - you should ask for him to guide you! It is incredible to see how they lived in these tunnels and what both sides had to contend with in the war (traps, tunnels, jungle warfare). A must do in Saigon!

It was an interesting trip but it can be noisy and graphic. So not very suitable for children younger than 7. Educational and interesting but do not purchase anything with bullets as you will need to throw them out when at baggage scanning for international flights.
Did half day tour excellent tour very knowledgeable guidegreat insight and Tuan is a great guide.Highly recommend this .

The tour was made most exciting by having our tour guide Mr Tuan from Vietnam Adventure Tours / VIP tours who had fought in the Vietnam War and with the way he had shared his experiences and challenges fighting and surviving the war. He has shrapnel and bullets in his body until today and he recollected the details of fighting along the American GIs and being trained by the Americans for war as he was an officer in the South Vietnamese Army.
The host was very knowledgeable and the tour was exceptional. It gave each visitor a glimpse of the Vietnamese determination to be self governed and the horrors of war on both sides.
Great experience with a super tourguide who shared many personal experiences with the group of his time fighting during the Vietnam War

When the couple walked away, they were able to explore the narrow tunnel, cooking, and get a glimpse of the base of Viet Cong.  Watch the amazing traps for enemies and play the admirers!  Be careful not to get lost in the imaginary cave maze scale!

Very interesting visit to these tunnels, so important in the history of war.  Very well explained the background and every detail of the organization of the Vietnamese guerrillas in the area near Saigon.  Entering the tunnels is a magnificent experience.  Thanks to Ms. Duyên for the kindness and detailed explanation.  A very pleasant visit.

Highly recommended! Pha out tourist guide is the best tourist guide I have ever had. She was nice & friendly and helpful. And the Driver huy he was so friendly too. I highly recommend again it was really nice trip and unforgettable trip .

This complex network of underground tunnels, bunkers, and booby traps provided shelter and a strategic advantage for Viet Cong soldiers during the Vietnam War.  We learned how they used ingenious methods to conceal themselves and ambush their enemies. Crawling through the narrow tunnels, even for a short distance, gave us a newfound appreciation for the resilience and resourcefulness of the Vietnamese people during this tumultuous period.

This was a highlight, such an amazing experience - sure a lot of it is touristy (shooting the AK47, for all the $$$ was still a fun experience) but learning about the history if fascinating. Bury yourself underground, crawl through the tunnel system - just look out, it can get claustrophobic in there despite being small!

Amazing day tour to the warriors place: Cu Chi TunnelI booked the half-day tour of the Cu Chi tunnels with my hotel, they connected with our tour ( VN Bikes) guide. I highly recommend it. I did the tour with Beautiful Flower (XUYEN) and Mr. love. They made the tour great and very personal. She explained in complete detail about the place and was really kind.If you like history of Vietnam war, you'll be very impressed with the tour. It is very eye-opening.

There is a lot to see and take in at the Cu Chi Tunnel’s. The guide was very informative and great sense of humor. War is always such a sad event and wish we could all learn from the mistakes of the past.The bus left from District 1 at 8.15am and we returned at 1.30pm.If your game you can have a shot at many different guns : AK47, M16, M60 to name a few. The M60 was a lot of fun however beware the bullets don’t come cheap.
Tunnels have air-conditioning not too hot and you guide can choose short or long tunnels for traveling underground,...
Well conducted. Efficient without a queue. The guide was excellent, history and explanations done very well. Definitely recommend.

This tour was amazing! Our tour guide Tommy, a wonderful person, explained the history and the background of these tunnels pretty well and always had really good energy.You will see the tunnels and learn more about how people lived in those, how they are build and if you want to you can also go into one. Just amazing! Overall a wonderful experience I would definitely recommend!!!! Thank you
